description:
Players have to roll dice and move their cars in a Trivial Pursuit like board with Roman places like a Roman driver and collect tokens for paying parking at the end of the game.

Some places let user to pick cards sometimes helpful, some other times disadvantageous cards, other places let the player to pick a Quiz/Challange card and win tokens.

After visiting a required place, is the time to stop in a parking slot, pay one token for a parking card and hope it's a free parking.

Everdell Kindergarten Promo cards :

This pack contains 6 cards that may be played with the original Everdell base game.

4X kids (critter card)
2X kindergarten (building card)

These cards are not meant for play with My lil' Everdell.

The Kindergarten card allows you to use an occupied token to play a Kids card for free, and you may do this twice during the game. If playing with Newleaf, you may not use two Golden occupied tokens on this card, though you may use one Golden occupied token and one normal occupied token.

Also contains 4 cards for my lil' everdell

2x My Lil' Everdell Main Deck Critter Cards* (Teacher and Coach)
2x My Lil' Everdell Main Deck Location Cards* (Kindergarten and Playground)

Ma-Riot is a blindfolded board game themed on the magical sport of "Ma-Riot" from another world. This is Wonderland, a land in another world. This year, the popular Ma-Riot League begins again. This is the gateway to success for young mages. Players become up-and-coming mages and form teams called "clans" with stuffed animal "puppets" to compete in the Ma-Riot League.
The game takes place on a 5 by 5 grid, every player has their own copy of the board on which they place their puppets so the other players can't see them. You command them to guess the positions of the invisible opposing player's Puppets and attack them by unleashing spells. Sometimes, Mags' trump card, the Clan Spell, is used to change the course of the game. Players earn points by hitting other player's puppets, first player to earn the desired amount of points wins.

boardgame: Battletech: Force Packs Record Sheets – Wave One
description:
A booklet of record sheets for the A Game of Armored Combat box set, Beginner Box set, Clan Invasion box set.

Also included are record sheets for the Clan Command Star, Clan Heavy Striker Star, Clan Elemental Star, Inner Sphere Command Lance, Inner Sphere Battle Lance, Legendary MechWarrior Pack, Salvage Box: UrbanMech.

description: This is a booklet of record sheets for the Clan Ad Hoc Star, Clan Fire Star, Clan Heavy Star, Clan Heavy Battle Star, Clan Support Star, Clan Striker Star, ComStar Battle Level II, ComStar Command Level II, Inner Sphere Fire Lance, Inner Sphere Direct Fire Lance, Inner Sphere Heavy Lance, Inner Sphere Heavy Battle Lance, Inner Sphere Striker Lance, Inner Sphere Support Lance, Inner Sphere Urban Lance, Salvage Box: Shilone.

description: This is a booklet of record sheets for the Wolf’s Dragoons Assault Star.

description: This is a booklet of record sheets for the UrbanMech Lance.

Word Buzz! is a word game similar in style to Password: A player draws a topic card, keeping it hidden from their teammates, then must describe the topic for their teammates to guess within a set period of time. However, before beginning their description, the player must also roll a 1d6 "forfeit die", the result of which affects what they may say to their teammates. For example: a '1' means the player may only speak in the past tense; a '2' means the player may only use words of six or fewer letters; a '6' means the player may only speak in rhyme; and so forth.

Teams score a point if their player can adhere to the forfeit conditions and the teammates guess the topic. If the player does not adhere to their forfeit, the opposing team may hit the buzzer and score a point, and also end that player's turn. If the player's teammates are unable to guess the topic, the opposing team has one guess. If correct, they win a point, if not, no points are scored.

A bonus point may be scored if the player adheres to their forfeit and manages to avoid saying "um" or "er", even if their team does not correctly guess the topic.

Play then passes to the opposing team. The first team to reach ten points is the winner.

In High & Low, a quick card game for 2-4 players, players will be playing cards numbered 1-5 to a 5x5 tableau. If they play into a row or column with, respectively, a higher or lower value than all other rows/columns, they must take a High or Low token. The goal of the game is to collect an equal number of high and low tokens.

Lowest score wins (most numerous token type minus less numerous), with a couple variants for tiebreakers in case there is a tie.

The challenge comes in anticipating opportunities to collect tokens on subsequent turns, so as not to get stuck late without good options. Thus it includes aspects of hand management and anticipating opponent play.

The game can be played with a couple standard card decks, removing 25 cards numbered 1-5 (5 of each), in addition to chips or other generic component to act as High and Low tokens. A full rules document is available in the game's file section here on Board Game Geek.

—description from the designer

boardgame: MYSTERY BOOKS Dysturbia: The Case of the Dead Crow
description:
An abandoned house in the middle of the woods, an ominous presence, & a dead crow with a cryptic note attached to its neck... Do you have the guts to explore Creek Village and uncover the truth behind this dreadful case?

"The Case of the Dead Crow" is an elaborately crafted puzzle book with bonus content both within and beyond its covers.

boardgame: ESCAPE Dysturbia Advent calendar: A Taste for Revenge
description:
For years, fine-dining restaurants "Arcobaleno" and "Der kleine Löffel" have been in fierce competition over who can serve the most popular and exclusive Christmas dinner. And each year, envy, malevolence, and conspiracy dominate preparations for the feast. But when confronted with kidnapping and death, it becomes clear that this year's long-running feud is about to reach a new level. Find out who's willing to play dirty and serve up a side of murder with their Christmas dinner.

24 letters, each with challenging puzzles to unravel a thrilling story, all in one box for each day of Advent!
family: Misc: Advent / Christmas / Jule calendar game

description:
It's a simple running game. The aim of the game is to be the first to reach the last field. When a pawn lands on certain squares, the events from the game rules must be observed.

—user summary
family: Folk Tales & Fairy Tales: Peter Pan

description:
Tension mounts at Illumina University when Dr. Emma Lindberg disappears without a trace. In her wake, a trail of destruction: a damaged telescope, an erased hard drive, and the ashes of every shred of research she'd ever written. But that’s not all – eerie symbols etched into the floor suggest an unspeakable darkness has been unleashed.

The university turns to your detective agency for answers, and as you begin to peel back the layers of this mystery, you discover that the forces at play are far beyond anything you've encountered before. And as the clock ticks, you realize that the fate of the entire metropolis may rest in your hands...

"Pandemonium" is a 120-page desk calendar packed with 52 puzzles and a captivating storyline set in the dark and gloomy metropolis of Dysturbia.

Three Hags is a hand shedding game for two or three crafty players.  Be the first to empty your hand by adding combos of increasing power to the pile.  Hags, Imps, and Brooms are special cards that come in handy for more powerful combos.  Play your cards right and earn a big scoring bonus at the end of the round.

Three Hags utilizes a stock pile that players will draw from each time they play a combo.  With new cards comes new possibilities.  Will you hang on to cards with hope of a stronger combination later, or use them early on?  Use your combos and special cards wisely to empty your hand and win the round.

—description from the designer

In By the Seashore, you are a beachcomber searching the shoreline for beautiful things like sea glass, shark teeth, and sand dollars to add to your collection.

Play starts with six face-up cards in the middle of the table. On your turn, you can either trade up by discarding a card and taking one card of higher value or trade down by discarding a card and taking two cards of equal or lower value. Once you collect either a three-of-a-kind or a three-in-a-row, you can score that set; add one card to your tableau, pass one card, and dedicate the last for scoring. (You'll only score for symbols that you have dedicated in this way.) Different symbols shown on the cards have different scoring mechanisms, so cleverly arrange the cards in your tableau to maximize your points.

Play continues until everyone has at least five cards in their tableau, then you score. Whoever has the most points win!

—description from the publisher

You get stuck in the Diabolical Elevator, Solve the puzzles and take the elevator to 10F! Hurry, you only have 60 mins!

—user summary

Piégés dans un ascenseur infernal, vous n'avez pas le choix: résolvez les énigmes, atteignez le dixième étage et méfiez-vous des apparences.
 
     Des cartes "énigmes" pour aller d'étage en étage
     Des cartes "code" pour trouver des combinaisons secrètes


Déconseillé aux claustrophobes!

—description from the publisher

Diversión con (Fun With) is a trivia game system played in 12 rounds.
In each round, a question card related to three randomly drawn knowledge cards is read.
Simultaneously, everyone at once chooses an answer, getting a point if the answer is correct.
Whoever at the end of round 12 has scored the most points wins the game.

In Diversión con animales (Fun with Animals) questions are related to the name, weight, height and origin of worldwide animals.
family: Organizations: Board Games Association of Creators in Spain (LUDO)

description:
Diversión con (Fun with) is a trivia game system played in 12 rounds.
In each round, a question card related to three randomly drawn knowledge cards is read.
Simultaneously, everyone at once chooses an answer, getting a point if the answer is correct.
Whoever at the end of round 12 has scored the most points wins the game.

In Diversión con banderas (Fun with flags) questions are related to the name, capital, population, temperature and surface of European countries.

description:

In each round, a question card related to three randomly drawn knowledge cards is read.
Simultaneously, everyone at once chooses an answer, getting a point if the answer is correct.
Whoever at the end of round 12 has scored the most points wins the game.

description:
HIJACK is a card game about terrorists taking over a plane and trying to get away with it, with the most cash in hand, by the time the plane has landed, crashed, or exploded in mid-air.

It is played without turns: as and when they choose, everyone just announces which card they're going to play, and on whom. An example exchange of cards might be:

Ady reveals that he is the pilot, and tells the others not to kill him because he is the only person able to land the plane. (There is only one PILOT card in the deck.) But Beth has a PARACHUTE card, so doesn't care if the plane crashes; she plays a SHOOT card on Ady, who avoids that with a DODGE. Colin has the other PARACHUTE and also SHOOTs at Ady. Deb has a FLUKE card, so she could save Ady with that, but she also has a SURVIVOR card so expects to walk away from the inevitable plane crash, should the pilot die, so she withholds that for later. Beth reveals her PARACHUTE card and jumps out of the plane. But Colin then plays PROPELLOR, which makes a gruesome mess of Beth. He then jumps out of the plane as well, surviving the crash with a total of $7m in CASH cards. Deb is the last player on the plane, so plays her SURVIVOR card to walk away with $9m CASH and wins the round.

ColourQuest is a strategic board game that involves navigating a map using color cards. The aim of the game is to travel across the map and collect the most points by capturing colored territories.

Players all start with their coloured token on a predetermined stating locations, then they draw five coloured cards from the colour deck. The first player then draws a territory card, and this territory becomes the target for that turn.

Using the colour cards, player must move across the map, capturing territory's to score points. At the end of each turn players discard a number of cards, depending on locations captured. The game continues until all territories have been captured.

Players must strategize which territories to capture to maximize their points. They must also carefully manage their color cards to ensure they always have a match to move. Players can also try to block their opponents' movements by occupying key territories.

The game ends when all territories have been captured. The player with the most points at the end of the game wins.

—description from the designer

boardgame: ノラネコ ボスネコ (Noraneko Bossneko)
description:
ノラネコ ボスネコ (Noraneko Bossneko) is a game from Japan. Stray kittens are in the park run by a boss cat. The grumpy boss cat will only let one kitten stay, the one that gets close but doesn't disturb. So they must sneak up quietly to the boss cat but not get caught out.

The game has a grid which the five kittens must advance across, and a chart measuring how angry the boss cat gets when disturbed. There are 25 stray cat cards (walk, run, make eye contact, look away, blink, 5 of each), and 8 boss cat cards for relaxing, normal, alert, vigilant and bad mood.

Players choose cards from their hand to move their kitten. The kitten furthest to the left moves first, and kittens in columns go from top to bottom. After the kittens move, a card is turned up for the boss cat's mood, and a marker might move on the scale. If the marker reaches the top of the scale, the boss cat lashes out and then becomes calm again, resetting the marker back to zero.

The kitten that successfully gets to the boss cat first is accepted and wins the game.
user summary

Players move with their token on a 40 space round track by 2 dice very similar to Monopoly. According to the space landed on, they may do a specific action. 21 spaces allow to buy a specific share. Any following player landing there must pay a dividend to the owner (similar to the rent in Monopoly). In both cases, a spinner decides the exact amount. If you have all 3 shares of the same category, you may invest up to 3 times 3000 guilders to cash in 5 times the amount for each investment.
In the beginning of the game, players receive an equal amount of event cards. One of it must be played when landing on one of the 8 spaces "beursdag". Players may swap, buy and sell shares among each other.
After an agreed time or when all players but one  are broke or when the bank runs out of money the richest player wins.

—user summary

boardgame: 勇者対策会議 (Yusha Taisaku Kaigi)
description:
勇者対策会議 (Yusha Taisaku Kaigi) is a tactical card game from Japan. Players take on the roles of Generals serving the Demon King. As a hero approaches to attack the Demon King's castle, the Generals must work together to defeat them. But if the hero is vanquished, a single winner is decided, based upon the strength of attack each General secretly held back in their hand.

Players send monsters to attack the hero, with the monsters card showing a value in stars. The players also use dice which amend the attack, and also determine how the hero moves. The hero can use weapons and items to defeat the monsters.

If the hero is killed, the stars left in each player's hand are totalled up for each player, with the most stars winning. If the hero reaches the castle, all the players lose. But a player can win the game by sudden death by managing to assassinate the hero in a single stroke.
user summary

The Judgment Table is an exciting board game that features two different game modes - competitive and cooperative. In this game, each player is assigned a capital sin and two secondary sins, and must work to identify the original sin(s) by asking and answering questions. The game is both fun and challenging, as players must use their wits and strategy to uncover the correct answers.

With its unique and engaging gameplay, The Judgment Table is perfect for players of all ages who enjoy strategy and deduction games. The game is easy to learn but difficult to master, ensuring that every playthrough is different and exciting. The customizable rules also allow players to adjust the game to their liking, making it a versatile addition to any game collection.

Whether you're playing competitively or cooperatively, The Judgment Table is sure to provide hours of entertainment for you and your friends.

—description from the publisher (English)

The Judgment Table es un emocionante juego de mesa que presenta dos modos de juego diferentes: competitivo y deductivo. En este juego, a cada jugador se le asigna un pecado capital y dos pecados secundarios, y debe trabajar para identificar el pecado original o pecados (en caso de que hubiera más de uno) haciendo preguntas y respondiendo a ellas. El juego es divertido y desafiante, ya que los jugadores deben usar su ingenio y estrategia para hacer las preguntas y responderlas de manera correctas.

Con su jugabilidad única y atractiva, The Judgment Table es perfecto para jugadores de todas las edades que disfrutan de los juegos de estrategia y deducción. El juego es fácil de aprender pero difícil de dominar, asegurando que cada partida sea diferente y emocionante. Las reglas personalizables también permiten a los jugadores ajustar el juego a su gusto, convirtiéndolo en una adición versátil a cualquier colección de juegos.

Ya sea que juegues de manera competitiva o cooperativa, The Judgment Table seguramente proporcionará horas de entretenimiento para ti y tus amigos.

—description from the publisher (Spanish)

"Recently graduated has a bomb defuser and the most dangerous device is your first job... a nuclear bomb contained inside a card!"

Try to disarm the NUKEDOKU card before time runs out or everything will blow away!

Set a timer and start to roll the dice allocating the results on the empty squares of the NUKEDOKU card, but remember that the numbers can't be repeated on the same file, column or boxes!

—description from the designer

boardgame: 勇者対策会議Ⅱ (Yusha Taisaku Kaigi II)
description:
勇者対策会議 II (Yusha Taisaku Kaigi II) is a tactical card game from Japan, re-working 勇者対策会議 (Yusha Taisaku Kaigi) with new rules and additional characters.

Whilst the main gameplay is the same, the players' characters now have special abilities they can use to improve their position. And the hero is now accompanied by hunters and priests to add the approach to the castle.

Players take on the roles of Generals serving the Demon King. As a hero approaches to attack the Demon King's castle, the Generals must work together to defeat them. But if the hero is vanquished, a single winner is decided, based upon the strength of attack each General secretly held back in their hand.

Players send monsters to attack the hero, with the monsters card showing a value in stars. The players also use dice which amend the attack, and also determine how the hero moves. The hero can use weapons and items to defeat the monsters.

If the hero is killed, the stars left in each player's hand are totalled up for each player, with the most stars winning. If the hero reaches the castle, all the players lose. But a player can win the game by sudden death by managing to assassinate the hero in a single stroke.
user summary

Do Not Hump is a novel take on roll-and-write games, using a common pool of dice per round as the incoming train to be humped.  Players form rail car sets which can then be "humped" together into like groups on their rail yard sheet that you can then "switch" to score.  After a set number of rounds, best humper wins!

The term Do Not Hump was stenciled onto particularly delicate rail cars so that it would not be forced down a hill by gravity into the right group of cars, potentially damaging its contents.

Omaha Spur is a “sharpie-rails” variant of traditional 18xx games for 2-6 players set in the greater Omaha/Council Bluffs area. It is a city-scale game that simulates the development of railway Systems, passenger and freight routes, and industries roughly during the period 1870 to 1930.

It generally follows traditional 18xx rules borrowed from 1830. There are stock rounds where shares are bought and sold, and operating rounds where tracks are laid, stations placed, and trains run for revenue.  Some parts from 1830 are necessary to play the game.

Rather than laying tiles, players draw track sections and place station tokens with their companies' colored sharpies to both indicate completion and ownership.  Companies can lease track from other players to complete their routes

The revenue values of hexes increase as game phases change and industries "pop-up" in those hexes at the end of each operating round from a pre-programmed chain of industry tokens.

As with traditional 18xx, players' portfolio and cash holdings determine the winner.

Published exclusively in the 2023 edition of TraXX Mainline magazine, 1864: Forty-Niner takes on the Transcontinental Railroad as a variant of 1861/67 set in the United States. Featured as in-game elements are the UP’s Bailey Yard, Moffat Tunnel, Lucin Cutoff, Sun Valley ski resort, the Credit Mobilier scheme, and the Forty-Niner's route itself!

—description from the designer

An uproarious party game of unleashing your inner villain! Invite
Maleficent, Jafar, Captain Hook, and Ursula to your game night! Shout out their famous catchphrases, and match other players with high fives, sword fights, and other wicked actions. Then race to grab a spoon and score! Evil laugh your way to victory in this fiendish frenzy!

—description from the publisher
family: Folk Tales & Fairy Tales: Peter Pan
Folk Tales & Fairy Tales: Sleeping Beauty
Folk Tales & Fairy Tales: The Little Mermaid

description:
Launch your fast-driving friends from Radiator Springs on a fun and friendly race. Whoever finishes four laps first wins! But watch out! Too much speed can send them crashing through Tail Light Caverns—and on a wild ride back to the checkered flag!

—description from the publisher
Test. Testing.
The players take turns to roll and move. The first player to reach space 30 wins the game. On some spaces events caused by Haribo products hinder or benefit the players.

A single card dextery game where only one thing matters: have the ring card!

If you don't have the ring, take it from whoever has it. If you already have it, use your reflexes to avoid other players.

As the ring bearer, you must keep the ring for several turns if you want to win the game, but it will cost you life force. If you run out of life force you will lose. And as a non-ring bearer, your efforts will be concentrated on stealing the ring, and recovering life force.

You can also use character cards to boost your skills and actions during each game.

—description from the designer
testing

A simple trivia game with a pop culture theme. On your turn, pick a card from the deck and give the the first clue. Then in playing order, the other players try to guess the character you're describing to get a max of point. No one gets it right! No worry, give a second clue and start asking the other players again for a lessr amount of points. Still no good answer, the third and final clue is read.and players may make a final try  for minimal score. The game ends when a player reaches 100 points.

—user summary
 Faites un saut dans le monde imaginaire grâce à ce jeu-questionnaire amusant ! Le Top 100 des personnages fictifs vous fera redécouvrir vos histoires favorites sous un autre angle ! Pour remporter la partie, soyez le premier à atteindre 100 points en trouvant les bonnes réponses avec le moins d’indices possibles.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Cards of the Dead: The Road (Day 2)
description:
The Road is the first expansion for Cards of the Dead: The Board Game, where the survivors will continue their journey through the road outside the city.

Broaden the base game experience with The Road.

Find new weapons, ransack new abandoned locations and square up to new varieties of zombie while surviving for one more day.

The Survivors will need to continue their journey along the course of a long road, rather than the mazy streets of the city.

boardgame: Cards of the Dead: The Laboratory (Day 3)
description:
The Laboratory is the second and last expansion to Cards of the Dead: The Board Game.  In it, players will expand on the original game adding a completely new zone, and also adding a brand new game mode: "New Game +"

Broaden the base game experience with The Laboratory.

Venture into the laboratory where the virus first emerged, square up against new varieties of zombie and find the cure for humanity it's too late.

Humanity is not yet lost and still has a chance. Find the Pathogens needed and produce a cure for the virus while fleeing from the hordes of zombies.

boardgame: Dungeons & Dragons: Onslaught – Never Split the Party
description:
Co-op comes to D&D Onslaught! In the Never Split the Party scenario, four players cooperatively play as the monsters - and the adventurers are the enemy! Adventurer behavior is dictated by behavior cards, unlocking a new way to play the 12 characters from the core set. Each kit will receive 6 scenario sheets and 6 decks of double-sided behavior cards – run the scenario in-store and/or pass them out to excited players!

—description from the publisher

ミツカルタ (MITSUKARUTA) is a Japanese speed word game.

This is a word game to find words with 3 or more letters in a row.
If you use 4 letters, you get 4 points, and if you use 5 letters, you get 5 points. Try to find a long word using as many letters as possible. The player with the most points wins the game, and if he or she can use all seven letters, it's a one-shot win!

It was produced and sold by Miyabi Games at Tokyo Game Market Autumn 2020 and remade by JELLY JELLY GAMES in 2021.

---

「ミツカルタ」は、場にならんでいる文字から、3文字以上の言葉をみつける新感覚のワードゲームです。
4文字使えば4ポイント！ 5文字使えば5ポイント！ なるべく多くの文字を使った長い言葉を探しましょう。7文字全部の文字を使えれば、なんと一発逆転勝利です！

2020年に雅ゲームズより製作販売された旧版を、JELLY JELLY GAMESが2021年にリメイクしました。

boardgame: 恋愛禁止学園 (Renai Kinshi Gakuen)
description:
恋愛禁止学園 (Renai Kinshi Gakuen) is a hidden identity game from Japan. 'Gakuen' is high school, and 'Ren-aikinshi' is the rule talent agencies apply to their stars forbidding personal relationships (because they think it deters the fans). So the game tile means a love-ban at high school. In this game, a player must find the one they are destined to fall in love with. The player count is 5 to 9, because of the minimum five roles in the game.

Set in a school where romance is prohibited, the goal is to fulfill the love between the main character and the destined person. However, if the disciplinary committee among the students finds out about the relationship, there is a danger of expulsion.

Players have five roles:
1. Protagonist looking for a destined person
2. A person destined to do his best to be confessed to by the main character
3. A friend who supports the main character and the destined person
4. If confessed to by the main character, the only rival
5. A disciplinary committee member who finds the person of destiny and expels him from school

Only the main character's role is revealed, and the identity of the other players is unknown.

Using the prepared character cards and combination attribute cards, you will play a role and perform events that are common in school things. Even if you know the other player's identity, the only way to tell it to the main character is through role-playing.

Depending on their role, each player has a different win condition.

An expansion pack was published later.
user summary

boardgame: The Lord of the Rings: The Card Game – The Dream-chaser Hero Expansion
description:
Long have the Elves of the Grey Havens build majestic ships to sail into the West. No one knows the seas as well as they, but these Noldor sailrs and navigators must still be ever vigilant. The sudden threats of severe storms or fleets of pillaging Corsairs always lurk on the horizon.

The Dream-chaser Hero Expansion for The Lord of the Rings: The Card Game includes 8 playable heroes and all of the player cards and customization options originally featured in The Grey Havens and the Dream-chaser cycle of content. With iconic characters such as Denethor, Glorfindel, and Círdan the Shipwright, this expansion is a must-have for players looking to complete their collection.

This expansion can be used to build or enhance player decks for any The Lord of the Rings: The Card Game scenario or campaign.

—description from the publisher
family: Collectible: Living Card Game (Fantasy Flight Games)
Game: The Lord of the Rings – The Card Game (LCG)

boardgame: The Lord of the Rings: The Card Game – The Dream-chaser Campaign Expansion
description:
For an age past, Elves have departed from the Grey Havens, sailing the western ocean of Middle-earth in beautiful white ships, often never to return. But a storm is brewing on the horizon, and something foul awaits on a forgotten isle of Númenor.

In the Dream-chaser Campaign Expansion for The Lord of the Rings: The Card Game, one to four players sail across the oceans of Middle-earth, battling Corsairs on the high seas and exploring uncharted ruins of Númenor, all while defending the Grey Havens from fierce raiders. Over the course of nine scenarios—which can be played standalone or as part of a larger campaign—the heroes must confront pirates, wraiths, sea monsters, and a persuasive villain who commands a fleet of powerful ships.

This expansion includes all of the scenarios originally featured in The Grey Havens and the Dream-chaser cycle of content, now repackaged into one box along with all-new campaign content.

—description from the publisher
family: Collectible: Living Card Game (Fantasy Flight Games)
Game: The Lord of the Rings – The Card Game (LCG)

boardgame: 恋愛禁止学園 拡張セット (Renai Kinshi Gakuen Expansion Pack)
description:
恋愛禁止学園 拡張セット (Renai Kinshi Gakuen Expansion Pack) is an expansion pack for 恋愛禁止学園 (Renai Kinshi Gakuen). It adds new cards, new characters, new abilities, and new rules to allow 2-5 players or for playing in two groups.

The new characters in the game are Chiaki Takakura (an Ore-sama type - reformed trouble-maker)and Hinano Aizawa (a Yurufuwa type - soft and gentle). With the addition of characters, Boy Love play and Yuri play are possible even with 9 people (forms of popular manga romance genres). The additional characters allow the new rules to be played.

There are 8 new Character Attributes, expanding the game for all rules sets. The added character attributes include: 'blood relatives', 'unknown gender', 'Otaku', 'Yandere' and other styles from popular genres.

The two new rules sets are Coupling Jijiki, and Group Dating. Coupling Jijiki is for 2 to 5 people, and lets players create couples by combining characters and attributes. The player must improvise an explanation as to why these two belong togetehr and the players vote to agree or disagree. If the couple is accepted, it is played in the game combined. The couple gains a Like Token which is a new feature in the game.

Group Dating lets players form two groups of 4, 6, or 8 people. Attribute cards are mixed together and exchanged across the groups. By role-playing your attribute, you can find your destined partner, and also reveal confessions from the other group. But because your attributes are now known to others, you have to be careful keeping your identity concealed from your group.
user summary

Century Big Box contains the three titles in the Century game series — Spice Road, Eastern Wonders, and A New World — along with all promo items released for these games and a new mini-expansion, Golden Deals.

Each game can be played independently, but they can also be combined with one another or played all at once, giving you seven different ways to play.

The Golden Deals expansion consists of clear cards with effects on them, giving you something new to acquire and allowing you to modify the cards in play.
family: Versions & Editions: Big Box Versions of Individual Games

description:
Century: Golden Deals is an expansion for Century: Spice Road, whether you're playing that game on its own or combined with one of the other Century titles.

Golden Deals consists of clear cards with effects on them, giving you something new to acquire and allowing you to modify the cards in play.

boardgame: Lone AstroTank: A Solitaire Sci-Fi Wargame
description:
The AstroTank will be developed in the twenty second century to counter perceived threats from as yet undiscovered Alien species, a threat heightened by the discovery of ‘faster than light’ space travel, the possibility of which had previously been doubted. Finally completed, the AstroTank will be required in the early twenty third century when first contact is made with an aggressive Alien species who want nothing more than to colonise as many planets as possible for their own consumption. Despite some attempt at a peaceful settlement, the Aliens will breach a preliminary agreement and construct a base on our own Moon. As one of Earth’s non-negotiables, it will result in all-out war between the two species. The Earth’s newly formed Astro Defence Force (ADF) will be ordered into combat for the first time.

Lone AstroTank is a hex and counter wargame that puts you in charge of a single AstroTank and its three Crew members as it is dropped onto different planets to assist with the removal of the Alien presence. You will need to manage the Crew to enable them to operate the AstroTank in the most effective way possible, whilst facing dangerous, aggressive and terrifying Aliens in a selection of varied and challenging Missions. Whilst this game is not a ‘narrative driven’ game as such, with a little imagination it will provide memorable stories. Some events are randomly generated, but the Player generally has at least some degree of control over the level of risk they wish to take and how vulnerable they leave their AstroTank and its Crew to these Events.

—description from the designer

description:
Fussball Fieber is a card game for 2 players. The soccer ball is moved across the playing field using playing cards. The game is played in two halves. Players try to score goals and win with tactical moves such as corner kicks, shots on goal, penalties, free kicks, throw-ins and goalkeeper saves.

—user summary

boardgame: Lost Ruins of Arnak: The Missing Expedition
description:
Follow a trail to learn the fate of Professor Kutil and other missing explorers in Lost Ruins of Arnak: The Missing Expedition.

In this expansion, you can test the strategies offered by two new leaders, explore new paths to knowledge on two new research tracks, and build your expedition team with new artifacts, items, and assistants.

This expansion can simply be added to the Lost Ruins of Arnak base game, or it can be discovered as part of a solo or two-player co-operative campaign that consists of six chapters, each with a different set of rules, goals, and achievements.

This expansion requires the Lost Ruins of Arnak base game and is fully compatible with the Expedition Leaders expansion.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Murder Mystery Party: A Cuban Killing
description:
The Cuban Nova in Havana.  In 1952. is where the rich and famous come to play.  This is the height of the season but not everything has gone according to plan.  Yesterday evening, the hotel manager , Rico Ricardo, was found dead. And as the police believe  - he's been murdered.  That means that the guests are now suspects:-

C.D Gonzalez- Rico's business partner an one of the wealthiest men on the island
Lolo Cabana - A showgirl whose friendship with Rico took hr off the stage and into management
Sir Ian lemming - A British writer of spy stories and rumour has it maybe a spy himself.
Lady Lemming - Sir Ians wife and a successful film actress in her own right,
Lady Common - Lady Lemmington's sister , whose husband died tragically young leaving her an immense fortune.
Che Guava - A Cuban playboy and would be politician
Nico Marx - The most celebrated of the American comedy troupe the Marx sisters
Butch highway - An American writer whose love of dangerous sport is topped only by his love of himself.

Rabbids are back for epic 2 player (duel) or multiplayer (battle royale) battles!

Choose your hero among the magician, the pirate, the knight, the scientist, the ninja and the hunter. Adapt your strategy according to your hero and your deck to win.

Make the most of your Moona crystals to use your weapon, stance, item and trick cards to take on the other players. But beware of your opponents who will try their best to eliminate you with their card combos which, depending on the hero, can be devastating.

This game is for all strategists who like to build their decks but also for those who love general brawls and are looking for glory and fame.

Let the battle begin!

The Illimat Syzygy Coin: A bronze challenge coin that when used as an okus has official gameplay properties. The coin uses the Luminary Sun & Initiate Moon illustrations by Carson Ellis from the game Illimat.

The Luminary Matriarch added a special rule when using the Syzygy Coin as an okus. When a player clears a field and collects the Syzygy Coin, they must immediately flip the coin; this occurs before drawing cards, refilling the field, or revealing the Luminary.

If this flip reveals the Sun, it’s Opposition. The player who claimed the coin must give one card from their hand to each of the other players in the game, beginning with the players to their left and continuing clockwise.

If this flip reveals the Moon, it’s a Conjunction.  The player who claimed the coin must take one card chosen at random from the hand of each player, beginning with the player to their left and continuing clockwise.

The effects of the Syzygy will leave one or more players with more than four cards in their hand. This is temporary. Players only draw cards at the end of the turn if they have fewer than four cards in their hand.

The coin was originally commissioned by Benjamin Franklin to circulate the meeting dates of his local Philadelphia society chapter. Today, its minted in partnership with the artisans who bring the worlds of The Lord of The Rings, Game of Thrones, Labyrinth, and The Kingkiller Chronicles into the palm of your hands, Shire Post Mint.

boardgame: Something Wild! Star Wars Classic Darth Vader: Pink Edition
description:
A fast-paced, light card game for the whole family! Featuring a collectible pink Darth Vader Pop! mover, as well as your favorite characters from the iconic Star Wars universe.

A line of card games featuring beloved characters and collectible Pop! pocket figures. Play numbered and colored character cards to make sets and runs. Score a set to snag the character figure’s special power that helps you win! The first player to score three powers wins the game. Combine multiple games to add more character cards, more Pop! figures, and more powers to your game!

One Pocket Pop! Darth Vader mover that lets you use Powers.
Nine classic Star Wars characters in full color card art.
Use new Powers to swap cards, score points, and take the Darth Vader mover away from your opponents!

Special Pink Edition makes a great Valentine’s gift for someone you love.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Something Wild! Marvel: Holiday Baby Groot
description:
A fast-paced, light card game for the whole family! Featuring a collectible Baby Groot Pop! mover, as well as your favorite characters from the iconic Marvel universe. Power Cards add fun new twists to the classic card gameplay. Combine with other Marvel Something Wild! games to add more characters, Pop! figures, and Powers to your game!

A line of card games featuring beloved characters and collectible Pop! pocket figures. Play numbered and colored character cards to make sets and runs. Score a set to snag the character figure’s special power that helps you win! The first player to score three powers wins the game. Combine multiple games to add more character cards, more Pop! figures, and more powers to your game!

One Pocket Pop! Baby Groot mover that lets you use Powers.
Nine classic Marvel characters in full color card art.
Use new Powers to swap cards, score points, and take the Baby Groot mover away from your opponents!
Baby Groot’s festive Santa hat and special winter versions of the characters make this the perfect holiday gift!

SILK HAT is a game where players use tangram type pieces to get other players to guess words from clue cards. Each card has four options for the artist, worth a different number of points based upon the difficulty.

Players guess at the same time while the artist is forming the shapes into their clue; once someone guesses correctly, both the artist and the correct guesser earn points equal to the difficulty of the clue.

The first person to 6 correct guesses or at least 13 points, wins.

Monsters Love Vegas is a flip-and-write game in which you trade Vegas items for better Vegas items!

The goal is to have the best items worth the most points at the end of twelve turns. On your turn, you trade items you have on your player sheet for what is available from one of the nine cards. New cards are flipped randomly each turn, giving you new options for trade.

—description from the publisher

Described on the box as "An exciting way for kids to learn about the importance of recycling while developing problem-solving, critical thinking, and strategizing skills!"  Recycle Rally challenges players to clean up the town by rolling dice to move along the board and collect tokens.  Play continues through three in-game weeks and the player with the most points wins...unless there are too many tokens in the landfill, in which case all players lose.

The card game of kittens and spells!

These wizard kittens have accidentally unleashed the contents of a book of curses! You and your fluffy friends must collect the escaped curses and put them back before the librarian, Professor Whispurr catches you!

This Library Edition of Wizard Kittens includes an additional character cat mat, Cinder, as well as the Magical Monsters Expansion with enough cards for a 5th player! Van Meowsing also joins your courageous crew of kittens to help defeat new magical monsters!

An adorably magical semi-cooperative set collecting card game by Magpie Games.

—description from the publisher

Seeker's Quest is an LDS scripture based card game. Each card in your hand is a verse of scripture. Take those verses from your hand and play them onto the tabletop into chapters. Verses build into chapters, and chapters merge into Books. As you complete books, draw blessings until you're a winner!

In addition, most verses have special rules, or effects, that are based on the meaning of the scripture. These effects make those verses even more powerful in the game. Each one can change the course of the game a little. Combine this verse in your hand with that verse on the table, and you could just draw the winning blessing! Look for the “Pearl of Truth” verses for extra power!

After the game, trade verses from your collection. The more you can collect, the more you can combine into the strongest deck to play with your friends. Learn Strength, Faith, Wisdom, Histories, Life, and Trials to complete the Seeker's Quest!

—description from the publisher
family: Religious: Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints

description:
Wealthy nobles treasure the eggs of dragons. Some think they taste great with salsa. Others want to raise baby dragons. Either way, they’re willing to pay you in bags of gold for risking your skin to steal the eggs. How much are you willing to risk? Each toss of the dice shows how many eggs you can steal. But each time you try to steal, there's a chance the mama dragon will wake up and flame you! Just how far will you go for the fortunes of the eggs?

—description from the publisher

Wyrmheist is a push your luck dice game about stealing eggs from a dragon!

boardgame: On The Spot: The Hilarious Party Game To Hot Seat Your Friends
description:
On The Spot is the hilarious party game to hot seat your friends.

You get to ask thing you would otherwise never know--you will finish this game closer to every single player.

Original is rated R, and there is a family version rated PG.

The player with the closest birthday get the red 'You Are On The Spot' first. Everyone takes 5 cards from the stack.

Each round:

Start the 2 minute timer.
Begin grilling the player 'On The Spot'.
Moving clockwise, players ask 1 question at a time.
Discard the used card in front of the player 'On The Spot'.
Pick up a new card, so you always have 5.
Once time is up, the 'You Are On The Spot' card moves clockwise.
Rinse and repeat.

Optional play to win: Save all the cards you answer. At the end, count your cards. Most questions answered wins!

WARNING: You may learn too much about other players. EXTREMELY DEEP AND RAUNCHY. **This is a party game designed for a young adult audience** There is a different family version with wholesome questions.

NOTE I: You are allowed 1 skip no-questions-asked each round. Group decides consequences of skipping more than 1.

NOTE II: Group is allowed to ask for 3 "story times" each round so player 'On The Spot' can further explain their answer. Pause the timer by laying it on its side during story times.

—description from the publisher

Toast to the top with Toasty Toasts! Combine delicious Topping cards, use sneaky Actions, and create the tastiest Toasts spread to score the most points!

Toasty Toasts is played over several turns, where each player may take a number of actions before passing the turn to the next player on their left (clockwise). Turns continue until the “Time to Eat!” card is drawn, at which point the game ends, and players proceed to the scoring phase.

Each player turn can be split into three quick phases (Draw, Play, Hand Check). Once the active player has completed two actions—or passed—they check their hand to ensure they have seven or fewer cards. Should their hand hold an excess of seven cards, the active player must place cards from their hand into the discard pile until their hand holds seven cards. Play then passes to the player on the active player’s left.

Once the “Time to Eat!” card is drawn, play ends immediately. Each player counts their score and the player with the most points wins!

description from designer

In Coup you must eliminate the others by wisely using the skills of your cards (or lie using skills of characters you don't have). It seems easy but... be careful! If you are challenged and you have lied you will lose your card. The last person to survive with at least one of their cards wins.

    Coup X includes Coup + the Reformation Expansion, except for the Inquisitor figure.

description:
Swords & Souls adds two new enemies to the base game that bring new abilities, challenges and play styles, meet The Navy and The Ghosts.

Being a notorious pirate crew may have its advantages but it also means your list of enemies grows. From the Royal Navy seeking to rip out any ship that sails under a black sail to the relentless souls of the Ghost pirates. These two new enemies provide an increased level of challenge and complexity to your base game of We're Sinking.

The Commander Add-on provides fourteen new cards that can be added to your Archangelis Core Set.

Eight of the cards are the commanders:  Powerful archangels of legend:  Michael, Gabriel, Raphael, to name a few.

At the beginning of the game, players can either select a commander of their choice, or they can be handed out at random.  The card is placed face-up in the player's play area, and grants a global bonus throughout the game.

The remaining six cards are the new Title cards.  These cards are shuffled into the Holy deck of the Store:  If purchased, they can be placed in the player's play area face-up, granting bonuses to specific angels and demons.

The world is full of mysterious and mystical places!  The Archangelis Core Set provided players with various powerful locations across the United States.  With the World Locations Add-on, you can now combat for control of locations around the world.

The add-on contains twelve new location cards, that can either be shuffled into the location cards from the Core Set, or substituted for the Core Set locations.

Bring more friends to the battle!

The Four Player Add-on includes all the cards needed to expand the Archangelis game to allow 2 to 4 players.

The card included are the base deck cards required for an additional two players:  14 Erelim cards, 6 Nephalim cards, and 10 Ophanim cards.

Here comes trouble!  With the Unlikely Allies Add-on, you'll be able to add more demons, profane humans, foul curses, and unholy relics to your Archangelis game!

This add-on includes: 2 new demon cards, 8 new humans, 6 new miracles, and 6 new relics.  The sacred cards (white back) can simply be shuffled into the Holy deck...and the profane cards (red back) can be shuffled into the Infernal deck.  And that's it...you're ready to go!

-description from designer

Get the full Archangelis game experience with the Core Set, plus four add-ons all in one box!  In this deckbuilding game, you will purchase angels, demons, humans, miracles, and relics from the store...building your deck until your army of angels is ready to conquer locations around the world!  Conquer enough locations...and it will be time for the final battle against your opponents!

The Core Set contains 100 cards, all that is required for two players to play Archangelis.

The Four Player Add-on adds the ability to invite up to two more players to your game.

The Unlikely Allies Add-on introduces new, sinister strategies.  It includes 22 new demons, humans, miracles, and relics...of a more evil variety.

The Commander Add-on contains 14 new cards:  Power commanders you select at the beginning of the game, providing global bonuses.  Additionally, it adds Titles which can be purchased from the store...making your angelic and demonic forces stronger.

The World Locations Add-on contains 12 new locations from around the world, to supplement the United States locations from the Core Set.

Comes with a deluxe rulebook, as well as a deluxe box big enough to contain the Core Set and all Add-ons.

-description from designer

Episode 0 (available on #FreeRPGDay) sets the stage for nine 30-minute episodes, formatted like a TV show ending in a huge season finale! This unique RPG is perfect for first-time Game Masters, with its episodic pacing, immersive world, and approachable rules!

—description from the publisher

The mini-expansion contains one new double-sided card to integrate with the base game:

     Architect (Architekt) on one side
     Investment broker (Investitionsmakler) on the other side

Players can take destruction to the next level with Marvel: Crisis Protocol – Immortal Hulk. This alternate version of a fan-favorite character harkens to his incarnation in the smash-hit Immortal Hulk comic book run. With an all-new miniature, new Team Tactic Cards, and new Stat Card abilities, the Immortal Hulk has eternal staying power on the tabletop.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Marvel: Crisis Protocol – M.O.D.O.K. Scientist Supreme
description:
Players hover to new heights of petty crime with the Marvel: Crisis Protocol – M.O.D.O.K. Scientist Supreme character pack.

This unique version of the character is both sinister and wild, giving players unforeseen methods to engage and disrupt their opponent. With an all-new miniature, new Team Tactic Cards, and new Stat Card abilities, M.O.D.O.K., Scientist Supreme aims to take control of the tabletop.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Star Wars: Legion – Clone Commander Cody Commander Expansion
description:
One of the Galactic Republic's most dependable Clone Commanders comes to the table in Star Wars: Legion – Clone Commander Cody Commander Expansion.

As the commander of the 7th Sky Corps, CC-2224, known as "Cody", has developed a distinguished reputation as a skilled strategist and fierce fighter. This pack brings him to the tabletop with three miniatures that can be assembled in a variety of poses and with multiple weapon and head options. In addition to making use of Cody's skills on the battlefield, players can also alter their strategy with his three signature Command Cards.

—description from the publisher

A.V.G. – The Flying Tigers is a book-based solitaire (or up to three player) air war game that recalls when a ragtag group of American pilots volunteered to fly for China against the Japanese. Recruited from the reserve officer ranks of the Navy, Marines, and Army, one hundred pilots signed contracts to fight. Of those, 99 went to Burma and China. Working together under Claire Lee Chennault, together they gave the nationalist government of Chiang Kai-shek an “instant air force”.

Equipped with the fast and well-armored Curtiss P-40B Tomahawk, the pilots of the American Volunteer Group (A.V.G.) held the line against a numerically superior, better-trained cadre of elite Japanese pilots, including a number of aces, who flew aircraft that were far more maneuverable.

Soon after the Japanese attack on Pearl Harbor, the A.V.G. began its combat operations. It wasn’t long before they scored their first success. On 20 December 1941, the pilots of the A.V.G. intercepted a Japanese bombing raid flying to bomb the western Chinese city of Kunming. Of the ten Japanese light bombers that were dispatched that day, the A.V.G. claimed three. A fourth bomber crashed on the way back to its base in occupied French Indochina. On Christmas Day, they flew again, this time downing 24 Japanese bombers and fighters.

It wasn’t long before TIME Magazine covered the story of the Flying Tigers, celebrating their victories against Japan. By April 1942, just five months after the Flying Tigers combat debut, Hollywood was already filming a movie about the outfit, starring John Wayne. Their fight boosted home front morale and inspired thousands to join the US Army’s expanding air force. In six and a half months, the Flying Tigers, as the A.V.G. came to be known, downed 297 Japanese aircraft at a loss of just 14 of their own.

In this game, you take on the role of the Operations Officer in charge of managing the A.V.G., its pilots, aircraft, fuel stocks, spare parts, and airfields. You will face the Japanese as they attack relentlessly, hoping to decimate your forces and bring the Chinese leadership in Chongqing to surrender to Japan’s rule. Hanging in the balance are hundreds of thousands of Japanese soldiers who are engaged in the war in China. If China were to capitulate, the bulk of those soldiers would be available to fight in New Guinea and Australia – or perhaps continue to advance to the west and attack India.

In the game, A.V.G. - The Flying Tigers, The future of the War in the Pacific hangs by a thread. Your assets are three squadrons of Curtiss P-40B fighter planes and the pilots of the Flying Tigers in your command. You must apply them well to defend China, take the fight to the Japanese, and achieve victory.

The game utilizes the roots of the same proven narrative-style system of the top-selling game, MAG-23 Guadalcanal. At the center of gameplay is your War Diary, your aircraft complement, and your pilot roster. A Chinese village-based early warning network will provide reports of incoming Japanese raids. As well, you will fly morning reconnaissance missions to identify targets on the many Japanese airfields, and then return to strafe them later in the day. Your forces will assist the British and Australian squadrons in the defense of Rangoon in Burma as well as fly to protect the Burma Road and the aerial lifeline of C-47 transport planes flying “The Hump” from India to China to bring in supplies, spare parts, fuel, ammunition, and equipment.

The Japanese will not be an easy opponent. On almost every mission, you will be outnumbered by their fighters, the Type 97 and Type 98, and face bombing runs from light and medium bombers. The Japanese will strafe your airfields, advance with their ground forces to take Burma and ultimately attempt a crossing into western China at the Salween Gorge.

A.V.G. – The Flying Tigers is a fast-playing solitaire air war game that is in the narrative-style. It is highly engaging and fun to play – and it is easy to learn. Each of the missions can be completed in less than thirty minutes. Utilizing the MAG-23 System, the game is not a simulation of air-to-air combat between individual fighter planes, nor a realistic depiction of the air war. It is an entertaining and educational air war game that allows you to experience and learn about the Second World War and its impact on China.

A.V.G. – The Flying Tigers is for experienced and novice war gamers alike. Though dice play a role, the outcomes of your missions and the campaign depends on you. The story of your time in China is as you write it. Will your pilots succeed? How many of your men will become aces? How many will be shot down? Will the Japanese defeat China? The outcome of the war in China is in your hands.

-description from designer
