description:
Inspired by it's predecessors, Super Duper Werewolves is a group role-playing game composed of VILLAGERS and WEREWOLVES – whose objective is to kill off the other faction.

With the introduction of Ability cards, gone are the days of playing a plain, boring VILLAGER every game. Ability cards equip all players with (you guessed it) special abilities, regardless of their faction, that can be activated throughout the game. From a WEREWOLF with a MASK or a MONARCH with OLD AGE, to a VILLAGER with OMEN, no game will be the same again!

Each player must place their workers in different areas to get resources or new workers. They must catch fish, get bread and wine.
To gain new workers you have to deliver wine in exchange.
To gain victory points you must feed the citizens of Village over 11 turns.

In December 1944, in some of the most bitterly cold weather experienced on the West Front.  German forces thunder into the snow-covered Ardennes forest, opposed only by a few exhausted Allied troops as German armor and infantry forces raced across the allied lines and into the countryside.  The Allied player is in a desperate struggle to hold the front line and repel the all-out German offensive.

"Point Blank Winter Victory" is an expansion module for Lock 'n Load Publishing tactical World War 2 squad card "Point Blank V is for Victory." "Point Blank Winter Victory” is for 1-2 players and adds exciting new combat units, such as the German Tiger II, more famously known as the King Tiger tank, and the American M4A3 tank.  New terrain and objective cards and been included expanding already on the large terrain types in the game.  "Point Blank Winter Victory” takes you out of the dog days of summer and leaves players to contend with freezing weather with our added new snow and fog rules.  Each of the 12 new scenarios included presents the players with a unique situation involving squads of men, support weapons, leaders, and individual armored fighting vehicles.

This game pits the forces of the Allied and German forces against each other in the frozen Ardennes forest during the Battle of the Bulge. Each player has victory conditions determined by the scenario in which to defend or take objectives, seek and destroy their opponent’s units, or one of many other different scenario objectives.

The game is played on an abstract map board made up of terrain cards in the game and managed through a distance system that accounts for the range to targets, line of sight, and defensive attributes. The player has units that start out on the map and gradually work their way towards their objectives by advancing through the battlefield, all the while conducting combat actions against their opponent or defending their troops from return fire or whatever hell awaits them. Players draw cards from a common action deck where they will play actions on their units on the map board. The game is an IGOUGO impulse system, and turns are managed when the action deck is exhausted. (Some scenarios may require multiple deck exhaustion to finish the game). Actions in the game consist of Fire, Move, Assault, Rally, etc. The action cards contain dice icons on them to determine random results.

One of the unique features of the game is that it contains a deck of terrain cards that are not part of the action deck. As players change terrain, they will draw a terrain card in which their moving units will occupy. Some action cards, such as Recon, help players manage what terrain they occupy, but your opponent may have other plans for your moving troops during their turn.

The game can be played in about an hour (depending on the scenario size), and if you cannot find an opponent, then try the game solo system. In general, the gameplay is fast and exciting and compares to such legendary game systems as Up Front.

-description from publisher

Shrapnel: The Official BattleTech Magazine puts your loyalty to the test amid unrelenting BattleMech combat that spans the war-torn battlefields of the 31st century and beyond!
The head of a security team must seek unusual allies to keep the First Prince of the Federated Suns safe from assassination. An aging Elemental warrior imparts lasting lessons to a young cadet when pirates threaten everything he holds dear. A young IndustrialMech pilot must use the only weapon he knows in the face of rampaging occupiers.

In this issue, you’ll visit the Unwearied Second’s 1,000th birthday celebration, a gala attended by the upper crust of Lyran society. Then roll up your sleeves and dive into testing a dangerous but poweful experimental weapon system. Stand firm with the Nova Cats, who try to hold back the inevitable tide of destiny, and travel to the Mercenary’s Star to navigate through scams and con artists in order to find the right outfit to call home.

Sign your contract and seek victory, with the help of mercenary-advice articles, technical readouts, unit digests, an in-depth look at the true nature of the “Death to Mercenaries” edict, and more—all by BattleTech veterans and new recruits.

2-4 players compete on a 9x9 squared board. 4 players are recommended. Roll for how many spaces to move. Gameplay is fast paced. The win condition is to take out all enemy players. Best to play best out of 3.

Choose dice numbers 1-4 and put face up in corners. 1 goes first. Roll for how many spaces to move. Center squares are blockades. Land precisely on enemy to destroy them. No moving backward.

-description from designer

Gatefall: Monsters is an expansion to the Gatefall Core Set: Standard Edition and includes a stylized take on classic monsters. Each fighter brings new abilities to the game.

The Monsters base set includes The Monster, The Mummy, and Dracula and comes with a new gameboard.  Optional add ons include the Werewolf, the Creature and the Invisible Man.

It’s the classic game of Sorry with a GIANT twist! Start by placing your 3 giant pawns on your corner of the big, 64” x 64” vinyl mat gameboard. Spin the spinner to move your pawns out of the starting area, and then the fun begins! Players race to move their pawns around the board, sliding forward on Slider spaces if they’re lucky and getting knocked back to Start if they aren’t. Be the first player to get your 3 pawns around the board and safely to Home, and you win! SORRY! GIANT EDITION’s large vinyl mat is at home on your living room floor, or take it outside to take the competition outdoors! Simple to play but loaded with suspense, strategy, and a little luck, SORRY! GIANT EDITION is big, unforgettable fun for get-togethers with friends and family.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: The Amazing Life Hacks Card Game
description:
Life Hacks is a card game where players must attempt to pick the real Life Hack from three seemingly-bizarre answers on a red Question Card. Get it right and you keep the card and the points. Watch out for green Challenge Cards, where you must DO the Life Hack and prove it's real in order to keep the cards and the points!

—description from the publisher

Speedy Words fans will get to play their favorite word game again with a TWIST! Quickly come up with words that start with a given letter and that represent a certain category. Find words of the opposite category with the TWIST version of the game.

The 15 game cards in this Promo Pack were originally released from 2016 through 2020, through a variety of methods.

These cards can be shuffled into your Dungeon Deck during setup. It is not recommended to include more than one copy of each card in your game - if you already have a copy of a given card from its original release, you should play with only one of them in the Dungeon Deck.

Some of the promo cards in this pack were designed to work with specific CLANK! expansions. If you are not playing with the related expansion, it is recommended that you don't include these cards in your Dungeon Deck. (Or, when they appear during the game, ignore them and set them aside.)


     Mummy Hunters - This card works well with The Mummy's Curse.
     Trailblazer - This card works well with Sunken Treasures, The Mummy's Curse, and Expeditions: Gold and Silk.
     Lightning Reflexes - This card works well with Adventuring Party.


Included Cards:


     Dire Wolf
     Brawn vs. Boots
     Enchanted Dice
     Magic Spectacles
     Snack Table
     Siren
     Wishing Wall
     Friendly Watcher
     Mummy Hunters
     Wand of Time
     Expedition Leader
     Trailblazer
     Opal Apestone
     Door to Before
     Lightning Reflexes

boardgame: Murder Mystery Party Case File Puzzles: The Art of Murder
description: In Murder Mystery Party Case File Murder Board Puzzle - The Art of Murder, you gather and construct evidence to coordinate with the central murder board to solve the crime. A high profile case has suddenly landed in your hands...A 47 year old male and prominent gallery owner in Manhattan, was found dead in his studio. You must now work independently or with others to identify the killer’s motive, means and opportunity to commit the murder. Open The Art of Murder 6 Puzzles in 1 Murder Board Mystery to start the investigation.
family: Series: Murder Mystery Party Case Files (University Games)

description:
A solo game that requires nothing more than a few dice. The rules are simple enough to memorize, and short enough to fit on a business card.

Roll 3 action dice to manipulate the dice in your 3x3 square, using the actions on the card. Get 1 point for each row, column, or diagonal that has:


     the same colour
     the same number
     an ordered sequence


After 4 rounds, count up your score.  A score of 6 or more is considered to be a win.

—description from the designer

A festive party full of joy, music and… chillies! Now and then, a hot pepper pops up from the crowd and disappears again. Will you be the fastest to react once the right combination of the chillies is shown?

A fast-paced observe-and-act card game for the entire family, where the situation is continually changing. The player with the highest number of cards wins.

How to play?
Put the three double-sided cards with tasks (coloured edge) within easy reach of all players in the middle of the table.
Shuffle the remaining cards and deal 12 (16) of them to each player. Players keep their cards face down in a drawing pile in front of them. 
The active player turns their top card and puts it on their discard pile, face up. If one of the conditions shown on the task cards is met, the players try to be the fastest to cover the corresponding task card with their hand.

The conditions of task cards:

     All cards are showing 6 different kinds of chillies (some of them can be represented multiple times but each of them MUST be represented at least once).
     At least 5 identical peppers are being shown.
     Exactly 2 pairs (2 peppers of one and 2 peppers of another kind) are being shown.


If multiple conditions are being met at the same time, the first player to cover the highest priority task card wins the round. The player who wins the round chooses and takes any of the discard piles with one of the cards helping to meet the condition on the top. He places these cards at the bottom of his drawing pile.
The game ends when a player discards the last card from their drawing pile. The player with the highest sum of cards in their drawing pile and their discard pile(s) wins the game.

EspañaIQ is a game for Spain lovers and its communities. You will be able to do 400 questions with 3 answers, with many topics. From Altamira to present, geography, gastronomy, illustrious personalities, artists, scientists, prodigious inventions, athletes, this game will help you discover the most fascinating stories and facts of our lands and neighbors.

Quatro City is a one-of-a-kind wooden puzzle with unique pieces and a catching detective quest to be solved. Each piece with a detailed and bright illustration is soaked with the mysteries. This puzzle brings leisure activities to a whole new level by adding interaction and activity to routine puzzle assembling.

The first challenge facing you is to assemble the puzzle of the numerous streets of Quatro City. Having thoroughly scrutinized the mazes of the Quatro City, you will have all trump cards to get to the truth of the mystery that welcomes you on the next step. Scan the QR code to fully dive into the atmosphere of the town and continue the interaction with the game on your device.

From now on, you become a detective’s assistant who investigates the catastrophe in the City. Meet Mark Eidos - your partner in solving dozens of quests, riddles, and mysteries to unravel the secrets somebody is hiding from people. He will be a starting point for your new goal - becoming a professional sharp-eyed detective.

The game goes in an involving story-telling manner enabling you not only to observe the events but to take part and appear as a character.  This engaging narrative keeps you participated in the investigation and makes it entertaining.

Do not put the assembled puzzle away - separate pieces will help you to solve the quizzes and riddles. Additional detective tools, such as magnifying glass and vacuum pen, will be extremely useful for catching even the smallest details of the city parts.

Get to the truth of the mystery.

     1943 pieces
     4 reference poster for each part
     30 quest quizes and riddles
     1 visual novel
     8 side quests
     Access to website or App for solving quests
     Detective magnifying glass
     Set of 4 Parts of the detailed and bright illustration

„…three, two, one… ready or not, here I come!“ Cute animals in the forest are playing hide-and-seek, looking for the best hiding place. Can you find them all? Hide-and-seek is designed for players up from the age of three. There are several difficulty levels to choose from.

Goal of the game is collect as many animal cards as you can.

One player is a seeker. Seeker chooses six yellow animal cards and puts them picture up on the starting squares called HOME BASE. Then he shuffles all the blue animal cards and put the pack on the CARD PACK square.

He turns the cards on the starting squares face down. He moves individual yellow cards randomly from the starting squares to the hiding squares (HOLLOW, NEST, ANTHILL, BEEHIVE, THICKET, STONES, TREE STUMP) on the board. One of the squares will remain empty.

Now all the players take turns, clockwise. At the beginning of their turn, each player turns the blue animal card on the top of the pack. Then he takes an attempt to find the same animal on a yellow card hidden on the board.

If he succeeds, he keeps the pair.
If not, he returns the blue card to the bottom of the pack and places the yellow card on an empty hiding square of his own choice back face down.
If a player thinks that the animal is not currently hidden on the board, he announces this, puts the blue card face down in front of himself and takes a new one. Then he try to find the matching yellow card for the new animal. (BUT! the players get two penalty points on the end of the round, for every animal which is eliminated this way and yet its matching yellow card is found to be hiding on the board.)

Once all the hiding animals are successfully matched, the players count the cards they won and note the corresponding number of their points. This closes one round of the game.

The number of rounds corresponds to the number of players.
Players with the most of points on the end of all rounds of the game is winner.

boardgame: The Naval SITREP: The Journal of Naval Miniatures Wargaming #63
description:
The Naval SITREP is published twice a year by the Admiralty Trilogy Group. It supports the Admiralty Trilogy game system with scenarios, articles on history and technology, and rules expansions & corrections.

The Naval SITREP Issue #63 was published in October of 2022.

Table of Contents


    Features


The Swedish Navy and Air Force and Annexes C - L for Swedish Vessels and Aircraft Currently in Service

Designations and Names of Chinese Aircraft

PRC Fighters, Bombers, and Attack Aircraft of the 20th Century

CaS Scenario - Action off Ushant, June 1944, by Paul French

DoB Scenario - the Battle of Cape d’Orange

Adding British Gunboats

Winning Operation Sutton

Additions to the Red Fleet


    Rule Changes and Corrections


What is a Crossing Target?

Running Operation Sutton at Historicon 2022

Using Antiship Missiles for Land Attack


    Product Updates


New Ships from Shapeways

Brown Water Miniatures


    Book Reviews


Handbrake! Super Etendard Fighter-Bombers in the Falklands/Malvinas War, by Mariano Sciaroni & Alejandro Amendolara

Airborne Antisubmarine Warfare, by Michael E. Glynn

Tower of Skulls, by Richard B. Frank
family: Magazine: The Naval SITREP – Journal of Naval Miniatures Wargaming

description:
Doni Pepperoni is a crazy observation game!

Find matching ingredients and claim pizza slices before others do! But be careful what you say and do—if you lose focus, you’ll get a burnt slice. The players’ goal is to collect as many slices of tasty pizza as possible.

—description from the publisher

Doni Pepperoni to szalona gra na spostrzegawczość!

Znajdźcie pasujące składniki i zgarnijcie kawałki pizzy, zanim zrobią to inni. Uważajcie też, co mówicie i robicie – chwila nieuwagi i trafi do Was spalony placek. Waszym zadaniem jest zdobycie jak największej liczby kawałków smakowitej pizzy.

—description from the publisher (Polish)

The pets were playing hide-and-seek in the house, made an awful mess and lost their favorite toys. Help them find the lost toys, and they will come to you gratefully! Who will find the most pets?
In the Pets & Friends game, you will be looking for toy symbols that will help you acquire pet cards. Each earned pet card is not only points at the end of the game, but also special abilities you can use during the game!

—description from the publisher (translated)

Zwierzaki bawiły się w chowanego w całym domu, robiąc przy okazji straszny bałagan i pogubiły swoje ulubione zabawki. Pomóżcie im odnaleźć zagubione przedmioty, a odwdzięczą się i przyjdą do Was! Kto z Was odnajdzie najwięcej zwierzątek?
W grze Pets & Friends będziecie szukać symboli odpowiednich zabawek, które pozwolą Wam zdobyć karty zwierzątek domowych. Każda zdobyta karta zwierzątka to nie tylko punkty na koniec gry, ale również specjalne umiejętności do wykorzystania w trakcie rozgrywki!

—description from the publisher

Po prostu P Prostokąty (Simply S: Shapes) is a game in which every idea counts! Suggest the secret S-password to the other players, using only rectangles!

     See selected slip showing secret subject.
     Start showing shapes suggesting secret subject.
     Show, suggest, symbolize, stage, steer...! Someone says solution!
     Success? Score!


Simple? Somewhat...

—description from the publisher (translated)

Po prostu P Prostokąty to gra, w której liczy się każdy pomysł! Pokaż pozostałym zawodnikom sekretne hasło na literę P, używając do tego wyłącznie prostokątów!

     Pobierz papierowy prostokąt.
     Przedstaw powiedzenie przy pomocy prostokątów
     Poukładaj, poruszaj, podpowiadaj prostokątami! Pozostali próbują podać prawidłowe powiedzenie.
     Podałeś prawidłowo? Pobierz prostokąt – punkt.


Proste? Przekonaj się!

—description from the publisher

ReWording is a postcard word game that is for 2-5 players. Designed by Ian Howard.

In this competitive word game, players anagram and embellish words to secure the most points at the end of the game.

Each turn, the active player either: adds a new word by writing it in one of 5 sections on the shared postcard board; OR builds on a previously-added word by adding and rearranging letters. A player may instead pass, dropping out of the game.

Once all players have passed, score each section of the board individually. The player with the most words in that section scores 1 point for each word they didn’t write. The player with the most points wins!

boardgame: Arkham Horror: The Card Game – The Circle Undone: Investigator Expansion
description:
In the grim and brooding town of Arkham, arcane secrets lie buried with the dead. When sinister forces threaten to unveil these truths, it is up to a brave, foolish few to put a stop to it.

The Circle Undone Investigator Expansion for Arkham Horror: The Card Game features six investigators and contains all of the player cards and customization options featured in the original The Circle Undone cycle of products. Keep your allies’ minds from breaking as the psychologist, Carolyn Fern. Solve supernatural mysteries as the private investigator, Joe Diamond. Throw money at your problems as the millionaire, Preston Fairmont. Prevent terrible rituals as the redeemed cultist, Diana Stanley. Stay ahead of trouble or run headlong into it as the athlete, Rita Young. Or, tap into a mystical bloodline as the entertainer, Marie Lambeau.

The Circle Undone Investigator Expansion can be used to build or enhance investigator decks for any Arkham Horror: The Card Game scenario or campaign.

—description from the publisher's website
family: Game: Arkham Horror – The Card Game
Game: Arkham Horror TCG – The Circle Undone
Series: Arkham Horror Files (Fantasy Flight Games)

boardgame: Arkham Horror: The Card Game – The Circle Undone: Campaign Expansion
description:
Judgment. Temperance. Justice. The Tower. The soothsayer’s tarot wove a tale of a grim future, hard to put out of your mind. But when you learn that four people have disappeared without a trace from an estate in French Hill, you begin to wonder if the cruel fate told in the tarot is meant only for you, or for the entirety of Arkham.

In The Circle Undone Campaign Expansion, one to four investigators delve into the mysteries of Arkham’s past, uncovering its macabre history and the motives of those who dwell in its shadows through eight scenarios, along with a unique prologue scenario. Will these chosen few embrace the fate they have been dealt? Or will they forge their own destiny?

This expansion combines all of the campaign and scenario content from the original The Circle Undone cycle, which was the fourth cycle of expansions for Arkham Horror: The Card Game. Players only need a copy of the Arkham Horror: The Card Game Revised Core Set to dive into the story.

—description from the publisher's website

description:
Your dice are going to war! Using the Army Men d6 Dice Set (or any twelve standard six-sided dice), two players go head-to-head in this simple dice game. Perfect for children of all ages, especially those younger ones looking for something to do on a rainy day.

Ante one of your dice, roll the rest, freeze 1 or 2, and continue until you are out of dice.  Then your opponent does the same.  Whoever scores lowest (with 1s counting as zero points) takes back their ante while the other is discarded.

Lowest total score when one player is out of dice wins.

Inspired by the Tripps classic dice game

boardgame: Space Jam: A New Legacy – Serververse Showdown
description:
Introducing Serververse Showdown for Space Jam: A New Legacy, a card game that brings the story of the blockbuster family film to life in a strategic battle on the court! Serververse Showdown features fan-favorite characters from Looney Tunes and world-famous basketball players! Recruit your ultimate dream team and play to win, or risk being stuck in the Serververse with Al-G Rhythm forever!

As you play, cards will be drawn from the Game Deck and placed on either your court or your bench. Abilities will activate, including game-changing Hacked! cards that can rewrite certain rules mid-game.

Players take one of three actions on each of their turns:

Shoot! - Choose a character on the court to shoot, gaining cards from the Game Deck facedown as points

Substitute! - Rearrange cards between your court and your bench, setting up bigger plays

Glitch! - Eject one of your cards from the bench, letting you trigger their abilities a second time

It's a low complexity, high fun pick up and play card game. The first to 21 points (or the highest scorer once the deck runs out) wins!

Fly all over the world, to each of the cities in your itinerary, using your Travel Tokens and Standby cards wisely. Build an efficient itinerary that minimizes travel time and avoids weather. A good strategy will improve your odds of making the flight and each extra die you use gets you another chance at flying first class. Just make sure to have enough Travel Tokens for that last flight for the win!

The head of the crafter’s guild has retired and we must find a successor. Tradition is that the best two crafters create two sets of gems as fast as possible. The first to get them will be crowned “Master Crafter” and rule the guild. The other… Nobody really remembers who comes second.

This is the chance you’ve been waiting for! Get ready to race for fame, money, and a well deserved promotion.

Roll your dice, obtain materials, and craft gems or acquire improvements. Would you invest in tooling, or go ahead crafting gems trusting your luck?

---

Each colored dice has different odds of generating resources. i.e. yellow dice are more likely to generate green resources, and brown dice are more likely to generate blue.

Each turn you will roll your dice to generate resources and choose how to invest them: acquiring dice, purchasing cards to tweak your dice rolling, or just crafting gems. Some are much harder to craft than others!

Chose wisely and let the faster craftsman win!
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Clash is a card game for 2-4 players and the age target is 7 and above. Clash is a simple and easy game to play with some level of strategy involved.

The game starts with each player setting up a defense, after which they have to destroy each other’s defenses and then beat each other out in a duel. The last player standing wins the game.

The game consists of attack cards with different numbers, as well as 5 different action cards. The attack cards are primarily used to attack the opponents, whereas the action cards have different functions.

Gameplay (How to play):


     Shuffle the deck
     Deal 8 random cards to each player.
     All players set up 3 Defense cards, which leaves 5 cards in hand.
     In turn, use the Attack cards to destroy enemy defenses and use the Action cards to turn the tide of the battle to your advantage.
     The game is played in a clockwise direction and the person to the left of the dealer starts the game.
     All players will in turn try to destroy each other’s defenses.
         Players are allowed to perform up to 2 moves (plays) when it’s their turn as long as the moves are not of the same card type. E.g. it’s not allowed to play 2 Attack cards or 2 Action cards, but instead, playing an Action card followed by an Attack card is allowed.
         Use the Action cards to turn the tide of the game to your advantage
         Attack enemy defenses and destroy them all
         When the opportunity comes, reinforce your defense
    
     When a player loses all 3 Defense cards, then the player may be challenged by other players to a duel. The duel is carried out by both the duel initiator (the attacker) and the player with no defenses left (the defender) playing an attack card faced down, after which both cards are revealed at the same time.
         If the defender's card is weaker than the attacker's card, then the duel is lost and the defender is out of the game.
         If the defender's card is stronger than the attacker's card, then the duel is won and the defender is rewarded with +1 new Defense card (placed from the cards in hand). The defender gets to continue the game.
    
     The last player remaining wins the game.


—description from the publisher

Working on a ranch can be an exciting adventure, full of challenges, triumphs, and unforgettable experiences. Whether you're rounding up animals into their corrals, or taking them to the state fair, there's never a dull moment when you're living and working in the heart of the countryside.

Imagine the thrill of loading up your prize-winning cows, pigs, chickens, and sheep onto a trailer, driving them to the fairgrounds, and watching as they compete against the best animals in the state. It's a chance to show off your hard work, your skill, and your dedication to raising the finest animals around.

Of course, running a ranch is a big job, and sometimes you need a little help. That's where hiring farm hands comes in. Whether it's a few extra hands during the busy season or a full-time specialist to help manage the day-to-day operations, having a team of skilled workers can make all the difference.

Roll on the Range simulates all of that adventure into a single page PNP roll and draw experience. Designed and illustrated by Danny Devine, this new print at home game will be a favorite among ranchers of all ages.

Roll the dice. Allocate the dice by number order on the various actions selection cards. Players will take turns drafting and taking actions on their score sheets, unlocking new skills and bonuses along the way. From herding together animals into fenced configurations, to winning prizes at the state fair, you'll have plenty to explore in Roll on the Range.

—description from the publisher

Get slapping in this exciting, fast-paced card game where Ninja Cats battle and Cucumbers attack. Play against others to control the deck as you flip and slap your way to victory. Win cards by battling weapon wielding Ninja Cats, but be ready to steal the deck as you beat your opponents by slapping card combos first.

Players take turns flipping cards into a pile at the center of the table. If a player flips a number card, game continues to the next player. If a Ninja Cat card is flipped, the battle begins! The following player then has the number of chances indicated on the card to flip over their own Ninja Cat card. If they do, play continues to the next player, and the battle begins again. If they don't, the player who initially flipped the Ninja Cat card wins the pile!

Keep an eye out for card sequences which can be slapped at any time during the game, as the first person to slap them gets to collect the pile. But watch out for that sneaky CUCUMBER, because when it's flipped you must ATTACK! First person to slap the cucumber wins the pile! Special action cards make the game more intense by forcing players to shuffle or swap their whole deck. Collect the whole deck to win the game!

-description from designer

A farming game where players roll the dice to move around the board and purchase land, cattle, and hogs. Players sell crops, cattle, and hogs for money. Players deal with weather events and disasters. The goal is to have the most money when the game ends.

The Vornox and the Mylikar were once allied races. Their ties forged through the ages by cooperation and trade. That peaceful period of time, now known as the Age of Light, is barely a distant memory to the now warring nations. No one knows exactly where the line was drawn between these former allies, but the carnage that followed is now etched in stone, and continues to this day.

In Galactic Tactics players take on the role of two warring alien races. Players move around the game board using four unique ship types that the players drafted at the beginning of the game. Each ship offers a tactical movement sequence that can eventually score victory points by landing on one of three enemy planets, thus earning victory points. Ships can also be captured during battle, which can lead to a military victory by elimination/stalemate. Players will continue to move their pieces around the board in an effort to win the game by scoring enough victory points, or by forcing a stalemate.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Treasure Thieves is an action-packed, fast-paced, family-friendly card game. The goal is to have the most Treasure in your hand by the time the last card is drawn.

Simple enough, but there is a catch! Everyone is against you. Other players will do whatever it takes to nab your Treasure. Also hidden within the deck, are Treasure Thieves who will steal your booty from you.

Luckily, the deck holds cards that help you defend and protect your valuables, as well as do a little thieving of your own. Hey, it’s not called Treasure Thieves for nothing!

The game gets more intense with every turn, because less cards in the deck, mean less opportunities for you to score big.

So, if you’re into card games, treasure, pirates, or time travel get your hands on Treasure Thieves.

—description from the designer

World War II has ended. The Third Reich has been destroyed. Former war- time allies, the United States and the U.S.S.R, grow increasingly suspicious of each other’s motives and plans for a post-war world. As this suspicion grows, both nations will seek to exert their will on the global chess board of geostrategic politics and espionage.

Spy Wars is a two-player game. Each player will lead their nation’s Secret Service during the 1950’s era. Players are tasked with deploying their spies to collect enemy Intel (Intelligence) enabling them to carry out missions, and if successful, to steal enemy secrets.

The key objective for players is to gain the most Victory Points (VP). There are 3 ways to do this: (1) successfully complete missions, (2) steal enemy secrets, and (3) advance on the space-race track.

Winning the game:
The game ends after 6 game rounds, or if the VP counter is on a ’8’ space on the victory point track at the end of a game round.

This game is a low ink prototype entry in the 2023-wargame-print-and-play-game-design contest, and gained 2nd place in the best multi-player game category:

https://boardgamegeek.com/thread/3023014/2023-wargame-print-and-play-game-design-contest

—description from the designer

Disclaimer: all art work appearing in or associated with this game is placeholder (temporary) artwork. The game designer claims no copyright on any of the game artwork.

description:
Embark on a perilous quest through the depths of the steaming jungle, in search of lost treasure.

Using a special deck of cards, deal 4 cards each turn onto 4 stacks. Using only the current top card on each stack, discard all but the highest level cards of the same suit. If you ever discard 4 or more cards in 1 round, you can draw a Boon card for an added power. Once you cannot remove any more cards, the round is over and you deal 4 more cards. Keep playing until all cards have been played and you're unable to remove any others. Add up your points and see if you won!

Use your luck and your wits - with some help from bonus cards earned on your adventure - and you may uncover the priceless lost idol!

Invasion of Mars Shooting Game is an English 1950s target game. The game sees Martian targets with guns drawn perched on a cardboard frame. A background scene on the inside box has some great retro graphics of Dan Dare like spaceships and the Earth.

The game-play sees players use a simple gun with rubber bands to knock the Martians down and score points. Points are accumulated and the winner is the player with the most points after an agreed time.

—user summary

The Wales Space Treasure Game is a spin and move game produced as a promotional game in the late 1950's by the Bank of New South Wales in Australia.

The game sees players using a numbered spinner and starting on earth they traverse a path through the Solar System to finish on Pluto. Along the way a number of detours can be encountered as well as things like cosmic dust, Martian Saucers and Space pirates that will cause delays in reaching Pluto. The winner is the first to reach the Space Treasure chest on Pluto.

The promotional game was included in the Bank of New South Wales (Wales Bank)showbag from one of Australia's Royal Shows in the late 1950's. Showbags are a very popular and distinctive feature of Australian shows (the Australian term for fairs or carnivals) and companies are keen to promote products, samples and brands in their Showbags.

boardgame: The Search for the Golden Boomerang Game
description:
The Search for the Golden Boomerang Game is children's board game based on an Australian biweekly Radio serial during the 1940's.

The popular serial spawned several children's fiction magazines centered on the concerns of an Aboriginal boy named Tuckonie. The game appeared as a supplement in 1942 to one of these magazines where 11-year-old Tuckonie and his best friend Peggy, daughter of white settlers search for a golden boomerang

A simple roll and move game sees players follow a path in search for the Golden Boomerang. Dangers along the way such as headhunters, cave of wonders and an underground river can impede or assist movement. First to reach the Golden Boomerang is the winner

Under the big trees of a mystical forest there is a mine with precious magical jewels solidified between the rocks. The little animals of the forest extract them and take them to their burrows to survive the scarcity of food in difficult seasons, as they have discovered that these gems increase the size of the fruits, others increase their sweetness and others regulate their temperature, which keeps them fresh for longer. But the gems are very besieged by the forest fauna and it is not easy to get the best ones.

In The Enchanted Burrow, players roll the dice and write down the results on a sheet with the intention of optimizing their score. Players have to complete sets of gems, improve burrows, save fruits and rush to win bonuses before opponents in various puzzles with different challenges in each game. Which burrow will have the most magic points and the best food before winter arrives?

—description from the publisher

Tic Tac Slash is an 18-card game the takes the classic game of Tic-Tac-Toe and revamps it in a new and exciting way.

Choose X's or O's, deal out the cards, and have fun!

How to Play:

     Randomly give each player the X or O card. This determines your symbol for the game.
     The player with the “double X card” will go first and play that card. 
     Next, the other player lays down their single O card where it overlaps the opponent’s card according to placement rules.
     Shuffle the remaining cards and deal them equally between both players.    
     Play continues back and forth until a player gets three of their symbol in a row (a win!) or all cards are played. If all cards are played, the player with the most symbols showing wins! Otherwise, it’s a Slash (tie). Play again!


Placement Rules:
1. It must overlap one or more cards with a minimum of one space.
2. It must overlap a maximum of three spaces. 
3. Cards must be placed vertically, but can be flipped 180°.

Pim Pam is a ping pong themed card game that's quick to learn and quick to play. It's perfect for when you only have a few minutes but want to have some fun. The 18 cards fit easily into your pocket or purse so you can take it anywhere!

Two players are matched up against each other and compete in a fast game of ping pong. Players take turns playing a card from their hand. You can only play a card if it is one less, the same, or one more than the card in play. If you're unable to play a card your opponent scores a point. First to 5 points wins!

family: Mechanism: Give a Clue / Get a Clue

description:
Carom Mini Golf is a miniature golf simulation game, with endless possibilities and amazing replayability. The rules for movement and interaction are simple and easy to learn, so you can enjoy your visit to our mini golf course in no time.

The course consists of 6 holes of different shapes, filled with hexes. The aim of the game is to putt your ball from the tee within the starting zone into the hole on the other side. The balls move across the hexes in straight lines, according to the value on the die. The score represents the number of strokes needed to put the ball in the hole. After completing the course, the scores on the individual holes are summed up and the player with the lowest score is the winner. The players may agree to play the whole course, to play just on a few selected holes, or any other way they wish.

-description from designer

The game includes 9 white pawns (soldiers), 9 black pawns(soldiers), 2 red pawns (robbers).
At the bottom of the board place the soldiers, black on black and white on red. Their movement is only forward from space to space. The two robbers are placed anywhere on the towers of the robber's castle. They may march forwards and backwards to the next space, or vault sideways over a soldier to the next space, thereby capturing the soldier. The soldiers have lost if they no longer have enough white or black soldiers to fully occupy the castle. The robbers cannot be captured, but lose if the castle is occupied or if they are so surrounded by the soldiers that they can no longer move.

—user summary

Once more is a quick, solo wargame where you will take your squadron of four biplane fighters out on missions to support the troops at the front or clear the way for them to move forward. You will need to make decisions about when you attack and when to coordinate your fighters against an enemy of aerial fighters and ground troops. Be careful, resources are scarce and once one of your planes is shot down, there are no reinforcements to fill the ranks.

—description from the designer

Ransom War is a card game that features fantasy creatures in an epic game of war!  No longer do you simply match high and low numbers on an ordinary deck of cards as in traditional game of war.  Now, battle it out in a fantasy theme! Roll dice and compare stats and special abilities to determine who wins each battle.  At the end, it is not who has the most cards but the person who has the most gold in their ransom from captured and victorious units!

—description from the designer

The year? 1800. The locale? Vienna, Austria, the City of Music. 9 years have passed since the death of Mozart, Beethoven has just premiered his first symphony, and you are an aspiring composer looking to be the next maestro to leave your mark in the annals of music history. Will you succeed?

In Motifs, you will be placing your composer, assistants, and apprentice tokens around the city of Vienna, purchasing supplies, gaining inspiration, making acquaintances, and obtaining commissions, pupils, and patronages. When not gallivanting around the city, you will be home writing compositions to be performed and published. The more you perform and publish, the more your prestige and opportunities grow. Are you ready to begin your journey to become the most prestigious composer in all the land?

Dinn is a fast-paced action card game where you harness legends and myths from all of human history and literature. You must use strategy, and luck to defeat your opponent.

Each player begins the game with three Heart cards. Play takes place over a series of rounds. During each round, both players reveal one Hero and up to two Equipment or Action cards. In addition to various game-changing effects, these cards contribute to the Heroes’ Power. After all cards are resolved, the player with the weaker Hero must shatter a number of their Heart cards.

When your opponent’s last Heart card is shattered, you are victorious!

Dinn starter decks are the ideal way to jump into the game. Designed to allow pick-up-and-play, and provide a simple entry into the alternate reality game.

Every single card is a puzzle. Solving these puzzles will navigate you through a maze of custom-built clues: websites, professionally recorded audio-files, blog posts, and videos.
Every element of a card can be part of its puzzle. Its art, name, attributes, ability, and identification codes are all items we’ll use to craft puzzles.

You will have to take each element into consideration when attempting to solve a puzzle.
family: Theme: King Arthur / The Knights of the Round Table / Camelot

description:
The Beast attribute deck focuses on famous beasts, creatures and behemoths from myth, and legend. Everything from the mighty Leviathan, to the ferocious Minotaur live in this deck.

It gets better the closer to defeat it gets. Like a cornered animal, your opponent must defeat you quickly, or they’ll soon regret it.

The more shattered hearts in your graveyard, the more powerful your Heroes get. Additionally, this deck is filled with Heroes that are treated as shattered hearts when they’re in the graveyard to add a new strategic element to gameplay.

The Horror attribute deck focuses on iconic monsters and horrible creatures throughout history. The Heroes that lurk in the heart of the Horror deck live up to their name.

This deck utilizes the fear of the unknown. The Hero you play at the beginning of the turn, may not be the Hero in play at the end of your turn.

Keep your opponent on their toes as you exchange the Heroes on the field with Heroes in your hand at different points in the game.

This deck also introduces new terrifying mechanics that allow you to play Heroes in unconventional ways.

The Divine attribute deck is made of gods, angels, demons and prophets throughout human history. Divine intervention is almost always in your favor when you play these cards.

The Divine attribute focuses on control. Specifically, how many cards you have in your hand, and your opponent has in theirs. Ten, five, two. These numbers will define the strength of your Heroes.

The Essence attribute deck is made of up of events, actions and the unchanging elements of life. The creatures of the dark forest, the unmovable mountain, or the simple virus.

It plays by a very specific set of rules, and your opponent will be playing by those rules as well. Will the card your opponent draws next turn be their card, or will it be yours? You’ll be the one to decide.

The Given attribute deck is made up of machines, creations and engines scattered throughout humanity's past, and future.

The Given attribute focuses on continual improvement. The Heroes you play at the end of the game will receive benefits from the Heroes you played at the beginning. It’s best to defeat a Given deck early, because defeating it late may not be an option.

Roll your dice to move your investigators through the house, placing room tiles as you go.  Use evidence to complete and illuminate the 3 rooms and collect spirit cards for points.  One player will be dealt the Autumn card, marking them as possessed by her.  Autumn can jump from one player to another, so no one should be stuck with her the entire game.  While they are possessed, that player works against the other players.  Once the 3 rooms are completed and illuminated, the players must escape through the portal in the corn maze before Autumn can capture them all.

Introduction from the game manual:

You are an experienced paranormal investigator...  The best in the field, to be transparent.  You take your decades of experience and knowledge and use it to investigate the house where Halloween Never Ends...  Spirits roam trapped within the walls by an evil and unseen force.  Investigate the house, illuminate all three rooms, set each trapped spirit free and then escape through the portal within the corn maze.

But beware, for the guardian of the house, the evil scarecrow named Autumn, is out to trap you, making you the newest slave of the great and terrible nightmares of the house where Halloween Never Ends!

YOLO! is a fast-paced, strategy card game built around winning the stock market and going “to the moon”. Experience the absurd world of stonks, get filthy rich, and have fun messing with your opponents using hilarious meme cards. It takes a bunch of stonks and tendies to go to the Moon, so get out there and start earning!

HOW IT WORKS:
- Take Turns
- Collect stonks to win
- Receive gains to get filthy rich
- Strategize with Options & Memes

HOW TO WIN:
Collect 3 Moon Tickets (completed sets of Stonks) and take your crew to the Moon.

—description from the designer

"It is said that in ancient times, the völvas of the different clans met to attract the good graces of odin and thor.
They practised galdr, runic magic. 
To transmit their prayers, they sought to approach Vedrfölnir, the great eagle at the top of Yggdrasil. To flatter him, it would be enough to pluck 12 stars from the sky. 
The path to the great eagle is a perilous one, and the Norns will have to help you tame Sleipnir, outwit Loki, trick Nidhogg and coax Ratatosk... ".

The game consists of collecting Odin or Thor runes, each rune card giving a certain number of points indicated at the bottom of the card. To do this, the player will try to accumulate the cards in order to obtain 12 stars (except in the case of modifying effect cards). If one of the players exceeds the goal, the others will share the runes in the stand.
The winner of the game is the player who has collected the most rune points at the end of the game.

—description from the publisher

In the last few years, "Paletto" has been one of our best-selling games. On the request of many customers, the author, Dieter Stein, has come up with simplified rules so that "Paletto" can now also be played with children from 3 or 4 years and up. The color die brings the luck factor into the game, and children take a mischievous pleasure if they can snatch a playing piece away from another player.
As soon as children are able to assign colors, they can also play "Paletto."

Please note: "Paletto Plus" comes with its own game instructions, including a color die. It can only be played with the "Paletto" basic game.

The delivery of Paletto Plus consists of:

     1 x Game instructions
     1 x One 20-mm color die


—description from the publisher
family: Versions & Editions: Junior Versions of Grown-Up Games

description:
The Frankenstein manuscript has been stolen from the Villa Diodati. The residents of the villa protest their innocence, but one is lying. who stole it How was it stolen? And why? Follow the clues with your investigation team and try to answer these and many other questions. Will you solve the riddle of the Frankenstein manuscript?

In each Q case, you try to solve a mystery case with 32 clues, with players revealing one clue at a time until all cards have been revealed or discarded. During your turn, each player must perform one of the following actions:

A) Reveal information:
Choose a card from your hand and place it on the table, so all players can read or see the entire information.
We recomend you read out loud all shared info when you place it on the table. If you play a clue that happens to be irrelevant
to the case, you lose points at the end of the game, but be careful! Some clues are vital to resolve the case.

You can share and expose your theories at any moment and talk about the cards you have in your hand but you cannot show them to the other players and you may only read out loud the words written in bold or the text framed inside an image:

At the end of the game, when all clue cards have been revealed or discarded, you must check carefully all the available information and prepare a theory of what happened, working all together. Then, open the questionnaire and answer all questions. During this phase of the game, you can speak freely about your discarded cards, or the information you remember of them. Each right answer will add two points.

dual EXPANSION to DEER LORD!

Le freak, c’est chic!

GEEK is for brainiacs who would rather interact with popular culture than other people. ASYLUM will take you to the brink of insanity.

This dual expansion favors us *special* people, so play with friends who don’t mind acting, looking or being weird.

The GEEK expansion gives you heated debates over fist fights or dance routines (or anything physical like that), it lets you pummel your opponents with pop culture knowledge. Proclaim yourself the Stephan Urquelle among the Urkels.

The ASYLUM expansion let’s you fly over the cuckoo’s nest to unleash ALL the crazy! No point in playing sneakily with this set of totally absurd cards that will send the game right to the nut house.

Contains
    22 GEEK cards
    32 ASYLUM cards

-description from publisher's website

A game about news paper reporting, journalism and ads from the world of news papers. It's meant to be played in a classroom with six teams but can also be played with only three people. The first team to the goal wins. Advance by finding information in the paper, answer a quiz question or identify a drawing.

Whilst some Winter Sports enthusiasts might disagree the Slider event down a narrow,twisting and banked ice track isn't really a spectator sport. With only one sled on the run at any one time each might seem very similar to all the others give or take the odd 'drama' of a brush with an ice wall, or a trip too high up a banked curve, etc. But one aspect makes every run different and exciting - the intermediate Time Checks that are flashed onto large electronic scoreboards for all to see, so that everyone is aware just how close the sled currently on the run is to the run-leader, how much time needs to be made up in the remaining Sectors. Take those time checks away and, dare I suggest, you have a boring spectacle for live and television spectators?

But all this is lucky for us when presenting a replay simulation of the sport.

In sliding events start speed is vital. After 50 metres we get the first time check and that reflects how quickly the sled has been launched down the runway - in the team events that involves strength and running speed and a quick and tidy entry into the sled and settlement into the seats, in individual events 'pushing off' with feet or gloved hands.

But also the start is all about getting the sled moving as quickly as possible on its journey down the course, for what speed is achieved in that first 50 metres is the momentum that will build up over the run as the weight of sled and crew and the laws of gravity get to work. In 'Sliders' we provide a Start Rating, based on actual start times, which not only sets the first segment of elapsed time for the run but also determines what we call Raw Speed (momentum) which is carried forward to the next Sector. So a good start will bring two advantages, a low Elapsed Time and a good Raw Speed carry forward. Start Ratings range from 0 to 10.

Driver Skill (the range is 0 to 4) can be used in two ways. From Sector 2 onwards one DS can be used to increase the RS by 3 before the time for that Sector is determined, or used to improve the sled's Stability (ST) by 2. This represents the driver's ability to keep to the best racing line, and to ensure that the sled is 'in shape' and stable. The increase of 3 RS converts to a time saving of 0.03 secs., but again, as RS is carried forward, that is 0.03 secs. for this and each of the remaining Sectors, so gaining that advantage high up on the track is reflected all the way down. Stability is both a driver and team function, with the driver dictating the sled's line and the crew using their weight to keep the sled at the best angle and maintaining the narrow runners in best contact with the track. High up the course stability isn't really a problem, but as speeds build steadily down the run it becomes a vital factor and there is less time to get everything right. Once a sled starts to get 'out of shape', like RS, it is carried forward to the next Sector and as each Sector has a Stability Check it becomes progressively more of a problem, maybe needing the expenditure of a valuable DS to help correct the situation.

Initially the Stability Rating can be as high as 8, and until that is reduced there is unlikely to be a problem, but with lower Ratings failure of the Stability Check is likely and this will eat further into the ST figure and also reduce RS. 'Braking' (most sleds have some form of crude brakes) is a last resort for RS is automatically reduced by 20 in regaining some (1D6) STABILITY points.

Solace is a traditional patience (solitaire) game played with a set of Black Ice pieces. It is more reminiscent of Carpet than Spider in that a single game lasts a very short time. The game starts with ten randomly created nests, and ends when no more monochromatic towers can be created. The score of a game is the number of full towers sitting directly on the playing surface.

Koniggratz, 3 July 1866 serves as a follow up to the previous CSL title "Mars la Tour" which uses the same Mark Hermann inspired system with a few new updates. Koniggratz (also known as the battle of Sadowa) was the crucial battle which determined the victor of the 1866 Austro-Prussian war for Germanic hegemony. Unlike traditional wargames, Koniggratz uses a unique sequence of play featuring pulses and formation changes in order to provide a more competitive experience between two players, and a more educational experience for one.

COMPLEXITY: 6/10
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
The outstanding game of chance, knowledge, strategy and more...

A trivia game that uses a map of Europe as the centrepiece.

The object of this game is to gain countries on the game board to achieve a chain of 5 or more connecting countries in the map.

5 Letter Wordlet is a fun and addictive word puzzle game that can be played by people of all ages, and is especially suitable for word game fans of scrabble, upwords and dabble or boggle games. In this entertaining family board game, players compete to discover the secret 5-letter word using the same wits, strategy, deduction and a bit of luck that are popular in games like mastermind and codenames. The player to first identify the secret word wins the round, and the player with the most wins at the end of the game wins big! 5-Letter Wordlet from University Games is both competitive and addictive.

—description from the publisher

Who will be landowner.
Each player own one or more farm, at the beginning of the game, but with out animal, machines, fields or barns. 
The player who at the end of the game have the highest value (animal, machines and money) wins the game.
Each player is given 1 or more pawn, 10.000 Dkr and one or more farm (one for each pawn), depending on the numbers of player.
On your turn you roll the dice and move your pawn that many steps. 
If you end on an animal or machine, you get that card, either from the bank or if another player has that card, from them. If you have 2 of the same animal, you exchange the card with a double animal card, that you can not lose.
If you end on a farm, that is own by another player, you can exchange animal cards, to try and have a pair
If you end on a field or barn, and nobody own that, you get it, if another player own that field or barn you will pay them 1000 Dkr
If you end on the Bank, you must pay the bank 2000 Dkr
If you end on Rodekontor yopu must pay your taxes 3000 Dkr

The game end when you decide, or after a certain time.
The winner is the player with the highest value (animal, machine and money).
The value for the Horse or cow is 2000 Dkr each, Pig and Sheep is 500 Dkr, Car is 5000 Dkr, Tractor, sowing machine & self-binding machine is 2000 Dkr, the rest is 500dkr.

—user summary

Make the best colorful celebration for Yordi's 30st birthday!
It's a microgame played with 30 cards in 5 turns.

Each turn you need to pick (by drafting when playing with two) your cards and choose which you want to use as a letter and which one as a number in your festive decoration. Ideally the sum of the numbers is 30 (because it's Yordi's 30st birthday) while you spell the name Yordi. You can also score for colors to make it more challenging. The player with the most points wins.

-description from designer

boardgame: Final Girl: Lore & Scenario Book – Series 2
description:
This 64-page staple-bound booklet includes:

A backstory for each killer in Series 2
A backstory for each Final Girl in Series 2
Tips on playing each Final Girl in Series 2
A scenario for each killer/location combination in Series 2, with special rules and setup instructions for each scenario

Tri-balism is a game based on the Great Clan Gathering of the Nine Trolling Tribes. A moot called by the Shamanic Collective at which a careful balancing of egos, animosity, historical rivalries, paranoia, and toxic stupidity had to be attained else all chaos would ensue.

A traditional fun, family-friendly, card matching strategy puzzle game, Tri-balism is based on some very simple rules which promote strategic thinking and decision making. Tri-balism is designed to be played by 2 to 4 people but can accommodate more if necessary, and also has a single player puzzle mode.

The aim is simple, play 'tri-cards' to create 'gatherings' and score crystal-points.

Crystal points can also be expended to perform certain moves which allow players to manipulate the game board in an attempt to win even more points.

The player with the most crystal points wins.

—description from the designer

Promo card for Earth. Two-sided, with an African Lion on one side and a Koala on the other. Available during the Season 9 Tantrum House crowdfunding campaign.

African Lion (Panthera leo):
Claim 2 Fauna objectives

Koala (Phascolarctos cinereus):
2+ Flora, each with 5+ Growth
Your classic Monopoly but Blackpink edition for kpop fans.
family: Versions & Editions: Versions with IP after the original release

boardgame: Awkward Family Photos Greatest Hits: Expansion #1
description:
Inside this box are 100 comedic, dramatic, thrilling and action-packed movie lines you, your friends and family will use to caption 40 hilarious images from the hit website, AwkwardFamilyPhotos.com.

With easy rules and no movie knowledge required, play with the enclosed cards or add them to the original game for hours of fun.

Avatár is the first expansion released to the Hungarian CCG Mítosz.
It contains 15 cards per pack from the base game and guarantees one rare and five uncommon items.

Second expansion to the Hungarian CCG Mítosz and the first one which brings new cards. It introduces the Egyptian Mythology.
With this expansion 129 new cards become available. One pack contains 15 cards from this 129.
It also brings a new card type (maneuver) and extends the core game's ruleset.

After 10 years of the original Mítosz a redesigned game with new graphics is available.
The basic game concepts are the same:

     you have a deck of (min) 60 cards
     you have one Prominent God from one of that four ancient mythology (Celtic, Greek, Norse, Egyptian) in your deck, who gives you unique powers
     you must control cards which give you Power Point
     whoever first control 12 Power Points, wins
     you have to defend your people and attack your enemy's people or leaders


The core set contains 300 cards and also 4 promotional cards were created which were available as awards on competitions.

—user summary

Third expansion to the Hungarian CCG Mítosz and second one which brings new cards. It does not introduce new Mythology, but extends all the previous ones.
With this expansion 84 new cards become available. One pack contains 15 cards from this 129.

ユーテル(YUTERU) is a Japanese "Ogiri" game.
"Ogiri" is a form of comedy that focuses on improvisation by providing a funny answer on the spot for a question.

For each picture in the theme, the player chooses a dialogue card from his or her hand that fits the situation. At this point, the player must think about "what the person or animal is saying" and play the card that he or she finds most interesting, because only if the parent player likes the answer will the game score points. This is because only if the answer is liked by the parent player will the game be scored.
The player who scores more points wins.

This game was produced in cooperation with "Shochiku Entertainment" and "JELLYJELLYGAMES" in Japan.


ユーテルは、かんたんな大喜利ゲームです。

プレイヤーは、お題の写真を見て、手札から状況に合った台詞カードを選びます。このとき、プレイヤーは"写真にうつる人や動物が何を言っているのか"を考え、最も面白いと思われそうなカードを出す必要があります。なぜなら、その答えが親プレイヤーに気に入られて初めて、このゲームの得点になるからです。ゲームを繰り返し、より多くの得点を獲得したプレイヤーの勝利です。

このゲームは、日本の"松竹芸能"と"JELLY JELLY GAMES"が協力して製作されました。

boardgame: Sleeping Gods: Distant Skies – Skis and Poles Promo Card
description:
Originally available as part of Pack Druid from the Dice Tower 2023 Kickstarter campaign.

A new piece of equipment - Skis and Poles!
Effect: Travel 2 and Remove Low Morale (Must start on Cold Obstacle or Steep Terrain Obstacle)

Wizards are mysterious and elusive. The evidence of their time in the world is scant save for their deep, labyrinthine dungeons. These strongholds are brimming with Loot - ancient relics, magickal baubles, mountains of gold - and guarded by fearsome foes and evil creatures of the wizard’s own dark creation. Should tooth and claw prove insufficient, these wizards have further reinforced their dungeons with wicked traps and magickal safeguards. The world is full of these hidden treasure hoards and your group of Adventurers has just found one.

Some wizards are richer, more magickal, and more cunning than others, yielding a greater bounty from their Loot-filled dungeons. But heed this warning - once you enter a wizard’s dungeon you must kill the Boss if you ever wish to leave. Of all the wizard’s creatures, none is more fearsome or more perilous than its most trusted and wretched enforcer.

But peril be damned, you’re an Adventurer! Now get looting!

Wizard’s Loot is a tabletop card game about exploring dungeons, slaying monsters, and hoarding loot! As you and your group of fellow Adventurers explore a wizard's dungeon together your individual goal is to be the Adventurer with the most Loot, but all of you must cooperate if you hope to leave the dungeon alive.

In order to escape with sacks full of sweet, shimmering Loot you must defeat Enemies, collect their Essences, and slay the dungeon’s Boss. Once the Boss has been slain, and the Adventurers escape the dungeon, the Adventurer with the most valuable Loot is the winner.

Each player will get a chance to lead the group, explore the dungeon, and make choices along the way. You can turn over every stone or previous looter's skull you come across in your insatiable search for Loot, or you can race from room to room hacking and slashing your way through the wizard’s hordes. Wizard’s Loot is a game about exploration and choices, and will reward - or punish - players for the risks they take. But choose wisely - your collective decisions will determine if all of you make it out alive with bags full of Loot, or if your corpses are added to an ever-growing pile in the wizard's dungeon!

—description from the publisher

Draw, swap, and switch out colorful three-sided game cards to watch your gem take shape. Uncover all of one color to build a jewel of gemz. Watch out for action cards that speed up or slow down progress. Be first to gather six gemz cards of the same color to win.

Setup involves dealing three gemz cards to each player, face up. If an action card is dealt at this time it is placed back in the deck and dealing continues until each player has three cards.

To start play, everyone lays the three cards they were dealt side by side in a semi-circle. The youngest player goes first by drawing one card from the top of the draw pile. If it's a gemz card they add it to their circle. If it's an action card they follow the instructions. Turn-based play continues to the left and players lay down every gemz card they draw until their circle is full. Players may only have six cards total at a time; once their circle is full, they must choose to discard their newly drawn cards or swap them with an existing piece of their circle.

The game continues until one player has gathered 6 gemz cards of the same color. Luck-of-the-draw and the game-changing SWAP cards make sure that no player can coast to an easy win. The rules are simple enough for younger children, teens, and adults to all enjoy playing together.

Includes: 63 triangle-shaped game cards: 11 each of green, orange, red & blue Gemz Cards; 4 Wild Multicolor Gemz; 15 Action Cards (7 Swap, 4 Lose a Turn, and 4 Lose a Card). Clear, easy-to-read instructions included in a compact game box.

This booklet, which features 40 different Munchkin cards, Steve Jackson Games has gathered some of their very favorite promo cards from the archives so that everyone can have a chance to enjoy them! Part of their successful August 2021 Kickstarter campaign, this booklet will liven up all your Munchkin games!

A coloring book full of pixel art where you create your own brushes and compete to become the best coloring artist!

Components (For each player)
Colored Pencils (each sheet has 6 specific basic colors)
A PixelBrush Sheet
3 d6 dice

How to play!
Each turn cross one turn spot and roll 3 dice to activate each one in any order you want. For each die choose a custom brush / single color brush to paint on the canvas or on any of your custom brushes (creating them in that way).
The colors needs to match on the canvas and white fields can't be painted on!
The game ends when you manage to fullfill all the color fields on the canvas or you run out of turns.

Ephemeral Path is a quick and customizable skirmish game for two players. Ephemeral Path uses no set miniatures or figures but instead allows players to play with either printable pieces or miniatures they already own. Players create customized stats and ability combinations for each figure they play with by making figure stat sheets on the game's website and printing them off. Games are short, and use no custom dice or cards. Other than standard polyhedral dice, everything players need to play with is printable.

Story: The paths have opened up across space and time. Intelligent beings of all types have been lured through the mystifying black gates that mark the path’s root. Whatever the being’s reason for embarking along the path, science, curiosity, instinct, their aspirations have shifted to mere survival. Once the gate had been activated it vanishes shortly after, never returning. Those who managed to traverse the path found themselves on a desolate planet, with no way home. The planet grew more crowded and dangerous by the hour as intelligent creatures from every corner of the universe flooded its barren surface. After some time, the gates that mark the head of the path began to appear on this planet as well. With resources running thin, most chose to take their chances with these newly opened gates. If they could only get to them before they close.

The game is played on a 40cm x 40cm play area with a round, 9cm gate placed on the board. Each player begins the game with a set of between two and six figures.  Each figure has only two or three actions per game so players must use their actions precisely in order to maximize their chances of winning.  Players win by having the most figures in contact with the gate after each player has run out of actions.

—description from the designer

Goosed is a trick taking game for four players.  It uses traditional playing cards and two poker chips.

During the game, partnerships for each round are determined by who plays the the first goose card and who captures it.  Those two players are trying to capture as many geese as possible without capturing all of them.  If that team does capture all of the goose cards, they will earn a scoring penalty.

The scoring system for Goosed will keep players on their toes.  Teams share the points they earn for capturing geese, BUT each individual player earns points for the tricks they capture.  This forces players to balance working together with getting as many tricks of their own as possible.

Partnerships in this game are temporary, and there can be only ONE winner.  Whoever has the most points at the end of the final round wins.

-description from designer

In March 2023, partymeeple ran a roll n write evening in a small bar in Western Australia. To this end, a custom design, paying tribute to the small bar, entitled Henry on 8th, was designed.

A DJ (dice jokey) rolls two D6 for everyone in the room and sprukes the result over the PA. 
Music, gently swirling adds to the atmosphere.

This is a very simple game designed for folk who are not familiar with modern board games. The two die are different colours (red and white) The winner was the first player to score 16 points or who ever had the most points after 30 die rolls.

1862: USA&Canada is a railroad construction and stock game. The author prefers to play with 4 or 5 players! Historical specifications have been taken into account as far as possible. In other places, history should and must be rewritten. It is based on 1862, set in the same region and with the same companies. When developing 1862: USA&Canada, attention was paid to a reduce the playing time. A game lasts about 240 to 300 minutes.

Players invest in railway companies. They create a rail network with track tiles on the game board and drive these routes with their trains and generate income. The player with the most shares in a railway company is its director. He decides whether this income will be distributed to shareholders as a dividend or whether it will be added to the company's treasury for future investments. With 7 train types from 2 to 8, the progress over time and the technical development is shown.

As with many games in this series, there are similarities within the rules, but many have subtle differences that give each and every 18xx game its character.

These include: The companies gradually come into play in 3 groups. She can buy back shares, even from players, which is good for the company but bad for your share limit.

Bonus rides and tokens are integrated into the game in a new way. Trains can be bought either as "normal" trains or as E-trains and many other little things!

-description from designer

ハンプヤード (Hump Yard) is a game from Japan. The game was originally published by project-ecn, and when that publisher reformed as 636GAMES (ロッサムゲームズ), they republished the game under new box art.

Connect, assemble, and launch freight trains!

This game is based on the theme of the 1960s Japanese National Railways freight depot. Become an employee of the switchyard responsible for logistics in Japan, sort the approaching freight trains, and set off freight trains heading for all over the country.

Departed freight cars score differently depending on their destination. Advance the game to your advantage while reading other players' destinations and moves.
publisher's summary

boardgame: Stone Age Survival: A Solo Roll & Write Adventure
description:
Step back in time and face the unforgiving prehistoric world in Stone Age Survival, a solo roll-and-write adventure where you take on the role of a stone age hunter-gatherer. Utilize your skills, plan your hunting path wisely, and strategically gather resources to thrive in this harsh environment. Beware of fierce predators, natural hazards, and scarce food sources as you traverse the unforgiving landscape.

In this single-page A4 print-and-play game, all you need is a pencil, paper, and a 6-sided die (d6) to embark on a journey filled with strategy, resource management, and primal adventure. With unique character skills, a points-based scoring system, and limited exploration opportunities, Stone Age Survival offers a challenging and engaging gaming experience. Prove your resilience and adaptability as you strive to become the most successful hunter-gatherer in the land!

—description from the designer

boardgame: Adventure Party: The Savage Lands A FREE RPG DAY Exclusive
description: Adventure Party: The Savage Lands A FREE RPG DAY Exclusive is a "quickstart", or demo, version of the game Adventure Party, distributed at Free RPG Day 2023 to promote the upcoming Kickstarter campaign.  Adventure Party delivers a robust role-playing experience that can be enjoyed within 2 minutes of explanation. In this cooperative guessing game, 3-6 players take the roles of adventurers in a classic fantasy role-playing world, facing monsters and attempting heroic deeds.

Unavowed is a pocket-sized two-player card game about palace conspiracies played with a deck of just fifteen cards. Players begin by drafting a hand composed of members of the royal court, then play a series of rounds.

In each round, players place one card face down in a secret meeting, and count up in rank to determine the winner with the higher value. The lower-rank card is revealed and loses the meeting, while the winning card stays hidden. Certain cards have the chance to reveal a specific opponent if it was played in the meeting.

When one player wins three meetings, the round is over, but the defeated player has one last chance to secure victory by assassination. If they can guess which of the opponent's cards played during the round - face up or face down - is the highest rank, they win instead!

The loser of the round draws a new card into their hand, both players pick up their cards, then play for the best of three rounds.

A series of over 70 (seventy) board game prototypes, dated from the late 1980s through to the late 'noughties', from retired Civil Servant and game enthusiast Trevor Warren.

Donated to The Museum of Board Games (Summer, 2022) - along with a file of correspondence with prospective UK-based publishers - the games have been extensively reviewed, catalogued and photographed, forming the basis of an exhibition and a series of BGG blog posts.

While many folks 'dabble' at board game design, Mr Warren's remarkable collection is a tribute to one person's devotion to - and ambition for - his favourite hobby.

The Museum of Board Games intends to publish a number of the games as part of a documentary book package.

A word making & guessing party game for filthy people.

- Pick and pass letter cards
- Build a word, play it face-down
- Race to guess other players' words
- Use prompt cards to direct your word-making & guessing, and to score extra points
- Bad language scores double!

The game ends after up to 3 rounds (determined by player count), at which point the player with the highest final score wins.

—description from the publisher

Paragons: Age of Champions is a High Fantasy Drafting/Deck-building TCG-like for 2-3 players which is designed specifically to replicate and enhance the experience of a traditional TCG draft tournament. It is an all-in-one boxed set which means no pay-to-win in this easy-to-learn, hard-to-master competitive PvP card game.

Players begin by choosing one of three "Paragon Classes": the Warrior, the Mage, and the Ranger. These classes specialize in different things and will inform the choices you make during the draft phase.

The Warrior: Fill the Combat Board with mercenary recruits and buff them up to achieve utter domination.

The Mage: Burn your opponents with powerful spells and manipulate the board state to your liking.

The Ranger: With a variety of traps and tools at your disposal, keep your opponent on their toes until you strike with a swift and calculated onslaught.

Build your army of mercenaries around your strategy and what you think your opponent is likely to do as you take turns drafting from a common pool of cards. Then assemble your deck and seize victory in a bombastic slug-fest full of combat, tactics, and surprises that is sure to delight fans of the genre.

—description from the publisher
