Gemble is a push-your-luck card and dice game for 2 to 4 players. Players take turns rolling the dice and gambling to collect suits of gem cards matching private goals indicated on gem tokens. However, they will need cards from as many suits as possible in order to make bets.

The rules are simple: roll the gem die and carry out the following actions:

1.Collect one card if there are cards in the pot (market) matching the color of the die roll.

2.Make a bet by rolling the dot die if there are no cards in the pot matching the color of the die roll, or, if you want to collect more than one card from the pot.

If you make a succesful bet you may collect one card for a single match or all cards of one color for a double match. If you fail, you must move your bet cards to the pot.

At the end of the game, the cards matching your private goals will add points to your score while the other cards will subtract points.

-description from designer

Create your own army using the ink pad and cards.  Add weapons, headgear, and faces! You can even name them!

Then take your troops into battle against your opponent and try to capture his/her (its) troops.  Capture your opponent's general and you've won the game

In Best Story Wins, Players will use the 150 prompt cards as a starting point to share their best (and worst) stories. Impress the group with the best true story of the weirdest way you've ever made money, or a wild celebrity encounter, and much more! The juicier, and more personal, the story, the better. Spare no detail because the group votes on whose story is the best!

250 cards to challenge your friends to see WHO CAN DO IT... Compete against your friends to see who can swear in a foreign language, take the worst selfie, or pretend to ride a mechanical bull. Do the challenge first or best to win.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: 世界巡游: 中国篇 (World Tour: China)
description:
世界巡游: 中国篇 ("World Tour: China") is a new standalone title in the series, following the Australian, European and American installments! This time players will travel to famous spots all over China to find souvenirs, discover local rare animals while enjoying the great rivers and mountains of the motherland, taste local cuisine and spend a wonderful and unforgettable vacation.

—description from the publisher (translated)

boardgame: Maximum Apocalypse: Allies of the Rapture
description:
The Allies of the Rapture expansion compiles all of the extra Kickstarter Exclusive content from our previous campaigns. This includes the solo focused Allies expansion, the Rapture apocalypse, murder clowns and gremlin monsters.

"Los Angeles is in the depths of what can only be described as the Rapture. The streets are filled with demons, imps and angels running amok and plunging the city into utter chaos. Our only chance is to work with a few guardian angels and prevent the 4 horsemen of the apocalypse from showing up.

With 30 Rapture monster cards, 15 promotional monsters, and a 23 card solo Allies deck, the Allies of the Rapture expansion collects all the solo and promotional content for the Maximum Apocalypse series into one box. The Rapture Apocalypse focuses on teh 4 Horsemen of the Apocalypse, if these 4 bosses are ever all in play at the same time, the game is instantly lost. Please note that all of this content is included in the Legendary Edition of Maximum Apocalypse"

Wind & Waterfalls is inspired by the game Snakes & Ladders (originally called Mokshapat or Moksha Patamu).

A fantasy-tinged twist on a classic design, Wind & Waterfalls has travelers making their way up the mountain in a spiral formation. Will you get carried up by a strong wind? Will a waterfall carry you down, hindering your progress? Watch out for wild weather conditions, too.

Friends & Foes in the form of cryptids and creatures of the forest will either help or hinder your progress. Beware the Wild Cards - they may not be in your favor.

Wind & Waterfalls await. Welcome to the Mountain.

—description from the designer

Two rival business magnates are competing to take control of a high-rise office tower. 
Each owner operates a business on their side of the building.

Before leaving for the day, each of the owner’s employees pulls the shades on their windows to reveal random letters.

When the owners arrive back to work in the morning, they check to see what words have been made across the windows on their side.
The owner whose side of the tower has the best scoring words, wins that day.

After several days, the owner with the top score will control the building.

Word Magnates is a strategic word game for two players, aged 7+. It is a game of building a tower of two-sided magnetized letters without your opponent knowing what words they are making. It’s a game of secret word creation.

COMPONENTS:
 - Lasercut tower with "windows" to view the letters.
 - Built-in score board.
 - 30 wooden black magnetic tiles.
 - 30 wooden white magnetic tiles.

Each set of 30 magnetic tiles is coloured black on white or white on black and have point values (1-5).

SETUP:
1. Insert the tower into base. 
2. Spread all the tiles face up.
3. Uncouple any tiles stuck together.
4. Group the tiles by colour into black and white sets.
5. Each player takes 10 black vowels (two each of A, E, I, O, U) and 10 white vowels.
6. Flip and shuffle the remaining tiles upside down within each group. Important: Do not mix the colours!
7. Each player takes half of the tiles from each group, placing them secretive in their own area.
8. Decide who will play which colour.
9. Grab paper and pencils for scoring.

GAME PLAY:
Either player starts by attaching one tile of each colour together and dropping both combined tiles into one of the three tower slots. Take turns attaching and dropping tiles. Make sure you always drop your coloured tile in front of you with the opposite tile as the other colour. Each player can only see their side of the tower. Try to drop letters such that their placement will help you make words and thwart your opponent.

FORMING WORDS:
As letters are dropped, words of three or more letters will eventually be formed in any of eight directions. These can be top to bottom, bottom to top, diagonal, across or backwards.

Keep dropping letters until the tower is full with 24 tiles (12 drops per player).

SCORING:
After completely filling the tower, scoring takes place. Players write down their words formed and tally the letter values. Players validate each other's words. Each player maintains their score on their built-in scoring track.

After scoring, turn the tower upside down to dump out the letters. Separate each set of tiles and detach the magnetic tiles.

BONUS POINTS:
Any player finding one word that their opponent missed will collect those points!

WINNING:
After several rounds, the first player to exceed 200 points (or a predetermined target) will be the winner. In case of a tie, the highest word made in the current round breaks the tie.

-description from designer

The longer, the better!
Match the symbols on the tiles to trigger a chain reaction and earn points. The longer the reaction, the better!
Every move is a puzzle that requires perceptiveness and planning. Finding the best option gives great satisfaction. With a small box and fast gameplay, it's the perfect game to take on the go.

—description from the publisher

The title: Reakcja łańcuchowa travel: Dołącz ogniwo would be translated to Chain reaction travel: Add a link. In this game players add tiles to the grid of tiles (which starts from the size of 5x8).
On their turn a player has to draw a tile and add it on top of one of the five grid's columns. After the placement they check if there is a tile with matching symbol on the left, right and bottom (in this order) side of the added tile. If there is a match the player takes the other tile (not the tile just placed) and add it to their pool. After taking a tile from the grid it may happen that there is an empty space, so every tile the has empty space beneath it drops down, which may cause more matches (the matches are checked only with the just added tile) and so on.

The game ends when one column is empty or the stack is depleted.

The players earn point from individual tiles, objective tiles (contracts - gathered during the game) and secret color tiles (assigned at the beginning of the game).

—user summary

Eye vs Eye, the second set in the episodic Viewniverse card game series, finds the Blinkers separated on the espionage world of Spytopia.  Eyenstein and Iris must rescue Hawk who has been captured by his Spytopian counterpart.  Their problems escalate as they encounter Silas, Eyenstein's evil nemesis, who is trying to steal blueprints for a device that could transport the Blinkers home.  Join the Blinkers as they attempt to rescue Hawk and thwart Silas' evil plans.  If the Blinkers escape Spytopia, catch them in the next episode - Prehistoric Park.

The number in the top-left-hand corner of each card indicates the number of Viewpoints the card is worth and the text at the bottom is the action to be performed. In turn, players simply draw a card from the Draw Pile and play a card from their hand into their Field of View.

At the end of their turn players count the Viewpoints of the cards in their Field of View, the first player to collect 100 or more Viewpoints wins. Viewpoint Revisioned gameplay is swift to dive into and deceptively strategic. Each card provides you with Viewpoints towards your objective as well as changing the game state in a variety of ways.

Prehistoric Park, the third set in the episodic Viewniverse card game series, transports the Blinkers to Menagerie, a world that is in the midst of a power struggle between the nefarious Doctor Crichton, a geneticist who has been creating peculiar creatures, and the rebellious Neandeyethal, one of Doctor Crichton's creations.  Join the Blinkers as they are forced to pick a side whilst avoiding being eaten by the inhabitants of Menagerie.  If the Blinkers escape Menagerie, catch them in the next episode - The Winking Dead.

The number in the top-left-hand corner of each card indicates the number of Viewpoints the card is worth and the text at the bottom is the action to be performed. In turn, players simply draw a card from the Draw Pile and play a card from their hand into their Field of View.

At the end of their turn players count the Viewpoints of the cards in their Field of View, the first player to collect 100 or more Viewpoints wins. An added alternative way to win is to be the first to collect three Regent Creatures. Prehistoric Park gameplay is swift to dive into and deceptively strategic. Each card provides you with Viewpoints towards your objective as well as changing the game state in a variety of ways.

Help Eyenstein and his one-eyed companions, Iris and Hawk, return to their home world of Myclopia after a lab accident strands them blinking around the Viewniverse.  Harness the power of the Viewniverse by collecting Viewpoints to help the Blinkers return home!

The Winking Dead, the fourth set in the episodic Viewniverse card game series, sees the Blinkers finally getting a chance to relax on the idyllic world of Paradise.  Their respite is cut short when they discover that a Zombeye plague is infecting the citizens of Paradise.  Join the Blinkers as they try to eradicate the Zombeye plague.  If the Blinkers escape Paradise, catch them in the next episode - Illuminati Wars.

The number in the top-left-hand corner of each card indicates the number of Viewpoints the card is worth and the text at the bottom is the action to be performed. In turn, players simply draw a card from the Draw Pile and play a card from their hand into their Field of View.

At the end of their turn players count the Viewpoints of the cards in their Field of View, the first player to collect 100 or more Viewpoints wins. An alternative way to win, is to be the last player to have seven zombeyes their Field of View and be overwhelmed by the zombeye plague. The Winking Dead gameplay is swift to dive into and deceptively strategic. Each card provides you with Viewpoints towards your objective as well as changing the game state in a variety of ways.

The game will be funded by Kickstarter, launching June 10.  You can check out the page and click a link to be notified when we launch.

https://www.kickstarter.com/projects/huninthesun/s55-wings-over-the-atlantic

Seaplanes were used for impressive flights across the Oceans in the early 20th Century. One of the most successful planes was the Savoia Marchetti S.55, used by many pilots for their solitary and mass flights.

This game allows players to challenge the huge expanses of the Southern and Northern Atlantic, the weather, and mechanical failures to reach cheering crowds in far off-cities, on the path of Francesco De Pinedo, Italo Balbo, João Ribeiro de Barros, and others.

Each of the 2 to 5* players must organize his own mission, balancing time and perils; a solo variant is also included. Each turn, the player decides whether and when to take off toward the next, carefully chosen objective. Longer flights mean higher risks and more rewards. It might be that delaying takeoff for better preparation makes sense. Game events are taken from historical pilots' diaries. Basic rules are fun for everyone, while players opting for a more realistic game choose which advanced rules to add, from plane technology enhancements, to night flights, to more involved weather, and other elements.


    There is a 5th Player stretch goal included in the Kickstarter. 


—description from the designer

This expansion is compatible with all previous editions of Snowdonia.

Snowdonia: Grand Tour takes players on a railway building adventure around the world. This is the biggest Snowdonia expansion yet, featuring 12 lovingly crafted scenarios and an all-new campaign mode.

Scenarios
Every scenario in Grand Tour comes with its own bespoke, immersive board. These scenarios use their boards to explore new elements of the design and offer more adventurous experiences than Snowdonia’s previous instalments.

Scenarios Included:

- Demerara-Berbice
- Uganda Railway
- Isle of Wight 
- Trans-Australian Railway (Updated for the new board system)
- Qinghai Tibet (Updated for the new board system)
- Darjeeling and Himalayan Railway (A reworked and expanded version of Alubari)
- Liverpool Overhead Railway
- Florida Overseas Railroad
- Cleobury Mortimer
- Tokyo Metro
- La Belle Epoque
- A mystery scenario not yet revealed to the public.

Campaign Mode
Each player takes on the role of a famous figure from railway history, competing over 4 scenarios to determine who runs the best railway construction company.

Players’ characters are represented by a 10-card deck they can use to enhance their actions. Between games, players can upgrade their decks and potentially collect that scenario’s unique souvenir.

It is compatible with every previously released scenario and comes with a unique souvenir card for each of them. Campaign mode also has a dedicated solo mode in which the automa gains strength as the player does.

Four animals decided to take fate into their own paws and set off to Bremen to join the city orchestra. Unfortunately, on their journey, they encountered some robbers...
The clever animals thought up a good way to scare them: they stood in a high stack, on each other’s backs, and began to roar loudly.

In the game, you gather your group of animals and try to lay them long enough to overcome the robbers. You win if the length of animals tiles you collected are longer than the robbers tiles.

—description from publisher
family: Folk Tales & Fairy Tales: Town Musicians of Bremen

description:
The Woblet games are played with two-sided letter tiles.
Make the longest word or crosswords. Find a secret word or pass on the hot potato. Always try to play faster than your opponents.

Exciting or relaxing. Competitive or chill. Creative and above all: fun.

You play all at once and don’t have to wait until it’s your turn. The rules of the games are explained within a minute and you play Woblet at any desired level. From short words with young children to extremely difficult tasks for the real word artists.

The average duration of each game is a couple of minutes.
Each game box contains enough game material for about 6 players. 
With the contents of a box you can play Woblet in any language. (Latin alphabet).
The rules in different languages and extra game possibilities can be found on the website.

A cooperative variant for Respuestas Abiertas: Imágenes, delivered in the March 2023 Ludoty Box.

Players will try to get more points than the ghost player over 6 rounds. If the duo is correct, they will score points. If they are wrong, the ghost player will score points.

A cooperative variant for Misión Antártida, delivered in the March 2023 Ludoty Box. Includes 1 die.

In Cooperative mode, each player will use a game sheet, but at the end of the game all players will add up to a single joint final score.

boardgame: Feet First Into Hell: Orbital Drop Combat Skirmish Game
description:
Feet First Into Hell is an easy to learn, succinct, squad-based sci-fi miniture skirmish game. It features unique orbital reinforcement rules and high customizability! Prepare to drop!

Feet First Into Hell is designed to get you into the action as quickly as possible. FFIH is built to combine the dramatic action and tension of games with high customizability, for a fast to play, accessible, and most importantly, SUCCINCT wargame.

In FFIH, players recruit their miniature "drop teams,” and then equip them with a wide array of weapons and versatile gear. By avoiding long, stingy army lists, the customizability is in your hands. You want to run a bunch of roided out armored space goombas? You can. Want to run a bunch of cyber-shinobi? You can. Your drop team is your drop team — but the game still maintains a fair balance through point allotment.

Once you have your team, you deploy from orbit, dropping your forces strategically onto the board. FFIH uses a straightforward d6 system to simulate the hectic battlefield. Players have a variety of movement options, including dynamic reactions that interact with the unique cover system that discards hits rather than requiring more math.

Lock, load, drop, roll, repeat.

—description from the publisher

This supplement covers all the rules required for introducing heavy weapons and vehicles in the 1-48TACTIC game.
Covers Hard and Soft targets, HE, AP and HEAT shells, Heavy MG, rifle grenades, bazooka and other man-portable AT weapons, mines, incendiary and explosives, upgrade cards and much more! You will also find included stat cards and profiles for many of the vehicles and weapons most commonly used during the “Battle of the Bulge”.

Welcome to the Bizarre Bazaar, where you turn ordinary into extraordinary!

Bizarre Bazaar offers a humorous and memorable way to teach players new vocabulary while immersing them in a fusion of reality and myth.

Each round, the Buyer draws a Role card and becomes a mundane human or a fantastic creature. All other players use Quality (adjective or gerund) and Item (noun) cards to invent Quality Items. These combinations are placed in bags that are shuffled and distributed to new, unsuspecting players. To the best of their abilities, players then attempt to sell the bizarre contents of their bags, and the Buyer chooses which one of these Quality Items wins the round. The players who created and pitched the chosen Quality Item each receive one of the winning cards as a reward. The Buyer switches roles with the player to their left and play continues until a predetermined number of rounds has been completed. Prepare for mutually assured amusement!

—description from the publisher

Tiuma, our beloved city-state, will fall from grace unless we put an end to the gohut threat. Get your troops ready, sharpen your wits, and attack their nests. The general who claims the most gohut's heads will be rewarded.

Based on the book "the dance of the gohut" Gohut is born, a strategy board game for two players in which each general must direct their troops towards the gohut camp trying to avoid the rival army.

On alternate turns, players must move, attack or rest one of their 3 chosen units set up on the board, following their unit's movement rules and then flip their unit token which will grant different moves or attacks for next turns, and if within range of the gohut camp will be able to capture one.

The player who manages to catch three gohut or has more captures when one of the players loses 6 units will be the winner.
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
La Oruga is a massive starship, capable of bringing a crew of thousands on a centuries-long journey to found a base on the distant exoplanet Mariposa. However, the ship’s Directorate has become increasingly authoritarian and resistant to change, even as it becomes clear that Mariposa is already inhabited with intelligent life. You are the head of Rift, a resistance movement trying to covertly sway others to your side, seize control of the ship, and change its destination.

ExoShip is a solo spatial puzzle game. Cards represent the ship's founders, which can be manipulated by your actions, randomized events, or by the passage of time itself. A card representing the ship moves decades along a timeline each turn as it travels across the table from Earth to its destination planet. As the ship moves each turn, the eldest founders die while a new generation of founders are added.

At the start, all founders are loyal to the Directorate but their cards can be flipped to join the Rift resistance movement, unlocking powerful unique abilities and earning points toward winning the game. Members of Rift must avoid the watchful eyes of Directorate founders or risk being "disappeared" during the suspicion phase!

Actions include: sway an adjacent Directorate founder to your side, use a Rift founder’s ability to manipulate the grid, or replace a Rift founder with the top founder card from the stack. Once per game you must take an action to change the ship’s destination to the uninhabited exoplanet Axios, but only if the ship’s captain belongs to Rift!

Win by swaying enough points worth of founders to the Rift movement and flipping the destination planet card before the ship arrives. Do you and the Rift founders have the conviction to guide La Oruga to Axios and establish an ExoBase?

—description from artist

Salamini ("Oh Salamis!") is a simple but not simplistic game of trireme battles during the Classical Greece period (500-336 BC).

Rules are just two pages long, but include all the features of ancient naval warfare (shooting, ramming, boarding), are fast and fun but also tactically challenging.

A small and quick naval encounter can be fought between two squadrons of 8-12 triremes, or a bigger battle can be arranged, for a standalone game or as a part of a campaign.

A link to a free STL file of a 1/1200 trireme is provided in the rules.

—description from the publisher

In Fatty Tuna, players race to expand their sushi empires, gather ingredients, and trade with other players in order to be the first to complete their menu. Many of the game mechanics—collecting resources from hex-tiles, trading cards with other players, rolling to determine your move—will feel familiar to board game lovers, making the game easy to pick up. However, there are plenty variables and event cards that can make the game infinitely more layered and strategic.

Up to 5 people can play Fatty Tuna, and it can take anywhere from 30 minutes to 2 hours, depending on which menu you chose to play with and the number of players.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Twilight of Divine Right: With Fire and Sword – Eastern European Battles in the Period: 1605-1676
description:
'With Fire and Sword' is a supplement to the Twilight of Divine Right rules and need to be read in conjunction with these rules. It includes the rules, amendments and additions to fight the battle and wars in Eastern Europe in this era.

The players can field Polish-Lithuanian, Russian, Swedish, Cossack, Crimean Khanate (Tatar), Ottoman and other smaller armies in the wars of this era. A series of wars in this era transformed the world and helped shape the modern world.

These rules are accompanied by three scenario books featuring battles across the period.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

boardgame: Twilight of Divine Right: With Fire and Sword – Scenario Book 1: The Rise of Sweden, 1605 – 1629
description:
This book covers the rise of Sweden before their intervention in the Thirty Years War in 1630. It covers the wars of the Polish Lithuanian Commonwealth with Sweden in 1600–1611 and with the Russians in 1609-1618. Also the later war with Sweden in 1626 – 1629 when Gustaf II Adolf transformed the Swedish army. In addition there are some scenarios from the Commonwealth’s wars against the Ottomans in 1620 – 1621.

The booklet features 11 scenarios from 7 historical battles

Battles featured:
Kircholm, 1605 
Klushino, 1611 
Chochim, 1621 
Gniew, 1626 
Dirschau, 1627
Gorzno, 1629
Trzciana, 1629

boardgame: Twilight of Divine Right: With Fire and Sword – Scenario Book 2: The Deluge, 1632–1660
description:
This second book covers the battles during the period sometimes called the ‘Deluge’. The 'Deluge' period saw the Polish Lithuanian Commonwealth attacked by many armies. The booklet concentrates on the war of 1648 -1657 against the Cossack rebellion under Khmelnytsky. Also the campaigns of 1655 – 1660 is against the Swedes. In addition the Russians war against the Commonwealth in the Smolensk War of 1632 – 1634 is also covered. The first war that Russia started to modernise it's army. It is common for allies to be present on one or both sides in these battles.

The booklet features 8 scenarios from 6 historical battles and campaigns.

Battles featured:
Smolensk, 1633
Pyliavsti, 1648
Berestecko, 1651
Batoh, 1652
Zarnow, 1655 
Warsaw, 1656

boardgame: Twilight of Divine Right: With Fire and Sword – Scenario Book 3: The Wars for the Ukraine, 1654–1676
description:
The third scenario book concentrates on the shifting war for the control of the Ukraine in the later part of this era but which continue to this day started to exercise some control over the Ukraine. The Thirteen Years War of 1654 – 1667 featuring various combinations of Polish-Lithuanian, Russian, Cossack and Crimean Khanate (Tatar) forces. In addition some of the scenarios are from the Commonwealth's war against the Ottomans and Crimean Khanate as they renewed there attempts to dominate the Ukraine in 1672 -1676.

The booklet features 11 scenarios from 9 historical battles and campaigns.

Battles featured:
Konotop, 1659 
Polonka, 1660
Chudow Campaign, 1660
1st Lubar, 14th September 1660 
2nd Lubar, 16th September 1660 
Slobodyszcze, 1660
Kanev, 1662
Chocim, 1673 
Zurawno, 1676
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Seriously!? is een snel kaartspel wat makkelijk te spelen is, maar moeilijk te beïnvloeden. Dat laatste creëert veel speelplezier en af en toe de behoefte om heel hard 'SERIEUS!!' te roepen.
Door kaarten uit je hand te spelen probeer je op de beste score te eindigen. De monsters op de gespeelde kaart bepalen hoeveel stappen je verzet op het scorebord en in welke richting. Actiekaarten zorgen voor leuke en speelse twists, waardoor je je het hele spel zult afvragen wie er nu uiteindelijk gaat winnen.

Het spel kan op twee manieren eindigen en dat heeft weer invloed op de eindscore. Je positie op het scorebord kan namelijk in pluspunten geteld worden of in minpunten.

Dit spel biedt Seriously veel speelplezier aan 2 tot 8 spelers. Daag iemand uit voor een chaotisch 1 op 1 spel of val van je stoel van het lachen als iemand net heeft ontdekt dat ze een negatieve score hebben behaald in plaats van te winnen. Verwacht het onverwachte!

ENG

Seriously!? is a fast-paced, easy to play but hard to control game that ensures tons of fun. Move around the scoreboard by playing cards from your hand. The monsters on your card determine how many steps you move and in which direction. Action cards provide fun twists which will keep you guessing throughout the entire game who will win.

Steer towards one of two unique endgoals, but be prepared for the rules to change at the last second. And if someone else wins, you earn yourself the right to shout out "SERIOUSLY!?" at the top of your lungs.

Seriously!? is a lovely game for 2-8 players, which is played in rounds of mere minutes. Enjoy battling in a chaotic 1v1 or die of laughter together when someone realizes they've just gained a negative score instead of a win. Expect the unexpected!

—description from the designer

NOTE! Funding campaign was cancelled on 2023 and campaign might be relaunched at some later date.

In Rolling Zombies, you will find yourself in a  post-apocalyptic world, where the living dead roam the wasteland, and five factions battle for survival. Each faction controls a base and sends out their members to scavenge for resources, explore ruins, and fight off zombies and rival factions.

The game is played using mechanics from the popular "Yachts" dice game, where players roll a set of dice to achieve various combinations and scores. In Rolling Zombies, players roll dice to move their characters, collect resources, and fight off
zombies and rival factions.

Zombie hordes spawn randomly and start moving around the map. Players get points for destroying them, but the horde can be also turned to start moving towards another direction.

Victory points are awarded for defeated enemy units. There is also an ancient fortress in the centre of the map and defeating it awards victory points and game end. At the end victory points are given also for the area controlled by each player.

-description from designer

In Kitunda, a game for two to four players,  in which only the chess pawns are available for placement on an 8x8 field of play. The eight pawns of each player reside in a “trench or dugout” alongside the outer edges of the game board.

A plague has crippled the “royals” (rooks, knights, bishops, and king) leaving the queens of four different nations to attend to them. The Kitunda, Swahili for chess pawns, are given a crystal (die), which they can use to access the powers of the royals.

Each roll of a die allows any pawn to enter the field of play using the capability of a royal piece. For example, a 1 on a die allows any chess pawn to move like a rook. While a 6 allows any pawn to move like a bishop, and so on.

The objective is to capture all opposing pawns until only one victorious player remains.

-description from designer

Players state sentences using the number on the card they flipped, the symbol on the die they rolled, and the numbers on the cards in their hand. The players accumulate cards by putting flipped and played cards in their pile in front of them. Whoever has the most cards at the end of the game wins!

-description from designer

Go back 50,000 years in time and take the lead of a nomadic Neanderthal clan. Explore diverse territories, develop essential survival skills and fight against the elements of nature.

This is the challenge that Engis provides. There are 8 game rounds, divided into three phases each:

In Phase 1, players skillfully use their Neanderthal pieces to explore and conquer a hexagon of territory, which will be incorporated into the individual board forming a different scenario each game.

In Phase 2, everyone can perform three main actions in any order: Move parts across territories, Develop skills and Craft prehistoric objects.

In Phase 3, players choose scoring cards, drop them on the table and score, always looking for the best moment in the game to use each card.

At the end of the game, the storms of nature selected at the beginning, come into play and must be circumvented by the nomadic tribes. The player with the highest score wins and ensures the survival of the species!

Note: Engis is a city in Belgium, where the first fossil of Neanderthal man was found in 1829.

boardgame: Monopoly: Marvel Studios' Black Panther – Wakanda Forever Edition
description:
Black Panther fans, get ready to enter the Marvel Universe with this exciting Monopoly game inspired by the Black Panther: Wakanda Forever superhero movie. It's an epic race to control key locations, build temples, and secure as much Vibranium as possible. Play on the side of the Wakandans or support the Talocanil using different colored custom Monopoly tokens and buildings. The last player with Vibranium when all other players have lost theirs wins the game. Will the forces of Wakanda or the mighty Talocanil claim victory in this fierce battle across land and sea?


     BLACK PANTHER: WAKANDA FOREVER THEME: This edition of the Monopoly game features artwork, characters, components, and gameplay inspired by Marvel Studios' Black Panther: Wakanda Forever movie
     BOARD GAME FOR AGES 8+: Players choose a side in this exciting Monopoly board game for Black Panther fans. Play on the side of the Wakandans or the Talocanil in an epic clash across land and sea
     CUSTOM ZINC MONOPOLY TOKENS: Players choose a silver colored token to play on the side of the Wakandans, or a gold colored token if they're supporting the Talocanil
     BATTLE FOR VIBRANIUM: In this exciting Monopoly game, players race to control key locations and add buildings to turn the tide of battle. The last player with Vibranium when all others lost theirs wins
     FUN FAMILY BOARD GAME: Get family and friends together to play Monopoly: Marvel Studios' Black Panther: Wakanda Forever game. It's great for family game night and makes an awesome gift for Black Panther fans


—description from the publisher

boardgame: 부동산 거물: 첫 번째 도시 – 한국어판 특별 확장 (Magnate: The First City – Korean Exclusive Promo)
description:
Promo created for Korean Edition features a unique set of 4 new tenant cards and 8 corresponding tenant tokens with a special Korean cultural theme.

Tenants: Fried Chicken Joint, Idol Agency, Examinee, Korean Rice Wine Brewery

"Do you not feel, through a kind of instinctive intuition that cannot be analyzed but that is certain, that the ground is shaking once again in Europe? Do you not feel—how shall I call it?—that a revolutionary wind is blowing?"
– Alexis de Tocqueville, Souvenir (January, 1848)

33 years after the Congress of Vienna, Continental Europe still lives under the reactionary system designed by the Austrian Prince Metternich, the dominant figure of the age. Outside of England and France, absolute monarchy is the norm - and even in the latter, Louis-Philippe, the “bourgeois king”, is busy rolling back the people’s hard-fought liberties.

In the east, the empires of the “Holy Alliance”, Austria, Russia, and Prussia, enforce ruthless repression against progressive ideas - whether nationalist, republican, democratic, liberal, or socialist. But the time is ripe for revolution, and the conflagration of 1848 will shake this old system to its core in a whirlwind of insurrection and new ideas. “People’s wars” will rage.

1848: Springtime of Nations is a 2-player CDG depicting the unprecedented wave of revolutionary activity that swept across Europe in the titular year of 1848. Based on the hit area control system pioneered by GMT’s 1989: Dawn of Freedom, and Twilight Struggle, it pits the forces of “the Revolution” against those of the “Counterrevolution” in a struggle to decide the future of Europe, and the world.

The Revolutionary player controls the various insurgent and progressive movements trying to usher in a new order, while the Counter-Revolutionary player leads the forces of reaction aiming to preserve the status quo. They will fight over and across the empires and nations of Europe, attempting to suppress their enemy’s influence and extend their own through a combination of political and military force - all the while navigating myriad historical events of the time.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
Tribes of The Lost Island is an area control civilization builder. The goal of the game is to gain control of a mysterious island. Multiple tribes wash ashore on the outskirts of this lost island. As you take turns settling and moving across the island you will gain two types of resources: Population and Knowledge. Population is used for expanding your tribe and building new settlements. Knowledge is used to unlock new technologies from the tech tree and gaining an edge over your opponents. The game is finished when a player either achieves a settlement victory by spreading their tribe across the map, or by achieving a technology victory by building a city and defending it from rival players.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Beyond the Boar's Peak mountains sits the Empire. From a seat of power within the walled city of Whitehold, The Autarch plans a sorcerous apotheosis...

Six Winters is a cooperative fantasy campaign game inspired by Mythic Persia. Blending storytelling with tactical dice puzzles and troupe character management, the game features an expanding world shaped by your choices.

As The Empire grows in power, you guide the fate of Brightdune, a land of many peoples and customs. Each session, you will deploy a rotating cast of characters to strengthen your community and fuel the resistance.


     Craft and Narrate: Create evocative assets using resource dice and use them to collaboratively narrate scenes.
     Your Chronicle: Players choose which scenes are important to the history of Brightdune.
     Beyond Combat: Scenes are thematic and diverse, you might retrieve the Owner of a Thousand Horses' horses, answer Scorpion-Tail's riddle, or help Qurbaga woo Pox Face.


You have six winters to break the Autarch's hold, or watch as your home falls into misery and shadow.

—description from the designer

boardgame: 異世界ラブレター Love Letter
description:
The legendary board game that has sold over 3 million units worldwide has been reborn in another world. Featuring 16 unique characters and new rules with increased strategy!

Key differences from normal Love Letter:


     One copy each of cards 1-16 (including new effects upon being played)
     Players start with a "Love Letter" which can be used to protect against being knocked out once per game. Can be considered a "one time shield". Some card effects directly target it.
     With only one copy of each card guessing is more difficult, but there is the potential to earn an extra victory point as a reward for guessing correctly.
     When the world is "lit up" highest numbers are best as per normal. When the world is "dark" lower numbers win any comparisons.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

boardgame: I'm A Poet (and yes, I know it)
description:
I’m a Poet is the hilariously absurd rhyming word game where you race to figure out rhyming riddles, fast!

Everyone gets the same two clues: words that rhyme with answer, and a little poem about the answer.

Figure it out, grab Willy and earn the card!

Two Degrees is a cooperative tile-laying game where you create a civilization that mitigates the rise in global temperatures. Grow Forests, build Wind Farms and Connect Cities via high speed rail to keep temperatures from rising by two degrees.

During the build phase, players take turns adding world tiles to their civilization. The department head of each department will record any progress. For example, if you place a tile that creates a Wind Farm, the head of the Department of Energy will move their tracker from 0 Wind Farms (red) to 1 Wind Farm (yellow).

After the build phase, players move into crisis mode where they must draw crisis cards and deal with whatever crises are on the cards. These crises will vary in severity based on a Report from a specific department. For example, the crisis card "Coal Power" will cause global temperatures to rise based on a Report from the Department of Energy. Temperatures would rise by 0.0 if the report is blue, 0.1 if the report is green, 0.2 if yellow, or 0.3 degrees if the report was red.

Your goal is to keep global temperature rise below two degrees for the duration of the game (10 rounds, each round has one build phase and one crisis mode). You'll score your civilization at the end based on the number of Forests, Wind Farms and Connected Cities you have.

-description from designer

Amrtaka: Battle for Immortality – Forge Your Legacy in a Mythological War Will you seize immortality or fade into legend?

Dive into an epic struggle inspired by the ancient Hindu legend of Samudra Manthana (The Churning of the Cosmic Ocean). As a warrior-king, lead your kingdom against rivals in a race to capture Amrit—the elixir of eternal life. But immortality demands cunning: forge alliances, outmaneuver foes, and harness divine power in this deeply strategic board game where every choice echoes through mythic history.

Game Explanation

Try the Amrtaka Chat Box!

Got questions about gameplay, rules, or setup? Just ask!
No more rulebook flipping — get instant help anytime.
? Stuck on a mechanic?
? Unsure of turn order?
? Need quick clarification?

Type it in the notebook Chat Box and keep your game flowing!
? Smart. Fast. Simple.
Make your Amrtaka journey smoother and more epic!

https://notebooklm.google.com/notebook/bce095f1-fbda-45e9-925e-38d2ece9e3ea?authuser=1

★ Why You’ll Be Hooked:

Dual Paths to Victory: Race to 60 victory points (“drops of Amrit”) by immortalizing lands (15-point bursts) or conquering rivals (5-point strikes). Choose wisely—each path demands opposing strategies!

Mythology Brought to Life: Gods and demons aren’t just lore—they’re game-changing mechanics. Deploy 10 hidden god-followers whose phased powers reshape the board across 30 turns.

Revolutionary Portal System: Bend space with personal portals to teleport armies instantly. But beware: place them recklessly, and lose them forever to enemy conquests.

Tense Action Economy: Every turn burns precious weapons (needed even to move!). Sacrifice short-term gains for long-term setups, balancing card abilities, armies, and divine boons.

Phased Epic Scale: The game evolves in three acts, driven by god-follower movements: claim lands early, unleash divine powers mid-game, and scramble in a “power vacuum” as time runs out!

★ Key Mechanics That Shine:

Strategic Combat: Attack with matched weapon types (melee/ranged) and outpower defenders using card values + army bonuses. Lose your standee’s land? Suffer crushing setbacks!

Dynamic Board Control: Make lands “immortal” for huge points… but lose exclusivity and thin the action deck. Trade territories to reposition or upgrade holdings!

God-Follower Powers: Reveal hidden abilities in Phase 2 (turns 11–20) for explosive one-time boosts—then adapt when gods withdraw in the endgame.

Brutal Consequences: Face possible elimination if stripped of all armies AND weapons… yet your portals linger, haunting rivals even in defeat!

★ Immersive Components:

10 Circular Lands looping in a cosmic ocean, divine zones, and a central island.

48 Action Cards packed with directional choices, teleports, and tactical boosts.

Army/Weapon Tokens (red/gray armies, melee/ranged weapons) and Portal Tokens for warp-speed tactics.

Deluxe Edition: Swap standees for 3D warrior-king miniatures and track points in a magic flower bowl!

Perfect for fans of Inis, Kemet, or Cyclades who crave deeper integration of theme and strategy. Amrtaka masterfully blends Hindu mythology with knife-edge decisions, ensuring no two games unfold alike. Will you hoard Amrit for your kingdom—or shatter rivals to claim eternity?

Ready your weapons. The ocean churns. Immortality awaits.

The Temple of the Holy Family was built for decades. We did it - God's House inspires admiration. Only the stained glass windows are missing. They will appear soon. The dark, Gothic windows will be illuminated with countless colors, and the paintings and inscriptions will remind you of extraordinary events from the Bible. Each stained-glass window will tell the story of a different character, and the wonderful work will be crowned with the Latin words GLORIA DEI, because everything is for the Glory of God.
In GLORIA DEI, each player takes on the role of a stained glass maker. He receives his own boards with windows, which during the game he will fill with pictures showing fragments of stained glass.
The winner will be the one who will make thoughtful, logical decisions, who will be lucky and who will show perceptiveness.

Wanderers discover events from the Old and New Testaments, and with them interesting, often surprising tasks.
The texts on the cards of this game are arranged in such a way as to simultaneously refer to the Bible and to the everyday life of the players: the talents they received, creative ideas, interesting passions, great, important events, as well as everyday matters that may seem it's trivial.

Players travel through the biblical land together discovering new cards. They always win together by reaching the end of the expedition, but they try to make their result as high as possible.

The aim of the game is to get to know each other better. The game teaches communication. By answering questions, you share your observations, ideas and dreams, and by listening to your loved ones, you can learn many interesting things about them.

By playing Przygodziaki you collect family points. Once you've tallied them up, take a look at the scoring table on the last page of the rulebook to see what you've accomplished together. Remember, you always win.

The game brings the figures of saints closer. It shows their images, presents important events and curiosities from their lives.
The goal of the game is to collect as many pictures and cards about saints as possible, from which you can also create the highest scoring combinations.

You take on the role of a prisoner on a remote planet, incarcerated for crimes you didn't commit. However, you soon discover that you're not alone on this planet, as an alien race called the Synth has invaded and taken over the prison facility.
As the cell doors unexpectedly open, you find yourself in the midst of chaos, with Synth soldiers rampaging through the prison halls. Armed with nothing but your wits and your fists, you must fight your way through the Synth forces to survive and find a way off the planet.
The game is a single-player, action-adventure game with RPG elements, set in a sci-fi world. You'll need to scavenge for resources and level up your skills to overcome increasingly difficult enemies and obstacles.

Fly your way to victory! Gather enough cards to send airplanes through the different stages of takeoff, and then high into the sky! Navigate weather problems, mechanical failures and your opponents' strategies in order to successfully launch your whole fleet. Get all of your planes off the ground before your opponent's do to win. This game has engaging, realistic gameplay because it was invented by a pilot!

Contents:
100x Cards,
12x Wooden Planes
4x Runway Pieces
4x Hangar Cards
Illustrated Rules

Become a candidate for the chief of a clan who can communicate with animals (Wanyama) and borrow their power and win the race to determine the clan's chief with the help of the animals.

In Wanyama: The Zodiac Attacks zodiac animals attack after hearing rumors of a race from rabbits, and race with the help of these animals.

This game is played by gaining points as you draw cards and play them against your opponents, you can play this game in a short time with a steady rhythm.

Stock Shock, stock market card game.  Each player’s goal is to become the richest person after 6 rounds by strategically managing their investments through purchasing and selling shares in 8 various industries.

All players start with $8M cash and aims to grow that through 3 moves each round by investing in industries that go up and action cards that allow players to gain cash and shares.

Industry prices go up and down at the end of every round with an event card picked up and affecting all players equally.  All players have a scorecard that they adjust with a whiteboard marker.

This casual, filler card game is a fun and approachable way to understand the basics of the stock market.

—description from the designer

SLOTKER playing cards are based on a standard 52-card deck of poker playing cards, with each card displaying one of six classic and familiar slot machine symbols (Lemons, Cherries, Oranges, Plums, Bells and Bars), with each card simulating one reel of a 3-reel slot machine.

The goal of most SLOTKER card games is to make the best or highest-ranking 3-of-a-kind hand, with either poker card values (above the card suits) or slot machine symbols (below the card suits), and the games are designed to simulate the action of a 3-reel slot machine, often with progressively-increasing payouts (antes required by players) when a single round of hands results with no winner.

Slot machine symbol rankings are based on the quantities of each slot machine symbol distributed throughout the deck and make a smooth and logical transition into poker card value rankings.

3-of-a-kind poker cards outrank all 3-of-a-kind slot machine symbols, and when two or more players hold the same three matching slot machine symbols, the winner is determined by the highest-ranking 3-card poker hand, using standard poker rankings, from highest to lowest:  Flush, Straight, One-pair and High-card hands.

—description from the designer

All your favorite WWE Superstars and Divas in one board game. Step into the ring. Your incredible WWE Superstars, events and technology are all here. Buy mezzanine seats and move to ringside seats, then watch the money rain down. Negotiate and manage your way to the top, but be careful, because there's only room for one.

For everyone else, there's bankruptcy. Market for success, you can own it all!

Doctor Esker has left behind another mysterious book full of puzzles written in his own hand. Nobody has cracked his cryptic codes yet. Are you up to the challenge?

The game provides a deck of 83 cards which contain ten devious puzzles to solve. You can play through the puzzles solo or together with family and friends. Great for a party, a game night, or a lazy afternoon.

Each puzzle is composed of a set of cards. You can solve the puzzle by reading, comparing, arranging and pondering the messages, drawings, and artwork on the cards. Solving the puzzle reveals a sequence of numbers. When you think you’ve got it, gather the solution cards corresponding to the number sequence and flip them. If you see a message or image, you’ve solved the puzzle. Persevere to the end and receive Dr. Esker's greatest praise.

-description from designer

A nsfw party card game of bad language, crazy drawings and hilarious mistakes.

How to play

     Draw each other using our patented colour-popping scratch cards. 
     Add your faces to the game, along with some challenge cards, then deal.
     Take turns to flip over a card and shout the answer based on a few simple, yet tricky, rules. 
     Your card could be a colour, a swear word, or even your own face! Each card is designed to mess with your head – the more mistakes, the more laughs.
     If you get the answer wrong (or take too long) you’ll be forced to pick up cards. Get it right and you lose that card. The first player to lose all their cards wins.



     Mess with your heads – A tricky combination of colours, swear words and pictures will confuse even your smartest of friends.
     Make it your own – Your creations become the star of each game. Use the colour-popping scratch cards to create your own rules using words and pictures, then keep them for future games.
     Choose your challenge – With a variety of extra challenge cards to add to the mix, no round will ever be the same.
     Laugh at your friends – watch your friends fail while they decide to swear or not.


The long awaited follow up from the makers of F**k. The Game.
A Snap variant for young children. Instead of 'snapping' the same cards, players here must 'snap' cards that rhyme. Example: Boat and coat. The one who collects the most cards is the winner.

The Phoenix Expansion features seven unique black puzzles that offer the most points and keep smaller pieces relevant even in the late game. With fresh shapes to complete and new strategies to uncover, the Phoenix Expansion brings exciting new replayability to Project L and keep you engaged for hours on end.

The weather outside is gloomy - but inside, the cats are ready to have some fun. The mice have invited them to play hide and seek: find four mice to win the game. It’s not that simple though - those pesky mice just won’t keep still! Meanwhile, the dog is curious and wants to play... but wherever he goes, he is in the way.

Playing the game:
When it is your turn to move, spin the spinner and move your cat the number of spaces the arrow indicates. You are allowed to leave the center room in any direction but afterward, you must move your cat in a clockwise direction on the outer circle. Landing on a specific space will trigger some event, depending if there is a mouse on it or not.

Winning the game:
The winner is the first player who finds their four mice and then goes to the room where the dog is to impress him.

The situation is critical!

Uncontrolled development and climate change have brought your city to the brink of chaos…

Together, take matters into your own hands and transform your city into "Evolutopia", a city that takes care of its inhabitants and the planet.

Citizens of Evolutopia, it's up to you!

You have to implement changes in the city to make all the existing problems disappear but also to make sufficient progress in 3 of the 5 key indicators. To reach this goal, you will select and assign experts to carry out the evolutions.

But these experts will also have to be used to manage crises which, if they accumulate, will cause you to lose the game.

Each of you will be assigned special powers (roles) and may have access to good plans to help you accomplish your common mission.

Players either all win together or all lose together.

—description from the publisher

The popular card game LEVEL 8: redesigned for children 6 years and up, but loads of fun for all ages!

Four cards in hand, on your turn pick up a card, complete a set if you can, and discard a card. Each player has their own discard pile, and you can take from the pick up pile, or any discard pile.

Collect 4 animal cards that match your tile, and get the tile for that animal. The first to get 8 animal tiles wins.
family: Versions & Editions: Junior Versions of Grown-Up Games

boardgame: Descent: Legends of the Dark – The Betrayer's War
description:
Descent: Legends of the Dark – The Betrayer's War, a.k.a., Descent Act II, is a sprawling and deeply co-operative dungeon crawler driven by player choice – all powered by an app that allows for games to run without a Game Master. The game's immersive storyline is packed with twists and turns sure to thrill players of all skill levels. Campaigns can be played solo or with up to four players, requiring co-operation and skillful teamwork to overcome challenges.

The Betrayer's War builds on the exhilarating dungeon-crawling adventure of its predecessor by introducing new villains, quests, and mechanisms. The game sees players returning to Terrinoth, where they find themselves in an unlikely alliance with their greatest enemy: Waiqar the Betrayer. They have to work together to battle against armies of dragons and the sinister machinations of the Uthuk Y'llan. All six heroes from the original game return to defend their legacy, each equipped with a new hero card and a variety of abilities to aid them in their battle against the impending darkness and the Dragonlord Levirax.

—description from the publisher

Larry “The Squatch” McCallister, publisher of “The Cryptid Chronicle”, is needing new pictures of the 
monsters and creatures that hide in the fridges of our world. Their story MUST be told!

It is up to YOU, intrepid photographer, to collect pictures of these fascinating beasts. Not all cryptids are created equal, however, and Larry will pay you based on their current popularity. Sell Larry as many photos as possible, but be careful not to wind up with too many photos in your hand at the end of the game...as anything not sold will COST you money!

Now get out there and collect those photos, and make the people believe!

…………

A turn consists of picking a face-up card from one of 9 stacks, or playing a series of cards to match and score adjacent (or diagonal) face up cards. Scored cards are set face down by the player.

The number of cards required to match increases, based on previous matches, and once something has been matched to its maximum amount that specific match can no longer be scored!

The game ends when 3 stacks are empty. Cards in your face down pile are scored, based on their value, but any cards in your hand are worth negative their value!

—description from the publisher

Samson and Delilah is a fast, asymmetric, social deduction and drafting game for two players. Delilah helps both Samson and the Philistines but whose side is she on? Will Samson be strong enough to win the fight or should he flee instead?

The goal of Samson is to defeat the Philistines.
The goal of Delilah is to either aid Samson in defeating the Philistines or trick Samson into believing she is aiding him but secretly working against him. If she has 2 or 3 locks of hair, she is on Samson's side.

1 player must be Samson. The other is Delilah.
Each players character faces them.
Shuffle the 18 card deck and place it between the players.
Each player draws 2 cards.

boardgame: G.I. JOE Mission Critical: Vehicle Pack #2
description:
Four new Vehicle cards bring more firepower in the fight against Cobra.

Includes: LAW, Phantom X-19, Polar Battle Bear, and Tomahawk.

Get your memory racing for the right characters in this game full of emotions! Players simultaneously tap cards matching either a character or one of the 6 emotions. Memo Rush improves visual perception, memory and emotion recognition.

Quickly tap the Character card(s) matching the characters shown on the flipped Memory card. The first player to get 8 Character cards wins.

The oldest player, then going clockwise, flips a Memory card face up. Each Memory card shows 3 of the 42 characters depicted on the Character cards. Players simultaneously tap face down Character cards matching one of 3 characters on the flipped Memory card.

—description from the publisher

Divide your play group into two teams and compete to give 3 answers to creative prompts. For example, the prompt might be "Give me 3 things to never say during a job interview?" Each team alternates giving 3 answers in 10 seconds; whichever team fails first, loses the round. First to win 10 rounds wins the game!

Welcome to a land of fantasy and mystery, where the fables of old have fought for the prestigious honor of being remembered in the storybooks. Narrator, you have been chosen to determine the fate of your characters - Do you have the skill to guide their plots to be remembered forever?

Storybook Battles is a tactical card game for 2 or 4 players that puts you in command of characters from your favorite stories! Tactically play, maneuver, hide, and reveal cards to capture your opponent’s characters as you create your very own adventure. Be the first player to capture enough plot points and the writers will remember your story forever - you win!

Players are free to play with whatever set of characters they want; they don't have to be from the same book - "Peter Pan Vs. The Queen of Hearts?", no problem! By combining books, you also unlock Expert and Team modes of play. Expert mode includes deck-customization for a serious best of three match. Team mode is a 2 Vs. 2 match for four players to enjoy working cooperatively for a shared victory!

There's so much to explore - which book will you open today!?

—description from the designer
family: Folk Tales & Fairy Tales: Peter Pan

description:
Stratic is a 2 player abstract strategy game, played on regular 8x8x8 hex board, requires 40 pieces in two colors, optionally can use additional tiles placed on the board to change its layout.

Players place 2 pieces on the board in their turn trying to take control over goal fields/tiles. Pieces and goals on board have area of influence preventing placement directly adjacent to them unless placed adjacent to own piece. Pieces are captured when adjacent to 3 or more opponent pieces or if there is no valid path (blocked by opponent area of influence and pieces but also goals) to own base. Base is two pieces stack (or dedicated piece) placed by players on goals and as part of initial setup.

—description from the designer

Dinosaur Dash is a game for the whole family.

Designed with simple mechanics for ages 3+ whilst having a depth of choice in gameplay that engages older players up to age 12. Choose which card to play each turn to move yourself forwards and land on bonus movement spaces.

Play competitive or cooperative mode. In competitive mode you race against the other dinosaurs to reach the finish line first. In cooperative mode you must work together to make sure all players make it to safety before the raptors catch you. This game cultivates strategy, decision making skills, cooperation and mathematical skills.

Blast off for Earth in a lunar-made Valkyrie spacecraft and bring back amazing new technology from the home planet!

This expansion for Moon includes a Valkyrie token for each player. Once per era, you can place your Valkyrie on the launch board, taking a bonus as you do - the earlier you launch, the more choices you have. Then, pay the resource cost to obtain a new structure card from the special market. New cards will be available in each era, including such wonders as Beef Trees, Rover Factory, and Wormhole. These are added to your base, giving you even more opportunities for maximising your engine and constructing the most popular city on the Moon!

Also includes three new Expedition and Reputation cards for the base game.

—description from the publisher

The Rune Forge adventure book introduces a different way of playing D100 Dungeon, and will give you choices much like a choose your own adventure game. It uses all of the D100 Dungeon rules from the main rulebook with a few modifications. Also included are new rules called "Random Dungeons", that can be implemented into any of your games; whether you're playing a quest, campaign, or using the World Builder expansion.

You will need the D100 Dungeonrule book to use this book

THE RUNE FORGE ADVENTURE

Deep in the mountains to the northeast of the Arcane Tower is the Ruin Forge. It was built by the dwarves exactly 100 years after they completed the Arcane Tower. Work began in the year -2489 and took just nine years to complete. The Council of Seven, who commissioned its construction, chose its location beneath the Irontorn mountains, in an effort to hide it away from preying eyes, but alas it fell foul to a powerful demon named Vakanar. Vakanar is one of a handful of demon lords that first emerged from the Demon Gate after its construction in -2790. He craved a huge arcane power and set off on a mission to capture the White Tower. Along the way he summoned a horde of minions, but when he reach the Arcane Tower he learnt of a more powerful structure being built, The Rune Forge. He changed course and waited for its completion. Whilst the dwarves dug, he planned the attack and on the day the Rune Forge was finished, Vakanar and his minions devoured its caverns, spreading torture and fire to its darkest corners. Many dwarves escaped with their lives, but an unlucky few were left behind and were tortured to death to reveal the secret of the nine rune crystals the arcane council placed within its depths. One crystal for each scripture of magic and two more for balancing the arcane power of the forge.

Numerous battles took place to regain its control, but alas the Arcane Council and their battle mages failed to breach its depths. Then, after the Great Icing of -2300 and The Melting of -1400, the location of the Ruin Forge became lost. The Lair of Vakanar (another name for the lost forge) is now where the demon lord and his minions use the power of the nine arcane crystals to sustain and power their existence, preventing them from diminishing back to the demon realm.

You have heard this tale a thousand times, as it's told in every tavern across Terra. Warming your back to the camp fire, you keep watch and recount the story you overheard a few days ago in The Broken Claw, a scruffy little inn at the heart of Blitsworth…

By chance, you have found the entrance to the Rune Forge and must now delve its depths, find the forge and use its power to enchant an item. If you can present the enchanted item to the Council of Seven at the White Tower, it will prove you have found the location of their Lost Forge and secure a handsome reward.

—description from the publisher

The challenging expansion for all fans of the cult game STADT LAND VOLLPFOSTEN.

43 especially challenging categories will make even the smartest minds sweat. Fun guaranteed!

This game promotes knowledge and creativity!

The EXPERT EDITION contains an exciting variety with 43 different categories.

Examples: is made of wood, is smaller than a soccer, can grow, you do every day, is dangerous, a feeling, luxury item, piece of furniture and many more!

Innovative letter bar against cheaters.

Just wipe the letter bar with your index finger. When one of your teammates says "STOP", the letter to play with is fixed.

This XXL game block in A3 format contains 50 sheets. This provides for many extensive game evenings with family and friends.

Perfect to give away or the next vacation.

GAME PROCEDURE:
Each player receives one side of the A3 block and places it in front of them. A time is set for each round (we recommend 2 minutes as a limit). The innovative letter bar helps to determine the letter. Then it is a matter of entering all the terms as quickly as possible. There are 13 terms sought per round.

POINT DISTRIBUTION:

     You can't think of a correct term = 0 points
     At least one other player has the same term as you = 5 points
     No other player has the same term as you = 10 points
     You are the only one who can think of a term in the category = 20 points.


In addition, there are so-called FULL POST fields where you always get double the points.

—description from the publisher (translated)Die anspruchsvolle 
Erweiterung für alle Fans des Kultspiels STADT LAND VOLLPFOSTEN

43 besonders herausfordernde Kategorien bringen auch die klügsten Köpfe ins Schwitzen. Spaß garantiert!

Dieses Spiel fördert Wissen und Kreativität!

Die EXPERTEN EDITION beinhaltet eine spannende Vielfalt mit 43 verschiedenen Kategorien.

Beispiele: besteht aus Holz, ist kleiner als ein Fußball, kann wachsen, macht man täglich, ist gefährlich, ein Gefühl, Luxusgegenstand, Möbelstück und viele mehr!

Innovative Buchstabenleiste gegen Schummler.

Wische einfach mit dem Zeigefinger über die Buchstabenleiste. Wenn einer deiner Mitspieler “STOPP” sagt, steht der Buchstabe fest, mit dem gespielt werden muss.

Dieser XXL-Spielblock im DIN A3 Format beinhaltet 50 Blatt. So ist für viele ausgiebige Spielabende mit Familie und Freunden gesorgt.

Perfekt zum Verschenken oder den nächsten Urlaub.

SPIELABLAUF:
Jeder Spieler erhält eine Seite des DIN-A3 Blockes und legt diese vor sich hin. Es wird eine Zeit pro Durchgang ausgemacht (wir empfehlen 2 Minuten als Begrenzung). Die innovative Buchstabenleiste hilft bei der Ermittlung des Buchstabens. Dann gilt es so schnell wie möglich alle Begriffe einzutragen. Pro Runde sind 13 Begriffe gesucht.
PUNKTEVERTEILUNG:

     Dir fällt kein richtiger Begriff ein = 0 Punkte
     Mindestens ein Mitspieler hat denselben Begriff wie du = 5 Punkte
     Kein Mitspieler hat den denselben Begriff wie du = 10 Punkte
     Dir ist als Einziger ein Begriff in der Kategorie eingefallen = 20 Punkte


Außerdem gibt es sogenannte VOLLPFOSTEN-Felder bei denen man stets die doppelte Punktzahl erhält.

—description from the publisher
