Someone has introduced Cuckoo's into the bird sanctuary!

It's up to you (the players) to rescue as many eggs (cards) as possible before the game ends. The first player to achieve 2 victories (complete sets of egg cards) is the winner. This is a push-your-luck card game.

At the beginning of each game, you will randomly remove several cards from the nest (draw deck). This makes it difficult to count cards (and thus make the game too predictable), but also enhances the push-your-luck aspect of each game turn.

By drawing a 'Multi-Cuckoo' card, you are able to steal up to 2 eggs (cards) from any opponent, but beware that if you draw an opponent's Cuckoo card, they are then able to steal up to 2 of your collected (set) cards! When a player passes for their turn, the other players have the option to force you to draw an extra card. Don't forget the Angry (Bird) Parent card, which makes you drop all the cards that you have hitherto collected (if they are short of a complete set)!

So do you play your turn safe, or do you Push Your Luck?! Whatever you decide, it will drive you Cuckoo!

The cards have been designed with colour-blindness taken into account, showing shape and colour on each set of cards.

Components

Each game comes in a tuck box. In this up to 6 player game, each player picks one of the 6 shaped/coloured decks, which contain:

5 x 1-point cards
3 x 2-point cards
1 x 3-point card
1 x Multi Cuckoo
1 x Angry Parent card
1 x Play + 1 card
4 x Cuckoo’s of your shape / colour
1 x Win(g)dicator

Enhancing the experience, but danger is around the corner

Removal of several cards from the nest at the start means not only is it more difficult to card count, but also enhances the push your luck aspect.

By drawing a Multi Cuckoo card, you are able to steal up to 2 cards from any opponent; but beware if you draw an opponents’ Cuckoo card, they are able to steal up to 2 of your collected cards. When a  player passes, other players have the option to make them draw an extra card. Don’t forget  the Angry Parent card, that makes you drop all cards you collected that go.
 Do you play it safe, or do you push your luck?
Whatever you decide, it will send you Cuckoo!!

boardgame: Hegemony in the Late Han Dynasty
description:
Hegemony in the Late Han Dynasty – The Rise of Heroes is a board game that supports 2-5 players. It includes hand management, campaigns, resource production, region control, and other mechanics.
In the game, the player controls a hero and his camp during the Three Kingdoms period in ancient China. Each player has an initial general and army. Get the most territory, win the most victories, recruit the most generals and the most Fame Points by managing different actions, different administrative measures, using different generals to fight, produce and upgrade through strategy cards

Deadball Junior is a tabletop game for one or two players that introduces children as young as five to the joys of baseball. Based on the bestselling Deadball: Baseball With Dice, Deadball Junior is lightning fast, easy to learn, and adaptable enough for any kid to make their own. Designed for kids to enjoy with the help of an adult—at first!—it does not require reading to play.

Deadball Junior strips baseball to its essence—hits, runs, and outs—and builds from there. Each at-bat is resolved with a single roll of the dice, allowing for half hour games between teams from the present, the past, or the child’s own boundless imagination. After a few games, a child will know the difference between a single and a double, between a double play and a fielder’s choice. They’ll know when to bunt and when to steal, how to cheer when they win and pick themselves up when they lose. Most importantly, they’ll have  a glimpse of how fun baseball can be—and that’s a joy that will last forever.

- Deadball Junior requires only two dice—a d20 and a d6, which you can get online or at your local games store. Aside from the dice, this campaign will give you everything you need to play, including:
- The rules: a basic set for new players and advanced rules like defense and base stealing for kids to add as they master the core game.
Instructions for creating basic and advanced players, with Bonus Traits like “Speedy Runner” or “Power Hitter” that let the stars stand out.
- A guide for adults that includes advice on teaching the game and, when necessary, losing on purpose.
- Five simple rules for kids to follow: “Have fun! Be nice! Make up rules! Winning doesn’t matter! Always say, ‘Good game!’”
- A ten-team international league, the Global Circuit, that features the greatest young players the world has ever seen.
- A beautiful playmat that can be printed in three different sizes.
- All the digital supplements.

Grab the game and start playing today!
family: Versions & Editions: Junior Versions of Grown-Up Games

description:
Your favorite food-based dodgeball card games now have an expansion pack! Take your battle to the next level with all new battle cards and inflatable tortilla shields. Block and load!

This is a shield expansion for Throw Throw Burrito and Throw Throw Avocado. You must own either game to play.

You can now use inflatable Tortilla Shields to block incoming burritos or avocados. Collect cards. Launch new battles. Throw squishy foam burritos or avocados. AND block with tortillas.

Contents:
-2 Inflatable Tortilla Shields
-36 Burrito Battle Cards
-36 Avocado Battle Cards
-Instruction Manual
-2 Repair Patches

A golf dice game with numbered dice. Players take turn rolling the dice to attempt to win by getting the lowest score.

How to play:
Roll all nine dice. Remove the dice with the lowest score(s). Players are required to remove at least one dice, but players are allowed to remove more than one if they wish. Then the player must choose the dice in the order of the hole in the scorebosrd. (Par 3,4, or 5). Repeat the first two steps until all nine dice have been selected. The player then adds up the numbers on all dice to get their nine hole score. Lowest score wins.

In the card game Бавовна, you have to collect a set of cards and create the perfect trap for the occupying tanks by setting up decoys with washing machines and fueling the farmers' tractors to quickly rid the enemy soldiers of their machinery.

How to do it? Play the cards correctly and try to be the first to collect the right combinations of card collections.

boardgame: The Aetherial War Card Game: Chaos and Consequences Core Set
description:
Designed for solo play, or with up to two friends, the Aetherial War Chaos and Consequences Core Set comes with everything you need to adventure through an exciting Sci-Fi Fantasy setting. This Role Playing Card Game gives players all they can handle with quick, tactical battles, and intense boss missions.

Upon completing a campaign, players will be able to open an unlock pack included with the Core Set. Each pack will feature entirely new content, from new Equipment pieces, to new enemy factions, and even new bosses!

Additional expansions will add even more variety to enemies, new playable characters, and new variations on gameplay to keep players coming back for more.

—description from the designer

Galactic Infamy is a competitive, sci-fi themed board game in which players take on the roles of bounty hunters in search of the galaxy's most notorious crime lord.

Fight and capture bounties, then turn them in for rewards of Credits and Infamy. Purchase better equipment and skills to take on more difficult bounties and defend yourself from other players.

The first player to turn in any crime lord triggers the end of the game and the bounty hunter with the most Infamy wins!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Rabbito: A Game for Pushy Rabbits for 2-4 Players
description:
Rabbito is a light strategy carrot-taking board game for 2-4 players.

Each player (on their turn) rolls both dice, places a carrot on the board, from the bag, and then moves the highest dice rolled to push carrots (and other rabbits) down the warren holes which they rake up with their wooden rakes and fill their burrow cards as fast as possible.

The first rabbit to score 7 carrots (for a quick game) or 14 carrots for a full game is the winner. It's a quick, light family game for ages 5 to adult.

On the surface, Rabbito is a simple game of rabbit racing and carrot stealing, but you'll need to use your noggin to plan your best moves, position your rabbit for maximum pushiness and avoid getting shoved down a warren hole and losing your carrots.

-description from designer

Albuera 1811: Beresford vs. Soult depicts the fiercest battle of the Peninsula War. Both engaged armies suffered terrible losses. Players can check if success could be achieved with lower cost.

Rules concentrate on infantry maneuvers on Napoleonic battlefield. It is important to form battalion into proper array in the right time. Intuitively Line gives advantages during musketry fire and Column is powerful in hand-to-hand fighting. Important is also placing an infantry battalion regarding to neighboring units. Flank-to-flank deployment gives farther bonuses in ranged combat and forms solid order of the whole army. An infantry battalion can detach a company of skirmishers. It is useful for screening  battalion's main body and harass enemy units with volleys. The best form of defense against cavalry is Square. In the box are rectangle counters. They represent line with broad front. This simply solution shows British 2-row line historical advantage over French 3-row - rectangle counters occurs more often in allied army.

Cavalry aspect is not so detailed, so cavalry is simply deployed in line formation.

Artillery can move or fire. Of course horse artillery can do both actions during one activation. The biggest advantage is a range of fire.

Command and control is simple. Players have certain Activation Chits and select some of them. Activation Chits of both players are placed in one Activation Pool. Blindly drawn Chit indicates formation which can move and initiate close combat. Melee is very bloody – both sides suffer losses, but the risk must be taken, because won clashes give Victory Points.

Non-activated units do not move, but they are allowed to fire. Thanks to that the whole board is used and crucial events can happen everywhere, not only in activated map sector. The most common effect of fire combat is Morale Check. If unsuccessful, unit can withdraw – decision is up to the opposing player, so both players are involved for the whole gameplay.

Generally random factor is big, but players can feel reality of Napoleonic battlefield. The winner is player who properly deploys his units and uses opportunities given by Fate of War.

Game scale:
One game turn – 15 minutes of real time
One hex – 150 meters
One strength point – 100 infantrymen or cavalrymen
One artillery counter – from 1 to 8 guns

-description from designer

description:
Catro Chip is a strategy game for 2 people. Each player gets the figures, cubes and plates of a color. The figures are placed on the baseline, the rest of the material is placed ready.
From the basic position, 2 fields are pulled with each figure. This basic train can be changed by either putting a cube with a sensible number to the figure or adding a tile that allows a directional dwelling up to a maximum of 90 ° or even uses both. If a figure is on the field on which the train is finished, it is removed from the game. This also applies to your own characters.
You won if the opponent either no longer has any characters or can no longer pull because he has already used up his cubes.

—user summary

The "short game" is half a 18xx and half a Eurogame.
It playable with the components from the base game.
In addition, a small number of special cards, tokens and the rules are needed. 
The game material is available in the files section.

Children can easily learn colours with this funny game. 
Every child has a nice, colorful bear with which they collect some wooden pearls on a track. The child who has collected all 6 pearls at first wins. 
In a second variant, the children need to collect the pearls in the correct order. 
Whereas in the first two variants, the children are playing with a dice with colours, they use a dice with numbers from 1-6 in the third variant.

The game includes a DVD which features 10 quiz tournaments with interesting questions, images, movies and 4 answers to select from. One tournament consists of 6 questions. The player who answers all of them correctly, wins.

Poker Revolver is a semi-abstract game where both players take the role of a gambler-duelist in the Wild West. You are minding your own business at the poker table and someone challenges you to a dicey duel.

Players roll their dice secretly and simultaneously (grabbing their bullets), and then they take turns placing a die one by one (loading) onto the board (in the shape of two revolver cylinders) to see who can make the better poker combination (e.g. a Pair, Straight, Full House). Knockout your opponent before he knocks you out. Only one person walks away as a winner from a poker table.

This game involves a balance of randomness (your initial dice roll) for variability, skill (placement of your dice), as well as bluffing (just like poker).

—description from the designer

Theo Centipedes comes home and doesn't remember where he left all his slippers! 
Every child gets a board with the sleeping Theo and some places where to put his slippers. All cards are shuffled and put face down on the table. Then the players take turns clock-wise. Each player announces, which card he is looking for - eg. the one with 3 blue slippers - and then takes the next card and puts it face up on the table.- If the card matches what the player announced, he gets the card and put it on his board. 
The player who is the first one with all cards on his board wins.

—user summary

boardgame: My Very First Games: The Duck Game
description:
The animals are looking for their hats and the players can help them out. In the cooperative game, players work together to match six hats before the star tiles are turned over. In the competitive game, players try to be the first to uncover and match three hats.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Combat Commander: Battle Pack #8 – Minor Nations
description:
Combat Commander: Battle Pack #8 -- Minor Nations is a reprint of scenarios from the original Combat Commander (Europe and Mediterranean) with counters specific to the minor nations involved.

				
				
					COMPONENTS1-1/2 countersheets
Eight 8.5" x 11" scenario pages
Battle Pack folder
One 9" x 12" ziplock

Includes units and weapons for:

     Brazil
     Canada

     New Zealand

     Yugoslavia


NOTE: CC: Minor Nations is not a complete game and requires ownership of both Combat Commander: Europe and Combat Commander: Mediterranean to play.

Developed by Kai Jensen.

Combat Commander: Europe and Combat Commander: Mediterranean (both required)

Included in:

family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
This Master Edition combines both CC: Europe and CC: Mediterranean into one package and includes the new Minor Nations Battle Pack material as well. This version is how the game was originally conceived and designed.

Combat Commander is a card-driven board game covering tactical infantry combat in the European Theater of World War II. One player takes the role of the Axis forces while another player commands the Allied forces. These two players will take turns playing one or more “Fate” cards from their hands in order to activate his units on the mapboard for various military functions.

Players attempt to achieve victory by moving their combat units across the game map to attack their opponent’s combat units and occupy as many objectives as possible. The degree to which a player succeeds or fails is measured by a scenario’s specific “Objective” chits, the destruction of enemy units, and the exiting of friendly units off the opponent’s board edge.

A game of Combat Commander is divided into several measures of Game Time. There is no sequence of play to follow, however: each Time segment is divided into a variable number of Player Turns, each of which may consist of one or more Fate Card "Orders" conducted by the active player. Fate Card "Actions" may generally be conducted by either player at any time. "Events" — both good and bad — will occur at random intervals to add a bit of chaos and uncertainty to each player’s perfect plan.

SCALE: Each hex of a Combat Commander map is roughly 100 feet of distance (about 30 meters). Each complete Player Phase abstractly represents several seconds of real time. Each complete measure of Game Time abstractly represents several minutes of real time.

Each unit in the game is approximated as either a single Leader, a 5-man Team, or a 10-man Squad. Radios — and individual weapons larger than a pistol, rifle or BAR — are represented by their own counters.

Components:
    6½ countersheets
    One 24-page Rulebook
    One 52-page Playbook
    Eight PAC sheets (6 two-sided nationality cards, one Terrain chart, one misc. play aids chart)
    439 Cards (6 decks of 72 cards each, 1 Fate card, 6 nationality reference cards)
    24 Maps on 12 double-sided 17" x 22" sheets
    One 8½" x 22" Track Display
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
A fast and deadly blend of hide-and-seek and press-your-luck! An asymmetrical, hidden movement game with a post-apocalyptic-punk-rock theme. Up to four Mice earn points scavenging high-value food (day-old sardines, rogue cereal bits) from a colorful apartment with a randomized floor plan.

Stay hidden while you scavenge because one player is the powerful Cat, protecting its hoard and earning points for each Mouse it catches. The Cat draws Traits, such as "Hiss-terical," and "Chonky" to get an edge on the Mice, who each have unique abilities to help them stay hidden, scavenge for scraps and escape with their lives!

Aim of the game is always to build a tower in the order of colors required by a playing card as quickly as possible, either alone or in a team (depending on the mode), and then to be the first to place your hand on the construction acceptance card. The first player to collect 10 points in the form of playing cards wins.

3 different game modes ensure a lot of variety and limitless fun.

Includes 20 building blocks made of beech wood

RoboMon Island Adventure is a prequel story in a more condensed format. You'll take on the role of Gerald, a RoboMon Ranger who has been shipwrecked on an island and has to figure out a way back to the mainland. During your adventure, you'll be able to find and capture over 30 different RoboMon, compete in battles, and solve puzzles to move the story along.

It is a standalone expansion that includes 10-12 hours of gameplay.

—description from the publisher

Players use a combination of personal and shared randomly delt cards with one letter each on them to create a word. 
Other players place bets on which of the shared letters will be used.
Winner is the player with the most money when the bank runs empty

boardgame: エルフの森、燃えるってよ (The Elven Forest is burning)
description:
The king sent out a request throughout the entire island to find a living elf. The reason remained a mystery, but rumour spread that the king, growing old witouth an heir, was looking for the secret of longevity that elves are believed to possess. The entire nation is in uproar, searching for elves, and your land is no safer from that great ambition. Finding an elf and bringing it to the king would make your land a most prosperous one. However, your ambitions prevent you from helping to achieve your king’s goal. You must find the elves somehow and establish a trade relationship with them. Not only the treasures, but also the resources and maybe the secret of their longevity could be a great help in achieving your ambitions.

In this game, players go through a draft sequence to select the Character Card they will be using for a turn. Players pick between one of two abilities off the character card they drafted to help them in their goal of placing down 6 pioneering card to acquire victory points and win the game. Failure will result in burning down the forest and depending on the ending, points will be calculated differently in regards to the cards placed down on the table.

Will you have what it take to play dirty? Or will you play fair to achieve a prosperous ending?
publishers: 深夜二時のドワーフ (Dwarf at 2 a.m.)

description:
Zoom - 4 marbles in a square.
The shortest route is not always the best.
Which player will be the first to make a square of 4 marbles on the inner part of the board? The game is played from the outer edges (starting position) to the inner parts of the board - every marble can be moved as far as the player wishes but only in straight lines. It is important to know that every empty space on the circumference of the board can be used to access the inner part of the board.

TriHop - a tactical triple jump
In this game the player moves from the inner part outwards, to the circumference of the board - every move requires you to jump at least once around a corner.

The implementation of TriHop is exactly the opposite to Zoom. At the start of the game the board is empty and the players alternately position their pieces on the inner part of the board. The player to place the last marble makes the first move and his first triple jump.

The aim is to reach desired spaces with your own marbles first.

Whoever can form a square or rectangle first wins. The goal of the game must be reached by turning a wooden plate. If both players reach the goal in the same turn, there is a draw. At Brändi Quadros, the material impresses. It is played on four wooden boards, each with eight wells in which a wooden ball fits. The wooden panels, in turn, are placed on a felt pad. As simple as "Brändi Quadros" seems to be, after a while your head will start spinning - you don't want to give your opponent a finished rectangle.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: The Royal Limited: Murder On Board
description:
A stormy night. An isolated train traveling through the English countryside. It’s the perfect spot for a heinous crime. But the killer didn’t account for a renowned detective on board…

This 3-card expansion for The Royal Limited adds Detective, Victim, and Murderer cards along with some gameplay changes.

What would a train be without a little murder mystery? In this 3-card expansion, you must find the victim and catch the killer before time runs out or lose the game.


The player’s goal is to, by the end of the game, have the detective find the victim and arrest the murderer. To do this, they will move the detective along the train and land on spaces with the assigned VIP.

In addition to the normal scoring, both the victim and murderer cards must be flipped to green, or else you lose.

Business is booming! Running two lines on parallel tracks allows for maximum profits, as long as there are enough passengers.

Second Line is a 4-card expansion for The Royal Limited that introduces additional train cards in a new color and allows players to operate a second train below their first line.

To play with the Second Line expansion, shuffle all train cards together to begin. Draw one additional card each turn and place cards into either row following the normal rules. Beware: the new grey train cars can only accept passengers that match the number (as well as VIPs). Keep your trains close in length, as you’ll lose automatically if one is too far behind when the game ends.

The player with the highest score by lining up 10 cards with cute characters on them wins! Each character has different abilities, and the key to success in this card game is the game of tactics in deciding who to collect.

You probably have heard Mr. Owl hooting, but have you ever heard him tooting?

Join Mr. Owl in Hoot or Toot, a twist on a traditional memory game in which you need to match his hoot and toot sounds with illustrated tokens. Shake the little owl and listen carefully to the sound he makes, then flip a token on the table to see whether it matches the sound. Remember where each token is to collect them faster! The first player to collect six tokens featuring each color wins.

During the Spanish War of Independence in the early 1800s, Napoleon's army occupied the centuries-old Islamic fortress and palace in Granada: the Alhambra. When the French finally withdrew before advancing Spanish and allied forces, the French commander ordered the destruction of the Alhambra with explosives. But one Spanish soldier, José García, despite having injury and wounds from a previous battle, raced through the 26-acre complex, disarming bombs and saving this amazing piece of history.

José García Saves the Alhambra is a solitaire game commemorating this story. All you need is a regular deck of cards and one six-sided die to play. Download the game for free from the itch.io webpage.

—description from the publisher

You are an Explorer tasked with determining what happened aboard a now-abandoned spaceship. You’ll document what you find while trying to avoid a perilous end.

You will use custom deck of locations to build a completely unique ship with every playthrough. Start with a set of 20 Standard Rooms, which you customise with 10 Non Standard rooms depending on the different combinations of Contracts and Wrecks you can choose to explore.

To be able to explore the Wreck, you'll need to spend a limited resource: Oxygen. Every breath counts, and you'll need to think ahead as you explore. Delve too far, investigate too thoroughly and you might not make back out of the Airlock. However, spend too little time aboard the Wreck, and you risk failing the demands of your contract.

—description from the publisher

かばんにいっぱい ("Through the Woods") is a set collection and shopping game. Everyone becomes a forest animal and prepares for hibernation. During the three seasons of Spring, Summer, and Autumn, travel the path while collecting the blessings of the forest in your bag and shop at the Bear store at the bottom of the forest. Animals who are ready for the most wonderful winter retreat can stay at the Bear's Hotel and ride the super swing.
publishers: Banjo Yugi Seisakujo BGM (盤上遊戯製作所 BGM)

description:
Laniakea: Across Dark Space, is a 1-4 player game. You will take on the role of a Solitary Celestial; exploring the galaxies of Laniakea in order to achieve ultimate ascension. Each new galaxy you discover could hold the resources you need to advance your abilities; some may even be ideal locations for you to build new Sanctums, Gateways or thrones. To aid you on your journey, you will bring life to new beings of your own creation, with powerful special abilities.

At the end of the game, the player with the most victory points will become the most powerful being in Laniakea, and be crowned the winner.

Main Action Phase:

The main action phase is where players will be able to move around the board, gather resources, recruit powerful Entity cards, and build various assets around the board.

Gameplay begins with the start player, proceeding clockwise until the game ends.
During their turn, the player must draw Cosmic Power cards from their deck until they have five displayed in a row in front of them. Players may perform the actions shown across their Cosmic Power card display in any order. Once a Cosmic Power has been used, players should add it to a face up discard pile. A player may perform as many actions as they wish, but they must end their Main Action Phase with at least two Cosmic Power cards still in their display area.

Upkeep Phase:

Once a player has concluded their Main Action Phase, they must then decide whether to keep any Cosmic Power cards they have left on display, (which may be used on their next turn), or discard any number of them. After a player has completed their upkeep phase, the next player begins their turn.

Additional Actions:

During your turn you may perform any number of the following additional actions at any time:

1. If you have any “Instant Action” Entity cards dormant (face-down) on your player board, you may turn them face-up to activate the action on them.

2. If you are currently positioned on a galaxy with a Gateway, you may move to any other Gateway at any time (Though no resources may be harvested).

3. You may place resources from your personal supply onto any available Galaxy resource slots.

-description from designer

You and your fellow players have just stepped into the exciting world of coleopterology: the study of beetles! Travel around the world, assemble your collection of specimens, and present your findings to museums and magazines to become the most recognized expert in your field!

Pins and Beetles is a hybrid set collection and tile placement game. Players collect Beetle cards from eight families and use them to score points, either with Features that depend on the size of their collection, or Exhibits that place tiles in the Museum. Once the Museum is full and no more Exhibits can be played, the game ends and the player with the most points wins!

—description from the publisher

Jiroyu Catch! is the speedy-party game,
The first player who can play a card until there are no cards left in their hand wins.

Each card has different actions. which players must use both sensitivity and consciousness in looking at the situation

Easy to learn, fast to play, quick to finish if it hurts to lose. Start playing again.

Incubate is a number puzzle game.

The player must try to adjust the temperature (number) of the playing card to the value that the target wants.

The player who can reach the target value first And still able to maintain that status Will win

The game allows players to figure out ways to adjust each card's function. along with a special card in order to reach the goal as quickly and stably as possible

Ya Never Know is a trick-taking game for 3-7 players played in 7 rounds. At the start of each round, players bet on the number of tricks they think they will win this round. Then, players take turns playing cards to score tricks. Once all cards have been played, players compare their score to their bet and gain points if they bet correctly.

At the end of the seventh round, the player with the most points win.

-description from artist

No one knows when the giant fruits started growing, but everyone agrees they make a legendary punch. Become the head of one of four factions — orange, lemon, grape, or strawberry — and lead a band of rollicking revelers to victory.

Compete with the other factions to bring the most desired ingredients to three massive punch bowls before they're served. Fish out your opponents' fruits, add droves of your own, and introduce special ingredients to tip the balance. When each punch is served, the winner adds some to their punch cup, along with coveted ice cubes for varying bonuses.

Punch Bowl includes elements of area control, as well as a unique deck-curation mechanism in which players manage an ever-diminishing deck. Outmaneuver your opponents to win eternal glory and an overflowing cup of punch!

Everyone starts the game with the same deck of 12 cards. Each round, draw three cards. Choose one to remove from your deck, one to keep for later, and one to play. At the end of each round, roll the serving die to add a ladle to a punch. The player with the highest value of fruit in their quadrant wins the punch and gets to add fruit to their cup. The game ends when a player runs out of fruit or you can’t draw three cards at the start of the round. The player with the most points wins.

—description from the publisher

Smugglers and Cruisers is a 1930's game where one players plays a smuggler trying to evade the revenue men along the remote Northern Australian coastline. A second player has 5 revenue men to try and surround the Smuggler so he cannot move. If the Smuggler can evade the revenue men and reach one of several caves on the Northern Australian shore he is the winner but if he is trapped by the revenue men then the other player wins the game.

Produced in Australia by the National Game Company in Ballarat and credited as Australia's first large scale manufacturer of board games. The game came in two sizes a larger square box and board and a smaller rectangular box.

Brickyard is a pick up and delivery game for two players, where you run a brick supply company trying to fulfill your customer’s orders for the day. Unfortunately, bricks are in short supply at the Brickyard and your competitor is trying to satisfy their customers’ orders as well. It is a frantic race of getting the most bricks in the short amount of time.

You will have four orders to fulfill for the game. With each turn you are moving your dump trucks around the board to different dig sites. Once there, you will load as many bricks tokens as your dump truck can hold. The first player to fulfill all four orders wins the game. Plan your day well, for your opponent is certainly planning theirs.

Game Overview: Players will have 12 action points (AP) to use for their turn. Players then take turns moving their dump trucks around the board to get to certain dig sites. Here they will load up bricks that  their customers want. Players will take their loads to the unloading zone and place them on their order sheets. Once unloaded, their trucks will go back into the Brickyard to get more bricks.

-description from designer

boardgame: Masters of The Universe: Fields of Eternia The Board Game – Orko
description:
Orko is a fully playable character in the game. His Magic Tricks allow blocking enemy spells for the entire round! Besides, he can also Levitate - so he has the upper hand when we talk about Movement stat.

Each backer who will pledge during the first 48 hours of the campaign will receive Orko Miniature (with game card) for FREE. Tabletop scaled mini designed with the classic toyline series look. Do not worry about our little fellow disappearing after this period.
family: Game: Masters of the Universe – Fields of Eternia

description:
FOOTBALL IN YOUR POCKET
Rollarama World Football comes in a pocket-sized tin, so you can take this kids card game (shootout dice and all) wherever you go.

RECREATE THE EXCITEMENT
Like real football, Rollarama World Football is full of the same drama as the beautiful game, making it the ideal football gift!

GREAT AT HALF-TIME
Rollarama is simple and quick to play – perfect for a ‘flick about’ at breaktime, or as a family board game for kids and adults at home.

—description from the publisher

Coded Tower is a cooperative game that introduces children to the basics of a computer-free programming. Players work together and program the wizard's moves to collect the treasures hidden in the castle tower. Using sequences and algorithms, magic doors and movable rooms, they try to outsmart the ghosts and become coding masters.

The game features double purpose cards (direction of movement, special action), grid movement and action programming. There is a simple variant for young apprentices, as well as a challenging one for advanced spell casters.

—description from the designer

Remate Final is a game of satirical humor about the sale to the public for 2 to 5 players and with games of an approximate duration of 30 to 45 minutes.

The players are dependents and co-workers in a clothing store.

The objective is to finish the game by keeping your job and being the clerk with the most sales.
 To achieve this, they will have to overcome the calamities of this trade, serve the most horrible customers, juggle to finish all the tasks of the day, cooperate or compete against their peers and survive the constant threat of his irritable boss.

boardgame: Tank GP: A Racing Game for One or More Players
description:
Tank GP is a racing game but also a wargame. It is not meant to be serious, realistic or historically accurate! It is meant to be fun, quick to play and have opponents which are challenging to race against but easy to operate. Up to ten different Tanks race against, and fire at, each other across ten different tracks with the ultimate aim of being the first to cross the winning line. Tanks range from strong, slower heavyweights such as the Tiger and the Churchill, to weaker, faster light tanks such as the M3 Stuart.

The game has been designed primarily as a solo game but there is no reason why two or more players cannot race against each other. Indeed, tanks controlled by the game system, used in solitaire play, can be added to a multiplayer game to fill out the race as and when desired.

It is also possible for the player(s) to control more than one Tank. More than one Race can also be run by the same Tanks, on the same or different Tracks, to create a competition.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Marvel: Crisis Protocol – Nick Fury, Sr. & Howling Commandos
description:
Nick Fury, Sr. and the Howling Commandos arrive ready to smash Hydra in this new pack for Marvel: Crisis Protocol! An elite soldier who has fought in theaters across the world, Nick Fury, Sr. is a strategic and tactical mastermind who never rests in his mission to keep monsters at bay. He is joined by the Howling Commandos, an elite unit that has accomplished many great deeds on the field of battle. Operating together as a single miniature, they are ready to take on the most difficult missions regardless of the challenges before them.

Colonel Nicholas Joseph "Nick" Fury is an elite soldier who has fought as a member of the U.S. Army in theaters across the world. Drawing on his vast combat experience, Fury is a strategic and tactical mastermind who never rests in his mission to keep monsters at bay.

The Howling Commandos are an elite operational unit that has accomplished many great deeds on the field of battle. As a part of the U.S. Military, they fought side-by-side with then-Sergeant Nick Fury as an integrated unit of specialist operatives. Known for completing the most difficult missions regardless of the challenges before them, the Howling Commandos are renowned for their unorthodox tactics and range of combat skills.

Contents:
1 Miniature
  ‣ Nick Fury, Sr. & Howling Commandos
1 Scenic Base (50mm)
1 Character Stat Card
2 Team Tactic Cards
3 Tokens

The dynamic expansion of the cult game STADT LAND VOLLPFOSTEN around the theme of sports.

The ultimate game block with 23 creative and fun categories for an unforgettable gaming experience among sports fans. Fun guaranteed!

Sports fun for family & friends

The SPORT EDITION includes an exciting variety with 23 different categories.

Examples: Sport, Tennis Pro, Sports Injury, Unsportsmanlike Conduct, Sports Nutrition, Racer and many more!

Innovative letter bar against cheaters.

Simply swipe your index finger over the letter bar. When one of your teammates says "STOP", the letter to play with is fixed.

This ultimate game block in A4 format contains 50 sheets. This provides for many extensive game evenings with family and friends.

Perfect to give away or the next vacation.

GAME PROCEDURE:
Each player receives one side of the A4 block and places it in front of them. A time is set for each round (we recommend 2 minutes as a limit). The innovative letter bar helps to determine the letter. Then it is a matter of entering all the terms as quickly as possible. There are 13 terms sought per round.

POINT DISTRIBUTION:

     You can't think of a correct term = 0 points
     At least one other player has the same term as you = 5 points
     No other player has the same term as you = 10 points
     You are the only one who can think of a term in the category = 20 points.


In addition, there are so-called FULL POST fields where you always get double the points.

—description from the publisher (translated)

Die dynamische Erweiterung des Kultspiels STADT LAND VOLLPFOSTEN rund ums Thema Sport.

Der ultimative Spielblock mit 23 kreativen und lustigen Kategorien für ein unvergessliches Spielerlebnis unter Sportsfreunden. Spaß garantiert!

Sportspaß für Familie & Freunde

Die SPORT EDITION beinhaltet eine spannende Vielfalt mit 23 verschiedenen Kategorien.

Beispiele: Sportart, Tennisprofi, Sportverletzung, unsportliches Verhalten, Sportnahrung, Rennfahrer und viele mehr!

Innovative Buchstabenleiste gegen Schummler.

Wische einfach mit dem Zeigefinger über die Buchstabenleiste. Wenn einer deiner Mitspieler “STOPP” sagt, steht der Buchstabe fest, mit dem gespielt werden muss.

Dieser ultimative Spielblock im DIN A4 Format beinhaltet 50 Blatt. So ist für viele ausgiebige Spielabende mit Familie und Freunden gesorgt.

Perfekt zum Verschenken oder den nächsten Urlaub.

SPIELABLAUF:
Jeder Spieler erhält eine Seite des DIN-A4 Blockes und legt diese vor sich hin. Es wird eine Zeit pro Durchgang ausgemacht (wir empfehlen 2 Minuten als Begrenzung). Die innovative Buchstabenleiste hilft bei der Ermittlung des Buchstabens. Dann gilt es so schnell wie möglich alle Begriffe einzutragen. Pro Runde sind 13 Begriffe gesucht.
PUNKTEVERTEILUNG:

     Dir fällt kein richtiger Begriff ein = 0 Punkte
     Mindestens ein Mitspieler hat denselben Begriff wie du = 5 Punkte
     Kein Mitspieler hat den denselben Begriff wie du = 10 Punkte
     Dir ist als Einziger ein Begriff in der Kategorie eingefallen = 20 Punkte


Außerdem gibt es sogenannte VOLLPFOSTEN-Felder bei denen man stets die doppelte Punktzahl erhält.

—description from the publisher

The famous STADT LAND VOLLPFOSTEN - now in English!


     Play block in A3 format in English.
     This XXL block is probably the most versatile city-country river of all time. (ENGLISH LANGUAGE]
     Do you know a soccer player with M? Or a spice with G?
     Do you know a musician with B and quickly think of a vegetable with F?
     52 fun categories will make any amateur thinker sweat. Fun guaranteed!

Das berühmte STADT LAND VOLLPFOSTEN – jetzt in englischer Sprache!


     Spielblock im DIN A3-Format auf englisch
     Dieser XXL-Block ist das wahrscheinlich vielseitigste Stadt-Land-Fluss aller Zeiten. (ENGLISCHE SPRACHE)
     Kennst du einen Fußballspieler mit M? Oder ein Gewürz mit G?
     Kennst du einen Musiker mit B und fällt dir schnell eine Gemüsesorte mit F ein?

Der ultimative Spielblock mit 29 kreativen und lustigen Kategorien für einen unvergesslichen Urlaub. Spaß garantiert!

Dieses Spiel fördert Wissen und Kreativität!

Die CHRISTMAS EDITION beinhaltet eine spannende Vielfalt mit 29 verschiedenen Kategorien.

Außerdem gibt es sogenannte VOLLPFOSTEN-Felder bei denen man stets die doppelte Punktzahl erhält.

—description from the publisher

Tipi is a boardgames for kids. The player have 5 indians that he should hide under some plastic teepes. An indian can be hidden only if the teepee is empty. The player has to remember which teepees are empty or full. A wheel will determined how many indians should be hidden in a turn (from 1 to 3) or if all teepees should be mixed.
The payer has to announced if a teepee if empty or not before putting his indian below. If it's empty and the player was right, the indian is hidden. If it's full and the player was right, the indian that was here is expulsed and the player can put his own. 
The first player who hides all his indians wins.

Use the elastic cord to help the Hedgehogs pass safely through the Hedgehog Highway!

Be the first person to get all your Hedgehogs to the other side.

A hectic but fun game requiring precision, skill and a little bit of luck.

Giggles GUARANTEED!

Fail to Grow is a fast and fun game about the many ways life can trigger our fixed mindset, and how we can recognize those moments and respond to them with a growth mindset.

The game is for 4 players who will play cards to build a fixed mindset.

Each player has a hand of cards that are played to develop a fixed mindset by playing Fixed Mindset cards on themselves while playing Growth mindset habits on their opponents.

The winner is the player with the largest total of fixed mindset points.  The game is designed to understand fixed and growth mindsets to see what habits encourage both.

Component Details:
1 game board, 4 story boards, 71 story cards, 14 wild cards, 1 large custom 6-sided die, 1 score pad, 2 pencils, 1 game play instructions pamphlet

On The Spot is the hilarious party game to hot seat your friends AND FAMILY. I mean you are absolutely grilling each player with no remorse for a full 2 minutes.

You get to ask things you would never know. You will finish this game closer to every single player.

Exactly like On The Spot Original, but rated PG instead of R.

The game is simple.

The player with the closest birthday get the red 'You Are On The Spot' first.

Everyone takes 5 cards from the stack.

Each round:

Start the 2 minute timer.

Begin grilling the player 'On The Spot'.

Moving clockwise, players ask 1 question at a time.

Discard the used card in front of the player 'On The Spot'.

Pick up a new card, so you always have 5.

Once time is up, the 'You Are On The Spot' card moves clockwise.
Rinse and repeat.

Optional play to win: Save all the cards you answer. At the end, count your cards. Most questions answered wins!

NOTE I: You are allowed 1 skip no-questions-asked each round. Group decides consequences of skipping more than 1.

NOTE II: Group is allowed to ask for 3 "story times" each round so player 'On The Spot' can further explain their answer. Pause the timer by laying it on its side during story times.

—description from the designer

Enjoy a new taste of Bitoku with Bitoku: Resutoran, a three-module expansion that you can add to the base game as you prefer. Do you want the full menu? Add all of them! Do you want to try a bite? Add just one, and enjoy the banquet. Itadakimasu!

Modules include new Iwakura rocks, new Yokai cards, and three new types of Kodama Lake. Also, Resutoran includes a new deck of cards that will allow players to activate certain effects during the game, such as modifying dice results (whether their own and opponents') or gaining a lot of points.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

boardgame: 1940: The Last Days of May -- A Panzer Grenadier Campaign Study
description:
1940: The Last Days of May is a Panzer Grenadier Campaign Study. with 11 scenarios from the final days of the German invasion of France, in two chapters, each with a battle game to link the scenarios together. You’ll need Panzer Grenadier: 1940 The Fall of France and Road to Dunkirk to play all of the scenarios.

The scenarios come from the updating of 1940: The Fall of France to the fourth edition Panzer Grenadier rules.  There wasn't enough room in the new playbook format for all of the scenarios, so the extra scenarios became this Campaign Study.

-description from publisher's website
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
A copy of wooden components and cards included in copies sent to backers of the Kickstarter (and sold after).

Includes a wooden active player token, 25 wooden leaf tokens, 75 wooden soil tokens, and a 12 card calendar pack that adds 24 game modifiers (cards are double-sided).

Promo Cards: During setup, one card is placed faceup next to Fauna board. If affects all players, for the entire game.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Brain Train is an educational math game where players use math and dice to defeat or hold off an oncoming zombie horde. Player characters add special abilities and allow up to 4 players to join in a cooperative fight.

Each turn, players will roll 3 of their 4 six-sided dice. The fourth represents the player's life total. Using addition, subtraction, multiplication and division, the player will try to match target numbers on the zombie cards in order to stop or defeat the zombies. Players must use all 3 dice each turn. At the end of the turn, the remaining zombie cards move closer and new zombies join behind them. Players beware, if a zombie card moves close enough to attack you will lose one life point from your life die.

The game ends when the player has received 6 zombie bites or when the players have defeated the boss zombie.

-description from designer

The spring-fresh edition of the cult game STADT LAND VOLLPFOSTEN around the theme of bunnies and eggs.

The ultimate game block with 26 creative and fun categories for an unforgettable Easter with family and friends.

This game promotes knowledge and creativity!

The EASTER EDITION includes an exciting variety with 26 different categories.

Examples: something green, color for colorful eggs, typical spring, on the picnic blanket and many more!

Innovative letter bar against cheaters.

Simply swipe your index finger across the letter bar. When one of your teammates says "STOP", the letter to play with is fixed.

This ultimate game block in A4 format contains 50 sheets. This provides for many extensive game evenings with family and friends.

GAME PROCEDURE:
Each player receives one side of the A3 block and places it in front of them. A time is set for each round (we recommend 2 minutes as a limit). The innovative letter bar helps to determine the letter. Then it is a matter of entering all the terms as quickly as possible. There are 13 terms sought per round.

POINT DISTRIBUTION:
    - You can't think of a correct term = 0 points
    - At least one other player has the same term as you = 5 points
    - No other player has the same term as you = 10 points
    - You are the only one who can think of a term in the category = 20 points.
In addition, there are so-called FULL POST fields where you always get double the points.

—description from the publisher (translated)

Die frühlingsfrische Edition des Kultspiels STADT LAND VOLLPFOSTEN rund ums Thema Hasen und Eier.

Der ultimative Spielblock mit 26 kreativen und lustigen Kategorien für ein unvergessliches Osterfest mit Familie und Freunde.

Dieses Spiel fördert Wissen und Kreativität!

Die OSTER EDITION beinhaltet eine spannende Vielfalt mit 26 verschiedenen Kategorien.

Beispiele: etwas Grünes, Farbe für bunte Eier, typisch Frühling, auf der Picknickdecke und viele mehr!

Innovative Buchstabenleiste gegen Schummler.

Wische einfach mit dem Zeigefinger über die Buchstabenleiste. Wenn einer deiner Mitspieler “STOPP” sagt, steht der Buchstabe fest, mit dem gespielt werden muss.

Dieser ultimative Spielblock im DIN A4 Format beinhaltet 50 Blatt. So ist für viele ausgiebige Spielabende mit Familie und Freunden gesorgt.

Perfekt zum Verschenken.

SPIELABLAUF:
Jeder Spieler erhält eine Seite des DIN-A3 Blockes und legt diese vor sich hin. Es wird eine Zeit pro Durchgang ausgemacht (wir empfehlen 2 Minuten als Begrenzung). Die innovative Buchstabenleiste hilft bei der Ermittlung des Buchstabens. Dann gilt es so schnell wie möglich alle Begriffe einzutragen. Pro Runde sind 13 Begriffe gesucht.
PUNKTEVERTEILUNG:
    • Dir fällt kein richtiger Begriff ein = 0 Punkte
    • Mindestens ein Mitspieler hat denselben Begriff wie du = 5 Punkte
    • Kein Mitspieler hat den denselben Begriff wie du = 10 Punkte
    • Dir ist als Einziger ein Begriff in der Kategorie eingefallen = 20 Punkte
Außerdem gibt es sogenannte VOLLPFOSTEN-Felder bei denen man stets die doppelte Punktzahl erhält.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Stadt Land Vollpfosten: Das Kartenspiel – Junior Edition
description:
High quality hinged lid box in cool design made by DENKRIESEN.

The principle is as simple as it is ingenious. Whoever thinks that Stadt-Land-Vollpfosten can't work well as a card game will soon be proven wrong.

The game is explained in no time and the fun can begin. There are no open questions and the game rounds are entertaining and exciting. Due to the 200 different categories, there is always enough variety.

The child-friendly card game from STADT LAND VOLLPFOSTEN with 120 playing cards for an unforgettable gaming experience among friends.

200 creative and funny categories make every hobby thinker sweat. Fun guaranteed!

Due to the 3 different editions, a huge variety is offered. In addition to the family-friendly Classic Edition, the child-friendly Junior and the naughty Red Light Edition bring a certain variety to the game night!

How to play: Each player is randomly dealt 8 letter cards. Now each player must place a word matching one of his letter cards as quickly as possible. Important: The first letter of the word counts and you do NOT have to form a term from the letter cards, as is sometimes the case in other games, e.g. Scrabble.

So it gets exciting: The 60 category cards contain a total of 200 different and funny categories:

Who can put down a "profession" first? Or a "reason for separation"? What about a "city abroad"? Can you quickly think of a "Famous Dead Person" or a "Famous Actor" for your letter cards?

Important now: The FASTEST player wins each round and at the end the player who first got rid of all letter cards wins the game. The 10 additional action cards make the game especially exciting.

Of course, there are also FULL FIX cards in the game that either act as jokers or are used as tricky counters.

—description from the publisher (translated)

Hochwertige Klappdeckel-Schachtel in coolem Design made by DENKRIESEN.

Das Prinzip ist so einfach wie genial. Wer glaubt, dass Stadt-Land-Vollpfosten als Kartenspiel doch nicht gut funktionieren kann wird schnell eines besseren belehrt werden.

Das Spiel ist im nu erklärt und der Spielspaß kann beginnen. Es bleiben keine offenen Fragen und die Spielrunden sind kurzweilig und spannend. Durch die 200 verschiedenen Kategorien ist immer für genug Abwechslung gesorgt.

Das kinderfreundliche Kartenspiel von STADT LAND VOLLPFOSTEN mit 120 Spielkarten für ein unvergessliches Spielerlebnis unter Freunden.

200 kreative und lustige Kategorien bringen jeden Hobbydenker ins Schwitzen. Spaß Garantiert!

Durch die 3 verschiedenen Editionen, ist eine riesige Vielfalt geboten. Neben der familienfreundlichen Classic Edition bringen die kinderfreundliche Junior und die unanständige Rotlicht Edition die gewisse Abwechslung in den Spieleabend!

Spielablauf:
So wird gespielt: Jeder Spieler bekommt per Zufallsprinzip 8 Buchstaben-Karten ausgehändigt. Nun muss jeder Spieler möglichst schnell einen Begriff passend zu einer seiner Buchstaben-Karten ablegen. Wichtig: Es zählt jeweils der Anfangsbuchstabe des Wortes und man muss NICHT aus den Buchstaben-Karten einen Begriff bilden, wie es in anderen Spielen z.B: Scrabble manchmal der Fall ist.
Es wird also spannend: Die 60 Kategorie-Karten enthalten insgesamt 200 verschiedene und lustige Kategorien:
Wer kann zuerst einen “Beruf” ablegen? Oder einen “Trennungsgrund”? Was ist mit einer “Stadt im Ausland”? Fällt dir zu deinen Buchstaben-Karten schnell ein “Berühmter Toter” ein bzw. kommt Dir sofort ein “bekannter Schauspieler” in den Sinn?
Wichtig nun: Der SCHNELLSTE Spieler gewinnt jeweils die Runde und am Ende gewinnt der Spieler das Spiel, der zuerst alle Buchstaben-Karten losgeworden ist. Durch die 10 zusätzlichen Aktionskarten wird das Spiel besonders spannend.
Natürlich gibt es auch VOLLPFOSTEN-Karten im Spiel die entweder als Joker wirken oder als kniffliger Konter genutzt werden.

—description from the publisher
family: Versions & Editions: Card Versions of Non-Card Games
Versions & Editions: Junior Versions of Grown-Up Games

boardgame: Stadt Land Vollpfosten: Das Kartenspiel – Woozle Goozle Edition

family: Versions & Editions: Card Versions of Non-Card Games

description:
Hope, Chronicles of Osmae is an immersive and narrative Dungeon Crawler set in a dark fantasy universe. 
Leader of one of the 4 ruling Houses, you are fleeing your homeland, facing a terrible threat. The only haven available is Osmae, the island of your Goddess Aya, an unknown land where you will set up camp.

This game offers an alternation of :

    Cooperative exploration phases of "Dungeon crawler" type, with miniatures, strategies, fights & quests. Players join forces to overcome the dangers of Osmae and level up their characters. 
    Competitive camp phases with worker placement mechanics, where players compete and seek to take power while preserving their interests and increasing the influence of their House.


—description from the publisher

Educational game developed on the basis of the principles of Montessori pedagogy.

A rich game to free the imagination! Create fantastic stories with cards, shaped elements and the exclusive composite!

A game to stimulate imagination and creativity with the magical scenery of the castle and all its 3D characters to move around the environment.

The child will have fun inventing many stories set in a fantastic climate by making all the characters interact.

With this game the child develops his creativity and critical thinking, 2 of the Life Skills recognized by the World Health Organization.

Ani Bee, Ani Mee is a fun cooperative memory game. You are all in a band with the same goal: to sing your best at a farm festival! Your task is to recreate the sounds of 9 animals, in the order they appear on stage. This must be done before the melody points end. Get ready to make noise, and be careful not to make mistakes.

Ani Bee, Ani Mee to zabawna kooperacyjna gra pamięciowa.
Działacie jako zespół, w którym wszyscy mają ten sam cel: zaśpiewać jak najlepiej na festiwalu farmy! Waszym zadaniem jest odtworzenie 9 odgłosów zwierząt w kolejności, w jakiej pojawiają się na scenie. Trzeba to zrobić, zanim skończą się punkty melodyczne. Przygotujcie się na odtwarzanie dźwięków, ale uważajcie, by nie popełniać błędów.

One day in the 21st century the city was destroyed and isolated by a zombie attack. The last nine, who barely survived, must now try to survive on the remaining resources. Of course, things may not go amicably...

Players roll dice to move around the city, enter locations to search for items in the form of cards and use these cards to battle other players and wandering zombies. Players can also raid other player's home bases in the form of their start locations. The last player standing is the winner.

One Page Expedition is a print at home roll and write game for one to four players where you take a role of an explorer in untouched lands. Your job is to delve into new continents and explore them to the best of your abilities. During each of your turns you will visit parts of geographical regions all over the map. Each region you research will give you points depending on the progress of your exploration.

Players use only one common game sheet to play. Game has very simple but appealing mechanic. Just roll two dice, pick two sectors and make your camps.

—description from the designer

Lista, Lista Przebojów is party game full of all-time hits!
Make your teams, choose your captain and guess hits of more than 170 artists!

Draw 9 category cards, take 3 of them, give the rest to second's team captain, who chooses also 3. Discard the rest.
Then place a card on 1 of 3 tasks: describe an artist by one word, quote a lyric, play on kazoo (or sing mormorando) a hit - your team has to guess an artist. And you have only 30 seconds for all of this! Who'll guess 8 of 9 categories - wins.

Nine categories, e.g.: '60, '70, '80, '90, 21st century, current hits.

Retro style graphics, lot of fun, a lot of memorable emotions! Play, sing, guess, have fun!

boardgame: Ghost in the Attic Cooperative Game
description:
It’s lights out for this unique board game that you play in the dark! Featuring a glow-in-the-dark game board and light-up ghost mover, players work together in this cooperative game to escape the haunted manor and lock the ghost inside. This one-of-a-kind game is sure to become a family game night favorite!

Players learn strategy, shared decision-making and cooperation

—description from the publisher

What is the theme and goal of the game?

Fancy catching flies? This card game is for you!
Be the best serial fly catcher getting less number of flies at the end of the game. 
Heaps of flies make you crazy but luckily action cards, means Frustrated cards, may upturn the game at any game. This is a game of strategy, but with some luck involved.

About the cards and the game-play

It is a game for +8YO and for 2-4 players.
Including 54 blue point cards (the flies) and 43 orange game cards.

At the beginning of the game, you deal 5 blue point cards and 4 orange game cards. You need to always have 4 game cards in your hand. Flies (blue points cards) move from a deck to another player deck.

Depends on game cards you play, you decrease your own points (flies) and/or increase points of the other players.


     If your card is marked with a specific symbol, you can play a pair to use them simultaneously, which doubles the flies given and decreases yours accordingly.


You can given points to the player of your choice if you play a game card with a star on it. 

     The "Frightened Fly" card gives flies to everyone unless you've got an action card like "Block" card or "Back To You" card. 
     The "Serial Fly Catcher" card decreases your number of flies. 
     The "Swap" card allows you to exchange your "points cards or seat!
     The "Skip" card means the next player to your left misses a turn.
     The "Draws" card allows the players playing it to take a card from any players hand. 


The end of the game

The game immediately ends when the first player has no game cards left. Each player count their point cards to know their final score. Caution : +30 points per "Frustrated cards", action cards, remaining at the end of the game!
The player with least number of flies wins the game and is the Serial Fly catcher.

This FFF card game is created by a family for families. The design and quick stories on each card make the game unique!

—description from the designer
An expansion to transform your one-session party game to a 10-run campaign.

A relatively simple 12-turn point-to-point (sudden death) campaign game about General George Patton's attempt to fight through France's Lorraine and break through the fortified Siegfried Line behind the Saar River before Winter 1944.
If his player scores big successes at the start, his fuel may *not* be diverted to Field Marshal Montgomery's (over-, it turned out) ambitious Market Garden paradrop north into Holland.
Abstract regimental/brigade level 27 US pieces, 18 German, and 6 (German) Fogs of War.  (For solitaire play, the German infantrie and FOW pieces can be turned upside down and randomly mixed.)  Optional stand-up pieces for German infantrie and FOW pieces.
50% hit probabilities of Disruption possibly culminatig in elimination.  3 grades of road.  Bonus to US infantry for prepared, artillery supported attacks.
Free to print and play on CoatneyHistory.
Mapboard compatible with classic Champer & Sackson game Major Battles and Campaigns of General George S. Patton (1973), but PLL has a better movement and combat system, including risking No Retreat! orders.

—description from the designer
