boardgame: The Elder Scrolls: Betrayal of the Second Era – Valenwood
description: The Valenwood expansion brings the home of the Bosmer into The Elder Scrolls: Betrayal of the Second Era. Featuring a new map, new enemies, new equipment, and a new set of quests, encounters, and delves, this expansion is packed with elements that can be used in this new region and throughout your games as a whole. Plus, grow your character building options with the new Security and Illusion skill lines and the Arcanist and Burglar classes.
family: Video Game Theme: The Elder Scrolls

description:
Assemble the Loki’s and build your deck with multiple versions of the Trickster God! This 55-card set not only introduces the various Variants of Loki, but also their adversaries!

Both Loki and the TVA are following the events of the Sacred Timeline closely, but there’s only one who knows all that is, was, and will be.

Look forward to understanding the truths at the end of everything, all found in Vs. System 2PCG: Marvel Studios’ Loki!

—description from publisher

Stack matching monster cards in order to create sequences of the same color or pattern and win monster tokens. A winning sequence is 3 cards aligned horizontally, vertically, or diagonally. 
Be the first to collect 5 monster tokens and win the game!

Introducing our newest 200-card Giant-Sized Starter! House of X is the first issue of the three-part Heroes of Krakoa Story Arc. Professor Charles Xavier reveals his master plan to build a nation for mutants on the living island of Krakoa, beyond the reach of any human leaders who would dare threaten mutantkind!

—description from publisher

boardgame: Roll Camera!: Murder Mystery Story Pack
description:
GASP! The screenwriter of your murder mystery movie has been...MURDERED! Even worse, they were keeping the ending to their whodunnit script a secret until shooting began. Now you don't know who the fictional culprit in your movie is supposed to be! And all the characters are suspects...

You'll just have to figure it out as you go. Plant clues in the scenes while shooting, and you'll probably narrow it down by the end. Right?? Roll Camera!

Roll Camera! Story Packs give a little thematic cinematic boost to your madcap movie-making, with all-new components and a small rules twist. The Murder Mystery Story Pack brings a new dimension to the Set grid, where careful camera placement determines which Suspects you can rule out as your movie's culprit. This pack includes 10 whodunnit-themed Scene cards, 3 new Ideas, 3 new Problems, 1 new Equipment card (all backwards compatible with the base game) as well as 15 Suspect cards and a punchboard with new Backdrop strips and a new main board action.

On top of all that, there are intriguing clues scattered among the Scene cards, creating a devilish puzzle which, when solved, will reveal the ACTUAL killer of your movie's screenwriter...!

The Murder Mystery Story Pack can be played as an isolated module, replacing certain main game components for a highly thematic one-off game, or simply mixed in with the rest of the game and any previous expansions.

-description from designer

boardgame: Freakz in the Sheetz: The Freaky Fryday Pack!
description:
From back of title card

New Pack, New Rulez, Freakz!

We've added NEW memez and captionz to our Freakz in the Sheetz menu! Play by REVERSE rulez!

Finally play a trivia game that’s all Australian based trivia! Perfect for Australia Day festivities, holidays, camping and fans of the land down under. The first player to complete their boxing kangaroo image wins – find out who the ‘bloody know it all’ is! Watch out for steal cards, someone might pinch a bit of your roo!

In the pristine little neighborhood of Gnome Crossing, the competition for the highly coveted award of “Yard of the Month” is fierce. The residents will stop at nothing to win, including sabotage. While some gnomes will beautify and protect a garden, some gnomes will curse it, resulting in quite the eyesore.

Will you be the first to fill your garden with 10 flowers and be crowned “Yard of the Month,” or will you be the laughing stock of the neighborhood?

Yard of the Month is a family-friendly card game for 2 - 6 players, ages 8 and up. In the game, each player (neighbor) is competing to have the best flower garden. You have access to resources to either help or hinder a garden, including the garden gnomes Gnick and Gnack. While Gnick will keep your garden safe, Gnack's goal is to curse it, but that isn't anything a hammer can't handle.

Originally released in 2016, Yard of the Month (Revised edition) offers new art, revised rules and new cards to enhance gameplay.

—description from the designer

Random Quest is a d6 pen and paper tabletop fantasy adventure game for solo and co-op play. Slays monsters, overcome challenges, find treasure, and gain experience points.

Explore a randomly-generated dungeon and defeat the monsters that inhabit it. Level up your player character and attempt to overcome increasingly difficult encounters.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Rolling Realms: Biddy & Walter Promo Pack
description:
Expand your copy of Rolling Realms with this set of 6 promo cards, inspired by cats and their cravings.
To play this realm, you must be prepared for your cats’ cravings. At the start of every turn, your cats will want to eat, play, or sleep. Make sure you are ready to serve your cat, or you will end up with grumpy cats and no stars!

—description from the publisher

Expand your copy of Rolling Realms with this set of 6 promo cards, inspired by Rolling Realms’s Minigolf solitaire mode and actual minigolf.
To play this realm, you assign your dice to 3 minigolf lanes. You’ll want to hit your golf ball hard enough to complete a lane in just a few strokes. But be careful, you can’t hit the ball too hard. Use your dice wisely to complete all 3 lanes.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Rolling Realms: The Isle Of Cats Promo Pack
description:
Expand your copy of Rolling Realms with this set of 6 promo cards, inspired by The Isle Of Cats.
To play this realm, you must use your dice to either fill boxes, or to uncover treasure maps. Treasure maps will give you resources, but to complete this realm you must make clever combinations of the boxes you fill and the treasure maps you uncover.

—description from the publisher

Celebrating over 10 years of Alien Frontiers, Alien Frontiers Edition X offers the quintessential experience of this beloved dice-placement game.

An alien frontier awaits the brave and daring! This new planet will be harsh, but if you have the skills to manage your resources, build a fleet, research alien technology, and settle colonies, the world can be yours!

Alien Frontiers is a strategic board game of resource management and planetary development for two to four players. During the game you will exploit orbital facilities and alien technology, building colony domes in strategic locations to control territory in the newly discovered world.

With new expansion content, you can explore new strategies and outwit your opponents in this colony quest. Upgrade your playstyle and take the challenge of this outer space strategy game.

Includes the new Data Core Expansion, Factions, Agendas, Outer Belt, and additional expansion pack material to elevate the gameplay thrill and strategy.
family: Versions & Editions: Big Box Versions of Individual Games

description:
The year is 2169 and mankind has pretty much filled up the Earth. It has been 200 years since man first set foot on the Moon. Now there are cities on the Moon and Mars, and mining communities in the asteroid belt. But our Solar System is just the beginning. It might be home, but Earth is the only truly hospitable planet nearby. However, recent breakthroughs in warp travel mean that interstellar voyages are finally a reality.

There are several extrasolar systems that have been discovered to have planets and moons which appear to be very Earthlike, and likely hospitable. The nearest of these is in the Gamma Leporis tri-star system, 29 light years away from Earth. A series of moons orbiting a gas giant named Yutu show breathable atmospheres, tolerable radiation levels, reasonable temperatures, liquid water, and plenty of land. Now, the race is on. Terran, Lunar, Martian, and Belter organizations are competing to be the first to send humans to one of these habitable moons and establish a permanent human colony. It will take research, determination, and a bit of luck to be the first to arrive. Do you have what it takes to drive the next great space triumph and win the Race to the Moons?

Overview:
Race to the Moons is a worker placement game where 2-5 players (solo variant included) are working to research the technology needed to complete an interstellar mission and be the first to found a human colony on a moon system around a gas planet in another solar system. Players must research technologies that affect different parts of the mission: Journey, Arrival & Landing, and Colonization, as well as Training their crew. Players must also work on building rockets, building facilities (research labs, factories, launch stations, and more), managing money, and improving public opinion. Players race to have their rockets arrive at the destination and set up a successful colony to earn enough points to beat the competition.

—description from the designer

Exploding Kittens is a highly-strategic, kitty powered version of Russian Roulette. Players draw cards until somebody draws an Exploding Kitten, at which point they explode and are out of the game. To avoid exploding, they can defuse the kitten with a laser pointer or catnip sandwich, OR use powerful action cards to move or avoid the Exploding Kitten. In this edition, you can also use the squishy Cat Burglar figure to steal cards from opponents. But be careful! Being a great burglar also makes you a great target!

Cat Burglar is not compatible with non-English Versions of Exploding Kittens and Expansion Packs.

1904. The Japanese have invested in the city of Port Arthur, a crucial naval base for the Russians. The city is heavily defended by redoubts and forts. The Russians are confident that the Czar will be proud of their defense, despite the continued bickering among their generals. The Japanese have 100,000 men for the siege, but soon realize taking the city will take more than just superior numbers. The Emperor will accept no more delays. The stage is set.

One player commands the Russian forces and the other player commands the Japanese army.

—description from the publisher

The Siege of Port Arthur is a low-to-moderate complexity tactical game on the Japanese siege of Russian Port Arthur during the Russo-Japanese War
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players
Series: Battle Series of World Battles (Historical Game Company)

description:
1837. Cristino commanders plan an ambitious three-pronged attack to annihilate Carlist forces in the North of Spain and hopefully bring an end to the war. However, the planned concentration of forces goes awry. Carlist General Don Sebastian takes advantage of the situation with a lightning march to reinforce a Carlist force threatened bu a Cristino army of Spanish and British auxiliaries near Oriamendi. The stage is set.

—description from the publisher

The Battle of Oriamendi is a low-to-moderate complexity tactical game on the pivotal battle during the First Carlist War in Spain.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players
Series: Battle Series of World Battles (Historical Game Company)

description:
The city's rogues and thieves prepare for the most famous tournament in the country. The thieves are already meeting in the capital and waiting for the signal to start. The first to get a treasure from the Royal castle wins.

A game for 2 to 5 players where speed of dice rolls and cunning are all it takes to win.

Goal: Be the first rogue to get a treasure by gathering 3 cards of the same treasure.

Time is running out, an evil witch plans to curse your village! The villagers must figure out who is the Witch and capture her before it is too late, but can they figure out which craft is witchcraft?

Which Craft is a unique hidden role card game for 4 to 7 players, lasting from 10 to 30 minutes. Unlike many hidden role games, Which Craft does not have ‘nighttime’ mechanics. Throughout the game you will be able to keep an eye on your opponents, and narrow down the possible suspects. Additionally there is no player elimination!

Players take on the identities of various villagers, the Witch or her mischievous minnion the Black Cat. The villagers must collect items related to their Craft so that they can prove themselves innocent and try to figure out who the Witch, while the Witch secretly gathers potions to unleash her curse upon the village!

It was a routine mission back in time, but everything has gone terribly wrong.  While searching the abandoned mansion of a wealthy Parisian, you find yourselves captured by an angry mob who have mistaken you as supporters of King Louis XVI and Marie Antoinette.  Now, you have been locked up in a shabby prison cell with a view overlooking the dreaded Guillotine.  In just over an hour, you are scheduled to be executed before a massive crowd.  It is up to you to find a way out of your prison cell, to blend into the crowd of Revolutionaries, and to rescue your fellow agents before it is too late and they face their fate at the Guillotine.  Pulling off an escape like this will test the nerve of even the most experienced escape artists.  Whatever you do… don’t lose your head.

About Escape the Crate escape room boxes:
Each box has over a dozen unique puzzles which all fit the theme. Every item in the box has a purpose and will help you escape in time. Clue are hidden everywhere and in this game you need to think inside the box. Each box is a complete story.

These missions can be challenging and it is very possible you won't escape before the time limit. You can always reset the game using our instructions or you can continue playing past the time limit to see the mission to completion.
Every challenge you will face will have a set of hints, which you can choose to reveal when your team needs them. If you are completely stumped, even after the hints, the solution is also provided.

Note: You will need a fully charged computer or mobile device to play this escape room.

This is a fun family game, for adults and children of the house, which reproduces the day to day life of the city of Soria. Players will have fun going through the streets and trying to acquire establishments and companies that are part of Soria and its daily life. The game of Soria puts the players in contact with the main streets and the most outstanding monuments of the city.

A card game as simple as it is fun set, as you may have already guessed, in the world of fishing.

The two or more players will draw to create their starting deck from the equipment deck, i.e. boat, rod, reel and hook, and the totality of points determined by the equipment will affect which and how many fish can be caught. In the course of the game it will be possible to improve one's equipment in rounds where variables such as bonus cards will also influence the strategy to be able to catch bigger and bigger fish that will be worth more in the final count.

It will be possible to conjure up storms against one's opponent and steal equipment to slow him down, a true elemental and non-fishing competition.

The time of the game is punctuated by the 9 bonus cards which, once awarded, will trigger the final account where the most capable - but also the luckiest - fishermen will take home the coveted fishing trophy. You will receive one point for every 5kg caught and in the event of a tie, the one who caught the most fish will win.

Remedio is a historical adventure game. You solve tricky puzzles in escape game style, explore the world of knowledge in Southern Europe, North Africa, West and Central Asia in the 14th. Century and experience adventures together.

This cooperative game can be played several times and offers three different puzzle routes to search for the cure for the Emir.

Collect the longest dogs in this zany, fast-paced game for 2-5 players.

Drafting from rotating hands of five cards, players construct their dogs from matching sets of heads, middles and ends. Longer dogs containing more cards will score more at the end of the game, but only if you manage to complete them.

Will you settle for a bog-standard pooch? Or stretch both your dog and your luck for a chance of a higher score?

—description from the publisher

Have you always dreamed of beating Bandido but never managed to? Managed to win a match in 20?

Your time has come. Now go!

To give you a little help in your matches, the "Go Now!" they only have 4 exits (as opposed to the original game where there are 5 or 6 exits) and greatly improve your chance of capturing the bandit.

Use one of the 5 "Go Now!" as the starting card of your game, in place of the original card and try to win with this "little help".

Play with children? Use the cards to simplify your games!

Numbered from 1 to 5, these cards are dealt separately. Collect them all!

Have you always dreamed of beating Bandido but never managed to? Managed to win a match in 20?

Your time has come. Now go!

To give you a little help in your matches, the "Go Now!" they only have 4 exits (as opposed to the original game where there are 5 or 6 exits) and greatly improve your chance of capturing the bandit.

Use one of the 5 "Go Now!" as the starting card of your game, in place of the original card and try to win with this "little help".

Play with children? Use the cards to simplify your games!

Numbered from 1 to 5, these cards are dealt separately. Collect them all!

Your time has come. Now go!

To give you a little help in your matches, the "Go Now!" they only have 4 exits (as opposed to the original game where there are 5 or 6 exits) and greatly improve your chance of capturing the bandit.

Use one of the 5 "Go Now!" as the starting card of your game, in place of the original card and try to win with this "little help".

Play with children? Use the cards to simplify your games!

Numbered from 1 to 5, these cards are dealt separately. Collect them all!

In the game, each player takes 8 discs from a character (cat or mouse).

The first player places a disk on the table, starting a disk stack.

The second player can choose to place a new disk on the table, starting a new disk stack or place a new disk on top of an existing disk stack or move the disk that is on top of an exiting disk stack

And so it goes on alternating shifts.

Each disk stack can have a maximum of 3 disks.

The first player to have 5 character discs appearing on the table wins the game.

In no time you can look at the disks that are under the stack.  When removing a disk from the top of a stack, it is mandatory to move it.

Having survived tremendous upheaval following sudden, cataclysmic solar activity, forces still left on Earth and around the solar system vie for control of resources, land and technology.

Pressing out from their makeshift refuges, armed with giant mechs, the remnants of human factions forge new alliances and wage war to survive what might be lurking just over the horizon.

This book contains rules for:

     Equipping your HE-Vs with new motive systems
     Using new secondary mission objectives
     Fielding new support assets like Ultra-Light HE-Vs, vehicles, and infantry squads
     Organizing and playing in Steel Rift campaigns


—description from the back of the book

In the near future, in the wake of crippling solar flares, the remnants of humanity fight for resources, order, and sometimes glory.

Steel Rift is a fast paced skirmish game featuring highly configurable mechs called HE-Vs. Played on a 3’ x 3’ table, it’s easy to set up almost anywhere.

Reinforcements have arrived.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Stoic is a placement game with simple rules. There are many abstract games in which you capture your opponent's pieces by sandwiching them between your pieces, but in this game, you cannot place a piece in a position where one of your opponent's pieces is sandwiched between two of yours. The player who cannot place a piece in their turn loses.

This game is included in Generic Board "Hexagonal" set.

—description from the designer

One of the attainment games where you try to reach the other side of the board.

You start the game with a fixed initial placement, and each turn you move one of your pieces, which must jump over exactly one other piece. The piece jumped over can be either color.

You win if your piece reaches the farthest end of the board, but you lose if you run out of pieces to move.

This game is included in Generic Board "Hexagonal" set.

—description from the designer

Squish is an unification game with the theme of "condensation".

Players take turns moving one of their pieces one space at a time toward an friendly piece on the same line. If a player's piece moves to a space occupied by an opponent's piece, it is captured by replacement. If there is no friendly piece on the same line, the piece cannot move.

The first player to move all of the pieces on the board adjacent to each other or to prevent the opponent from moving wins the game.

This game is included in Generic Board "Hexagonal".

—description from the designer

Unlace is a blocking game (a game of fighting for room to move) derived from Squish. Starting with the same layout as in Squish, players take turns moving one of their friendly pieces to an adjacent space. However, the moved piece must belong to a larger group than the one before the move.

The player who fails to move during their turn loses.

This game is included in Generic Board "Hexagonal" set.

—description from the designer

A game in which the object is to connect opposing edges of the board with your pieces, like Hex.

On each turn, the player places their piece in the outer area, and then another piece in the inner area as if it launches from the outer one just placed. Only if the piece placed in the outer area is adjacent to other pieces or their rows, the second piece can be placed by jumping over them.

This game is included in Generic Board "Hexagonal" set.

—description from the designer

You are a goblin land baron and member of the Hut Barons guild -- purveyors of magical real estate for weefolk, bigfolk, and everyone in between. Laid out in a square grid, the buildings of Cartref possess magical effects that vary depending on their residents. Use your wits and magic to amass the greatest wealth in the budding city of Cartref.

On your turn, take 2 actions from the following options. (A) Build a new building and invoke the magic of adjacent goblin buildings. (B) Fulfill a guild Mandate and invoke the magic of bigfolk (human) buildings. (C) Impel the gnomes in one of your gnomish buildings to use their magic. (D) Purify the bad karma (pollution) emitted by the buildings so that you can invoke them again.

Players score Mandates (objective cards) twice -- once when initially fulfilled and again at the game's end. This creates the tension of a race while also provides a framework for strategic planning through the very last turn.

—description from the publisher

A postcard wargame that places you in the role of a sniper during WWII

Game takes 15-45 min.
Components: 2d6 and the 2 counters that come with the game.

The goal of the game is to take out as many target enemies as possible while trying to survive.

The game is played by rolling dice in a variety of tables and deciding the best moment to take out your target. But watch out and don't push your luck too far! Because for every shot you take, the enemy may react to it through the game's Enemy Behavior system and things can get quite dicey for you in no time...

VP's are won by amount of enemies eliminated and  kill track milestones.

Game can be played as a single mission, campaign and Legendary sniper (insane mode).

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

boardgame: Golden Journal Number 51: Kursk – Dogs of War
description:
The II SS Panzer Corps controlled three mechanized infantry divisions made up of party militia and lavishly equipped with tanks, vehicles and artillery. They spearheaded the southern flank of the German offensive at Kursk in July 1943, where the Soviet 5th Guards Tank Army decisively blunted their advance.

This Golden Journal supplement includes four new scenarios highlighting these Corps.  There are two versions of this: one with just the supplement that uses the default pieces in the games, one with a set of pieces in an all black color scheme.

—description from the publisher

You need Fire in the Steppe, Kursk: Burning Tigers, and Kursk: South Flank to play them all.
family: Series: Avalanche Press Gold Club Golden Journal Supplements

description:
Of Two Minds is a trick-taking, shedding card game for 3-5 players, playable in 30-60 minutes. It is a traditional-style card game, playable with 60 cards, 1-10 in 6 suits. In addition to classic trick-taking mechanics, it involves elements of hand management and turn order management.

In the game, players will try to win tricks in order to win the most points from tricks and be the first to shed all of their cards from their hand. The player who leads the trick plays two cards face-up, representing two tricks. Winning one of the two tricks nets points and allows the player to retain the lead. Losing both tricks will lose them the lead. Winning both will result in negative points, meaning that the ideal outcome for the leader is winning only one of the two tricks.

Scoring is one point per card, -1 point per card for those where the leader won both tricks, and -1 point for any cards remaining in hand.

The game is played over a number of rounds equal to the number of players, or six rounds in a 3P game. The player with the most points wins the game.

—description from designer

Gardo is a tile-laying game for 2 to 3 players where you aim to score the most points.  Each player will take 3 actions on their turn, choosing from 2 possible actions: lay a tile or place a cube.

The cubes are in each players color and show a value corresponding to their influence - but the numbers are not known to your opponents until the game ends.

The tiles show various paths on them and completed loops at the end of the game will score for the players with the most influence in that area.  Certain incomplete areas will also score.
designers: Charlie Hishakaku (チャーリー飛車角)
publishers: Board Game Production Committee (ボドゲ製作委員会)

description:
The Travel Bug Board Game (Australian Edition) will take you on a trip of a lifetime.

See Australia by road, rail, air or sea. Collect photos of landmarks, plants and animals, enjoy amazing travels and return home safely. Earn points by travelling and collecting souvenirs.

How to play

Travel around by using different types of transport (4WD, Plane, Cruise Ship, Train)

Score points depending on the length of your trip.

Collect Souvenirs

Score points for collecting different plants, animals and landmarks.

Travel Bug coin

Which travel bug have you caught? – a strong desire to travel (wanderlust) or travelers stomach bug? Toss a coin and find out!

Try Your Luck

Got a Try Your Luck card? – find out if good luck is on your side today! In the Travel Bug game, good luck will bring you exciting travels and more souvenirs, while bad luck could make you face wild crocodiles, spiders, tropical cyclones and even a bushfire.

—description from the publisher

It Rolls for Ivan is a set of table top wargame rules for fighting Brigade-Divisional level actions in the Russian Civil War. The rules are based upon the "For Whom The Dice Rolls" Spanish Civil War rules. They are designed to enable games covering a large area, with sweeping cavalry movements and dogged infantry. Detailed sections cover the uniqueness of the war, from the reluctance sometimes of armies to obey their orders, to Armoured Trains and the various odd looking armoured vehicles employed.

—description from the publisher

Node is a sister work to Skirt. Like Skirt, the active player places a pair of pieces in the outer and inner areas, but in Node, the second piece is placed in a space in the inner area that is on the same line as the two pieces in the outer area.

The victory condition is also the same as in Skirt, if you can connect one of the opposite sides with your pieces, you win.

This game is included in Generic Board "Hexagonal".

—description from the designer

Orochi is a connection game with a somewhat quirky goal.

Each player is in charge of one of the two colors, but can also place a piece of the opponent's color through the game. If, after your placement, there is a piece adjacent to a stone of the same color in four directions, you may exchange that piece for a piece of the opposite color and get an additional turn.

Play until there is only one space left on the board, and the player with the larger group adjacent to the last space wins.

This game is included in Generic Board "Hexagonal" set.

—description from the designer

Mabi is a unification game in which the goal is to create the largest group of your pieces possible.

Starting with the initial position as the diagram, players move one of their pieces either vertically or horizontally, one step at a time. However, if a pieces is adjacent to a larger group of opponent's pieces, that piece becomes paralyzed and cannot move.

If you can make a larger group than your opponent in the end, you win. In case of a tie, the first player to build a group of that size wins.

This game is included in Generic Board "Square".

—description from the designer

Tiptoe is one of those games where players compete for space to move.

After each player has placed their pieces two for each on the board, the turn player adds a friendly piece from their group at a distance equal to the size of the group.

The player who cannot place a piece in their turn loses the game. The game can be also played with diagonal adjacency allowed, or with a hexagonal board.

This game is included in Generic Board "Square".

—description from the designer

Zong-Heng is a game in which the object is to create larger groups by adding pieces by shifting rows and columns of pieces.

You can shift the rows starting from the piece on the dark square, but you can only move the rows that contain more of your pieces.

This game is included in Generic Board "Checkered" set.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Stadt Land Vollpfosten: Das Brettspiel – Rotlicht Erweiterung
description:
Discover now the official red light expansion of the STADT LAND VOLLPFOSTEN board game. Included are 354 wacky and kinky categories. Only playable with the original STADT LAND VOLLPFOSTEN board game!

—description from the publisher (translated)

Entdecke jetzt die offizielle Rotlicht Erweiterung vom STADT LAND VOLLPFOSTEN Brettspiel. Enthalten sind 354 abgefahrene und versaute Kategorien. Nur mit dem original STADT LAND VOLLPFOSTEN Brettspiel spielbar!

—description from the publisher

Binary is a territorial game uses a checkerboard. The goal is to create larger groups by placing a limited number of pieces. The game is unique in that empty squares adjacent to pieces of the same color are also included in your territory.

This game is included in Generic Board "Checkered" set.

—description from the designer

Incorrect Checkers is a game born from the wrong setup of Checkers (Draught). Although it bears the name of Checkers, there is no capture, and the aim is to reach the goal while jumping other pieces like in Halma or Chinese Checkers.

In this game, friendly pieces can be jumped over at any time, but only one opponent piece can be jumped over during a turn.

This game is included in Generic Board "Checkered" set.

—description from the designer

A hundred years have passed since the world witnessed the first detonation of an atomic bomb, marking the beginning of a new era for human kind. An era which now has ended as the last atomic bombs ever produced have been used in war. Only a small fraction of the world's population has survived, consisting mainly of various military groups whose days are numbered as radiation poisoning from nuclear fallout, as well as the uncontrolled spread of biological weapons, is quickly claiming the lives of the last survivors.

As a leader of a remaining military faction, you have devised a plan to take the survival of humanity into your own hands. You have set up a base in the northernmost regions of Scandinavia,  an area which has been spared from the worst bombings. Before the war, the world's most advanced space faring operations were being conducted here and you have managed to secure a disassembled but otherwise functional lunar rocket left behind from those times. On the Moon's surface, a self-sustainable base was once constructed as part of the Swedish space program, but it has not been inhabited for many years now. If you could get a crew there, that base could serve as a new home for the last humans. And maybe in a distant future when Earth is habitable once more, a return trip could be possible. However, it seems as though your enemies have had the same plan. A race to secure the fusion batteries and rocket components needed to initiate the launch sequence is ensuing, in what will be the last battle on earth.

In order to win, you must balance your time between exploring new territories, investing in resource production, advancing the tech-tree, conducting research, mobilizing troops and attacking your opponents.

—description from the designer

This is a card game for two players.
The order of the cards in each other's field is switched by actions to play a three-way game.
The player who wins 9 times or gets 3 cards of the same pattern first wins.
The game is decided in a short period of time, about 10 minutes.

—description from the designer

Paint the Roses is a 2-5 player cooperative puzzle game set in the whimsical world of Alice in Wonderland. You and your teammates have been appointed the new Royal Gardeners for the Queen of Hearts but fail to arrange the garden according to the Queen's changing whims and the last thing you hear will be, "Off With Their Heads!".

The Deluxe Edition includes upgraded components like acrylic tiles, a custom GameTrayz insert and the Escape the Castle expansion all in one box.

Paint the Roses Deluxe includes Escape the Castle an expansion to Paint the Roses. After years of being trapped in the castle grounds, you and your fellow gardeners hatch a dangerous plan - to gain the help of a Wonderland friend, trick the Queen of Hearts, and make a daring escape to freedom.

Deluxe Edition Contents: 1 Deluxe Game Trayz Insert, 1 Deluxe Dual Layered Garden board, 32 Deluxe Shrub tiles, 1 Deluxe Dual Layered Greenhouse board, 132 Whim Cards, 30 Clue Tokens, 3 Notepads, 3 Character Miniatures, 4 Flower Tokens, 1 Velvet Bag, 1 Royal Log Notebook.

Includes Escape the Castle Expansion Deluxe: 5 XL Castle Keys, 5 Deluxe Mad Hatter Tea Tables, 3 Eat Me & 4 Drink Me Tokens, 10 Queen of Hearts Cards, 20 Cheshire Cat Cards, 9 Mad Hatter Cards, 18 Tweedledee & Tweedledum Cards, 9 Alice Cards, 10 White Rabbit Cards and 8 Jabberwocky Cards.
family: Misc: Watch It Played How To Videos

boardgame: Hiroshima (fan expansion for Age of Steam)
description:
This Hiroshima map for Age of Steam is themed around the area's association with the color red: the Torii gate at Itsukushima Shrine, Momiji Manju (a maple-shaped cake), and the local baseball team.  As the game progresses, many cities will change to red.

All new city tiles (other than the red one) begin on the board, covering red cities, and some of the non-red cubes are removed from the game.  When you would add a new city, instead, move one of the new cities that is on top of a red city to a town hex.  Delivered red cubes are worth additional victory points at the end of the game.

The game also features special "enhanced" actions.  Once per game, a player may use an upgraded version of the action they choose from the auction.  Additionally, once a specific enhancement has been chosen by a player, the other players may not use that one.

boardgame: Marvel: Crisis Protocol – Red Guardian & Ursa Major
description:
Mikhail Ursus is Ursa Major, who possesses the ability to transform into an enormous humanoid brown bear. His government may have initially made Mikhail a target, but he was eventually enrolled in a special agent program by the nefarious Professor Phobos. He was trained as the agent Ursa Major and fought for the government before parting ways with his former masters. No matter what team he’s affiliated with, Ursa Major fights for the Russian people with honor and a love for his country.

Nikolai Krylenko is the Red Guardian, a superhuman born in Russia with the power to withstand nearly all electromagnetic, kinetic, and gravitronic energy. The sibling of Darkstar, Krylenko has long served as the hero Vanguard. As the Red Guardian, he wields a modified vibranium shield that can capture and enhance his abilities. The eighth man to don the uniform of the Red Guardian, Krylenko’s reputation as an accomplished soldier and defender continues to grow.

Contents:
2 Miniatures
  ‣ Ursa Major
  ‣ Red Guardian
6 Bases
  ‣ 2 Round Scenic (50mm)
  ‣ 4 Round Scenic (35mm)
2 Character Stat Cards
3 Team Tactic Cards

SHIPHEAD is a "shedding" (or ladder down) card game where you aim to be the first to get rid of all your cards. Each player starts with cards in the hand, cards on the table face down, with face-up cards on top of those. You shed them to a center pile, then the next player needs to play an equal or lower numbered set of cards (up to four cards total, of the same rank), or take up the growing center pile.

When one player goes out, the other players receive points for the cards they hold. (You don't want points.) Symbols on the cards influence the counting of those points. After the final round, the player with the least points wins. SHIPHEAD is a fast paced game and accommodates 3-7 players. It's a fun way to start or end a game day. Kids who love games can play it too. It offers meaningful decisions and a compulsive feeling--"got to get rid of my cards"! The most powerful cards in the game are also the greatest liability. Do you get rid of them at the first opportunity, or save them for a decisive moment? SHIPHEAD comes in a sturdy box with 108 cards, a page of rules and a page of illustrated examples. SHIPHEAD features fun ship-headed cartoon characters on the cards.

Since each player is usually getting rid of 1-4 cards per turn, the ratchet squeezes, the cards in hand are getting fewer, and the play pushes toward a conclusion for concise play. Timing is everything. When do you take risks, or how do you avoid them? When do you put pressure on the other players? How do you manipulate the flow? Can you wait for the right moment to make your big play? ShipHead moves along quickly. When do you use the cards in the hand, and when your cards on the table?

ShipHead offers fast and spirited play, tension, and meaningful decisions.

-description from designer

In Wooden-Legged Justice players take on the role of detectives from the Han City Xiezhi Gate Investigation Division, tasked with solving criminal cases by analyzing highly realistic case files. These files include a wealth of materials, such as autopsy reports, crime scene reports, communication records, and surveillance footage. Players must utilize various investigative techniques to uncover the truth behind each case.

As the layers of truth are revealed, new puzzles and plot twists emerge, keeping the players constantly engaged and on their toes. The game requires one player to assume the role of the "Police Consultant" to facilitate gameplay. In this immersive and thrilling experience, players will not only test their deductive skills but also dive deep into the intricate world of crime and justice.

LOONG VS. TIGER （龙争虎斗）The game of loong vs. tiger combines many traditional local poker playing methods in China, such as "fighting for the upper reaches", "fighting the landlord", "1000 points", "whipped eggs", "tractor" and even "please close your eyes after dark". I believe you will feel familiar after playing and inspire more novel ways to play....一款可以供4~6人游戏的卡牌类游戏。在中国传统扑克牌的基础上增加了8张各具特色、特点的功能牌。让游戏不精算也能玩，使之千变万化，变幻莫测。可谓是地主类卡牌游戏的巅峰之作！
publishers: 龙争虎斗工作室 (Enter the Dragon Studio)

description:
In Gem Getter, you and your friends will compete to dig the most efficient mine and become the greatest getter of gems. A game is played in 9 rounds.  Each round you will...

ROLL to determine which polyomino dig patterns are available,
DIG your chosen pattern into the mine, and
COLLECT heaps of gems with unique scoring conditions.

Efficient digging of rows/columns and around boulders will reward you with bonus gems! Find oil to keep the digging machines running a little longer with small bonus dig patterns.

There are 8 unique mines to dig in, and 2 roll sheets to choose from for plenty of variety. A game of Gem Getter plays in around 15-30 minutes, and any number of players can join you as long as you have enough sheets!

The Roll-With-It System is a roll-and-write adventure game system featuring thematic variety and endless adventure possibilities due to its flexible core rules system.

The system's basic rules provide a “skeleton” rules framework on which all of the adventure modules are structured. Each adventure module puts the “flesh” on the rules with theme and creative new mechanisms.

The Roll-With-It Fantasy Boxed Set includes all six adventure modules in the Fantasy series:


     Fortress of Terror
     Temple of Despair
     Maze of the Minotaur
     Lake of the Dragon
     Pyramid of the Mummy
     Mines of Darkness


—description from the publisher

SECRET AGENT CARD GAME | From the CIA, MI6 and the KGB, spies have changed the course of history. The Spy Alley Card game is your ticket to the world of espionage. Outwit your opponents and show them you have mastered the skills it takes to be the next Secret Agent

YOUR MISSION | The goal of the Spy Alley Card Game is to conceal your identity and outwit your opponents. Collect your spy equipment/documents before your opponents reveal your hidden identity, all the while trying to unmask your opponents. Be careful! A small gesture, a slight hesitation, or slip of the tongue, are all easy ways for your friends and family to oust your country and knock you out of the game

boardgame: The Centenary Game, No.1 Discovering Victoria
description:
The Centenary Game, No.1 Discovering Victoria is a roll and move game that is of historical significance in celebrating 100 years of the founding of Melbourne, the capital city of the state of Victoria, Australia.

The game shows a map of Victoria with major towns and information. Players race around Victoria starting from Portland (first Victorian settlement) to major border towns before finally arriving in Melbourne. Landing on some towns can delay or advance your journey. First to reach Melbourne is the winner.

boardgame: Write or Die: The Romantic Period
description:
In Write or Die: The Romantic Period, players simultaneously draft cards to strategically build upon a budding writing career, fulfill personal bonus objectives, and remain in good health. Should a player die, they continue to play from the beyond but focus on cards available to the deceased. After each round, players recount the latest life update using the drafted hand for inspiration.

With cards influenced by the lives of the great writers of the time—Jane Austen, William Wordsworth, Mary Shelley, Edgar Allen Poe—you forge your own path to write your name in the stars. With 108 unique cards, no two stories are ever the same. The player with the most successful career dead or alive by the end of three rounds wins the game.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Artisans of Splendent Vale: Recharge Pack
description:
Recharge your copy of Artisans of Splendent Vale so you can play the campaign again! By swapping out a few components, you'll be able to journey back into Splendence and continue to explore!

Contents:
8 Quick Change Cards
1 Rosha Companion Card
76 Sticker Cards
1 World Map
4 Character Sheets
1 Rulebook

boardgame: SCP The Board Game: Weapons And Armour Expansion
description:
Adding items and weapons and aurmor to the game players may use to help them complete their objectives.

Also adds four new SCP's players may play as.

boardgame: 源平奥州六大合戦 (Six battles of the Genpei war)
description:
義経戦記：源平奥州六大合戦 (Yoshitsune Senki: The Six Great Battles of Genpei Oshu) covers battles during the Genpei war in Japan (1180–1185).

The game system is chit-driven and gives a fluid game.
The combat system is simple: roll a six-sided die, add the dice to the commander's combat value. The highest total wins; if the total difference is 4 or more, the enemy unit is eliminated.

In addition, special rules are included for each battle.
In the Ichinotani Battle, an ambush unit under Yoshitsune's command is used. 
In Dannoura, when the 'tidal current' chit is drawn, any Heike unit can act in the first half of the game, and any Genji unit can act in the second half. This is to show the change in momentum. 
In Yashima, a 'target' chit determines if any Heike unit can act. 
In Mizushima, a weather chit is drawn each turn and if the 'eclipse' chit appears, the Kiso army unit is disrupted.

Number of counters: 252 pieces
Maps: 6 small maps
Scenarios: 6 (Ichinotani, Dannoura, Yashima, Uji/Seda, Mizushima, Azugashiyama)
Game scale: Depends on the scenario
Difficulty (5 levels, 5 being the highest): 1

(translated and adapted from publisher's Japanese description)
family: History: Conflicts of the Heian period – Japan

boardgame: Sleepwalkers: Imperial Rivalries and the Great War
description:
„All nations want peace, but they want a peace that suits them.“ (Admiral „Jackie“ Fisher)

The outbreak of the First World War, often called „the seminal catastrophe of the 20th century”, is one of the most controversial topics in human history. Many Europeans had condoned war, but hardly anyone could have wanted the unprecedented slaughter that began in 1914. Why did it happen? How could it happen?

Sleepwalkers is a simulation game about diplomacy at the brink of war, covering approximately the period between 1900 and 1918. Players represent the governments of the major powers Austria-Hungary, Britain, France, Germany and Russia. Vying for prestige, they impose tariffs, arm, expand and forge alliances. Random events such as the Dreyfus Affair, the Boer War, the Berlin-Baghdad Railway, the Russo-Japanese War and the Black Hand add to the tensions. Crises are sure to erupt, forcing each player to decide whether to mediate or to mobilize. Can you avoid war, while striving for a peace that suits you?

Players: 1-5 (best with 3-5)
Complexity: low
Components: 8-page rules booklet, 12"x16" map, 55 die-cut counters. You also need 1 six-sided die and an opaque container like a mug which are not provided with the game.

—description from the publisher

Four pairs of tiles, themed for Centurion, for play with any Profile System game.

Cataphract
Chariots
Hoplites
War Elephants

After players select their bags of Soldier Tiles and the gameboard is set up, they take their appropriate-colored tiles from Roman Auxiliaries and add them to their bags.

Cataphract - Starting Side: slide straight ahead/behind one space straight ahead/behind, defense one space away left and right, move two spaces away left and right; Flip Side: slide straight left/right one space straight left/right, defense one space away straight ahead and behind, move two spaces away straight ahead and behind;
Chariots - Starting Side: slide straight ahead one space away straight ahead, strike one space away left and right, move one space away diagonally right and left (forwards), jump two spaces away diagonally right and left (forwards); Flip Side: non-capturing move one space left and right, move one and two spaces straight behind, slide straight ahead one space away straight ahead, jump two spaces away straight ahead;
Hoplites - Starting Side: move one space away straight ahead, right, left, diagonally right and left (backwards), defense two spaces away straight ahead, right, left, diagonally right and left (forwards); Flip Side: defense all spaces 2 away diagonally forward and straight, move one space straight ahead, two spaces left and right, strike one space away left and right, non-capturing move one space away diagonally left and right (backwards);
War Elephants - Starting Side: smash straight ahead and diagonally right and left (forwards) one and two spaces; Flip Side: move one space diagonally, dread one space away orthogonally

Four pairs of new tiles, themes for Centurion, for play with any Profile System game.

Cleopatra
Marcus Aurelius
Jupiter
Scorpion

After players select their bags of Soldier Tiles and the gameboard is set up, they take their appropriate-colored tiles from Emperor's Pack and add them to their bags.

Move descriptions:
Cleopatra - Starting Side: move one space orthogonally, command two spaces left and right; Flip Side: slide backwards, dread all other orthogonal and diagonal spaces one away; I: summon on both sides
Marcus Aurelius - combination of move, non-capturing move, non-capturing slide, and full formation on both sides of the tile
Jupiter - move, jump and strike on forward spaces, both sides of the tile
Two pair of tiles as submitted by the campaign Imperators (Frumentarii and Aeneator), for use with any Profile System game.

boardgame: Hoplomachus: Victorum – Aprilis and the Fool
description:
This expansion for Hoplomachus: Victorum contains both a new hero and a new scion to add to your game.

Aprilis is a hero without a home, cursed to roam the ancient world in search of glory with only her trusty steed and her questionable wits. Coalemus is the god of foolishness, and some say his promotion to scion is his greatest prank of all - but on whom?

Playable together or separately, the two halves of Aprilis and the Fool inject more variety and a bit of ancient levity into your copy of Hoplomachus: Victorum.

—description from the publisher

A pair of Gladiator tiles for use with any Profile System game.

Starting side: strike one space orthogonally, non-capture move one space diagonally
Flip side: non-capture move one space orthogonally, strike one space diagonally

Three pairs of tiles, themed for Centurion, for play with any Profile System game.

Camel

Camel - Starting Side: strike one space left and right, move one space straight behind, slide straight forward one space away diagonal left and right (forwards); Flip Side: strike one space left and right, move one space straight ahead, slide straight behind one space away diagonal left and right (backwards); 
Legio III Italica - Starting Side: move one space straight ahead or left, move two spaces straight behind, non-capturing move or full formation one space right, jump two spaces away diagonally left and right (forwards), strike straight ahead two spaces; Flip Side: move one space straight ahead, move two spaces straight behind, non-capturing slide right/left one space away diagonally left and right (backwards), jump two spaces away diagonally left and right (forwards), strike straight ahead two spaces;

The players are a group of adventurers. After today's quest, you have come to a popular diner called "Barrel’s". Sadly, you are broke and don't have enough money to order a single beer. But don't worry! The restaurant has a beer dispenser that pours beer for every coin.
Stack your coins one at a time in front of you until someone decides to challenge everyone on the amount of coins they are ready to flip. Up the stakes by outbidding the Challenger and become the lucky adventurer who will fill their mug. The moment frothy foam is revealed though, the challenge is stopped and the player with a filled up mug drink it up!

Beer or Air plays like a variant of SKULL with with differences including scoring rules and special character abilities. Get your adventurer drunk and filled with happiness!

The rules originated from a desire to wargame the encounters between submarines and ASW forces portrayed in books such as Red Storm Rising (Tom Clancy and Larry Bond), and in films such as ‘The Hunt for Red October’ and ‘Crimson Tide’.  I also was keen to use the 1/700 scale submarine and ship kits I was building as they are such great kits and I couldn’t find any rules!  Modern naval wargaming has often suffered from being seen as too complex to wargame on the table top because of the technological complexity and therefore now is thought perhaps best suited to computer simulation.  Existing table top rulesets such as ‘Harpoon’, ‘Prosub’ or ‘Modern Naval Battles’ either require detailed calculations and although very accurate, are often not suited to an evening club game or are designed as an abstract card based system that doesn’t allow miniatures to be used.  
The difficulties arise because these sets are comprehensive and encompass all facets of modern naval combat with the submarine aspect a minor player.  There are in reality several distinct aspects to modern naval warfare.  At one extreme, carrier or land based airstrikes or missile strikes can originate several hundred miles away and yet be resolved in seconds, whilst at the other end the stealth and uncertainty of submarine combat can take daring, guile and be enacted over short distances where collisions are a real danger.

By concentrating on submarines and ASW specifically I have tried to develop a playable (I hope), fun and yet challenging set of rules that capture the specific tactics and tension of the ‘aerial dance’ beneath the waves, with variable levels of complexity that can be added as desired.  The core rules are unashamedly simple to try and give a playable game on a club night and I have resisted the temptation to add extra complexity.  Further rules will follow in subsequent updates.

A pack of 8 new playable characters. This includes:

The goal of the Magical Forest board game is for players to populate the forest with special creatures. However, this is not as simple a task as it seems. Mushrooms only feel good in a community, while magic trees like to live with a couple. The unicorn can be a propitiator for witches and fairies. And the dwarf likes to rest under the mushrooms, in the shade of the trees.

At the beginning of the game, everyone gets their own forest board, then we put together the pine tree elements - everyone has to put them in the same places! No more tiles can be placed on fields with such a pine tree. Then, each player must put 5 tiles of each character (dwarf, magic tree, mushroom, unicorn, fairy, witch) into the canvas bag and shake the bag well.

The game will last for 5 rounds and players will place 6 tiles on their board in each round - 2 tiles are placed face down first, then flipped over when everyone is done. After that, everyone passes the other cards to the left. This will continue until the bag is empty.
Note: It is advisable to place the received tiles in such a way that they will be worth as many points as possible. The rulebook describes in detail which magic creature has what extra

The scoring sheet for the game also helps with scoring at the end of the game. Whoever has the most points will win.

The game can be played in several ways: in addition to the basic game, there are target cards at different levels of difficulty, which can also be added to the game as difficulty. Among the target cards, the easy and the hard ones can always be obtained by the person who succeeds in completing the task first, and the medium-level card is dealt at the end of the game to the player who has the most magical creatures of the type requested by the target card.

A thoughtful, somewhat strategic board game with nice graphics, in which cards are passed using a drafting technique.

The Tell Me, Kuflik! board game is a creative and interactive game designed to encourage storytelling and creativity in players. The basic idea of the game is for players to create a story in which different characters have different adventures and experiences.

The game requires a board on which the different characters and locations are represented. During the game, players roll dice and choose a character or location from the board according to the result. Each player must then tell a short story involving that character or location.

During the game, players have to invent the stories, so creativity and imagination are one of the most important parts of the game. The aim of the game is to encourage storytelling and creativity in players and help develop communication and verbal expression skills.

The game can be played by 2-5 players and usually takes 30-45 minutes to play. The game is also suitable for younger children, as there is no violence or danger involved, and all players have to do is tell a story. Overall, Tell Me, Kuflik! board game is a fun and creative game that can be an excellent choice for playing time between family or friends.

Battle of Georgia Landing is a solitaire Civil War wargame.  It represents an historical clash of arms that took place in south Louisiana on October 27, 1862.  You play the role of General Alfred Mouton commanding Confederate forces at Labadieville, Louisiana.   Your artificial intelligence (AI) opponent is General Godfrey Weitzel in command of Union forces.  Bisecting the battlefield and a key terrain feature is Bayou Lafourche.  Your goal is to stop the Union advance by eliminating Union regiments and capturing the Union supply train.  You lose if your units are eliminated or retreat off the map or if General Mouton is incapacitated due to his health.

–description from designer

Tides: Beach Party is a free PNP play sheet, posted with permission of Button Shy Games, that allows you to play Tides with up to four players; no other components needed! Just print one of these for each player and you're good to go.

Letterbox is a 1+ player game about making words. Each player receives 5 double-sided square cards with a letter in each corner of the card. Players shuffle the cards and then begin play. They are allowed to rotate the cards, but not change their positions in the hand. Importantly, as long as the cards are all on their light side or all on their dark side, it is always possible to find a word no matter what order the cards are in.

In the solo variant, players have special challenges and additional rules to make the game more challenging.

—description from the designer

Escalation: Nuclear War in Europe is a comic-book-styled game of espionage, warfare, and nuclear exchange. It is a game for 1-2 players, which begins its story at the height of the Cold War. You take the role of NATO or Warsaw Pact commander-in-chief.

Each turn, you start with a set of action cards, which you use to perform actions and trigger events. Your successes bring you closer to victory but escalate the crisis at the same time. The initial Cold War stage allows you to use agents to perform intelligence operations. But when the game escalates to the next (Conventional War) stage, the full force of the army and airforce is brought to bear.

Now you conduct military offensives by advancing your armies across the enemy land, supported by your bombers and agents. If you gain a lot of ground quickly, the enemy falls apart, and you are victorious. But if you fail to sustain the advance, another escalation becomes imminent, crossing the threshold into a nuclear war.

The game comes with a Tutorial designed to teach you the game quickly and easily. You can play the full or the short version of the game, giving 2-4 hours of average gameplay time. It also features a Solo Bot system specially developed for this game. This is the first in a series of games that are quick to learn, easy to play, and exciting to experience. Special attention has been given to the system's user-friendliness and visual style.

—description from the designer

The game is played on an ordinary checkerboard. Each of the two players has 12 double-colored pieces or men(one black and white, the other green and red). The players place their men on the black squares of the 3 rows closest to them, so that the dark side (black or green) comes up.
Players take turns advancing their men on the black quares, only one square at a time. You are forced to jump over an opponent's man as soon as your own man touches that of your opponent and there is a free space behind him. Here, the opponent's man that has jumped over is taken along and placed at the bottom on the free space behind it. The jump must be continued if there is another opportunity. With each jump, only one enemy man can be taken as a prisoner. The man captured last is always at the bottom of the board. This is how towers are built. A tower moves or jumps like an ordinary man. If a tower is jumped over, only the top man may be taken and captured. If a man reaches one of the last 4 squares of the checkerboard, it is turned over so that the light side (white or red) comes up. He becomes a king. If a tower arrives on one of the last squares, only its top piece may be turned over. Such a tower moves or jumps like a king. A king may move forward or backward one or more squares or capture.
The game is decided when all of a player's men or kings are captured or when they can no longer be moved. The number of kings of both players gives the number of points with which the game was won.

—user summary
publishers: Gesellschaft für den Vertieb moderner Spiele

description:
Supremacy: Arms Race is a space wars boardgame in a book! Everything you need to play the game is included in the one book, including cut out assets, ship standies, game boards and an 8 scenario campaign.

It's 2930 and humanity has found a new home planet, thrown from Earth by a sentient machine system some years ago. However, it wasn’t the only species to flee into space – lycans were the first to run, finding refuge on a distant planet. Now, after centuries of silence, the lycans are resurgent. The machine intelligence on Earth has also been developing advanced weaponry to derail humanity’s hopes of a new beginning.

Are you going to be humanity’s hope or downfall?

Supremacy: Arms Race is a diceless system that runs on 'Action Points.' The faction and commander you choose will determine the amount of AP you will receive each turn and it's up to you how you spread those points across your fleet, either offensively or defensively? But, be careful as attacks also cost AP. So, do you hang back and upgrade your ships or unload a barrage of artillery at your opponent? Do you split your AP and do a bit of both or outmanoeuvre the enemy and board them, ripping their ships apart and removing their upgrades? In the end it's a game of strategy and war!

—description from the publisher

2050

Large space corporations struggle for leadership in developing the Red planet. Shuttles of competing corporations enter Mars's atmosphere. Each newcomer contributes to Mars's development to become a part of the colony and the new world. Do you think it's a sci-fi story? Nope, this is a quite real image of the future. And we must be ready for it.

You have to explore the hostile landscape of Mars and find vital resources to build your colony consisting of different modules. The player who did it first wins the game.

Each player's turn consists of two phases. Firstly, a player rolls a die to determine how much oxygen they have for the second phase. In the second phase, the player expends that oxygen on various actions, like moving a unit, exploring resources, extracting resources, attacking a module or another unit, healing a unit or fixing a module, building a module.
If you have too many of the same resources, you can exchange them in the game's reserve using a fixed 4:1 price. You can also trade with other players using the negotiated exchange rate.

The main goal of the game is to build as many modules as you can. And the gameplay looks simple - find the necessary resources and build the module. But don't forget other players can attack your modules! You have to plan your actions thoroughly and watch out for other players. Once you build a module, you receive a one-time bonus for your next actions (or a penalty for your opponents).
The game lasts until some of the players build the required number of modules.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

boardgame: Tamashii: Chronicle of Ascend – Catie's Diary
description:
With this expansion, we will go back several years before the action of the main storyline in order to find, get to know, and convince Catie on our side.

This expansion introduces a new body to the game, a unique scenario, special Data tokens, new tiles and a deck of Catie's Diary cards.

boardgame: Tamashii: Chronicle of Ascend – Forbidden Chapter
description:
This expansion introduces a new story that tells about some deeply hidden secrets that are strongly unfavorable to ASCEND.

The machines will do everything in their power to prevent players from discovering these uncomfortable stories. Contrary to the main storyline, the adventures of Forbidden Chapter mostly take place in provinces and smaller villages, avoiding the neon lights of big cities.

This expansion includes:

     4 completely new scenarios
     1 special AI who has his own unique rules for moving around the map
     6 new bodies
     10 map tiles with a new graphic design, including a new start tile
     a new type of hacking tokens

boardgame: Polyversal: UN Quick Reaction Force Expansion
description:
One of five initial Battlegroups of miniatures and pre-designed Combatant Tiles for Polyversal.

The UN Quick Reaction Force is a mixed land and air-based force that brings sleek anti-gravity tanks to the battlefield, fearsome tracked Encegon Plasma tanks, mobile infantry carried into battle by Wombat transports, Ocelot and Quoll Support Tanks, and light Janus walkers for scouting and suppression. Advanced Leehark VTOLs provide air support for the UN as the Battlegroup conducts peacekeeping operations worldwide.

This set is a balanced 750-point UN Battlegroup we currently offer for Polyversal, loaded with amazing miniatures and pre-designed die-cut Combatant Tiles. Featuring miniatures from Brigade Models, Plasmablast Games, and TT Combat (formerly Hawk Wargames), it provides a great way to quickly get a coherent force on the table for the UN.

—description from the publisher

The UN Peacekeepers Expansion is a standalone Battlegroup including 6mm and 10mm models (Ares) and Combatant Tiles for use with Polyversal's UN faction. This Battlegroup totals 750 points if fielded with standard psychological profiles. The UN calls upon the Peacekeepers whenever a heavier response is needed for any battle or uprising. Land-based, the battlegroup is anchored by a pair of Maarghast Heavy Assault Tanks with Twin Heavy Plasma Guns and Missiles (Plasmablast Games) and two Ares Walkers (Hawk Wargames/TT Combat). Three Encegon Tanks with Twin Heavy Plasma Guns (Plasmablast Games) provide medium fire support coverage. Three Phestorm AA tanks (Plasmablast Games) provide anti-air support in the absence of covering UN aircraft. 40 Industrial Infantry (Dark Realm Miniatures) are carried into battle by three Ghizar Recon APCs (Plasmablast Games).

This set is a balanced 750-point UN Battlegroup we currently offer for Polyversal, loaded with amazing miniatures and pre-designed die-cut Combatant Tiles. Featuring miniatures from Plasmablast Games, Dark Realm Miniatures, and TT Combat (formerly Hawk Wargames), it provides a great way to quickly get a coherent force on the table for the UN.

—description from the publisher

The OPFOR Minutemen battlegroup is a mixed land and air-based force that opposes the UN. Led by hulking Siafu walkers (Microworld Games) and Missile Drones (Microworld Games) with air cover from powerful transport-capable Dragonfly VTOLs (Microworld Games), Catroux APCs (Brigade Models) deliver infantry to the fight. Ratel wheeled tanks (Brigade Models) and Wolfbite Missile Trikes (Plasmablast Games) provide protection and recon for the group.

This set is a balanced 750-point OPFOR Battlegroup we currently offer for Polyversal, loaded with amazing miniatures and pre-designed die-cut Combatant Tiles. Featuring miniatures from Brigade Models, Plasmablast Games, and Dark Realm Miniatures, it provides a great way to quickly get a coherent force on the table for the OPFOR.

—description from the publisher

The OPFOR Texas Militia Expansion is a standalone Battlegroup including 6mm models and Combatant Tiles for use with Polyversal's Opposition Forces (OPFOR) faction. This Battlegroup totals 750 points if fielded with standard psychological profiles. The OPFOR Texas Militia has its roots in the former US state of Texas and often operates far from home. The UN has learned to tread carefully when encountering the elusive and well-organized members of the Texas Militia. OPFOR forces around the globe rally behind this growing group of men and women who fight and win strategic victories against UN forces.

This land-based battlegroup is led by three Growler Infantry Fighting Vehicles (Microworld Games). Support from three Warthog AA/AP tanks (Microworld Games) provides balanced firepower and mobility. Heavy firepower comes courtesy of two Vorace Walkers (Dark Realm Miniatures). Six fast Landhawk Scout Vehicles (Microworld Games) provide advance recon for the main body in most terrain. Scarab Armored Personnel Carriers (Microworld Games) protect and transport the 40 Pax Arcadia Standard Infantry (Dark Realm Miniatures) to any location.

This set is a balanced 750-point UN Battlegroup we currently offer for Polyversal, loaded with amazing miniatures and pre-designed die-cut Combatant Tiles. Featuring miniatures from Microworld Games and Dark Realm Miniatures, it provides a great way to quickly get a coherent force on the table for the OPFOR.

—description from the publisher

The Mercenary Balkenkreüz GmbH Expansion is a standalone Battlegroup including 6mm models and Combatant Tiles for use with Polyversal's Mercenaries (MERCs) faction or to supplement the highest bidders among UN or OPFOR factions. This Battlegroup totals 750 points if fielded with standard psychological profiles. Led by the feared and well-connected recluse Victor Köenig, Germany-based Balkenkreüz GmbH employs only the most reliable and relentless mercenaries money can buy. Known to work for any group willing to pay enough, the mere mention of Köenig's involvement in an operation inspries confidence in those hiring him and anxiety in those who must face him.

This mixed land and air battlegroup uses high-tech anti-gravity drives on all vehicles to float above the battlefield for enhanced mobility. The massive and awe-inspiring Valgaross (Plasmablast Games) provides incredible firepower and intimidation as the center of the group. The group relies upon light and fast protection from four Nizzlebats and three Jagons (Plasmablast Games) to flank the sides of the Valgaross and provide recon. The devastating twin plasma cannons of two Gammaran tanks (Plasmablast Games) provide medium fire support as two Dralke fighters (Plasmablast Games) streak overhead. Four Zargan Armored Personnel Carriers (Plasmablast Games) provide transportation for the 40 Operators (Special Forces Infantry, Dark Realm Miniatures). A truly versatile and powerful Battlegroup, these well-equipped mercenaries are ready for action- once the payment clears.

"If you are willing to pay, then we are willing to fight." -Victor Köenig

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Tamashii: Chronicle of Ascend – Stretch Goals: Lost Pages
description:
This box includes new bodies, new district tiles, and four-legged companions who help you in exploring the world.

You also get new scenarios and bosses. Another functionality is the new “Living City” mechanic. Living city are random events that will diversify the game and affect specific locations.

EXPAND YOUR TAMASHII EXPERIENCE: Unlock an array of exciting stretch goals with the Lost Pages expansion, enhancing your Tamashii adventures.
UNVEIL LOST SECRETS: Delve into the mysteries of the Lost Pages, revealing hidden lore, powerful artifacts, and captivating characters.
NEW ADVENTURES AWAIT: Embark on thrilling quests with additional scenarios and challenges, offering endless hours of immersive gameplay.
ENRICHED ARTWORK & CONTENT: Immerse yourself in the enriched artwork and content of Lost Pages, crafted with attention to detail.
FOR TAMASHII ENTHUSIASTS: Perfect for Tamashii enthusiasts and collectors, this expansion elevates the excitement of the gaming experience.

boardgame: Nimitz: A Tabletop Game of Naval Battles in the Second World War
description:
Nimitz is a tabletop game of battles across the seas and oceans of the Second World War. Command squadrons and entire fleets as you zoom in for intense surface actions or zoom out to play entire campaigns across hundreds of miles of ocean and land. Choose one of five major navies: Britain, Germany, Italy, Japan, or the United States.

Create sea battles or fictional scenarios based upon famous historical campaigns. Make decisions that move units on, below, and above the sea.

The basic Nimitz game can be played by two or more players in 1-2 hours.

The Halsey campaign system can accommodate many players and can be as large in scale as your imagination permits.

boardgame: Mini Crimes: The Sign of a Paw
description:
In your hands you have an investigative case that you can play alone or with friends. It takes little space, a bit of time and a lot of ingenuity. 
But remember, one case leads to another!

Six chapters for six compact boxes to carry with you in every situation: the box itself contains the rules of the game, and along with the deck of cards contained in it, it will be enough to get all the clues necessary to the resolution!

Mini Crimes is a cooperative investigative game in which you'll put your observation and deduction skills to the test to solve a mysterious case. You'll analyse a crime scene and up to 10 clues. 
Choose carefully and solve the mystery!

In Mini Crimes: Metterci lo zampino (Mini Crimes: The sign of a paw), on one side, an apparently impossible heist at the museum of ancient history and, on the other, an artist of cat burglary spotted in the same city. Coincidences?

—description from the publisher

In your hands you have an investigative case that you can play alone or with friends. It takes little space, a bit of time and a lot of ingenuity. 
But remember, one case leads to another!

Six chapters for six compact boxes to carry with you in every situation: the box itself contains the rules of the game, and along with the deck of cards contained in it, it will be enough to get all the clues necessary to the resolution!

Mini Crimes is a cooperative investigative game in which you'll put your observation and deduction skills to the test to solve a mysterious case. You'll analyse a crime scene and up to 10 clues. 
Choose carefully and solve the mystery!

In Mini Crimes: Copia imperfetta (Mini Crimes: Imperfect Copy), the owner of a camera company was found dead in his office. It’s time to zoom on all the details.
