boardgame: Memoria Bluff: Pee-Poo / Vomit promo card
description: A promo card offered during the Ulule Crowdfunding campain. No special effect except telling memories about pee, poo or vomit.

boardgame: Memoria Bluff: Squeaky bed / Condom promo card
description:
A promo card offered during the Ulule Crowdfunding campain.
Allows you to remind and tell a memory about a condom or a squeaky bed (no special effect).
Wiem lepiej: Piłka nożna is an expansion for Polish version of Bezzerwizzer game. It contains 60 questions about football and one category tile.
Wiem lepiej: Wielkie miasta is an expansion for Polish version of Bezzerwizzer game. It contains 60 questions about big cities and one category tile.

Wiem lepiej: Niezapomniane filmy is an expansion for Polish version of Bezzerwizzer game. It contains 60 questions about 
movies and one category tile.
Wiem lepiej: Wynalazki i odkrycia is an expansion for Polish version of Bezzerwizzer game. It contains 60 questions about inventions and discoveries and one category tile.

boardgame: どれかな？うみうしる (Which one? Sea Slug)
description:
Reasoning Card Game

Players are divided into [one respondent] and [supporters].
Respondents should cooperate with supporters who give hints in order to find [Atari's Sea Slug] among many sea slugs.
[Respondent] who was able to find Atari and [Supporter] who finally led to Atari got 1 point each.
One round is until the respondent finds Atari or all loses, and the
game ends when the respondent has completed a round for all players (completed all rounds).
The player with the most points wins!

Conde Col launched a challenge: assemble a card sandwich without having ingredients in hand. Let's go! Call the gang and devour this game several times.

—description from the designer

O Conde Col lançou um desafio: monte um sanduíche de cartas sem ficar com ingredientes na mão. Vamos lá! Chame a turma e devore este jogo várias vezes.

—description from the designer (Spanish)

Players will try to win nearest elections. Their task will be to get more political support than their opponents. Players will use both legal and illegal actions to get voter`s support. 
To win you have to destroy your opponents reputation and get as much support as you can.

Вибори ("Elections") is a humorous card game depicting usual political campaign. The game focuses primarily on creating from 1 to 3-part political campaign for each round. Players have from 4 to 7 rounds to win as much voters tokens as they can.

—description from the designer

Porta das Lendas is a board game that brings together legends from Brazilian folklore. During the game, you will open doors, collect flowers and other items to be the first to assemble a clan of legends. But be careful, not all of them are for fun...

—description from the designer

Porta das Lendas é um jogo de tabuleiro que reúne lendas do folclore brasileiro. Durante a partida, você vai abrir portas, coletar flores e outros itens para ser o primeiro a montar um clã de lendas. Mas tenha cuidado, pois nem todas estão para brincadeira...

—description from the designer (Portuguese)

Get ready to enter the tranquil and picturesque world of Apple Grove! As an apple farmer, you'll roll the dice to determine which types of apple trees you can plant in your own personal orchard. But beware - the unique color wheel mechanic means you'll need to think strategically about which trees to place next to each other to avoid being penalized and forced to build fences.

As you grow your orchard, you'll collect juicy apples and score points by placing trees of the same color next to each other. With the selection of each tree, you'll need to make careful decisions about which trees to plant and where to place them, in order to create the most fruitful apple grove.

With beautiful artwork and immersive gameplay, Apple Grove offers a unique and relaxing experience that's perfect for a fun evening with friends and family. So gather together, roll the dice, and get ready to experience the excitement and challenge of growing your very own Apple Grove!

—description from the publisher

This is a card (and dice) game for 2-5 players (Solo mode is possible too). Players must travel trough various locations of Transylvania, in order to locate and destroy Count Orlock’s (aka Nosferatu) coffins, a vampire who preys on the wife of his estate agent , before the latter manages to reach the town of Wisborg, bringing with him the plague, the black death. The game is inspired by the 1922  movie “Nosferatu: A Symphony of Horror” a silent German Expressionist horror film (now in public domain) directed by F. W. Murnau.

The game is played in rounds with a day and night phase. During the day players play cards from their hand to move, obtain resources and search different  locations of Transylvania to find the hidden coffin's. In the night phase however their endeavor becomes dangerous. According to the cards drawn, may be attacked by wild animals, bandits and even Nosferatu himself.

In the same time the vampire travels in the opposite way heading to Wisborg in order to bite and make Helen, Hutters wife, his eternal bride. Nosferatu is accompanied by swarms of rats that will infect the entire town with the deadly black plague.

In order to save their town, players must fight to stop the vampire, retaining alongside their human state. Otherwise they may be coverted to possessed or vampires and attack their fellow players.

-description from designer

boardgame: Aliens: Another Glorious Day in the Corps – We're in the Pipe, Five by Five
description:
"Initiate release sequencer on my mark, three, two, one, Mark!"

"Two-four-oh.  Nominal to profile."

"We're in the pipe, five by five.  Strap yourselves in boys, we're in for some chop."

This expansion brings the Sulaco's dropship crew to Aliens: Another Glorious Day in the Corps cooperative survival game.  Fly the friendly skies as you fight off alien stowaways while attempting to rescue the survivors of the disaster in the air processing plant.  On the ground, the two crew members provide technical support for your Marines in Campaign missions and Bug Hunts, along with new pilot and quartermaster experience tracks.

Meanwhile, the marines discover four new Alien variants, The Brute, Guard, Scout and Skulker, which add a new dimension to the Xenomorph hive's urge to absorb the occupants of Hadley's Hope.

Santorini: The Riddle of the Sphinx is a co-operative expansion for Santorini that adapts the competitive experience into an ongoing adventure that players navigate together.

In this expansion comprised of a series of scenarios called "riddles", players will seek to complete tactical objectives to solve the riddles. However, if players exhaust their supply of Santorini tower pieces before an objective is completed, the riddle remains unsolved and must be played again.

Each successfully solved riddle allows players to progress on the adventure map and unlock new gods to add into future riddles.

Can you complete the full adventure and solve the riddle of the Sphinx?

—description from the publisher

description:
This pack contains 15 additional missions (46-60) that can be played seamlessly with the B 17 Twenty five mission base game.

—description from the designer

The space race is heating up once again, and governments have issued their challenge: Develop a viable Mars program in four years. The catch? Your government will supply only a fixed amount of funding per year. The rest of your budget and the success of your mission depend on the success of the projects you fund...but you're not the only one in this race!

4 Years to Mars is card-based, with individual player boards to track mission objective progress. The game can be played solo, co-operatively or competitively.

In the first of four program years in the competitive game, players spend their "space bucks" (Spucks) on funding research in their four space station experiment bays. In the following years, players fund projects to further their mission objectives and provide additional funding for the next program year. Research topics reduce the cost of corresponding projects. In the base game, action cards can be played to help your program as well. After four years, government funding ceases, then the player with the most points wins, with points being awarded for progress in each of the eight mission objectives. A bonus is given to the first and second players to complete the entire objective.

In the co-operative game, all players work together to complete the eight mission objectives before the end of the four program years. In order to advance a mission objective, players must work together to fund projects that create sets of mission objective icons. For example, to advance your rover mission objective once in a five-player game, you must have a set of five cards with mission objective icons. Unlike sets for the competitive game, cards can be used for multiple icon sets.

In the single player game, the goal is also to complete the eight mission objectives before the end of the four program years, but some cards are removed from the market after every round, so you must plan for the next year.

All of the projects in the game are based on real NASA projects and products.

-description from designer

boardgame: Family Feud Survey Showdown Card Game
description:
Draw a question card, the first player to shout their answer out gets the first chance to win the face-off. Each incorrect answer gets an x card. You win by being the first team to collect 5 Family Feud survey cards.

Contents:
49 Question cards
3 "X" cards
2 Instruction cards

Small - 11 Allied vs. 7 German units - hex-and-counter northwest Europe 1944-45 campaign game starting with D-Day, using the factor differential plus Special Factor system of Postcard Barbarossa! and (Banner of War's beautiful edition of that,) Unternehmen Barbarossa - possible Pressed Attack *and* Desperate (No Retreat!) Defense - which makes it unpredictably good as a solitaire game too.

Army level, monthly turns. Possible early May 1944 D-Day, risking bad weather.
Shore bombardment and Allied air supremacy in support.
Atlantic Wall, bocage, Ardennes, and Siegfried Line.  Special breakthrough/breakout properties for 3rd U.S. Army, 2nd British, and German 5. and 6. SS Panzer Armies.
OKW Strategic Reserve to be sent to the Russian Front (requiring Western Allies to achieve their victory objectives a month sooner) or to be used for surprise attack in the West.
Allied 1st Airborne Corps.
Battles are fought within hexes.

—description from the designer

description:
The year is 1959.  The next decade will unleash a cascading series of events that will catapult the Cold War into a new, farther-flung region of the world. Twilight Struggle: South Asian Monsoon is the latest stand-alone two player game in the Twilight Struggle series.  This game covers the era from the Tibetan Insurrection in China to the normalization of relations between the People’s Republic and the United States.  It is a four turn game, longer than Twilight Struggle: Red Sea, but much shorter than a full game of Twilight Struggle.

Twilight Struggle: South Asian Monsoon is intended to provide experienced players of Twilight Struggle with a new, more nuanced challenge.  The nations represented in this game – in South Asia, the Indian Ocean and along China’s Asian periphery - are more remote and independent minded.  All of the countries represented joined the Non-Aligned Movement.  Unlike other parts of the globe, the United States and the Soviet Union can only project military force into the region with difficulty.  In short, this is the region of the Cold War where nations felt emboldened to assert their independence and resist the bi-polar world that the superpowers had created elsewhere.

Twilight Struggle players will be asked to contend with newly independent countries, the relationship between the Non-Aligned Movement and increased tensions along the DEFCON track. The game incorporates a new system called “Engagement Actions” and players must plan with the future of China after Mao.  The ability of players to master these new factors will be the key to victory in Twilight Struggle: South Asian Monsoon.  The game also highlights the history of many giants of the Cold War era that receive less attention in other parts of the Twilight Struggle system: Nehru, Mao, the Dali Lama, Zhou Enlai, Indira Gandhi, Khrushchev and more all make their presence felt in this pivotal region of the globe.

The game featuresbackward and forward compatibility.  Cards from South Asian Monsoon will be usable in Twilight Struggle, and cards from Twilight Struggle will be usable in South Asian Monsoon.

-description from designer

description:
Jefe De Jefes is a Mexican Drug Cartel inspired card game where players run their own cartel and destroy the competition to become the ultimate drug lord. In order to win, players use their action cards strategically, spend loads of drug money, and negotiate deals with opposing cartels. In the end, there can only be one boss.

—description from the designer

Everyone get in and close the doors! One colorful wagon after the other is attached to the funny animal train. The order is determined by the large colored die. Which train will be the first to leave? Thanks to the exciting game principle and the popular railway theme, the basic colors are memorized in a flash. The extra stable puzzle pieces are perfectly adapted to the needs of children and are also ideal for free play - who puzzles the longest train?

—description from the publisher (translated)

boardgame: Dungeon of Norub: a pen & paper dungeon crawler
description:
A pen & paper dungeon crawler for 1 or 2 players. You face a 5 level dungeon, which rooms are filled with monsters, traps and special features.

Main features:


     You can even play it on a small notebook
     No map drawing
     No eraser needed
     Fast combat: a single roll could end a fight (if you're lucky enough)
     No printer needed: character sheet is very simple


In order to play Dungeon of Norub, you'll need:


     a paper sheet: even a notebook is big enough
     a pen
     some dice: 1d20, 1d10, 1d4, 1d6.


The gamemplay is quick and the inventory management is minimalistic. A full game lasts about 30-45 minutes. It has a roll & write feel, due to the fast nature of the game.

Four levels of difficulty are included in the rules, and you can easily adjust it to your own difficulty level.

—description from the designer

Players build up the village of Jerusalem into a Town by placing businesses, public services, recreational facilities, places of worship, extra roads and houses in an attempt to climb the Social ladder the highest (the gaining of 'footprints', which are VPs). Players may be able to demolish each other's work, vote on new Municipal structures (a Cathedral, an Airfield, a Reservoir - giant pieces that overlay whole sections of the landscape) and - generally - create a satirical vision of a typical English Town.

The playing area is a freeform space comprising a river and a Roman road dividing the area into each player's home sector containing their Mansion. Buildings can appear in any sector - the choice of the player guided by their own goals and the goals on shared higher-level buildings.

Jerusalem is a sandbox world.

—description from the designer

How really old are you ? is a hilarious and revealing game with more than 300 funny questions designed in order to discover your real age.

—description from the publisher (translated)

¿Cuántos años tienes realmente? es un juego desternillante y revelador con más de 300 entretenidas preguntas pensadas para diagnosticar tu auténtica edad.

—description from the publisher

Taming the Slugiraffe is a solo roleplaying game where players take on the role of an insignificant background fantasy creature as it journeys into THE PIT to find the fabled SLUGIRAFFE. Can you survive the PIT and gather what you need to TAME THE SLUGIRAFFE?

What's it like?

It's a bit like Dark Souls if you were playing as a Pikachu trying to catch a Snorlax. The monsters are nasty, the world is dark, but you're just hoping to find a big slug-giraffe thing to make the life of your people much easier.

What's the set up?

You play as a GIBLET, a small background fantasy creature – the kind of thing that gets kicked off a cliff (without being hurt) for comic relief in films and TV. You and your people like a happy life, but there is a story a mystical creature that once would ferry and care for your tribes. You wish to return your people to this bountiful golden age – but to do this you must find, and tame, a SLUGIRAFFE. The only place they exist is within THE PIT. You, brave GIBLET, must descend to find the creature, and return a hero.

The System

The game uses a random exploration system and simple dice combat and event resolution using 2D6 (originally based on the excellent TUNNEL GOONS system by Nate Treme). Depending on the monsters you defeat or friends you make along the way you'll roll a different sized dice on the D20 loot table. Naturally, the most beneficial items are those in the top range of dice rolls.

Players draw their progress through the pit. Each location has it's own interaction or event which might be useful for gaining the resources to find and tame the SLUGIRAFFE. They will need to work with the pit dwellers to improve their chances of survival by trading items, performing rituals, and eventually finding a way to track the mythical creature.

Exploration

Exploration is a case of creating paths and exploring new biomes and settlements using the provided map. Players roll for exploring biomes, and if they're lucky they'll find a settlement. Once a connection has been made between two locations the path can be travelled freely. If you want to find somewhere new however, you'll have roll for exploration again, and deal with whatever encounters lurk there.

Combat

Combat is deadly and ruthless – and your choices about what you carry with you will make all the difference to your survival. Simply beat the number requested on 2D6, plus any useful items, and victory is yours. Failure means losing an item, or an item slot. If you run out of item slots, you've been squished. On the plus side, if you fail your quest, they'll just send another one of you. You are, after all, only a GIBLET.

Taming

One you have gathered your gear and found the location of the SLUGIRAFFE, it's simply a case of chasing it down and taming it. Your weapons are no good here, as the ancient beast is indestructable. The game is a balance between arming yourself for your survival, and being equipped to capture that ancient creature towards the end of the adventure.

The Zine

We'll be printing a 16 page zine with all the rules needed to play the game solo. The zine will be in colour throughout. You'll have to bring your own dice, writing implement and sense of foolish adventure.

The zine will also come with character sheets and token sheets printed within for easy photocopying, Digital versions of these will be available to all backers.

—description from the designer

El fin de este juego es conseguir licenciarse el primero.

El juego de la mili sigue en orden cronológico el servicio militar: Desde la entrada, pasando por la jura de bandera, ascensos, maniobras, etc... Hasta la licencia.

The goal of that game is getting the first in having the licence.

The mili's game follows a chronological order in millitary service: from entering, pledge allegiance to the flat,promotions, manoeuvres... untill the licence.

boardgame: 1001 Odysseys: A Day at the Beach Microadventure
description:
Promotional 3 card micro adventure given out at GenCon 2022.

From the publisher:
"Sample this 3-Card Micro-Adventure to get a taste of our collaborative science-fantasy game 1001 Odysseys.
In the full version of the game, players operate the mission controls of the spaceship Odyssey to explore ten beautiful planets and read entertaining stories featuring fun, fascinating, and downright weird characters."

A compilation of 4 expansions for Fishing Lessons:


     Fishing Lessons: Family Friends
     Fishing Lessons: Fabled Fish
     Fishing Lessons: Weather
     Fishing Lessons: Leland's Tackle Box

The game contains 14 dice in different colours, as well as a description of actions triggered by black dice..
The black dice trigger effects that are useful to the active player or harmful to the other players.
E.g. you can take a dice from another player, reroll all untaken dice and take all 3's.

—user summary

Have fun racing around the galaxy to collect infinity stones with your 3D Marvel playing pieces (5 figures available).

You and your team need to reach Earth with more infinity stones than Thanos on his glove

In this coop game you will fight Thanos to retrieve from him the stones.

Players will throw the dice to move forward on the path to earth but be aware some steps may generate actions such as losing a stone or retrieve a stone from Thanos or fighting Thanos.
When each player reaches Earth, you have to fight Thanos a last time. Throw a dice, add your tokens’ to improve your strength and compare it to his throw, the highest can stole a stone for the other one.
When each player has ended their turn, if The Avengers own more stones than Thanos, they WIN!

1A contains cards, which show coloured numbers.
The unusual thing is: The cards are printed on both sides, and both sides have their meaning for the game, of course, depending on which card is discarded. The sum of the two numbers on top of the pile is always calculated, and this determines the action that is carried out; for example, one may wish for a color, the opponent must skip or must draw cards.

—user summary

Bitte schön! contains dice in different colours.
The black die is rolled, which determines which number of dice the active player may take. They may re-roll and keep the gained score or lose all new points.
The colours of the other dice allow special actions.

—user summary

Crash Octopus: Pink Pirates is an expansion to Crash Octopus that contains two modules:

Module 1: With the arrival of the Pink Pirate, who is plotting to steal the treasure, Crash Octopus becomes even wilder! If you add the Pink Pirate, up to five people can play. One player acts as a pirate and aims to steal the treasure of the other players.

Module 2: With the addition of the "Kid Octopus", chaos breaks out on the sea. When used in conjunction with the Octopus Head, the Kid Octopus Head makes the Octopus' attacking action even more threatening.

The Crash Octopus Pink Pirates Expansion includes the following components:
4 x Roses
4 x Wine Bottles
1 x Small Octopus
1 x Pink Pirate Ship
1 x Octo-Cannon
2 x Pirate Flags

Error Crash! contains cards with colours, numbers and symbols.
Similarly to "Mau Mau", careds are placed on top of each other using colour and number.
The symbols trigger effects, e.g. Drawing cards, choosing a suit, sitting out.

The cards are printed on both sides; numbers on one side are black and on the other side, they are white. The cards, thanks to a special symbol, have to change the played side regularly, and hand cards as well as draw and discard piles have to be turned.

There are two variants of the game.

In the first variant, you lose after 10 rounds of not being able to play a hand card.

In the second variant, you play for six rounds; the player with most scored points wins.

—user summary

Error contains 60 cards, which show numbers in different colours.
Everyone places one card face down in front of them and one card is revealed from the deck, which is the mission card. Now everyone reveals their discarded cards. The object of the game is to get as close as possible to the mission card.

Each card also contains a symbol, which is processed before comparing the numbers. An arrow, for example, makes all discarded cards move clockwise to the next person, a swap symbol allows to draw a card from the deck and place it on top of any of the discarded cards. In total, 5 different symbols make the game fast-paced and unpredictable.

—user summary

The year is 2230. Giant City-States known as the Great Civitas have replaced nations. You control your own cyberpunk city and choose to rule it as you wish. Entice the planet's people to flock to your utopia or manipulate, steal, conquer, crush your way to victory.

CIVITAS 2230 is a strategic game for gaming groups that love tactics, political swindling, challenging scenarios and total story immersion. A "learn as you play" game that can be tailored to fit the time you have at the table, as well as your choice of cooperative, versus. or solo modes.

The game is controlled by a rigid Game Round structure. Players play out from their starting rectangular Presidential Mats and build their city by placing the Hexes they can afford to build. Hexes provide further bonus resources (Reputation, Ether, Ion, Material Cubes, or Policy Points) to allow for more Hexes to be drawn and placed in later turns, thus increasing the size and power of the player's city.

Workers are placed to activate certain Hexes, and unique characters, known as CNXpats, provide more extraordinary bonuses and special abilities to manipulate play in favour of the player who activates them. Other elements like drawing further Hexes, bidding for policies and earning additional bonuses for a player's total population, make up the rest of The Game Round.

Players win the game by reaching either 25 or 50 overall Reputation depending on the scenario.

—description from the designer

Adventure alone, together or as enemies through the endless Labyrinth beneath the Highlands of ancient Alhba (Scotland). This pocket-sized adventure will have you and up to three friends or enemies battling ancient Kings for magical weapons, facing down hordes of undead souls and beasts, haggling with the Hag o' the Winter for your very life, using the Labyrinth's everchanging passageways for your advantage, whispering mystic incantations in old Gaelic, standing on the edge of the Eternal Chasm on the brink of being subsumed by Evil itself... and all in your lunch break! If you love the moody storytelling aspect of classic fantasy RPGs, and easy rules that make for fast-flowing action thick with atmosphere, you will adore this game. Best played on an old wooden table under candlelight with a tankard of Scottish ale or honeymead.

For solo gamers, it is most fun to play Labyrinthians with two of the provided characters, who you can use to come to your aid in times of high peril, attain the characters' personal quests, or vie against in moments of merciless betrayal or greed.

For those who want a longer game or want to enjoy Labyrinthians as a multi-session campaign, there are expansions to the core game too. The Labyrinth has three new levels: the sewers, the earthen and the magma levels. And there is a three-part prequel wilderness campaign: The Woodland Way, the Bogs of Moorh, the snowy path to The Secret Tree, where lies the entrance to the Labyrinth itself.

—description from the designer

Hanamikoji: Kickstarter Goodies 2 are the stretch goals unlocked during the Kickstarter campaign of Hanamikoji: Geisha's Road launched on October 26, 2021.

The goodies include:
3 Special Guests
2 Item Cards
5 Geisha Powers
1 Scoring Sheets
4 Special Postcards

boardgame: The Battle of Malta: Operation Pedestal 1942
description: The Battle of Malta: Operation Pedestal 1942 is a two-player game of struggle of Malta Convoy in August 1942. As the commander of the British fleet, you aim to deliver the convoy to Malta with as little damage as possible. Or as an Axis commander, aim to annihilate the WS21S convoy and its escort fleet. Winning/losing is determined by the number of transport ships that have arrived and the number of escort ships that have been sunk.

Battle of Sandepu, 1905 is a two-player game that simulates the Battle of Sandepu, January 25-29, 1905, during the Russo-Japanese War.
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Battle_of_Sandepu

The unit size of the game is a regiment, and the goal is to recreate the appearance of the battle from January 26, the day after the Japanese army abandoned Sandepu, using simple rules using cards.

One plays Grippenberg, the commander of the Russian 2nd Manchurian Army, who is ordered to attack Sandepu, and the other plays Naofumi Tatsumi of the IJA 8th Division, who is ordered to defend this area.

The Russian commander-in-chief, Kuropatkin, was not very enthusiastic about the offensive due to factors such as the weather, lack of supplies, and when the Nogi's IJA Third Army, which had taken Port Arthur's fortress, would come to help. Therefore, the Russian player needs to proceed with the strategy while looking at Kuropatkin's mood, and the Japanese player has to deal psychological damage to Kuropatkin as one of the strategies.

boardgame: あにまる亭のメイドちゃん (Cafe Animals)
description:
Cafe Animals is a trick-taking game where you want to score the most points.

The game uses a deck of cards featuring several suits and the same range of numbers, as well as a gray suit with higher numbers than the other suits. It is a "must follow" game, but the highest card of the lead suit, or the gray suit, wins.

Additionally, the game includes one extra card for each suit, without a number.  One of these is inserted into the deck and will serve as the timer for the hand.  The others go next to the deck.  The winner of a trick can choose to take the cards played to the trick as points or take one of the suit cards. While the suit cards are visible, the smallest number will win in tricks which were lead by a card of one of those colors.  Additionally, taking such a card alters a players scoring calculation.

boardgame: Taira Ships, Minamoto Horses: Genpei Naval Records
description:
Taira Ships, Minamoto Horses: Genpei Naval Records is a board wargame based on naval battles from February 1184 to April 1185 during the Genpei War.

In general, the Minamoto clan and the Taira clan are the focus of attention in the Genpei War, but in this game, the Seto Inland Sea's "Hometown Navy" is the main character.

Both the Taira clan and the Minamoto clan must continue to fight in order to gain their allies, who are sensitive to the consequences of the war.

In naval battles at that time, when neither nautical charts nor systematic information was maintained, it was important to have a deeper knowledge of the "local sea".

In this game as well, you can experience how having a local navy on your side who knows the local sea will greatly affect the outcome of a war.
family: History: Conflicts of the Heian period – Japan

description:
Masakado's Last Battle is a two-player game of the Battle of Shii, Taira no Masakado's last battle during the Tengyō no Ran, a brief medieval Japanese conflict from 935 to 940.
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tengy%C5%8D_no_Ran

50 counters are divided into 12 Masakado's units, 29 Imperial Government's units, and 9 markers.

Each hex represents about 50 meters. Each unit represents about 300 soldiers.

Issue game of "Bushi Life" #4.

Thorny Issue at Jieting is 5th game of War Drum Games' "pocket war" series. This mini-game simulates the Battle of Jieting in 228, the last battle of Zhuge Liang's first Northern Expedition during the Three Kingdoms period in China.

50 counters are divided into 16 Shu Han units, 25 Cao Wei units and other markers.

Rulebook is online only.

boardgame: 锋从磨砺出 (The Sword's Edge Emerges from the Sharpening)
description:
The Sword's Edge Emerges from the Sharpening is a two-player game of the People's Liberation Army's hypothetical invasion of Taiwan.

The counters include about 100 "red" units, about 50 "blue" units, and over 540 markers.

The map depicts a part of Tapei City, New Taipei City and Taoyuan City.

Each unit represents a company/platoon. Each hex represents 1km. Each turn represents 15 minutes.
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

boardgame: 冲冠一怒 (One Wave of Headgear-lifting Anger)
description:
（冲冠一怒）One Wave of Headgear-lifting Anger is a 2-3 player game set in the battle for ShanHai Pass, at the final days of Ming-dynasty. The 3 factions of Shun, Qing and Ming’s remanent GuanNing Army engages in the delicate 3-sided struggle.

In March 1644, Li Zicheng’s Shun-dynasty breaches Ming’s capital Beijing, and Emperor Chongzhen commits suicide seeing the collapse of his dynasty. Noticing this, the Qing-dynasty decided to seize the opportunity and attempt to enter inner China. Now both the Shun and Qing have their eyes fixed on the strategically critical Shanhai Pass on the Great Wall. At that time, the remanent of Ming’s elite GuanNing Army under the command of Wu Sangui is stationed at the pass, and will become a force that could easily tipped the balance.

In the game, players activate their generals by playing the respective cards. Each general can activate a set number of units, often only the units under his direct command. When unwilling to play a card, player can also conduct a basic action instead, which is weaker but allow you to preserve your hand. However, once 5 basic actions (shared among all players) are conducted, the turn would immediately end. Players must plan carefully to control the flow of battle to the favorable pace.

Components:
1 A2 size mounted map
62 wooden blocks (50 for units and 12 as markers)
47 counters
24 cards
3 Player panels
16 dice

—description from the publisher (translated)

困兽犹斗 – 日德兰海战（Battle of Jutland） is the 7th game of Kilovolt Design's mini war series, it's a small wargame depicting the famed battle of Juland: the greatest naval engagement of the great war, and probably one of the most studied surface action in human history. The game covers the entire battle from first contact between the battle-cruisers, to the withdrawal of fleets in 9 turns (each turn represents roughly 30 mins). 
Each counter in game represents a squadron of ships, and are separated into 3 major types: capital ships, cruisers and destroyer/torpedo boats. Capital ships have the best firepower and range, their attacks could also suppress the enemy squadron and reduce the accuracy of their fire. Cruisers are fast and nimble, ideal for protecting the battleline from torpedo attacks. The destroyers makes up their lack of firepower by the best mobility and the ability to launch torpedo attacks: those attacks could render the target in a disorganized state as the ships in the squadron attempt to evade the torpedoes and breaks up the battle line, resulting in diminished combat capabilities over the next turn.
Components:
1 Rulebook
1 Player Aid card
1 325x320mm Mounted Map
60 Counters
1 Die

boardgame: 昭昭天明 (Manifest Destiny of Ming)
description:
Manifest Destiny of Ming is a hexagonal wargame set in the final years of the Ming Dynasty. The game aims to find balance between historical authenticity and playability, allowing the player to experience and potentially write a different end for the Ming Dynasty.

The game portrays the entire reign of the Chongzhen Emperor, showcasing the conflict between 3 major factions: The Ming dynasty, the Shun rebels, and the Manchu Qing.

Ming dynasty, even weakened from various famines, floods and economic chaos, still enjoys an overwhelming advantage in resources, which must be used to both contain the peasant uprisers and stalling the Qing invasions simultaneously. At the same time, player must try to please the Chongzhen Emperor by gaining a set number of “victories” each turn, otherwise, Ming generals might be arrested or even executed by the Emperor for neglection of duty.

The Shun rebels consists mainly of weak peasant uprising forces, there are an almost endless supply of them, as natural disasters and the ongoing war resulting in more and more farmers to lose their land and living. As the uprising forces score victories, the rebel forces can upgrade their forces into stronger “rebel bands” or even elite “old bands”. After gathering enough strength, and taking a province capital, the rebel can declare to fund a new dynasty and contest the mandate of heaven from the Ming. By doing so would allow them to gain taxation and recruit stronger units consistently.

The Qing-dynasty (initially Later Jin dynasty), based in Manchu, lacks the number, but have some troops of the highest quality in game. During the early game, player will need to aim at the conquest of Korea and Mongolia, when this is done, this allows the proclamation to found Qing dynasty, and further strengthen their strength by allowing more troop replenishment ability. After this, Qing player’s main aim would be to breach the LiaoXi corridor, which is heavily fortified by Ming-dynasty with multiple fortresses. This will be a prolonged conflict, and sometimes need the player to wait until the Shun rebels has forced Ming-dynasty to draw forces from the LiaoXi garrisons before striking.

The game is centered around the collapse of the Ming-dynasty. As the natural disaster strikes, or when forces plunders settlements, corruption markers will be drawn for the province. These markers would deduct this turn’s income of the Ming player. And when the corruption adds up to a unbearable value for the province, the entire province would collapse and there will be no means for the Ming-dynasty to regain control of this province in game. As more and more province collapses, the mandate of heaven will be lost from the Ming-dynasty, and its forces would become more and more likely to defect after been defeated in battle.

The game have 4 scenarios: 3 short scenarios each lasting 3-4 turns, and 1 full campaign scenario lasting 9 turns, allowing the players to experience the entirety of 18 years of reign of the Chongzhen Emperor.

Components:
1 Mounted Map (840x590mm)
1 Rulebook
7 Player aid cards
560 counters
2 dice

—description from the publisher (translated)

It was the end of Eastern Han Dynasty, which chaos in the court and starvation among people. AD 184, Zhang Jiao the leader of Taiping Sect incited his suffering believers to raise an uprising called Yellow Turban. For a time, the flag of "the sky is dead, and the yellow sky is ready" rose everywhere, sweeping the Central Plains. Start with crusade against the Yellow Turban, co-existence of heroes and the separation of vassals joins the "Three Kingdoms".

The heroes with vivid images and distinct personalities are shining and falling on the stage one after another, and the greatest charm of this era is also bursting out. The story of the Three Kingdoms has been deeply rooted in people's hearts through the records of the Three Kingdoms and the Romance of the Three Kingdoms. It has become a well-known legend of heroes and is still one of the most attractive times in ancient China.

This game uses hexagon map, makes location of each city more precise, bring more flexible for different armies and tactics. In the vast map, the player's strategic vision will be more reflected.

—description from the back of the box (translated)

"Now that the south has been set, and the armor is sufficient, the three armies will be rewarded, and the Central Plains will be set in the north."
"Chu Shi Biao", Zhuge Liang, Prime Minister of the Han Dynasty

The historical simulation board game Kong Ming's Northern Anabasis focuses on Zhuge Kongming, an outstanding statesman and military strategist in Chinese history, the embodiment of universally recognized wisdom, who combines loyalty, filial piety, righteousness, and strategy.
"Kong Ming's Northern Anabasis" is a two-player card-driven board game (CDG), which describes a series of military operations between Shu Han and Cao Wei in 228-234 AD, with the goal of competing for the Guanlong area.

—description from the publisher (translated)

In May 1942, after winning the defense of Moscow, the Soviet army tried to launch a general offensive against the German army. It assembled six army groups and several supporting troops to form two fronts, the Southwest Front and the South Front, and planned to regain the strategically important Kharkov. The Second Battle of Kharkov broke out.

However, in just 20 days, the Soviet army, which had lost the winter bonus, suffered a disastrous defeat in the Second Battle of Kharkov due to command errors and underestimated the strength of the German army, losing nearly 260,000 people.

The defeat was so crushing that the Soviets tried to hide it or avoid talking about it long after the battle ended.

"Schlacht bei Charkow" is an independent work dedicated by Kuro Neko Design Workshop. After at least 3 years of various physical tests, it tries to restore the most exciting Second Battle of Kharkov.

In the game, one side player will play the role of Bock, the commander of the German Army Group South, and the other side player will play the role of the Soviet Southwest Strategic Trio (Commander Timoshenko, Political Commissar Khrushchev, and Chief of Staff Bagramyan).

—description from the publisher (translated)

This mini-expansion includes 6 Animal Companion cards.  These helpful animals will give players an initial benefit during setup, as well as a one-time use action during the game.

Animal cards can be given to all player or just to newer players who want assistance while competing against more experienced players.

Include inside Darwin's Journey: Collector's Edition and was available as an add-on during the Kickstarter campaign for those backing at the base game only pledge level.

The Battle of Gazala was the culmination of Rommel's work on the North African battlefield of World War II, and Rommel was promoted to Field Marshal through this. Relying on his inferior force, he displayed an astonishing command art, and finally conquered Tobruk Fortress and opened the gate to Egypt. The victory of the battle marked the climax of the Axis offensive in North Africa and played the prelude to the Battle of Alamein.

This game is a card-driven game. The two players will play the role of the German-Italian coalition commander: General Erwin Rommel, the commander of the African Panzer Army, and the British commander: General Claude Auchinleck, the commander-in-chief of the British Army in the Middle East.

It was the early morning of May 27, 1942. Rommel successfully deceived the enemy, and just led his army to detour to the rear of the Gazala line of defense. The "Operation Venice" is about to start......

—description from the publisher (translated)

(translation from the text of MoDian's crowdfunding page)

This game represents a series of decisive battles between the Allied forces and the German forces in the Normandy area of France from June to August 1944 when the Allied forces landed in Normandy and marched inland.

Number of players in the game: 2-4 people
2 players: the German forces player and the Allied forces player.
3 players: the German Panzer Group West player, the German 7th Army player and the Allied Forces player. Or: the Allied British 21st Army Group player, the Allied Twelfth United States Army Group player and the German forces player.
4 players: the German Panzer Group West player, the German 7th Army player, the Allied British 21st Army Group player and the Allied Twelfth United States Army Group player.

This work includes the following accessories:
1 map, about 28cm x 38cm in size
About 70 pieces of cardboard counters
About 10 six-sided dice
There is no packing box and no paper rulebook for this game. You need to scan the QR code on the map to download the game rules.

The Dragon and The Bear is a two-player wargame of a hypothetical China-Soviet War in 1969.

One commands People's Liberation Army and the other commands USSR Army.

The map describes the Northeast China (ex-Manchuria) and a part of Russia and Mongolia. Each unit represents a division.

The theme and components look like SPI's classic The East is Red: The Sino Soviet War, but all of the components are originally designed.

The holdup was perfect... A sound plan! Millions in a bag, fleeing by plane, yes, really, a sound one. Everyone begins to relax, but suddenly the engine sputters! Then the plane begins to fall... Yes, the holdup was perfect and they had anticipated everything — everything but enough parachutes to go around…

This expansion for Bahamas adds new playable characters, new action cards, a new turbulence cards module and money bags with holes.

Do you consider yourself an art connoisseur? Or, maybe, you know nothing of fine arts? This game works great for both cases!

Artline is a game of associations based on gorgeous paintings from the world-famous art museums. Current edition includes 96 original masterpieces licensed from the Musée du Louvre in Paris.
During the game, the players develop a common grid of cards on the table and define common features for each row and column.

Each player starts with 5 cards in hand, and the goal is to get rid of them. One card is played per turn, and you may:

1. Just extend a row/column and draw a new card. The card you play has to fit just one feature of a row or a column. Come up with one if there’s none yet!
What could bring a Picasso painting and a classic portrait together? Let's say, a white headdress! Now all the paintings you play in this row or column must contain a person with the white headpiece.

OR

2. Play your card at the intersection and don’t draw a new card. The card you play has to fit both features of a row and a column. Think of one or even both of them!

Your aim is to get rid of cards, but you don't draw a new one only if you manage to place a card that matches both row and column features, so you have to be very attentive, come up with challenging features, and think ahead to play all your cards on the table!

– description from publisher

The 112 cards from Artline Louvre, showing paintings from the museum’s collection, are compatible with Dixit (but have a different back side design).
family: Theme: Fine Art and Art Museums

description:
During a regular workday, bees tirelessly look forward to attending and please their queen in order to gain her sympathy and proximity. To accomplish this, bees go out into the fields and explore them in search of the best flowers, to make the sweetest honey, according to their monarch's tastes.

In Bees with Hammers, players will become worker bees and compete against each other to fulfill the preferences of their queen, regarding a specific group of flowers. To do so, they must obtain the most points possible by placing their hives as close to the queen as possible and strategically considering the flowers used.

The player to obtain the most points at the end of the game will be the one who gains the trust of their monarch.

—description from the designer

This world once devastated by what were called the great calamities, was the spectacle of the most devastating actions.

Years passed and since then peace had prevailed, but the sages of today hid the monsters of yesteryear. Secrets, treasures and characters were sealed and ignorance had become the shield of the peoples who survived.

Namma is an exciting board game that provides an immersive and entertaining gaming experience. With captivating storytelling, immersive music, and a collectible card system, this game delivers an unforgettable gaming experience that will keep you hooked for hours.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Semper Invicta (fan expansion for T.I.M.E Stories)

description:
Welcome to the combat card game where you take on the mantle of a major holiday and duke it out with other holiday mascots - including the big red guy himself - to become the new Reason for the Season.

Players take turns drawing Calendar Cards and performing the actions on the cards. In between Calendar Cards, players can beef up their defenses or attack other players. Once Santa randomly appears, it's an all out brawl for holiday supremacy, with the player who slays Santa being the winner.

boardgame: Ashes Reborn: Red Rains – The Frostwild Scourge
description:
Phoenixborn and Chimera Expansion Decks! The Phoenixborn Rin, Lulu, Aradel and Koji return to battle, this time teamed up against the mighty Chimera; The Frostwild Scourge! Each of these returning Phoenixborn come equipped with a new signature card, and ANY of them can be used with the ALL NEW natural-magic preconstructed deck included.

—description from the publisher
family: Game: Ashes – Rise of the Phoenixborn

description:
THE BATTLE OF MARDAVHAIN - COLLECTIBLE CARD GAME

During the game, players can embark on a journey to the ancient land of Mardavhain, where peace has been disturbed by evil forces, and battles have been raging ever since. Become the strongest and rule the land!

DIFFERENT MODES


     Choose which deck you want to play with. Will you play with the basic deck, a shared deck with your friends, or will you play with your own deck?
     Choose your game mode. In each round, you can draw new character cards, or you can play in survival mode, where each player has a set number of cards, and there are no additional card draws


HOW TO PLAY:


     2-4 players can play on the game board.
     You need to occupy territory from the other players. At the beginning of the game, strategy is important because you need to place character cards for defense and action cards that other players cannot see. Then, the battle for territory begins. The outcome of the battle is decided based on the character cards' health points, attack and defense values, using dice.
     The winner is the one who holds the most or even all the territories.


HOW TO WIN

The game lasts until all the character cards from the deck are used up. The player with the most territories wins the game.

SURVIVOR MODE:


     In this game mode, the setup is the same as in the regular game, but after battles, players cannot draw new character cards. They must continue to play with the cards they used for the attacks.
     In this case, the HP value of the character cards that participated in battle remains the same, and players must continue to fight with these cards.


—description from the designer

boardgame: Kingdom Death: Monster – Banshee Bedlah and Loincloth Promo Cards
description:
This is a set of promo cards that was given out with the Vitanvox miniature, which itself is not part of the game.

The promo centers on the Banshee Dura special rule. It contains one Banshee Bedlah gear card, which confers this rule to the survivor wearing it, a Banshee Loincloth which strengthens the effect, and one Glossary card that describes the rule.

Banshee Dura X
A special rule. At the start of your act, gain (or lose) howling tokens until you have X of them. During your act or a survival opportunity, you may spend 1 howling token to do one of the following: 

     Gain [move], which must be spent immediately, limit once per round.
     Gain [activation], which must be spent immediately, limit once per round.
     Perform a survival action using the howling token in place of survival.
     Gain +1 STR token until the end of the round.

boardgame: Kingdom Death: Monster – Fig Tree/The Plant and the Stone Promo Cards
description: This is a promo set consisting of the Fig Tree Innovation card and the related The Plant and the Stone Settlement Event card. These were included with the Skrelle miniature, which itself is not part of the game.

boardgame: BattleTech: Welcome to the Nebula California
description:
Welcome to the Nebula California (AKA Explorer's Report: California Nebula) offers a few new venues for of-beat BattleTech adventures far from the hustle, bustle, and yet predictable chaos of the Dark Age-era Inner Sphere. Here, far from known civilizations (and from HPGs!), are worlds, societies, and technologies unlike any seen back home! Special rules, back-stories, and yes, even record sheets, are included for a host of new (and hopefully unique) new...things, ready for play in your (really) advanced BattleTech and A Time of War games.

Released as an April Fools product for 2023.

boardgame: BattleTech: Plenty of Room in the Nebula California
description:
Plenty of Room in the Nebula California is a special follow-up to Welcome to the Nebula California, with more off-beat settings for BattleTech adventures far from the hustle, bustle, and cyclic chaos of the Dark Age-era Inner Sphere. Here in this space—where the very laws of logic, physics, and biology seem warped and twisted beyond recognition—worlds, societies, and technologies have arisen among populations long lost to the realms humanity now calls home! Special rules, backstories, and record sheets are included for a host of new (and hopefully unique) … things, ready for play in your (really) advanced BattleTech and A Time of War games.

Released for April Fools 2023

KUGO is a flick based dexterity game made out of all-natural, solid wood. But this isn't just one game. This is a box to make lots of games, You can create minute long flip tricks or trick shots or sets up longer team-based games like "Capture the Flag" or "KUGO K.O." And you even create your very own games!

KUGO gameplay centres around a saucer-shaped disc that we call "The Puck". Flick the puck through tricks, games and obstacles. It's that simple, if you can flick, you can play.

Built for all ages, built to last, use the pieces in the box to build games, create tricks and unleash your imagination in a world of flick-based fun!

—description from the designer

boardgame: Warhammer Quest: Cursed City – One Man Army
description:
The Cursed City of Ulfenkarn is a dangerous place, dangerous enough to put an entire party of heroes through their paces as they meet wave after wave of the unquiet dead in the desperate quest to depose the Vyrkos vampires.

The original boxed game pitches eight heroes against a swarm of undead gribblies  who lay wicked claim to the mortal souls who call the city home.

But what if the city had had just a single champion, one renowned across the realms for their unquestionable might? Could a mighty enough hero have taken on the forces of Radukar alone.

This free scenario – entitled One Man Army – aims to answer these burning questions, with a trio of the mightiest heroes in all the Mortal Realms who must fight alone to rid the streets of the Soulblight Curse.

Choose from Gotrek Gurnisson, The Light of Eltharion, or Gardus Steel Soul and lay waste to your foes. This download is designed for one-off journeys into the dark bowels of the Cursed City, but if players are truly brave they can attempt to play through an entire Ulfenkarn in Peril campaign…

The hilarious dinner party game! Keep your eyes peeled for the hidden signals… Will you spot the Suspicious Spuds!

The host deals a coaster face down to everyone at the table with one Suspicious Spud shuffled in (2 for 10+ players). The host then chooses a number between 1 and 3. Each player finds the corresponding number on their coaster and does the actions listed and subtly say specific words anytime throughout the dinner. As the Suspicious Spuds will not have any actions or words on their coaster, they must pay attention and bluff their way through the dinner to remain undetected.

At the end of the dinner, each player points to who they think is the Suspicious Spud(s). If the Spud(s) remain undetected, they win!

The year is 1942 and you are charged with mobilizing your nation and battling to victory in the first fully industrialized, mechanized, global total war.

For the first time in modern history, development of new weapons, manufacturing capacity and global supply chains turned entire national economies into war factories, and every citizen into a military target.

To win, you must allocate your national resources to research, develop, build and deploy the war machines needed to defend your position and conquer the wealth and resources that will power the next step of your strategy.

War Leader 1942 is a map-based strategy game that allows 2-6 players to each control Axis or Allied factions in order to win the Second World War. The game is played on a large map, with detailed markers and counters keeping track of the location and movement of military units. Each player faction has a record-keeping Mobilization card, and all combat is conducted using a step-by-step procedure executed on the Battlespace card.

—description from the designer
A game based on the 1980's children's cartoon. This edition has a hex-shaped board with numbers connecting the spaces.
A move-along a path game based on the children's cartoon. The game box opens like a book to reveal the board.

boardgame: The Sword and the Flame: Zulu War Action Deck
description:
A dedicated Action Deck for the Zulu War collector and gamer, with special Event Cards representing aspects of the War of 1879, and graphics evoking Isandlwana, Rourke's Drift, and other famous battles.

A sheet explaining the use of the cards in a game is included.
family: Game: The Sword and the Flame

boardgame: The Sword and the Flame: Terrain Effects Deck
description:
This latest Game Deck adds a new layer of challenge—and opportunity!— to your table top actions with either of these classic rules sets.

Instead of one rule to describe the effects of all terrain on movement, cover, and visibility, now with the flip of a card even more excitement and suspense enters the game.

Since when in foreign lands, often tropical, is every stretch of jungle, or hill side, desert, swamp, track, etc, identical in difficulty to cross, or see into, or through, or provide the same cover from fire? And why does every creek, stream, or even river have the same conveniently firm bottom that makes crossing speed predictable?

The Variable Terrain Effects Deck now gives players the hope that a given area of terrain might be easier to march through—but also the risk that it’s tougher than it looks!

More than ever, the use of Scouts to recon the ground ahead takes on new importance--not merely to spot the enemy, but to make sure of the ground you might meet him on.

And life for the Games Masters who host tabletop games definitely gets easier. Now they can layout such trees, hills, and other miniature terrain features as they have without having to suggest just how “thick” the forest is, or any other hints suggesting the degree of difficulty.

When first entering a terrain feature, the British/Imperial Player will draw an appropriate terrain card type--forest/jungle, one of three types of water hazards, swamps, sand/beach, rough ground, or track—and read its effects, from a walk in the park to practically impassable. A simple printed marker (provided for all types) with the number of the card drawn and the terrain type is placed at the entry point for future reference, and it’s as easy as that!

There are 48 individual Terrain Effects Cards, six each of the 8 types, plus 14 cards on which are printed 6 numbered markers with the names of the terrain types on the opposite sides, plus 1 card with instructions for use. The markers are cut out for use in play and may be stored in the same plastic re-closable bag the deck arrives in.

—description from the publisher's website
family: Game: The Sword and the Flame

description:
Two armies battle on a magnetic board to find and destroy the enemy command base.

The game is setup by placing the vertical board between the two players. Then magnetic pieces are placed on the board and moved in turn by the players. When a place is moved on a square in which the other player also have a piece, this one falls due to the magnetic force and it is considered destroyed.

When a player loses his base (the star shaped piece) the game ends and the other player wins.

Every player has:

     1 command base (Star)
     4 tanks (could only move on ground)
     2 boats (could only move on the sea)
     2 airplanes (they move everywhere)


—user summary

Stairs is a stacking game consisting of a simple rule set. The goal is to place your piece higher than your opponent.

All the turn player has to do is basically move one of their pieces to the adjacent space. However, the player must move it to exactly one level higher than before the move. The player must also move the piece on the lowest stack that can be moved.

—description from the designer

Comune is a placement game using rectangular house pieces. The game distinguishes three types of placement depending on the angle at which the pieces are placed. You can place your pieces next to each other at any angle, but not next to the opponent's pieces at different angles.

The goal is to create larger groups at each of the three angles.

—description from the designer

Estate is a connection game using a hexagonal board. The board is divided into five concentric areas, each with a different value.

Each player allocates their income points to each area and places their pieces on the board, as if they were buying lots. The winner is the first player to connect two opposing sides with only their own pieces.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Kiwi Chow Down: Gooey Dessert Expansion and 5th Player Extension
description:
Gooey expansion and 5th player extension are add-ons that were unlocked during the Kiwi Chow Down Kickstarter campaign.

The contents of the add-ons are:
1 paper rules sheet
1 plastic miniature
1 cardstock player board
5 plastic snap bases
20 wooden kiwi tokens
36 cards: 2 leader cards, 9 action cards, 14 power cards, 10 goal cards
1 reference card
1 cardboard punchboard with: 1 island section, 6 victory points, 6 nest tokens, 1 turn order nest

Sky Scouts is a wartime board game produced to promote sales of a popular Boot Polish from the Australian company Nugget Pty Ltd.
The game board has 4 players selecting 4 tokens of a famous WW2 allied aircraft. Object is that each player has to get all their aircraft onto their landing strip. This is done by following a path similar to Parcheesi. Certain squares on the path show helpful or detrimental instructions to a players progress.

Winner is the first player to have all of their aircraft on the Aircraft Runway.

Cups 'N' Dice is a boardgame that provides many of the public domain cup and dice games all in one game set. This includes "Farkle", "Yatzy", "Bunco", "Mia", and many more games. These games normally involve pen and paper scoresheets, but cupsndice.com provides them in a digital format.

Most of the games provided by Cups 'N' Dice are public domain. By pairing the game set with a digital UI at the website, cupsndice.com also provides games that are unique from the public domain. This includes "Cant The Dice Game" a team based secret signal game, and traditional "Liar's Dice" with the newly added power-up dice, team-play and mystery-cup.

Speaking of Liar's Dice, the website provides a rule generator that can create over 200,000 variations of "Liar's Dice" games. You can play the simpler presets, but once you get comfortable with the game you can randomize the rules and play a different version of "Liar's Dice" every time

Hit the Gas! is an exciting and straightforward game. Simply choose one of the characters and join the race. Players must continually upgrade their cars and use various tactics to slow down their opponents. Don't worry if your car falls apart in the middle of the track; you can always select another character and jump back into the action!

— description from the publisher

boardgame: Warlords in the Last Years of Han Dynasty
description:
At the end of the Eastern Han Dynasty, foreign relatives ruled over power, eunuchs ruled the country, and the Han court was at sunset, and the glory of the past was no longer there. First there was chaos caused by the Yellow Turbans, and then there was the dictatorship of Dong Zhuo, and the world has not had a single day of peace. The times make heroes, and many heroes and heroes emerged at this time, and they gathered in the Central Plains one after another, ushering in the war-torn era at the end of the Han Dynasty.

In 190 A.D., the princes from all walks of life formed a coalition to attack Dong Zhuo, and Dong Zhuo was forced to retreat and move westward to Chang'an. In 191 A.D., the coalition forces were disbanded due to discord, and the princes had ulterior motives in their hearts, and they did not hesitate to fight each other in order to compete for territory. The following year, Dong Zhuo was killed and Lü Bu fled. Beginning in 191 A.D., the seven forces in the Central Plains have gradually developed into powerful heroes through conquests and mergers. Lu Bu, the flying general who is unparalleled in the country, Yuan Shu who proclaimed himself emperor in Huainan, Liu Bei who took over Xuzhou, and Liu Biao who sat on the sidelines. Father and son of Sun Jian and Sun Ce, Cao Cao, the hero of troubled times who fought a decisive battle in Guandu in 200 AD, and Yuan Shao, the overlord of Hebei......

Warlords in the Last Years of Han Dynasty can accommodate 2-7 players to play the game at the same time. It is divided into 8 historical scenarios, 2 instructional scenarios and 1 hypothetical scenario. Because the Romance of the Three Kingdoms, one of the four great classics, has a huge influence on later generations, the game design is based on official history and supplemented by romance, so as to avoid players being difficult to adapt for a while. In the "advanced rules", more realistic optional rules are provided, which will simulate the history of the end of the Eastern Han Dynasty to a greater extent.

—description from the publisher (translated)

boardgame: Волшебная бродилка: в стране знаний (Magic Wanderer)
description: Players in the "Magic Wanderer" travel through the land of knowledge, and in order to successfully complete the route, they need to answer a variety of questions about the primary school program and not only. For correct answers, players receive magic fives that help them get out of traps, open ancient gates on their way and overtake other players.

boardgame: 粉碎计划：东山岛1953 (Plan Shatter: Dongshan Island, 1953)
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
"Your sleepy little city is on the decline and some new investors want to help change that.  Now you'll compete with other cities, securing the best building projects to gain the most prestige in hopes of become the Greatest Little City."

In this game, players will have two actions to perform.  Gaining tiles and placing them within their city.  Gaining a tile involves the Investor's Market, a series of building tile stacks arranged in a rondel.  The active player moves their Investor clockwise to the desired stack, claiming the top tile.  The Active Player pawn then moves clockwise to the next Investor in the rondel.

Between turns at the Investor Market, players can then add these buildings to their cities.  New tiles must be placed adjacent to their City Planner, who then moves onto the newly built tiles.  Each building type scores differently.  Once four piles have been depleted, the game is over.

Greatest Little Cities: Urban Sprawl is the larger box version of Greatest Little Cities, a Mint Tin Game.  The tiles are not only significantly larger from the original but the number of tiles of each type has been rebalanced to add a 5th Player from the original.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Kings of the Castle: The Battle of the Imjin River April 22-25, 1951
description:
Kings of the Castle is a moderate complexity level war game simulation of the climactic battle fought along the Imjin River during the Korean War.  Players draw formation activation markers to determine what units activate, and a six sided die ro resolve combats (air and artillery strikes as well as combat between opposing ground units), as well as other game events.

A complete game of Kings of the Castle is composed of the following:

•    One rules booklet
•    Two 11" by 17" maps
•    325 un-mounted, single-sided counters
•    Two players’ aid sheets with charts, tables, and record-keeping tracks.

Note that players will need to provide one 6-sided die and an opaque container (cup, envelope, etc.) to play the game.

Each night turn of the game represents two hours of time; day turns represent 4 hours.  Each hex is approximately one third of a mile across. Allied Infantry and both sides’ Heavy Weapons units represent companies (about 200 to 250 men). Chinese infantry units represent battalions (about 800 men). Allied armor/tank units represent 10-12 armored fighting vehicles.

-description from designer

According to legend, mighty gnomes dwell in the deep forest. As the chef of the mushroom tribe, you seek to prove to the king that you are his best leader, but there are many competitors. Consequently, you seek the aid of mighty gnomes. They will join your tribe if you gather enough resources in the forest and climb up the ladder to activate actions in the attic. Their powerful abilities will help you improve your actions, and their scoring criteria allow interesting combos and engines to achieve victory!

Heigh-ho, heigh-ho, to the kingdom I love to live! Right before the king's inspection, hardworking gnomes try to improve their kingdom in just 5 days. They summon 4 gnome groups every day and assign 3 of the best to each task. In the evening, after a long day of hard work, the gnomes are tired. Gnomes unassigned rush to their cozy bedroom because the next day they must work before others are fully rested. How impressive will the kingdom be after 5 days?

—description from the designer

Each player puts a meeple on a black dot on the turntable und pays a coin to the large yellow dot in the middle of the turntable. The players take turns to roll the die and move the turntable accordingly in any direction. If a meeple gets in front of the dot in it's own color, it leaves the turntable and has to beginn anew. On the roll of a 6 the turntable isn't rotated and the player gets a coin from the yellow dot.
A player wins the game, if he is the last one on the turntable.

boardgame: La Bataille de Mont Saint Jean: Expansion
family: Series: La Bataille (Marshal Enterprises / Clash of Arms Games)

description:
Justas is a board game of card duels, bets and ingenuity set in medieval tournaments in which each player embodies a knight who must fight against the rest of the participants in the different modalities to rise as champion of the kingdom representing his house.

It will be just as important to know how to play your cards as to take into account those of the other contenders for the title, winning will depend sometimes on strength and other times on cunning.

Objective of the game: for each player, it is to remain with coins throughout the turns so that the rivals are eliminated when they run out of any. At the moment when there are only two, there will be a final dispute between them, competing in the 3 modalities of the tournament, the one who wins in at least 2 of them being proclaimed champion.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet
family: Organizations: Board Games Association of Creators in Spain (LUDO)

description:
Can you fill all the tiles with pictures? Or maybe you will choose a more difficult variant of the game and be the one who finds the card depicting the biblical story you are reading the fastest?

This smart and colorful game will make you spend your time well and while having a great time, you will learn the stories of people who met the Lord Jesus.

The goal is to determine which description describes which image. You win if you have full sets of paintings.
Enquêtes et Filatures is a rummy like card game in which players try to build a feasible criminal investigation, that could be lead to a murder or a suicide.

boardgame: Marvel Champions: The Card Game – NeXt Evolution
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable
family: Collectible: Living Card Game (Fantasy Flight Games)
Game: Marvel Champions – The Card Game

description:
Specifically designed for fighting the Mahdi, his Ansar, and the fanatical "Fuzzy Wuzzy" against British and Egyptian Units.  The deck consists of 48 full color cards.

—description from the publisher
family: Game: The Sword and the Flame

boardgame: 800 Fighting Englishmen: Northwest Frontier Action Deck
description:
Perfect for use in your games on "The Grim," this Deck is illustrated with images of the 2nd Afghan War battles and famous--and infamous!--personalities.

—description from the publisher
family: Game: The Sword and the Flame

description:
Quest for the Trident is a quick, exciting game about exploring the dangerous seas to find the powerful Trident of Poseidon! Venture into the unknown. Sink your friend’s ships. Control the ever changing seas. Gain exciting powers!

Rules Summary:

Create the map as you go! Each time you move into a blank space, you flip over a new tile from the deck to fill it. Tiles will have different effects when moving onto them, listed on the tile itself.

The goal of the game is to acquire the Trident, and bring it to one of your hideaways to win the game.

Every turn, you roll the dice to see how far you are able to move this turn. You can also attack other players and steal their stuff, or try to steal their hideaway. Gaining a hideaway gives you special powers that will help you navigate the seas.

At points during the game, you will be able to shift the seas by turning some tiles face down, and swapping them around. This will keep your opponents guessing where the dangers are hiding!

boardgame: 800 Fighting Englishmen: Zulu War Action Deck
description:
Illustrated with authentic images of the Zulu War of 1879, including samples of Zulu Regimental Shield Designs, as well as famous characters from this dramatic conflict, this Game Deck will even make Isandlwana seem like an "Arts Thing!"

—description from the publisher
family: Game: The Sword and the Flame

boardgame: 800 Fighting Englishmen: First Boer War Action Deck
description:
Using many contemporary illustrations from the 1st War (not the 2nd!), this Deck provides views of key personnel, Majuba, Boer Weapons, and more.

—description from the publisher
family: Game: The Sword and the Flame

boardgame: 800 Fighting Englishmen: Egyptian War Action Deck
description:
Loaded with fine illustrations from the period, including the bizarre as well as the standard contemporary press views of key personalities and events of this short but sharp campaign.

The Egyptians were the only standing, National Army the British fought in the Era of TSATF, and while their higher leadership failed to stand up against Woolsley, many junior officers and units fought cleverly, professionally, and with unquestioned bravery. The Egyptians were not a "walk over" opponent in fact--nor on the table top, either!

—description from the publisher
family: Game: The Sword and the Flame

boardgame: 800 Fighting Englishmen: Boxer Rebellion Action Deck
description:
The Boxer Rebellion Deck is "weighted" to represent the mix of Allied and Chinese units/factions which characterized the conflict. Including the usual Command Bonus cards, the 800FE Boxer Rebellion Action Deck totals 56 Cards, 8 more than in the other 800FE Decks.

The Chinese are represented by 12 cards cuing Boxer units to Move/Fire, plus cards for units of the four Chinese Armies engaged between Peking and Tientsin. There are five cards each for units of General Nieh's "Tenacious Army," General Sung-Ching's "Resolute Army," General Tung-Fu-Hsiang's "Kansu Braves," and General in Chief Yung-Lo's Manchu Bannermen.

For the eight combatant Allies, the Action Cards are provided in the same proportions as each nationality comprised the total forces engaged (prior to the arrival of the German East Asia Brigade). Consequently, there is one card each for the Italians, Austrians, French, and Germans, two each for the British, American, and Russians, and five for the Japanese.

If any of these contingents are not represented on your table top, the otherwise unnecessary cards may be used to cue Movement/Fire of other, more numerous units in your collection. Similarly, European and Chinese Army units not present may have their cards removed from the Deck prior to play.

By this arrangement, the Players on both sides will not only have to deal with the usual uncertainty of order of Movement and Fire of their own and the enemy's units, but must allow for each "faction" of his forces having their own say when they feel like, rather than as the C-in-C might-- exactly as in the real conflict.

—description from the publisher
family: Game: The Sword and the Flame

boardgame: The Pursuit of Happiness: Promo Pack
description:
All Promo Cards for The Pursuit of Happiness ever made (and some never before seen!)

CONTENTS
1 Community Card
1 Experience (Trip)
1 Experience (Happening)
6 Basic Projects
1 Single-Round Project
5 Group Projects
1 Item
1 Activity
1 Job
1 Child Trait
1 Life Goal
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

boardgame: Cold Case: A Move and Write Escape Room Experience
description: In Cold Case: A Move and Write Escape Room Experience, a print-and-play board game for 2-4 people, the players attempt to solve the puzzles in an escape room! During the game, they collect the clues scattered around the room and use them to solve the puzzles before time runs out. At the end of the game, whoever contributed the most to solving the room wins!

boardgame: Alien Frontiers: Data Core Expansion Pack
description:
The Data Core Expansion Pack includes 8 new Alien Tech cards to be used with the Alien Frontiers base game.

-description from the publisher

Included in Alien Frontiers Edition X but also made available as a stand alone expansion.

The expansion comes with a rules card and eight new alien technologies cards, two copies of four unique alien technologies. When playing with just the base game the rules recommend mixing only one copy of each alien technology into the alien technology deck. When playing with an alien technology deck larger than that included with the base game, whether from expansions or promos, then the rules recommend including both copies of each of the four alien technology cards.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description: Northern Skies is a fast paced bidding card game for 2-5 players, played in 3 rounds and lasts around 30 minutes. Northern Skies’ simple mechanics allow players to learn the game fast and the depth will leave you wanting to play again and again.

The background of the story is set in World War I, when Germany was defeated. After Hitler came to power, he reorganized his armaments at a very fast speed and took Poland as the primary attack target. Although Poland was promised by Britain and France to maintain the integrity of Poland’s territory, the German army entered Poland smoothly due to the “concession” after the negotiations between Germany and the Soviet Union. The territory, and won a lightning-fast victory, and later historians have dubbed it a successful example of "blitzkrieg".

—description from the publisher (translated)

The scenario of "Blitz Poland" in the game tried its best to restore the crushing trend of the German army in history. The unique combat mechanism and fast-paced advancement speed (in a lightning-fast way, the duration of a single round is shorter), which strengthened the attacking experience of German players, enough for players to experience the fun of the Blitz game.
