During the game, players can support, move and fight in each turn. It allows players to experience the joy of restraining each other. Whether it is a strong attack on a point or a siege to aid, as long as it is a tactic you can think of, you can experience it in this miniature war and plan a strategy!

The game also sets up imaginary scripts and entertainment confrontation scenarios, and also supports single-player solo scenario to meet the psychological needs of mass players.

—description from the publisher (translated)

Swords: Cut the Deck is a tabletop role-playing card game based on the award-winning webcomic, SWORDS. In this game, a variety of odd heroes use funny and fearsome weapons in epic skirmishes. Who will prove their skill with the blade? And who will die by the sword?

Cut the Deck brings a full roster of fantastical heroes into the fray wielding dozens and dozens of strange and mystical swords! With tens of thousands of unique builds, it cuts different every time you play.

Three Junk cards that were given out with a plushie.

—user summary

Having trouble falling asleep during the Camp Phase? Maybe DC, the lovable demonic cat, can help! This 10” plush comes with poseable horns and three (3) exclusive Junk cards (the Stuffed Mouse, the Flattened Coin, and the Charmed Bracelet) that you can add to your Vagrantsong campaign!

—description from the publisher

Solitaire game of the Battle of the Bulge, published as a special supplement of MC Axis #61.
The player plays the role of German Army commander, aims to break through the allied front lines, capture important bases, and break through to Antwerp.

Solitaire game of the Battle of the Coral Sea, published as a special supplement of MC Axis #65.
The player plays the role of Imperial Japanese Navy commander, use Shōkaku, Zuikaku and Shōhō to attack Yorktown and Lexington, and aims to invade Port Moresby.

Sblocca la Porta: Storia is an escape room for children.

Lots of riddles to solve with wit and logic and clues to discover in order to complete the adventure. An original and modern game that will involve young and old in a whirlwind of fun! A puzzle adventure and a journey to be enjoyed by recovering all the clues and solving the puzzles while training the mind and lateral thinking.

Included in the box are 3 adventures set in 3 historical periods: the Middle Ages, Ancient Greece and Ancient Egypt.

Millenniums have passed.  The Nisblitts, aliens not of our dimension, have discovered the remains of humanity and all of earth's other ancient relics.  In order to study the emotions of these bizarre humans, the Nisblitts have revived one of these creatures and attached its mind to a device filled with fragments of 21st century language.  The only problem... they didn't realize that they had actually revived a Tyrannosaurus rex.

Players will take turns becoming this talking Emotional rex that must speak sentences in emotional ways.  The Nisblitt players will then assess these emotions with their data tokens.  Correct guesses on the emotion award points for both the Nisblitt players and the E-rex player.  The player with the most points at the end of the game knows what it truly means to be "human".
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Plenty of people tried to gift Leland a new tackle box, but he always swore by this one. Perhaps it'll hold something useful for you, too.

This 4-card expansion for Fishing Lessons adds Tackle cards which must be played to win. Shuffle and randomly add 2 of them each game, with one face-up to start. When adding a card to the Lesson Row from your hand, you may play a face-up Tackle card as well. Tackle cards cover an existing Lesson card and replace it with a powerful action for the turn before being discarded to reveal the original Lesson. After playing a Tackle card, flip the other one face-up and play it on a subsequent turn. Play both before the game ends or you'll miss the boat!


A tackle card is played on top of an existing lesson card. When Step 2 is resolved, the actions on the tackle card are resolved –and then the tackle card is discarded right after it is finished. In this way, the tackle card replaces that lesson card only for the one turn, and in the next turn the lesson card underneath would be available again. The tackle card must be placed on top of a lesson card, and cannot be played on its own. The tackle card cannot be played on top of the lesson card added during the same round.

The difference between sun and storms is all the difference when fishing. Better check the forecast before you get out on the water! 

This 5-card expansion for Fishing Lessons introduces Weather cards. On any turn in which you replace a card in the Lesson Row from your hand, draw a Weather card and follow its specific placement rules. Each of the 5 unique cards adds a twist to your fishing trip: altered movement, flipped fish, and more!


When a player elects to replace a lesson card in Step 1, they draw a weather card. The weather card is drawn after the lesson card is placed, and is read and activated before Step 2 is resolved. Each weather card is different and has a unique effect on it.

Example card: Sun - The warm water drives some fish deeper. Select a type of fish, and flip all of the cards with that fish face-down.Ignore this effect if all lake cards are face-down already.

A set of the board and pieces for checkers, its variants, and abstract games that require a checkerboard. The board is available in two sizes, 8 x 8 squares on the front and 10 x 10 squares on the back. The set includes rules for English Checkers and International Checkers, descriptions of many other traditional variants of checkers, and rules for the designer's three original games Zong-Heng, Binary, and Incorrect Checkers.

Rolling to the Elections is a roll and write game based on draft mechanics and dice rolling to obtain majorities. The game is designed for two players, although it supports a solitaire or multiplayer game mode.
 In Rolling to the Elections, each player play for being the president of a country by taking the role of one of the two candidates, either from the left-wing or the right-wing . To win the game, the player must obtain the majority of seats in the parliament.

There are five versions of the game: Spain ("Rolling to La Moncloa"), Germany ("Rolling to Kanzleramt") France ("Rolling to Elysee"), United Kingdom (Rolling to Downing Street) and USA (Rolling to the White House). In each version different rules are applied, adapted to the electoral model of each country and its regions, and they can be played independently.

—description from the designer

Ariol le Kolektor is a card game for 6+yo children. It theme derives from the French Cartoon series Ariol. Goal is to collect 4 Chevallier Chaval vignettes. Therefore players draw and play cards. Each card has an card effect either collecting cards or interacting with the other players.

In Forest of "Number Spirits", players fulfill contracts by placing certain patterns onto the forest map (such as forest cubes, rock squares, or water ovals).  The fulfillment of the contracts grants the players special effects and lets them cross of certain digits on their bingo cards.  Players earn points by completing bingos and certain contracts.

The game takes place over two rounds and the player with the most points at the end of the second round wins.

boardgame: Masters of The Universe: Fields of Eternia The Board Game – Rise of the Snake Men
description:
Adds Snake Men Faction.
6 new characters, 10 dice, 15 soldiers.
Path of Destiny Quest,
133 cards
family: Game: Masters of the Universe – Fields of Eternia

boardgame: Masters of The Universe: Fields of Eternia The Board Game – Spell of Separation
description:
HORDAK IS READY TO CAST THE SPELL OF SEPARATION! WHOSE SIDE DO YOU TAKE?

The Evil Hordak has discovered the mysterious Starseed hidden deep within Eternia's core. Now, he plans to tear the planet apart to obtain its incredible power. Will you help him create the 'Tablet of Separation' and the 'Triad of Discindium' to fulfill his dreams? Or are you going to stand against the Evil Horde and become Eternia's saviour? 
With the arrival of the Evil Horde, the war takes a cosmic turn. Using new warriors, like Hordak, Leech, Catra, Scorpia and more, test your strategy as you battle in three-faction gameplay with unique path of Destiny boards for each faction. Train your soldiers to become elites, merchants, and sailors to keep up with the Evil Horde as it tries to take over all of Eternia. Conquer or be conquered. There are no other options. 
Become Eternia's conqueror or its saviour with 21 brand new boss card, 136 various cards, 3 unique Path of Destiny boards, and many more great components.

Box contains:

1 x Mission book 
1 x Combat Grid
10 x Wilds Die 
6 x Hero models 
15 x Evil Horde Soldier models
3 x Path of Destiny Boards 
27 x Evil Horde Soldier rings
137 x Various cards 
5 x Various tokens

Requires the base game in order to play this expansion.
family: Game: Masters of the Universe – Fields of Eternia

boardgame: Masters of The Universe: Fields of Eternia The Board Game – Stretch Goals
description: Add 14 new characters across the initial 4 factions, (9 to the Masters of the Universe, 3 to the Evil Warriors, 1 to the Evil Horde, and 1 to the Snake Men), as well as 4 new encounter allies.
family: Game: Masters of the Universe – Fields of Eternia

description:
Weeds is played either one-on-one or in two-on-two teams. From a deck of 4 cards, players take turns choosing a card with a Tetris-like figure and drawing it in the shared garden. The last player or team to be able to draw a figure wins.

At the start of the game, 6 ability cards that cheat the placement rules are selected. During the game you can choose 3 of these abilities for your team. If you choose too early, the ability may not be used optimally. If you choose too late, your opponent may already have taken it.

Throughout the game, players collect water cans that allow them to refresh the card marked. But use them wisely, because they can also give your opponent new opportunities.

All players compete for space on a shared game board, which provides a high degree of player interaction. The 2v2 version encourages discussion around the table about who will sacrifice themselves for the team and block the opponent. Alliances are already formed - now it's time to get personal.

Sounds simple? It is. But if you don't coordinate your attacks with your partner and choose your abilities correctly, you'll quickly become locked in. And then it's up to your partner to be the "last plant standing".

Nominated for Best Presented Board Game and Best Innovation in Board Games at Fastaval 2023
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Exordium is a 2 player, 3X (Explore / Examine / Extract) board game comprised of multi-use cards.  Players are part of the Exordium Expedition, studying the exoplanet and its Artifact; a towering piece of architecture that may be biological, or the work of some forgotten species.

Players will Explore by placing Outposts on the board, Examine by sending Drones to the Artifact, and Extract resources from the Terrain they’ve explored, gaining points for each. The player with the most points at the end of the game wins.

Exordium is a board game that uses cards in place of other components.

boardgame: ワールドバケーション (World Vacation)
description:
World Vacation is a game which takes place on an inflatable globe and a player's travel record form.  The game sets the players as millionaires with too much time and money who set about traveling around the world.

Each player is assigned a starting city, which will also be where they end their trip, and a checkpoint city.  They are incentivized to visit certain cities, and not travel over 4000km. Each player marks the legs of their flights on the globe with a roll of tape.

At the end of the game, players earn points for the total distance traveled and types of sights seen; they lose points for crossing the paths of other players and taking too long of flights.

LUCRE is a trick taking card game for 4 players.  The nameless Guilds care about one thing: coins.  Each player controls a single Guild, and will play cards from their Guild deck (13 cards, each with a Value of 1-13 and worth 1-3 coins, each with no Value and 1 coin on the back) in an attempt to acquire more coins.

Each game, the 8 Purse cards are seeded randomly from each player’s Guild deck, meaning that every game, the Purse cards and the Guild cards available to each player will be different.  At the end of 8 rounds, the player with the most coins wins.

This promo for CATAN: Dawn of Humankind contains the Shaman's Prophecy card and the Dice Cave dice "tower."

The Shaman's Prophecy card is added to the starting hand of the player to the right of the start player. It can be used in the action phase to move an explorer 1-2 intersections. Once used, it is passed to the player on the right. It counts towards the number of cards held so can be stolen and can be discarded when a player must do so when a 7 is rolled. If that happens it is given to the player to the discarding player's right.

The Dice Cave, when assembled can be used to roll dice into, like a dice tower.

boardgame: Green Team Wins: Tantrum House Promo Pack
description:
Collection of 30 cards for use in the game. There are 10 different new cards in each of the three categories that are all unique to this specific set. All of the cards are related to pop culture.

The card content was created through a collaboration with Tantrum House for their Kickstarter campaign.

boardgame: Green Team Wins: Dice Tower Promo Pack
description:
Collection of 30 cards for use in the game. There are 10 different new cards in each of the three categories, all unique to this particular promo pack. All of the cards are related to a mix of board gaming, Florida, and pop culture.

The card content was created through a collaboration with The Dice Tower for their Kickstarter campaign.

-description from publisher

Team game where the Patient team tries to describe their very delicate issue to the Doctor team. Due to the delicate nature of the issue the Patient can only give clues as what is the problem without using words. It's up to the Doctor to successfully uncover the actual issue. After 2 minutes the teams switch, the most successful team wins.

The King is dead, and you and your friends try to claim the empty throne. You will be fighting for the crown through various rounds. The goal is to score the highest points by the end of the round and claim the coveted crown.

In Crown Battles: Claim the Throne, players face different goals each round, such as completing missions or forming alliances. Special Ability cards add strategic depth, offering actions like stealing coins or changing the trump suit. The player with the highest score at the end is crowned the ultimate sovereign.

This Promo consists of one card and one red dice (d6).
It replaces the normal rules for red Frankys.
When scoring, any number of red Frankys may be assigned to the red dice. These red Frankys may then be re-rolled. Each dice that shows a red Franky again may be rolled again (until it no longer shows a red Franky). Each dice that now shows a lightning bolt is worth as many points as the red dice shows.

—user summary
Add an icy dungeon, new enemies from the frost lands and a sliding mechanic.

The Mini-Expansion adds asymmetric Player powers for your Heroes.
A print-and-play version in German is available for free at the Amigo-Spiele website.

A Mini-Expansion for 3-4 players.

Equip your Heroes with individual powers!

Game-preparations:
In addition to choosing your Hero take the Hero-Board as well as the suitable Hero-Power. (Hero-Powers of not selected Heroes go back in the game-box and are not considered in the game)
Take 3 single-coins (value 1) and place them on the 3 marked spots on the front-side of your Hero-Power. These are your "Action-coins" (keep them separate from your other coins during the game).
If you play with "Mascha" (the bear) you also need the duel-marker (otherwise only needed for the 2-Player-Variant of the base-game).

Rules:
You can at any time during your turn but only ONCE a turn use one of the Hero-Powers that are in play. To do so you need to "pay" with one of your three "Action-coins" (resting on your Hero-Power).
If you choose the Hero-Power of your Hero the coin goes back into the general-supply. If you choose a Hero-Power of another Player the coin goes to the coin-supply of the respective player.
Note: Since you have only 3 "Action-coins" you can only use up to three times any Hero-Power in play. Once an "Action-coin" is used it becomes a regular coin - either in the general-supply or as payment to another player.

End of game/Scoring:
Every leftover "Action-coin(s)" on your Hero-Powers become regular coins and count therefore towards your total.

Hero-Powers:

Phil (the capricorn)
Exploration - you get an extra roll for the this turn (so you're allowed to roll 4-times instead of 3-times, "X"s are still not allowed to re-roll)

Mascha (the bear)
Determination - add this turn 2 swords to your total, if your swords go in the middle than place your duel-marker with your dice (remove the duel-marker when your dice leaf the middle)

Iva (the hawk)
Persuasive power - after you rolled your dice re-roll all "X"s (you have to use this Hero-Power before you take your next roll and the effect is only for the ONE roll you used it after, Note: since you're only allowed to use a Hero-Power once a turn there are no second re-rolls)

Emmit (the otter)
Teamwork - take a yellow or red die from your Hero-Board as if you had rolled a yellow- or red-die symbol in the previous turn (the die is now part of your group - until he shows an "X")

In Dwarves: The Fall of Karnarak, you incarnate a dwarf clan leader. In order to protect your home, the famous citadel of Karnarak, you must collaborate with the other clan leaders against a powerful dragon trying to take it.

Among clan leaders, you discuss and decide a plan of action, always taking into account the special abilities of each clan, and the functions of each room of the citadel. During the siege, events that can affect your plans will happen, often for the worse.

75 EVENT CARDS: Destabilise your strategy, make spawns appear or siege engin threaten your citadel
60 SPAWN CARDS: Make enemies appear way too close for comfort
60 TROOP CARDS: Allow you to fight the spawns
60 RESSOURCE CARDS: Allow you to buy upgrades, specialists, beer, divine intervention, or forge special items
8 SPECIALIST CARDS: Allow you to work better
8 UPGRADE CARDS: Allow the citadel to work better
5 CLAN LEADERS: Your identity for the game
5 WARLORDS: Your sworn enemies, soon to be defeated hopefully.
1 MAP OF THE CITADEL: Your board, and your home

Defend the fortress against hordes of enemies, upgrade your citadel, hire specialists in your clan, and above all, try to defeat the dragon, together.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Insular Attraction is a game about two Islands so madly in love they can't help but try to crash into each other. Two players take on the role of these islands trying to get to each other at speed. Not too fast and not too slow!

Insular Attraction is a pencil and paper game. Players choose a secret starting speed, then take simultaneous turns selecting actions, attempting to approach one another at the perfect speed.

Cunning Thieves is an exciting card game for 2 to 5 players in which you are going to test your perception, cunning and strategies to get more treasures tan the others thieves before the night ends.

Each turn players do the following: 

     May use an action card. 
     May discard a treasure card, if so, Draw 2 cards from the deck. 
     End the turn and draw 1 card from the deck. 


To make a treasure a player must have 3 treasure cards with the same picture.

—description from the designer

Dioses del Nilo is a strategic and tactical board game for two players based on Egyptian mythology. The objective of each player is to defeat their opponent by skillfully moving their pieces on the board.

How to play?

The game is divided into two phases: the placement phase and the combat phase.

Each player's field is divided into two: vanguard and rearguard.

Each player must make a movement in each phase in an orderly manner and alternate with the opponent.

Placement phase:
In this phase the players both take 2 tiles from their tile column (the bottom two tiles). Then in turn (starting with the one with the clock on their side of the field) Place a token inside the field on any space you want.

Tokens that are placed in the rear can be placed face down.
Tiles placed in the vangauard are always placed face up.
Once a player places a token, he passes the other player's turn by sliding the clock card to the other player's side.
Once all the players have placed their 2 tokens, we proceed to the combat phase.

Combat phase:
Starting with the player who has the clock on his side of the field, he can make one of the following moves:

Move a soldier to an adjacent square (left or right, up or down) The soldier cannot be moved diagonally.
Attack an enemy square.
End the turn.
Once a player has made a move, it automatically passes to the other player and the clock moves to their side of the field.

Attacking and Defending
When a soldier attacks an enemy square, if there is an enemy soldier on that square, then both fight. The higher number wins.

The losing token "perishes" and is placed on top of its face-up god card [Graveyard]. Tokens that have perished in battle can be consulted by the opponent at any time. If there is a tie, both pieces perish.

The winning soldier must recharge energy, so he places himself at the top of the face-up token column, waiting for his turn to return to action.

—description from the designer

Negamco's Professional Bowling Game.

Card and Dice based bowling game.   Bowler cards represent bowlers from the 1970's.   A die roll result is cross referenced to a pin results table to obtain the results.

Very similar to APBA bowling.

boardgame: Ramar of the Jungle: Big Game Hunt
description:
This game is a spin and move game where the object is to capture valuable animals in the jungle. The game board is filled with face down animals and by spinning a spinner and then moving your playing piece onto one of those animals you capture it. When a player lands on an animal, his playing piece remains on that spot until his/her next turn and other players cannot land on or move over that spot. They must use their roll number to move around the opponents piece.

The game continues until all of the animals have been captured. Turning over each animal reveals a value attached to that particular animal. The player who captures the most valuable animals wins the game.

boardgame: Heroes of Short Track Racing: Collectible Card Racing Game
description:
Heroes of Short Track Racing is a game that has driver cards for real life short track racing drivers. Each driver has a card that has their name, hometown, class of car and what set they are included in. There is a picture of the driver's racecar under that and the game card is on the bottom. The back of the card is the drivers bio. There is also a set of blank name sprint car drivers based on real life drivers. The driver the card represents is fairly easy to tell by looking at the number on the wing and the official World of Outlaws point standings from 2023.

The game card has a section for Action Numbers, Movement Range and Passing/Defending rating. The Action Numbers tell you if that driver can attempt to pass on that lap or if they are trying to keep their position. If a driver has the Action Number, you roll on the Movement Range area to see what happens. The driver can roll to not have anything happen on that lap, they can be able to challenge the car in front of themselves for position. They can automatically pass the car ahead of him or they can automatically pass the car just ahead and challenge the car ahead of that one. The Passing & Defending ratings are used when the driver is attempting to pass the car ahead.

Feud takes inspiration from popular RTS video games such as Age of Empires. Designed for 2 or 4 players (1v1 or 2v2), Feud allows players to take on the role of a king who must gather resources, build structures, train armies, and engage in battles with other kingdoms to expand their empire and defeat their enemies.

Gameplay in Feud is a mix of resource management, city-building, and military strategy. Players must collect resources like food, wood, and gold to build structures, train units, and research technologies.

As the game progresses, players must make strategic decisions about which technologies to research and when to attack their enemies. Each player’s ultimate goal is to be the last king standing and control the most territory. The game is won by the player who has conquered the most territory and eliminated all of their opponents.

Another feature of Feud is that it incorporates an element of bluff. Each player’s stronghold is kept secret until it is scouted by their opponents. This means that players can create elaborate feints and ruses to deceive their opponents into thinking they have a strong or weak position.

DIE-Minions is a strategic dice drafting game where players compete to secure control of four different factions. Each turn, players will move their mad-scientist on the 3x3 board to a new location where they will capture (i.e., draft) 1 die if there are at least 2 present. Based on the die taken, a die from an adjacent location is removed, re-rolled, and placed into the reserve pool. If there are dice in the reserve, the player then place 1 die on any location with an opening for that faction (i.e., color).

Game play continues until each location has 1 die only (9 dice on the board). The player with the highest total influence (sum of dice) in each faction wins control of that faction. Controlling a faction at game end grants a player victory points equal to the sum of the faction dice left on the main board. That is, only the 9 dice left on the board score points for any player.

Players must carefully draft the right dice to gain control of the faction while also ensuring that any faction they control has high scoring dice on the board at the end of the game.

-description from publisher


Truly, it was a GREAT Idea!

World domination was waiting; all you needed was a few extra hands.  What could be the harm in collaborating with those other mad scientists? You’ve got this! Honestly, it was a good idea. Experimental substances, electricity, a little weird science, and presto! a multitude of minions to do your bidding. There’s no such thing as free will, right?

Actually, it was a bad idea. That much applied genius was bound to have unintended consequences. How could you have known that your perfect minions would split into four personalities that refused to get along? It was clearly that other guy’s fault!

Really, it was a terrible idea. Your grand plans must wait while you correct this complication.  But getting back to your proper glory requires sacrifice…

DIE-Minions - it’s not only what you need for victory, but what must be done!
-from the back of the box

"Every caterpillar dreams of becoming a butterfly someday. However, only one caterpillar will become one in this game. Throw dice to add segments to your caterpillar (or mess with your opponents!) The first one to reach the top of the banana tree will fre the butterfly trapped within the bunch of bananas!"

In this children's game, the players will throw a custom dice with various imageson it which will indicate what action they will take for the turn: a banana or a butterfly will let them add respectively one or two segment of their color to the caterpillar ), the pineapple and the palm tree lets them steal respecively one or two segments from other players (both from their reserve or their caterpillar) before adding them to their own caterpillar, the fish forces the player to give one of their segment to another player and lastly the clam will allow them to steal one egment from each opponents before adding them to their own caterpillar.

Once a caterpillar grows big enough, its owner immediately wins the game : the banana bunch on top of the tree will open up and cause all other caterpillars to fall down !

Small (Game Up) expansion containing:


     6 Advisor tokens
     6 Battle cards
     3 Evolution pyramids (its replace the Evolution board)

Small (Game Up) expansion containing:


     3 Kemet Power tiles

     1 Vision Serpent Priest miniature

Small (Game Up) expansion containing:


     1 Scolopendra miniature
     1 Scolopendra token
     1 Scolopendra card
     30 plastic Corn

This expansion lets you play Yucatan up to 6 players.

Components:


     2 player aids
     2 City boards

This expansion lets you play Yucatan in Solo Mode.

Components:


     Bot board

     Marker token

language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

boardgame: 割算: １・２・３からはじめるトリックテイキング (Division: Trick-taking starting with 1, 2, 3)
description:
In the trick-taking game 割算: １・２・３からはじめるトリックテイキング (Division: Trick-taking starting with 1, 2, 3), the deck consists only of cards numbered 1-3.

Each player starts with a pair of cards in front of them that make a two-digit number, then in turn order they each play a third card that divides this number, e.g. 12 divided by 3 or 23 divided by 2. Whoever has the largest quotient wins the round, scoring points equal to the divisor they played, then for each player the second numeral in the dividend drops down in front of the divisor to create a new two-digit number. The winner then leads a card, and everyone follows.

Keep playing tricks until the cards run out. Whoever scores the most points wins.

Rules.

1. The game is played on an 8x8 board, but it can also be played on boards of different sizes.

2. The first player places 1 stone at their discretion on the empty board.

3. The second player places 2 stones according to a protocol in 2 moves: 1Drop - 1Free.
Drop move: the player places their stone in place of an opponent's stone, moving that opponent's stone to a free adjacent square that is diagonally or orthogonally adjacent.
Free move: the player then places their own stone in a free square that has no stones adjacent diagonally or orthogonally.

4. Players take turns placing their 2 stones according to the 1Drop - 1Free protocol on the board until it is no longer possible to place Free moves. At that point, players proceed only with Drop moves.

5. When placement possibilities are exhausted, the groups sizes for each player are counted. The player with the largest
group wins, in the event of a tie, the size of the second largest group is counted and so on.

"Groups" refer to a single stone or a set of stones of the same color adjacent orthogonally.

—description from the designer

An Escape Game... for children! Embark on a fabulous adventure in a land far away! Princess Elisa has been kidnapped by a dragon! You have 45 minutes to free her, before the dragon decides to devour you all. Time is running out, will you be able to solve the puzzles, without getting lost in the lair of the beast?

Included: Box with 1 booklet of 32 pages, 40 cards, 1 poster ans 45-minute soundtrack to immerse yourself in the kingdom of knights and dragons!

Corporate Life is a worker placement and area control game with special actions. Each turn players choose an ACE card from a hand of 3 and then select 1 of 3 possible actions:

A: Authorized Action - to place or move workers within the organization. 
C: Claim Credit - to score points with the board on your journey to CEO. 
E: Executive Event - Seize opportunities created by well meaning, but ill-advised executives.

Positioning Cubers into key organizational positions grants a player access to department bonuses. Some departments protect your Cubers from actions and events, some augment Claiming Credit, and others alter a player's hand. Leveraging these department actions is key to getting ahead of your competition!

Play continues until one player gains access to the CEO's office (25+ Promotion Points). All players take an even number of turns and the player with the most promotion points is appointed the new CEO and wins the game.

This is not a business simulation! It is a lighthearted poke at office politics, bizarre corporate structures, and the underhanded action of those that subscribe to the lifestyle of Corporate Life.
-description from publisher


Welcome to Corporate Life…

You’ve always known you should be in charge. When it comes to directing others, Hollywood’s titans would be wise to seek your advice. That’s why you joined the Inc.Co LLC team - to seize the CEO reins. Unfortunately, your understanding of how it’s supposed to be and that of the Board of Directors is a wee bit out of alignment. Somehow, you’ve landed in a mailroom job. But things are about to change. All you need is the right Cubers (Cubical Workers) in the right position and the company will be yours!

'Corporate Life is a game of competitive manipulation and control. Players vie to maneuver Cubers into key department positions, seize credit for the work of others, and pursuit of that coveted CEO promotion.
- from back of box

boardgame: Indiana Jones: Throw Me the Idol!
description:
Do you dare to enter the temple? Thrilling physical action and blazing bouts of card play await you! In this race for fortune and glory, two teams are pitted against each other—Indiana Jones versus his bitter rival Belloq! Gain the most points by outmaneuvering your opponents in laughter-filled, out-of-your-seat challenges!

Players compete in different timed events in dexterity challenges, using props from Indiana Jones movies!

-description from publisher

PRISM - Trick the Light Fantastic is an 18 card trick-taking game in which players aim to capture cards containing 3 colors with the goal of collecting many cards with one color and/or completing sets of cards that contain all 6 colors.

The 18 card deck consists of cards valued 0 to 7 with an uneven distribution. Each trick, a target card is displayed that players will compete to capture. The highest card played wins the target, except a 0-card beats a 7-card. No player may play a card of the same value as already played in the round. The trick winner claims the target card and the card they played becomes the new target card for the next round.

A game lasts a fixed number of tricks based on the number of players. At game end, players score for their best color and for complete card sets that contain all 6 colors.
-description from publisher


Trick the Light Fantastic…

A single white light enters the PRISM and exits as a rainbow of colors. Now it’s up to you to capture those colors as quickly as possible before they fade away. Should you focus on just one specific color or attempt to capture the full spectrum? You’ll have to decide quickly because your options – much like the colors – will vanish.

'PRISM is an ultra-fast trick-taking game for 1 to 5 players using just 6 colors and only 18 cards. Players have only a few turns to trick the light fantastic and claim their victory!
- from back of box

The Franciscan Diamond is an 18 card trick-taking game played with an asymmetric deck of 4 suits. Each suit has a different number of cards.  The game starts with players taking a hidden role and proceeds over 8 rounds.

Each round begins with 3 draft passes where cards are exchanged between all of the players. After the drafts, play consists of tricks where players attempt to capture cards aligned to their secret role while avoiding collecting cards aligned to their role's nemesis. To help keep roles secret, scores in all four suits are kept for each player until the end of the game.

At the end of the game, players score points only for their role cards and lose points for any their nemesis cards obtained.  Cards acquired from the other two suits do not gain or lose the player any points. The player with the highest total score wins.

The Franciscan Diamond requires players to play differently based on their assigned role. It is important to try to uncover the roles of others so that the drafts can ensure the right cards end up with the right players to create tricks that are favorable to a player's goals. This manipulation must then be balanced against keeping your secret role hidden. The result of this mix is an unusual trick-taking game that fans of noir mystery can enjoy.
-description from publisher

The Crime of the Century…

…is about to be committed.  A Master Thief has set her eyes on The Franciscan Diamond and is plotting to steal it, but an equally talented Private Eye is on her trail. Seeking to distract the gumshoe and working her own angle is the Femme Fatale. But it’s no cake walk for her either because The Cops are hounding her every move, all while trying to avoid being played the fools by the Master Thief. Who will emerge victorious from this caper is a mystery!

'The Franciscan Diamond is an 18-card trick-taking game starring four classic characters of a Noir Mystery: the Master Thief, the Private Eye, the Femme Fatale, and The Cops. Players start the game with a secret role and aim to win tricks that score their objective cards while avoiding collecting cards linked to their nemesis. After 8 scenes, the case is closed and the player that best played their role emerges as the victor!
- from back of box

A game of Six Ways 2 Sunday is played over 2 rounds consisting of 6 tricks each. The catch is that the six tricks in each round are not played in sequence, but collectively. That is, a player might play a card to any of the 6 tricks on a given turn. Each trick is limited to 1 card less the number of players, thus not all players will participate in all 6 tricks. The tricks are only resolved when the last card valid card is played and all 6 tricks are completed.

Each trick is won by either the highest or lowest card played depending on the individual trick. Playing a losing card to any trick enables the player to take the special action associated with that trick's day:

Monday: Lets a player move a previously played card.
Tuesday: Lets a player immediately take another turn.
Wednesday: Lets a player change the win condition (high vs Low) for any trick.
Thursday: Lets a player remove a card that has been played.
Friday: Lets a player swap the cards between any 2 tricks.
Saturday: Lets a player draw an extra card into their hand.

All players have an identical deck of 13 cards from which they will draw a hand of 4 cards. The game ends when the second round is completed.  Players score points for each trick taken and additional points for capturing other players' highest and lowest valued cards.
-description from publisher

Every win comes with opportunity costs. Make a move to secure one goal and you might find yourself out of luck everywhere else you look. Every loss invites possibilities. Lose ground on one objective and you might just find new options emerge. Can you balance these factors to secure a win?

'Six Ways 2 Sunday is a tricky trick-taking game about gaining control and seizing opportunities. In each of two rounds, players play to and compete in 6 tricks simultaneously. It takes more than winning tricks, though, because players also score for capturing their opponents’ best cards. Careful timing and crafty play can turn the tables in the blink of an eye. Players must consider all options because there are Six Ways 2 Sunday and even more to victory!
- from back of box

boardgame: Magic: The Gathering – March of the Machine
description:
March of the Machine is an expansion for Magic: The Gathering that focuses on New Phyrexia's invasion of the multiverse. It introduces Battles to the game, a brand new card type—the first new card type since Planeswalkers were introduced in Lorwyn. Additionally, one card per pack is pulled from a bonus sheet of Multiverse Legends consisting of reprinted legendary creatures from all across the different planes, with new art showing them fighting in the war.

Five Commander decks were released alongside the main set as tie-ins, featuring the return of the popular Planechase format. Each deck contains 10 Plane cards.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Unleash your inner warrior and master the art of combat in Martial Clash, a thrilling 2-player board game that combines strategy, skill, and heart-pounding action. Players choose their martial artist character, each with unique stats and techniques, and engage in a fast-paced battle to be the first to claim 2 win tokens. Experience intense rock-paper-scissors style movement phases, strategic dice rolling for combos and counters, and use powerful secret techniques to turn the tide of battle.

To win a player must get 2 WIN tokens, a player gets a WIN token each time the character health of the other player go down to 0.

Game occurs in 2 phases: Movement phase and Combo phase.

Movement phase, players pick a card from the movement cards (hit, block or grab) (rock, paper, scisors mechanic)

The player who won the movement phase becomes the atacker and starts the combo phase.

In the Combo phase, the atacker do a re-roll and lock of a  pool of d6 trying to get the right combinations to do differents techniques.

-description from designer

Justice is about to be served - HOT, fresh, and brimming with tears.

The tables have turned when a group of crustaceans at the mercy of some dastardly chefs gained the power of a vengeful spirit. Now they’re fired-up to exact revenge and claw their way out through anything – or anyone – before reaching the Boiling Point!

Take control of crabs, lobsters, and shrimps climbing over each other to the top in an attempt to fight back the chefs and escape. Play cards to climb, claw, push and pull your way through to the top of the pot. Drink the tears of your enemies to gain powerful boosts. Collect the most points across two tracks: ‘Escape’ and ‘Revenge’ by getting crustaceans out and taking down the chefs. The player that is the best at satisfying both the taste of freedom and vengeance wins!

The taste of victory is sweet but paired with vengeance it’s even sweeter! Gain points for crustaceans rescued, fingers pinched and sous chefs clinched to go down in crustacean history.

—description from the designer

boardgame: New Jersey (fan expansion for Ticket to Ride)
description:
Ticket to Ride: New Jersey is a fan-made expansion to the Days of Wonder, Inc. Ticket to Ride series created by Alan R. Moon. This expansion introduces a whole new board, and a new ticket mechanic. It is designed for players who are already familiar with Ticket to Ride’s standard gameplay and mechanics.

Ticket to Ride: New Jersey is designed to be played with up to 6 players.

Self-published as a craft-it-yourself set for non-commercial use. All rights reserved to the Ticket to Ride name, gameplay, mechanics, and imagery by Days of Wonder, Inc., who have not endorsed or play-tested this expansion.

To play with this expansion, you will need to provide some components taken from Ticket to Ride or Ticket to Ride Europe.

Kitten Kingdoms is a delightful and engaging card game that transports players into a whimsical world where adorable kittens rule over various realms. There are four distinct factions: Fluffington Aristocats, Purrville Innovators, Clawtopia Rogues, and Whiskeria Ambassadors. Each faction offers unique abilities and strategies, allowing players to craft their own style of play and outwit their opponents.

Fluffington: Focuses on drawing cards
Purrville: Focuses on rotating kittens
Whiskeria: Focuses on playing recruitment cards
Clawtopia: Focuses on disruption and chaos in the game

This game is a deck-building style game where players build decks of cards to recruit their own or opponents' kittens with a unique rotation mechanic and recruitment mechanic.

There are a few types of cards in the game:

Kitten Heroes - These are the cards you are trying to recruit.
Kitten Lords - These are powerful leaders of the factions that give high scores and excellent bonuses for those players that recruit them.
Recruiters - These attach to Kitten Heroes and give bonuses.
Motivators - These are cards you play during your turn to motivate kittens to join your cause.
Pounce - These cards can be played at any time and are usually for disrupting your opponent.

The game ends when 6 of the 8 Kitten Lords are recruited. Scores are then tallied, and the player with the highest recruitment score wins.

Games usually last 30-35 minutes but this is highly variable as 6 of the Lords need to be found.

boardgame: 손맛 전자 빠른 수리 (Hand Repair by Sorting)
description:
Fix the television by sorting the components. The first player who repairs get the job. Find a way to efficiently sort the cards!

'Hand Repair by Sorting' is a game where players sort number cards in ascending order. The player who sorts the cards first wins the game. All players progress through the rounds simultaneously. Each round consists of 'flipping cards' and 'shuffling the deck'.

In the 'flipping cards' phase, each player flips over one card and performs an action that allows them to sort the number cards. In the 'shuffling the deck' phase, each player has the option to shuffle all the used cards back into the deck.

-description from designer

boardgame: Advanced Dungeon Hero (fan expansion for Dungeon Hero)
description:
Expand your adventures furhter, with this 10 page supplement for Dungeon Hero and AdvanceQuest!

This expansion includes rules for Resting, Healing, Magic Items & Potions and content generation meant to be used alongside the game's adventures.

An educational Bulgarian game (as of 2023 only available in the Bulgarian language). In this competitive card drawing game the goal is to collect 6 cards of the same colour (4 types in total). Card trading, attacking players and encountering destructive cards by chance are also elements present in the game.

—user summary

Представи си за момент настолна игра, чрез която всяко дете може да опознае България и да научи за нейното културно-историческо наследство, докато се забавлява с приятели и близки.

Игра, която е достатъчно лесна, за да не се отегчават малчуганите в разучаване на правилата ѝ, но е толкова забавна и разнообразна, че да не омръзне и за миг.

Игра, която е подходяща за малки и пораснали деца, и може да се играе навсякъде и по всяко време.

Не е нужно да си я представяш, защото...

Тя е вече тук!

Запознай се със СЪБЕРИ БЪЛГАРИЯ.

Trick of Chicken is a trick-taking game for 3 to 4 players.  The game uses an irregular deck of 3 suits.  It is a must-follow game, but the winner will be the person who played the lowest ranked card in the suit with the highest sum in the trick - with some special cards, such as the 2 which doubles (x2) other cards of its suit.

Cards from tricks are placed around a central disc showing the three suits.  Specifically, the winner will take their card face down in front of themselves, but the other cards from the trick go face down next to the wheel in front of the spot for the suit which won the trick.   The winner of the last trick takes all of the cards from the sector of the wheel which matches the card they won the trick with, setting them in front of themselves face up.

Players score points for face down and face up cards. The game takes place over several rounds and the player with the highest total score wins.

London, 1893. A prominent paleontologist has been murdered, struck down by a fossilized dinosaur bone. A group of naturalists, gathered to hear the victim speak, provide an abundance of witnesses and potential suspects. The Royal Society is in an uproar and Scotland Yard is at a loss. Only the world’s greatest consulting detective, Sherlock Holmes, can unravel this mystery.

his case folder contains the following components:

An entry from Dr. John Watson’s personal diary
    Details on Sherlock Holmes’ hand-picked investigators
    A special section of The Illustrated Police News, a Victorian tabloid featuring reporting on the scandalous murder
    “Welcome to Burlington House,” a brochure and map of The Royal Society’s headquarters (and scene of the crime!)
    A list of Holmes’ personal contacts and informants throughout the city
    Full-color 18×24″ map of 1893 London
    A 36-page city directory with over 500 residential and business locations
    Log sheets for tracking investigator movements
    Sealed ‘Solve Envelope’ with questions for the end of the investigation

boardgame: The Case of the Blinded Birder
description:
Bockerly, England. 1955. Near the sleepy English village of Bockerley, a reclusive birder is shot in the woods. The police declare it a hunting accident and no suspects are brought to trial. The case goes cold until a sparky spinster with a lace umbrella and gossip’s ear finds proof that it was murder. Was it his overbearing mother? His dead wife’s violent brother? A poacher on the run from his past? And why are the police saying less than they know? Help her and her bibliophilic friends solve this classic cozy mystery.

This case folder contains the following components:

‘Personal & Confidential’ envelope with Introductory letter from Cecily Miller
    Investigator details
    Section of the Bockerly Chronicle, featuring reporting on the murder
    Full-color 12 x18″ map: “The Village of Bockerly and Surrounds”
    A 12-page city directory with hundreds of residential and business locations
    Log sheets for tracking investigator movements
    Sealed ‘Solve Envelope’ with questions for the end of the investigation

Los Angeles, CA, 1948. A young starlet is found murdered in her own home. The LAPD has a strong lead and a likely suspect, but the girl’s mother wants no stone left unturned. Are the police correct in pursuing a gangster, known for harassing actresses and not taking no for an answer? Should they look closer at her hot-blooded matinee-idol ex-boyfriend? Or even the rising Los Angeles District Attorney who nearly lost his job and marriage after his affair with the starlet went public?

Study the clues. Send out your team. Solve the case.

This case folder contains the following components:

Transcript of initial interview with the client, Rose Fitzgibbon
    Details on the Knight Detective Agency’s investigators
    A section of The Los Angeles Times featuring reporting on murder
    “While You Were Out” notepad message
    List of expert contacts and informants throughout the city
    Full-color 17×23″ map of 1948 Los Angeles
    30-page City Directory with over 500 residential and business locations
    Log sheets for tracking investigator movements
    Sealed ‘Solve Envelope’ with questions for the end of the investigation

Atmosfear, the iconic horror-themed board game, has returned in its 30th Anniversary Edition, and it's as thrilling and immersive as ever! This modernized version of the classic game comes with updated components (through the Kickstarter campaign) and a nostalgic nod to the original, making it a must-have for both longtime fans and newcomers alike.

Components:
The 30th Anniversary Edition of Atmosfear comes with high-quality components that are visually stunning and tactilely pleasing. The game includes a beautifully illustrated game board, six character pawns to collect keys for, multiple types of cards (Time, Fatez and Chance), and 2x 6D with the Gatekeeper's head in them! The game now comes with a QR code to find the video of the game on Vimeo instead of a VHS or DVD featuring the iconic Gatekeeper, who sets the tone for the game with his sinister presence. The components are durable and well-designed, adding to the overall immersion and enjoyment of the game.

Gameplay:
The gameplay of Atmosfear remains true to the original, with some modern updates that add to the excitement. Players take on the roles of six different characters, each with their own strengths and weaknesses, as they navigate through the various regions of the board, trying to collect keys and make their way to the center to face the Gatekeeper. The video is used as a timer, adding a sense of urgency to the game, and the Gatekeeper's appearances on the screen are both thrilling and chilling (and loud!).

One of the standout features of the 30th Anniversary Edition is the addition of new event cards, which bring a fresh level of unpredictability to the game. These cards can affect players in various ways, such as forcing them to swap places, lose turns, or face challenges that can alter the course of the game. The combination of the video, cards, and unique character abilities creates a dynamic and engaging gameplay experience that keeps players on their toes and eager for more.

Theme and Atmosphere:
Atmosfear is all about creating a spooky and immersive atmosphere, and the 30th Anniversary Edition does not disappoint. The game's theme of horror and suspense is brought to life through the video, which features the iconic Gatekeeper, who taunts and challenges players throughout the game. The game board is richly illustrated with dark and eerie artwork that adds to the overall ambiance. The combination of the components, gameplay, and theme work together seamlessly to create an immersive and thrilling experience that will keep players on the edge of their seats.

Fun Factor:
Atmosfear 30th Anniversary Edition is an absolute blast to play. The combination of the video, event cards, and unique character abilities create a highly interactive and unpredictable gameplay experience that keeps players engaged and entertained from start to finish. The sense of urgency created by the timer adds to the excitement, and the overall atmosphere of the game is thrilling and immersive. The game also encourages player interaction, with opportunities for strategic decision-making, alliances, and betrayals, making for a highly enjoyable and memorable gaming experience.

Overall:
Atmosfear 30th Anniversary Edition is a fantastic board game that successfully revitalizes the classic horror-themed game for a modern audience. The components, gameplay, and immersive atmosphere make for an engaging and thrilling experience that will keep players coming back for more. Whether you're a longtime fan of the original or new to the world of Atmosfear, this anniversary edition is a must-have addition to your board game collection. Get ready to face your fears and embrace the darkness with Atmosfear 30th Anniversary Edition!

Melting Planet is a cooperative game, that is, in this game the players do not compete with each other but rather work together for a common goal, in this case, to keep the planet habitable for as long as possible.

Melting Planet has been exposed to the use and abuse of its natural resources that increases the temperature by 0.5°C, 1°C, 1.5°C and 2°C.

The challenge for the players is to align the necessary interventions (decks) to save the planet at different scales, that is, to assemble the stairs of each deck.

—description from the designer

SOE: Lysander is the first of a new set of low-cost, book-based games in the "Special Missions" series that put you directly into the cockpit.

Your mission is as a pilot in Great Britain's most secret aviation unit, "A" Flight of RAF 161 Squadron (Special Duties). You will fly Special Operations Executive (SOE) agents, wireless operators, saboteurs, assassins, political figures, heads of the French Resistance, and others into and out of German-occupied France during the darkest times of the Second World War.

By moonlight, you will navigate your Westland Lysander Mk. IIIA (SD) aircraft across the Channel and over occupied France to find a distant landing zone deep behind enemy lines. You will encounter flak, German patrols, small arms fire, night fighters, and the constant threat of being discovered by the Gestapo.

SOE: Lysander is in the solitaire narrative style and with a small table footprint and easy-to-learn rules. While the gameplay is designed to be straightforward, the challenges you will face are extensive. Your goal is to keep your assigned Circuits supplied -- these are underground partisan cells in the French Resistance. Your operations begin in April 1943 and your mission continues until just before D-Day.

The odds of survival are slim. Do you have what it takes?

Available through Amazon (worldwide): https://www.amazon.com/dp/B0C9SLG2ND/

The boxed set of game maps and assets (game counters and chits) is also available from Blue Panther!
https://www.bluepantherllc.com/products/soe-lysander

—description from designer
family: Series: Air War Book Games (Historic Wings)

description:
Splintercard is a 1 or 2 player science fiction wargame contained in a single 5x7 inch card you can print for free. Most games last less than ten minutes.

Try to destroy your opponent's city. Take turns playing cards which either control your forces, launch nukes, repair your city or skip a turn. There's more to it - the stakes grow as time moves on, there are multiple strategies to win, and a solo mode is included.

Deep in the woods at The Cabin, primal horror waits in every shadow.

Feral Instincts drags the nightmare into the forest, centered on The Cabin — a rustic yet foreboding location ringing with eerie howls and unseen menace. This expansion introduces two new killers: the lethal Huntress, expert in deadly traps, and the feral Werewolf, a predator that reacts to every sound and pursues survivors with brutal strength. Each comes with unique miniatures, killer boards, and skill cards that reshape how hunting is approached. Survivors must also contend with trap tokens and treasure boxes, plus new items that deepen strategic play around noise, movement, and risk.

Inside the box:
- 1 Killer game board with map "The Cabin"
- 1 Survivor game board with map "The Cabin"
- 4 Killer miniatures (2 Killers x2)
- 2 Killer boards
- 26 Killer skill cards (2 Killers x13)
- 4 treasure box tokens
- 8 trap tokens
- 1 organizer
- 4 items
- 1 Expansion #1 rulebook

In the abandoned Laboratory, something not meant to live now wants to feed.

The Amorphous Peril expansion plunges survivors into a forgotten Laboratory overrun by two terrifying entities — The Unidentified and The Strangler — whose grotesque forms and unpredictable attacks turn clinical hallways into deadly gauntlets. This expansion’s Lab map, killer miniatures, ability cards, and thematic tokens heighten tension with dangerous terrain and brutal surprises. Survivors must adapt their tactics to evade multiple threats and leverage the new items and environment challenges this setting brings.

Inside the box:
- 1 Killer game board with map "The Laboratory"
- 1 Survivor game board with map "The Laboratory"
- 4 Killer miniatures (2 Killers x2)
- 2 Killer boards
- 26 Killer skill cards (2 Killers x13)
- 10 core tokens
- 1 organizer
- 1 Expansion #2 rulebook

description:
At The Castle, ancient defenses have become nightmares in their own right.

Lethal Immortals sends players into the forbidding Castle deep on a lonely hill, where old stone and hidden mechanisms conspire against those who enter. Within these walls lurk new killer forces — a collective mind of sentinels and horrors bound to the fortress known as The Statues — hiding within plain sight and attacking those who enter their claustrophobic corridors and deadly rooms. You also get a new survivor figure, George Carpenter, a smart, brave and caring professor, with his talented mind and professional knowledge, he has to use all his smarts to outwith the Killer.

Inside the box:

- 13 Killer skill cards

- 1 Killer assistance token

A dynamic party game with lots of 3-level questions!
In Su o Giù? one player answers the question on the card and the others have to bet whether the correct answer is higher or lower. Play bonuses, score points and win after 3 rounds.
So many questions with different themes, impossible to know them all.

In Starlight you play as a galactic trader buying and selling ancient alien relics for profit. Players manipulate the price of relics by changing the board (which is made up of 16 Location Cards). Prices can be further nudged using Research Cards, but players should make sure not to run out of fuel and have to rely on Investors!

—description from the publisher

The Shattered Isles introduces a brand new world, with three new Beasts, three new hunters, two maps, four contracts, and a heap of new and exciting gameplay mechanics. Shattered Isles comes with 4 new Action Cards, an updated and improved way of drafting your cards, 13 new Items, 13 new Beastly Talents, and 4 new Contracts that change the way you play the game.

Beasts: Vacker, Thalduk, Akkeron

Hunters: Eivind, Fara, Valdi

New Mechanics


     The two new maps feature Hidden Paths: pathways that can only be accessed by characters native to the same habitat the path originates from.
     Ruins can be found on the map. Begin a turn in the same location as a ruin to unlock forgotten and powerful magic.
     Split Actions can be played in combination with any other card for the highest flexibility possible.


—description from the designer
An expansion that allows for playing with three players.

boardgame: Prisoner's Trilemma Game Theory in Action
description:
The Prisoner’s Dilemma is the most famous conundrum in Game Theory. Two criminals are caught by the police for a minor crime. They are suspected of a major crime which, if convicted, would put them behind bars for a long time indeed. The police separate the two partners in crime and offer them a bargain. If they betray the other prisoner, they will be released. If both stay silent the police will only be able convict them of their minor crime. If they both take the offer and betray each other they will both serve a long sentence. In Prisoner’s Trilemma: Game Theory in Action, players are presented with a variety of moral dilemmas where they have three possible choices to make. Players can choose to cooperate, betray or compromise to make the best of a difficult situation.

OBJECTIVE – The objective of Prisoner’s Trilemma is to work through moral quandaries each with three possible choices presented to the players scoring points based on the outcomes.
GAME PLAY – In each of five rounds, players are presented with a moral trilemma. Players play cards from their hand representing the degree to which they cooperate, betray or compromise in the situation. Payoff cards are revealed in each round which indicates points earned from the decisions the players make.
GAME END AND WINNING – Prisoner’s Trilemma ends after five rounds, when the players considered five trilemmas.
THEME – Game Theory Exploration

—description from the designer

In Coloma: New Prospects, the game becomes deeper and wider, with heavier mechanisms, more sophisticated engine-building, and added paths to victory. It also flips the script on the Boom/Bust system — making it so players want different outcomes depending on what’s at stake.  Additions include:


    Player Board Extensions and Waterfall Tiles - Each player board is now extended to include a network of wilderness trails to explore. Each time you Bust, you may advance one space. To trailblaze a safe path loaded with valuable bonuses, you can add Waterfall tiles (which replace the Rivers from the core game).



    All-New Decks of Cards - Each player gets a completely new deck of Town cards to replace the ones from the core game. These cards offer a variety of advanced engine-building mechanisms.



    New Rotating Barker Tile -  The rotating magnetic Barker tile is replaced with a new one that offers more ways to gain Barrels from the Outlaws. It also brings Buster into the multiplayer game.


—Description from the publisher

Easter is finally here and the annual egg-hunt is a long-awaited favourite for both young and old. Sharpen your sight, be the fastest egg hunter and above all - loot the most candy. Let the adventures found in Easter Quest begin!

In Easter Quest, each turn begins by revealing a card that depict an egg that matches an egg token somewhere in the forest (on the game board). All eggs appear to look the same but they actually differ slightly. The faster you locate the right egg token, the more likely you are to reach it before anyone else.

The game comes with both a basic and an advanced variant.

In the basic variant (children's variant) you will also need luck with the roll the dice and choose smart paths to be successful.

In the advanced variant (family variant) you need to find the shortest path AND count the correct number of steps, before anyone else, to be successful. Do not forget that you can "warp" by leaving the game board at one end and enter it at the other side.

The first player to reach the egg gets to loot it by drawing a candy token from the bag. The player with most candy win (as in real life).

The perfect game to play during Easter, or on any other day of the year.

In Cascadia: Landmarks expansion, players create sprawling habitats and place beautiful natural landmarks within them. Landmarks give each environment its own look and feel, while also providing dynamic endgame scoring bonuses!

In addition to the Landmarks module, this expansion includes more unique wildlife scoring cards and habitat tiles to add variety and enough components to play Cascadia with 5-6 players.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

boardgame: Monster Hunter World: Iceborne – The Board Game
description:
A mysterious song is echoing over the New World and causing the mass migration of Legiana. Now, the Research Commission needs YOU to investigate. Become a hunter of the Fifth Fleet and head to an uncharted arctic tundra where unknown threats roam wild. Protect the new Seliana basecamp from monsters unlike any you’ve faced before. Claim monster loot from your successful hunts to craft awesome new equipment, so you can hunt bigger and badder beasts until you’ve mastered Hoarfrost Reach!

The follow-up to Monster Hunter World, Monster Hunter World Iceborne: The Board Game is a cooperative arena combat game bursting with all new quests, exciting new gameplay, and a whole new line-up of MASSIVE monsters to hunt! It introduces some game-changing Iceborne twists. Navigate frozen terrain. Use your Clutch Claw to get close to the beasts. Find bonus info when tracking to help in the fight ahead. Manage your stamina in a new way.

Like World, Iceborne will plunge you into an open world where monsters won’t sit back and be hunted. Every fight is a boss fight. The monster controls the flow of combat, pushing you and your party to the limit — and each monster is wildly different. It’s crush-or-be-crushed with beasts as mobile as these!

Choose your hunter based on their unique weapon and playstyle. Pick which infamous monster you’ll be hunting across the ice. Track the creature in a quest driven by your decisions, avoiding pitfalls and collecting bonus resources to help you in the challenges ahead. Then dive into card-driven combat where teamwork, positioning, and stamina are key, targeting the monster parts you need for the equipment you want to craft — without getting wiped out!


     Play multiple epic campaigns with different combos of hunters, monsters, and equipment. The more monsters you slay, the better the weapons you can craft, the bigger the monsters you can hunt!
     Every monster comes with single session arena quests, so you can plunge right into combat.
     Monster Hunter World Iceborne: The Board Game includes solo mode.

A two-player card game based on BCU (Baniak Cthulhu Universe) - well-known in Poland streamed RPG sessions.
In this game your goal is to arrange played cards on the board in such a way as to lure the opponent to your side of madness.

At the beginning, each player must build their investigator deck up to a maximum of 20 cards with a mix of enemies, vehicles, items, artefacts, gods. Remember that they should be connected with each other by at least one of the three keywords.
Investigators and enemies have high attributes; while vehicles move other cards; items, spells and artefacts are weaker but can perform special actions; and outer gods bring huge changes in the world (board), but require an opened gate.
Cards (in a well-assembled deck) will resonate with each other causing mayhem on the board and creating un-obvious traps.

Players alternately play one card at a time, trying to turn the opponent's cards to their side (light or dark). We do this with the attributes on the sides of the cards and their special abilities.
You cannot hide cards in your hands, you must play either from the top of the pile or from the two available slots in the stash. So the cards are open to both players and we can try to anticipate his movements. But so is he! Whoever gets more followers at the end wins.

Something Wild is a line of card games featuring beloved characters and collectible Pop! pocket figures. Play numbered and colored character cards to make sets and runs. The first player to score three powers wins the game.

With Something Wild: Indiana Jones, you can score a set to snag the Indiana Jones figure's special power to help you win!

Combine Character Cards in sets and runs to score points, just like in Rummy. Use Power Cards to add a fun twist to the classic card gameplay.

Combine multiple Something Wild! games to add more character cards, more Pop! figures, and more powers to your game!

A survival/strategy board game that takes place during the last Ice Age. Players navigate the board and participate in the actions at their leisure. But like life, the game can be unpredictable and force you into action and even take everything you have accumulated away from you. Work with the other players or go it alone as you fight for survival in a world full of prehistoric beasts such as woolly mammoths and sabre-toothed cats.

—description from the designer

Visit amazing sights, taste a variety of foods, and bring home great souvenirs in Tiny Travels. Whoever experiences the most within seven days wins!

Each round, one player rolls four dice and chooses two of them to pursue activities at twelve different locations. All other players get the remaining two dice. For each die, you cross off one matching number box of an activity at your current location. If you cross off all boxes of an activity, you get the activity symbols associated with it. Activity symbols are your main source of points. You can also choose to move to an adjacent location once per turn and use one or both dice there.

Lastly, the player who rolled the dice has to recommend an activity they pursued for the other players to follow.

The game ends after 21 rounds have been played. The final score is calculated, and the player with the most points had the most fulfilling journey.

Football: Play It Again is a 1-2 player board game that simulates key moments in a football/soccer match. As a team coach or a spectator, you roll three dice (two 6-sided and one 12-sided die) to generate the event that occurred on the field.
The game has high playability, realism, and statistical accuracy. It allows matches to be played with retro teams taking into account the rules and playing styles of play in the era.
Sample events: long shots, headers, goalkeeper parades, tackles, cards, ricochets, injuries, the influence of soccer stars, unusual events.
The game includes 32 unique fantasy national teams (640 players) which can be used to play fantasy World Cup.
Players use tactics (basic and advanced) to attack or to defend the score. The player who scores more goals wins the game.

—description from the designer

S.U.N. is a strategy board game played with cards and tokens.

Each player represents one of the species of the space federation that competes with the others to be the most technologically advanced without open hostilities. The first player to reach 21 technology points wins.

It combines the obtaining of resources to build technologies and get the points to win, with an important component of negotiation with the rest of the players. The diplomatic proposals are advantages that you can obtain, but they have to be voted among the players for their approval, so you will have to negotiate to convince them. You will have the opportunity to be President, or to become a despotic Emperor.

You can keep your promises, or not. And you can play Subterfuge behind the backs of your peers...

You decide the reputation you want to have in the Federation.

—description from the designer
