boardgame: Star Wars: Rivals – Series 1: Character Booster Pack
description:
Star Wars: Rivals is an expandable card game in which the light and dark sides of the Force face off, with each player building a squadron from characters throughout different eras of the Star Wars universe.

Each Star Wars: Rivals – Series 1 – Character Booster Pack includes one character from the light side or dark side that can be added to the squadrons in the standalone Premier Set or that can be played on their own as a squadron against a squadron of the opposite side.

Each pack consists of the character, its related character card, two location cards, a game die, five action cards, and 16 tokens and markers. Series 1 of the Booster Packs consists of nine "light side" characters, nine "dark side" characters, and eight "hologram" characters, four of each side.

Playing with Lovecraft is a Lovecraftian Card Game to children 6+ years old.

Objective
Explore the main deck to capture Elder Ones, Deep Ones, Ghouls and find Gates to make points.

Gameplay
The game is played in turns. Every turn, players explore a main deck opening a card (gate, elder one or monster) and each player choose a card with a number (force 1-9).


     Gates: Make each player discard the choose card.
     Elder Ones: The UNIQUE high card capture a card.
     Monsters (Deep Ones and Ghouls): The UNIQUE low card capture a card.


After 9 turns the game is end and a player with more points wins.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Thorgal: The Board Game – Companion Cards
description:
Embark on a journey through the land of Thorgal with a cast of diverse and dynamic characters by your side. Each companion brings unique abilities and strengths to the table, allowing you to tackle challenges and defeat enemies easily.

Includes: 8 cards

boardgame: Fancy Feathers: It is getting colorful!
description:
New pheasants are spotted on the path. Thus, you immediately hit the trail to offer your baroness some variety of game.

This first expansion for Fancy Feathers offers 6 new types of animal cards.

Look out for the Crested Fireback, Sumatran Pheasant, Kokass Pheasant, Eared Pheasant, the Barking Dog, and use the Long Hand Net.

Same as the base game of Fancy Feathers, this expansion works with up to six players, but to play with more than two people, you need additional copies of the expansion to have the required numbers of animal cards.

—description from the publisher

Sprawdzian (Test) is an educational game for 2–4 players (or teams). It contains a booklet with 1080 questions and answers from categories like biology, geography, physics, chemistry, history, Polish language, sports, mathematics, culture and English and German languages.

The goal of the game is to fill all the fields on a player's board with matching cards. Cards can be acquired by correctly answering the questions — 1 cards per 1 correct answer. The winner is the player who fills his board first.

Global Mining was conceived and developed by real miners who are also fans of board games. It is a turn-based strategy game whose goal is to get the best result consisting in creating a global company dealing with the exploitation of all minerals and raw materials. The map of the main game is a map of the earth with a representation of actual resources. The game will be produced in 4 versions:

     Full Edition: 180 map tiles,
     North and South America: 60 map tiles.
     Europe and Africa: 60 map tiles.
     Asia and Australia: 60 map tiles.


Each edition will contain a basic set of pawns for each player: Geologist, Mine, Pipeline, Seaport, Railroad, Oil Rig and a proportional number of resource pawns: Oil Barrels, Gas Barrels, Coal, Gold, Silver, Copper, Iron Ore, Rare Earth Metals, Nickel, Magnesite, Stone.

Global Mining is a turn-based game, the winner is the player who scores the most points within a certain time. Points are earned by: discovering natural resources such as Gold, building mines, building pipelines, building railways, building seaports, establishing transport lines for ships.
Global Mining is based on linguistically universal symbols. The game manual in the first edition will be translated into: Polish, English, German, Spanish, French, Czech.

Spelet om Allsvenskan: Säsongen 2023 is the first expansion of Spelet om Allsvenskan. It adds new teams, new rankings, new schedules, and two new deck of cards to replace the content in the basic game.
This expansion, released only in Polish, contains the contents of both Munchkin Legends 2: Faun and Games and Munchkin Legends 3: Myth Prints.

A free Print and Play solitaire game based on the cold war. Perfect for educational purpose or just to have fun!

In this game, you are the leader of one of these two superpowers, and all you have is your strategy and instincts to help protect and lead your nation to glory. Can you outwit your opponent and come out on top?

The fate of your nation rests in your hands.

The game takes place in 18 rounds that retrace 18 real events during the cold war. Each event is represented by a card that describes the details of the event. The objective of the game is avoid a nuclear holocaust and, at the end of 18 rounds, being the nation with the highest dice value in at least two areas of influence and win the cold war.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Munchkin Zombie 3/4: Ręka, noga, mózg w kanale
description: This expansion, released only in Polish, contains the contents of both Munchkin Zombies 3: Hideous Hideouts and Munchkin Zombies 4: Spare Parts.

Compete with fellow Sasquatches in this push-your-luck card game to prepare the most delicious barbecue using questionable ingredients. Foul play is never off-limits, but stay away from nosey cryptozoologists that try to disrupt your feast.

Each turn, start by going on a Hunt to gather Ingredients and Recipes by flipping cards from a central draw pile into an open draft. Push your luck by flipping more cards to have more options to choose from, but make sure you don't run into a Cryptozoologist because they will surely ruin your Hunt.

After Hunting for Ingredients, head back to your BBQ Pit where you commit Ingredients to your Recipe cards in order to cook the dishes and score points.

The first Sasquatch to cook an Appetizer, a Main Course, and a Dessert triggers the final round. After the final round, Sasquatches tally up their points to find out who is the BBQ Pitmaster.

—description from the designer
Expansion, released only in Polish, contains the contents of 8 boosters: Fairy Dust, Munchkinomicon, Monster Enhancers, Game Changers, Dragons, Dragon's Trike, Undead, Princesses.

boardgame: Marvel: Crisis Protocol – Squirrel Girl & Gwenpool
description:
With Marvel: Crisis Protocol – Squirrel Girl & Gwenpool, players will turn the laws of reality into an open negotiation. This duo of fourth-wall-breakers will suspend disbelief as they bend the usual flow of gameplay to suit their own unique playstyles. Equipped with Team Tactic Cards that make them perfect tank-busters, this duo comes ready to warp the battlefield into a far stranger place to play.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Star Wars: Legion – Ahsoka Tano Operative Expansion
description:
Star Wars: Legion – Ahsoka Tano Operative Exapnsion brings a new character to the fight for the Rebellion.

A key member of the founding of what would become the Rebel Alliance, Ahsoka Tano took on the codename "Fulcrum" and worked tirelessly to supply vital intelligence to the burgeoning Rebel cells. Ahsoka brings both her potent skills with her lightsabers as well as her years of experience as a commander during the Clone Wars to the battlefield. In battle, Ahsoka uses her agility and skills to help protect her fellow troops while she positions herself to strike down the most dangerous threats using her mastery of the Jar'Kai lightsaber form. Combined with her four signature command cards Ahsoka is sure to defend the Rebellion through its most vulnerable time.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Star Wars: X-Wing (Second Edition) – Galactic Empire Squadron Starter Pack
description:
Star Wars: X-Wing (Second Edition) – Galactic Empire Squadron Starter Pack contains everything a new player needs to fly into battle in support of the Galactic Empire. From miniatures to measurement tools to dice and tokens, all the core components for Star Wars: X-Wing can be found in this set. The four iconic Galactic Empire ships included in this starter boast enough firepower to crush any opposition and show the galaxy the true might of the Galactic Empire!

—description from the publisher
family: Game: Star Wars X-Wing Miniatures Game

boardgame: Star Wars: X-Wing (Second Edition) – Rebel Alliance Squadron Starter Pack
description:
Star Wars: X-Wing (Second Edition) – Rebel Alliance Squadron Starter Pack contains everything a new player needs to fly into battle alongside the heroes of the Rebel Alliance. From miniatures to measurement tools to dice to tokens, the core components of Star Wars: X-Wing can be found in this set. The four iconic Rebel Alliance ships included in this starter are the perfect tools to return peace to the galaxy in the name of the Rebel Alliance!

—description from the publisher
family: Game: Star Wars X-Wing Miniatures Game

description:
FAST, MERCILESS ADVENTURE AWAITS!

Assemble your team of rag-tag heroes, defy all logic and warnings, and head into the abysmal world of Aezmae to defeat the evil Maedorn in one final, epic showdown. Labyrinth Adventures is a dungeon-crawl adventure gamebook series for 1-4 heroes ready to embark upon the adventure of a lifetime!

DELVE INTO:

☑️ BRUTALLY RELENTLESS COMBAT: Hit first, hit hard! Build your defences to survive!

☑️ QUICK PLAY THAT'S STRATEGIC & TACTICAL: A low-rule set makes gameplay quick and satisfying while remaining deep. Each session lasts 1 hour.

☑️ VICIOUS PERILS AND EPIC REWARDS: Each step might be your last. Will you find a trove of riches or meet your demise?

☑️ ENGROSSING LORE: Immersive backstories, terrifying enemy stats, and fantasy tales that draw you into the darkness!

☑️ SELF-GENERATING DUNGEONS: Watch mesmerizing labyrinths unfold through simple D6 rolls.

☑️ UNIQUE CHARACTERS EVERY TIME:  Specialized upgrades give you the power to evolve your hero the way you choose.

The adventurous sailing competition on the wadden sea
A game for sailors and not sailors. for young and old.
Everybody gets a boat and six flags. Put a flag on every island.
If you get the tide right you get an extra dice. If you get the wind right you get an extra dice. The one who is the first to return back in the harbor of Harlingen, wins the sailing competition.
Watch out for the seal :)

—description from the designer

Play Soccer is a fun and simple card game inspired by video games and football cards. You will have to form the line-up that best combines the skills of your players to score the most victory points and win the game.

At the start of the game each player receives 10 player cards and 1 goalkeeper card to place in their playing area to create a 1-4-4-2 formation.

Each turn you can take a player card from the transfer market and replace it with another card from the line-up. Each player has different skills: tackling, crossing, passing, shooting and foot dominant. Depending on where each player is in the line-up and how he is combined with other player cards, victory points will be awarded. In addition, goalkeeper cards function as goals that can bring extra points.

The game comes to an end when the cards in the player draw deck are used up. Whoever has the most victory points wins the game.


Play Soccer es un divertido y sencillo juego de cartas inspirado en videojuegos y cromos de fútbol. Tendrás que formar la alineación que mejor combine las habilidades de tus futbolistas para lograr la mayor cantidad de puntos de victoria y alzarte con la victoria.

Al comienzo de la partida cada persona recibe 10 cartas de futbolistas y 1 carta de guardameta para situarlas en su zona de juego creando una formación 1-4-4-2.

En cada turno se puede coger una carta de futbolista del mercado de fichajes y sustituirla por otra carta de la alineación. Cada futbolista tiene diferentes habilidades: entrada, centro, pase, tiro y pie dominante. Dependiendo del lugar que ocupe cada futbolista en la alineación y de cómo se combine con otras cartas de futbolista, se obtendrán puntos de victoria. Además, las cartas de guardameta funcionan como objetivos que pueden aportar puntos extra.

La partida llega a su fin cuando se acaben las cartas del mazo de robo de futbolistas. Quien tenga más puntos de victoria, se llevará la victoria.

A food delivery game in a dungeon. Simple rules that can be explained in less than a minute, highly interactive strategy game Dragon Eats.
You need to collect the food items shown on the order card and complete the order to earn points. This is a set collection game that utilizes the timetracking mechanic. 
Released at Tokyo Game Market 2023.

—description from the designer

Paneveggio will immerse you in 1700 close to the Paneveggio Forest where generations of renowned luthiers such as Amati, Stradivarius or Guardani took the precious wood to build the famous violins we still hear about today.
Players will embody young luthiers who are launching their own workshop after leaving their mentor.
They will try to earn as much Violin points (VP) as possible and offer their most beautiful work of art to prestigious musicians of that time by alternating work sessions and rest.
The game is based on a musical score mechanics, the notes are then representing planned actions.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
The first print run of Skeptics included a Ten Card promotional pack as a thank you for helping our first project succeed. This was included with all Kickstarter copies and the rest of the copies during the first print run.

The Exclusive Ten Card Promotional Pack includes the following:

x1 Special Item Card - Thurible
 x1 Museum Environment Card - ORB
 x1 Asylum Environment Card - Cold Spot
 x1 Common Environment Card - The Other Side
 x1 Item Card - Music Box
 x4 Clue Cards - Old Vase, Ghostly Presence, Coffer, Under Pressure
x1 Promotional Pack Rules Card

A micro game that fits in your pocket, the Aetherial War: Untold Adventures is perfect for on-the-go dungeon exploration within the Aetherial War universe. As the little brother to the Aetherial War Card Game, don’t let the small size fool you: this game packs tons of replay value and loads of achievements to try and accomplish. It may all fit inside a tin, but that doesn’t mean it can’t hold its own for when you’re looking for a fun, light-weight game to break out and put away in under 20 minutes.

—description from the designer

With a state-of-the-art time machine powering our vessel, traveling through time or visiting one of many parallel worlds is as simple as putting down a boarding pass. Have a good trip!

5つの並行世界と5つの時間を自由に行き来する「PARALLEL TIME MACHINE VOYAGE」。
旅行者の皆さんは、搭乗券をうまく使って最高の旅を満喫してください。

A 40-card deck of special missions, plots, and objectives from which players can draw to add extra intrigue to their games.

The goal for most of the independent crews operating throughout the Ravaged Galaxy is that one big score that will set them up for life. Such scores are few and far between, so crews typically find themselves taking whatever jobs they can scrounge – anything for a quick credit. Anyone can sell an alien artefact or a set of neural-fuses, but smart captains know that there are myriad ways to turn a profit.

This expansion for Stargrave consists of 40 Side Hustle cards – missions, plots, and tasks that add variety, depth, and new tactical challenges to wargames in the Ravaged Galaxy. Each card presents a specific objective - and the rewards for achieving it. Some of these missions must be revealed, while others are kept secret.

Is your crew attempting to insert a data virus into a key system? Locate a political prisoner? Uncover evidence of a secret scientific cabal? Every crew is out to grab some loot, but what's your captain's side hustle?

Squeaky Clean is a solo deck-cycling strategy game about laundering more than just clothes.

You play as a scrappy upstart in the grime-stained criminal underworld, running a laundromat that’s a little too good at cleaning dirty money. Across eight rounds, you’ll draw from a deck filled with resources, crimes, cops (the Fuzz), and opportunities to upgrade your laundering operation. Spend crew, stash cash, avoid heat, and rotate, flip, and repurpose your deck’s cards to your advantage. But watch out—if the Fuzz catches you with too much heat, you’ll lose your gains. Balance risk and reward, and upgrade your laundromat to Level 3 before time runs out to win.

Gameplay unfolds through clever deck manipulation: each card is multi-use, offering choices that ripple through future turns. The more crimes you commit, the more you must time your laundering cycles and bribe the cops—or gamble on pulling the right “thumbs up” card to avoid disaster. Optional difficulty settings and campaign-style progression offer varied challenges, from “Disorganized Crime” to full-blown “Queenpin” status.

Think of it as a gritty noir puzzle dressed up in dish gloves and moral ambiguity. A little dirty. A little brilliant. Very, very fun.

Tilting at Windmills is a 17-card, hand-management, charming, puzzle-y game.

Life is nasty, brutish, and short when your nose is to the grindstone. There is wood to chop; grain to mill; cloth to weave. Sure, you've got six (seven? who can keep track?) dependable if not-particularly-bright children who help with the chores. But then some misguided nut comes along and tears a hole in your sail with his lance. Use your limited energy wisely to keep your windmill from falling into ruins, season after season.

—description from the designer

In the 14th solar month of the year 2235, the merchant ship Antares encountered an unknown alien entity.

During their return trip home, the crew diverted from their heading to investigate an archaeological find on a distant moon, orbiting a small binary system, outside the Alpha Centauri cluster. The site was found to contain artifacts from an ancient race that had long vanished from that system. Among the ruins the crew uncovered an alien chrysalis. Believing it to be one of the artifacts it was unknowingly taken on board.

Six days after their departure from the moon the alien was released from the chrysalis, and it was free and roaming the ship. The crew made preparations to abandon ship, believing that the alien was hostile. They were correct in their assumption.

Using the collected audio and video surveillance data from on board the ship the events that followed have been pieced together.

This is the record of the events that occurred on the Antares while the crew tried desperately to abandon ship.

Play out the crew's final hour as they attempt to abandon ship. Antares is geared for easy solitaire play, but can be expanded to up to 7 players.

Antares is a thematic, space horror game. The actions of the crew are up to you, and the choices you make may well determine who survives, and escapes the doomed vessel Antares, and who falls victim to the alien lifeform.

Antares is the first game in the Antares series. Telling the first chapter of the story of the doomed ship Antares, and its alien cargo.

Antares is playable solo or co-op.

Game play

Build the Antares using the Cabin and Corridor tiles. The ship must have all corridor ends connected to Cabins. Different builds of the Antares offer different levels of difficulty.

Choose your crew selecting one side from each of the seven crewmember cards. Each crewmember has their own unique ability and choice of two starting locations.

Complete the Navigation, Reactor, & Mainframe Access objectives. Spending an action in the Bridge, Reactor Room, or Mainframe Access cabins will advance the matching counter 1 spot forward. Use of the Air Ducts can expedite movement through the ship, but the alien lifeform has found them to be useful as well.

Collect enough O2 Canisters and Spacesuits for any surviving crewmembers before they head to the Escape Craft. Flame throwers may be found in the Cargo Hold, and can be used to ward off an alien attack. Each encounter with the alien lifeform can cause the crew to experience Fear, Terror, or Paranoia.

Avoid the alien lifeform. As the crew complete objectives and collect precious items they are constantly at risk of attack from the alien. After each action performed a Scan card must be drawn which will indicate if the alien attacks, or if the crew is safe for one more round.

Escape if the crew manages to complete all objects they may regroup in the Escape Pod, and execute the Delta Launch Code to escape the doomed Antares merchant vessel.

—description from the designer

In Dead Center, you must kill twelve zombies surrounding your 3x3 cabin before the deck runs out. You do this by playing cards into piles within the cabin to attack adjacent zombies. The power and precision of your attacks is determined not by the card you played, but by the two other cards on the same line.

Dead Center is a solo game playable with a deck of regular playing cards, with rules created by Wil Su and made available free online as part of his "Isaludo" series.

boardgame: The First Boba Tea Shop in London
description: In The First Boba Tea Shop in London, you are the franchisee of a famous Taiwanese milk tea stand, managing an increasingly agitated line of customers outside your store desperate to try the newest craze. You need to play as many cards as possible into four ordered queues. If someone tries to cut in line you can either kick them out, or clear the whole line. To win, you must go through the whole deck without discarding any four cards of the same rank.

In Foursquare, you are the mayor of the fictional Flavortown, a trendy neighborhood known for being the birthplace of cutting-edge restaurants. New restaurants (cards) will be placed one at a time in a 4x4 grid. Placing the highest or lowest card in a
row or column causes all other cards in that row or column to flip over, as other restaurants go out of business or try to challenge the newcomer. Your task is to end up with sixteen open restaurants in your city, while preventing too many from being closed at once.

As part of the Isaludo collection, the rules can be found in this forum thread.

In Syndicate, you lead a large-scale police sting operation in 1930s Chicago to infiltrate the mafia. You must slowly gain the trust of the mafiosos, bringing your team of undercover agents slowly up the ranks. Promotion is difficult, as you may only bring in people that have enough rapport and trust with the other existing members of the same rank. Once you’ve promoted one agent above the highest level, you can take down the whole operation in one swoop.

As part of the Isaludo collection, the rules can be found in this forum thread.

You are Sheryl Holmes, terror of the first-grade playground. Known far and wide (at least three schools over!) for your observational skills, you have a reputation of being unstoppable at hide-and-seek.
In Hide and Seek, you will be placing cards around a grid of hidden playmates, trying to match their suit. You may reveal anyone’s identity at any time, and you score if the cards you placed match the playmates’ suit. The more playmates you find, the easier it is to narrow down the identities of the rest, so you must strike a delicate balance between guessing too early versus too late.

As part of the Isaludo collection, the rules can be found in this forum thread.

Compart is a territory game for two players: Black and White. It is played on the intersections (points) of an initially empty square grid (board).

Black plays first, then turns alternate. On your turn, for every viable area with one or more empty points, place a stone of your color on one of those points.

A viable area is a viable point along with all other such points one can reach from it through a series of steps onto orthogonally adjacent viable points. A viable point is a point that is empty or occupied by a friendly stone.

When the board is full, the player with fewer stones on the board wins. If there is a tie, whoever placed the last stone wins. Ties are only possible on even-sized boards.

To make the game fair, the second player will have the option, on their first turn only, to swap sides with their opponent instead of making a regular move.

				
				
					NotesWalling off areas of the board with your stones multiplies enemy placements, but be mindful that your opponent will try to use those extra placements to enclose new areas that multiply your own placements.

-description from designer

During the setup, overlay the appropriate Alternative Production card onto your Faction board's Production section.

—description from the publisher

The expansion consists of four cards representing alternate production. Images suggest a connection to the four basic factions, however, nothing in the rules mentions that.

—user summary

boardgame: Title Wave: The AI Movie Game
description:
We asked an AI to create NEW titles for popular movies (henceforth referred to as clues). The AI created our clues using aspects of the movie’s plot and characters, but it's not obvious what the AI “knows” about these movies, so we’re never sure about what it’s going to spit out. Some clues go off script and bits of the real world creep in, with the names of actors, directors, or aspects of the movie’s critical reception. And sometimes the computer tells us things that come out of nowhere, like an incorrect movie quote or a #badhashtag. This unpredictability add an element of randomness, and hilarity to the game.

Players win by guessing the original movie title based on the AI generated clue.

With 6 distinct ways to play and the potential for many other "house rules" and customization. Title Wave offers replayablity and creativity that many triva-style games lack.

Example clues

     "Defying the Orders of the USS Enterprise and the USS Cougar" ~ Top Gun
     "Eliminating He-Who-Must-Not-Be-Named: A Regulus the Animagus & Gryffindor Co-Op Study" ~ Harry Potter and the Order of the Phoenix
     "A Study of Humanoid Individuality: 'You’re Welcome' When Stepping Into Your Own Story" ~ Moana


—description from the designer

King Tea has infiltrated the Realm of Time. Help Cup Joe and his Coffee Time friends stop King Tea from making it Tea Time permanently across the 5 Worlds; or sound the trumpets and join the crumpets (King Tea’s mindless minions of mayhem). "The fate of the 5 Worlds hangs in the balance as you decide whether to side with Cup Joe and or King.


     Choose to play as one of four Coffee Time Heroes: Cup Joe, Thermos, Little Miss Hot Shot, or Donette. Or, join the crumpets and play as one of two villains: King Tea or the zombie crumpet named Roamer.



     Coffee Time is an introduction to strategy games for 2-6 players and ages 8+. Players take turns following the instructions on their unique player cards. 


Heroes, begin by using or trading item cards, then they move forward toward the activation chamber by rolling dice. At the end of each move heroes draw from the Journey Deck where they can be launched into a battle, suffer at the hands of the Duchess or gather valuable items.

Roamer tries to knock heroes back to start and prevent them from reaching the Realm of Time, and steals valuable item cards from heroes. King Tea spends his turns changing the World Clocks and sending out his royal guards to thwart the heroes.


     Heroes make their way to the Realm of Time to try to defeat royal guards and King Tea. If they can successfully defeat all 5 guards and King Tea, then the heroes win the game. 



     The Roamer and King Tea have extremely powerful cards that they can utilize strategically, once per game, to thwart the heroes or turn the World Clocks more quickly. If the tea characters change all 5 World Clocks to Tea Time, then the villains win the game.



     Coffee Time is a fast-paced game that takes under an hour to play. Coffee Time is brewed espresso-ly for families and friends with fun infused into every detail.


—description from the publisher

Fantasy Planet is a science-fantasy skirmish game played on an ever-changing battlefield. You are a hero sent to explore a new and mysterious planet brimming with mana. Through tactical cunning and military prowess your goal is to scare off any opposition and claim this planet as your own. But the journey is not without risk: the terrain of the planet is ever-changing and volatile. Shape the planet and use its terrain to your advantage to win.

At the start of the game you take turns building up the board by laying down hexflowers. Each flower has two sides with seven hexes on each side. Every hex has one of six different terrain types that govern how you can move across the board. Stepping in lava will burn your foot and trying to swim across a large body of water is impossible lest you know how to fly. Setting up the board to your advantage and utilizing the terrain is detrimental to your success.

You take control of one the game’s six asymmetric factions vying for control of the mysterious planet. What faction you choose to play will highly influence your experience. Each faction presents a different twist on a fantasy race. The orcs have been taken over by plants, the dwarves got taken over by a malicious AI, and the dragons are now bleak images of their former selves due to an abundance of cross-species unions.

Each turn you will draw a bunch of cards from a deck that you can play by spending mana. In the beginning you will only have access to cards that let you put more units onto the field. But, whenever you play a unit you get to draw four cards and add two of them to your deck. These new cards will let you alter the terrain, cast spells, and trigger abilities unique to your faction. Elves can lay traps, the undead cast curses, and the turtles can build structures to name a few. Building an effective deck tailored to both the battlefield and your opponents’ strategy is a sure path to victory.

If you like magic, science fiction, fantastical creatures and tactical gameplay then this is the game for you. Take the chance and step into a rich alternate universe with a fresh take on playing with a modular board.

—description from the designer

In NecroMansters, players create piles of overlapping monster-themed cards onto their play area or bench in order to attack and defend. Card stacks need three distinct types in order to attack or defend: health, special ability, and attack. Once a stack has all three types, players can boost the power of their stacks by topping them with special cards.

Players seek to eliminate opponents by attacking undefended lanes, but may also switch active and benched stacks to protect themselves.

Each card has unique flavor text and illustration, with no two cards being exactly alike.

—description from the designer

This is 2 player game for young adults, family’s or children that are ready to play tactical games.

The goal of the game is to reach the other side of the game board by placing tiles on a grid. These tiles contain circles that determine where your pawn can move and where it can not. Moving is done by playing cards that show directions and players can also move their opponents pawn.

To the Point is a tactical move-and-card game that takes about 30 minutes to finish.

-description from publisher

You are an outcast. The society you were part of turned its back on you and disowned you. They say you committed a heinous crime or, perhaps, were accused of a mistake too serious to be overlooked. You were cast out of your life, denied help and ostracised.

Now, you walk through a hostile world trying to regain the shreds of your lost dignity. You are willing to risk your life in an almost impossible mission to vindicate yourself in the eyes of those who were once your own. But life alone is very difficult and the challenges you face threaten to overtake you with every step you take.

In Paria, by Cezar Capacle, we will live the intense experience of a solitaire role-playing game that proposes us to enter a setting that we can create completely at random. Dare to explore a world that you will have to create as you build your character and guide his or her steps through an emotional story that is aimed at deciding if you will achieve redemption or if you will be lost forever in solitude.

Create your own game world by rolling on the setting tables and with the help of the oracle tables.
Develop each scene by delving into your character, the causes that led to him or her becoming an outcast, and the elements that appear in the narrative.
You can replay Pariah as many times as you like, as your own imagination, the world creation system and the oracles will ensure that your story never repeats itself.

In Ronin, by Tiago Junges, you play a lone samurai who has been stripped of his rank due to a dark past that you must uncover. As a ronin, you will travel the paths of an imaginary feudal Japan, visiting villages and towns where the fragments of your history are hidden.

Armed with your sword and relying on your spirit, you will have to face enemies, rivals and even supernatural creatures waiting to put an end to your pilgrimage. Not only that, but three mysterious villains, who seem to be part of your past, are waiting to do away with you. How are you related to the great main villain and why is he so interested in you?

Customise your ronin with choices about fighting style, appearance, family background, secrets... and more.
Live with your recurring nightmare, a dream whose origin and meaning you don't know, but which plays a fundamental role in the development of your story.
Face not only combat, but also moral decisions as you travel the roads in search of the truth.

Your team, consisting of LKA profilers in training, has come a long way. Now you are facing your first real case. Your instructor will give you a file with all the evidence of a murder case. During your hunt for the perpetrator, you will experience a multidimensional detective board game based on a true story.
As real detectives, you have to follow every lead, including cell phones and the Internet. It is therefore necessary that you have at least one internet-enabled device when playing. At The Unknown you have access to a realistic database in which you can find suspects, listen to interrogations and solve the case!

In this game you play as a team and not against each other. So nothing stands in the way of an exciting game evening!

Fleets of Renown is a free Print-and-Play Game for 2 to 8 players designed to bring dynamic and fun games based on real historical naval figures from both fame and infamy. Choose your Fleet, Captain and Crew before commencing battle on the High Seas to see which player’s Fleet has the most Renown!

After the Fleet and Crew have been chosen, you can place your Ships and Safe Port ready for Phase 2.

Phase 2 sees each player engage in battle by moving their Ships across the map into broadside positions to be able to fire their Cannons at the opposing Ships. Using tactics and strategy, sink each opponent’s Ships to be victorious!

description:
The Mechanic is a very compact card game for 2-6 players (4+ ages), you can learn how to play it in less than 3 minutes. In the box you can find 84 cards: 12 special engine cards, 66 gear combinations cards and 6 mechanic character cards.

The mechanics of the game is similar to Mexican Train. Each card has a combination of two gears, one on each end. Each player builds their own mechanism by matching and connecting the gears by type and color. The goal is to be the first player to play all of their gears.

Special engine cards are used to begin the game, but also to split the sequence into two, so players will have the opportunity to play on more than one side of the mechanism, giving them more options to get rid of all their cards. Gears can be arranged in several ways to build different mechanisms

-description from designer

Hex map, Brigade-Regiment level units, 1 day/turn.

Published in issue XI of Parabellum Magazine. Spring 2023.

description: In the co-operative card game Concord (コンコード), players attempt to complete missions as members of a magic school, playing out the correct five spell cards without directly consulting one another. The Swarm savors flesh and circuit; it merges and couples and corrupts in  a wetware nightmare where neurons  and tissue become logic gates in its petahertz cycles. The Swarm is viral,  and when it has consumed everything  on planet Ramses, it will look for another host.

You will take the role of a colonist attempting to escape the planet Ramses.  You must take your character to the only known zone of safety, where you  can rendezvous with a rescue ship. Ratty Ruckus takes the classic game of tic-tac-toe to a whole new level with unexpected surprises and lots of rats. Players must create a sequence of matching rats in a line within the 4 x 4 grid either horizontally, vertically, or diagonally. The first player to line up their rats takes the cheese. Each successful sequence earns a point and ends the round, the first player or team to reach three points wins the game.
—description from the publisher

User summary:
This game is marketed as Tic-Tac-Toe taken to the next level. While it is inspired by Tic-Tac-Toe, there are several key changes, including up to 3 players (or teams of 2 each) rather than just 2 gamers facing off. And spaces are not always owned by one player for the duration of the game, as other players can take over a space or co-exist in a space. With the need to get 4 in a row on a 4 x 4 grid, the game is still (but much less) susceptible to the "gridlock" of Tic-Tac-Toe that leads to draws among skilled players.

Can be played by 2 or 3 players, or with partner teams can be played by 4 or 6 players; not with 5 or more than 6 players.

boardgame: Zombicide: White Death – Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles: Timecrash

This strange new land is also rife with mystical energy that the Turtle Team can channel into their attacks to better defeat the Zombies. And it turns out, there’s still some pizza too!

description:
Hexcape, A story-based riddle game, designed for board game enthusiasts, that blends physical cards and technology to create a unique gaming experience through your phone.

Players will need the Hexcape app on their phones, a deck of cards, and occasional props. Hexcape is designed for one to four players working together as a team.

Hexcape is a game that involves stacking cards to reveal new numbers and solving puzzles through an accompanying app. Some cards in the deck come with QR codes that can be scanned using the app to unlock unique features, which may include things such as:


     Clues to help solve the puzzle
     Interactive animations
     Mini-games and challenges
     Access to new storylines


Players must use their creativity, problem-solving skills, and the resources provided by the app to progress through the game's storyline and uncover the secrets of the Hexcape universe. With its unique blend of card-based gameplay and digital elements, Hexcape offers an immersive and engaging gaming experience for players and all skill levels.

The first story is called “Gift from the Divine”, and is inspired by Greek mythology. It’s a story where the players must help a girl become a demigod by gaining the favor and approval of the gods. This adventure has different endings, making it a unique and exciting journey.

—description from Hexcape Game Team

Designed for 2 to 6 adult swingers, this x-rated game for gay males is not for the shy or timid! Clothes will come off, and nudity will soon follow!

Pick a piece, roll the dice, obey the space! Reward and penalty cards mix up the action, while bonus paths offer additional routes to help or hurt your chances of winning!

Once you amass enough cash, or have no clothes left, you've won! Select another player, and enjoy 5 minutes alone with them in the nearest bedroom!

Don't be discouraged if you weren't the male picked, you'll get another shot in the next round!

M.A.A.S. IMPACT is a tactical level game of man-to-man combat in a dark future. Players manage a squad or full platoon of heavily armed and armored soldiers as they navigate through blasted terrain to accomplish their mission. This could be to capture or hold terrain, break through the enemy lines, or simply destroy enemy soldiers.

Each soldier is tracked with his or her own data card, allowing the capabilities of each to be hidden until engaged. Ten different soldier types, each with its own abilities can be selected as part of a squad, through twelve different scenarios across ten different tabloid-sized (11x17 inch) geomorphic maps, allowing for hundreds of different battlefield configurations. An included “design-your-own” system makes it easy to create your own scenarios.

The game's action is managed through the use of a deck of "action cards" that provide quick combat results as well as random events.

Also included are guidelines on how to play M.A.A.S. IMPACT using miniatures, either with or without the map sheets.

Components include:


     Rules Manual
     Geomorphic Map Sheets (10)
     Scenario Cards (12)
     Player Aid Cards (3)
     Counter Sheets, double-sided (3)
     Soldier Cards, double-sided (126)
     Action Cards (72)
     Sheet of Hidden Setup Cards
     Movement/Range Gauge (for miniatures)


In addition to the print-and-play components, the option to purchase a set of professionally printed soldier cards is available. This deck of cards also includes hidden setup cards as well as a “marker test” card that can be used to test the ability to remove wet-erase or grease pencil marks from the cards before possibly damaging the soldier cards.

NOTE: Testing on cards purchased from WargameVault indicate that "ultra-clean washable markers" (such as those available from Crayola) or "china pencils" are the best options. Dry-erase markers should NOT be used as the ink stains the cards.

Fire your powered armor, heat up your weapon pack, and prepare for mayhem in the wasteland with M.A.A.S. IMPACT!

—description from the designer

description:
It’s the Wild Wild West. You are a cowboy, minding your own business drinking shots at the saloon bar and someone challenges you to shoot dice.

You need to shoot quicker, and shoot better than your opponent to win the duel. Before you roll, you bid on the number of dice that you will use (i.e. your gun drawing speed). After you roll your dice, you target your opponent's body parts. Knockout your opponent before he knocks you out. Only one person walks away from a duel. This game involves a level of deduction while pushing your luck.

—description from the designer

Wölfel was developed as a nice alternative to the card game Doppelkopf. This game, too, is about tricks and points and finding partners! Randomly, dice are drawn from the bag and hidden behind a privacy screen. The green hunters play together, as do the two players without hunters (= forest dwellers). Who plays with whom is partly decided only at the last trick. Compared to Doppelkopf, this game is less intense, more relaxed. But even as an expert, winning without some "dice luck" is hardly possible. Each dice color has different numbers, abilities, and interactions.

—description from the publisher (translated)

boardgame: Lords of Hellas: Odysseus Promo Board

A promo hero with the following abilities:

Special Ability: When Odysseus is in a Region with a City/Sparta, that Region's Population Strength is lowered by 1 for you.

Starting Bonus: At the beginning of the game, Odysseus may be placed in a different Region than your starting Hoplites.

The Sword Arcana Expansion is the first expansion for Genshin Tarot, the Fan Made Genshin Impact Board Game.

Add 14 additional playable characters to the base game, each represented by an authentic swords suit minor arcana tarot card and illustrated by a different renowned artist.

Bring the Raiden Shogun home, along with Yomiya, Diona, Rosaria, Hutao, Kazuha, Ganyu, Yanfei, Xiao, Xinyan, Eula, Razor, Bennett, and Albedo!

boardgame: Race to Ace:  An Aerocatures Game!

Race to Ace is a fast-paced card game that throws three to five players into a swirling, free-for-all aerial dogfight.  Each player pilots a high-powered jet fighter and must make split-second decisions to be the first to shoot down five enemy aircraft and become a fighter ace!

—description from the publisher

You’re a wizard, using sorcery to summon creatures more powerful than those of your opponents. You'll use magic gems to buy creature cards that will earn you points at the end of the game. Some will gain bonus points from other creatures of the same element type, so it's wise to focus on collecting one type of creature (earth, water, or air).

But to create life, you must take life - you’ll need to sacrifice some creatures in order to earn magic gems. This is done by simply turning your creature cards upside-down at the beginning of your turn. But be careful -dead creatures will subtract points from your final score, and they can only be revived witha specific wand or spell.

Once a player has 7 creatures (living & dead),all other players take one more turn. In the end, points are tallied and the wizard with the highest score wins!

—description from the designer

"The glacial meltwater forms giant lakes. And from minuscule spores that have resided in the deeper layers of the ice, a new life form emerges. And the lakes become home to the Scyphoz.

The Scyphoz harbor vast knowledge and wisdom. We will settle near their lakes to learn from them. We will bend to their will to honor them. Complete the journeys they prepare for us. We will serve and be gifted.

That is the call of the abyss." —Nàdair

The Revive: Call of the Abyss expansion introduces the mysterious life form Scyphoz, which lives in the giant lakes that are formed by meltwater from the glaciers. By building near lakes, you gain devotion points, which let you access the new and powerful Scyphoz cards. You can also use the switch action to initiate journeys, sending out your citizen on missions to gain more devotion points.

Aside from 16 Scyphoz cards, 18 journey cards, 3 lakes, and the devotion track, this expansion contains 15 new machine tokens, 9 slot modules, 9 new citizen cards, and four new tribes, with all of the content (other then the Cordycephians tribe) being compatible with solo play. The four new tribe boards feature only an advanced side, and each has its own unique rules, advanced abilities, and technologies:


    The KAIROS have two modes — day and night — and their abilities depend on the current mode. Switching between the two modes at the right time is crucial to this tribe's success.



    The GIB'WARKS has access to powerful gadgets that boost their switch actions, and they may unlock more gadgets for even more flexibility. Their tribe ability lets them reset their switch, which is powerful because the switch action is even more important now that it lets you initiate journeys.



    The OSTARIUS build huge gates which they can switch on and off, either helping or hindering other players. Gates require quite some space, but if placed well, OSTARIUS may temporarily block off parts of the game board, possibly securing them for themselves.



    The CORDYCEPHIANS leech other players, taking advantage of their cards, their buildings, and even their technologies. The CORDYCEPHIANS must therefore adapt their strategy to the opponents they are facing.


—description from the publisher

description:
Tutankhamen is a visually fast game with various levels of difficulty for the whole family.
An adventure for intrepid archaeologists.
Will you be able to decipher the hieroglyphs and successfully enter Tutankhamun's pyramid?
Mummies, scorpions, mazes, traps, enchantments... Be careful! The pyramid is full of dangers.

In each round, a hieroglyphic card is revealed that indicates to the archaeologists the character that awards points. Archaeologists will have to be quick to cover the correct character with the palm of their hand before the rest.

Distant Forest, Distant Mine, Distant Fields, Transport of Captives, Ancient Library, Pink Caravan, Welcome Gifts, Scouting Tower, Balista, Abandoned Village and Ancient Temple.

boardgame: このねこはうさぎですか? (Is this a cat or a rabbit?)
description:
You are a worker working at a rabbit cat sorting area. Let's do our best today to sort the rabbits and cats.

The rabbit has long ears and a short tail, and the cat has short ears and a long tail. Everyone knows it. But this is difficult.

In this game, the lead player plays any one card and declare whether it’s a cat or a rabbit.  Afterwards, each following player must player a cat (long tail, short ear) or a rabbit (long ear, short tail) accordingly.  If they cannot play the required animal, they either play a rabbit/cat to lose a trick and hands black chips to other players or play a new species and make a trump in order to win the trick.  The card with highest trump or highest responsibility value wins.  If all player can play a card of the needed animal, bonus points (2 white chips) will be given.

At the end of a round, the single player with the most collected black chips do not score points; otherwise, each white chip scores 1 point.

Thus, in this game, the player would like to declare an animal that every other player can follow.
publishers: やすものゲームズ (Yasumono Games)

description:
Game idea
The flower fairy Flora is desperate: All the beautiful colors have disappeared from her neighboorhood. With seed balls that grow colorful flowers, she wants to make everything colorful again. But she needs your help to do so.

Can you manage to make your flowers bloom? The brave bird will protect them from the hungry snail and the rainbow will shine, but beware when the heatwave comes or the whirlwind messes everything up ...

Aim of the game
Collect the different seed ball cards and place them on top of each other in your neighborhood in the order 1 to 5. If you don't have any matching cards, Flora the flower fairy will help you. A bird can protect them against snails and the whirlwind threatens your flowers as they blossom. Survive the heatwave together and be happy when the rainbow makes your flowers grow faster.

Watch the seed sprout and grow into a beautiful flower – the neighborhoods finally become colorful again.

The first with 3 fully grown flowers wins.

—description from the publisher

Metal Melee is a multiplayer card game for 2-4 players that uses AI-generated artwork and strategic card abilities. Players use cards depicting Robots and Mechanical Titans to assemble an army and fight each other on the battlefield of Mechs. The objective is to defeat all other players' Bots and collect the most Power Shards from the Bots and Titans you destroy, emerging as the Metal Monarch and the champion of all things metal.

Featuring 176 playable cards, where no two Bot, Titan, Utility, and Pilot cards are the same, all with beautiful artwork and abilities geared for maximum mechanical mayhem. Metal Melee is for avid players of competitive card games and new players alike. Our mission is to develop a game with enough depth and variety to keep you coming back for more, all with a short and comprehensive rulebook so that you can break it out even at your more casual game night and introduce new players to a match of robot fisticuffs.

The first to collect 12 Power Shards from destroyed bots is the winner unless a player is the first to raise an army of 6 Bots and 1 Titan. Players start the game by selecting between three game modes and building their decks in either a random build, grid draft, or one collectively shared deck. Players then choose a Power Plant, which has unique abilities to be used throughout the game and generates Energy for you to use each round. Improve your Energy Production by drawing Energy Crystal cards from a stack of cards adjacent to your deck called your Power Mine and then attaching them to your Power Plant. Alternatively, you may discard them from your hand to your Junkyard for a faster boost of Energy to be used that same turn. Play Bots and Titans from your hand onto your field to assemble your army. (Your field is made up of a front row that can hold 3 Melee Bots, and a back row of 3 Ranged Bots, with Support Bots that can go in either row. Titans are placed adjacent to these rows in their own designated space.) Utility cards can be played to help you maintain an edge over your opponents and protect your own field, and Pilot cards can be attacked by your Bots or Titans to grant them extra strengths or new abilities. Combat damage is determined one card at a time, with each card on your field selecting to do a basic attack or use any activated abilities. The player who reduces an opponent's Bot or Titan to 0 Health Points earns Power Shards equal to the value indicated on the destroyed card. Metal Melee is easy to pick up and learn, but challenging to master with its wide range of card interactions where no two Bot, Titan, Utility, and Pilot cards are the same.

While we celebrate the advancements and achievements of modern artificial intelligence and have made it a feature of the AI Edition of our game, rest assured, this could not have been developed without the countless hours of testing, iteration, prototyping, and the most essential ingredient; flesh-and-blood (with some sweat and tears too). We are also releasing Metal Melee: Pixeled Edition at the same time, with handmade art in the style of 80's arcade-style gaming featuring pixel-art-themed cards.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Mia and Me: Mia Mia das schnelle Kartenspiel
description: Card game on the theme of "Mia and Me".

The imaginative worlds of Roblox meet the Monopoly board game! In this edition, players get to buy, sell, and trade some of their favorite Roblox experiences. Instead of properties, players collect Games, Dev Teams, and Game Promotions in their quest to outlast their opponents. Collect Robux for a daily login bonus, sell limited items, deck out avatars, or get bloxxed by noobs. There's no telling what the next roll of the dice will bring

Players move around the board as some of their favorite Roblox Icons - Bloxy Award, Classic Noob, Domino Crown, Mr. Robot, Builderman's Construction Hat, or Dominus.

Nine new cards for the common cards deck. Funny illustrations relate to the Imperial Settlers: Empires of the North game.

—description from the publisher (translated)

description:
Two extra dungeon cards (Grotto and Lost Tomb) for Tin Helm, which will extend the length of each dungeon by 1 room. This makes each level last a bit longer, but both cards up the difficulty slightly and add random monsters and loot!

These cards are included in Dustrunner.  These cards are also available in the Gnome Pack #1 by Grey Gnome Games on the Game Crafter.

You are thrown in to a world of mystery, blackmail and deception. Solve the puzzles and break into the online vault - before it's too late.

A sports photographer, has stumbled across a story of match-fixing and blackmail at the Marseille Tennis Championships. A bank of files and documents are being used to threaten a leading tennis star and they need your help to disrupt the plan.

How to play: Solve all nine puzzles to reveal the passcode to the online vault, break into the vault where the files are hidden and delete them all before it’s too late!

Flight Plan is a card-pulling, investment, and market strategy, squeeze-your-eyes-tight-and-hope-for-good-luck game. Players use strategy to try to dominate the global airline industry by developing a network of routes, acquiring fleets of aircraft, and establishing hubs around the globe. However, before you can operate in more than two regions, you must reach critical economic thresholds. And each turn, a daily briefing card inserts an element of chance, so even the best-laid strategy can be overcome. The daily briefing can increase your revenue, make you pay penalties, or end your turn. A few can ruin your airline.

The world is separated into six operating regions: North America (NA), South America (SA), Europe (EU), Africa (AF), Asia-Pacific (AP), and the Middle East (ME). You are only able to operate in two regions until specific resource goals are met. The board has 100 Routes that connect 57 cities. 15 Cities have been identified as hubs. You can win in one of three ways:


     Be the first to provide service to all six regions using a network of connected routes; connections to multiple regions can go through a single city.
     Establish a hub in any three of the six operating regions. 
     Own all the short-haul routes in two adjoining North/South regions: [North America | South America], [Europe | Africa] or [Asia-Pacific | Middle East].  


—description from the designer

boardgame: Blood on the Clocktower: Clocktower Con 2023 Experimental Characters
description:
Limited Edition Supplement for Blood on the Clocktower

Distributed at Clocktower Con 2023 - April 14-16, 2023 as ammends for one of the game designers having to resign from attending at short notice

Character and Reminder Tokens for Several New and Experimental Characters for Blood on the Clocktower

Townsfolk: Alchemist, Amnesiac, Atheist, Balloonist, Bounty Hunter, Cannibal, Choirboy, Cult Leader, Engineer, Farmer, Fisherman, General, Huntsman, King, Knight, Lycanthrope, Magician, Nightwatchman, Noble, Pixie, Poppy Grower, & Preacher

Outsiders: Acrobat, Damsel, Golem, Heretic, Politician, Puzzlemaster, & Snitch

Minions: Boomdandy, Fearmonger, Goblin, Marionette, Mezepheles, Organ Grinder, Psychopath, Vizier, & Widow

Demons: Al Hadikhia, Legion,  Lil' Monster, Riot, Lleech, & Leviathan

Traveller: Gangster

Fabled: Storm Catcher

Jeu du Skating-Rink represents another form of Goose variant: the introduction of two tracks, each of 63 spaces. The players divide themselves into two opposing teams, each playing on a different track. Additional interest is provided by crossing the tracks: at these points, if the two teams come together, they ”come to grief and fall” and both must start the game afresh. Other hazards are marked by red or blue numerals on the respective tracks, both tracks having the same set of hazards, though not in corresponding order. The hazards are illustrated by scenes near the space’s number. For example, "skater taking a rest - wait twice his turn to throw the dice" occurs at space 43 blue, and space 10 red; ”a player who asks for a match and passes one turn” occurs at blue 35 and red 47; while "lost his purse - each player pays him 2 to help him out of his difficulty" occurs at blue 27 and red 28. The game is won by reaching or surpassing space 63, so reverse overthrows are not played. There are no spaces in which the goose-doubling rule applies; in fact, the track length is the only connection with the classic Goose game. The firm of Saussine was one of the major French manufacturers of games between its founding in 1860 and closure in the 1960s. Their games are often original (the founder, Leon Saussine, held several patents) and are always attractive, making good use of chromolithography, as in the present exhibit. As well as catering for the French market, they aimed at an international clientele, here indicated by the fact that the separate rule sheet is printed in both French and English.
(Adrian Seville)

boardgame: Delvers and Wanderers: New Classes for Four Against Darkness
description:
Delvers and Wanderers is a new class book for Four Against Darkness (similar to Wayfarers and Adventurers). In these pages, you will find the following classes:
The Spearfighter; The Doomed; The Light Gladiator; The Chaos Warden; The Kukla; The Bulwark; The Vagabond; The Ranger; The Gnelf; The Murchik; The Ogre Grub-Man.

Also included nine new Secrets, new animal companions and an adventure for all levels, "Down in the Sewers of Jamash", with a full set of new dungeon tiles, written by Daniel Casquilho.

—description from the publisher

With the new privileges gain onetime and permanent advantages to do even more for the good of Faiyum.

This first expansion for Faiyum offers privileges, that you keep separate from the cards in your hand.

The permanent effects change or add to the effects of your action cards, while onetime effects usually offer actions you can use once in addition during your turns.

—description from the publisher

This game is the new deck building game. The player can select and take the new cards in the hand from the discarded pile at the end of the turn. No random.

The players collects the monsters from the field.
Their monsters are added to the deck.
The Boss round is happened every three rounds.

Each cards has the points. After 12th round, the winner is the player with the most points.

This is the series of "Monslaught".
The rule is changed at some points from Monslaught in Halloween.

You are an athlete of the word and you will show your ability in each Discipline.

You can run a marathon, do a triathlon, or win a mini-Olympics. You are going to go in search of all the medals.

Suite to the podium and do the Olympic lap.

Take many medals and break the world record!

The SAS rugby team the Tigers play against their arch-rival: the Pitbulls! It is the last match of the season, the final that will determine the winner. But the Pitbulls are playing dirty. To make sure they will win, they have Vince and his friends locked in the school. Choose your favourite #LikeMe character to play. Help get Vince to the match in time for the Tigers to win the final! But beware, the Pitbulls have left traps and their mascot is lurking to slow you down. A race against time! Will you manage to escape in time?

This is a cooperative party game. So you work together to win. Thanks to the different combinations of game cards and the game board which is different every game, this Escape Game is infinitely replayable.

Welcome to the fight for arachnid survival. The lives of arachnids are never easy, and surviving is always part of a complex and never ending game of hunting, eating, growing, evolving and learning. In Arachno Evo you will step into the shoes of a Spider from one of these four different species - Black Widows, Tarantulas, Jumping Spiders, or Spiny Orbweavers, and will be competing with the other spiders to be the fittest at the end of the game - ensuring the continuation of your species.

Hunting, gathering resources, stealing other spiders' egg sacs, gathering knowledge and undergoing mutations will be the keys to survival. But, don't forget to ensure that some of your offspring comes to life to perpetuate your evolved traits and rule the arachnid realm. Each spider species will compete to collect protein, dna strands, new knowledge, new tools, silk and steal other spiders offspring. Every piece of scrap you collect may be the little advantage that will get you one step closer to domination.

Arachno Evil is a tableau and engine building, tile and set collection competitive game for 1 to 4 players that is played in rounds with five phases each:

In the PRODUCTION phase you will be running your engine to generate resources based upon your current knowledge base (the engine is built on your tableau).
The second phase is the RESOURCES COLLECTION phase during which you will collect additional resources (tiles) that got caught in the central spider web that urdergoing constant change while you and your competitors try to control the best sectors of the web.
Then get into the KNOWLEDGE GATHERING phase when you will invest your generated and collected resources into learning new abilities and adding them to your tableau - at the same time, you will be trying to breed your offspring.
ELIMINATING FUTURE COMPETITION is the fourth and the cruelest phase of the round, it is during this phase that you will steal other spiders' egg sacs and make sure future competition to your offspring is kept to a minimum. Stolen egg sacs may be put to use in two different manners, as protein ingestion for you to open new spaces in your knowledge base (your tableau) or as war trophies added to your set collection.
The fifth phase is MAINTENANCE, during which you will discard any resources collected from the web (tiles) that won't fit in your limited inventory, replenish items and refresh exhausted elements of your tableau.

—description from the designer Alexandre C. Serpa
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

boardgame: Resisting Revolution: A Cuba Libre Expansion
description:
Resisting Revolution is a sequel expansion to Volko Ruhnke & Jeff Grossman’s Cuba Libre, covering the period from the rebel victory in 1959 through to the climactic showdown with the US in 1962. During this time the new regime became increasingly alienated from the US, began to grow closer to the Soviet Union, and faced internal dissent from several sectors of Cuban society. The main expansion game charts the processes and actors that shaped Cuba during this period of struggle, with four players controlling either the new revolutionary Government, US-backed attempts to destabilise and overthrow the government, the growing Soviet influence and eventually military presence on the island, or the urban and rural Resistance to the revolution.

Highlights include:

     An entirely new four-player COIN game, Resisting Revolution, that tells the story of the consolidation of Castro’s government from 1959 to 1962.
     New mechanics for Government and Resistance Leaders, US and Soviet Escalation, US Intel and Soviet Materiel, and Regional Alignment towards Castro’s regime.
     A miniature two-player game, Days of Pigs, that explores hypothetical variants of the failed Bay of Pigs invasion. 
     Sixteen new Event cards for the original Cuba Libre game, drawing on research published since the game was released and covering certain topics in more depth, such as women’s role in the revolution, the urban wings of M-26-7 and other anti-government organisations, and the tools of repression used by Batista’s government.
     A new three-player variant scenario for Cuba Libre that approaches the revolution with a slightly different framing, and also allows for a shorter game with a reduced player-count.


—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
In Not That Much one of the players chooses an item for sale, anywhere on the internet.

The others must guess the price, writing it on their card.

The three players to go closer to the real price win points... but the seller outwins if he can "bust" all the buyers, because you have to guess an amount, but "Not that much"!

—description from the publisher

LUPIN is a team based trick-taking game. Players become characters in Maurice Leblanc's novel  and compete for jewels. The gentleman thief Lupin and his lover Raymonde must steal as many jewels as possible. The genius boy Beautrelet, investigator Ganimard and detective Holmes must guard the jewels. As the game progress, you will naturally learn to think like them.

A round is divided into 1. Preparation 2. Planning 3. Crime 4. Scoring.


     Preparation: Deal role cards and jewel cards.
     Planning: Lupin makes plans to win as many jewel cards as possible.
     Crime: Each team competes to win jewel cards based on trick-taking.
     Scoring: Score based on whether the crime was successful or not.


The game is played for several rounds until a player scores over 813.
A round is a team match, but the team changes every round, so the game is individual.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Low: A Fantasy Adventure Board Game
description:
LOW is a tabletop fantasy dungeon crawling adventure game for 1 to 4 players. Embark on perilous quests to rescue villagers, recover lost artifacts, wield dark relics, and defeat evil monsters. Survivors may return to the village to rest, recover, upgrade equipment, and recruit additional party members before continuing the adventure. Perish in the deep or emerge a legend!

Players take on the role of fantasy heroes working cooperatively to complete quests. The rules are identical whether playing true solo (one player controlling one hero), solitaire with multiple heroes, or with friends each controlling one or more heroes. Team up to increase your odds of success, or delve into the darkness alone if you dare!

—description from the designer

Do you love word games? Do you hate word games? Almost Words is for you. A lighthearted, witty party game in which players are given a topic and two minutes. Spelling and grammar don’t matter as players trade and arrange uniquely-designed multi-purpose letter tiles to create clever responses. Players pitch their response to the judge, who picks their favourite. Fast-paced and endlessly funny, Almost Words is enjoyed by people of all ages, gamers and non-gamers alike.

Almost Words is played over a series of rounds. Each round is played as follows:

Reveal a Topic
The judge reveals the top card of the Almost Cards deck. This is your topic for the round.
Each other player takes 10 random tiles and places them face down on their presentation mat.

Arrange and Exchange
The judge starts the 2-minute sand timer.
Arrange
Flip your tiles face-up and create a response to the topic. 
Your response can be any number of words.
You don’t need to use all 10 tiles.
Spelling and grammar are optional.
Each tile can be used in multiple ways as letters, numbers, pictures or symbols.
Discover creative uses for your tiles. There are no limits, just what the judge likes best! 
Exchange
If you don’t like your tiles, you can trade them with other players in real time.
The clock keeps ticking during all trades. 
The judge announces when time is up. Then it’s time to…

Pitch Your Nonsense
Each player takes a turn pitching and revealing their response to the table.
On your turn, set up your creation with a little story or sales pitch to explain how it relates to the topic.
Your pitch might be an elaborate tale setting the stage for the perfect punch line, or it might be short and sweet.

Judge your Friends
The judge picks their favorite based on whatever criteria they please.
The chosen player is awarded with the Almost Card for that round.
The player on the judge’s left will judge the next round.

—description from the publisher

Cubano is a territory placement game, using cubic dice and wooden sticks. This nice and clever material can also be used to play several other games.

In the main game, "Cubano 2 dice", each player has a color and tries to form the biggest set of connected dice of his own color through three phases. At the beginning of the game, all the cubes and sticks are put in a common reserve.
During the first phase, each player rolls two dice and take from the reserve sticks and cubes according to the dice rolled (own color = 1 cube, other colors = 4 sticks).
During the second phase, which starts when there is no stick left in the reserve, players can collect cubes, place cubes and sticks on the board (a cube must be surrounded by sticks) and also steal cubes and sticks from other players stocks.
During the third phase, which starts when the reserve is empty, players tries to place their remaining sticks and cubes, and can also turn cubes already placed on the board on their own color. The game ends when the last cube is placed, and the player with the most connected cubes wins.

—user summary

Perfil Express Filmes e Séries brings a new way to play the classic 20 Questions game with categories inspired by streaming and movie screens!

The Game comes with 100 themed cards with movies and TV shows, each card has 10 tips worth up to 10 points. The first tip is less revealing, and the following ones progressively increase the chances of success.

This version was specially designed to be played by two people or two teams, without the need for a board.

With a new scoring system on a marker, the winner will be the one who scores the most points during five rounds, that is, after five cards have or have not guessed their identities.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

boardgame: Star of Alladore: The Official Rangers of Shadow Deep Fanzine
description:
In this scenario-filled fanzine, the Rangers must quell a revolt in the heart of the capital of Alladore, investigate a hamlet stuck down with plague, explore the dungeons beneath an abandoned tower, sneak into a gnoll occupied village, and defend a group of refugees as they flee the frontlines of the war.

Organized and edited by Rangers of Shadow Deep creator, Joseph A. McCullough, and presented with Barrett Stanley’s usual moody artwork, this collection gives five fan creators a chance to shine and to explore some of the aspects of the war against the Shadow Deep that are outside of the main narrative.

Third Option is a solitaire game played on a mission mat where the player selects, deploys, and maneuvers teams and assets representing U.S. clandestine services with an emphasis on paramilitary operations against adversaries around the world. A “Phase” system is used along with dice, cards, and counters to play out varied missions. The world map is divided into the six Areas of Operations for the United States Military Central Command. Within these geographical areas, the Special Operations Command known as SOCOM, and the U.S. Intelligence apparatus execute highly covert actions against hostile forces who threaten the national security interests of the United States and its allies. Missions that require the use of highly covert methods and plausible deniability by the government are carried out by the Central Intelligence Agency, Special Activities Center (SAC) also known as the “Third Option”. 
As the player, you take control of the highly secretive Special Activities Center division in order to plan and execute missions in dangerous, volatile, and unpredictable areas across the globe. The careful and detailed planning, reconnaissance, and execution of missions assigned will determine the success of the individual missions and/or a combination of missions to make up operations. Gameplay is divided into three easy-to-follow Phases clearly identified in the rulebook, flowchart, and Mission Mat for easy reference.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Masters of the Universe: Battleground – Wave 4: The Power of the Evil Horde
description:
The Evil Has Returned!

The deal has been struck. Trapped in the extra-dimensional prison Hordak agreed to teach the dark arts to the young alchemist in return for his freedom. 
Now the ancient leader of the Evil Horde is preparing enormous forces to seize what is rightfully his and conquer Eternia. The dark times are upon us. 
Will our heroes be able to overcome this new threat,
or is all hope lost? The Evil Horde is here.

—description from the publisher
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

boardgame: The Sword In Africa: Safari Event Deck
description:
First appearing in the 20th Anniversary Edition of TSATF, The Sword In Africa (TSIA) helped establish "Darkest Africa" as one of the most popular new theaters of Colonial War Games allowing for exciting and exotic skirmish gaming on the Dark Continent.

A total of four (4) Game Decks are provided for TSIA, the first two ideally suited for Campaign and Map Movement Games.

The "Safari Event Deck" provides random occurrences that happen while trekking toward whatever goal the Party may have. Besides animal encounters, there are Native Tribes--hostile and friendly--unmapped terrain, rain forests, trackless veldt, good hunting and bad, food, water, and issues with your Bearers, and the inevitable calls for Hongo (tribute) from the people whose lands you cross.

It's never a dull moment when you know that most anything can happen at any time when on Safari!

This Event Deck consists of 30 unique cards, as ever in full color with period art and graphics for even more "atmosphere". An extra card lists ideas for use so players may find their own comfort zone before letting the cards determine their fate!

—description from the publisher
family: Game: The Sword and the Flame

boardgame: The Sword In Africa: River Event Deck
description:
As the title suggests, this is the Riverine partner to the TSIA Safari Deck.

Whenever a party of Explorers, Hunters, Soldiers sets out to travel up or down any of Africa's many rivers and waterways--whether by raft, canoe, or steamer--there are special risks and opportunities. Sudden rapids, waterfalls, swamps, Native ambushes from the banks, attacks by fleets of war canoes, encounters with hippopotamus, crocodile, elephant, and the ever present risks of submerged obstacles, engine troubles, damages to the vessel and its parts, risks of capsizing, loss of stores--and people!--are only some of the things that make for games the likes of which you've never played before.

This deck has its own special "ambiance" with particularly appropriate contemporary art and other graphics, as well as the obligatory "extra card" with ideas for use.

—description from the publisher
family: Game: The Sword and the Flame

description:
A humorous card game for 1-6 players. Play the cards you're dealt, score the most points, and leave before Death enters the meeting, ending the game.

Players are stuck in a tedious corporate meeting. The kind that keeps them from doing important things and bores everyone involved. The only way to leave is to play the cards in your hand. Take a coffee break, show a Powerpoint presentation, answer your phone, and more. Any excuse is a good one to get points and exit. When Death shows up, no one is safe.

boardgame: The Sword In Africa: Action Deck
description:
As with those for other TSATF games, this 52 card Action Deck prompts the order of movement and fire, as well as severity of casualties, from “Lightly Wounded” to “Dead.”

But, TSIA’s rules call for use of a standard deck of playing cards to be read in ways different from the traditional TSATF game model. However, this dedicated Action Deck eliminates all need to consult the rules on how to read the cards, and includes special rules frequently overlooked in the heat of play (such as hits on previously wounded figures, and the risk of death from poisoned weapons). There are also six special cards to cue rolling in the basic game for "Random Episodes."

More unique period graphics and an extra card with simple instructions and options for use of the Action Deck round out the set.

—description from the publisher
family: Game: The Sword and the Flame

boardgame: The Sword In Africa: Battle Event Deck
description:
In TSIA, the rules call for using a standard card deck, of which six cards cue the players to check for “Random Episodes” by rolling dice on one of 5 different tables. But the new Battle Event Deck eliminates all that, and includes almost half again as many “episodes” as appear in the original rules. Now there are fifty Event Cards, most of which have variables practically guaranteeing no two games can ever be alike.
