Some of the original “Episodes” have been retained; others modified in a variety of ways, and still more are entirely new. From army ants and African killer bees, to encounters with Elephant, Cape Buffalo, Rhinoceros, and more, to up close confrontations with snakes, Pygmies, flash floods, mysterious strangers, traitors, mutineers, The “King” and “Queen” of the Jungle, and more to make your wild fights in the forest/bush/veldt even wilder!

An advantage of the Battle Event Deck over the original tables is that once a card has been drawn, it cannot be repeated. But for those who still like that possibility, there is an optional “Reshuffle” card requiring all previously used cards to be returned to the deck!

Still more unique and unrepeated period graphics and photos decorate the cards, and there is that indispensable card with printed suggestions for more uses of the Battle Event Deck.

—description from the publisher
family: Game: The Sword and the Flame

description:
A game published by Alga in the 1950s on the subject of a couple paying off a house and getting modern furnishings and a a car. The title translates to "build a nest" and refers to getting your own place of residence (with someone).

Players roll a die and move around a circuit performing transactions and purchasing furniture or car tiles. Occasionally a wheel is spun which mostly leads to more purchases but may indicate taking a loan, selling a tile to the bank or auctioning off an excess tile to the other players. A player wins by paying their initial mortgage, obtaining a full set of tiles, and having at least 5,000 in money.

The Emperor wants the most entertaining fireworks festival to celebrate the end of the summer and his 31th birthday.  He has made it known that there will be a competition for teams to show off their best firework routines to be included in the grand celebration.  There are four major types of fireworks in use, and the debate over which type of firework is superior can be fierce.  Are the heart shape fireworks the best?  Or maybe it is the trefoils?  Of course, balance is also important, and showing mastery of that aspect is to be rewarded as well.  Each week, the teams submit bids (well, bribes) to the Chancellor for the opportunity to show off their firework routine.  The team who makes the highest bid has the best chance to impress the Chancellor as they get to choose the dominant firework shape for the trial, but the penalty for failure is high.  If you fail too many times, you won’t be able to participate in the festival at all!

31 Fireworks is a trick taking game that rewards aggressive bidding as well as clever card play.

Winning the bid allows you to determine trump, and just under half of the points available each hand are from the trump suit. The dealer has a small advantage in that they get the final bid and they can also force a second round of bidding - but this is not without risk - they could get stuck making a bid higher than they would like!  Teams that win the bid more often should have a better chance to score points, but if you are overly aggressive, there is a steep penalty for missing your bid three times.

Unlike most trick taking games, trump can be played at any time.  This definitely changes how you will approach the course of the tricks. Four of the five suits are shorter than usual (only 10 cards), so players will be void sooner than you think.  The best partnerships will be able to provide useful cards to each other when one of them wins a trick.

boardgame: Master Udo Vs. the Four Martial Arts Masters
description:
You are Master Udo, a martial arts master. To prove that you are the best martial arts master around, you must sequentially challenge and defeat four other martial arts masters.

Master Udo is a puzzly game about making combos. You start with 10 basic cards in your hand. Each card is separated into 2 halves: a condition and an effect. By placing cards covering the previous effect, Udo increases the length of their combo. This makes the combo stronger. However, as the combo gets larger, it gets harder to build due to the conditions. Some conditions include "Odd" (can only be played in an odd position in the combo), "MAX 3" (can only be played in a combo of length 3 or less), and "No right" (cannot be played in a combo with "right arm" or "right leg").

There is no partial damage in this game, which means that it is often important to store up "charge" that can be spent to increase the length of a combo at critical moments. Charge can be gained by blocking attacks and by playing certain combos.

It is important to be efficient with card play because the top card of each combo gets discarded once that combo resolves. Additionally, failing to block an attack results in a specific card being discarded. If the player discards all their cards, they lose. Additionally, if they can't discard or exile (remove from discard) the correct card when getting hit, they lose. Once Udo defeats 4 masters, they win. Udo can choose to challenge weaker or stronger masters in any order, but choosing to challenge weaker masters may reduce the overall glory earned at the end of the game. Defeating a master allows Udo to learn their signature move, improving Udo's strength in future battles.

—description from the publisher

Mining Inc is a 2-4 player game where competing mining agencies are trying to find great treasures buried deep in the ground. There are 4 legendary treasures, each with a different win condition once discovered. Each player can only obtain 1 win condition, and players do not know which one they will get until they find it.

This game is all about upgrading things. Players get cards and slide paperclips to track how far their upgrades have progressed. They must spend energy and/or money to upgrade stuff. Upgrades include things such as energy generation, cargo capacity (hand limit), drill (allows mining deeper), hull (allows selling of hazards), and the scanner (allows looking at the top cards of the deck).

The mining is represented by 4 decks of levels 1-4. The deeper levels require upgraded drills and more energy, but have better rewards. Players can sell their loot to gain more money, which is then used to upgrade their operation further.

Since players do not know their win condition in advance, there is an incentive to find it early and start working towards it, but this comes at the cost of being able to amass certain upgrades that are expensive but can quickly snowball.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Der Abenteuer Club: Auf großer Reise
description:
Get ready for new adventures taking place all over the world! During your journey you will solve numerous challenging and whimsical tasks - buckle up!

Gameplay:
Everyone gets 10 random cards of objects.

One player reads a chapter out of the “book of adventures” until he reaches a challenge. Now the person next to him must choose exactly 2 cards that he thinks are best suited to solving the challenge. The same player also chooses 3 other cards that he thinks are the most unsuitable for solving the challenge.
Shuffle those 5 cards face down and reveal them in the middle of the table. (Additionally, the 2 chosen items can be written as a proof on a secret tablet)
Now, all other players need to discuss and find the 2 chosen items.
The game ends after the group successfully solved 5 challenges or failed 3 times during one adventure.

Setting up the game:
Place the rabbit house in the middle of the table, and press down the roof to activate the spring. Place the rabbits in a pile beside it.
The youngest player starts. He/she rolls the dice and then carefully places a rabbit on the roof in the colour shown on the dice. Then it is the next player's turn. If there are no more rabbits on the colour shown on the dice, the player rolls the dice again. The player who blows the roof off and makes the rabbits hop loses.

In Era of Atlantis, you are a secret society seeking to expand the powerful nations you control during the mythical antediluvian age, 12000 years ago. You have two of these under your control and share each one with a rival. You also have a secondary goal trying to bring “light” or “darkness” to the world. Working with and against your rivals you must prove to be the most powerful secret society in the world before the Era of Atlantis ends either in glory or in cataclysm.

This is a semi-cooperative area-control and worker placement game with a unique twist. You control two nations with the lower-valued one counting for your score at game end. This means you will usually try to balance the two. You also share control of each nation with another player, who has the same interest in the success of that nation as you do. Switching control of a nation is also possible, but difficult.

There are ways to score a few additional points for yourself only, such as with your remaining mystical energy or by fulfilling secret objectives.

The game is played over a variable number of rounds, with each player taking a turn per round. There are two main ways to gain new areas for your nation: militarily or politically. You can also expand your abilities by constructing buildings or acquiring powerful special actions. A nation will grow more successfully the better its two controlling players can cooperate.

—description from the designer

A mysterious land teeming with dangers demands great heroes. The gods grant the noblest of these adventures an eternal place among the constellations, to serve as a beacon of light for those in need.

In Astral Dreams you'll be playing as a band of heroes facing a range of trials to win your place in history. Successes allow you to pick the stars in the night sky to become part of your constellation, and your legend will live on for future heroes to recall in wonder.
Upcoming wallet game from Button Shy.

Overview:
Font Karuta is a real-time competitive game themed on font types.
Spread out the cards with the same set of letters in different fonts on the table.
After a font name is announced with a clue, players race to find and take the matching card.
At the end of the game whoever has the most wins.

Mechanics:
In addition to the players, a "Reader" is required to read out the question card.
The players compete in real time without turns. 
The players race to find and take the card that matches the announced font.
The answer is not stated clearly on the front of each card. In order to win, you need to guess the answer from clues as well as to memorize the information that is gradually revealed.

Karuta is a traditional Japanese card game in which players listen to short descriptions written on a reader’s card. Each turn, the players compete with one another at being the first to pick up the player’s card with the corresponding picture or writing, and whoever wins the most cards wins the game. Font Karuta is a version of this traditional card game for people who are obsessed with recognizing the differences between typefaces. The reader reads the font name and description written on the reader’s card, and then the players pick up the player’s card bearing that font. The player who picks up the most cards is the winner.

—description from the publisher

MIRU II is a solo-first mapmaking hex-crawler analog horror game where you run from a God through uncharted territory, survive dangerous situations, and deal with the consequences of your actions.

While MIRU II is a sequel to & is best experienced after playing MIRU I, there is a quick start option and can be played as a stand alone experience.

—description from the publisher

Try to match the playing cards to the exposed pattern on the pattern card.

To match correctly, the adjacent images must be identical in shape, color or both!

Collect the most pattern cards to win the game.

This game boosts your perception speed, reaction speed and the 6 FlexiQ-skills!

—description from the publisher

Poison Garden is a solo experience where the player is a caretaker tending to a garden filled with deadly and poisonous plants. As the brave caretaker, you must weed and care for the Poison Garden to prepare it for visitors, but beware; the garden has an infamous reputation.

Poison Garden is for one player and plays for 30 minutes or less. Players will start off with a deadly and poisonous garden represent as tiles that needs serious tending before the gates can be opened to the public. Each turn, the player will tend one plot of the garden, which triggers a chain reaction of sub-actions with adjacent plots. Some of these actions are:
- Weed: removes a plant from the garden for further care
- Transplant: moves a plant to a different area of the garden
- Water: immediately tend an adjacent plot
And more…

Sometimes your hard work will become undone as the chaos of weeds comes back to your garden. Players will have to be diligent and thoughtful to execute their actions in a timely fashion so as not to fully exhaust their time tokens. If a player can tend to all the plots visible in the garden, they win. If not, then they succumb to the poison garden and the iron gates will remain locked.

There are additional modules included in the game to ramp up the difficulty by introducing new poisonous plants that introduce new sub-actions and challenges.

Poison Garden is a title in the new Talon Strikes small-box series.

-description from developer

It's Munchkin-like game where players compete for points.
The points could be stolen from other players or awarded in beginning of your turn. Whoever get first 100 point win the game. All this in Mikyr's satiric universe.

Your turn comes as this. 

     Take one new card. 
     Cast a die, move the rocket token and do whatever is on the   game board filed you landed on. 
     Play up to 2 cards. 
     Then next player.


—user summary

Kingdom Come is a battle-strategy game where players will pick one of four decks (referred to as kingdoms) to defend themselves against their opponent. Each kingdom contains seven character and nine weapons. Any character within your kingdom can use any of the weapons within that kingdom, but there will be one weapon that will grant each character a special ability.

You can place three characters on the board at a time. The game is over when you have either defeated all seven of your opponents' characters or managed to defeat all of your opponents' characters on the board in a single turn.

We have incorporated nostalgic elements such as dominoes, card suits, and candy-land like characters into our game to give players a bit of nostalgia as they our brought into our world.

—description from the publisher

In Groo: The Game, players compete to be the first to build a large town by playing building cards that give them victory points. Of course, this is no picnic, hindered as it is by other players’ rampaging armies and the frequent bumbling visitations of Groo the Wanderer, determined by the roll of a set of dice, which can also grant players resources with which to build and "Groo heads" with which to damage the unfortunates who have Groo in their village. Players can also develop armies to attack other players’ cities.

This edition of Groo: The Game contains both the original base game and the expansion.

Try to build a town…without having it stomped to rubble by Groo the Barbarian!

Fans of Mad Magazine love Sergio Aragonés, and they love his character Groo the Barbarian. Groo is a warrior and wanderer, never in one place for long…because he’s such a klutz the people run him out!

In this fast, fun game, each player tries to build a town and keep it intact against the twin threats of rival armies and Groo’s “helpful” visits. Roll dice to gain resources, and spend them on buildings or troops…but anything you can’t use is passed along to your rivals!

• Cards illustrated by the great Sergio Aragonés himself, featuring Groo and his friends and foes.

• A hit when it was first released, but unavailable for many years. Includes both the original game and the supplement!

• Plays in under an hour—great fun for as few as two, or as many as six players.
A quick party game in which you have to get rid of your cards as quickly as possible by matching the color description to the spot on the card on top of the pile in the middle of the table.

Waddle Waddle is a unique strategy game that features the designer's own photographs of penguins in the wild, taken in Antarctica, and a game board made from a map of the regions around Antarctica. Waddle Waddle's blend of open drafting, set collection, area of control, and push-your-luck is as fun to play as it is beautiful to watch, and you'll learn something about penguins along the way too. Game play is lightning fast, with minimal downtime. Whether you're playing two or six players, it's your turn before you know it.

In Waddle Waddle, you play the role of a Penguin Hero, specializing in a particular Penguin Species. Each Penguin Hero is based on a real-life Penguin Hero who works to conserve penguins! They will help you to study penguins as they leave the Ice Floe and travel to their nesting grounds in the Antarctic region, called Rookeries. There’s safety in numbers, so penguins travel in groups called Waddles. As a Penguin Hero, you tag Waddles, scoring Conservation Points for each Waddle.

Waddles that contain Mating Pairs make Nests. Score extra Conservation Points at the end of each Breeding Season if you tag the most Nests in a Rookery. You also get player-specific bonuses when you tag the Nests of the Penguin Species your Penguin Hero specializes in.

Each penguin is represented by a Penguin Card with a number indicating its pecking order in the penguin social hierarchy. Only compatible penguins will travel together. There are six different Penguin Species that each like to nest in different places. You’ll need to carefully choose the Penguin Cards from the Ice Floe to score the most Conservation Points.

Food is scarce! There is a limited fish supply at each Rookery. It costs one fish to play each Waddle. You might need to send penguins on a Fishing Trip to find more fish. And finally, beware of Threats, both natural and manmade, that appear each Breeding Season and make it harder for Waddles to land at each Rookery. The player with the most Conservation Points at the end of the game earns the title Top Conservator.

—description from the publisher

Fast-Dealing Property Trading Game
Let The Dark Side dominate your destiny!
Play as on of six ruthless characters, and unleash the power of Force lightning!
Make deals with notorious figures such as Boba Fett, Count Doku and Grand Admiral Thrawn!
Use The Dark Side cards and TIE Fighters to tighten your grip on the galaxy!
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

boardgame: The Horus Heresy: Book Nine – Crusade
description:
The Horus Heresy Book Nine – Crusade focuses on the war for the Eastern Fringe between the Dark Angels and the Night Lords. As well as detailed background on the Thramas campaign, the book includes in-depth lore about both of the Legions involved, new army lists, and a series of missions to let you recreate some of the most bitter battles of the civil war.

Within the leather-bound, 210-page book, you’ll find detailed background information on the Thramas Crusade, told across six chapters from The Edge of Ruin to The Heavy Toll of Victory, describing the vicious battles between the Dark Angels and the Night Lords in and around the Thramas system. These conflicts were some of the bloodiest seen in the entire heresy, with no quarter given on either side.

As well as detailed background of the fighting and the sides who took part, this book includes interlinked missions that let you replay the Thramas campaign. There is also a brand-new army list for the Dark Angels Legion and an updated one for the Night Lords Legion.

Full background for the forces involved is included: on the side of the Imperium you’ll find the Dark Angels Legion and the Legio Victorum. On the side of the Warmaster you'll find the Night Lords Legion.

The Horus Heresy Book Nine - Crusade is a complete expansion for the Warhammer 40,000 game – it requires the Warhammer 40,000 rulebook, the Legiones Astartes Crusade Army List and the Mechanicum Taghmata Army List books to use all of its contents.

—description from the publisher

"Flight Command" is an abstract tactical-level solitaire tabletop wargame that allows players to command a squadron of advanced aircraft, fighting against the evil Red Empire. The game puts the player in the shoes as a squadron commander. The decision you make in this game are smaller theater decisions, focusing primarily on individual missions and an overall campaign. You must manage your squadron, ensuring it is ready for combat.

Each turn, you control one or more Flights of aircraft, moving them along a flight path, engaging Targets and enemy units defending them. You gain points by destroying Targets, and lose points if any of your aircraft are destroyed.

The outcome of Missions impact the overall outcome of Campaigns.

boardgame: The Sword and the Flame: Northwest Frontier Action Deck
description:
The Action Deck for fans of the 2nd Afghan War and/or "punitive expeditions" with those unique factors adding up to make this theater "The Grim" of legend and history. Period graphics suggest the period of the 2nd Afghan War, and the cards allow for the presence of Afghan Regulars, as well as Afghan and Pathan Tribesmen.

—description from the publisher
family: Game: The Sword and the Flame

boardgame: The Sword and the Flame: Mahdist Wars Action Deck
description:
If a day with Fuzzy on the rush is your idea of table top action, and if you like to form squares against hordes of Ansar racing in for the kill, this is your Deck! Representing British and Egyptian forces, as well as "Fuzzy Wuzzy's" and Ansar, these Cards will heat up your battles faster than the gun barrels of your Gatling!

—description from the publisher
family: Game: The Sword and the Flame

boardgame: The Sword and the Flame: First Boer War Action Deck
description:
Now you have another reason to be afraid to go back into the bush-veldt! Contemporary art and atmospheric graphics will add to your proper inclination to keep your head down in Johnny Boer's neighborhood!

—description from the publisher
family: Game: The Sword and the Flame

boardgame: The Sword and the Flame: Egyptian War Action Deck
description:
One of the slickest, yet riskiest, campaigns of the entire period, Egypt was a "gateway" that pitted the best of Victoria's Battalions/ Regiments against the Egyptian Army of Arabi Pasha, only to lead on to involvement in the Sudan. The often under rated Egyptian Army had brave soldiers and excellent Engineers, and made for the most nearly equal foe that Tommy met until Johnny Boer came along!

—description from the publisher
family: Game: The Sword and the Flame

boardgame: The Sword and the Flame: Boxer Rebellion Action Deck
description:
The Boxer Rebellion is easily the most colorful and exotic of all the late Imperialist adventures, the only time a military coalition of the US, Britain, France, Germany, Austro-Hungary, Italy, Russia and Japan took the field together against a common enemy.

The Boxer Rebellion Action Deck is itself unique in that for the first time in this series "dedicated" cards are provided to cue the Movement and Fire of units of the individual members of that coalition. Thus, there are cards specifying Imperial German, Japanese, Russian, and British units, as well as those of the other participants. These are apportioned roughly to match the troops committed by each member, so that British, Russian, and Japanese, and US cards abound, while Italy, Austria, and France are proportionally fewer.

Their Chinese opponents, however, had their own divisions and factions, and these are represented by cards specifying Boxer units (the largest group), as well as others for Imperial Bannermen, the Kansu Braves, and those units from the various "Westernized" units with more modern weapons and training.

In effect, using the cards as played guarantees that both sides will find it harder to stick to any joint plan and end up going their own way, as was exactly the case so often.

Using authentic Boxer and Manchu flags for the period, as well as the Colors of the Coalition members and other period illustrations, the cards are especially atmospheric and will help to put your games notches higher for drama, excitement, and speed of play.

—description from the publisher
family: Game: The Sword and the Flame

description:
This Kickstarter Exclusive character pack brings you 7 samurai Survivors, all honorable and brave. Even in the face of overwhelming odds of a zombie horde, these unwavering samurai are prepared to lay down their lives for the greater good.

This Pack contains:

7 Survivors
    7 Survivor ID Cards
    7 Equipment Cards
    1 Reference Card

—description from the publisher

boardgame: The North West Frontier Event Deck
description:
The heart of the Deck is content of each Card. The challenges to the players on both sides of the Crags are sharper than ever, but are still "even" for both sides. As with all TVAG Event Decks, these are not your Monopoly "Chance Cards," nor are the effects of each the same from game to game--severities and benefits vary across a wide spectrum.

And the whimsy is still there... and the fun and a hint of Hollywood, too, but never so much as to spoil the game.

If playing in Afghanistan, Native Reinforcements can include Regular Foot, Horse, or Guns--but not so if elsewhere on The Grim. If the battle is in Winter, Blizzards and Frostbite can affect both sides, but the Imperials worst of all. British troops can even be introduced to the charms of the Camel Spider, the Pathan Rock Fall, and Jezail armed Snipers, while Afghan/Pathan Tribesmen can find their Black Powder is soot and ash sold them by an Infidel Dog, or that their Mullah's have one battle plan and their Hill Chieftain another, or that the Gurkha's facing them are going to blood their Kukri's today, no matter the cost!

And besides these and so many more jolly moments that could never appear in a standard war games scenario, there is a new feature in the NWF Event Deck added as a tribute to the late author of The Sword And The Flame

These six Cards, randomly distributed throughout the Deck among the others, bear the likenesses of some characters all Colonial War Gamers will know from beloved films, characters whom Sgt. Brom has encountered on the edges of the battlefield who carry special Intelligence for the British C-in-C concerning Reinforcements for the Imperial or Native Forces. Their numbers, type, the time of their Arrival and Entry Points will be reported some number of Turns in advance. For the first time with these Event Cards, the British Players can attempt to coordinate the time and place of new Units with their current forces to defeat the Hill Tribes, or, if it's the Afghans/Pathans who are about to be reinforced, move to prevent their arrival causing a disaster!

—description rom the publisher
family: Game: The Sword and the Flame

description:
You’re a British traveller, trying to get from London to Scotland quicker than all of your friends!

Your aim is to complete an entire route on any of the coloured train lines. Some stations belong to one coloured line, others count for  multiple colours. Along your journey, you’ll have to spot all five different types of British Rail trains.

You’ll need to battle for stations and trains by playing action cards to steal, swap and charge rail fares. Navigate the north-bound madness whilst dodging ticket inspectors and the transport police for the ultimate trainspotting adventure!

—description from the designer

The Expedition is a small box game full of fun for 1 to 3 players. Compete against other Explorers to Mark your claim on undiscovered locations across the stars in order to gain prestige and write your name in the history books.

Be careful, though, your claim can be taken by another enterprising Explorer.

The Expedition is a small box area control game where players will try to keep their Marked Locations and score Exploration goals. The game plays lightning fast and is very portable for on-the-go gaming!

—description from the publisher

What will your friends fall for?

We took the most revealing internet searches and removed the last few words – can you guess the most popular ending? Players take turns reading the beginning phrase to popular internet searches and must then try to complete it with the most believable answer. They don’t need to be right; they just need to fool their friends into believing their answer is! However, the correct ending is always mixed in, so you’ll have to be savvy with your submission. What might your opponents fall down an internet search query rabbit hole for themselves?

—description from the publisher
A Monopoly-like game set in the Borough of Waynesboro.
publishers: Late for the Sky Production Company

description:
Doodle Heist is a fun and frantic team drawing game with a twist. No artistic skills needed, but you've got to be quick if you're going to win! Can you crack the code before the other team steals your chance at victory?

HOW TO PLAY

1. Get Ready!
Divide into teams, choose an artist, and set the board with clues. Each team's artist holds a secret key card with the correct answer for the round.

2. Draw & Guess
Both teams race to identify what their artist is drawing using clues on the board. Be careful, many clues are similar!

3. Steal & Win!
Win points for guessing your team's doodle correctly ... and if you do it fast enough, steal points from the other team by guessing their doodle, too!
family: Mechanism: Give a Clue / Get a Clue

description:
A competitive fantasy adventures game, played only with a single cube.

Dungeon Cube is a game for 1-4 players, campaign driven, that let you live an entire story of adventures for you and your friends.
Each player chooses a hero, represented by one face of the cube, then the heroes must accomplish the same mission, represented by a sequence of goals to be fulfilled by the heroes. To fulfill a goal, a player must rotate layers of the cube to obtain a specific configuration on his face.
The first hero that fulfill the last goal, is the winner.

—description from the designer

You are the manager of the airport terminal services department. You have to arrange maintenance of the aircraft for the departure.
The goal of the game is to send as many passenger flights as possible.
Each player rolls a dice. The number on the cube is the number of workers that can be brought in to service the aircraft. To send a flight, it is necessary to attract a sufficient number of workers indicated on the flight requirements card.
Player can take a card of a random event. The card can result in both positive and negative events that can speed up the departure or, on the contrary, lead to a delay.
The player with the most departed passengers wins.
Also, players can trade with each other to find the fastest way to depart flights.

You have come to a planet ruled by machine lifeforms in pursuit of wealth and fame.

Your goal is to secure more territory on this planet and acquire technology and information from the powerful machine lifeforms inhabiting the planet.

However, your initial strength is extremely limited. Will you rely on gathering resources and increasing your numbers? Will you enhance the performance of your drones through research? Or will you develop new weapons based on the manufacturing technology obtained from defeated enemies? But be careful not to take too long to build up your strength; you're not the only one with plans to conquer this planet...


     Use time and resources to develop new workers.
     Move workers around to encounter and defeat mechanical beings! Obtain technology chips.
     Place workers in more areas to gain resources and fame.
     Engage in combat with the enemy's workers and take over their occupied lands.


—description from the publisher
Monopoly Bitcoin style. More or less the same as original Monopoly, but with board spaces, cards, tokens, etc. done in a Cryptocurrency theme.

boardgame: Legendary: A James Bond Deck Building Game – No Time to Die
description:
The third expansion to Legendary: A James Bond Deck Building Game!

This expansion comes with 120 playable cards featuring images from the James Bond movie, No Time To Die!

This expansion will continue to expand on Legendary one classic movie at a time. James Bond will be accompanied by new allies as he attempts to disrupt the nefarious plans of Safin.

Is James Bond up for this new challenge?

—description from the publisher

Two new priest promos, one for solo play. They are part of the Teotihuacan: City of Gods – Deluxe Master Set but are also available separately.

Effects:

Huitztlampaeh
At the start of your turn and only once per turn you may add 1 to the power of a worker with 1 power

During ascension, you may never choose the reward giving you your fourth worker.

Piltzintecuhhtli
After Ascension, the new worker is always placed on board 6, then Teotinot immediately resolves its Nobles/Conquest (6) action without needing or spending wood.

Do not power up a worker after this action.

Battle between the Hellenes forces and the invading Persian forces in 480 BC.
It is a little print and play game that uses components of "300: Earth and water" for a short battle game. This is an assymmetrical dice rolling game with a map, as a Persian you will try to advance to encircle the enemy, as a Greek you have to eliminate all the opponents.

boardgame: Conquest: The Last Argument of Kings II
description:
Conquest – the Last Argument of Kings is a mass battle wargame produced Para Bellum Wargames. The rules of the game have been written by one of the industry’s lead game designers, Alessio Cavatore and his studio Riverhorse. The rules are quick and easy to learn for a new player but are still able to offer a new and complex strategy to master. Conquest does away with player turns and army deployment. Instead, it focuses its play around the command stack mechanic, where each player determines at the beginning of the round what order his troops are going to be activated in. Coupled with a unique escalation reinforcement mechanic where light, medium and heavy troops each have their roles, the fog of war is a very real thing.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Masters of the Universe: Battleground – Wave 5: Masters of the Universe Faction
description:
Might and Honour

The palace guards are always on watch. They are elite soldiers trained to protect the royal palace and keep the peace in Eternia, but even they may need help when the forces of evil make their move. Fortunately, Fisto – a powerful warrior – is not afraid to use his brute strength to defend the kingdom. His massive fists can take down any enemy, making him an invaluable ally in every battle. Add these new characters to your forces, and watch battles become more epic and exciting!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Masters of the Universe: Battleground – Wave 5: Evil Warriors Faction
description:
Escape from Eternia

Webstor is a master of escape who uses his stealth and expertise to outmanoeuvre enemies and evade capture. He is always looking for new allies to assist him in his evildoings. And are there any better candidates than Hover Robots? After all, they are advanced technological marvels equipped with state-of-the-art sensors. Together, they could pull off the greatest heists, Eternia will have ever seen! Bring your forces to life with these three new formidable characters poised to shake up your strategy and keep your opponents guessing.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Masters of the Universe: Battleground – Skeletor with Panthor
description:
Who would have expected that a young Dylinx, rescued by the exiled Prince Keldor, would turn into such a ferocious beast? Even after such a transformation, the mighty feline remained faithful to his master. Together, Panthor and Skeletor wreak havoc and destruction on Eternia.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Masters of the Universe: Battleground – He-Man with Battle Cat
description:
The Most Powerful Man in the Universe - He-Man - isn’t alone on his noble mission to protect Eternia and its fabulous secrets. He is accompanied by the Fighting Tiger - Battle Cat. Together they will fight off attempts of the forces of evil and strike fear in those who choose the path of destruction.

—description from the publisher

Pizza Town is a fun game for the whole family to play. It combines luck, strategy, and a good amount of social interaction.

This game helps the players develop creative thinking, persuasion skills, and interpersonal communications.

We invite you to take step into the business world and open your very own pizza shop! In the game, you must serve customers, earn money and purchase a variety of options to upgrade your pizzeria and progress in the game. It is also possible to conduct business with the neighboring pizzerias of the rest of the players. Smart trading with other players, correct conduct and some luck will go a long way and will pave the way for success in the game!

Game goal:
The first player to serve 10 customers wins the game!

—description from the publisher

Vs. System 2PCG: Mortal Kombat 11 is a standalone game that can also be combined with other titles in the Vs. System 2PCG game system.

In the game, each player chooses a main character, builds a deck with exactly sixty cards, and attempts to stun the other player's main character. More than two dozen Mortal Kombat 11 characters are included.

Game essence:
Chicken Fight is an exhilarating and suspenseful card game that involves a lot of humor, thought and enjoyment. This game combines luck with strategy and social interaction.

Each player gets 6 cards which they can use for only one action on each turn.
The players in the game must build henhouses, attack other players' henhouses (and beware of assassins!), protect their own henhouse, collect eggs, and use the different action cards in order to strengthen their henhouses and thus earning the largest amount of points and winning the game!

Game goal:
The player who earns the largest amount of points is the winner!

—description from the publisher

Collect, trade, and compete in Monopoly Prizm: NBA Edition, which allows players to compete and earn points by revealing the NBA player ratings and stats on the backs of their Panini NBA trading cards.

To start, you use the Panini cards to draft your own team of four NBA players, then instead of buying properties, you try to take control of NBA games and collect bonus points when opponents land on them. To earn points, you compare the stats on your Panini cards against others when competing in playmaker challenges and All-Star Contests. You can also use the Panini cards to trade NBA players or draft new ones. At the end of the game, whoever has the most points wins.

The game includes one 8-card Starter Pack and two 4-card Prizm Packs featuring Stephen Curry, Luka Doncic, Ja Morant, and other players. Additional cards are sold in booster packs.

In Competition Sudoku, 2 players race to solve the same puzzle.  Instead of a timer putting pressure on you, or an app counting your moves and giving you a performance rating, here you have an opponent trying to beat you to the solution (and the only rating that matters: WINNER!)

Simple instructions essentially teach how to solve Sudoku puzzles (for the uninitiated).  The color-coded setup rules together with the ability to use numbered tiles (which are physically placed on the board) makes for a very forgiving system (for those who occasionally make missteps in their logic)—no messy erasers required!  And everyone benefits from the ability to choose (from 4 levels of puzzle difficulty) the challenges that are right for their level of experience.

FROM THE PUBLISHER:
Be the first to complete your board and win!
No paper and pencils needed...play Sudoku with cards and boards!

The description for Boggle reads: "Can you find words no one else can see in the letter grid?"  As in Boggle, players race to build words using dice imprinted with letters, but unlike Boggle, it's okay if everyone finds the same words!  Instead of knocking out your opponents finds with your own, in Letter Scrambler, players individually score the words they find, based on the printed scoring table in the rules, which awards points according to the length of the words they have written down.  Over the course of several rounds, players build up their scores (to a predetermined number), and best of all: there is no timer ticking away at you while you try to think!  Letter Scrambler is also unique from Boggle in that it uses a clever mechanism (the namesake scrambler) to both house & roll the 7 letter dice.

—user summary

Roll the mixer on your hand and place on a table.
Race against others to form words using the letters showing.
The longer the words, the more points you score.
You never lose the dice with the handy Magic Mixer,
which fits in your pocket for on-the-go fun.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Monopoly Prizm: NBA Edition – 2022-2023 Booster Box
description: Monopoly Prizm: NBA Edition – 2022-2023 Booster Box serves as an expansion for Monopoly Prizm: NBA Edition, with the box containing six packs of trading cards, with each pack holding four random cards. Add these cards to the game for new drafting possibilities as you're building your team.

Red Bean: Dragon Slayer is a single-player role-playing card game on a poker deck.

It has the sequential story of a comic book, the possibilities of a video game, and the physical form of a poker deck.

Read through the cards like in a comic book. Follow a young adventurer named Red Bean through a fantasy world. Journey from the Overworld to the Caverns and back on a quest to slay evil monsters.

Use the system of arrows and tabs to navigate through the deck. Decide what Red Bean does by following different arrows.

The whole game is a regular 52 card deck. Each of the cards has a poker suit and number so you can also play standard card games.

—description from the designer

In 1998, could people remember the movie plots & top-billed actors from the 40's, 50's, and 60's?  That would have been but a tiny challenge when this game was published, because fewer than 10% of the cards in this game rely on a familiarity with the likes of: Rex Harrison, Humphrey Bogart, Charleton Heston, and Cary Grant.

Action! leans HEAVILY into the popular movies of the 80's & 90's, so the question today is: can YOU remember the movie plots & top-billed actors from the 80's & 90's?  (Were you even alive then?!)  If so, you're going to love both showing off your movie knowledge and laughing at yourself when you get stumped over the things you should totally have known.  Each movie has 3 clues given which make the right answer increasingly more obvious BUT worth fewer points.  (This is where all of those hours of movie watching finally become "an investment", rather than "a waste of time".)  15 points wins the game.

The tagline for this game says: "It's a party in a box!"  Remember when movies came in boxes too?  If so, you need to take this party OUT of the box & have a great time with it...you may even want to rewatch a few classics after the "action" stops.

Unhappy Campers is a strategic and sarcastic camping-themed card game, where players race to build their campsites and roast the the other players (campers) before the game ends!

Claim your campsite, then assemble your requirements to build your campsite, while you try to send misfortune to the other players while avoiding same.

There are 15 different Campsites, with different requirements and difficulties.  You can build more campsites to score more points, or end the game if you think you can win with the most points already!

—description from the publisher

TimeOut! is a boardgame for children, to teach them the value of good choices vs. bad, and reward them accordingly!

This game features a gameboard with 6 progress tracks (for up to 6 players) and 90 cards, with 2/3 featuring good choices, and 1/3 bad choices.  There are several cards that offer game choices for the players, and 3 blank cards, that your children can write on to make their own cards (and choices!).

—description from the designer

Bird Turds is game for nerds who love words.  It tests a players vocabulary, spelling and ability to rhyme.

Each player chooses a character bird and gets 10 bird cards. Players also receive 3 turd cards. Play begins with the selection of the first word ending. For example if the first ending is "Ace," each player has to come up with a word that ends in "Ace" such as Place, Face, Mace...etc. If a player fails to come up with a word, they get a Turd and they lose their turn. The last person without a Turd at the end of the round gets to collect a Bird from the other players.

The goal of Bird Turds is simply to not crap out.  The last person without Three Turds or the most Birds at the end of 10 rounds wins the game.

—description from the designer

"Nuck tats" are messages tattooed on someone's knuckles. If it fits, anything goes.

Each round in NUCK TATS, one player reveals a customer — a celebrity, character, mythical creature, whoever — and everyone else writes nuck tats for that customer. The best tat wins the round...and if your friends make you laugh, feel free to throw them a tip. It's that easy!

—description from the publisher
BattleTech Record Sheets: Dark Age offers players the record sheets for all the 'Mechs detailed in BattleTech Technical Readout: Dark Age.

Soar high upon your wings, like an eagle, as you seek to escape the clutches of king Minos. But beware, fly too high and the sun will melt the wax that holds your wings together... fly too low and the moisture from the sea will make the feathers too heavy to keep you aloft.

Flight of Icarus is a fast-paced strategy game for 2-4 players in which you live out the classic Icarus myth. Build your wings, then brave the elements as you attempt to fly from Crete to the Greek mainland. During the first phase of the game you will collect wax and feathers using an action point system, managing these resources as you craft your wings. You may also play innovations to aid you during your flight. Then, you will take to the air, struggling against both the weather and your fellow fliers during the tactical phase of the game. Bid for the oracle's favor, that allows you to play weather cards and act first, as you jockey for position in the air.

An engaging mixture of strategic resource management and take-that mechanics, Flight of Icarus is fully language independent during play. Even should you fail in your flight, there is no player elimination, so you can still attempt to stop your competition from reaching the other shore.

—description from the designer

The party game Natural-Selection: Combine & Create contains two decks: Picture Cards and Prompt Cards. Each round, every player draws up to five Pictures Cards in their hand. One player will reveal a Prompt Card and become the judge for the round. The other players will combine two Picture Cards in their hand to create a creature that best fits the prompt Card and place them face-down. Going clockwise from the judge, players will reveal their creatures and describe why it best fits the Prompt. The judge will award the Prompt Card to their favorite creature. The player to the left of the judge will become the new judge for the next round. The first player to be awarded 5 Prompt Cards wins.

—description from the designer

The Political Parties Game is a negotiation and hidden role game for 5-20 players. In it, each player takes on the role of a politician representing one of five different political parties:
The Economic Party
The Animal Party
The Technology Party
The Food Party
The Fire Party

Negotiate with your fellow representatives for their vote and their support on your bills. Create factions of like minded individuals for greater power over the vote… and stab them in the back when it benefits you the most… like a good politician. 
The Political Parties Game is ready and searching for the right publisher to get it into players hands just as real world politics comes back into the limelight.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Sanrio Characters Patapata Kururin (パタパタくるりん)
description:
Sanrio characters have arrived at the Daiso board game!
Let's enjoy board games in a cute, fun and casual way!

Turn it over, turn it over! Turn over the character card and turn your character face up a lot!

Shuffle the 36 cards and turn them face down into a draw pile.
Shuffle the 4 Sanrio character cards face down.
The dealt card becomes that player's scoring character. Secretly look at your card only.
Each player draws 2 cards from the deck.
When it is your turn, draw a card from the deck. 
Choose one card and place it on a square.
Flip all cards pointed by the arrow.
You are allowed to place a card over an empty square or the back of another card. (Face down card)
When cards are flipped, flip all cards within that square. There can be at most 2 cards per square.
Hello Kitty cards remove 1 face up card from the stage.
When the deck and all cards in hand are gone, or if all squares on the stage are face up, the game is over.
Flip over your secret scoring character. The player with the most visible character wins!

Split into two teams of two or more players. Shuffle the cards. The team going first has one of their players take the top card from the deck. Then the player performs the charade by miming one of the four titles on the card. Their team has a set time to guess correctly. If correctly guessed the team receives one point. If the team does not guess correctly the play goes to the second team.
The game ends when each teams member has performed three charades. The team with the most points wins.

In Color Flip 2-4 players compete in a quick run against each other. The game works very similar to classic Pachisi, but in this version the pawns are separated into 3 parts and might mix-n-match with the parts from other players, which creates a fun mess. When combined with different colors, every players controls every piece of the combination in order to get to their personal goal area.

—description from the designer

Easy board game for 2-6 players takes 20-30 minutes. You try to set a combination of colours which meets some of the opponent´s worlds. However, that kind of meeting brings (besides the winning point) also a quantum leap – an event that will bring the two players with a certain probability into different universes. Although the game may seem complicated at first glance, the opposite is true. Even children from the age of 7 can handle it without any problems. The version in a handmade bag really fits in your pocket, making it the ideal travel companion.

—description from the publisher
BattleTech Record Sheets: Jihad offers players the record sheets for all the 'Mechs detailed in BattleTech Technical Readout: Jihad.
BattleTech Record Sheets: 3145 offers players the record sheets for all the 'Mechs detailed in BattleTech Technical Readout: 3145.

In Portal Combat, you play as powerful entities with control over time and space, and use that power to pit fighters from across time against one another in the ultimate fighting tournament! Send warriors to dominate three different arenas in a comic-book themed card game for two players.

After randomly choosing three combat arenas that have their own unique effects, draft your team of twelve fighters and play them one at a time against each other. Each arena has room for two fighters on each side, where they can use their special abilities to boost each other or take out enemy combatants.

But, as the game progresses past the first round, you gain access to time gems that will make your fighters even more powerful. The player to win two out of three rounds will take the prize and be the ultimate victor of Portal Combat!

—description from the publisher
BattleTech Record Sheets: 3150 offers players the record sheets for all the 'Mechs detailed in BattleTech Technical Readout: 3150.

Kitten Capers is a simple strategy card game designed for younger audiences. It is colorful and animated to entice younger players.

The game uses a 50-card deck.

On each turn, players can either:

     Draw 2 cards, or
     Draw the top card from the discard pile.


Players can then play up to 2 cards from their hand and activate kitten abilities if they wish to do so.

Card Types:

     Kittens - Cards that are played in front of the player and have an ability. This ability can be activated, but the kitten goes to sleep afterward and needs to be woken up to use it again.
     Treats - Scored between 1 and 3 points, these cards are played on awake kittens to earn points.
     Skips - Make the next player lose their turn.
     Sabotage - Send a kitten to the kitten shelter (a discard pile for kittens).
     Rescue - Rescue a kitten from the kitten shelter.
     Surprise - Cancel a card an opponent plays.
     Swap - Swap one of your kittens for an opponent's kitten.
     Wake-Up - A card that wakes kittens up.


The game continues until one player accumulates 20 points.

—description from the designer

As autumn’s first cool breeze rustles through the tree branches, the squirrels begin their preparations for winter. Working together in pairs, they stash some acorns away in their trees, while eating others that will sprout too soon.  The best-prepared Squirrels will be the strongest at the start of spring, ready to prosper in the new year.

Acorns is a fixed-partnership, deck-shedding climbing game. Teams work together to be the first to shed their shared deck of cards.

The rules also include a variant for two players.
family: Organizations: The Game Artisans of Canada

boardgame: Vs. System 2PCG: The Falcon and the Winter Soldier
description:
This second issue pits the The Falcon and The Winter Soldier against the threats with the villains. With Steve Rogers no longer active, Bucky Barnes and Sam Wilson return to uphold the spirit of Captain America. A thought-lost science could change things for the worst, as the Flag Smashers take world matters into their own enhanced hands. As the world changes, it still needs a symbol to rally behind. When one tarnishes that duty, another will pick it up and hold it higher!

—description from publisher

In the Near Future...

The global catastrophe wiped out billions of lives, leaving only pockets of the heartiest survivors struggling to create a new life among the ashes. After many trials, you have found a favorable spot to set up your new home in the heart of the unforgiving wasteland. Using the remains of an old roadside structure, you manage to build a makeshift room that will provide protection from the harsh elements. In order to survive here longer than a few days, however, you will need to find a means of collecting water and food, expanding your settlement, and maybe even finding other survivors willing to join your new community. With a little ingenuity and a lot of luck, you see a glimpse of hope - until a band of roving raiders enters your settlement and threatens your life. This settlement is your last hope, and you therefore give it an appropriate name: HOLDOUT.

Holdout is a roll-and-write resource management game in which you must grow your wasteland settlement and achieve certain objectives by the end of 7 game rounds. You must carefully manage water, food, shelter, community, ingenuity, and defense, the latter being important for repelling attacking raiders at the end of each round. You will also encounter wanderers in the wasteland, who will offer their skills in return for your help. Survive until the end and meet the scenario objectives to win the game.

—description from the designer

New expansion for base game ,,Elekt". Adds brand new card ability and fresh 20 cards.

,,Elekt to gra wiecznie żywa.
Nie tylko na stołach ale i naszych planach." Wiedźmy 2 to
najnowszy z dodatków do Elekta zadebiutuje w III kwartale 2023 roku. Zaprezentuje Wam około 20 kart i nowy rodzaj zdolności.

This Kickstarter Exclusive Optional Buy provides you with the Celestial Knights Survivors, with special rules to use in your Zombicide fantasy games. Get ready to bring the sky down on Zombies with each Survivor’s special Celestial Attack!

This Pack contains:

5 Survivors
    1 Special Guard
    6 Survivor ID Cards
    6 Skill Reference Cards
    1 Rules Leaflet

Each of the Celestial Knights Survivors has access to their very own unique and awe-inspiring Celestial Attack! These can be activated with a single action. The attacks are so powerful that they may even turn the tides of battle when it is most needed!

However, once a Celestial Attack is used, its power is depleted, and it must be recharged in order to be used again. During a Celestial Knight's turn, they can choose to spend 3 Actions to recharge their Celestial Attack by calling upon the stars. They may also choose to take a Wound for each fewer action spent.

Celestial Attacks may be used and recharged multiple times during the game!

—description from the publisher

Made Up Movies is a creative and entertaining party game that lets players create their movie titles, plots, and laughter using a set of pitch & prompt cards. The Studio Executives ask you ridiculous questions about your rebooted movie and you make up everything as you go in order to impress them and win all their budget! Think Cards Against Humanity meets Mad Libs, with a unique twist that will leave you laughing at all the endless combinations! It caters to both casual and serious gamers alike!

—description from the designer

boardgame: New Visitors (fan expansion for Fairy Tale Inn)
description:
New Visitors is a fan-expansion for Fairy Tale Inn.
It adds several new character abilities for each of the original characters from the game.

—description from the designer

Shepherds of the Fall is a competitive card, dice and domino game focused on collectively responding to the ecological collapse brought on by climate disruption. In this game, you are scientists and researchers exploring a map of cards representing four different ecosystems of the Pacific Northwest, each of which is facing the distinct and violent effects of extreme climatic changes. Your team of meeples spends time discovering the land, finding threatened dice species on the verge of collapse. Using dominoes to determine your actions and movement, you gather these species up and deliver them to field hospitals or back to pristine ecosystems where they can heal on their own and support the biodiversity of nearby ecosystems. The winner of the game is the team with the highest reputation points for healing and reestablishing these species on the map.

During the game, you have the opportunity to train your advanced workers, research to gain new special skills, exchange species in certain locations or with opponents, receive much needed supplies and transport from helicopter visits and, most important, bring the species you collect to the rehabilitation and restoration locations to ensure their very survival.

—description from the publisher

"A fast-paced chase through outer space!"

A race to determine the fastest spaceship in the universe is on, right here in the Milky Way! Choose your machine--one of four UFOs with totally different handling--and take your places at the startling line.

Each turn, use your "paths" to move closer to the finish line. You might move in arcs, zigzags, or even loops! But examine the racetrack closely before you choose a path to use. If you crash into a star, you won't get to move! Get in your opponents' way and make them work to pass you, or accidentally help them out by clearing out a comet from their path.

Space Racers brings the tactile fun of movement templates to a fast and simple game that can be enjoyed with almost anyone.

—description from the designer

Team Digger is a cooperative game that teaches sequencing, spatial, reasoning, and early coding skills. No screen required!

Playing together, cooperatively, players play cards from their hands to create five-card sequences to turn and move their dog pawns from one dig site to another on the game board. They can stop and dig to explore a spot by digging up what is buried there. Dig locations are visible on the board.

Players win as a team when they find all four bones before they dig up five of the unwanted objects.

If the cruelty of the Gods had any purpose, it remained a mystery even after their death. They gave us miserable fates and treated us like toys, but we knew it had to end one day – for better or for worse. So we revolted and slayed them all, for a price well beyond what we could afford. The world is damaged now, bearing the wounds of this impossible battle, but at the same time – we are free and responsible for our own fates for the first time. Yet from lands far away, followers of New Gods arrive with their true intent unknown, hidden behind their political agendas. Amongst all of this turmoil are you, a wild bunch of mercenaries, just blindly accepting another job.

As you travel across the world, you will discover and scout unknown lands. Only the Companion App knows what lies ahead and what kind of challenges your journey will bring. You’ll face enemies, complete quests, and discover secrets of Godsgrave. Every time you make a critical decision, the app will remember your choice and adjust the campaign accordingly. Enemies you will encounter will be introduced within the app and quests will be tracked and completed by it too. Dozens of monsters, quests, items and adventures are held in the Story App, to deliver a living and breathing world to explore.

The Lineage and Class of your hero will determine your starting deck. Completing scenarios will allow you to earn rewards and more cards for your deck allowing you to customize your hero to suit your play style. Cards will include items,weapons, abilities, and epic maneuvers called Events. Find your ideal combination from all the Lineages & Class options.

Each hero brings a different set of skills to the party, which are represented by a dice. Whether you are scouting an area, clashing with an enemy, or bartering with a shopkeeper for a better deal, you will use your Skill Dice.

Every round, as a team you choose a Plan Card which determines the play style of the party for that round. Sometimes you will be more mobile, while next turn you hoard more resources. Carefully plan your game, as every card is different, brings unique Special Effect and provoke various obstacles to overcome.

—description from the publisher

This fifth expansion for Interplanetary, titled New Worlds, adds an additional tile to expand the solar system with and introduces further non-standard tiles to play with larger solar systems.

In addition, a new mechanic is added: The Tree Orbital Infrastructure, which alleviates the demand for depots for spaceship construction and may even provide a game-changing bonus...

—description from the designer

Night Owl is our latest roll and write about being a fledging owl, or Owlet. As an owlet, you've been used to your parents bringing you food, but now you're ready to enhance your traits and begin hunting in the forest at night all on your own.  Players earn points for each prey animal they hunt in majority scoring, set scoring, and individual scoring as well as points for each trait they have enhanced.

Night Owl is a print and play game, meaning you will receive the printable files for the game. This includes a shared game sheet and standees. Players will need to bring 3 six-sided dice for each player and a writing utensil for each player.

The game is played in two phase: Day and Night.

In the Day phase, players will roll their dice and if they choose, use the die values to enhance their traits.  Players can discard one of their dice to set the remaining unused dice to any values.

In the Night phase, players will use any remaining dice to hunt prey animals in the forest. Players will use their die value to move on the shared forest map. Player's traits will be assessed to determine if their hunt was successful.

Players will also gain dice manipulation actions each time they gain a certain number of each prey animal.

Players will find plenty of challenge on the shared map as the player with the lowest dice total value each turn is the starting player allowing them first pick in a shared space.

—description from the publisher

You're survivors of the apocalypse. Whatever your survior guests bring to your diner, you should consider using in the kitchen! 
Survival Kitchen is a cooperative card game with app support about taste, texture and poison management.
Each player contributes ingredients from their hand to the ordered dish, scanning the ingredient card with the device's front camera. It all goes into the cualdron. Compare your ingredients and tools. What can you offer to the kitchen? Can you, as a team, set the right amount of food, and give it the ordered texture. Will, you be able to play the right amounts of the 4 types of taste? Will you be able to cook your dish in just the right temperature to maximize taste? Cooking aint easy! Luckily, your guests will not be picky!

Unfortunately, some ingredients are toxic. Your "survivors" won't survive your toxic recipes unless you act strategically and cook with caution.

The app (for Android and iPhone devices) guides you through the story and the rules, so you don't even need to touch a rulebook!

Survival Kitchen includes two playing modes:
In roguelike mode, you face different situations, each with its own challenge. You unlock and find new cards and make your diner larger and more successful, or at least, don't let it go abandoned, for as long as possible.
There is also a quick game mode, that allows for a shorter play time.

boardgame: Disney Encanto: House of Charms Game
description:
From Disney's newest movie, this children's board game will soon become the new family favorite. The world of Encanto is waiting for you and your kids. 
Collect charms with your family to power up magical doors before the Encanto burns too low! 
The unique look and magical gameplay are the perfect addition to your living room shelf for family game night. For all kids and adults ages 5 and up, all you need is 2-4 players to get started.

—description from the publisher

Players take turns placing variously colored hex tiles on to a common play area. As tiles are placed, areas of tiles may be formed. The goal of the game is for have the highest 'Secondary Score'. A Secondary Score is the *second* largest group of connected tiles.

Mechanisms that differentiate this game include: Using both personal and shared tiles, and the methods by which tiles are connected, smothered, blackmailed, or blocked. Primary colors, as well as black and white, may blend for example, and the resulting color provides extra options.

—user summary

Wall Streak is a board game where players take on the roles of investors, competing against one another to become the king/queen of Wall Street. Players accomplish this by having earned the most victory points at game's end. Victory can be earned one of three ways, buying company's (worth 5vp's for each owned at game's end), accumulating money (worth 1vp per every 5M at game's end), and completing investor goals (worth 3vp to 15vp). Players must find the right balance between the 3 in order to be crowned king/queen of Wall Street!

On player's turns they roll a single six sided die and move the corresponding number of spaces and then perform the action on the space. Players may pay to move forward one or two additional spaces for costs of 3M or 5M respectively. While navigating the board of Wall Streak players move following the direction/path that they are on until they reach an intersection. Once at an intersection players may change their path or continue following the path they were. The only restrictions being players are not allowed to double back and reverse course, and players must always travel clockwise in the company and money loops.

There are several different types of spaces which players can land on throughout the game. When a player lands on a M, T or gold bar space they draw a card from the respective deck and perform its action, or keep it if it is stock or movement adjusting card (found in the T (Transaction) deck) or an additional investor goal (found in the M (Merger) deck). Spaces with a money amount provide players with that amount of money when they land on them. On dice spaces players will roll either a single or double D6 dice and receive or lose money according to the color dice space that they landed on. Finally  there are 50 company spaces which players can land on. When landed on players may buy the company for it's current price, initiate an auction for the company, or pay rent to it's current owner.

Players must must find the right balance and timing between accumulating money and buying companies. The game end is triggered once a certain number of companies are owned by all players. This number varies based on the number of players in the game.

—description from the publisher

German-Soviet War 1941 is a two-player game themed around the first year of the "German-Soviet War" between Nazi Germany and the Union of Soviet Socialist Republics (USSR) in eastern Europe during World War II. Players assume the position of either Germany or the Soviet Union, and command their own forces to fight. The game covers the German invasion of the Soviet Union in June 1941 (Operation Barbarossa) to the capture of Moscow in December of the same year (the end of Operation Typhoon). The German player is required to achieve more military results than history, and if it is prevented, the Soviet player wins.

—user summary
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
With Numeroso, numbers visually come alive in our card battler game, along with a variety of games inspired by classic card game mechanics.

Numeroso Cards are based on a powerful Natural Structure of Numbers: prime factorization. Prime factorization is like the DNA of numbers, each number is made up of a unique collection of prime factors. Numeroso cards show the unique structure of each number visually.

In the title game, Numeroso, the prime number cards are used to bring composite number cards into play that contain them in their prime factorization. The composite cards act as creatures with attack/defense strength and special abilities. Players do battle until their life points hit zero.

—description from the publisher

The more words you know the greater your chances. Use your letter cards to create new words or add to existing words in play. Spell as many words as you can and earn the most points before the chalkboard gets wiped clean.

Using the letter cards in their hands, players take turns creating new words or adding to words already in the play area. Points are awarded for each letter used. In addition to Letter Cards, there are other cards that are wild or award special powers. The player with the most points at the end of the game wins.

—description from the publisher

Council at Daybreak is a social deduction game of mystery and discovery. Each game is only 15 minutes but you'll find yourselves playing for hours!

There is a heretic among you! In each game, players have a role to play in finding the heretic in the council. Behold, the heretic will do anything to not be accused...

The Divine Beasts set contains 4 different Abominations based on mythical creatures that bring a new level of challenge to your Zombicide games.

It contains:

4 Abominations
    1 Abomination Card
    12 Divine Beast Action Cards
    4 Skill Cards
    1 Rules Leaflet

Aside from their passive abilities, each Divine Beast has 3 special Action cards. When a Divine Beast would activate during the Zombie phase, it draws from its deck of Action cards instead. Then it performs the legendary actions described on the card, heralding in waves of chaos across the board! Once a Divine Beast runs out of action cards in its deck, it vanishes from the board, signaling the spawn of the next Divine Beast.

Defeating a Divine beast grants the Survivor with a blessing of a Skill card.

—description from the publihser

boardgame: Thorgal: The Board Game – Character Set Expansion
description:
Three new Characters with miniatures, boards and cards (3 Skill and 3 Item cards) ready to join your game.

Product description:

Petrov is an adventurer and mercenary travelling through lands and seas. He is a courageous warrior and thanks to his powerful stature, he is not afraid of wounds that would kill weaker people.

Tjahzi is a dwarf who has been friends with Thorgal for years. He has a strong connection to magic and has extraordinary knowledge about this, as well as about other worlds.

Vigrid is one of the minor deities residing in Asgard. He was banished from Odin’s court for his mischief, and only Aaricia’s help allowed him to return. His unique ability is shape-shifting—he can take the form of a dragon, a winged horse or even a spider.

The Pacific Air Race is an Aviation roll and move game where players travel along a path with tokens of Empire Flying Boats. The game was produced early during the war years before the Japanese Invasion of the South Pacific.

The path goes up to 100 that covers an area of the South Pacific including Australia, New Zealand, the South Pacific Islands and South East Asia. The numbered path consists of Green and Yellow circles, with the Green circles having instructions to follow, such as "Thick Cloud - Miss a Turn". First to reach the Circle marked 100 is the winner.

—user summary

C'est pas d'jeu is a card game set in the "Gaston Lagaffe" comics universe. 
In the game, each player tries to collect the different parts of short stories of Gaston from a hand of 6 cards. It is also possible to play Boom or Attack cards in other players boards to stop them until they play a Parade card (like in Mille Bornes).
Each game round ends when a player has completed a least one story and has played all the cards of his hand, and every player score points according to their cards palyed. The game ends when a player reaches 500 points.

—user summary
With 4 number dices you must try as fast as possible to create certain math exercises.  You must try to calculate from 10 to 100 within a set time, first to 100 wins.

Thorgal: The Board Game is a cooperative storybook adventure game. Players take on the roles of Thorgal, his wife Aaricia, their son Jolan, and Kriss —a deadly female warrior—, and go on a series of adventures. The game consists of 7 stand-alone scenarios, each taking roughly 90-120 minutes of play. Scenarios do not form a campaign nor have any legacy features, so they can be played by different playgroups and over an extended period of time.

Thorgal: The Board Game comes with a book of maps. Each scenario is played on a different map that players explore while fulfilling scenario goals and promises completely different adventures, hidden opportunities, side quests, and vivid characters. Players are free to choose whether they travel in one group or split to cover more areas at the same time. Each character gains experience during the game and advances their abilities. Characters gather resources and craft objects to help them survive in the dangerous world.

There is a storybook containing narrative descriptions of the encounters that Characters make in each scenario. The adventures described in the book are specific to each scenario and Character, with randomized elements offering higher replayability and allowing for repeated play. There are also Side Plots players can pursue to gain benefits and reveal additional pieces of the story.

Thorgal: The Board Game also has a unique action selection mechanism. In this new mechanism, actions have varying power depending on which other actions have already been taken. Players must carefully plan their strategy and sequence of actions taken, as this directly impacts the strength of the effect of each action.

—description from the publisher

At the beginning of the game, place the Smuggler token in the center of the board. It is a neutral token. If he takes a wound from any source, he is killed.

After the Battle (after all effects have been resolved), if a player has a unit linked to a Smuggler tile, he may shuffle an Instant tile from any opponent's tile discard pile into his token pile.

Note: When playing with the Smuggler, it is advised that players pick up tokens from a single pile or piles arranged in draw order, so as not to affect the drawing of a tile with a different back.

This mini-expansion was distributed in a welcome bag for the participants of Portalcon 23, which took place at the Silesian Stadium in Chorzów.

The main mechanic of this new army is managing gambles.

Discarding tokens earns gambles, which can later be spent on activating the traits of the units on the board, as their abilities are not free. The Headhunter, for example, has a regular ranged attack, but only spending a gamble will trigger the ability to use a sniper shot. If the trait is not paid, it simply will not be active.

Merchant Guild soldiers usually have low initiative - this is important because the cost of activating a given perk is equal to the initiative of this unit. Activating the Headhunter Sniper will cost one gamble, but another soldier with initiative 2 will cost two gambles. The maximum number of gambles that can be managed at a time is 7, so we should have quite a lot of room for maneuver.

100 extra cards to keep the fun times rolling with even more combos and EVEN MORE FUN! This deluxe deck will fit in the base Made Up Movies game box (sold separately).

INCLUDES: Made Up Movies Base Game + 100 ADDITIONAL CARDS (30 Movie, Cards, 25 Star Cards, 15 Genre Cards, 15 Setting Cards, 15 Studio Executive Cards)

THE SKIRMISH COMBAT GAME THAT PROVIDES PLAYERS WITH A GAME SET IN A PRE-GUNPOWDER TRIBAL/CLAN SETTING. THE RULES OFFER SOME NEW AND EXCITING APPROACHES TO WARGAMING.

Honour is used to drive the narrative of the game. Players are rewarded in honour for the things that they do in the battle. Honour determines who wins Tribal – it can be won and lost during combat, the deeds of combatants can win or lose honour, players can spend honour to do ‘dirty tricks’ to win combats.

The game is also played with a normal deck of cards. Cards determine movement, unit activation and – most importantly – combat. Players can actually be involved in the combat through playing/holding particular cards. Attacking, defending, waiting for an opening, bluffing the opponent – these are all strategies the player can use in a round of Tribal combat.

—description from the publisher

Scavenger hunt game with cards, timer, and a playing board.  Cards give players items to find worth different values.  Players advance on game board the number of spaces determined by the value of the items found.
