De Pyme a Superempresa is a business simulation game that develops a simulation of the dynamics of operation of a small company that stumbles every day due to incidents, lack of time and, above all, lack of knowledge. The objective of the game is to learn to manage time, solutions, acquire knowledge and improve productivity.

Test your knowledge on the world of animals as you race through the Wild Webs! Play cards that describe the animals to move forward, and be the first one to reach your tent to win!
—description from the publisher

In Wild Webs, players are working with a hand of 3 cards to select 2 cards containing attributes of the animal in the next path space. Since that is not easy, players are allowed to take and use the top card (only) of other players’ discard piles to use instead. Players try to avoid leaving helpful cards on top after their turn. Usually a space only has 1 or 2 forward path links, so it is not always easy to advance along a direct path. The deck consists of 100 cards, with plenty of duplicates and some multiple options cards with 2 different attributes listed.

This game is marketed as an educational game that is a fun way to reinforce knowledge of animals and the “game builds observation, strategizing, memory, decision making & process planning skills.” Those are present, but to a limited degree since the design is intended to allow players as young as 6, and the complexity of game play is low.

—user summary

Assassins the Hotel is set in modern times but with a slight time line detour. It is based off the Blood of the Raven Skirmish Rules and is all about you playing as an elite Assassins completing their assignments!

To the outside world, you appear normal along with having an everyday 9-5 job. You get up every morning and waive at your neighbors as you collect your morning paper to read while drinking a fresh cup of coffee. As you sit and read the paper, you have a smirk on your face because you see your work from the night before as part of the unsolved investigations. The cops have no fucking clue where to look and will never find the answers they are searching for. Even if they did, they couldn’t fucking touch you since you are part of an elite secret assassination organization that brings death to anyone willing to pay for it. To all others, you are just a normal everyday businessperson but to your targets…you are an elite assassin from Angelus Mortis.

boardgame: The Pursuit of Happiness: Gratitude Pack
description:
The Gratitude pack is Artipias way of saying our thanks to everyone showing their support by backing The Pursuit of Happiness while it was still on Kickstarter. It was given as a gift to the backers who patiently waited for the release of the game and then later released for purchase on their website.

This mini expansion contains cards that add to the already existing Item/Activities, Projects, Community, Jobs and Experiences packs.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
3dbikes is a roll and move bike Racing Game with 8 floor tiles you can use to create whatever race you want, 3d printed minis and scenery, action cards, and different roles for each player.
You can simulate real bike tournaments like the Giro, or the Tour the France.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

boardgame: Marvel: Crisis Protocol – Rival Panels: Weapon X Program
description:
The famous rivalry between two of the deadliest weapons to emerge from the Weapon X Program hits the tabletop with this new pack for Marvel: Crisis Protocol! Logan once counted Sabretooth among his few friends as the pair fought beside one another on a covert team, but Sabretooth’s unshakeable bloodlust tore the team apart, and now they face off as bitter rivals. Featuring beautifully sculpted new miniatures of Logan and Sabretooth, this pack adds new versions of these iconic characters to players’ rosters and introduces the new Weapon X Program Affiliation to Marvel: Crisis Protocol. Additionally, players will also find 6 Team Tactic cards that further enhance Logan and Sabretooth’s abilities as well as several props that allow players to customise their bases.

—description from the publisher
A simple roll-and-move game themed after Gaston Lagaffe, published as an attachment to Spirou magazine.

The year is 2123 AD. Neo-Gravity Racing has become the biggest sport in the world. Get on the track. Hit the boost pads — but watch out for attacks! Do you have what it takes to cross the finish line first?

Neo-G Racing (Clear Duels Series) is a futuristic combat racing game for 2 players (competitive) that combines hand management, route building, sealed bidding, racing, layering, and grid coverage in a fun way. It consists of 36 cards (18 Clear cards + 18 Standard cards).

This game is part of our Clear Series, more specifically our Clear Duels Series, which focuses on competitive games for 2 players and combines Clear cards and Standard cards in many surprising ways.

—description from the publisher

The game is played by two players, each of whom takes turns to move. Each of the two players takes over a fleet. A fleet consists of six ships: two ships of the line, recognizable by three ship towers, two large cruisers, recognizable by two ship towers and two small cruisers, recognizable by one ship tower. The winner is the player who destroyed the enemy fleet except for one ship and rescued at least two ships from his own fleet.

After each hit a ship loses a tower and if a ship without tower is hit, ist's eliminated from the game. The sturdier ships are also the slowest. There is also land based artillery at the edges of the game board. A ship is hit, when it gets into the crossfire of two enemy shops or artillery.

The game was patented in 1903 and produced till at least 1909. In 1908 twenty games were purchased by the German emperor for the German navy.

—user summary

Card game with 5 attributes.

You draft blind and select one attribute.

You compare the attributes on both cards. The player with highest value gains both cards.

'Zammar!!Wars' is a batting game in which the number of fingers specifies the dungeon to go to and the treasure to be obtained.
If you want to go to a dungeon with a higher reward, increase the number of fingers.
However, only the player who goes to the dungeon alone can get the reward.
In other words, if you go to a dungeon with a high reward, you may clash with your rivals, and as a result, you may not get the reward at all!
Six more pads of pictures for Make the Difference.

A domino-based trick-taking game for 2-5 players.

Bummert is inspired by traditional trick-taking games like Schnapsen and Bezique by featuring meld system and a stockpile, while also following in the footsteps of 42 by using dominoes to achieve this. Unlike 42 however, it is not the "heavy end" of the domino that determines the lead suit, but instead the second player in turn order, who gets to decide which of the two led suits "lock in" and become relevant for the rest of the hand.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Das Beinhaus von Sedlec: MDXI Erweiterung
description:
The expansion brings 5 new modules for the game at once.

The Poets want to lie next to lonely, unhappy lovers.

The Merchants want to lie next to farmers but far away from villains.

The Champions want to have as many skulls of certain species as possible around them - and The Ultimate Champion wants to be with the other champions, of course.

And with The Relics finally comes the solo module for the game that many have been eagerly awaiting.

—description from the publisher (translated)

With over 400 challenges and more than 20 hours of gameplay
 Play with all the family and against Carol!
Whatever your level.  Join Carol Vorderman and play the Sudoku DVD Game with your friends and family. 
 Special Feature - Carol's Sudoku Secrets
Carol reveals her tricks and tips for beginners and how to approach and quickly solve any puzzle for more advanced players
 What you need to play this DVD Game: Television, DVD Player (to print additional game sheets, your PC or Mac system must be equipped with DVD drive and Adobe Reader 5.1)
 Contents: DVD, 120 Puzzle Sheets, 4 Pencils & Instructions.

La Famiglia’s influence has proven to be far broader than anyone suspected. In partnership with the FBI, you embark on a special mission to strike at the heart of the organization. You arrive on a foreign shore and find a package awaiting you. Will you be able to dismantle La Famiglia once and for all?

Codebreaker contains one full storyline made up of two parts, with dozens of puzzles to decipher and clues to crack. It also contains a La Famiglia pin upon completion of the game! Play with a group or on your own for hours of fun.

Codebreaker is the final game of The Famiglia Trilogy, the sequel to La Famiglia (Game I) and The Takedown (Game II).

—description from the publisher

Miyamoto Usagi is a beloved character in the world of Usagi Yojimbo, a comic created by cartoonist Stan Sakai. He first appeared in 1984 and has since become a popular icon, with numerous comic book series, toys, and even an animated television show based on his adventures.

Today, we’re bringing this honorable character into the world of Zombicide Fantasy for you to use in any of your Zombicide fantasy games!
You can add the Kickstarter Exclusive Usagi Yojimbo pack to your pledge!

1 Miyamoto Usagi Miniature
    1 Survivor ID Card

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Massive Darkness 2: Zombicide White Death Crossover Pack
description:
Bring non-exclusive Survivors and Zombies introduced in the Zombicide: White Death Kickstarter into Massive Darkness 2! The crossover pack contains cards for the following:

White Death
     6 Heroes: Ogon, Yasuke, Sarnaï, Dragomir, Lorna, Jenia
     2 Roaming Monsters: Khan Abomination, Defiler Necromancer

Eternal Empire 
     6 Heroes: Jianrui, Yinying, Shao, Zhi Shen, Zhi Yang, Fengbao
     1 Mob: Warden Brutes

Divine Beasts
     4 Roaming Monsters: White Tiger, Black Tortoise, Vermilion Bird, Azure Dragon

Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles - Timecrash
     7 Heroes: Raphael, Donatello, Leonardo, Michelangelo, April O’Neil, Splinter, Casey Jones
     1 Mob: Foot Ninjas
     1 Roaming Monster: Shredder
    14 Item Cards

—description from the publisher

Adella is a 2 player abstract strategy board game played on a stone tablet.

The pieces are cast from marble dust and represent medieval tall ships and the three fates (known as tides).

Players take turns rotating the tides to advance their ships and wreck or broadside their opponent's. Players use the ports to build fleets and armadas creating defensive and powerful formations.

To win a player must sink all their opponent's ships.

—description from the designer
Many cards with film cliches, everybody selects some and tries to find them while watching the movie.

Launching on Kickstarter on the 16th May 2023.

Go RPG is designed to give you that D&D/Pathfinder vibe - even when you're forced to play 'Go Fish' with your 5-year-old. Mates want to play poker? Why not play Poker skinned in RPG?

This isn't a D&D campaign It's a 2 in 1 combo:

Firstly it's a skin for any standard card game you play with your non-RPG friends. This is the deck of playing cards that all true D&Ders and RPGers should have lying around their house ready to be used on an unsuspecting NON-RPG friend or child. Acquaint them, addict them, dominate them! We have 4 decks to choose from, Heroes, Monsters, Locations & Equipment. Each with 14 unique cards built into a 4-suited deck of 56 cards.

Secondly, By combining all 4 decks it will enable you to play Go RPG Campaign:

It's Deckbuilding with an engine mechanic (meaning you'll want to build your deck in a way that it'll help you win the game faster.)

We've playtested this game for a while now with fantastic feedback. It's simple to explain yet dynamic in its gameplay. With options for Single player right up to 7 players or using additional decks to expand the players even further. You could play this with 50 players if you really wanted to!

Game styles include:

     Single Player
     Co-op play
     Competitive play
     Boss Mode where 1 of the players plays as the monster. Essentially an Evil Dungeon master!


Here's how it works:

Each player is dealt 3 hero cards, and a certain number of location cards are drawn based on the number of players. These locations represent quests from a local lord or lady regarding attacks that have been happening in that area. Players are tasked to go and kill the monsters in order to receive 1 item from the item reserve for every monster their party slays.
During gameplay, each player takes turns starting a round and selecting which quest they want to take. Players may entirely or partially opt-out of questing this round and instead head to the tavern to heal their party or avoid the quests that round. 2 players can go on the same quest, and the player who selected this location first will go first in attacking order. Once all players have allocated their quests, they can go and fulfil the quest by flipping over the monster cards to see what monsters they are attacking.

The objective of the game is for your party to end with the most gold value.

—description from the designer

Swords and Penguins (S&P) is a simple PRINT AND PLAY game where you create a hero, buy weapons, fight enemies and attempt to save the town of Friendsville! All is needed to play is 7 printed letter sized sheets, a pencil and 2 six sided dice!

GAME STORYLINE:
Friendsville is a penguin town in the north pole where everyone are friends. A walrus by the name of Gus however didn't feel accepted, so him and his team stole 3 eggs, one of the them being the kings. You, as the hero penguin, must be trained and then go on a quest to get the eggs back to safety.

HOW TO PLAY:
Once you create your hero and place your player token on the map, you then make your way across the landscape moving from space to space. Some spaces contain enemy's, chests, places to heal and shops to buy new weapons! You collect the stolen eggs by landing on the spaces where they lay, however the kings egg is protected by Gus, the final boss. Combat is done by chucking 2 dice with the bonus of your weapon and comparing it to the enemy defense.

Will you lose all your health and give up or will you retrieve all the eggs and save Friendsville?!

—description from the designer

boardgame: Le Sorcier met la Zizanie (fan expansion for Les Charlatans de Belcastel)
description:
This expansion does not change the basic game. It is a supplement that is played between the potion preparation phase and the scoring phase. 
"The wizard takes a dim view of the activity in the bazaar of Belcastel.
His servants discreetly slip enchanted scrolls 
into the Charlatans' belongings. 
While trying to prepare a potion, a Charlatan reads one of the recipes tampered with by the Wizard.
The unpredictable consequences ripple out to the other Charlatans and stir up trouble among them."

This expansion allows for more interactivity between players. It is compatible with the official expansions but they are not required.

boardgame: Timeline：中国古代史篇 (Timeline: China Ancient History)
description:
Timeline series aims to combine playing and learning together.

Timeline: China Ancient History is a card game. Each card depicts an invention on both sides, with the year in which that invention was created on only one side. Players take turns placing a card from their hand in a row on the table. After placing the card, the player reveals the date on it. If the card was placed correctly with the date in chronological order with all other cards on the table, the card stays in place; otherwise the card is removed from play and the player takes another card from the deck.

Note: This game can be combined with any other title in the Timeline series.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Timeline：中国近现代史篇 (Timeline: China Modern History)
description:
Timeline series aims to combine playing and learning together.

Timeline: China Modern History is a card game. Each card depicts an invention on both sides, with the year in which that invention was created on only one side. Players take turns placing a card from their hand in a row on the table. After placing the card, the player reveals the date on it. If the card was placed correctly with the date in chronological order with all other cards on the table, the card stays in place; otherwise the card is removed from play and the player takes another card from the deck.

Note: This game can be combined with any other title in the Timeline series.

—description from the publisher

Orbit Master is a trick-taking strategy card game that is based on the Zodiac. In a turn by turn gameplay, players choose 1 Sign to play and the first player to build a House of the other 11 Signs under their Master Sign wins the game. But beware, the 13th Sign Ophiuchus can poison your Master Sign and change the course of the game.

The 12 signs of the Zodiac have existed in peaceful harmony since the beginning of time. However, the murmurs of a 13th sign have persisted without worry until now. The Snake Bearer, otherwise known as Ophiuchus has taken drink of the snakes venom and become corrupt with poisonous power. That poison has opened a black hole ripping the Celestial Orbit asunder releasing the balance of the 12 signs.

The Celestial Orbit needs to be restored, but with an Orbit Master to keep its hold. Which sign has the ability to muster the other 11 signs to re-establish the orbit?  And which sign can keep itself from being poisoned by Ophiuchus as well?

Each player must choose a sign to play and the first to bring the other 11 signs under their house becomes the Orbit Master!

—description from the designer

Welcome to the world of Thieves’ Kingdom, where aspiring thieves from across the city gather to compete in the annual Tournament of Thieves. This quick paced card game blends cunning strategy, chance encounters, and a visually enchanting world. Players must outwit and outmanoeuvre their opponents to collect the most impressive treasures and earn the privilege of joining the prestigious Guild of Thieves.

Thieves’ Kingdom combines strategy, chance and deception as players draw cards from the deck or even snatch them from their opponents' hands, seeking the perfect balance of treasures and action cards to propel them to victory. With a diverse array of fantasy themes, including pirates, goblins, elves and dwarves, players will experience an enchanting world where diverse characters and fantastical creatures take centre stage.

Players take turns drawing cards and playing treasures or actions, with the goal of accumulating five treasures and the highest score in victory points. Treasure cards are played face down, with some containing hidden traps to challenge opponents. Action cards allow players to execute strategic moves that can impact the game's outcome, adding unexpected twists and turns.

The game ends when a player starts their turn with five treasures in play. At that point, victory points are tallied, and the player with the most points is declared the winner. With bonus points awarded for collecting multiple treasures of the same suit or even five trap cards, Thieves’ Kingdom offers endless strategic possibilities for players seeking to claim the top spot among the city's elite thieves.

-description from designer

WXRDS is a fast-paced, crossword-style word game where players collect letters that match words from specific categories. After collecting a set of letters, players form crossed words on their individual game boards.

COMPONENTS:


     99 letter tiles with point values 1-5
     6 gameboards with built-in scoring tracks
     web app.


SET UP:

Flip all tiles face up between players.
Give each player a game board and set the scores to 0.

GAME PLAY:

Start the web app to reveal a category.
Players call out words that match the category and grab the first letter of their word. For example, if the category is "In the Kitchen", someone could grab the letter "K" for knife.
The app sets a 30 second timer to encourage rapid collection of letters. In case an identical word is called out, the first player grabs the respective letter.

Ten category rounds are played in order to collect ten letters.

After ten categories, players then have three minutes to use their letters to form crossed words on their game boards (Scrabble-style).

SCORING:

After three minutes, players score their words. Letters placed on bonus positions get 2x and 3x points. All words must be found in the dictionary to be valid. Invalid words are removed. Any letters not on the board have their points deducted from the score. Each player marks their score on their scoring tracks.

After validation and scoring, return the letters to the common draw pile and start the app again.

WINNING:

After several main rounds, the first player to exceed 99 points causes the game to end. The player with the highest score is the winner. In case of a tie, play another round.

—description from the publisher

America Trips is a travel game across North America and Canada. The 2 - 4 players roll their 4 game pieces - the small travel group - across the game board and try to reach their travel destinations (the respective fields of the same color). On the way, they collect rings that give them advantages and letters that are drawn from a bag in order to form as many travel-related terms as possible at the end of the game.

—user summary

BONN TOUR - on the road in the federal capital - is a dice game for 3 to 6 players. The players have the exciting task of guiding a small group of visitors through the federal capital on foot, by bus, by S-Bahn or U-Bahn and by boat.

There are 4 different movement options, which are always done with a die. There are question fields on the game board where a question card is drawn that has to be answered.

At the beginning, each player draws 3 destination cards. The order in which they approaches these targets is up to the player. The first player to reach their goals still has to get to the Bundeshaus to win the game.

The manufacturer of the game is: Bouvier University Bookstore, Bonn

Auf leisen Sohlen ... is a "sneak game" from the carpet company desso.
The 2 to 4 players each receive 4 figures: a mouse, a cat, a dog and a child in one colour. The figures are moved across the board by rolling the dice. "Small pieces" are beaten by stronger pieces, including your own. The players try to get all their pieces into their own loophole (=goal) after going around the game board.

This is the Dutch Efteling edition of Monopoly from publisher Identity Games. Note: The box contains the regular play figures, no Efteling play figures are included!

In this enchanting edition of the well-known Monopoly game, you will discover Efteling in a completely new way. You won't visit the park, but you might become the all-important director of the World of Wonders! As an amusement park strategist you are responsible for the success of Efteling. You ensure that the guest can enjoy as many well-known attractions as possible, but it is also your job to offer enough fairytale overnight stays with cottages and hotels. During this adventure, the Small Message and Treasure Chest cards can help you in your mission. But as in every fairy tale, an unexpected danger can lurk here too. Do you know how to let visitors enjoy your park the most? Accept the challenge and win the game!

In this solo rogue-like card game, you are a Warp-Lancer, a space captain for hire. You and your crew have been entrusted with a vital mission: deliver crucial information to the Federation headquarters before the Devot's armada can destroy what remains of society.

During your turn, draw a Beacon card to determine what happens when you exit light-speed warp. Roll a die and follow the result in the case of an event or fight a ship with your equipped weapons and systems.

You'll need to manage your available energy to power-up the  appropriate systems for the situation at hand, keep track of your currency, missile ammunition, warp fuel and crew to survive the 5 sectors leading to the final boss fight.

Find or buy new weapons and systems to upgrade your ship if you want to keep your crew alive and your ship's hull above the breaking point. Space is dangerous and victory can only be attained by defeating the enemy's flagship at the end of your journey.

-description from designer

Set in a medieval fantasy setting known as the Aetherverse, four royal siblings battle each other for dominion over the world. Take control of a regent with unique abilities and construct an army of your design to claim the throne and vanquish your enemies. Ruler of the Realms offers different ways to achieve victory and with a game board that changes on a game to game basis it will continue to challenge your strategic mind.

Dog Daze is a card drafting game for 2 to 6 players where every player is the owner of a dog walking business hoping to be the highest rated gig in the city. During the game, you will hire 6 dog walkers and assign each of them a dog to walk. While the dogs are cute and cuddly, the dog walking business is ruff and far from friendly.
​
Players will draft their dog walkers from hands that are passed around the table. Whomever you don't hire may end up working for your competition.

Whenever a walker takes a dog out for their afternoon stroll, the owner will leave a review on your website. The better the match, the better the review! Some owners primarily want an inexpensive option. Some dogs will only be happy with top shelf treats. Match up your walkers with dogs strategically to get the best ratings possible. The player with the highest online rating wins!

-description from designer

Web sling into action with the Monopoly: Marvel Spider-Man Edition game. It's a fun game for kids to play as they imagine suiting up as a favorite Marvel Super-Hero to protect New York City from Super Villains. In this game, Monopoly tokens represent 6 heroes from across the Spider-Man Universe. Players get to take the subway for shortcuts, grab the Symbol of Great Responsibility to unlock a Super Hero's unique ability, and use Daily Bugle cards to scheme against opponents.

Includes: gameboard, 6 tokens, 6 Spider-Power cards, 24 Super Villain cards, 15 Daily Bugle cards, 15 Spider-Sense cards, 32 Blue Spider-Tracers, 12 Red Spider-Bots, 1 cardboard Symbol of Great Responsibility, 92 cardboard Web coins, 2 dice, and game rules.

Scale-Up! The Business Game

“I am done, I quit!” are the words you said to your manager
3 months ago. With $50k of savings, you are ready to start a
new adventure: launch the furniture business you have always
dreamed of starting.

A month later, your company (MyOffice Inc.) is incorporated!
The next step is to recruit a great team. After a few interviews,you hire Sophia as Sales Manager, Marc as Purchase Manager, now it's to start doing business; purchase your first products, market your company, sell those products and, hopefully, make a profit!

The game has 7 business cases each with 8-10 cards.

Read the business case on the front of the card. Don’t look at the back yet!

Using the online app follow and complete the tasks one card at a time.

Compare what you did with the solution on the back of the card. Each case has one right answer.

Square & Compass is a resource management game where your successes can have you the most popular architect in town, or left penniless at the drafting table. A worker placement games played over a series of rounds, for 2-5 players, and an average playtimes of 30 minutes per player. Games can be as short as 60 minutes, or as long as 150 minutes. Players use action tiles to harvest resources, hire and play technicians, process resources into building materials, and draft/play building contracts. The first player to successfully complete 7 building contracts ends the game. But there are a lot of different ways to play Square & Compass, from sustainability bonuses given when special resources are paid for contracts. Or special building combinations made when completing contract sets.

—description from the publisher

You're nearing the end to your Quest. All that's left is to defeat the pig-faced, thief king...

But No!

They’ve lured you to the caverns and taken your hard-won gear. Until now, you’ve ignored their small-time needs. You’ve avoided their minor missions. But now, you have no choice…

You must collect the bugs, find the mushrooms, round up the chickens, and more!

You’ll also fight some monsters and collect treasures, of course!

Side Quest Caverns is a two-player game of adventure and survival.

The game includes two main types of cards: Cavern Cards and Item Cards.

The 33 Cavern Cards have an open cavern entrance, a blocked cavern entrance or a locked cavern entrance. On their reverse side, these cards may hide monsters, characters with Side Quests, or other surprises. One of these cards is the EXIT.

The 36 Item Cards have the swords, keys, bombs and quest items you’ll need to survive the caverns. You also have two Hero Cards (red and green), one for you and one for your opponent. You use these cards with the two hearts (heart-shaped blocks) to track the number of shields you have.

Some Item Cards include Magical Items that give your Hero an extra edge!

As you and your opponent choose your path through the caverns, you fight monsters, collect items, and solve side quests. You also collect jewels and as you complete side quests you earn unique items that can help you in your journey. Trade with your opponent to help you both on your path.

Earn the most jewels to WIN!

—description from the publisher

It is a card game that is based on a traditional card game called "The Dutch." The goal of the game is to be the first player to have 3 Poulpy tokens. To gain a Poulpy token, a player should be able to discard their last card. The cards contain illustrations of Poulpy, an octopus with different expressions. Players attempt to remember & correctly identify Poulpy's expressions in order to win tokens.

Players start with 4 cards face down in front of them as their "line." On a player's turn, they must: (1) Draw and secretly look at the top card, (2) Play the card in the discard pile face up OR add it to their line & discard one of the other cards face up, (3) As an option, discard one or more cards if they believe these to be identical to the last card played. If the discard was a mistake, the player/s must take back their card and add a card to their line without looking at it. There are special cards in the game such as the "Switch" & "Peek" cards that players can use to exchange cards between other players and look at one of their cards respectively.

The successful trivia classic - reimagined for a new generation! This is a trivia game where everyone has a chance to succeed! Each question contains three clues that take you closer and closer to the correct answer. You can choose when to answer, but the more clues you hear, the fewer points you get.

Generous question content means it can be played multiple times!
Everyone has a chance to score and win.
Game concept that is successful all over the world.

The classic board game Monopoly in a fun Urbanus jacket
(Re)discover the best jokes, most iconic movie scenes and the funniest songs by Urbanus in this special Monopoly edition.

Buy a house in the Hittentitsesteenweg, visit the Maurice Potteke Pisplaats, sucker in the Gladde Iolensloot or maybe you will be fined for cleaning your doorstep with mayonnaise.

It's all possible in this Monopoly edition: explore the world of Urbanus!

—description from the publisher (translated)

The Man Who Was Thursday is a game inspired by a mystery novel of the same name written by G.K.Chesterton. As a representative of anarchists, police, or suspicious neutrals, you infiltrate your agent into a chaotic secret council in London. The council is made up of 7 members, each representing a day of the week, including the governor, Sunday, and the secret police, Thursday, whose identities are completely veiled. Over the course of two months, you'll have to maneuver various agents to further your faction's goals, either unleashing a world-shattering uprising or subduing chaotic madness.

Players combine action dice and agents to solve missions across London, and control a total of 7 regions according to the results. The game ends after 2 months, or after the 3rd round. At the end of the game, the player who controls the most regions wins the game. In the event of a tie, the player with the lowest surveillance value of their agent wins. For victory, you'll need to solve puzzles and guess your opponent's identity.

Now the time has come for the showdown, you have to cross London on a tight balance of power.

"Thy hand, great Anarch! lets the curtain fall, And universal Darkness buries All." -Alexander Pope

-description from designer

Each player draws a picture card secretly showing a profession. Alternately, they ask each other yes-no-questions to eliminate all other professions. The player who gets the solution first wins the game.
All cards show a male and a female person. The purpose of this game is to learn that independently of sex all people could join any profession.

—user summary

With the official expansion of the cult drinking game klattschen of the brand DENKRIESEN you have the perfect game to experience a humid and cheerful evening with your friends. The deck consists of 60 playing cards with 58 new instructions and unique tasks.

Those who don't follow the instructions or make a mistake in their execution will have to drink (called 'gossiping' in the game).

Also, all players must abide by the 3 basic rules throughout the game:


     do not use the word 'drink' (also do not use: drank, drank, drank, etc.)
     always drink with the left hand!
     do not swear!


—description from the publisher (translated)

Mit der offiziellen Erweiterung des Kult-Trinkspiels klattschen der Marke DENKRIESEN hast du das perfekte Spiel um einen feucht-fröhlichen Abend mit deinen Freunden zu erleben. Das Kartenspiel besteht aus 60 Spielkarten mit 58 neuen Anweisungen und einzigartigen Aufgaben.

Wer sich nicht an die Anweisungen hält oder einen Fehler bei der Ausführung macht, muss trinken (im Spiel ‘klattschen’ genannt).

Außerdem müssen sich alle Spieler während des gesamten Spiels an die 3 Grundregeln halten:


     Nicht das Wort ‘trinken’ benutzen (auch nicht: getrunken, trank, trinkst usw.)
     Immer mit der linken Hand trinken!.
     Nicht fluchen!


—description from the publisher

Apart is a game in which the goal is to distribute your pieces, as opposed to a game in which you create groups.

Initially, each player's pieces are grouped together, and players try to move them efficiently so that none of their pieces are vertically, horizontally, or diagonally adjacent to each other.

The distance a piece moves is determined by the length of the line to which it is joined.

This game can be played with Generic Board "Square".

With the official expansion of the cult drinking game klattschen of the brand DENKRIESEN you have the perfect game to experience a humid and cheerful evening with your friends. The deck consists of 60 playing cards with 58 new instructions and unique tasks.

Those who don't follow the instructions or make a mistake in their execution will have to drink (called 'gossiping' in the game).

Also, all players must abide by the 3 basic rules throughout the game:


     do not use the word 'drink' (also do not use: drank, drank, drank, etc.)
     always drink with the left hand!
     do not swear!


—description from the publisher (translated)

Mit der offiziellen Erweiterung des Kult-Trinkspiels klattschen der Marke DENKRIESEN hast du das perfekte Spiel um einen feucht-fröhlichen Abend mit deinen Freunden zu erleben. Das Kartenspiel besteht aus 60 Spielkarten mit 58 neuen Anweisungen und einzigartigen Aufgaben.

Wer sich nicht an die Anweisungen hält oder einen Fehler bei der Ausführung macht, muss trinken (im Spiel ‘klattschen’ genannt).

Außerdem müssen sich alle Spieler während des gesamten Spiels an die 3 Grundregeln halten:


     Nicht das Wort ‘trinken’ benutzen (auch nicht: getrunken, trank, trinkst usw.)
     Immer mit der linken Hand trinken!.
     Nicht fluchen!


—description from the publisher

Klattschen is a great party game for a sociable and (un)forgettable evening with friends. 
You compete in teams and have to draw or pantomime funny and naughty terms. 
The team partners then have to guess the term correctly. 
Only the winning team may advance and the team that reaches the finish line first wins.

—description from the publisher (translated)

Das klattschen-Brettspiel ist ein großartiges Partyspiel für einen geselligen und (un-)vergesslichen Abend mit Freunden. 
Ihr tretet in Teams gegeneinander an und müsst witzige und unanständige Begriffe zeichnen oder pantomimisch vorführen. 
Die Teampartner müssen den Begriff dann richtig erraten. 
Nur das Gewinner-Team darf vorrücken und das Team, welches zuerst das Ziel erreicht, gewinnt.

—description from the publisher

DINGSBUMS - "Das grundverkehrte Partyspiel" is the first game in DENKRIESEN's Party Fever series and has 336 cards on board with a total of 900 unique, crazy and funny sentences. Replace the "DINGSBUMS" in the statements with creative and funny answers and find out which text is from Dr. DINGSBUMS. This game guarantees rousing party fun for the next game night and is ideal for guessing, deception, camouflage and riddles. Easy to understand, DINGSBUMS comes with a clearly high addiction factor for young and old. Thanks to included game block and pencils for up to 8 players, nothing stands in the way of instant fun.

—description from the publisher

DINGSBUMS – "Das grundverkehrte Partyspiel" ist das erste Spiel aus der Partyfieber-Serie von DENKRIESEN und hat 336 Karten mit insgesamt 900 einmaligen, verrückten und lustigen Sätzen an Bord. Ersetzt das „DINGSBUMS“ in den Aussagen mit kreativen und lustigen Antworten und findet heraus, welcher Text von Dr. DINGSBUMS stammt. Dieses Spiel garantiert mitreißenden Partyspaß für den nächsten Spieleabend und ist ideal zum Raten, Täuschen, Tarnen und Rätseln. Einfach zu verstehen, kommt DINGSBUMS mit eindeutig hohem Suchtfaktor für Jung und Alt. Dank enthaltenem Spielblock und Bleistiften für bis zu 8 Spieler steht sofortigem Spielspaß nichts im Wege.

—description from the publisher (translated)

“An intense real-time hockey experience that fits in your pocket!”

cross check  is a thrilling, head-to-head micro dice game that simulates the intensity of ice hockey. Using a team of eager players through three timed periods, block shots, check, and shoot by rolling your dice as quickly as you can. With both a unique shooting and a penalty mechanic the tension remains high, and each decision is critical. Games can even go to overtime and a shootout! Do you have what it takes to win?

cross check  is a must-have for every game library - it checks so many boxes in such a small package - yes, it all fits in a mint tin! 
Captures the intensity of Profession Hockey with real-time action?  Check!
Simulates shifts in momentum?  Check! 
Includes Overtime and Shootout rules?  Check!  
Fits in your pocket and can go anywhere?  Check! 
Plays in less than 10 minutes?  Check!

OBJECTIVE - Outplay your opponent in a real-time, dice-chucking, hockey game simulation!

SETUP – Each team receives 4 full net tokens, 5 meeple players and 2 D6 of their color.

HOW TO PLAY – Roll simultaneously and continuously (in real-time) trying to score on your opponent and win the game before time runs out!

DICE ROLLS
SEVENS
    BLOCK SHOT – Add back ½ net token     
    CHECK – Remove 1 opposing player

DOUBLES
    SHOOT – If  pip number is higher than sum     of net tokens, remove 1 net token

DOUBLE 1’s
    MOMENTUM PLAY – Reset 1 net token to      full or replace 1 player
 
HOW TO SCORE
When opponent loses last net token you score! Yell Score and stop the clock. Both teams reset net tokens. Player tokens remain the same.

PENALTIES 
When opponent loses all players, they receive a penalty. Yell Penalty and stop the clock. Opponent loses 2 full nets. Restart the clock and play on!

DELAYED PENALTY GOAL – Team doesn’t have enough full nets to satisfy penalty.
 
PERIODS
Countdown timer 2 minutes per period. Play 3 periods. You can adjust playtime and number of periods as necessary. At end of period, players and net tokens reset.
 
OVERTIME
If tied after 3 periods, start overtime. Each team gets 3 player tokens and 2 full net tokens. 60 second overtime.

SHOOTOUT
If still tied, each team receives 4 net tokens. Before each team rolls they must choose how many nets to wager. Each net reduces opponent roll by 2. Once a net is used it  cannot be used again. After the wager, each team rolls both their D6. Higher score wins round. Play at least 3 rounds. Ties result in neither team scoring.
 
FAQ 
-Only stop the Clock for Penalties, Goals, or End of Period.

-You cannot add players of a different color to your team.

-You cannot have more than 4 full nets at one time.

It’s time for a change, so you move out of the bustling city, to the quiet, small town of Arbor Falls. You set out to collect quirky furniture to decorate your empty home. Along the way, you help your neighbors, meet new people, and clean up the town. Through Calendar Events, meeting visitors at the Inn, using tools from the Community Tool Shed, Donating money for town renovations, and achieving Town Tickets, you will have plenty to do.  You will fit in with the community so well!

Arbor Falls is a 1-4 player worker placement and set collecting game. Over 4 rounds, you will complete Neighbor Favors and save up to buy furniture. The Favors give in-game points and the furniture give end of game points in both theme and color sets.

You can play with all the 5 modules of the game to have the full game experience or remove any number of the modules to make the game accessible to children, non-gamer friends, and family.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: The Secret of Eldorado: The print & play escape game
description: The Secret of Eldorado is a 90 minutes long printable escape room to play with your friends. Follow the progress of the expedition through the companion app, solve puzzles, and help the expedition reach the lost Eldorado before it is too late.

The Climbers & Terrorcotta Walkers comes with two new types of Zombies which you can add to any of your Zombicide fantasy games!

Defeated Terrorcotta Walkers have the special ability to reform themselves from the remnants of smashed clay left behind by the Survivors if there are still other Terrorcotta in their Zone at the end of the round. To get rid of them once and for all, Survivors must not leave a single one standing in the zone.

Climbers have evolved long fingers and claws which they use to climb over obstacles. Ramparts, barriers, and ledges will not keep these Climbers at bay. Survivors must be prepared to defeat these fellows when their defense is inevitably breached!

It contains:

15 Terrorcotta Walkers
    15 Climbers
    18 Zombie Cards
    1 Rules Leaflet

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Keenograms combines the word-spelling of anagrams with the gameplay of keno/bingo.

Each player gets a 5x5 card with 25 letters of the alphabet on it. (There are 6 cards in the Popular edition, 16 in the Deluxe edition.) Player agree on length of the word allowed, e.g. 3 letters, or 5 letters, etc.  The dealer then shuffles the deck of 26 letter cards -- one for each letter -- and turns them over one at a time, giving players time to place a marker on that letter on their card, if they have it.  Once a player can spell a word of the required length with the letters they have on marked on their card, they call out and win the round.

A "mystery word" version has each player write a word of the agreed-on length on a slip of paper and place it under their card.  When they can spell their word with the letters called out, they win.

Other modes are similar to traditional bingo: cover a row of 5, or all 4 corners, or the periphery, or the center, or an aggreed-on alphabet shape.

In this word game, two teams compete against each other!

Guess the right letters and find the word faster than the opposing team. Further game variants increase the difficulty and fun!

First, each team decides on a code word and lays it out face down in front of them. Now both teams guess letters over several rounds to find out the word they are looking for. The team that cracks the code word of the other team first wins.

DOS Second Edition is the true sequel to the classic game of UNO. As that game's spiritual successor, DOS Second Edition plays a lot like UNO but with a few twists.

Like UNO, the object of DOS Second Edition is to be the first player to get rid of all your cards, but now there are two discard piles and players are allowed to add two cards together to help empty their hand faster.

On your turn you can play cards from your hand onto one or both discard piles by matching color, number, or symbol. You can also add two cards together to make a number match, but both cards must be the same color (for example, if the top card of a discard pile is a red 7, you can add together a yellow 3 and a yellow 4 and play them together as a 7 on that pile).

Watch out for classic UNO action cards like Skip, Reverse, and Draw 2 as well as the all-new Wild Play 2 card, which forces the next player to play 2 cards on their turn or lose their turn and draw 2 cards as a penalty.

Note: DOS Second Edition is has completely different gameplay than the earlier DOS card game (2018).

-description from designer

[Adapted from the box back and instruction sheet.]

Here is a magnificent mound of fun games that you can play, all of which will help children develop their spelling skills. This pack consists of instructions, and 100 small (42 x 62 mm) cards with letters, letter values, and illustrations by Tony Ross from David Walliams's book Awful Auntie. Also included is a list of words that are drawn from the [British] government's notes for Key Stage 1 and 2 spellings.

Games include:

CROSSWORD: Players take turns playing letters from their hand and a draw pile onto a growing crossword on the table, scoring the sum of the letters' values in the new word. When the deck runs out, the highest score wins.

WORD CHASE: Players each choose a 7-letter word from the word booklet, and then take turns in rummy-like play until they have the letters for their word in their hand.

WORD GRAB: All 100 letters are face down. Players take turns turning up one letter.  When someone sees a word that can be spelled, they call it out, collect the letters, and spell the word in front of them. You can also claim others' words by calling an anagram of their word, or by making a new word by adding new letters. When the cards are all face up, the player with the most letters wins.

GRID LOCK: 16 cards are placed in a 4x4 grid, and players write as many words as they can spell from those letters. In GRID LOCK II it's the same but with the requirement that letters be in an adjacent chain like Boggle.

SPELLBOUND: Four letters are placed on the table. In a turn a player places a letter on one of the four columns, at the top or bottom, and says a word that includes the letters in that column in that order. The first to empty their hand wins.

Players are astronauts that have crash landed on an inhospitable planet called Atalix. A rescue shuttle is sent from your space station, but with difficult terrain and unpredictable storms near the crash site the closest the shuttle can land is several miles from the crash site. Unprepared for the harsh planetary conditions your team must gather resources and collect parts of their crashed ship in order to survive. Players are encouraged to write their own story as they make decisions about how to survive. The game presents players with real, impactful choices that change the course of the game including their objectives, strategies, and challenges as the game progresses.

Escape from Atalix is a cooperative deck building game where players work together to survive and complete projects on the harsh surface of a distant planet. Each player manages their own deck of cards which they will use to move around the board, harvest resources, battle with hostile aliens, research the planets ecosystem, and upgrade capabilities with new cards.

Cards also represent energy, the more cards you have the more turns you can take before the ending the round forcing you to return to what's left of your ship. Each round that passes brings you closer to the brink of destruction. Researching the planet can help you upgrade your tools and increase your capabilities including the ability to retain cards between turns and add cards to your free play selection.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
After several days of rain, commercial pickers, hobbyists, hippies, and other back woodsy folk gather in hope for fungal treasures!

Outmaneuver your fellow misfits to forage for goodies, satisfy chef requests, and heal the earth. Grow and consume mushrooms to level up your abilities. You must hurry because increasing pollution and destruction will damage your foraging grounds.

In Mushroom Foray, 5 decks of cards representing regions are positioned randomly in a horizontal line. Below the regions, players drive their run down vehicles laterally to forage the regions and swap treasures at trading tents. On their turn, players can either stay in their region to forage for 2 cards or move 1 space in either direction to forage for 1 card. Then, players can play action cards, trade, or grow mushrooms.

There are two types of action cards: Victory Cards and Ability Cards. Victory cards are played face up in front of the player who satisfies the mushroom requirements displayed on the card. For example, “Save the Bees!” card requires 1 Reshi mushroom tincture to play the victory card. The first player who plays 8 victory cards wins the game.

Chef Requests are a type of victory card that are satisfied by gathering the mushrooms needed for recipes. Deforestation, Contamination, and Plastic Waste are special victory cards called destruction cards that are randomly drawn from the burn, forest, river, and plains regions. After these cards are drawn, they must be placed face up on their associated region deck until a player can satisfy the restoration requirements. All players lose if the burn, forest, river, and plains regions all have unresolved destruction cards for a full turn.

Ability cards grant players special skills. Each player can only play 3 ability cards, so the players will need to choose the ability and timing carefully. Each ability card can be played after discarding the required mushroom icons representing the specific regions where the mushrooms were foraged. Magic mushrooms can satisfy any mushroom icon (plains, forest, river). Magic Mushrooms can also be spent to reactivate any 1 played abilities. The player immediately performs the ability card action after playing the card face up on the table. Then, the player can choose to perform any of their previously played abilities in any order. Finally, in turn order, any opponent sharing the same space of the player who played an ability can choose to perform any 1 of that player’s abilities.

On their turn, players can also exchange cards from their hands with the trading tents. Tents are split into 2 halves with a number or symbol on each side. One half of the tent is always covered by cards. The numbers and symbols represent the required number and type of cards that can be traded for the cards presented on the other half of the tent. A player can place the required cards on one side of the tent to take the available cards.

Players will need to grow mushrooms to satisfy some of the victory cards. Each player has 4 cards arranged as a grow chamber: Prepare, Grow, Grain, and Tincture. At the start of the game, each of the grow chamber cards are greyed out. Players must satisfy the requirements to flip cards to activate each phase in the grow chamber. First, players must place a cultivatable mushroom (indicated by a mushroom icon) onto the Prepare card. Then, the player must discard the requirement cards to flip the Grow card. Now, the player will receive 1 mushroom token each turn onto the Grow card. Each mushroom token inherits the characteristics of the prepared mushroom card. Finally, the player can discard the requirements for the Grain and Tincture phases to flip their mushroom tokens into colonized grain or medicinal tinctures. Grain and tinctures are requirements for some victory cards.

The game immediately ends when a winning player has played 8 victory cards or when the burn, forest, river, and plains regions all have been damaged by destruction cards for a full turn. All players lose if the second condition is met.

-description from designer

This is an updated version of the ancient game of the same name.

Kariuchi is a race game using four 2-sided dice (4D2). "Kari" refers to the two-sided dice and "uchi" refers to casting them. The basic game is a 2-player game, in which both players try to move their 4 pieces along the path according to the dice rolls and try to reach the goal with their pieces as quickly as possible.

The board consists of 30 fixed points arranged in a circle. Each player's four pieces are not on the board initially, but are placed in their waiting area as reserve pieces. Starting from base point 1, the goal is passed after fixed point 30. The goal is not yet reached just by reaching Fixed Point 30.

To move a piece, the player moves one of his or her own pieces along a fixed point from 1 to 30 on the circumference of the circle, as many times as the number of "kari" dice cast from a set of four dice. The player is free to decide which piece to move in each turn. When a piece stops at a fixed point 5, 10, or 15, the player can change the direction of movement in the next turn and move the piece to a fixed point on the opposite side of the circumference as a shortcut. If the piece stops at the center point "A" during the shortcut move, the player can further change the direction of the move and choose to move in the direction of fixed points 20, 25, and 30.

If a piece you have moved stops at the position of a piece you already have on the board, you can stack the pieces. All of your pieces (up to 4 pieces) can be stacked on top of each other. The stacked pieces can be moved as a single piece. You can also separate the pieces and move only the top piece(s). If a piece you have moved rests on an enemy piece already on the board, the enemy piece is removed and returned to the enemy's reserve position. If a piece lands on an enemy stacked piece, all enemy pieces are moved back to the reserve position. The pieces are then separated and become individual pieces.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Thorgal: The Board Game – Promo Box
description:
The Promo box contains all components that did not make the voting poll for the stretch goals. It can also store some of the additional components from the core box, the contents of the Character Set Expansion, the Companion Cards, the Wooden Polyomino Tiles, and even the rolled-up playmat.

Contains:
- Muff (1 pet miniature and cards in six languages)
- Avrenim (1 pet miniature and cards in six languages)
- 51 plastic enemy tokens (12 minor, 12 moderate, 12 major, 6 massive, and 9 mystical)
- 12 translucent 'frosted' dice

The savage clash for domination.

Battle head to head as you vie for the loyalty of the three Orc factions. Build your strength, sabotage your opposition and utilise the skills of your allies to prove that you have the strength to lead the Orc Horde.

The mighty Orc Warlord is dead - now a new challenger will need to command the respect of their fellow greenskins to claim the Warlord’s mask!

You must fight to win the loyalty of the three Orc factions, drawing strategically from the Orc Horde while using powerful abilities to gain the edge over your opponent. Be mindful, however - while building the strongest Tableau is the name of the game, the collection of the six putrid Vile Ichors offers an alternate and immediate means to victory for either player!

A monopoly (from Greek monos μόνος (alone or single) + polein πωλεῖν (to sell)) exists when a specific person or enterprise is the only supplier of a particular commodity.

Monopoly is a roll-and-move game where players move around the game board buying or trading properties, developing their properties with houses and hotels. When opponents land on players owned property, the owning player collects rent from the landing player. Owning sets of properties increases the rent paid to the player who owns them, by the player who lands on the square. The ultimate goal is to have the most money and drive opponents into bankruptcy.

The classic Monopoly with constructions and other elements of Sankt Augustin, in Germany.

boardgame: Hard Level: Rise of the Necromancer
description:
Hard Level is print and play board game where you control your character in a stage like in console or PC platform game. Like in those games you will be controling your character on their way to the end of level. But instead of controler you will be using dice and pencil. You will face enemies, avoid traps, jump from platform to platform and do all the stuff you already know.

—description from the publisher

Players compete to build the biggest, successful chicken farm in 11 rounds or less. Foxes are a constant pest, but so can be your neighbors.

The aim is to reach the winning number of chickens before your opponents. You can do this by building, buying or stealing farm components, eggs and chickens themselves. Event cards can help or hinder your efforts.

Managing your egg production is key, but there isn't just 1 way to building a successful farm. You decide what works for you.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Roll and Rails: Italian Islands map pack
description:
Italian Islands map pack expansion for Roll and Rails game. This expansion includes 2 new maps:
Sardinia for solo play
Sicily for multiplayer game

boardgame: Roll and Rails: Poland map pack
description:
Poland map pack expansion for Roll and Rails game. This expansion includes 2 new maps:
Upper Silesia for solo play
Poland for multiplayer game

A collection of 3 word games to encourage fun and creativity in spelling. Working with guidelines for Key Stage 1 & 2 these games will help children develop spelling and word building skills, whilst maintaining the enjoyment of a card game.

Contents:
100 letter cards
40 Bookworm cards
1 minute timer

-from the back of the box

---

The games are:

Matilda's the Bookworm: With a hand of 7 cards, each player in their turn asks another player for a letter, who must give it to them if they have it, otherwise they draw a card. Then they spell a word from their hand or discard 3 cards, drawing to replace the cards. They get 1 bookworm card for a word of 3 letters, 2 cards for a word of 4 letters, and 3 cards for a word of 5 or more letters. The first player to have 10 or more bookworm cards wins.

Matilda's Wordgrid: Deal out cards to make a 4x4 grid. Players have 1 minute to write down all the words they can spell using the letters in the grid. The player with the most correct words gets 1 bookworm card. Repeat until a player has 5 bookworm cards and wins.

Matilda's Wordstock: One card is dealt to each of 5 columns on the table. From their hand of 10 cards, a player in their turn plays cards to a column to make a new word, using or covering previous cards, or discards 3 cards; cards used are replaced from the draw pile. Players get 1, 2, or 3 bookworm cards depending on the length of the word made; the first to 10 book worm cards wins.

boardgame: Roll and Rails: Austria-Switzerland map pack
description:
Austria-Switzerland map pack expansion for Roll and Rails game. This expansion includes 2 new maps:
Switzerland for solo play
Austria for multiplayer game

A collection of 3 word games to encourage fun an creativity in spelling. Working with guidelines for Key Stage 1 & 2 these games will help children develop spelling and word building skills, whilst maintaining the enjoyment of a card game.

Contents:
100 letter cards
1 Spinner

-from the back of the box


The cards have a letter on one side and 1/8 or a picture of the BFG (big friendly giant) on the other.

The games are:

Build a BFG: Each player has a 5 card hand face up on the table, and there is a 3x3 grid of cards in the center. On their turn, a player either spells a word from their hand with up to 1 card from the grid, or discards 3 cards; all cards use are replaced from the discard pile. The cards of the word are flipped, and the player keeps all parts of the BFG picture they lack.  The first to finish their picture wins.

BFG Crossword: On their turn a player plays cards from their hand to a growing crossword on the table. They get 1 point per letter in the word they make; the first to 20 points wins.

BFG Grab the Word: All 100 cards are placed letter-side down / BFG picture part side up. On their turn a player spins the spinner and flips over a card with the picture part selected. When anyone sees a word they can spell from the revealed letters, they shout the word and collect the cards. After all cards have been revealed, the one with the most cards wins.

boardgame: Roll and Rails: Czech-Slovakia map pack
description:
Czech-Slovakia map pack expansion for Roll and Rails game. This expansion includes 2 new maps:
Northern Ireland for solo play
Wales for duel play
England for multiplayer game

boardgame: Charlie and the Chocolate Factory: Marvellous Maths Games
description:
A collection of 3 math games to encourage fun and help with learning mathematics. Working with guidelines for Key Stage 1 & 2 these games will help children develop skills in addition, subtraction, division and multiplication, whilst maintaining the enjoyment of a card game.

Contents: 
87 number cards [numbered 1-10, colored purple or yellow]
13 Golden Ticket cards
4 playing pieces
2 dice

-description from the back of the box


The games are:

Charlie's Golden Ticket: Five golden ticket cards are on the table, and each player starts with one number card. On their turn a player draws a number card, and if they can combine the numbers in their hand to equal a golden ticket card, they claim that card.  The 5 table cards are replenished when they run out, and the first to get 4 tickets wins.

Charlie's in a Spin: The number cards are laid out to form a spiral playing track. Players start their pawns on the start card. On their turn they roll a die, move that many spaces, and say what the product of the new card's number and their die roll is; if incorrect they go back. The first to reach the center card by an exact roll wins.

Closest to Zero: Players start with 2 cards in hand. Yellow numbers are positive, purple numbers are negative. Players can request an additional card up to 3 times, then the player whose cards total closest to zero wins a golden ticket card. The first to have 4 tickets wins.
family: Books: Charlie and the Chocolate Factory

description:
The lost unit in Francis Tresham's 1825 series. Unusual for an 18xx game it is designed for one player (it can also be played with two players).

The objective of the single-player game is to take the minimum number of turns to run out the bank.

This is a stand alone game, although some of the needed components need to be drawn from another 1825 unit.
Regional Kit R4 can be used to extend the Unit 3 board or the Unit 4 board.

The Trio Play Tarot deck is actually three decks in one.

First and foremost the Trio Play Deck is a (78 card) tarot deck with a design that highlights the ruling elements of each suit/card within the deck.  Red is used for cards ruled by Fire (wands/clubs), Blue for Water (cups/hearts), Yellow for Air (swords/spades), and Green for Earth (pentacles/diamonds).

The Trio Play Tarot Deck can also be used as a standard deck of playing cards. The Knights and the Major Arcana cards have a design that readily distinguishes them from the cards you need while playing games like Gin Rummy or Poker. These differences in the design allow them to be easily found and removed when you are ready to play.

Thirdly, the design of the Trio Play Deck allows for it to be played as a color/number matching game similar to public domain games such as Crazy 8's created in the 1930's and Mau Mau created in the 1950's or other popular multi-color matching games like Uno created in the 1970's. The Trio Play is a shedding type card game in which the player's objective is to empty one's hand of all cards before the other players have a chance to.

The Trio Play deck uses action cards similar to the German game Mau Mau where you can inflict penalties on the other players forcing them to skip a turn, change/switch the order of play, or force the next player to draw two to four cards.  Wild cards have also been borrowed from traditions such as Joker's Wild or 8's being Wild in Crazy 8's, allowing you to use those cards in place of any color/number card you might need to continue your hand without adding more cards.

—description from the designer

The rules for the Trio Play deck are not in the card deck box, but available online.

The designer, Matt Green, is a graphic designer who also did the graphical design of the cards.

—user summary

Unspoken Words is a card game designed to encourage meaningful conversations within your immigrant family.

Each box holds 120 cards:

     100 question cards prompt conversations around 5 topics: Culture & Immigration, Relationship, Identity, Reflection, and Childhood. 
     20 action cards help break the ice and add a fun twist to the game.


Ways to Play

Take Your Pick
Take turns drawing a card from a category of your choice. Each player then answers the question or performs the action accordingly.

If I Were You
Draw a uestion card from a category of your choice, then have the other player(s) answer as if they were you. The player whose answer is closest to your would-be answer wins the round.

Shuffle and Draw
Shuffle the deck and have everyone draw a different card. Then take turns answering each player's question cards. Play action cards as a group.

Speed It Up
Shuffle the deck. Each player takes turns drawing cards and answering as many question cards as possible in 45 seconds. Skip any action cards that come up.

—description from the rules

Have you always wanted to throw a cake like Stan Laurel and Oliver Hardy or Charlie Chaplin did in black and white slapstick movies? Finally, you have the chance - and in colour. The winner is the person who is the most skilful at throwing cakes with different coloured icings and can stack the most matching icings next to each other.

Dorty jsou vrženy is a party game where age, knowledge or experience doesn't matter. But being skilful in throwing cards in different fashion might come in handy. And so a child can play with grandma or a bunch of adults - they all have similar chances.

Choose a card, pick one icing you like the most and throw the card on the table. If you connect the chosen icing on multiple cards, you score points! But beware, your scoring card dictates how to throw cards. With your eyes closed? Toss it like a disc? Or slam it to the table?

—description from the publisher

You are priests of Zarot tasked with building Temples. Play your cards to build temples or be a heretic by hoarding them. Keep an eye on the calendar as each discard shortens the time you’ll have to score.

In Zarot players are trying to score the most points by using their cards to form patterns called Temples. Each round a different suit is used to start a Temple. The players with the highest score after 5 rounds wins the game.

Discarding is optional, but all discards are placed around a Calendar card in the center of the playing area. Once the calendar is full the round is over so discard carefully.

You can also choose to score by being a Heretic. This means you are forsaking your duty to build Temples and simply hoard cards. At the end of the round, If you have no Temple card in your hand or in play, then you may add up the number values from all of your cards and that total is your score for this hand.

-description from designer

Sprout is a cooperative adventure strategy game about restoration and landscape creation. Instead of saving a world from the threat of impending destruction, players take on the role of four characters who find an already destroyed world and bring it back to life through peaceful actions such as walking, planting, and clearing away ash. (Well…. there’s also a threat of impending destruction. More on that in a bit.)

Players take on the roles of four “lifelings”, elemental sprites with the power to create life wherever they walk. When a lifeling pawn moves across the board, they trace their path by placing hexagon tiles that match their element. (Grass, water, soil, or ice) The tiles interact and stack, creating a unique, colorful map on every playthrough. Players must hurry to cover the entire map, called “the Barren”, with these “life tiles” before the end of twelve rounds, or else they lose the game by the volcano at the center of the Barren erupting.

Complicating the strategy, four enemy characters called the “flamelings” move around the board with randomized paths determined by dice rolls. Flamelings remove (“burn away”) life tiles wherever their tokens move and can also knock out lifelings, cause explosions, and spread ash tiles (which must be removed for the players to win). Players must work together, using their actions, unique special abilities, and tile interactions to avoid, trap, and distract the flamelings, who can respawn even after being knocked out.

The randomness of the enemy’s movements, as well as the unique landscapes created by players, necessitates new strategies every playthrough. For experienced players, difficulty can be adjusted by lowering the number of rounds the players have to accomplish their goal.

This game is appropriate for players who enjoy whimsical storytelling, light role playing, ticking-clock challenges, and the satisfaction of placing the last piece of a puzzle.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
A light card game, yet strategic and planned,
Buy cards or download an instrument in hand.
Match the suits at every turn,
And spread the samba for all to learn.

With a good combination, you'll get ahead,
Instruments of strings and percussions to be led.
Spend an action to invite a sambista friend,
And with them, build up your score to no end.

In your samba circle, you have autonomy,
But be careful not to break the harmony,
For a crossed samba is a big irony,
It hinders strategy and melody.

Last but not least, keep your eyes open wide,
For the malandros* who bring along an interesting side,
Adding interaction and excitement to the ride,
Making the game even more of a pride.


    An specific kind of "trickster", in Brazilian culture, associated to marginalized population from suburbs where the samba grew. 


—description from the publisher

BATTLE of TAROT is a two player card battling game using a single 78 card tarot deck inspired by games like Magic: the Gathering, Hearthstone, and maybe a little from MOBAs too.

“Armed with arcane spells, two medieval kingdoms meet on the battlefield for war.”

Play Minor Arcana suit cards from your hand to rally your armies on the battlefield to then march, flank, and battle the enemy until you can defeat your opponent’s castle. Each player is granted Major Arcana Divine Signs to cast as powerful arcane spells to turn the tides of war. Will the members of your court aid you in victory? Or will you be cut down and trampled into the sod by the advancing enemy? Only time will tell.

—description from the designer

In Favor: Gods of Oethera, players wield 1 of 6 unique characters to explore uncharted lands in a contest to win the Favor of the Gods. The first player to reach 10 points of Favor, or to control all 4 Settlement Banners at once, wins the game!

Using Action Points, players build the map as they search the Forests, Marshlands, Mountains, and Wastelands for Ruin Tiles, earning Relics to buff their stats before eventually offering them to the Gods for points of Favor. Favor can also be earned by capturing the Banner in each Region's Settlement, interacting with NPCs through narrative Events, and fighting other players.

Each turn begins by revealing a God Card and following the God's decree. The Gods can help players, hinder players, or alter the state of the board. Players then take their actions before drawing Action Cards to end their turn.

Favor offers multiple paths to victory, an open world feel, high replay value, and endless opportunities for big plays, sequencing, combos, and turn optimization. Who will come out on top, and win the Favor of the Gods?

-description from designer

boardgame: New Yankee Letter Cards for Word Building
description:
NEW YANKEE LETTER CARDS
FOR
WORD BUILDING

AND PLAYING ALL THE OLD LETTER GAMES
TOGETHER WITH THE FOLLOWING NEW ONES

Word Building
Out in the Cold
Puzzle Your Neighbor
The Traveling Letters
Patchwork
Yankee Loto
The Spelling Match
Syllabication
Speculation
Letter Pool

—description from the box

The box has over 200 letter tiles about 3/4" square.

Unfortunately the rules are missing. But undoubtedly the "old letter games" included Anagrams, which was very popular in the late 1800s and early 1900s.

—user summary

A local dungeon keeper has hired you and your company to construct some traps for their newly purchased dungeon. You have 8 days, thus 8 traps, to fulfill their request. Beware, however, as this client can be a bit picky. They only know what trap they want the day of so there's not a lot of ability to plan ahead! They may even want some "personal space" installed! Best of luck!

Roll a die and create traps to hopefully cover the whole board in eight turns. Try your best to leave no white spaces left, because if you fail, points will be deducted from your total score.

—description from the publisher

The local Mining Grounds are a hot spot for resource gatherers and collectors alike. You'll find all sorts of crafting materials out there: wood, dirt, stone, Pure Slimes, and may even run into a treasure chest or two. But it's important that we also keep our own greedy nature in check! You take what you mine. If you don't have enough space for it, tough luck -- there's a penalty fee you'll have to pay. Best of luck out there!

Roll a die and try to fit in the material corresponding to the number you rolled. Put the material in your backpack or if you don't want to, discard it and take away some points for later. Your backpack has a limited space. If you run out of space you will have to discard that item and pay a fee.

-description from the publisher

Who doesn't want to relive 2020?!  Right?  No?  Nobody???  Well, maybe if we could look back and laugh at it, right?  (Or is it too soon to bring up the election?  Or the absence of toilet paper?  Or the inception of mandatory mask wearing?)  Well...it's funny in the game, anyway.

In 2020 The Game, 2-6 players roll-and-move their way around the board, trying to acquire (read: "land on and not lose later") 270 electoral votes, FTW.  As you & your opponents use some of the best movers ever made (no lie—you can BE the toilet paper!!!), learn how many electoral votes come from each state in the union & cheer each other on as you all get closer to ending the game.

They thought of EVERYTHING for this one, folks!  The [Community Chest/Chance style] cards have every bit of the flavor of 2020, with a clean art style & tongue-firmly-in-cheek.  If you missed it IRL, now you can finally host your own "super-spreader event" or stage your own "peaceful protest", but whatever you do, don't get sent back to quarantine!  (Those dang variants...)

A variation on the classic Loaded Questions.  Where up to 6 players participate in a question/answer style party game.  The title is a play on words — where "loaded" = "rich" — because the game implements a scoring system which rewards players with "Money Cards" for: (1) accurately guessing which of their friends submitted which answer, (2) submitting the favorite answer, and (3) matching answers with the on-turn player.  This isn't a novel concept, but it does mean the social deduction element is *not* the only piece of the puzzle.  Each player takes 3 turns, so you can see the money piling up & the finish line drawing closer each turn.

boardgame: Masters of the Universe: Battleground – Hordak on Mantisaur
description:
While on Beast Island, Hordak spotted a gargantuan praying mantis and ordered his subordinates to ensnare her. The mantis tore an entire squad of Horde Troopers to pieces but she succumbed to Mosquitor's cunning plans. From that point onwards, the leader of the Evil Horde prides himself on his unique mount, the Mantisaur.

—description from the publisher

Mike Hutchinson's Hobgoblin is a fast-paced and thrilling rank and flank tabletop miniatures wargame set in the fantastical underground world of The Rotvärlden. With simple and adaptable rules, players can collect and field any fantasy army they can imagine and engage in brutal battles that last under two hours.

The game features original artwork from graphic artist CROM and encourages players to let their wild imaginations run free as they battle alongside cavern fortresses and underground oceans. Whether you're a seasoned player of fantasy army wargames or new to the genre, Hobgoblin offers a fresh and exciting take on the fantasy mass battle experience.

The core ingredients that make up Hobgoblin’s unique gameplay are:

     Rock-paper-scissors relationship of unit types, meaning no unit is ever master of the battlefield, and every sort of miniature can have a role to play in your army
     Simple and consistent stats and combat rules, making battles super-fast to resolve
     Magic, Cursed Artefacts and Fortune Cards provide a huge range of possible strategies and tricks to explore.
     Every design decision in the game pushes towards brutal efficiency and smooth, quick gameplay


—description from the publisher

In Hell of a Run, you'll embark on a wild and hilarious journey to become the next king of Hell. With your unique deck of cards based on the seven deadly sins, you'll race against other princes of Hell to reach Heaven first. The game is filled with strategy and surprises as players play cards to move closer to their goal while also sabotaging their opponents.

But it's not just about winning the race - it's about having a hell of a good time. With its immersive theme, creative gameplay, and engaging mechanics, Hell of a Run is a hit with board game enthusiasts and casual players alike. Get ready for a devilishly fun time!

—description from the designer
