Your strategic decisions, crew management, and piloting skills will determine each mission's success and your crew's safety.

Sitting in the cockpit, you can feel the uncertainty in the air. The mission briefing has just concluded, and you know that the success of this operation is crucial for the war effort. Your target awaits; a heavily defended enemy facility that must be destroyed, or rendered unusable. But beware, enemy interceptors, mechanical failures, and dreaded flak defenses will test your mettle every step of the way.
Will you navigate skillfully through enemy territory, win over the waves of hostile fighters charging at your bomber group, and drop your payload on target with precision? Will you effectively manage your crew, utilizing their expertise correctly, and make critical decisions under pressure? The mission's fate and your crew's lives depend on your skill, courage, and strategic choices.

Choose the crew for the appropriate bomber, select your missions and assemble the appropriate mission deck determined by the respective mission. Missions are flown by drawing and resolving the mission cards. Combat is performed by dice rolling and allocating certain ammunition dice.

But be prepared for the unexpected. The skies are dangerous, and damage to your bomber is inevitable. Can you manage critical systems failures, repair damages, and tend to wounded crew members while keeping your aircraft in the air?
As you embark on each mission, the risks are high, but so are the rewards. Completing precise objectives, minimizing casualties, and returning your crew safely will allow you to bring out the ol' lady for another mission and be one of the best.
Strap in, pilot. It's time to test your skills, courage, and resourcefulness in Through Hell and Back. Take command of your bomber, navigate treacherous skies, and make history with each successful mission.
Good luck, and may the deep blue go easy on you.

-description from designer
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
The holdup was perfect... A sound plan! Millions in a bag, fleeing by plane, yes, really, a sound one. Everyone begins to relax, but suddenly the engine sputters! Then the plane begins to fall... Yes, the holdup was perfect and they had anticipated everything — everything but enough parachutes to go around...

Play your role amongst ten characters in Bahamas and steal, lie, threaten, do everything you can to get the biggest part of the loot and land safely!

In more detail, each player receives a character sheet at random (each with a different special power), along with one starting card, one action card, and two cash cards. Among these starting cards are 2-5 parachutes (depending on the number of players), and you'll want one of these to survive at game's end! Use a number of dice equal to the player count minus one. Choose a starting player at random. This player rolls all the dice, uses one of them (drawing an action or cash card, stealing an action or cash card from an opponent, or using their special power), then they choose the next player who will take a turn. Once all the dice have been used, whoever didn't get to use a die becomes the starting player for the next round.

A "Crash" card is shuffled into the bottom four cards of the action deck, and when it's drawn, everyone without a parachute — or the lifeboat hiding in the action deck — plunges to their death. Whichever surviving character has the most money wins! Alternatively, if one player collects the three different FBI cards prior to the crash, this player manages to land the plane safely, bust everyone else, and win!

This edition includes the base game, a promo card as well as the expansion Bahamas: Trou d'air which added new playable characters, new action cards, a new turbulence module and money bags with holes.

Silk & Venom is a press-your-luck game about spiders building webs, catching bugs, and avoiding the rain. Build your web as big as your dare but be careful -- the rain might come and wash away all your work. Inspired by the Itsy Bitsy Spider, this game is for all ages and includes basic and strategic rules. Advanced rules add set collection and end-game scoring.

On your turn, you can choose to build your web, catch bugs, or collect bugs from your web. If your web gets too wet (either from rain or drops on your web) it'll break. The bugs you collect might have additional effects that will increase points but also add risk. In the end, the player with the most points wins. How much will you risk for the biggest meal?

Silk & Venom is the second game from Binxadinx and the first in a new nature universe of games from them. Game design is by David Bock (Rocket Ranchers) with illustrations by Lianne Pflug.

—description from the designer

Défis Nature - Dinosaur 1 (a.k.a. "Nature Challenge - Dino 1") is a quick and educational nature themed card game.

The goal is to hold all the cards of the game.
for shorter games, players can choose a playing time and the winner is the player with the most cards in hands at the end.

Each card shows a dinosaur with some infos and four or five caracteristics (depending of the edition).
The first player choose a caracteristic (for exemple "weight") and lays a card, in turn each player lays a card and the player with the highest value in this caracteristic win all the cards of this turn and starts a new one.

The cards are classified in 4 "lifespan" categories. 
The 3 shorter lifespan categories allows to change the choosen caracteristic of the turn (in order of priority)

Pieces drawn randomly from a bag react to others you’ve saved as you seek to build a set.

Included in Pyramid Arcade, and also as a PDF on the publisher's website.

In "R-ECO+" you can play two completely different games. One is "R-ECO", a hand management game in which you become a company that collects and separates garbage, collects more garbage than other players, and aims to become a designated company of the city. The other is "R-ECO Recycle", a competition game in which you become the person in charge of a waste sorting plant and aim to become the plant manager by correctly sorting and processing the waste.

"R-ECO" is a game where you skillfully handle the ever-increasing number of cards in your hand and score points. You can score points by putting out cards of the same type together, but it's not that easy. Proactive management is required, as the type of cards played dictates the cards to be withdrawn next. Also, if you exceed the limit of the number of cards in your hand, you will lose points, so you will be worried about playing with a sense of tension from beginning to end.

"R-ECO Recycle" is a bluff game where you put out your hand face up or face down. If you put it in the appropriate area for each type of card, it will be a score card, and if you put it in an inappropriate area, it will be a loss card. The player who gets this score or loss is the last player to put a card in that area. There is less risk if you put out one face up card at a time, but you can't outsmart other players. Sometimes you can get a bigger return by playing multiple cards face down.

boardgame: Magic: The Gathering – Zendikar Rising Commander: Land's Wrath
description:
Stake Your Claim

Choose your commander and overcome both friend and foe in this definitive multiplayer Magic format. Join forces with Obuun and watch the land itself rise to defend your cause. The more lands you play, the more powerful your forces become.

Obuun, Mul Daya Ancestor

An ancient elf spirit of the Mul Daya, Obuun is more influential in death than he ever was in life. A leader among his people, he would do anything to protect them--backed up by a mastery of the forces of nature.

Contents:

100-card deck with 3 new cards
Deck box
10 double-sided token cards

Plus:

Life Wheel
Strategy insert
Reference card

—description from the publisher

A competitive strategy game of sword, sorcery & mutations for 2 to 6 players. Each player controls a Knight, only 1 will survive and win.

The players will move on the map and collect items and abilities that will help them improve their knight and defeat the other players. Not only items such as weapons will be available but also traps, body mutations and "mystical powers" will improve the character to give them an advantage against their opponents.

The turns will take place in an always different fashion so that the order of the moves will be very important in determining who can attack (and kill) first. The players will wage war against each other on a no quarter battle, collecting loot for every kill.

The last man standing is the winner.

Seabound is a 3-6 player casual board game of sea exploration, discovery, and battle that requires wits and the luck of the dice to win. Set sail into unknown waters to discover new lands, recruit daring crew, and amass your fortune.

Seabound is an action economy dice game set on a flower tile hex map. After rolling a d6 dice to determine how many different actions you can take on your turn, you can then spend those on a number of different action types:
1. DISCOVER: The primary mechanic of the game. You can use your actions to roll to discover new lands that you have reached to uncover lost treasure. 
2. ATTACK: You can spend your actions to attack other players which will cause them to be SHIPWRECKED!, leading them to drop their plunder, available for you to scoop up. 
3. MOVE: You can spend your actions to move over water and land, costing different amounts depending on the type of terrain.
4. BUY: You can spend your actions and gold to buy crew, which give you special and unique abilities that can help you with all the different actions you can take.

The goal of the game is to have the most gold and discovery tokens, each worth 1 point, after all the undiscovered tiles have been discovered.

-description from designer

boardgame: World of Tanks Miniatures Game: American – T2E64 Super Pershing
description:
The American T26E4 Super Pershing is a Medium tank wearing Heavy tank clothing! With big Survivability and big Firepower this tank is ready to brawl with the heavies. Attach a full crew with a Mentor Commander and an Improved Rotation Mechanism to increase the Initiative and get a tactical edge.

Wave 10
Contents:
1 Miniature
  ‣ T26E4 Super Pershing
5 Tanks Cards

boardgame: World of Tanks Miniatures Game: British – Churchill I
description:
The British Churchill I is a Heavy tank for the Budget conscious commander. Where it shines is its flexibility with Upgrade Modules - upgrade to the Churchill III turret, swap out for the QF 2-Pdr Mk.X or 75mm Vickers HV gun and you can build this tank to fill whatever need you have.

Wave 10
Contents:
1 Miniature
  ‣ Churchill I
5 Tanks Cards

boardgame: World of Tanks Miniatures Game: German – Nashorn
description:
The German Nashorn continues the trend of big German machinery, but on a budget! With 6 Firepower this is a cheap way to bring some big firepower to the table. A Camouflage Net is a must to add some Survivability while installing the smaller 7.5cm Pak 42 L/70 with a Gun Rammer can provide you with some needed Initiative.

Wave 10
Contents:
1 Miniature
  ‣ Nashorn
5 Tanks Cards

boardgame: World of Tanks Miniatures Game: Soviet – T-54
description:
The Soviet T-54 is an absolute monster of a Medium tank. It's fast, it hits hard and it can soak up a huge amount of damage. Add on a Turbocharger and upgrade to the V-14 engine for an Initiative boost, and don't forget to Fill Tanks With CO2 to avoid taking unnecessary damage. A late war design that never saw initial production until '48.

Wave 10
Contents:
1 Miniature
  ‣ T-54
5 Tanks Cards

The deck consists of 7 animal cards, 7 food cards, and 7 location cards. Each card displays 5 clues about its subject. The picture on each card is repeated on one of 7 "endemic set" tiles. Each such tile displays a picture of each type and can be claimed by the first player to collect all three cards of the set during the game. One card of each type is secretly placed in an envelope, as in Clue. The rest of the cards are dealt.

A die determines which hemisphere a player may visit on his turn. A second die determines whether a player (i) asks another player to read aloud a clue about a specified card type in that other player's hand, (ii) asks another player to give him a specific card, or (iii) names one card of each type and asks another player to give him one of those cards. A location in a request must be the location that the player is visiting.

The game ends when a player correctly guesses the contents of the envelope. Points are scored for ending the game, for collecting the cards that constitute endemic sets, and for optionally reading aloud some clues displayed on cards that one receives during the game. Most points wins.

In the glamourous and competitive world of feline pageantry, only one cat fancier can be the best! Seek out and attain the finest felines, groom them to perfection, and then show them on the catwalk to claim your rightful title as the ultimate cat fancier!

Each round is divided into 4 phases;
1) players will secretly place their time cubes on their time cards to choose their actions and bonuses for the round (time cards represent how the fanciers use their time.)

2) players will adopt cats from the adoption area.

3) players can groom cats, learn cat tricks or upgrade their fanciers' stats.

4) players with groomed cats can show them in pageants (this is how you get points and win)

-description from designer

In Boil + Trouble, you and your friends play as witches, with the goal to snatch up and eat six children before the end of the thirteenth turn. Lure them to your Coven using magic spells and minions.

Children are manipulated along Direct or Twisted paths. Direct paths are clear, but twisted paths turn and cross each other, and you don't always know where a twisted path leads.   "Heroes" are the antagonists of Boil + Trouble, who attempt to rescue children and kill your minions.

Each player has a unique book of spells which can manipulate the board. Players can throw ingredients into a shared cauldron to cast their magic, however, other players use the same cauldron, and the wrong combination of ingredients will limit your spellcasting ability.

-description from designer

In 9th Circle, players assume the role of Demon Lords. Using Minions to take control of various Realms of Hell, these Demon Lords seek to gain favor with Malacoda. The more Favor a player has with Malacoda, the more powerful they will become. When any Demon Lord reaches 25 Favor, Malacoda is pleased, and the one with the most Favor at the end of the round wins the game.

Gameplay is divided into rounds consisting of four phases. The first phase involves sending minions to the board and influencing their movement. In the second phase, realm dominance is determined for each of the 8th Circle realms. The third phase requires players to exercise powers in a clockwise order from Malacoda. Finally, in phase four, the players score and award favor for the 9th Circle sacrifices and 8th Circle shades.

The game concludes at the end of the round when one Player attains at least 25 Favor OR immediately upon Malacoda returning back to the Realm he started in, his patience having run out. The Player with the most Favor wins the game.

-description from publisher

King of Battle is a strategic modern battlefield game for two players. It was designed by a US Artillery Officer, and was made to teach modern battlefield tactics to tabletop game players.

By planning artillery strikes one turn in advance, you will attempt to predict where your opponent will move their pieces, and then deal deadly artillery strike damage to the targeted square. Your tanks and infantry will attack in real-time as you maneuver your pieces to avoid your opponent’s artillery strikes and advance to victory.

Win the game by destroying your enemy or forcing them to surrender. You must surrender if you destroy too many of your own units through poor artillery planning, or if you find your artillery is outnumbered by your opponent's. Artillery is king, and you cannot function without them.

Due to variable terrain placement, every game explodes with new strategies. Guessing where your opponent will move their pieces provides an addictive twist on modern strategy games. The end game never drags; it is designed to provide a quick ending, so that you can start the game again.

Includes four additional magnet trays (expands base game up to 10 players!)

120 even more outrageous and awkward words, from "gnarly" to "bamboozle" to "toast"

198 totally fresh, wacky prompt cards
publishers: Evan and Josh's Very Special Games Company (Very Special Games)

description:
This is a simple race game based on the "newest invention [that] has spread, in a very short time, all over the world." Players roll a die and move flat metal standees of various citizens (a violinist, a maid, etc.) from space to space. The scenes between the spaces depict people around the world, all of them listening to or performing for the radio.

Some spaces allow players to follow radio waves, moving forward or backward as in Chutes and Ladders.

The rules of the game suggest "if you are tired of listening to your radio...the game will give you a nice change."

—user summary

Manipulation rummy with words.

There are 52 cards, 2 for each letter of the alphabet, and a wild Brain Card.  In their turn a player may play words from the 5-card hand, or extend existing words by adding letters to them, or take cards from existing words (as long as they leave a valid word) to make other words.

Points scored are: 
100 for 5 letter word from dealt hand. (All hands are redealt after that
70 for a 6 letter word 
60 for a 5 letter word
10 per letter for words shorter than 5 letters
10 per letter for extending an existing word
50 for using the Brain Card
-10 per letter for an incorrect word

The winner is the first to reach 500.

If a player can make a 5-letter word from their starting hand they score 100 points and new hands are dealt to everyone.

—user summary

boardgame: 広辞苑かるた (Dictionary Karuta: Kojien)
description:
There are countless entries in the dictionary. There are many words that you don't know. Let's find words you haven't seen yet!

One person reads the explanation card aloud, and the other person picks up the correct word card. Whoever gets the most wins.

—description from the publisher

広辞苑に載っている項目は、二十五万。その中には、知らない言葉もたくさんあります。さあ、まだ見ぬ言葉を見つけましょう！

一人が読み札を読み上げ、他の人はそれに合った取り札をいち早く取ります。たくさん取った人の勝ちです。

—description from the publisher (Japanese)

Choose your squad and play head to head with your friends in this football (soccer) based trading card game.

Collect cards and select your squad before choosing your formation. Pick a system that suits your players best. If you have a lot of good defenders then go for a more defensive position.

Once you've chosen your squad plus 3 substitutes, lay your cards face down on your side of the table. Once your cards are set out, you're not allowed to check them again, so choose wisely.

Flip a coin to see who goes first, then the starting player turns a card over and chooses a value (Defense, Attack or Control). Their opponent then chooses a card and says aloud the corresponding value (if the first player chooses Attack then the opposing player chooses Defense etc). Whoever has the highest value scores a goal. If the values are the same then the total value is compared. If the total is still the same then both players discard and nothing happens.

Play continues until all cards have been played and a winner declared.

Revolutionize your tabletop gaming experience with NEO SPARTA - the board game that blends classic mechanics with cutting edge A.I. technology.

The challenge is: Can one artist develop a AAA board game with the help of A.I. and launch it successfully on kickstarter?

NEO SPARTA is a medium complexity premium strategy board game set in a futuristic dystopian world.

It is designed for 3 to 5 players, revolves around combat, deck building, area control mechanics, and supports Augmented Reality.

Players control one of five asymmetric factions and their corresponding leaders:
•    The Cyber Elites led by Neonidas
•    The Mega Corporations led by Gorgo
•    The City-States led by Lysander
•    The A.I. Faction led by Nabis
•    The Black Hand Collective led by Cynisca

Players acquire resources such as Pelanors (currency),      Energy, and Influence by controlling areas on the game map.
These resources are essential for constructing buildings, recruiting and maneuvering units, utilizing card effects, and upgrading research.

The game can be won…
•    … by reaching 80 Victory Points, or
•    … by destroying two opponents' capitals, or
•    … by being the last player remaining on the board.

The game is lost…
•    … if your capital is not constructed before reaching 40 Victory Points, or
•    … if your capital is destroyed, or
•    … if your Pelanor debt exceeds minus 10.

Each regular round consists of five phases, commencing after an initial         event.
•    Drafting Phase: players assemble their decks by selecting cards.
•    Admin Phase: players collect resources, purchase cards, and build structures.
•    Units Phase: players build and move units, as well as play additional cards.
•    Combat Phase: players resolve battles, form alliances, and capture structures.
•    Victory Phase: air units return to their airports, players reveal quest cards, tally Victory Points for area control, retire cards, and repurchase cards.

Additionally, there are two Summit Phases during the game where new laws are introduced.

-description from designer
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
As a faction of Arctic animals take over territories and stockpile food to survive the winter. Fight the cold, avoid humans, and escape the polar bears!

In Sub-Zero, you lead a faction of arctic animals chosen from Walrus, Goose, Musk Ox, Bald Eagle, Arctic Fox, and Lemming.
The first player of the turn rolls a set of dice indicating the actions available for that turn, the players in clockwise order choose which dice to use and remove it from the pool available to the others.
From the season's black dice set, you can then place your animals on the board, collect food from a territory and lower its value, use your faction's special action (each faction is different from the others), spend a point to choose a specific action of the dice
From the blue die of the season, freeze a territory by lowering its value, grow grass on a territory by raising its value, place a human scientific expedition on a territory, collect food, or send a Polar Bear against the animals of the other factions.
The game takes place in 15 rounds divided into 3 seasons: spring, summer, and autumn.
At each change of season, the actions guided by the dice make the game more and more "tiring" for the animals, decreasing the proliferation of animals, reducing the possibility of collecting food, increasing the possibility of the presence of ice, humans and bear attacks Polar

Food points are earned by maintaining a majority in territories, collecting food during the game, and collecting components for game objectives, which are selected at the start of the game.

At the end of the 3 seasons, the player with the most points will have won the game and let his faction's pets get through Sub-Zero's cold winter!

This is a betting game in two parts: a colorful board with circular spaces and a small shaker "card" with three holes. The original version was probably the one themed around horse racing, but there are other variants, as described below.

Players put stakes (colored counters) on one of ten colors on the board - each representing a different horse - to either win or place in the race. Then, the player nominated as Banker shakes up the card, rearranging the colored chits inside. The first-, second-, and third-place horses are revealed in the shaker holes by color, and flipping the shaker over reveals numerical odds against them (also randomized). The Banker then pays out stakes multiplied by the odds, plus bonuses listed on the board.

The game comes in at least four near-identical varieties: the original horse race version with simple "Win/Place" spaces on the board; one themed around baseball, with baseball terms on the spaces (hit, foul, bunt, etc.); one themed around animals, with animal graphics on the colored spaces; and one themed around flowers, with flower graphics on the spaces. Each version includes an appropriate illustration on the box lid/shaker.

—user summary

The uproarious party game where players create zany businesses to solve peculiar problems in niche markets! Ideal for 4-12 players, ages 12+, each game lasts 10 minutes per player.

Creativity is key as players discover obscure market issues in the Market Research phase, devise ingenious businesses to tackle them in the Pitch Phase, and invest in the best pitches in the Investment Phase. The player with the most investment by the end of the game wins!

MARKET RESEARCH PHASE:
Draw 3 Market Research cards, and from those cards choose an interesting prompt and niche.

PITCH PHASE:
Formulate a business that would solve the problem found in the Market Research phase, take up to 3 minutes to think of a pitch, and finished with a 3 minute pitch to sell your sizzle and steak!

INVESTMENT PHASE:
Issue two Greenback cards to your favorite pitch, and one Greenback to the runner-up. The founder with the most investment by the end of 3 rounds winds!

With endless combinations of markets and problems, no two games are alike. So, gather your friends and dive into the wacky world of Niche Pitch, where the most outlandish ideas win big!

—description from the publisher

Chaves: Confusões na Vila, it's a fun card game that features 3 different ways to play, with the same components, all of them inspired by the characteristics of the characters from the most beloved village in the world.

In the first game mode, each player will receive 3 cards each turn from the deck and draft, passing 2 cards to the player on his left. After that, all players will place a card on the left and another card on the right, confronting them with those of the other players. Each card has an icon that shows which card it wins. In each space, it will be checked who won, and the winner receives the card as points. The first to score 25 points wins the round and scores points. At the end of 3 rounds, the player with the most accumulated points is the winner.

In the second game mode, each player receives 7 cards from the deck and wins the round by getting rid of all the cards in the hand. A card from the deck is opened in the center of the play area and, clockwise, players will try to play a card from their hand that has an icon related to the card in the play area. If not, he must draw 1 card from the deck. At the end of 3 rounds, whoever has won the most rounds is the winner.

In the third game mode, each player receives 2 cards from the deck. In each turn, the current player must perform one of the following actions: 1. play and apply the effect of his card; 2. Discard up to 2 cards and draw new cards from the deck. The round continues until one player reaches 25 points, thus winning the round. At the end of 3 rounds, whoever has the most accumulated points is the winner.

—description from the designer

An assortment of 12 Christmas-themed games. Most are party-type games of one sort or other. They are

Christmas Pairs- a basic memory matching game using a deck of cards with Christmas themed illustrations

Festive Guesses- a musical game where players try to guess a Christmas song while someone is humming it on a Kazoo

Santa's Letters- Player try to come up with answers that fit a category, with each round requiring use of a particular letter. Scoring is done for answers that no one else wrote down. Think Just One meets Facts in Five

Merry Quizmas- Trivia game with the twist that the answers are multiple choice and players need to contort their bodies into the letter of their choice. Trivial Pursuit and Twister have a baby

Santa's Sports Day- See how many players complete the 10 dexterity challenges.

Panto or Picture- Players guess what someone is either acting out or drawing. Smash up of charades and Pictionary.

Reindeer Hoopla- Dexterity game where players toss rings at another player who is wearing inflatable antlers

Clutch the Kazoos- Players try to collect seats of cards. When someone does they grab a kazoo from the middle of the table and the other players then also try to grab one. There is one kazoo fewer than players and the player who doesn't have a kazoo loses a "life." Play continues until all but one player have lost 5 lives.

Santa's Hat- Players write the name of 3 people or characters on a piece of paper and put them in Santa's Hat. Players are split into two teams. Each team takes a turn drawing a name and trying to figure out who it is. There are three rounds with rules like Time's Up.

White Lies- Bluffing game using the Christmas Pairs card. Each player in turn will discard cards face down saying what quantity and number they are discarding. The next player either "raise" the discard or calls out the previous player. The player who is wrong has to take the discard pile. the object is to be the first to run out of cards.

Christmas Characters- A 20 questions-type game where someone will be asked yes or no questions about who or what they have chosen.

It's Snow Joke!- Two players sit across from each other and try to keep a straight face while each one reads a joke from a card to the other player.

Players choose a dead poet to resurrect from obscurity by reaching the Center Ring and successfully overcoming the challenge of the Sphinx guarding the exit to the World of the Living.

Players become poets by creating their own poem in progress as they are prompted during game play!!

Prompts can come from great lines of poetry, random internet searches, or even from the last words of your recent text message!
Sabotage is also lurking– you might draw the card that makes your opponent move back 3 spaces or they may draw the prompt that allows them to give you a word you have to insert into your last line– or worse!
The Dead Poets you and your opponents are trying to rescue lived in different time periods and different countries, each with their unique poetry legacy to discover and inspire!

Buy Weapons, Beat Monsters, Earn a Promotion!
Experience the thrill of battling monsters and shopping for office supplies in A.O.L.S., an office life simulator where you and your coworkers must help in the recapture of subjects purposely released as part of an emergency release exercise, all while vying for Workers Points to impress the higher-ups and earn a promotion.

Summary
The game A.O.L.S. (An Office Life Simulator) is a board game where players are employees who are tasked with helping in the recapture of subjects that were purposely released as part of an emergency release exercise by their company's new owner, the Super Cool Place. The game has a cyclic pattern of shop phase, battle phase, and a "coffee break" phase. In the shop phase, workers point coins can be spent in the company shop in exchange for items, equipment, coupons, and even performance bonus cards. In the battle phase, players fight one monster from the monster pile using their equipment and office supplies card. Players will have a certain number of rounds to beat the monster, and the player with the most worked hours this week goes first. The game rewards workers points to those who excel in the assignment and perform the best, with the possibility of a promotion.

Lore
You are an ordinary office worker. The office building next door, dubbed the Super Cool Place, started the day by running a planned emergency release exercise of important monst- er, “Subjects” kept there. As a part of this planned emergency release exercise, the Super Cool Place has temporarily bought out your company and all of its employees. With new management comes a restructuring of the chain of command. Help in the recapture of these subjects to earn Workers Points. Spend these Worker's Points at the company shop to obtain office supplies to help in the recapture of another subject. Sell equipment and items earned in battle to obtain more Workers Points. The employee who impresses management the most after 4 battles will finally earn the promotion of their dreams! (Wins!)

So I asked Chat GTP what the "best" part of this game was...
"As an AI language model, I don't have personal experiences or preferences like humans do, so I cannot determine what the best part of the game is. However, I can tell you that the game seems to have a variety of mechanics, such as collecting cards, equipping equipment, battling monsters, and using office supplies, which could provide players with different ways to strategize and interact with each other. The game also seems to have a currency system, workers points, which can be used to buy items from the shop or gain performance bonus cards, providing players with another layer of decision-making. Ultimately, the best part of the game would depend on the individual player's interests and preferences."

—desription from the designer

boardgame: Fire in the Hole: The Salty Seadog Exxxpansion Pack
description:
The Salty Dog is a set of forfeit tiles that sit in the ship's squares. A forfeit MUST be completed whenever yer cannonball lands in a tiled square.

Includes 2 sets of instructions for a drinking version and a non-boozy version.

The game comes with several 3X3 grids in three levels of difficulty. A number appears in each of the nine spaces of a grid. Each player chooses a grid. Players take turns rolling four d12 PlaySmart Dice (two with 3 through 8 twice, and two with 4 through 9 twice) and then using mathematical operations to combine one or more of the numbers on the dice to equal one of the numbers in the grid. A chip is placed on the grid number, and the first player to complete a pre-selected pattern in the grid with his chips is the winner.

Experience the epic battle in a solo, co-op, or competitive combat adventure with a series of challenges to defeat the dark force that has overtaken the Land of Enchantment. The three strongest wizards have lost their lives fighting for the nation which is now on the edge of survival.

In order to restore peace to the land, the Enchantment council has called upon the six legendary warriors. Choose one of those heroes with unique attributes and special cards with asymmetrical abilities. Construct a deck of spell cards and upgrade your powers with summon creatures and enchantment relics which can be purchased with Fortune gained from winning challenges.

With six different variations, each offering a distinct gameplay experience, you can eliminate players or team up with your opponents. The game provides endless replayability that will keep you coming back for more. It's designed for players of all levels, easy to learn but hard to master.

But that's not all - Realms of Enchantment is paired with a digital platform that lets you dive deeper into the game's universe. Discover character stories, lost letters, diary entries, and more.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Last Full Measure: The Battle of Cedar Mountain
description:
The Battle of Cedar Mountain, August 9, 1862.

Jackson moves against Banks corps of the Union Army of Virginia, waiting for him near Cedar Mountain.

Last Full Measure is a free print-and-play game system of battles of the American Civil War.  Cedar Mountain is the tenth battle in the system.

A brigade scale game that uses regiment counters, as opposed to generic line markers, to spread the brigade over multiple hexes according to its size. Ranged artillery fire, cavalry charges, command and control, all in only eight pages of rules. Play aids include Player-aid card with CRT and other charts and tables; Eliminated Units card, Order of Arrival card, 336 2-sided counters, and a Battle/Scenario booklet.

Cedar Mountain's Battle Book includes two Scenarios; August 9th, the historic set-up of the battle; and An Earlier Start; A set-up earlier in than day than the historic battle, making the battle a meeting engagement and giving player more turns of play.

The map measures 16" x 18" at a scale of 250 yards per 3/4" hex.

Because of it's small map and low counter volume, and it's involving infantry, artillery, and cavalry; LFM:Cedar Mountain make for a good introductory game to the Last Full Measure system, and print-and-play wargaming.

The full game is available for download at: http://UhlanGames.us
as are Vassal modules and ZunTzu gameboxs for all LFM games.

“The ocean animals need our help! In this cute board game, players race to collect plastic waste floating among the waves and take it to the ship, cleaning up the water to protect sea creatures.”
--description from the publisher

In Mission Ocean, each player is working to rescue their selected sea creature from the plastic waste that is encumbering that creature. Players are racing against each other to be the first to remove the 6 pieces of plastic waste from their creature and place the waste into “The Ocean Cleanup” ship. That ship is actually a 3D cardboard ship with a colored waste hold compartment matching the players’ colors and is visually and tactilely engaging for young kids.

In essence, this is a “pick up and deliver” (in this theme “recover and dispose”) game simplified for young kids. It includes customized dice and tries to provide some real world context at an age appropriate level. This game is marketed as an ocean pollution educational game that is a fun way to internalize knowledge of how plastic waste affects sea life and part of the proceeds go to The Ocean Cleanup nonprofit.

-User summary

Air Mail was released as one of six in Milton Bradley's "Radio Series" of inexpensive race games costing 15 cents each. It debuted in the Bradley Games catalog of 1927 and was sold through 1933. A slightly larger but cheaper edition, at 10 cents, appeared in the 1943 catalog.

This is NOT the same as the bagatelle/marble game, The Game of Air Mail, published by Milton Bradley in 1931.

Air Mail is a simple spin and move game in which the object is to get your plane from New York to San Francisco and back again. Along the way, you get points for reaching different stations at major cities (but the race track is in simple columns and no station can be avoided).

—user summary
family: Theme: Mail / Stamps / The Post Office

description:
Welcome to Mindmeld, the party game where great minds think alike.

Mindmeld: The Party Game is a competitive, high-energy party game where you and your partner draw cards with key words to choose from and try to come up with a word that connects these two key words.

The twist?

You have to come up with the same word as your partner to score the most points!

Can you get on the same wavelength as your friends to lock in a victory?

Everyone at the table is playing, both with their partner and with all the other players; so if you don't match with your partner, you can still score points by matching words with another player (but not quite as many points as you would when you match with your partner).

Now for a little more detail: 
1) Everyone has a partner (there is a special rule in case your game group has an odd number of players)
2) taking turns, two partners each draw a Mindmeld card (each card has a set of 5 words to choose from). 
3) They will choose a word from the card and say, "3-2-1!" and shout out the word they chose from the card.
4) All players (including the partners that choose the cards) then write the word that comes to mind that will connect the two shouted words.
5) The partners that drew the cards, as soon as they finish writing their word on their erasable boards say, "3-2-1!" and every player shows their word and shouts it out.
6) Then you score the round- If you and your partner matched words, you each score three points. If you match with anyone else, you and that player score one point.

"That's it?", you ask. You bet! Like we said before, this game is as fun as it is easy to play. Young and old alike, this game scales to every age and type of player you can imagine!

Fast and fun, Mindmeld is a great filler or end-of-the-night game that players can join and leave at anytime.

Mindmeld: The Party Game. Get in sync. Be of one mind. And join the fun!

-description from designer

On your way home from completing a perilous 3-Star Quest, a stranger approaches you with the opportunity to participate in a 4-Star Quest. Having never attempted one before, there is much meticulous planning and harrowing danger to overcome. But the treasure. Oh, the treasure. Its very promise stokes the fire in your questing hearts!

—Description from Publisher

Quests over Coffee: Quest is a Quests Over Coffee side game that has you rolling 16d6 (14d6 is you're playing hard mode). The game has you rearranging those dice in certain orders so you can beat the quest's success criteria's, if you get all of them, you win! You also have abilities which can help you out in many ways, but can also be your poison if used wrong.

Just4Fun, a boy band from the early 2000s, planned to hold a reunion concert after years apart. During one of the rehearsals, Jake Morgan, the band’s frontman, died in a tragic stage accident. A superfan believes that Jake was murdered, and she’s willing to do anything to help you solve this case.

Contains no ciphers! You'll have to rely on your powers of deduction and observation to piece together this gripping murder mystery.

Difficulty: 1/5 - Easy

boardgame: Hunt A Killer: Earth Break – Omega Protocol
description:
Five years after the aliens released a deadly virus, those who are immune have taken up shelter in what was once an underground government facility. Welcome to NACS: North American Communications-facility. You are the most recent and last survivor to join NACS and you are contacted by an anonymous resident to investigate strange events.

Decode ciphers and solve puzzles to understand the true motivations of your fellow residents. Be the main character in a Sci-Fi adventure!

Difficulty: 5/5 - Hard

boardgame: Hunt A Killer: Earth Break – Frequency
description:
Welcome to the surface, survivor, where one wrong decision could mean the difference between life and death. After the events of Omega Protocol, you find yourself guiding a group of rangers through alien-controlled territory. Use your cunning and limited resources to keep your team alive and discover the secret to destroying the invaders.

WARNING: Earth Break: Frequency contains spoilers for the events of Hunt A Killer: Earth Break – Omega Protocol

Difficulty: 4/5

boardgame: Hunt A Killer: Murder On Ice
description:
Murder On Ice is a Membership Exclusive that is only available to Hunt A Killer subscribers.

Figure skater Sofia de la Torre is, you could say, on thin ice. After her coach of over a decade is found murdered, Sofia is the police’s prime suspect.

When Nathan LaPalma is found dead on the ice rink one day, everyone has the same question: why would someone kill a figure skating legend. Nathan, an Olympic-medalist-turned-coach, is famous both in and out of the skating community for his style, talent, and unbeatable coaching. And yet, someone had it out for the skating superstar. Could one of his own proteges be the killer?

You’ve been called in by Nathan’s student Sofia de la Torre to unspin the mystery of her coach’s death. And for good reason. The police are close to pinning Nathan’s murder on Sofia herself, and she’s desperate to find the real killer. Can you break into the competitive, and often secretive, world of figure skating enough to vindicate Sofia and track down the icy murderer?

To catch the killer and bring justice to Nathan and Sofia, you’ll have to:

Prove to the police that Sofia is innocent. You’ll have to prove that she’s innocent before you can move on to finding the real killer!

Unravel whispered secrets that the evasive skaters don’t want you to know.

Track clues and evidence across multiple episode boxes to build a complete picture of the crime.

Puzzle out secret messages, ciphers, and codes to unlock additional clues to the killer’s identity.

Difficulty: 4/5 - Hard

You are racing against the sand timer to build your own crossword. The other players are trying to beat you to itin building their own.

Every round you draw new cards and add them to your crossword.

When you get a letter that doesn't easily fit, you can always pass it to nobble your opponents.

The first player to use up all their cards ends the game and the player with the highest scoring crossword wins!

It’s 1848 and all anyone can talk about are the rumors of gold being found in the Sacramento Valley. Now’s your chance to try to get a piece of the prize, so grab your tools and start digging!

Rumors of Gold is a solo roll & write game set in the California gold rush of 1848. The player aims to get rich by digging for the most valuable resources and panning for gold.

Each turn, the player rolls three dice and uses them to explore a new area. The dice values are also used to build up the value of key resources – copper, silver, and gems – which can maximize the output of each dig. In addition, the player acquires rumors of gold, which can yield valuable riches when exploring the right river areas.

The player uses the dice values, and modifiers gained through the game, to choose the best times and locations to collect resources. When the player can no longer explore a new area, they add up their gold and the value of their completed digs to get their final score.

Board Game of the Month Club postcard game march 2023.

"The sheer horror when you find out Waitrose has just run out of avocados again... or when your wifi's only on 1 bar. These are just some of the terrifying and hilarious situations you will encounter in this card game."

The game contains 400 yellow answer cards and 100 black question cards. Each player is dealt 10 yellowcards. A "Card Master" is selected who picks a balck card and reads the question. Each other player selects one of their yellow cards and gives it face down to the Card Master who shuffles them and reads them out. They then select the they find funniest or most tragic and the player that submitted it takes the black card.

The role of Card Master then passes to the left of the previous one and the process is repeated until a predetermined score is reached or everyon has had enough. The player who has accumulated the most black cards is the winner.

Meet MahJong is an introduction for new players to the tile-based strategy game played all around the world. Learn the history and basic rules of both Chinese MahJong and the American variation. Beginners will build strategy and confidence as they play again and again!

Meet MahJong is designed with new players in mind and is easy to learn and play. Included in the instructions are alternate advanced rules for more of a challenge and recommended resources for learning more about MahJong. Players will build a foundation for learning the full game of MahJong with ease!

—description from the publisher

From the creators of the hit game WordARound comes a magical twist for Disney lovers!

In WordAround, players try to discover and read words written in a circle on round cards before their opponents. The player who guesses it correctly gets the card as a point. The first player to collect 10 cards or the player who has more cards at the end of the game wins.

Disney WordARound is perfect for both fans of WordARound and new players to the game who can enjoy hours of playing with friends and family.  As fast as you can, read the Disney word in the circle and shout it out.  It may not be as easy as you think! Each game card has a fan-favorite character on the back.

The game includes 100 cards with 300 Disney-related words.

—description from the publisher

The Raising Robots: Pets expansion introduces new Pet Robot Cards and animal-related Inventors to enhance the gameplay experience.

Inventors use pets to help manage their growing collection of robots. Generally, Pet Robot Cards allow you to activate other Robot Cards or obtain benefits that may vary based on the placement of the Pet Robot Card in your Play Area.

-description from publisher

“Survive, thrive, dumpster dive.” In a world overrun by humans, you play as one of 12 Cryptid tribes just trying to live the unseen life. Gather resources, evade threats, and gain territory using your abilities as either a Relict (Bigfoot, Jackalopes, Mole People, Yeti, or Skunk Ape), a Supernatural (Chupacabra, Werewolf, or Jersey Devil), or an Extraterrestrial (Aliens, Corn Dwellers, Mothman, or Flatwoods Monster).

With 90 Resource cards and 21 Threat cards, plus picture dice and dragon dice, and a few minor expansions, you can play up to six players for 1-3 hours. Rules include basic and advanced play, which centers around special missions for each cryptid, from the benign (Bigfoot’s ecological mission to protect habitat and wildlife) to the horrible (Mole People’s revenge scheme to blow up the Yellowstone caldera).

Fun stuff: Ally Species—make friends with animals!
Ally Cryptids—NPCs: Wendigo, Uk’tena, Hodag, and Fresno Nightcrawler
Hoax Cryptids: Saucer Drone, Bigfoot Hoax, Huge Owl, Crazed Razorback

Expansions include: Military Ruse, Zombie Outbreak, and Dinosaur Hunt! Also more human threats and more resources!

—description from the designer

The Battle of Waterberg (Ohamakari) is a low to moderate complexity tactical level board-wargame set during the German Herero War of 1905. It covers the German attack against the Hereros in German-occupied Southwest Africa.

August 11, 1904. The Herero forces have gathered near the Waterberg Mountains, far away from the German supply trains. The thick thorn bushes provide a natural defense. The Germans plan to attack from several different sides of the Herero camp to annihilate the Hereros once and for all. The Hereros have other plans: to ambush the Germans and exploit their numerical superiority. The stage is set...

The game takes about 90 minutes to play. One player commands the German forces and the other player commands the various Herero forces. Cards vary the game play.

-description from publisher's website
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players
Series: Battle Series of World Battles (Historical Game Company)

description:
Something has changed in Renature valley: mushrooms are suddenly shooting up everywhere, completely changing the way the game is played! A mushroom allows areas to be connected and enlarged, allowing another player to take over an area!

Transform the polluted valley back into an intact ecosystem, let the animals move in again, and plant adjacent areas to get points. Use the advantages that the mushrooms bring to expand individual areas with their help or join areas occupied by other players. While Renature is challenging, it gets even trickier in Renature Valley.

CONTENTS:

1 double-sided game board
8 Mushroom tokens
2 Cloud tokens

-description from publisher's website

The Battle of Delhi is a low to moderate complexity tactical game set on the siege of Delhi during the Indian Mutiny of 1857. It covers pits the Indian rebels against their British overlords.

1857. The Indian Mutineer forces have assembled at Delhi and have declared a reinstatement of the Mughal Empire. Their forces, which contain trained soldiers and a horde of "irregulars," are hell-bent on full independence. A British army has been sent to crush the rebellion, launch a siege at Delhi, and thereby reinstate their British dominion. The rebels have strongly fortified Delhi and are confident of victory. But will it be enough to withstand the British onslaught? The stage is set...

The game takes about 90 minutes to play. One player commands the Indian Mutineer forces and the other player commands the British forces. Cards vary the game play.

CONTENTS:
    48 game pieces (5/8" inch)
    A single rulebook (4 pages)
    20 game cards
    A canvas game map (11x17" inch)
    1 six sided die

-description from publisher's website
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players
Series: Battle Series of World Battles (Historical Game Company)

description:
Preparing for a trip to the Grand Canyon, the designers wanted games that were light, fun, and travel friendly.  To save weight and space, they put them all in one deck.

The Flex Deck combines: 

     traditional playing cards (suit and rank in top left corner)
     double-9 dominoes (middle of card)
     letter/word games (letter in bottom left corner)


The main goal of Flex Deck is to let the owners play with it any way they want. Their website was a place where people could post rules to games they have invented for the deck.

The deck includes rules to one omnibus game, Pick How You Play. In each round, players play either All Fives Dominoes, or Rummy, and the first to 200 points wins. Between each round, players play a word game where each makes a crossword from their hand of 15 cards, with the player using the most cards choosing whether to play dominoes or rummy for the next round.

—user summary

Four mages face each other on the battleground. They have to learn spells and fight each other. Like every battle royale game, only one can stand in the end. Choose your spells wisely and create your ultimate mage.

Wizards of Valendar is a strategic and tactic game which enforces the players of thinking about every move they will make. The positioning in the battle arena is key to hit your opponents with the right spells at the right time.

Let the battle begin and be the last one standing.

—description from the designer

This intuitively designed deck boasts 4 different game elements on each card face:


     Card Games. The top-left corner has a full standard deck of 52 playing cards + 3 jokers for all of your favorite card games. 
     Letter Games. The center circle has a full alphabet including 3 wild cards, for any word- or letter-based games. 
     Dominoes. The dominoes in the center edge to make a full Double-Nine dominoes stack. Play with only the red and blue cards and the 5-6 and 6-6 jokers to make a full Double-Six stack. 
     Matching Games. The corner shapes -- 14 shapes in 4 colors -- can be used for matching and memory games. 
     Travel Bingo. The deck also comes with 4 double sided cards with a 4x4 array of commonly seen objects for spotting games like Travel Bingo. 


This deck is designed to let you play what you want, when you want. The company's goal was to make the ultimate travel game, so the cards are washable and durable.

Rules for games to play with the deck are at the company's website.

—description from the publisher and the rulebook (Adapted)
family: Versions & Editions: Travel Versions of Non-Travel Games

description:
An un-traditional mashup of traditional role playing and drinking games for any casual get together or rawkus turn-up.
Role play a variety of hilarious space movie's cliche characters: Bounty Hunters, Androids, Aliens, etc.

Complete awkward physical challenges to help your crew or roll a 6-sided die to finish various tasks.
Failing missions results in real consequences and even... death.

Drink until you are far.. far.. gone.
Perfect for novice gamers and has very moderate (but not required) drinking.

—description from the publisher

Delvers of Eldrisar is a deckbuilding game focused on providing a deep strategic experience immersed in beautiful art while maintaining fast-paced and intuitive gameplay.

In Delvers of Eldrisar you take on the role of an ambitious warlock, kept alive long beyond your natural life by the power of your Essence Relics. Your goal is to conquer all of Eldrisar, but to do so you must eliminate any and all potential adversaries.

Each player starts the game with a small deck of 10 cards, representing their current resources in Eldrisar. Players take turns, utilizing their Minions, Treasures and Incantations to build their decks and attack their opponent. The first player to capture all of their opponent’s Essence Relics is the winner!

—description from the designer

Return to the vast Siberian wilderness in this expansion for Stroganov. This expansion consists of 4 different modules. You can combine these at will to create a new gaming experience each time you play the game.  As you cross the Turukhan river and establish new relationships with the local population, you will embark on new adventures that will spice up your game of Stroganov.

Module 1: the Siberian chipmunk introduced a new type of fur tile to collect. this might appear to have no apparent value at first, but a travelling merchant and collector will appear on the map, allowing you to exchange these rare furs for valuable advantages.

Module 2: the Hunting plans introduces a new way to help you fulfil the Tzar’s wishes. As you hunt for specific furs, you can spend hunting marker steps to lower the amount of requested fur tiles on any Tzar’s Wish.

Module 3: New Landscapes adds two new types of landscape tiles to the game. The Characters count as a wild type of landscape tile and give your permanent abilities for the rest of the game once you acquire them. The sacred places introduce a fifth type of landscape and will earn you VP at the end of the game if you meet certain conditions.

Module 4: the Diplomacy track will allow you to trade with the locals by showing signs of good faith. The more generous you are, the more of an advantage you can win over your competitors. Time your actions well and earn many riches and fame.

—description from the publisher

THEIA is a dynamic and engaging board game that brings the excitement of the tactical wargame to life. The game features an assortment of fantasy races, including Humans, Earthbound, Dark Walkers, and Marlock, each with its own unique army and lords. In THEIA, the ultimate goal is to lead your army to victory by vanquishing your opponents' forces on different battlefields.

To achieve victory, you'll need to master a vast array of tactical options. You'll have access to powerful spells from 4 different magical schools, each with its own strengths and specialties. You can also customize your heroes with a wide range of items, from weapons and armor to magical artifacts, to create an unbeatable combination of skills and abilities.

THEIA offers different experiences in one game, making it accessible to players of all levels of experience. At its simplest, THEIA is a basic, card-driven chess-like strategy game that is easy to pick up and play. The game mechanics are straightforward, and the focus is on using your forces to outmaneuver your opponents and gain the upper hand on the battlefield.

For those who crave a greater challenge, THEIA also offers an advanced tactical tabletop experience. This version of the game is more complex and strategic, requiring careful planning and execution to emerge victorious. With the double-sided map tiles, you can craft a unique strategy each time you play. This advanced version of THEIA is sure to test your skills and strategic thinking.

—description from the publisher

The zombie apocalypse has ravaged the world for generations. Survivors like yourself band together, forming settlements to protect against the undead, mutated, and marauding gangs. A horde of zombies has overrun your home, forcing you and your followers to pack up a caravan and race to safety. Lead them through unique landscapes, overcome story-based challenges, manage your resources, and try to find a new place to put down roots before your fuel runs out and the Horde catches up to you.

​Over 100 unique story encounters challenge 1-5 players to work cooperatively in the Wastes. Manage the safety of your survivors and resources while trying to find a new home. The game ends with the foundation of a new settlement on one of 36 terrain tiles. The group scores based on their final location, remaining resources, survivors, and how much of the Horde is still chasing them.

—description from the designer

Yafsiga is a diceless skirmish game for two players designed to utilize a single deck of poker cards to generate thrilling, tactical skirmishes between warbands in the remains of a once-great kingdom. Players will utilize face cards to facilitate activation of their units and use the numbered cards of the chaos deck to cinch the successes they need to triumph over their opponents. A bespoke scenario generator caps off the experience, thrusting both players into the maw of danger as their warband is forced to adapt to the changing landscape of the Bloomsreach.

Each player has the complete freedom to build the warband of their imagination using Yafsiga's roster system. Blending the line between Wargame and Roleplaying Game, players will stat out their profiles with as many bonuses they can afford and weapons they can muster. This is the Bloomsreach, after all. Your warband will need all the help they can get.

After you've played standalone games with friends and foes alike, dive into narrative play with Yafsiga's campaign system, offering points of origin and motives for your warbands with unique reward and injury tables and memorable merchants to visit between skirmishes. Play a simple three-act arc in a day, or prolong your campaigns into a larger narrative with your friends as your warbands grow in renown across the Bloomsreach and beyond.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Let's Go! To Japan: Grab Your Passport
description:
A vacation is more than just the activities you do and the sights you see. It's also about what kind of traveler you are! Are you the type of person who likes to cram as many activities as possible in one day? Are you the adventurous type looking the next thrill? Or are you the discerning type, who knows what they want?

Take on the role of someone like yourself or experience Japan through someone else's eyes with 18 asymmetrical abilities in this expansion for Let's Go! To Japan.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
A Monopoly-like game featuring place and things around Anna Maria Island, Florida USA.

Buy your favorite Anna Maria Island properties, increase your rent by collecting city blocks and trading them in for Keys to the City. Sounds easy? Well add in some traffic jams, property tax, and the IRS and it gets a little more difficult and a lot more fun.

6 unique movers: High Five, Big Smile, Heart, The Dog, Pretzel, and Gym Shoe.
publishers: Late for the Sky Production Company

boardgame: Let's Go! To Japan: Day Trip
description:
Let's Go! To Japan: Day Trip allows players to visit cities one of 10 other cities in Japan outside of Tokyo and Kyoto. Will you eat amazing street food in Osaka, visit the castle ruins of Fukuoka, or will you feed the bowing deer of Nara?

The Day Trip expansion adds 12 Day Trip cards which give players additional goals allowing for even more strategic decisions and adds Sunday to the game. Players can freely move Sunday between the start and end of their vacation.

It’s difficult to see all the cities you want to go to in a single trip. Sometimes taking a day trip can be an amazing way to see a nearby city without complicating your itinerary. Let’s Go! To Japan: Day Trip allows players to visit one of 10 other cities in Japan. Will you eat amazing street food in Osaka, visit the Castle ruins of Fukuoka, or will you feed the bowing deer of Nara?


-description from designer
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
The Cornucopia expansion contains new components that you can include in your games of Honey Buzz: Fall Flavors, in any combination. Some introduce new rules that alter gameplay, while others increase the variety that the game has to offer.

3 new Fruit Order cards let you sell to animals known for being not-so-picky eaters (goat, pig, and dog). These cards feature wild symbols which can be satisfied by any honey or fruit.

4 new Foliage Cards reference the adjacency of leaves in your hive, putting an emphasis on placement in addition to set collection.

8 new Toast Cards for the Harvest Festival module give you scoring options related to actions taken on your turn or related to the other expansion modules.

30 new Bonus Tokens can be placed on the Harvest Festival board, making the reward for retired workers even more bountiful.

Finally, 2 new Queen's Contests for solo play reference sold or unsold fruit, and a new solo die can be used alongside the solo dice from the Honey Pot mini-expansion to make dice-based solo play interact with all of the new modules in Honey Buzz: Fall Flavors.

—description from designer
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

boardgame: The Hunt for the Dragon Throne
description:
Up to 4 intrepid adventurers set off, each on their own individual quest to ascend to the dragon throne and win the game.  Players roll a die to (a) select their uniquely-powered character, then (b) move along the 109 numbered spaces on the winding board, and (c) resolve the effects of the various encounters described on the board spaces.  Finally, both (d) the combat system (between players & against the creatures on the board) and (e) actually moving onto the winning space at the end of the trail are both resolved through rolling the same die.

The Candyland-style board experience has several unique features.
The first 22 spaces are inside the castle, where you are in the "safe zone".  But even there you are not safe, due to things like space #3 ("Encounter a bandit, you lose the battle return to start") and #7 ("Encounter a tornado Go back 2 blocks") and #12 ("Feel unwell, take a rest and skip a turn").  Finally, right before you exit the castle, you may get lucky and land on #20 which says: "Spin the wheel to draw a weapon".  This spinner has 16 spaces which award adventurers with a free weapon, such as an "Algae Machete" or a "Fire Whip" or a "Sword of Ice"...unless you get 1 of the 4 "Better luck next time!" spaces.
Space #29 is a "Detour" right before the encounter with the Fire Dragon.  The detour all takes place within space #29, but it is a 10-space run around the terminates right at the Fire Dragon encounter space anyway—so, no avoiding this fight!
Lastly, space #109 is where the namesake 5-piece plastic dragon throne sits.  Players will lose their turns until they manage to roll the exact number necessary to reach this space.

Beyond the castle walls, combat becomes part of the game.  Combat losses force players to lose HP (which are represented by AWESOME wooden tokens), and when any player is reduced to 0 HP he must retreat back to space #22—with 109 spaces in all, and no control over where you land on a turn, this is a LONG way to fall!  You will have access to item cards, but your choices are limited here, because you can hold only 1 card at a time—the one you have may not be what you need most.

Other spaces offer opportunities to earn coins from die rolls, or sacrifice your next turn to acquire coins or HP.  Plot cards are encountered every few spaces, and if you land on them, they add both positive & negative effects (along with some thematic flavor text) just like you'd find in Monopoly's "Chance" or "Community Chest" decks.

"Pocket Ungame - All Ages Version" is another expansion for "The Ungame" that can be used as part of the original board game or by itself as a standalone talking game. Like other Ungame expansions, this one comes with 2 decks. Deck one contains light questions meant as ice breakers and deck 2 contains questions meant to spark deeper conversations.

In "Pocket Ungame - All Ages Version", players are encouraged to share their opinions, feelings, and beliefs through the asking of simple questions. Questions come from card prompts & encourage storytelling. Ideal as an ice-breaker as it is fundamentally non-competitive.

Contains 140 cards.
family: Admin: Outside the Scope of BGG

description:
"Pocket Ungame - Singles Version" is another expansion for "The Ungame" that can be used as part of the original board game or by itself as a standalone talking game. Like other Ungame expansions, this one comes with 2 decks. Deck one contains light questions meant as ice breakers and deck 2 contains questions meant to spark deeper conversations.

In "Pocket Ungame - Singles Version", players are encouraged to share their opinions, feelings, and beliefs through the asking of simple questions. Questions come from card prompts & encourage storytelling. Ideal as an ice-breaker as it is fundamentally non-competitive.

Contains 140 cards.
family: Admin: Outside the Scope of BGG

description:
Each round, the active player expresses their opinion on a wide variety of topics – movies, music, celebrities, politics, etc. Once they are done, play continues around the table with each player choosing to either draw a card and pass to the next player OR to pick up the gauntlet and deliver their own red-hot Rant on the topic!

Once both players have delivered their impassioned opinion on the subject, the rest of the players vote on which Rant was the best.

The RANTMASTER who delivered the more popular Rant receives a point, and any players who voted for the more popular Rant also earn points. The first player to earn 7 points is the WINNER!

-description from publisher

boardgame: Mind Reader: The Psychic Party Game
description:
A pair of players are asked the same 3 questions and answer them independently, hoping to match as many as possible. 1 match = 1 point, 2 matches = 3 points, 3 matches = 6 points. The first player to collect 13 points WINS!

Players may either select the category they want to answer questions from [and the other players will pick their partner], or they may pick their partner for the round [and the other players pick which category they answer].

-description from publisher

boardgame: UNO Ultimate Marvel: Add-on Pack – Ant-Man
description:
Ant-Man
Tiny but Mighty, Ant-Man not only can control an army of ants, but also shrink and grow in size. When Ant-Man is in a pinch, he can shrink to avoid danger or grow to absorb any damage he might take. His cards reflect this by using the famous Pym Particles he is known for. Ant-Man starts the game with Pym Particles that all have different effects. However, you need to use them in the right situation as his character power also uses the Pym Particles. Navigate the game by making the right choices when to use the Pym Particles and when not to.

Included in this add-on pack:

     One Uno Ultimate Ant-Man Deck
     Character specific Wild Cards
     Danger Deck Cards with Villains and more!
     2 Chase Foil Cards

boardgame: UNO Ultimate Marvel: Add-on Pack – Loki
description:
Loki
Glorious! Loki, the God of Mischief, is always up to no good. There is one thing that he strives for and that is to cause as much chaos as possible. Always makes sure you are paying attention to when a Danger Icon Card is played to get the most out of Loki's character power. Use his Simple Illusion Wild Card to copy another powerful Wild Card or utilize the Your Savior is Here Wild Card by using information you gather from other players against them.

Included in this add-on pack:

     One Uno Ultimate Loki Deck
     Character specific Wild Cards
     Danger Deck Cards with Villains and more!
     2 Chase Foil Cards

A card game / parlor entertainment based on the concept we know today as Mad Libs. One player, designated the "reader," reads the provided comical story with several missing phrases. The other players, among whom small cards with non-sequitur phrases have been distributed, are called upon to fill in the blanks with the phrases in their hand: "a salt mackerel," "a box of gum drops," "a long-eared donkey," etc.

The game directions do not state how the winner is determined. However, in other very similar Parker games of this kind (i.e. "How Silas Popped the Question"), the winner is the player who laughs the loudest.

The game debuted in the 1929 Parker Games catalog and seems to have been discontinued by the next edition.

—user summary

Pixel tanks are back! Do you remember how as a child you spent hours on end at an eight-bit console?
One of the most popular video games was "Tanchiki".
This board game will bring you back to the atmosphere of those hot battles.

Each turn you roll the dice and get a set of actions-maneuvers. For example: take a bonus, move your tank or shoot someone else's tank. Your goal is to destroy the opponent's headquarters or knock out his tanks until he runs out of lives.
Ten new Switzerland themed cards for Codenames.

Wortwiesel is a speed word-building game (German language). Each card has 2 letters. There are three types of cards: Cards with 2 consonants, cards with 2 vowels, and cards with 1 consonant and 1 vowel.

Two things are special:
- The cards can be placed next to each other or partially overlapping, so that one letter is covered. Just by smart placement of your cards you can cover and replace letters with those of another card. The combinatorics of just a few cards are breathtaking, and it is this puzzle effect which creates most of the fun. 
- The sand timer does NOT define an upper time limit for finishing an action. Instead, the timer defines lower(!) time limit i.e. the period before the next drawing of cards is triggered. It is merely an option: You do not have to trigger drawing immediately once the timer is finished! Instead, it is perfectly fine to take it easy and to grant yourselves more time before taking the next card.

The game starts by flipping the sand timer. Players race to build words out of their cards, using as many letters as possible. Individual letters which are in the way must be covered by other cards, but no card may be covered completely. 
Each player has four tiles with which to cover letters at the end or at the beginning of a word, and one wild card tile which can be used for any letter. These tiles can only be used once in the game.
When the sand timer runs out, any player can announce that they are drawing an additional card. All other players must then also draw another card. After a player shouts “stop”, the sand timer is started one last time. The other players may try to improve their word in that time. The round ends when the timer runs through again. Players mark their words on a score sheet – the longer a word is, the more points are awarded. 
Play three rounds. Deduct points for unused cards, get bonus points for unused tiles. The player with the highest combined score wins.

There's a significant learning curve involved: The more experienced you get the faster you will play. That's when the sand timer kicks in and becomes a tactical element: You can decide whether to rush to a solution or to trigger the next drawing right before your counterpart(s) can finish and conclude the round.

NOTE: The present version Wortwiesel (2023) is designed for German language. Based on the combination of letters and letter frequencies, it ist not directly playable in most other languages. 
NOTE by Author: Other language versions are in preparation

Are you having a party and looking for a fast, fun and clever cooperative game? Play Mind Match to test how well your mind matches with the minds of your friends! Within 5 minutes, solve the puzzles together using surprising and amusing means of communication. Three different mini-games test your communicative skills and intellectual bonding whilst you’re having a good time!

Mind Match is a collection of dynamic cooperative games with many puzzles you need to solve together before the time runs out. Each puzzle consists of 2 types cards ― each card stands for a part of the puzzle: a Question and an Answer. You have to find a player with the other half of your puzzle and solve it together! The more pairs of cards you match before time runs out, the more victory points you receive. Are you ready to make a mind match?

In Mind Match-1 you’ll find 3 mini-games: Icons, Emojis and Pattern Lock.

You can combine Mind Match-1 and Mind Match-2 to have more puzzles and fun!

—description from the publisher

Are you having a party and looking for a fast, fun and clever cooperative game? Play Mind Match to test how well your mind matches with the minds of your friends! Within 5 minutes, solve the puzzles together using surprising and amusing means of communication. Three different mini-games test your communicative skills and intellectual bonding whilst you’re having a good time!

Mind Match is a collection of dynamic cooperative games with many puzzles you need to solve together before the time runs out. Each puzzle consists of 2 types cards ― each card stands for a part of the puzzle: a Question and an Answer. You have to find a player with the other half of your puzzle and solve it together! The more pairs of cards you match before time runs out, the more victory points you receive. Are you ready to make a mind match?

Mind Match-2 includes 3 mini-games: Labyrinth, Tetris and Cars. 
You can find more mini-games in Mind Match-1.

You can combine Mind Match-1 and Mind Match-2 to have more puzzles and fun!

—description from the publisher

This is a very simple spinner-based game that teaches children basic change-making skills.

Each player gets a card showing a piggy bank with a different colored background and a handful of realistic metal coins in different denominations (five nickels, three dimes, and three quarters). Every turn, the spinner will tell a player whether to put 5, 10, 15, 20, or 25 cents in the piggy bank, which may require them to make change with the bank. The first player to put all their money in the piggy bank first wins.

—user summary

Many small animals are looking for a new owner: a kitten, a puppy, a guinea pig, even a chameleon! They need care and love, but unfortunately, there is not enough space in the shelter yet. Luckily, Lili and her friends - the players - are there to help! They're taking a few into their own home, and will look after them for a while until they find the best, loving owners!

Each player can collect care tokens (such as bathing, feeding, and playing) on their own game board, creating the perfect temporary home for one or two of the animals. But the game doesn't end there - you'll also have to find the best real owners for your pets! Once you've done that, a space on the board will open up for you to temporarily adopt another pet. Players earn points for the animals they cared for and delivered to their future owners. The player with the most points win.

As well as being endlessly cute and teaching responsibility in a subtle way, this game is also highly recommended because after two rounds your child won't be demanding that you take all the animals home from the shelter. This game shows that different animals have different needs and not all pets fit the same owner.
