Plus, Gondos Gazdik has great scalability! You can play cooperatively with younger children, helping each other and "brainstorming" together, and after a bit of practice, you can play competitively! Who will help the most pets find a home?


     * *


Sok kis állat keres gazdit: a cica, a kutyus, a tengerimalac, sőt, még egy kaméleon is! Gondoskodásra és szeretetre van szükségük, de sajnos a menhelyen már kevés a hely. Szerencsére Lili és barátai - vagyis a játékosok - segítenek rajtuk! Párat befogadnak a saját házukba, és egy ideig ők vigyáznak rájuk, míg meg nem találják számukra a legjobb gazdit!

Minden játékos a saját játéktáblájára gyűjthet gondoskodás-korongokat (például fürdetést, etetést, játszást), így teremtve meg a tökéletes átmeneti otthont egyik-másik állatkának. A játék azonban itt még nem ér véget, ugyanis a legmegfelelőbb igazi gazdit is meg kell találni a kiskedvencek számára! Ha ez sikerült, felszabadul egy hely a táblátokon, hogy újabb állatot fogadhasatok be átmenetileg. Minden ellátott állatka után pontokat kaptok, a legtöbb pontot gyűjtő játékos nyer!

Amellett, hogy ez a játék végtelenül aranyos és észrevétlenül tanít meg a felelősségvállalásra, azért is ajánljuk jó szívvel, mert nem fogja azt követelni két kör után a gyerkőc, hogy minden állatot vigyetek haza a menhelyről. A társas rámutat, hogy különböző állatoknak különböző igényeik vannak, és hogy nem minden kisállatnak ugyanaz a gazdi a legmegfelelőbb.

Ráadásul a Gondos Gazdik remekül skálázható! Kisebbekkel játszhatjátok kooperatív játékmódban egymást segítve és közösen ötletelve, egy kis gyakorlás után pedig mehet a versengő játékmód! Vajon ki segít a legtöbb kiskedvencnek otthonra találni?

-description from designer

Secret Number is a deduction game from Japan.

The publisher says:
Players are dealt two number cards each, and the object of the game is to reveal one of the two number cards not dealt and guess the number of the one not revealed. Players add, subtract, multiply or divide the two numbers dealt to them and reveal the result of their calculation. The game is a game of calculation and deduction in which players try to guess a hidden number based on the results of calculations revealed by other players.

プレイヤーには2枚ずつ数字カードを配り、配られなかった２枚の数字カードのうち１枚を公開し、公開されていない１枚の数字を当てることがゲームの目的です。プレイヤーは配られた２つの数字に足し算・引き算・掛け算・割り算のいずれかを行いその計算結果を公開します。他のプレイヤーが公開した計算結果をもとに伏せられた数字を推理する計算・推理ゲームです。
publisher's summary
publishers: 新ボードゲーム党 (New Board Game Party)

description:
Xi (“Iguana” in Chinese), is a much-loved pet of Lady Rose, the leader of the Red Dragons triad. Her fearsome cyber-jaw (filled with nano-venom) and powerful tail will easily incapacitate all enemies. She is a valuable addition to both the Red Dragons and Animals factions in the fight to survive San Lazaro!

—description from the publisher

This cross-faction expansion adds 1 new character who can play for both the Survivalists and Peacemakers factions from the Core Game.

Maria “Ranger” Gutierrez-Rivera is a Supervising Ranger in the Big Trees National Park. She is a crossbow expert and her unique fire/stun and healing abilities will help the Peacemaker and Survivalists factions better survive the CyMS onslaught!

It also includes a new scenario and 2 new forage cards.

—description from the publisher

Sixty-four square tiles display eight shapes, eight tiles for each shape. Four tiles are dealt to each player, and one tile is turned face-up. On your turn, add one or more tiles to a line of face-up tiles so that either all the shapes in the line are the same or all are different. Score one point for each tile in a line of different tiles, and two points per tile in a line of identical tiles. Double your score for a line of eight tiles. Replenish to four tiles at the end of your turn. Play until the tiles run out. Most points wins.

Players take turns rolling two dice and racing their vehicles around a 3-D track. The speed die dictates how many spaces a vehicle may move; the action die permits players to move obstacles or change lanes. Certain spaces on the track generate crystals that can be used to overcome obstacles or take short cuts or move faster. The first player to complete two laps wins.

boardgame: Last Full Measure: The Battle of Trevilians Station
description:
Phillip Sheridan, on a raid with the Army of the Potomac's cavalry corps to cut the supply lines to Richmond meets with the Army of Northern Virginia's cavalry corps under Wade Hampton at Trevilians Station.

Last Full Measure is a free print-and-play game system of battles of the American Civil War.  Trevilians Station is the eleventh battle in the system.

A brigade scale game that uses regiment counters, as opposed to generic line markers, to spread the brigade over multiple hexes according to its size. Ranged artillery fire, cavalry charges, command and control, all in only eight pages of rules. Play aids include Player-aid card with CRT and other charts and tables; Eliminated Units card, Order of Arrival card, 256 2-sided counters, and a Battle/Scenario booklet.

Trevilians Station's Battle Book includes two Scenarios; June 11th, the first day of the battle and the start of the campaign game; and June 12th, the second day of the battle.

The map measures 28" x 24" at a scale of 250 yards per 3/4" hex.

—description from the designer

Players take turns rolling dice and placing tiles of their color onto one of the nine squares that comprise a Minor Board. The nine Minor Boards are 3X3 grids that get added one at a time to cover one of the nine squares of a much larger, 3X3 Major Board. A die roll determines which of three symbols the played tile must display. Players score for winning a Minor Board (3 symbols on the board in one of many designated configurations, not merely 3-in-a-row), winning the Under (three Minor Boards of one player's color in a row), and winning the Over (3 won Minor Boards in a row). Play to 18 points or until all Minor Boards are won, in which case most points wins.

—user summary

boardgame: Sadie Cat and Mao: Robot Gophers
description:
Sadie Cat and Mao have stumbled upon a bad robot gopher situation! With your awesome memory skills, can you help the goblin capture all 3 before they completely ruin his yard?

Sadie Cat and Mao: Robot Gophers is a dice rolling and memory inspired game. Players will place 9 cards numbered from 1 - 9 face down in a 3x3 grid. Then they will roll 2d6, which correspond to different spots on the game board. It is then up to the player to find and remember which face down card is which number. Match this three times in a row and you win!

—description from the Publisher

This is an unusual game from Parker Brothers that involved a detailed map of the United States as the game board, lined with letters of the alphabet that were used to make radio call signs.

The game is supposed to begin in the players' home city, at their local station, and a spinner is used to contact other stations. That sounds like a normal race game, and apparently the player who achieves the greatest combined distance wins. However, the game also involves "special pins" and a "measuring device" which are not obvious at all. Multiple copies of the game board have been found with pinholes across their surface.

The game appears as "new" in the 1927 Parker Games catalog, but it has disappeared by 1929.

—user summary

In a sub-light combat duel, out-thinking, and out maneuvering your opponent is the task of starship command. Between attack, defense, and movement, careful power management is essential. Your decisions as the starship Captain determine whether the crew lives or perish. Will your race emerge more powerful or vulnerable at the conclusion of the battle? Come and find out….
Empire Star Battles attempts  to capture the danger and excitement of starship battles in our own galaxy. Skills, risk, and luck are tested as two or more players manage their starships in ever-changing conditions. Unique advanced movement, energy management, and record keeping make Empire Star Battles the game you hoped for in interstellar space combat.

—description from the designer

Look no further! You've just found Gentleman's Cards - full of uncompromising black humour, a best-selling card game for adults who know where the boundaries are in jokes and love to break them.

The rules are simple. You have two suits of cards. Black has questions, white has answers. One person puts a black one on the table, the others answer it with white. The owner of black decides which answer is the funniest and gives his card as a point. The next person plays a black card, becoming a judge, and you go on. Whoever scores the most points wins.

The game already has 6 "episodes" that can (and should) be freely combined.

That's 243,968 combinations! It doesn't matter how often you play

—description from the publisher (translated)

Nie szukaj dalej! Właśnie znalazłeś Karty Dżentelmenów – pełną bezkompromisowego, czarnego humoru, bestsellerową grę karcianą dla dorosłych, którzy wiedzą, gdzie w żartach są granice i kochają je łamać.

Zasady są proste. Masz dwa kolory kart. Na czarnych są pytania, na białych odpowiedzi. Jedna osoba wykłada na stół czarną, pozostałe odpowiadają na nią za pomocą białych. Właściciel czarnej decyduje, która odpowiedź jest najśmieszniejsza i wręcza swoją kartę jako punkt. Kolejna osoba zagrywa czarną kartę stając się sędzią i jedziecie dalej. Wygrywa ten, kto zdobędzie najwięcej punktów.

Gra ma już 6 “epizodów”, które można (i należy) dowolnie łączyć.

To aż 243968 kombinacji! Nie ważne, jak często grasz.

—description from the publisher

The game can be played competitively or cooperatively. Word cards are laid out in a 4X4 grid in Codenames fashion. Challenge cards come in six types. A player gives clues to his teammates to try to complete a challenge.

Examples of challenges are: use a designated word in the grid in a sentence that describes a targeted word on the challenge card; use an internet meme or emoji to describe a word in the grid; use a simile or metaphor to identify a word in the grid; and draw a sketch or act out a scene that will describe a word in the grid. Discard used grid words and replenish the grid.

Once a team has completed 4,5, or 6 challenge types (depending upon how difficult the players want the game to be), that team attempts a Final Word Duel in which one of the discarded grid words is secretly chosen, and three clues, based on a die roll, are given about the word. The clues will be to state a synonym or antonym of the word or to designate the position of a vowel or consonant in the word. The first team to succeed in the Final Word Duel wins.

In The Witch Pot players are kids trying to cast out the evil witch that has cursed their forest and village. They gather crystals, pumpkins, fireflies and mushrooms to brew potions while trying to avoid curses. Precision is essential in finding the perfect combination of potions that banish the witch and make you the village hero. Fast paced action with a quick learning curve makes The Witch Pot accesible and replayable while offering enough depth for skill development.

The game develops over a series of turns in which players throw rings trying to capture specific or random poles in order to collect ingredient cards or color tokens. Using these resources they are able to brew potions at the end of each turn. The first player to brew 3 potions is the village hero.

The competition element of the game is found in the fact that only the topmost ring around each pole is considered as the winning ring. Therefore, players have to make constant tactical choices.

Players can also try to capture the black pole in the middle of the game board. In doing so, they are able to steal other players color tokens and use them to their own advantage.

Ready, set, throw...

Players take turns displaying a card that depicts a work of art. A player chooses one of four words that he thinks best describes the work. The other players guess which of the four words he chose. Match half or more of the other players and put the card in the WIN pile; otherwise, put it in the LOSE pile. Players collectively try to win more often than they lose. Play a chosen number of rounds.

boardgame: Spellbound The New Word Spelling Game
description:
Spellbound The New Word Spelling Game is an exciting word spelling game for word game enthusiasts. There are 86 cards in a deck. 5 vowels each, 3 consonants with the exception of Q and X, which are 2 each. Each card has 1-10 points, with no two copies of the same letter having the same points.

The object is to spell words by building a word one letter at a time. The strategy is to see a word before your opponents do. Each player has options to add a letter, replace a letter, or discard and draw, to set up to spell or complete a word for the win. There is an opening bonus round just to put points on the board. Spellbound The New Word Spelling Game is more of a leisure game. If you have a strong vocabulary this is the game for you. If you like a challenge play Spellbound The New Word Spelling Game!

This game is actually 30 years from creation to completion and packaging. It started with 2 friends kicking ideas around back in 1992. Then we designed it. One friend printed a prototype. The other friend took the idea and made it a dream come true when the prototype was sent for manufacturing. The game was printed and packaged in 2014 for sale. Since then it’s been marketing and playing the game with many friends.

—description from the publisher (adapted)

Sunset Limited was released as one of six in Milton Bradley's "Radio Series" of inexpensive race games costing 15 cents each. It debuted in the Bradley Games catalog of 1927 and was sold through 1933. A slightly larger but cheaper edition, at 10 cents, appeared in the 1943 catalog.

This is a simple spin and move game in which a player wins by making a round trip, either to or from the "Golden Gate" at the center of the board and back again. The directions start with the following missive:

"Every boy likes trains and engines and hopes some day to have an opportunity to ride in the cab, and perhaps pull the throttle of the great locomotive. In this game of Sunset Limited, each player represents an engineer striving to bring his train safely and "on time" to San Francisco, the City of the Golden Gate."

It is suggested that if two players play, they may each wish to play with two "trains" (counters). If so, they only win by completing the round trip with both trains.

—user summary

Radio was released as one of six in Milton Bradley's "Radio Series" of inexpensive race games costing 15 cents each. It debuted in the Bradley Games catalog of 1927 and was sold through 1933. A slightly larger but cheaper edition, at 10 cents, appeared in the 1943 catalog.

This is NOT the same game as the slightly more complex/expensive "Radio Game" published by Milton Bradley in 1925.

This is a simple spin and move game in which players represent radio announcers moving from station to station across the USA. There is a "blue line" stretching from Bangor, Maine to San Francisco, California, which is the route followed by the first/third player(s). There is also a "red line" stretching from Miami, Florida to San Francisco, which is the route followed by the second/fourth player(s).

—user summary

Roll Builder is a roll-and-write game of city building, discovery, and legacy.

Players take on the mantle of leadership over historical cities, presented through individual game sheets, while the players themselves only need to have pencils and two dice.

Each turn, the dice are rolled to provide strength to actions, including building networks of roads, placing buildings, and constructing great wonders of the world. Managing resources and forwarding each society through sheet-specific innovations give different paths to victory, whether each player plays the same sheet, or competes using different ones that have been carefully balanced.

Either local or online, alone or with a crowd, history awaits those who dare to build a legacy that will stand the test of time.
Roll Builder is a "living game", where additional cities and other content will be released for free, usually through the feedback and input of the community around it.

-description from publisher
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

boardgame: Shadows of Brimstone: Gates of Valhalla – Map Tile Pack
description:
Shadows of Brimstone: Gates of Valhalla – Map Tile Pack contains, as you might expect, extra map tiles, cards, and missions for Gates of Valhalla.

Set of 8 Map Tiles (including 5 Rooms and 3 Passages) with Norse Lands on the Front and the Targa Plateau on the back. Includes 2 exclusive missions and required tokens, as well as 30 cards.

Map Tile Pack includes:

5x Double-sided Room TIles
  3x Double-sided Passage Tiles
  7x End Caps/Gate End Caps
  6x Map Tile Extender Pieces
  Assorted tokens and counters (including 6 new objective/clue tokens)
  Instruction Booklet with 2x New Missions
  8x Norse Lands Map Cards
  8x Targa Plateau Map Cards
  5x Norse Lands Encounter Cards
  5x Targa Plateau Encounter Cards
  4x Darkness Cards

Sins of Our Fathers is a may-follow trick taker for 3 players that features a hex board and variable end game conditions.

The game is played over the course of a single hand which consists of 13 cards per player.  Every trick will result in special actions awarded to players based on their position within a trick.

Players will manipulate an ever dwindling water supply to score points during the course of the game.

—description from the designer

This is a promo Bitoku (Meijo) card.After moving player's marker on the promo card at the Bitoku Path, you could get an Iwakura stone and place it in the Iwakura Rock area of your player board or place a pilgrim with it its sleeping side face up on any empty space on the Rock Path of your player board that is next to at least one Iwakura Rock(the Pilgrim cannot be used again).

Relive the fame and take things over in a special edition of the fast dealing, property trading game that pays homage to the Thin White Duke! MONOPOLY: David Bowie gathers the ultimate collection of the rock superstar’s greatest records for fans to buy, sell, and trade albums such as The Rise and Fall of Ziggy Stardust and The Spiders from Mars. Build Arenas and Stadiums atop Bowie’s discography with six custom sculpted tokens representing memorable studio albums: Major Tom, Black Star, Black Tie White Noise, Aladdin Sane, Diamond Dogs, and Ashes to Ashes. Sound and Vision cards will take you on the musical journey to be the furthest above the moon with any money to win!

—description from the publisher

Key Enigma presents an escape box that mixes puzzles, narrative and technology to immerse you in a challenging experience so carefully designed that it will be impossible to distinguish reality from fiction.

In this experience, you will investigate a mysterious circus that worships an ancient and unknown deity. You will focus on discovering the strange disappearances that have taken place there. Do you dare to face your fears and the unknown?

—description from publisher

Monkey Life is a fun friendly game made for families, kids and groups ages 7 and up. Experience time in the jungle as a monkey, encountering predators, who will try and steal your bananas, and poachers, who will try and trap you. They are both trying to prevent you from reaching your goal to get 12 Monkey Points. Be the first Monkey to climb to the tree of life, and become King of the Jungle!
 
Standard & Teams - Play Modes:
Monkey Life is a competitive card game for 2-6 players. In the "Standard" game, each player plays against each other. If you have 4 or 6 players, you may play in "Teams" mode. Either 2 players against 2, 3 against 3, or 3 Teams of 2 players each.
 
Magnetic 3D Monkey Pawns & Board:
To begin a game, each player will pick one of the six colored Monkey Pawns and place it on the Scoreboard, at the bottom of the tree. 
 
 
Quick to Learn:
The youngest player randomly selects one of the "Top Banana" cards, to reveal the strongest Banana Color for that Match.

Each player will collect the number of cards indicated in the "Setup" section of the game manual, and then the game will start.

Monkey Life is a strategy card game consisting of Rounds and Matches

The Round will start with the youngest player playing a card from their hand and placing it face up on the table. Play continues in a clockwise direction, with each player playing a card. The Round ends after all of the players have completed their turn.

At the end of each Round, the winner collects the cards. Winning strategies and rules are illustrated in the game manual.

A new Round begins with all players replenishing their cards, and the player that won the previous round will play first.

The game will continue until there are no more cards left in the draw pile, and players have no more cards remaining in their hand. That will end the Match.
 
 
How to Win:
At the end of each Match, players will count their points, and move their Monkey Pawns up the tree, to mark their total number of Points.

The first player to gain 12 Monkey Points, over a number of matches, will win the game and be crowned King of the Jungle.

—description from the designer

2-5 players play as captains who try to accumulate wealth and avoid losing their ships over sea and land.

The players use gold coins to buy crew and resources that allow them to sail the high seas where they encounter adventures and hazards marked on the map, while also fight or trade with other players.

Players can lose up to 3 ships and the game end when only one captain survived with his ship,

During game play each player in thier turn at sea place a compas to indicate direction of travel and roll 2 dices one indicate speed of travel and the other the sea conditions. Based on the results the player move thidr boat over the hexagon map.

If players ship land over a "JOLLY ROGER" markings they will roll the fate dice and either draw a Hazard or Adventure card that will tell a story and provide or remove crew , resources and gold.

To play at sea each player needs a minimum set of resources otherwise they will be drifting and if not recovered after 4 turns they will lose thier ship. After losing 3 ships players are out of the game.

While traveling at sea players can attack or trade with other players if their ship meet. A black flag ship indicate fighting intention.

While at bay players can trade or buy resources and crew.

-description from designer

In this game, you will assume the role of a treasure hunter on a cursed island, where your mission is to explore the island, uncover clues, and ultimately locate the treasure chest before your opponents do. In the course of your exploration, you may engage in strategic maneuvers against your opponents, forge alliances with the locals, earn their favor or face their retribution, and avoid the ghosts, which are possibly monsters you created along the way

Assume the role of Sheriff Ambrose King, and see if you can catch a murderer while snowbound at Big Elk Lodge!

As with all Deadbolt Mystery Society games, you step into the role of the detective. Examining the evidence provided, visiting different locations and using the password protected QR mechanic to uncover the truth.

Players win once they catch the killer, find what's missing or whatever else is needed to solve the case at hand.

When Walter Hudson, a wealthy entrepreneur, passes away, each member of his estranged family expects to receive their inheritance. As they gather at the estate, they are surprised to find you, a member of the Will Street Detective Agency. Walter’s will implies there is more to his death and more to his family than 
meets the eye. Can you comb through the evidence left by Walter and discover how many skeletons are in this family's 
 closet?

As with all Deadbolt Mystery Society games, you step into the role of the detective. Examining the evidence provided, visiting different locations and using the password protected QR mechanic to uncover the truth.

Players win once they catch the killer, find what's missing or whatever else is needed to solve the case at hand.

boardgame: Золотой ключик, или Приключения Буратино (The Golden Key, or the Adventures of Buratino)
description:
Золотой ключик ("The Golden Key") is a simple draw-and-move race game for children in "senior preschool," based on the classic Russian children's novel of the same name by Alexei Tolstoy (itself an adaptation of the Italian novel "Pinocchio"). It is a variation on a more common roll-and-move game with the same theme, but this is the only version to feature art by A. Barsukov.

In the game, players are trying to get to the Lightning Theater. On their turn, each player draws a card to see how they will move. The cards are portraits of Buratino and other characters from the book, along with a colored circle and a number. If the card shows a blue circle, the player moves their counter that many spaces forward. If it shows a red circle, they move that many spaces back. If it shows a yellow circle, the players lose a turn.

Similarly, landing on a space with a blue star allows players to hop forward to the next blue-star space. A yellow-star space forces players to lose a turn.

The game includes a three-dimensional toy theater element. At the end of play, the winner is encouraged to set up the three included standees - Buratino, Pierrot, and Malvina - in a little scene in the Lightning Theater.

—user summary

Magic Number is a Rummy style card game with a twist!
Played in 13 rounds, each round has an increasing number of cards with a different Magic Number as a wild card.
The aim is to collect the right combinations to be the first to come out and then enjoy watching opponents squirm to minimise their losses. The player with the lowest score wins.
What makes this game so much fun for all ages is that every round is a new opportunity to turn the game around. It’s anyone’s game until the very last round. Oh yes, and don’t mind the laughter when a player accidentally discards the Magic Number!

—description from the publisher

In Network Explorer you are the last line of defense against ruthless hackers attempting to take down the network. As a cyber security specialist, you must use your wit and strategic thinking to thwart their attacks and save the day. Can you save the network and prevent a catastrophic data breach?

—Description from Publisher

Rearrange the cards and move your die along the created node path, scoring the points along the path and collect the points. The die also doubles as a round counter for the game. And different symbols mean different things that can happen to you (points can go up, cards can be flipped etc.)

Magic Marbles is a strategy board game for 2-6 players who race  marbles around a track to the safety of their home. There is always the chance of landing on a magic spot leading to an encounter with the Generous Genie, Magic Carpet, Sinister Sultan or Fortunate Spell. Pickpockets and jugglers can sneak up – best to be on the front foot and hone one's own pick pocket and juggling skills.

Inspired by classic games such as Parcheesi, Ludo and Mensch Ärgere Dich Nicht, Magic Marbles is a modern game using themed function cards and a magic dice to inject excitement and game changing twists and turns.

The game is easy to learn. Each player receives a quick start reference card and playing cards themselves have functions printed on them.

The unique felt board design is ideal for travelling. It rolls up into a compact bag: easy to store in any cupboard, caravan, suitcase or bag.

—description from the publisher

New movement card, Nessie.

This card provides new movement options for your pieces.

This card was given out to participants in the Onitama tournament at the Tabletop Scotland 2022 convention.

In the cold, endless expanse, something unexpected appears. As word spreads throughout the Moonrakers with a strange concoction of trepidation and frenzy, one question lingers like an unanswered echo traversing the vastness of space. Are they friend... or foe?

Includes the following new cards:
 5 new alien crew cards
   * Metaxis
   * Karma Kesa
   * Col Dervok
   * Strada Rysh
   * Zardon the Enforcer
 5 new alien ship cards
   * Siphon
   * Fission
   * Ghost
   * SOS
   * Doom
 5 new alien contracts
   * Boarding Action
   * First Contact
   * Focused Fire
   * Gauntlet Run
   * Decoy Target

This expansion was only available for backers of the original Moonrakers Kickstarter Exclusive Edition and is compatible with all versions & player counts of Moonrakers.

Coalesce is an abstract strategy game for two players, based on the principle of unification.

Components:
One hexagonal board, size 5.
A sufficient number of black and white pieces.

Contract phase:
Player 1 places any number of black pieces on the board.
Player 2 chooses to play Coalesce (Black) or Divide (White).

Play:
Players alternate playing stones of their colour to an empty space, starting with Divide.

Goal:
If Coalesce connects all their stones into a single group, they win.
If this becomes impossible, Divide wins.

—description from the designer

Kufr 2 is a party game that is heavily inspired by the game Activity (1990). In Kufr 2, players will try to provide others with information that will lead them to guess the word on the playing card. This is done by drawing, talking or gesturing.

This game can be played in teams or collectively - where everyone tries to guess.

The uniqueness of this game comes mostly from one extra guessing game - where guessing players must ask yes/no questions and try to figure out the word within the time limit.

Also, the game contains relatively many playing cards, which allows for a large variety and replayability.

The box (in the shape of a suitcase) makes the game easy to carry and bring to the table, making it an ideal addition to various social events.

Twenty solution cards are dealt evenly among the players. Four problem cards are placed on the board to start the game, and a new one is added at the beginning of every player's turn.

On his turn, a player rolls a die to earn money, draws a solution card, and then resolves one or more problem cards by (i) discarding one or more solution cards that are the same color as the problem card(s) and whose sum of numbers equals or exceeds the sum of the numbers on the problem card(s); and (ii) paying the cost shown on the problem card(s). The resolved problem card(s) are worth victory points to the player.

After every round of turns, advance the Global Warming Index a distance equal to the values on the unresolved problem cards. Play until one player wins by reaching a certain number of victory points based on player count. Everyone loses if, before that happens, the Global Warming Index reaches 75.

Each card in a deck has a green side that displays an adjective and a black side with a noun. Orient the deck green-side up, then flip the top card to create a two-word, green-black phrase. Each player writes down a thought that the phrase brings to mind and scores one point for each other player's thought that matches his. Play until an agreed-upon stopping point.

Cults of Bleakpyre is a competitive, card-driven area control board game for 2 - 6 players, designed by Tom Truman and published by Trolls ’n’ Rerolls.

You are eccentric cults, rampaging through the dystopian fantasy city of Bleakpyre, harvesting the souls of decadent nobles and selfish peasants with every outrageous tool at your disposal.

Battling to control and win the powerful Amulet of Thrayax, you use actions to play combinations of Murder and Scheme cards to collect souls - AKA victory points - from the city and move them onto your sacrificial altar where they’re scored.

To claim the amulet, you'll need to bring all your most devious tactics to the party:


     Strike a balance between collecting spendable Peasants, high scoring Nobles, and scoring objectives
     Skilfully plan out your six possible actions per turn, which scale in cost
     Embrace the Amulet’s power, which enables better outcomes on your cards and bestows spendable Shards to cults
     Utilise your cult’s unique powers and cards to gain the upper hand


When the final round chimes, the winner is the cult that has beaten and hoodwinked the most souls out of Bleakpyre.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Academia: A Game of Administrative Bloat
description:
Academia: A Game of Administrative Bloat is an engine-building, action-management game about running a university that uses a unique action-selection mechanism.

Players are the boards of directors of their universities, choosing the big strategic initiatives from year to year. Each turn represents an academic year in which the player will choose one of four strategic focuses (actions) for their university. When selecting a focus, players choose one of the four offices at their university. Each ready staff member in that office works, triggering their effect. Each focus requires players to exhaust staff, meaning the game is about managing which staff are ready and when. Over the course of play players build facilities, hire staff, advertise, recruit students, form faculty committees and so on.

Every four years, students will graduate and affect their university's reputation: star athletes improve athletics reputation, high performers academic reputation, and so on. Players then admit new students, and begin again. After three cohorts of students have graduated, players score prestige from reputation, communications efforts, faculty committees, and personal objectives, then the most prestigious university wins.

—description from the designer

Village War: The Artefacts is a spinoff of Village War: The Calamity and is set in the same fantasy world based on Nigerian mythology.

It is a card drafting game for 3-6 players where they can play command cards of artefacts to avoid picking the last card of the deck.

There is a draw pile on the table, consisting of cards showing different artefacts with different abilities. The game lasts until the draw pile is gone. Whoever drew the last card, loses; of the others, the player with fewest cards in hand, wins.

On a turn, a player can play an artefact from their hand and use its power, discard a card, or draw a card. The artefacts' powers range from forcing others to draw cards, moving cards to other players, etc. The goal is to force someone else to draw the last card while having the least cards in your hands yourself, but not all cards count the same at the end, so a bit of uncertainty remains.

When science and magic intersect, an intense battle story begins!
MAGIWANDER is an action game in which players compete in multiple competitions by manipulating coins with a wand; the main attraction of MAGIWANDER is that it extends the experience of magnetism, which acts instantly and produces clear results, and allows players to experience magnetism itself as a magical force for extended periods.
Roll a Magical Coin around on your desk and you will discover that many of the magnetic forces you use in your everyday life are extended in ways you may not have thought possible! You may even discover that it affects the gaming field as well.
We hope you will enjoy your own magical experience with your family and friends.

—description from the designer
designers: Masakazu Takizawa (たきざわ まさかず)
artists: Masakazu Takizawa (たきざわ まさかず)

description:
I’m Pretty Savvy celebrates creativity, wit, and cleverness! It provides engaging entertainment and encourages you to approach the world with a fresh perspective.

Deliver creative answers to random questions and see how many cards you can answer in 1 minute. Your opponent can quickly get away with incorrect answers if you are not careful. So, pay attention and be ready to challenge impractical or incorrect responses.

—description from the publisher

Dive and grab fast. 
Research the ocean! Roll the dice and the dive begins: search the deep sea cards and find the suitable swarm. But the glowing sea animals never stand still. Look closely and don‘t go wrong, else there are penalties.

Abgetaucht und schnell geschnappt.
Erforscht den Ozean! Die Würfel fallen und euer rasanter Tauchgang beginnt: Durchwühlt gleichzeitig die Tiefseekarten und findet den passenden Unterwasserschwarm. Doch die leuchtenden Meerestiere stehen nicht still! Schaut genau hin und vergreift euch nicht, sonst gibt‘s Minuspunkte. Wer in fünf Tauchgängen die wenigsten Minuspunkte sammelt gewinnt.

"Heinzelmännchen" are a mythical race of creatures, appearing in a tale connected with the city of Cologne in Germany akin to gnomes, or elves.
According to a die throw, players move in turn on an abstracted board of Cologne to visit specific spaces (craftsman's workshops). For the first 4 spaces, the earlier they arrive, the fewer points they are rewarded, for the last space, the reward is the other way around and the players are rewarded more points. 
If 2 players are in the same quarter of Cologne (a "Veedel"), they must fight by throwing a die who may stay and who must return to his home base.
After the first player arrived at the 5th space, one of 4 cards are drawn to trigger finally the end. The player with the most points wins.

—user summary

boardgame: Tacopocalypse: A Tactical Bombardment of Queso! Promo Card
description:
A promotional card for the card game Tacopocalypse which adds a "take that" flair to the game.

The card's text says: "When this card is revealed all other players lose one Combo Plate without scoring it."

This promo card was available in Game Trade Magazine # 279.

Nab 'Em is a quick play card game for 2-5 players. Players secretly add Villains "onto the streets" then attempt to beat out the other heroes and capture them. A game plays in about 15-20 minutes. The rules are simple and easy to learn but challenging enough to keep the game interesting.

—description from the publisher

War has left its terrible wake. The Star Tower and the Night Tower have their truce, but for us in the lands between, everything has twisted, everything has shattered, everything has been scarred by their dread magic. And power-hungry factions stir. The Crimson Coven...the Eye Cult...the Doompipers. But let them have their mad schemes. We have ours. We find the shards, we harvest them, and we fuse fresh spells. Not because we crave power, but because we must so we might survive. I shall follow the trails of those who walked before me, and fashion my own magic before I perish in my SHARD HARVEST.

In Shard Harvest, you play as an aspiring mage in a land following a devastating magical war between the Star Tower (in the west) and the Night Tower (in the east). An uneasy truce exists between these two schools of magic, but their war has left the land between fraught with dangerous magical energies, strange factions, and hopeful villagers who wish to gain magical power for themselves by harvesting the shards that periodically emerge across the landscape, shards the talented can fuse into powerful spells. You are one such harvester. Can you survive the unforgiving landscape long enough to attain mastery or will you perish as the many shard harvesters before you?

This is a board game for 1 player. You control a shard harvester, seeking the power to create spells. Travel can be challenging and tiring. Encounters can be deadly and devastating. Your spells will help you survive the perilous wild. You might die from wounds or succumb to old age. If you have one or more shards collected and you reach either of the towers (the Star Tower or the Night Tower) you win the game, and your shard harvester may either retire or resume the harvest in future games. It may take several journeys for a single shard harvester to achieve the maximum victory.

This is a Make-as-You-Play game, which means that you will create and modify many of the components during play. The components include the rules, the game map, index cards, a pen or pencil, a pawn for your shard harvester, and a collection of coins or cubes for the shards. Each game of Shard Harvester will evolve differently, with different terrain shaping the lands between the towers and different spells available to gain. Moreover, there is no limit to the number of shard harvesters you can make to explore your world or the number of spells they can conceive.

—description from the designer

The unique hand management and dice interactions in this game replicate all aspects of the Canadian Football experience - from kickoffs to passes, running plays to quarterback sacks, punts to field goals.

It's everything you need to have a gridiron battle on your table without being overly complicated or convoluted. The turns are fast and furious, and the action is never-ending! Well, until the game's over that is... but you can always do a double-header!

Each card in the deck contaians an offensive AND a defensive play, each with modifiers to the dice to optimize your chances of successful plays. Use your wits and strategize to maximize your options! Dice rolls and card modifiers determine the success of offensive plays and yardage.

AND! There are 2 ways to play: A quick game runs through the deck once, and the high score at the end wins! Quickies take about half an hour. A regular game runs through the deck twice - the first time through the deck gets you to half time, and then you reshuffle and play out the second half. After an hour you will have an ultimate gridiron victory!

—description from the publisher

Play against your opponents to win the battle for classic horror supremacy!

In this action-packed game, your mission is to collect the six monsters needed to complete your horrific Empire: Dracula, Frankenstein, King Kong, The Bride, The Mummy, and The Wolf Man.

Be the first to gather your Empire, using action cards to manage your hand while lynching opponents' monsters! The trick is to have one of each monster AND NOTHING ELSE in your hand to win! Plan carefully!

This engaging, fast-paced game is for 2-4 players and takes about 20 minutes to play.

—description from the publisher

Play against your opponents to win the quest for Fantastic supremacy!

In this action-packed game, your mission is to collect the six assets of the Fantasy Empire: an Elf, a Mage, a Dwarf, a Fae, a Castle, and a Dragon.

Be the first to gather the Empire, using action cards to manage your hand while destroying opponents' assets! The trick is to have one of each of the assets AND NOTHING ELSE in your hand to win! Strategize, use your wisdom and guile and ye shall be TRIUMPHANT!

This engaging, fast-paced game is for 2-4 players and takes about 20 minutes to play.

—description from the publisher

Nobody ever said that being a top-notch secret agent was going to be easy!

Be the first to complete 3 Top-Secret Missions to be declared the Ultimate SPY MASTER. Use Action Cards to get what you need, sabotage other agents, and cause bedlam as you escape danger to carry out your assignment!

Remember - there are no friends among spies. Trust no one, use your guile to its greatest advantage, and good luck to you, Agent.

—description from the publisher

A brand new Gembearer (starting deck) for Gemwielders Deckbuilding Game. It comes with two full art versions of the core set Gembearers Hammer and Pistol.

A century ago, Discwielders patrolled the skies, securing the outer provinces of the empire. When the second emperor passed, many gave up their Rocket Shields to not get caught in the aftermath. Some of these discs, however, survived the decades, hidden away, waiting for a suited Gemwielder to be taken to the skies once again.

Not only does the Rocket Shield’s passive ability allow you to play power costed gem cards more reliably early, it also provides you with a steady stream of power throughout the entire game. The price for this headstart is a power hungry starting deck. Efficient power generation and power expenditure therefore become key features of the discwielding playstyle.

The figures (ships) may be moved back and forth on the lines from space to space. Crossing spaces can be moved forwards and backwards in all directions, but only to the next space.
At the beginning of the game, each party has to occupy their own ports and those of their colonies with at least one of their own ships. The ports of all states not involved in the game are neutral and may not be stormed by the warring factions, but can be occupied by any faction. The parties set up their remaining ships step by step. When all access lines to an enemy port are closed, it is said to be blocked. If the opponent manages to encircle enemy ships, these encircled ships are also considered captured and are eliminated from the game.
If a party has occupied the 5 nearest spaces in front of an enemy port, the port is considered captured and the ship in the port must be eliminated from the game and the besieging party must occupy the captured port.
A game is over when you manage to occupy at least two-thirds of all your opponent's ports, or when you have taken three quarters of your opponent's ships, or you have surrounded him so that he can no longer move.

—user summary

There's a party in the jungle today, and the animals are gathering on the ancient statues, but space is limited, and all the animals want to climb on top. If too many animals are on a statue, they all fall down and have to climb up anew.

In Dschungelgedrängel, players each have four statue cards in front of them. In their hands, they hold cards with different numbers of the four animals: elephants, tigers, monkeys, and parrots. The goal is to stack nine animals of a kind on top of the respective statues. On their turn, players can either play any number of cards of the same animal type on a statue, or they can play an action card that messes with other players' plans. If at any time a player has more than nine animals of a kind on a given statue, they all fall down and the statue is empty again.

The first player to fill three statues triggers the game end; at the end of that round, the player with the most filled statues wins.

Each player moves his road paver around a track according to the throw of a die. According to the color of the space landed on, draw one or more material cards. Most of the spaces are linked with a project or event card. The latter are to be followed, the first may be acquired if the required material components are spent. Drawn cards are replenished. Sometimes, you may trade components with other players. In the end, each project cards and corresponding sets of them are rewarded with victory points.

Terraforming Mars: Prelude 2, the sixth expansion for Terraforming Mars, focuses on cross-expansion effects, prelude cards with ongoing effects and actions, and more project cards for your tableau...not to mention five new corporations.

Most of the cards are specifically connected to other expansions of Terraforming Mars (Venus, Colonies, Turmoil), but the 5 Corporations and 15 of the Prelude cards are playable without any of these other expansions.

The Kickstarter Edition includes the Prelude 2 KS Promo Pack of 20 promo cards (Prelude, Corporation and Project cards).
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Available in the Gamefound Campaign.

Everything you need to go back to the start of a Solar 175 campaign!

Solar 175 is a legacy game. This means that as you play permanent changes are made to the components based on your decisions within the game itself! This recharge pack allows you to replace these components so that you can start the game afresh!

boardgame: Skytear Horde: Nupten Illusionists & Utsesh Horde
description:
These 2 expansions are a leftover from the Kickstarter. They include:

40 alliance cards, a pre-made Nupen deck all about slowing down the game while you collect the mentalist cards you need to unlock a game-changing combo.
    4 horde cards for the common set (Secret Passage, Raise the Dead, Utseshi Swarm, Onikai Legion) that you can mix with the 4 commons from the base box.
    21 horde cards, the Nightmares deck made of Mythical monsters such as harpies, golems, and chimaeras. These beasts are able to paralyze entire armies before the terrible arrival of the Utsesh outsider.
    6 outsider cards, The Dreaming Mathriarc, the Ancient Outsider, the Silent Ambassador.
    4 additional heroes, one per faction: Akimo, Shaidrus, Vorhild, Ixatosk.
    2 castles: the Cloud Gate and The Executioner’s Den, respectively for Akhuti and Haburat, the 2 heroes included in the pre-made deck.

-description from publisher's website

This expansion includes two new pre-made decks:

- Nupten Illusionists (Alliance deck) are assassins, rogues and mentalists able to impose their mind over matter, somehow bending parts of reality to their will. Arrogant beyond imagination, they have been the last faction to join the Alliance against the Horde.

- The horde from Utsesh (Horde deck) is made of mythical monsters such as harpies, golems and chimaeras. The few that survived the horde say these beasts are able to paralyze entire armies before the terrible arrival of the Utsesh Outsider, The Mother Sphynx.

This exclusive pack of cards was available during the Kickstarter campaing as part of the Deluxe Pledge and includes "Utsesh Horde Expansion" and "Nupten Illusionists Expansion". It was later made available together on the publisher's website

All decks can be mixed with those included in the retail box to provide additional combinations and customization.

-user summary

Nothing has been the same since the alien monstrosity darkened our scorched skies, their cybernetic shadow spreading dread across the Wasteland. Their eyes are dead set on our camps and water supply. We, the animal denizens of the Wasteland, have branded them, the CLAW! Old rivalries must be thrown aside as we dig deep and find strength together against this fearsome presence or risk facing possible extinction. Together we must recruit the fearless dwellers and unite the four clans; Rabbit, Skunk, Raccoon, and Squirrel! We’ll carve a hidden path beneath the shifting dunes to the heart of their mechanical bird and destroy it once and for all.

CLAW is a cooperative game where players assume the role of 1 of 4 factions to hold off the CLAW's assault over the course of 4 rounds. Players will build their decks, take actions, gather resources, avoid dangerous sandstorms, and construct epic vehicles to battle the CLAW to protect life in the Wasteland.

The CLAW will reveal its secret action once per round, giving players agency over its next big move while simultaneously unleashing its attacks on players by following arrow paths toward the water supply.

Can you outlast the threat of the CLAW?

Players take turns moving down a river a distance that equals the roll of a die. Some river spaces cause you to lose water, others to gain it. A player must have at least 10 gallons of water to take his turn; if he is short, he can take water from a community cistern that all players voluntarily contribute to. The player with the most water when all players have reached the end of the river wins. However, if one or more players has/have fewer than 10 gallons of water when the other players have finished, then everyone loses.

Estranho no Ninho is a fun trivia game of curiosities, absurdities and desperate guesses. In each round there is a new category (weirder than the previous one) and you have to guess as many answers as possible, but beware, some of them are outright lies, or...Strangers in the Nest.

On your turn, choose between:

     Place one of your egg tokens on one of the answers on the board
     Pass the turn and leave the round.


When all players have passed, check the answers. The winner is the one with the most correct answers.

Do cows kill more humans per year than sharks? Is it true that hippos can't swim? Bet against your friends each round, trying to avoid running into the Strangers in the Nest.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Attack and Defense: The Game of Kings
description:
A strategy game for 1 and 2 player.

Where each player chooses from a pool of 12 buildings/decks, 3 to fight against the other. Each of the buildings has its own kind of units and tricks.

The goal is to destroy the 3 enemy buildings with attacks. Destroyed buildings then have negative effects for the owner.

Then it has the calendar. A separate stack of event cards, these are revealed at the end of the day/round and have an effect for the next day.

—description from the designer

boardgame: ARCANA PROPHETIA: A Game About Fate
description:
Arcana Prophetia is an asymmetric, tarot-inspired game where 2 players face off in a battle of wits and prediction.

One player takes on the role of the Fates, who seeks to usher in the end of days by corrupting the pantheon. Standing against them is the Last Sovereign, who attempts to surmount the odds and constantly tries to divine the Fates' next move.

Each round, the Last Sovereign makes a prediction while the Fates choose a god to corrupt - represented by the Arcana cards. The Fates then plays the chosen card onto the Spread, on either its Corrupted or Sanctified face, depending on whether the Sovereign's divination proves true. Every god has their own unique effects; flipping, moving or otherwise manipulating other Arcana cards and the Spread. Should adjacent Arcana sum to 10, they will bind together, locking them in place. After nine short-but-tense rounds, the game moves to its conclusion, where the player with the most cards aligned to them wins.

No two cycles of Arcana Proheptia are identical - each of the arcana feature three different aspects, and the Fates can choose a face card that warps the game in unique, fun ways. Likewise, while the Last Sovereign often finds themselves on the back foot, they're not without tools. With a limited arsenal Sigils and the Relic of the Dead God, they must work to have predictions strike true, lest the pantheon falls to chaos.

-description from publisher

boardgame: 限時解鎖密室逃脫歷險故事擴充1 (Escape Room the Game: Family Pack)
description:
The new expansion pack for Escape Room the Game. The 3 stories are more family friendly. All 3 stories will form the whole adventure, in the Jungle!

Note: This is the Chinese version of Escape Room: The Game – Family Edition: The Jungle but does not come with the decoder and is therefore an expansion and requires a base game.
family: Series: Escape Room – The Game (Identity Games International B.V.)

boardgame: Moonlight Ascendance: A game of arcane strife.
description:
Moonlight Ascendance – A game of arcane strife is a competitive strategy game wherein you face off with up to four other players, each representing a faction fighting for power over the city in which the game takes place!

Moonlight Ascendance is played in rounds, in which the players take turns to spend their actions, treasures and hard-earned money to forward their cause. A player may win in three different ways, which vary depending on the faction they play as. While most have similar core abilities, their varying powers and goals make for differing and exciting playstyles.

During the game players may collect cards, inspired by mythology and folklore, that appear across the map. These might provide new ways for the players to spend their actions, or they might provide new abilities or boons.

The game can be played in multiple ways depending on the amount of players, and the cards and events make for a new experience every time you play. The simple core functions make the game easy to learn, but hard to master.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Time Guardian Adventures: Mayhem on the Moon
description:
Dr. Maxwell Mayhem has travelled back in time to 1969 and prevented the moon landing of the "Apollo XI" mission. The course of history then went completely off the rails, and now Mayhem not only controls the largest rocket manufacturer in the world, but also considers the moon his property.

But that's not all! With his giant bulldozers, he had an image of his face carved into the surface of the moon, so that you can't look up at the sky at night without seeing Mayhem's grinning visage. And no one knows what this megalomaniac will do next!

Time Guardians, the world is counting on you! Travel back to 1969 in the time machine. Undo Mayhem's manipulations. Put history back on track!

Inside the box in the shape of Apéricube cheese, the 400 questions and challenges are divided in 5 sections : People, Trendy, Waouh, Fiestas and Challenges.

During their turn, each player chooses a number between 1 and 400. If he succeds to answer the question or accomplish the challenge corresponding to the number, they score one point. The first player to score 10 points wins the game.

A variant of Halma. Each player has their pieces on their back row and you win by getting all your pieces to the opposite side of the board. On your turn you move a piece to a neighboring space in either of the eight directions, or jump over a single piece in a straight line.

boardgame: Space Is Unforgiving (And So Is This Game)
description:
A space-based cooperative multiplayer experience; a 2-4 player survival game where outrunning a supernova is the prime goal, all while managing food and fuel reserves, maintaining a ship, as well as player health. While doing so; you will encounter hardships from space giraffes to pirates that will work to prevent you from progressing.

You begin with a randomly selected ship, that may or may not have everything you need. You'll work together as a team to determine the best course of action. Do you need more food or fuel? Or is the Supernova right behind you, and you need as much distance as possible? Do you have to leave someone behind in order to save the other three crew members? And who brought onboard the radioactive sugar gliders?!

With plenty of randomized events, each and every turn is full of action and suspense, and no game will be the same! You can expect the average playthrough to be between 45 to 60 minutes in length; that is, assuming you live that long. Space is unforgiving, and so is this game.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
In "Barton in Bloom" players play the role of a member of an English village, all vying to become the most prestigious resident. They will do this by buying, planting, growing and harvesting plants, all so that they can help decorate the village cenotaph and have the best kept garden in time for the summer fête.

On a players turn they will use one of the shared worker dice to take one of the actions. This will increase the cost of that worker for all future actions. If actions are taken by the specialised worker they will take an enhanced action.

Rather than taking a normal action with a worker, a player may instead rest the worker gaining a benefit based on it's current cost and resetting it's cost back to 1.

Players will continue taking turns until one player has completely filled their garden using flowers and ornaments. At this point the players' gardens will be judged against a set of judge cards played throughout the game.

-description from designer

Plunge into a unique horror racing game set in Dante's Inferno that will keep you on the edge of your seat! With 20 beautifully sculpted miniatures representing historically infamous characters, a 3D board to manipulate, and spooky and terrifying baroque artwork, this semi-cooperative game will make you question what you would be willing to do to escape hell.

In Forsaken, the objective of the game is to escape from hell with the highest amount of FAITH (victory points) earned from benevolent acts, such as helping the player in last place. However, players can also choose to continue committing misdeeds and earning SIN (negative victory points) to reach the exit faster. Special milestones await players who risk earning SIN to advance ahead of the others.

At the start of the game, players will each receive a diary. Inside, you will have to create a tableau of cards to plan your day in hell. By adding these cards, you will establish your current and upcoming actions. When each day comes to an end, turn the page of your diary, and witness new actions unfold right before your eyes, leading to a fresh turn/day.

The main actions are:

     Movement (clockwise or counterclockwise), triggering events, traps, or milestones.
     Drawing: Stack cards on the right page of your agenda for when you turn the page.
     Help: Assist the last player on the board, helping them, and gain FAITH (VP).

In the wild, most humpback whales feed by lunge fishing, which is relatively ineff-fish-cient. Some pods, though, have developed the "bubble net" feeding technique, where a whale dives deep below the fish and comes up, blowing a spiral of bubbles, confusing the fish and driving them into a tight ball, leading to a higher-quality bite!

This is what we are replicating in Bubble Net. Each player is competing against themselves to eat more fish than they lose out to sea.

Player boards are divided into segments that match the points of the compass.

Set-up: Place a Fish token on every marked space, and the starting Bubble tokens on their spots. Shuffle the Fish cards and place the deck in the middle.
 On your turn, simultaneously with any other players, add one Bubble to the track. If the Bubble closes a segment, drive any fish present inward by one. All fish that are pushed into the very centre, which represents your Mouth, move into your Belly.

Next, place your two Podmate tokens. They cannot share a segment with a Bubble, but they close any segment where they are placed - a whole whale needs a whole segment.

The Fish respond: flip the top Fish card, and for every Fish icon represented, move all Fish tokens in that segment out by one space, unless the segment is blocked by one or more Bubbles, or a Podmate whale. Any Fish outside the spiral escape to the "Freedom" space, and can no longer be eaten.

Continue alternating moves between the Whales and the Fish until any player has made a Bait Ball (all Fish are within the center spiral), then all players Lunge. All Fish within the center spiral go into your belly, and any outside that area escape.

If there are more Fish in your Belly that escaped out to sea, you have won!

Bubble Net is a multi-player, meditative solitaire game that takes around ten minutes to play and can have as many players as have boards. Players share the Fish deck; each card affects all players, a bit like Bingo, so new players can join the game at any point, continuing when others reach the Bait Ball stage.

The interactive variant allows players to take on the role of their neighbors' Fish, with each person playing a Fish card from their hand face-down onto their neighbor's playing area before the Whales move, instead of drawing a single card that affects everyone from the Fish deck afterwards. Players may choose to score against each other instead of against themselves in this variant.

—description from the publisher

Meet Finley, a master of deception. You look left, Finley runs right. You look right, Finley's... well... we don't know.

Finley's escaped again, and you're assigned to investigate. Study the intelligence reports and the expansive full-color map to determine which building Finley is hiding in.

But here's the catch: you don't have all of the intelligence yet. It's a race between players to strategically swap intelligence cards and get the information needed to find Finley first!

Start the game by selecting a challenge card. Each challenge card has 12 affiliated intelligence cards, which are distributed evenly amongst players.

Review your intelligence cards, take notes on your Investigator's Notepad, and strategically swap cards with another player on your turn. Each card has a different piece of information that you can use to build a picture of Finley's movements--once you think you have enough data, you can guess Finley's location. The first player to correctly guess Finley's location on the map wins!

-description from publisher

Guide the animal folk of the forest from their humble beginnings in the village of Thistleden as they explore new lands, develop tools and abilities, and ward off predators and dangers in this civilization-building game for 1-5 players.

Each player has a 3x3 card tableau  representing their Den. During the planning phase, each player chooses a Denizen card in secret to add to their Den. Then all cards are revealed, and players place their Denizens cards in their grid, and activate all the cards in that row or column, each of which grants actions based on its type: Harvesters, Builders, Scientists, and Explorers.

At the end of the round, if the row is full, the player discards two of the cards from their active row (keeping only the one they played). Discarded cards are placed in their Legacy tableau, and adding strength to future actions as their civilization grows in age and knowledge.
 Players compete to explore new areas, build new Dens, research new technology, attract helpful Denizens and Leaders, and to defend Thistleden from threats.

-description from developer

In Springsign, you and your friends are influential town councilors in a growing frontier settlement. Right now, Springsign is a "quaint" little town (some might even say "dead-end" or "completely-untamable"), but under the right leadership its potential is limitless! Together, you will need to grow the town by constructing new buildings, keeping settlers fed, fending off bandits, and in many other ways keeping the town humming along. But there's a catch: this is not a cooperative game! The townsfolk will vote for a new mayor soon, and helping your political opponents won't get you elected. As the players develop town, they'll also have to outmaneuver and outwit one another to garner more support for their own campaign.

Springsign is a light/medium-weight, town-building, resource-management game. Over the course of a 90-minute playthrough, you will influence and make use of the town's interactive economy, develop powerful card engines, and find ways of snagging invaluable votes from your opponents. The game's innovative "Popularity & Votes" point system, where the players must time when to make best use of their popularity to win a vote, ensures that the game remains tight up till the end. The balance of powerful actions and limited resources make each turn tense and memorable, while the deeply-asymmetric player boards give each player a completely different playstyle and way of interacting with the game.

The goal of Springsign is to win The Election at the end of the game by having the most votes. To get votes, you'll need popularity, which is collected through a variety of actions and abilities.

When the chips are down, will you do what's right for Springsign, or will you push your way into office through anything (and anyone) who stands in your way? Those greedy enough to steal victory from their opponents will be able to win, and some players may be willing to risk the town itself in their race to the top!

—description from the publisher

Released via the Japanime Games patreon.

St. Valentine is promo card # KBP-002

Act: Shuffle a Warrior in the Netherworld into the Warrior Deck.
Act: 1 Energy: Change the Chaining Color of the last Warrior played to a color of your choice.

Released via the Japanime Games patreon.

Cupid is promo card # KBP-003

React: Defense 2. Choose an Attacker and put it in your Discard Pile.

Includes randomizer, card separator, and 5 copies.

Released via the Japanime Games patreon.

St. Patrick is promo card # KBP-004

Act: 1 Energy: Banish a Disciple II in any Discard Pile.
Act: Look at the top 2 cards of your deck, put one into your hand, and Banish the other.

Released via the Japanime Games patreon.

Jeanne D'Arc is promo card # KBP-005

React: 1 Energy: If a Warrior is banished, put it in its owner's discard pile or the bottom of the Warrior Deck if there is no owner.
Act: Put a Disciple III on top of your deck from your Discard Pile.

boardgame: Tanto Cuore: Memento Mori – Twilight Manor: Kickstarter Promos
description:
The Kickstarter for Memento Mori - Twilight Manor also introduced five new promos.

They can only be used with Memento Mori ruleset in play.

PR65 - Anise Deceased
PR66 - Cynthia Corpse
PR67 - Dead Eye Domino
PR68 - Maligned Aline
PR69 - Deadly Dermot
PR70 - Madame Damnable

boardgame: 三日月夜と紅茶の姫君 (Crescent Moon Night and Tea Princess)
description:
Get a lot of tea and sweets (scores).
Become a tea princess!
Flip cards from the deck to get sweets.
When pots and patrols appear, everyone takes action!
Let's go get tea faster than anyone else!
Hide from patrols quickly!

But the etiquette should be graceful.

Can you perform a given action quickly and skillfully?
It's important to know when to turn over the cards.
It's an action-type card-flipping competition game.

In addition, if you can get both the girls' favorite tea and sweets, there might be good things♪

Let's run maid cafes in Akihabara. And if you managed to own 10 maid cafes, you won.

There are various maids to hire, such as students, office workers, underground idols, and cosplayers. The picture is very cute, so that's the number one selling point.

Buy interiors and clothes in the early days. But it's all about creating an atmosphere. So paying a lot for interiors and clothes doesn't affect the game much.

Promote part-timers to star salesmaid or store managers by hiring part-timers in large quantities.

boardgame: ☆秋葉原の野望☆ 拡張キット (Akihabara's Ambition: Expansion kit)
description: This expansion introduces new maids, costumes and guests to the base game.

boardgame: ZU Tiles: Fruits Basket – Starter Set 1
description:
ZU Tiles: Fruits Basket turns the popular zodiac cursed family into a strategic tile placement game. Comprise your team with various members of the Soma family who have been possessed by the Japanese Zodiac animals and take family rivalry to a whole new level!

Players can capture tiles thought compatibility matches or battles. The first to collect 12 zodiac animals to join their banquet wins the game. To achieve this, players will place their tiles down in combination with actions and battles.

This ZU Tiles: Fruits Basket 1st Edition comes with a ton of components and limited run Kickstarter items! The tiles and other components feature the fantastic artwork of the Fruits Basket animated series!

Each Set has everything you need to start playing right out of the box. There are 2 decks in a Starter Set for a total of 6 Soma family members per Set. Players strategize to obtain points through battle or by mending family relationships and matching everyone's compatibility with one another. Earning enough points will break the curse of the Japanese Zodiac and allow members to finally become free.

Keep adding to the collection with Zu Tile Slabs, which are found in ZU Tiles: Fruits Basket – Booster Pack! Each starter set comes with one booster pack.

Starter Set 1 consists of the Rabbit, Boar, Ram, Dog, Horse, and Tiger tiles.

Starter Set 2 consists of the Dragon, Snake, Rooster, Rat, Ox, and Monkey tiles.

boardgame: ZU Tiles: Fruits Basket – Starter Set 2
description:
ZU Tiles: Fruits Basket turns the popular zodiac cursed family into a strategic tile placement game. Comprise your team with various members of the Soma family who have been possessed by the Japanese Zodiac animals and take family rivalry to a whole new level!

Players can capture tiles thought compatibility matches or battles. The first to collect 12 zodiac animals to join their banquet wins the game. To achieve this, players will place their tiles down in combination with actions and battles.

This ZU Tiles: Fruits Basket 1st Edition comes with a ton of components and limited run Kickstarter items! The tiles and other components feature the fantastic artwork of the Fruits Basket animated series!

Each Set has everything you need to start playing right out of the box. There are 2 decks in a Starter Set for a total of 6 Soma family members per Set. Players strategize to obtain points through battle or by mending family relationships and matching everyone's compatibility with one another. Earning enough points will break the curse of the Japanese Zodiac and allow members to finally become free.

Keep adding to the collection with Zu Tile Slabs, which are found in ZU Tiles: Fruits Basket – Booster Pack! Each starter set comes with one booster pack.

Starter Set 1 consists of the Rabbit, Boar, Ram, Dog, Horse, and Tiger tiles.

Starter Set 2 consists of the Dragon, Snake, Rooster, Rat, Ox, and Monkey tiles.

boardgame: ZU Tiles: Fruits Basket – Cat Beast Promo
description:
This Cat Beast Promo was released to Japanime Games patreon.

Str 6 Int 1
Place Cat Beast on any CAT. Gain control of the CAT. As an A, Cat Beast can move up to 2 empty spaces. After the current player's next 2 turns, discard Cat Beast and return control of the CAT to its previous controller.

Promo THD-FB1e1p

boardgame: ZU Tiles: Fruits Basket – Booster Pack
description: Keep adding to the collection with Zu Tile Slabs, which are found in booster packs! Each slab features extended art tiles, foils, alternate arts and more that can be collected or punched out and used in game. There are 5 random slabs per booster pack and 12 booster packs in a booster box.

Players take turns rolling dice and moving along the streets in their neighborhood. Players can stop at their house and draw cards that display either a positive "Save" number or a negative "Waste" number. Players can also visit three commercial buildings in the neighborhood where they can purchase service cards that will enhance their "Save" numbers. At the beginning of each round, each player will receive income that is equal to the "Save" minus "Waste" numbers on their cards. The game also provides ways to acquire extra "Save" cards and to get rid of "Waste" cards. Play ends when all the cards in the house have been taken. The player with the most points (Save minus Waste) at that time wins.

For 2 to 4 players, ages 7-107, Cowbell is an easy to learn, fast-paced card game made up of cow characters in four different scenes.  The object of the game is to be the first player to go out, but you can't go out without the Cowbell - and there's only ONE Cowbell Card in the deck.  You must have this card in order to play your last card and win the game, so everyone is trying to get their hands on the Cowbell Card and then go out of the game while they have it.  Players can steal the Cowbell from each other and the Cowbell can be rotated into or away from your hand depending on action cards played during a round.

The 57-card deck is made up of crazy cow characters like Cowabunga, Holy Cow, Cow Poke, Mad Cow and more!  Action cards include the Horseshoe Magnet, Don't Have a Cow, Cow Tippin', Milk It and Beef It Up - all with different actions that keep things mooooooooving right along!

Games can last anywhere from 2 minutes to 10 minutes - simply deal 7 cards to each player to start the game and take turns discarding matching scenes, characters, magnets or wild cards.  Get the Cowbell and try to go out before someone steals it from you.  If you don't have a play, draw until you do...it's sheer cow madness!

—description from the designer

"We have been the first victims; be careful, we will not be the last"
(President Negrín)

In the Service of the Republic: The game of diplomats, propagandists and spies (1936-1939) is a diplomacy, propaganda, and espionage game set in the troubled 1930s. It is semi-cooperative in nature and combines exploration journeys, resource management, hidden roles, political intrigue, and abstracted war.

In it, each player represents a diplomat (sometimes also a propagandist and spy) serving the Republican side during the Spanish Civil War. Throughout 3 game turns (which simulate the 3 years between September 1936 and September 1939), your objective will be to travel throughout the map (Europe and North America) in search of military, humanitarian and energy supplies, and once obtained, ship them to loyal Spain, so that the beleaguered Republic has some chance of survival. In the final part of the turn, the military rebels (supported by Nazi Germany and Fascist Italy) will launch several offensives and gradually conquer the cities that are still in the hands of the Republic. At the end of turn 3, World War II will break out (which will mean the salvation of the republican regime, as Spain is framed on the side of the allies against the Axis).

—description from the designer

In a library, you find notes from a famous explorer who disappeared 30 years ago in search of Atlantis. You set out on a dangerous journey to follow in her footsteps and find the legendary city. 
Every day, you open a door and solve a puzzle. The solution will tell you which door to open on the next day. Solve all 24 puzzles and save Atlantis from impending doom.
In case you get stuck, there are digital clues available on the publisher’s website.
family: Misc: Advent / Christmas / Jule calendar game

description:
A 2–4 player strategy game where players, as cultists, must construct a garden in which shrines worthy of dragon worship are to be built.

The dragons however, are very picky about how the garden is constructed, and each only favours a single colour of decoration.

Players must perform 2–3 rituals at these dragon shrines in and around the garden. The first player to reach this number wins.

On each turn, players play cards to either move their cultists or decorate the garden.

The rituals have overlapping requirements however, so more often than not, the players will have to fight over the shrines they wish to access.

Make use of powerful warp spells to traverse the garden quicker, or build them into the garden for a permanent boost.

May the best cultists win the dragons' favours.

-description from designer

Atlantis Omni is a unique, indie handmade war game for 2-4 players. In the lost City of Atlantis, four factions face each other with a common goal, to claim it. Their unique weapons are their Omnis and the elements of nature fire, water, earth, and void that must be moved strategically to survive and win. Two energy levels for the powerful to activate them and turn the game upside down. Nothing is unlimited, time is running out, energy is running low, every decision counts and the victory is uncertain even after the end...

There are 2 energy levels in the city and 15 Gravity cards that define the strategy of the game. Each faction has 4 Omnis, 1 Black Hole as base, and 5 Energy Wheels. The Omnis are flying to Energy Spots inside the City, to confront opposite Omnis or to obtain gravity blocks and upgrate energy levels. The Black Holes control the energy of each faction. Each faction use 1-6 energy per round. Play with strategy and bluff to place and activate Energy Wheels on energy Sources. The game ends at the moment that the Counter Gear, which is placed in the center of Atlantis shows 9 (9 rounds). The player who has the greater number of points from the Energy Wheels on the sources on the high energy level wins the game.
