The game has glow in the dark features.

-description from the designer

Try to make the best pizza you can. Take up to seven cards each turn, select an ingredient and be careful to not attract the fly that will ruin your delicious pizza. Get points by making pizzas with the most ingredients, while at least having the base dough. After three rounds, players count their points to be the winner.

Once Kharma starts to accumulate among your friends and family you will want to give it back to them and you won't be able to stop playing. If you want to win, you will have to play your cards wisely, ally and betray other players to accumulate the lowest Kharma score possible at the end of each round and win the game.

Based on the mechanics of the classic Middle Eastern game Tamalou, it combines scoring based on set collection, easy gameplay and cards with special abilities.

Embark on an extraordinary odyssey as a humble coal miner entrusted with the perilous mission to delve into the depths of the ancient Hazard Cave, where treacherous dangers and boundless treasures intertwine. With unwavering determination, carve three mineshafts in pursuit of hidden gold, defying the fate that has befallen countless others. Your quest is to emerge triumphant, laden with the greatest riches ever unearthed, and etch your name as the revered Hazard Miner.

Each turn you will take a set of three actions: Draw a new card, Place a card in a mineshaft, or collect gold from the a mineshaft. You must balance the risk of hazard cards with the reward of mining for more gold.

The game ends either when the mineshafts are completely full of cards or the player has collected 6 times. You will then rank the amount of gold you have collected against the other miners scores to see where you rank on your quest to become the hazard miner.

November 1970, an auction of Egyptian antiquities brings together the cream of the art world, in a hotel in the Swiss Alps.

The centerpiece: a statuette of the goddess Bastet.

But the day before the sale, everything rushes. A snowstorm prevents all communication with the outside world, a jazz concert takes an unexpected turn, tensions arise, not to mention the threat of an ancestral curse.

In the atmosphere of a luxury hotel, you will go in search of evidence. Thanks to a wallet of Swiss francs, you can buy certain information (staff testimonials, translation of certain documents, access to certain rooms or documents of the hotel), and even search the rooms of suspects!

During your investigation, you will have to solve puzzles and decrypt coded messages in collaborative mode to access additional clues.

This will allow many interactions between the players and will spice up the evening even more because the culprit will have to discreetly defeat this mission, without being unmasked.

Contents:
6 character booklets including press articles, map of the hotel, tickets, biography, agenda, advice and tips, etc...
    
1 organization booklet including:
- a notice & advice section
- an outcome and solution part
 30 large-format cards (testimonials, clues, search of the hotel, chapters of history)
     
1 guide for the perfect investigator
     
1 mini Art'cheo magazine
     
Access to the collaborative mission section on our website.

-translated from Kickstarter campaign

---

Novembre 1970, une vente aux enchères d'antiquités égyptiennes réunit tout le gratin du monde de l'art, dans un hôtel des Alpes suisses.

La pièce maîtresse : une statuette de le déesse Bastet.

Mais la veille de la vente, tout se précipite. Une tempête de neige empêche toute communication avec l'extérieur, un concert de jazz prend une tournure inattendue, des tensions se dessinent, sans oublier la menace d'une malédiction ancestrale.

Dans La Malédiction de Bastet, tous les invités sont suspects mais leur identité est confidentielle.

Vous incarnerez des personnages venus des 4 coins du monde.

Un homme, britannique et élégant

Un femme, franco-égyptienne et romancière

Un homme, égyptien et ambitieux

Une femme, suisse et émotive

Un homme, suisse et opportuniste

Une femme, égyptienne, froide et hautaine

How are Rainbows made?? Magical dragons race across sky chasing storms and leaving rainbows in their trail.

Rainbow Dragons is a fun set collection game with 6 color dice that is great for kids but has the strategic depth for adults too.

The game plays 3-6 players, setup is fast, games are quick, easy to learn and makes a great filler game at 25-45 minutes.

On your turn you roll a single rainbow die. It will show one of 6 colors that you can collect and create sets. If you like the die you put it down on your score card.

Otherwise you can offer the die to another player. If they say yes then you give them the die and they add it to their score card. After they accept your die you then roll 2 dice. You can now add those dice to your card or trade them for even more dice.

Players have to use strategy to know when to offer a die, when to say yes and when to say no. If a player says no the die goes back to the dice pool and it is lost to the player who rolled it. A player can continue to roll dice as long as the other players are willing to trade.

Once on your turn you can turn in a set of dice for points. You will get more points for higher sets of dice with the largest reward going to a complete rainbow (1 die of each of the colors). Your score is impacted by your neighbors dice so you are always watching what the other dragons are doing.

When you turn in a set you advance your marker down the scoring track towards the win. First player to the end wins the game immediately. The game also ends with the 3 rainbow or when there are no more dice when a player needs to roll. At this point the player with the most points wins.

-description from designer
A new way to play Carcassonne. You get the map and use your existing game material. This map will be played with the base game of Carcassonne and/or it’s expansions.

A Boggle-like game made for a classroom setting. Large letter cards are placed in a pocket chart that can be hung on a well, placed in a 3x3 or 4x4 grid.  Students then write as many words as they can see in the grid. Each word must start with one letter and then use adjacent letters in sequence, not using any letter card twice in the same word.

—user summary

boardgame: ABC / Animal Bird Fish Mini Card Games
description: This is a set of two Ed-U-Cards games -- A B C Mini Cards, and Animal Bird Fish Mini Card Game -- in a single plastic carrying case.  They both use miniature cards, 57 x 40 mm (2 1/4" x 1 9/16").

Revolutions and appreciation for traditions, fight for freedom, for rights and the pursuit of truth: Tertúlia is a game where you will gather and get to know great thinkers, their ideas, and the events that changed humanity!

So get your cards ready, because here's another opportunity to gather friends, impress your family, liven up the classroom, and even impress on a date!

Communists, liberals, conservatives, feminists, illuminists, and Greeks: these are the schools that compete for precious points to win the game. With these thinkers in hand, you can devise strategies to publish books and stage long-awaited historical events.

But beware: the competition is unpredictable and your opponents will want to put obstacles in your way to try to score points before you. Card negotiations and alliances can disrupt whoever is in the lead...

boardgame: Neón y Acero: Sombras de Ankar
description: Expansion for Neón y Acero, from NB Games. Introduces 63 new cards, with 3 hero cards, 40 resources cards, 10 exploration cards and 10 unique cards. Also, lets play games of four players with a single copy of the game.

Shuffle both stacks of head and tail cards, keeping them in separate piles. Place both piles facedown in the center. The player going first draws one head and one tail from each stack. Then the player begins talking like the head of the animal and walking like the tail of the other animal. Players must refrain from describing the animals.

Whoever successfully guess the combination wins one point. First player to get 10 points wins the game.

Includes 40 cards and rules.

Minimum of three players-one quizmaster and two players to answer the questions.

The quizmaster draws from the pile and rolls the dice to select which question will be answered.

Then the quizmaster reads out the appropriate question fro the teams to buzz in and answer.

Once the number of pre-agreed questions have been asked, the cards are tallied. The player(s) with the most cards wins

Includes 240 challenging questions on the arts, history, tv & film, food, celebrities, general knowledge.

Shuffle the cards and place them in the center of the table face down.
First player to unscramble the word(s) correctly wins the card.
The first player who gets 5 cards wins the game.

Includes 36 cards and instructions.

Take turns asking each other general knowledge questions. Each correct answer is worth 1 point. First player/team to 20 points wins.

Includes: 140 cards, rules

Sword & Sorcery is a fully cooperative fantasy board game where players control legendary Heroes with unique powers. Recalled from the dead and made immortal by the will of the Three Gods, the Heroes embark on a quest to destroy false and corrupted deities, fighting across land & sea.

In Sword & Sorcery: Abyssal Legends, The immortal heroes, reunited once more after months of solitude, sail their ship to the anchorage of the Ancient Mariner port to investigate a mysterious death at the Heavenshine monastery, but also to desperately seek the key of a terrible dream. A shared vision, set in a sunken city, that suddenly twists into a nightmare full of blasphemous tentacles, horrifying images, and ends with the silhouette of a titanic creature - the Great Elder One!

The Abyssal Legends storyline take place fifty years after the events of the Immortal Souls saga.  This new expansion is compatible with both game sagas: Immortal Souls and Ancient Chronicles — so all available heroes and enemies of both seasons can be integrated into Abyssal Legends and vice-versa to further enhance gameplay.

At least one Core Set {Immortal Souls or Ancient Chronicles} and an ACT II Campaign expansion {Darkness Falls or Northwind Tales} are required to play the six Quests included in this expansion.

Tricky War is a card game mixing predictive bid, trick-taking and war.

In Tricky War, bid on uncertain duels and make the right choices to avoid losing too many points during the war phase.

Goal:
End up with the most “point” tokens when a player loses their last one.

Components:
- A 52-card deck (4 suits, each with cards from 1 to 13)
- 40 “point” tokens (you can also use a pen & paper)
- 20 “bidding” tokens (you can also use a pen & paper)
- Something to remember the trump suit(tokens, unused cards…)

A game is played in several rounds. The game is over as soon as at least one player does not have any “point” tokens left.

A round is played in several phases:

1. Bidding
2. Choosing the trump suit
3. War
4. End of round

When at least one player has lost all their “point” tokens, the game is over and the player who has the most "point" tokens is the winner.

The rules of Tricky come with some variants to add more control, a partnership mode and a solo mode.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Love finding images in the clouds and sharing your findings with others? Do you like party games with simple rules? 
Туманный намёк ("Hazy Hint") is just such a game.

In each round, the active player has to find an image in one of the clouds on a given theme and voice it. Other players try to guess which cloud he has in mind. The more players choose your choice the better. But beware, not everyone thinks as you do!

Pick and guess associations by theme, reveal the other players' hints and you'll win.

—description from the publisher (translated)

It is the year 801 BC in the Neo-Assyrian Empire, Ancient Mesopotamia. Queen Sammuramat’s capital Kalhu is being attacked by the Enemy, and her bannermen are rallying to form a Queen’s Guard to defend the city.

This card game simulates a field battle outside the Gates of Kalhu. You will need two decks: One for the Enemy and one for Queen’s Guard of 2-4 players. ♠ represent Military, ♦ represent Supplies, ♣ represent Diviners (Priests) and ♥ represent Medicine.

The game is played by taking tricks, where the first-played card decides what suit to follow. Winning a ♠ trick means that the opponent side will take Military Power losses. The team that gets the opponent side down to Zero Military wins the game.

-description from designer

boardgame: Hunter x Hunter: En Route Vers Greed Island
description:
Embody one of your Hunter heroes! In this cooperative game, players embody Hunters that dive into the Greed Island game. They'll need to find all 100 Greed Island Collection cards to win. The Hunters will have to face many opponents and solve many missions to find them before Bomber!

The Genthru team is approaching the end of the game, they are only missing a few cards to collect! Gon, Kirua, Biscke, and their allies will have to take on perilous challenges and survive hard battles to stop their progression and finish the game for the very first time in history.

On your turn, each hunter can choose an action from the following:


     Resolve a mission to be rewarded with health or money.
     Fight and opponent for a chance to push Bomber back or gain a Greed Island Collection card.
     Shop bonus cards to get benefits.
     Rest to gain money and discard a mission or opponent.


The object of the game is to reveal the 8 Greed Island Collection cards, which correspond to a total of 100 Spell cards, before the Bomber group has reached the end of the Bomber group Progress track or before 3 Hunters are KO'd.

The Factory 42: Specialists and Golems Expansion will introduce five new elements.


    Specialists - In a community where everyone is equal, the Specialists are just a bit more equal. Due to their extraordinary skills, they don’t have to wait in line, nor are they subject to the arbitrary rulings of the Commissariat. They are the crème de la crème of all the workers of Odrixia.



    Cargadors - These massive metal golems operate tirelessly to serve and improve the community. They are excellent at any work to which they’re assigned but cannot deviate from a given task without supervision. Every Odrixian citizen is proud of their Cargador which is widely acknowledged as the best that technology can offer.



    Patriot Box - Every good citizen knows that informing the Commissariat of illegal activity is encouraged and such diligence is rewarded with food.



    Calendar Clerk - What the clerk gives the clerk can take away. The Calendar Clerk meddles with the Event cards.



    And the sixth player, complete with meeples (in stylish silver) and all required components. A sixth player makes the competition even tenser and forces players to think of alternative ways to achieve their goals since some locations will now be full quite early in the placement phase.

Stroganov: Big Box is a deluxe big Box that includes the Stroganov base game, as well as the Turukhan expansion.

In the 16th century, Russia began to expand eastwards to develop the vast expanses of Siberia. This phase in history is closely associated with the name "Stroganov".

In Stroganov, players try to collect the best furs to gain wealth and fame as they move across the vastness that is Siberia. They will journey through Siberia in spring, summer, and autumn before returning home each winter. After four years (rounds), the player who has best utilized their actions and collected the most victory points wins.

Turukhan expands on the base game by adding 4 different modules that you can combine at will to spice up your game of Stroganov, offering a lot of replayability.

—description from publisher
family: Versions & Editions: Big Box Versions of Individual Games

description:
Pond Poker is a fast paced card game for 2-8 players, ages 8 and up. It is easy to learn and quick to play. Game play is about 30 minutes.

Here's how it works: deal 5 pond cards to each player face down, then lay 6 more face up in the center of the table to form the pond. Each round, a player draws an action card and reads it out loud, setting off a frenzy of activity as players rush to complete the action within the nail-biting 10-second time limit. Beware, though - if you can't complete the action in time, you'll have to draw a penalty card!

As the rounds progress, the game only gets more intense with unexpected actions that can turn the game on its head at any moment.  At the end of 10 Action Rounds, all players score their remaining hand with the Score Sheet. Player with the most points wins! With 10 exciting action rounds, Pond Poker is the perfect game to add some chaotic fun back to your game night.

-description from publisher

Island Expansion pack. This expansion features a full 68x40 cm (27x16 in) game board that depicts the island of Taiwan. With this expansion, players can complete the panorama as the game goes on.

The board maps out some of the most important parts of the Taiwanese landscape: the Central Mountain Range, Huadong Valley, and Chianan Plain. Players can place terrain tiles following the original natural wonders' locations, or create a new version of the island on their own.

Aside from the game board, the Island Expansion pack adds in new animal species and real-life disaster situations for players to contend with. Players will also face the notorious typhoon disaster or East Asia.

Choose your hidden pirate role, convince the captain to pick you for their crew, and beat the others to the buried treasure! But watch out for the traitor - they will keep it all for themselves.

SlackJack is a fast-paced, hidden role game. Each round, one player takes the role of captain who must choose 1-2 pirate players to join them, aiming to form the best Blackjack hand. Every other player has hidden roles with powers and values that could be favorable or treacherous for their crew. Can you convince the captain that you are the right pick? Be warned: if the Captain picks the devious SlackJack, he’ll steal the whole pile of gold for himself.

Once the captain picks their crew, the remaining players form their own treasure hunting party and all roles are revealed. The team that is closest to 21 in pirate value will all win gold for their own pockets. Pass the captain role to the next player and head back out on another hunt. The game ends when any player reaches 5 gold: exactly enough to retire to a tropical, traitor-free island.

-description from publisher

Trivia, tasks, truths, and trick questions!  The goal is to answer before anyone else does to win the award.  The twist is that if you try to answer at the same time as another player, you both lose your collected white cards!  Oh, and there are also these pink cards! You can't lose pink cards, so if you win them, you get to keep them forever - well, till the end of the game!

Psst... the person who thinks they're the smartest in the room almost always loses!

-description from the back of the box
A conversation game that plays a bit like Uno, with cards which cause reversals in order of play and other surprises. The first player to get rid of their cards wins.

Prepare for a gaming experience like no other with 490 Cards! Combining dice, cards, and coins, this innovative game challenges players to forgive their debts and drive them down to zero.

In this exciting world, each player starts with 490 debts-to-forgive ("D2F"). The objective? Be the first to reduce your debts to zero and claim victory. Utilize dice rolls, multiplication factors, and strategic decisions to decrease your D2F or increase your opponents'. To simplify calculations, we've designed the 490 Cards® Scoreboard, a free app available this fall on your favorite app store.

The comprehensive 490 Cards Starter Kit provides everything you need to dive into the game. With 252 cards, including Inventory cards, Doubters and Disciples Domain (D3) cards, Grace cards, Grudge cards, and more, the kit ensures a varied and immersive gameplay experience. Eight dice of different types, including the custom 12-sided block dice, add further depth to your strategies.

But that's not all! The Starter Kit also includes 12 Compel Coins, allowing you to influence other players' actions and introducing additional layers of strategy. With the Simple Start Card, Comprehensive Rules Booklet, Scorepad, and Rigid and Tuck Boxes included, you'll have everything you need to enhance your game night.

Additionally, 490 Cards is not just a standalone game. It's a TCG/CCG with a booster box containing 36 packs of 12 cards. Collectors can amass the full 490 cards associated with the Alpha Instantiation release. The Full Series I boasts an impressive 1200 cards, with subsequent releases, Beta Invasion and Gamma Illumination, expanding the possibilities and rounding out Series I.

490 Cards. It's All About Forgiveness... No Trivia. The Possibilities are Endless. & There's a card for that...

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Bah! Humbug! and the 12 Games of Christmas
description:
Bah! Humbug! and the 12 Games of Christmas is a collection of 13 original holiday card games by 13 designers, reimplementing and expanding Bah! Humbug!: A Twelve Days of Christmas Bluffing Game  into a versatile box of games. At the heart of the collection is a 78-card "12 days of Christmas" deck featuring Victorian-inspired animal art. The box also contains 34 wooden pieces, a detailed rulebook, and several additional card sets which may be combined with the base deck in various ways.

The 2023 PnP/2025 boxed edition includes rules for these original games:


    Animal Menagerie (2 players) a card-drafting and set collection game by Emily Willix & Clark Willix
    Christmas Parade (2 players) a card manipulation game by Felix Falk
    Deckbuild the Halls (3-4 players) a trick-taking and deckbuilding game by Duncan Hecht
    Dress the Mantel (2 players) a tile-laying game by David Waldman
    The Duke's Daughters (1-4 players) a solo/cooperative game by Koyomi Kawasumi
    The Giving Spirit (3-6 players) a trick-taking game by Evan Halbert
    Ladies Dancing (1-3 players) a rondel and set collection game by Emily Willix & Clark Willix
    O Christmas Tree (2-4 players) a racing and press-your-luck game by Pedro Kerouac & Jan Spodek
    Post the Parcels (3-6 players) a pick-and-pass set collection game by James Gjaltema
    Snapdragon (2-3 players) a dexterity and press-your-luck game by Chris Stone (II)
    Swindling Suitors (3-4 players) a card-shedding and bluffing game by Galen McCown
    Woodland Winter (1-2 players) a solo/co-op strategy game by Ren Vice
family: Theme: Mail / Stamps / The Post Office

description:
Unfortunately for Witty, this isn’t the first time that she’s had to crush demons with her bare hands, but unfortunately for the demonic forces on board, she has a pulse blaster and combat mace this time around. A Warrant Officer on the ship Hades Hold, Witty made herself useful in many areas particularly in communications, fitness training and combat exercises. Her competitiveness, physical prowess and strength allowed her to show up many others on board in tests of skill, but she always maintained strong bonds and cared for those around her. Inspiring a community of like minded individuals who encourage and lift each other up, Witty found herself in a position of a leader of sorts for many on board, just by being herself. When the incursion happened, it was a terrible familiar feeling. Witty didn’t hesitate to rush into the depths of the ship to see if she could save anyone, and brutalize any creatures along the way.

In this official Demon Ship expansion pack you will receive: 

     1 resin printed Witty Survivor
     1 Base
     Fun rules to use her in your games of Demon Ship!

He's here to sneak around the ship!

In this official Demon Ship expansion pack you will receive:

     1 resin printed Malev Survivor
     1 Base
     Fun rules to use him in your games of Demon Ship!

Introduction:Leblin (from German: Lebenslinie, meaning "lifeline") is a drawless annihilation game for two players: Black and White. It is played on the intersections (points) of an initially empty square board. The recommended board size is 19 points per side, but boards of 13, 15, or 17 are also acceptable. There is also an off-board location called a prison. Each player has access to a sufficient supply of stones of their own color.

				
				
					Definitions:
     A lifeline is a maximal path of empty points in a straight line from a stone to the board edge or to a friendly stone. Stones can share their lifelines among several friendly stones without the need for empty points between them.
     A territory is one or more empty points connected to each other. You own a territory if all stones connected to it are of your color.
     An incursion is a player's placement into the opponent's territory. An incursion is minimal if it causes exactly one enemy group to be moved to the prison.


				
				
					Turns:Black plays first, and then turns alternate. On your turn, perform exactly one of the following actions:


     Place a stone of your color on an empty spot and then move all dead enemy groups to the prison. If the only stone you remove is the one you just placed, your placement is illegal.
     Remove an enemy stone from the prison. If the prison contains stones of both colors, remove pairs of stones of opposite colors until only one color remains, or none at all.


You cannot make a minimal incursion if your opponent has just made a minimal incursion anywhere on the board.

				
				
					End of the game:You win if the last enemy group is eliminated from the board.

To balance the game, before starting, the first player places several black stones in the prison, and then the second player chooses a side. This balancing method is called komi pie. For handicap games, the weaker player takes black and opens by placing a number of black stones on the board proportional to the difference in skill between the players.

—description from designer

boardgame: Шакал Архипелаг: Остров приливов (Jackal Archipelago: Tide Island)
description:
This is the new island for the game Jackal: Archipelago. You'll have an adventure on a tidal island. The palm trees are tall and the shores go under water at high tide.

Tide Island has, you might guess, tides - if you come across one, be kind enough to remove the three squares from the surf strip. A palm tree has appeared (not just a tile, but physically, in the form of a wooden figurine): you can climb on it and be in the lodge. But with the gold on the palm can not, you have to leave all the coins at the bottom - they are also in the set.

Mix this set with the ones you already have to build even bigger islands.

—description from the publisher (translated)

boardgame: Descent: Journeys in the Dark (Second Edition) – Trials of Frostgate
description:
The Trials of Frostgate are not for the faint of heart. Only the bravest shall be named Champion.

Trials of Frostgate transports 2-4 heroes to the Free City of Frostgate, where the Northern Tribes have gathered for feasting, dancing, and sacred rituals, including the famed trials. In four new challenges, the heroes of Terrinoth face grueling tests of courage and strength. Warriors will earn glory and tattoos of their totemic animals to commemorate their victory. Rise to the challenge. Experience a new adventure and face the Trials of Frostgate!
family: Game: Descent – Journeys in the Dark

boardgame: Descent: Journeys in the Dark (Second Edition) – The Delve
description:
If any have escaped the hidden realm of The Caretaker, their tale has never been told.

The Delve is an adventure for 2-4 heroes in which you must explore and survive the realm of the Caretaker. Collect loot and gain skills as you advance through a collection of randomly selected stages. As you expand your Descent 2nd Edition collection, you unlock new maps, monsters, and boss fights—untold combinations of heroes, classes, monsters, stages, and loot await. Experience a unique adventure each time you enter, and hopefully exit, The Delve!
family: Game: Descent – Journeys in the Dark

description:
Experience the thrill of Battle Royale with this card game. Be the last player alive, eliminating the rest of the players that you will find on the map. To do this, players will have to explore places and equip themselves with weapons, equipment and accessories on a map that will reduce its exploration area during the course of the game.

—description from the designer

The game consists of cards with silly and/or weird questions. On a turn, a player draws a card and reads one of those questions. Then everyone will write down an answer to the question. Afterwards, the same question is asked to ChatGPT. All answers, including the AI answer, are voted on – whoever gets most votes, wins the round. After 5 rounds, whoever won most rounds wins the game.

Solar Fleet Skirmish is a simple ruleset to get you playing a fleet-based spaceship skirmish game in a matter of minutes. While the rules are quite simple, they allow for a surprising amount of depth and replayability.

For fans of Star Trek, Battlefleet Gothic, Battlestar Galactica, or any kind of naval fleet game.

SFS allows you to develop a fleet with matched play of a variety of ship types, armed with various weapons, armour, and equipment.

SFS also includes several scenarios to get you playing right away!

-description from publisher's website

boardgame: Sabretache Napoleonics: A Game of Generalship and Tactics for the Napoleonic Wars
description: Command & control dominated rules for Corps level and above games. The units are the traditional ones- infantry battalion, cavalry regiment and artillery battery- but they are controlled in brigade groups by orders given with a few keywords. These are factored into a Tactical Engagement Chart that determines the type of engagement and frees the player from low level tactical decisions, allowing him to concentrate on the overall strategy.

Postcard Kursk is a fast, simple, but decision-demanding little hex-and-counter board-wargame about the 5th July 1943 Operation Citadel German Offensive. Hitler and his generals had misgivings about but prosecuted anyway, to catastrophic losses which decisively weakened the German Wehrmacht and then enabled the Russians to sweep all the way to the Dnieper River and even take Kiev at the end of 1943.

8 Russian and 7 German offensive and reserve - maneuver - units. Infantry implicitly in fortification lines on both sides.
Alternating turns, Germans first: move UnDisrupted units and then attack.
Same simple odds and Disruption-momentum combat system as in Postcard Brittany and Postcard Bulge, but additionally attacking odds can be rounded *up* in some cases.
8 turns.  Game ends at the end of Turn 8 Russian player-turn.
Winning – more points than at start – cities last occupied and in supply: Belgorod and Orel worth 1 each – other cities 2 each. 4 points more is a strategic victory worth 2 games.
Print off onto A4/8.5x11 for the units and A3/11x17 for the map, for more comfortable gameplay.
As with my other postcard games, anyone is free to print off (unaltered) copies en masse in postcard A6/4x6 or A5/5x8 size and format, to mail/give to family and friends.
Anyone interested in commercially publishing/selling this for profit, I have publication rights - this is not public domain - so check with me about licensing.

-description from designer
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description: Jungle Speed Collector is a new version of the famous addictive card game combining observation, speed and reflexes. Defend Megatropolis is a Solo (single player) ‘City Defense’ card game, with action selection and hand management.

In Defend Megatropolis, you will move your superhero around the city discarding Power Cards to defeat Threats, all while trying to foil the Villain and his Doom Device, the DoomBot! You win if you locate the Villain and defeat them! You lose if there are 4 DoomBot cards out on the playing area at the beginning of your turn OR a Location is destroyed. Careful! Breaking news! is the essential add-on for ScandalOh! It is a book for the most seasoned journalists that brings you:

- 36 exclusive games, specifically selected for you to enjoy investigating to the fullest.

Step into the world of Sunlight, a roll and write game that will transport you to the arid desert where cacti thrive. This game is perfect for players who love strategy and are looking for a fun and engaging way to spend an afternoon or evening. With a cactus theme, players will get to embrace the beauty of the desert and collect points by strategically placing their cacti on the grid.

The objective of the game is simple: collect as many points as possible by placing your cacti in the best position on the grid. Each turn, players will roll a set of dice that will determine the type and the location of their cacti. They must then decide where to place their cacti on the grid, taking into account the placement of other cacti and the scoring opportunities available. As the game progresses, the sun will move across the sky, casting its light on different sections of the grid. Only cacti that are in the sunlight will earn points at the end of the round, adding an extra layer of strategy to the game.

For players who prefer to play on their own, Sunlight also features a solo mode where you can play against an AI opponent, Nyx. In this mode, players will still roll the dice and place their cacti on the grid, but their decisions will also impact the AI's score. The AI will make its own decisions based on the player's moves. With its solo mode, Sunlight is the perfect game for players who want to test their skills and strategy against a challenging opponent, even when they are by themself.

-description from designer

description:
RUN ME is a fast-paced, strategic card game that is based on the popular rummy gameplay format, with a unique Linux command theme. It is played with a custom deck of 54 cards. The objective of the game is to win by forming valid combinations of cards.

The game's core cards are 20 command cards that represent Linux terminal commands. The 18 Option cards add more options to the game, and the 12 Path cards provide different ways for players to complete their combinations. The four Special cards increase the game's complexity.

The gameplay of RUN ME is simple, yet challenging. Each player is dealt five cards, and the remaining cards are placed face down to form a draw pile. Players take turns to draw one card from the draw pile and either discard a card from their hand and play a valid combination of cards. The game develops as players strategically discard or keep cards in hand, making calculations based on the cards they have, as well as the ones they still need to form a valid combination.  It’s perfect for Linux enthusiasts and rummy fans alike.

-description from designer

description:
Ultra Quick Pro Basketball is really two games in one.

We offer you the familiar method employed in Ultra Quick College Hoops, which resolves a game in two rolls of the dice, and a quarter-by-quarter method, which closely resembles the method employed in Ultra Quick Hockey.  The Quarter-by-Quarter method has provisions for "Big Games" as well as "Bad Games" and features a very unique and simple season normalization process.

We added the quarter-by quarter method as it provides a little more drama as the game unfolds, and only adds a slight bit of gaming time. The quarter-by-quarter method also allows you to play cross-era match-ups efficiently and accurately, with our season normalization ratings, so you can easily match-up teams from yesterday and today and get unbiased results.
Both methods are accurate and as the name implies, both methods play ultra-quick!

Each season has ratings for both methods of play.

The Printed game ships with the rules, charts, random number sheets and two six-sided dice.


        To Play, a season yearbook is required**

Welcome to "Cursed Gifts", a quirky card game where the goal is as amusing as it is devious: dish out "Cursed Gifts" to your pals and see them squirm! It's like a fun twist on Secret Santa where the gifts are meant to be dreadful!

Get ready to unveil a side of your friends you never knew existed... it's devilishly entertaining!

Each player is armed with a hand of 7 cards. Each turn, they have the chance to play up to 3 cards, but beware! Only one of these can be a "Cursed Gift", a hilarious surprise to any chosen opponent. It's a cunning game of strategic gift-giving!

Watch out for the danger zone! If a player accumulates 10 points in their Cursed Area, they're out of the game. To clinch the victory, you have to be the last prankster standing.

To aid you in your journey to becoming the ultimate trickster, you've got an assortment of cards at your disposal. From crafty Treacheries and robust Defenses to valuable Draws, humorously pointless Useless Gifts, and lifesaving Antidotes.

"Cursed Gifts" is all about action and reaction. When you're targeted (or even when you're not), you can respond by playing defenses, bouncing back Treacheries or Cursed Gifts, guarding yourself, or even sneakily stealing cards and swapping Cursed areas.

The possibilities for fun and mischief are endless in this light-hearted game. And the illustrations? They're just the icing on the cake, bringing an extra dose of humor to an already immersive game. Dive in and let the fun of "Cursed Gifts" take over!

-description from designer

The players take turns to spinn and move. The Race starts and ends in Baden-Baden. The distance between cities on the track is 40 km. The red numbers on the spinner determine the speed of the cars, while black numbers show the time needed for repair and maintenance. The white half-ring around the spinner with the green spaces symbolizes repair stations A und B. If a player gets on a black space (city), he puts a second meeple on space A or B. and move the second meeple (according to the black numbers on the spinner) to space 12 before he may move his firs meeple (racing car) further.
The first player to complete the round course and arrives back in Baden-Baden wins the game.

Introduction:Les (from Middle English: -les, meaning "without, empty, -less") is a board game for two players: Black and White. It is played at the intersections (points) of an initially empty hexagonal board of triangles. The recommended size is 9 points per side, but boards of 7 or 11 are also valid. Each player has access to a sufficient supply of stones of his own color.

				
				
					Definitions:
     A hole is one or more empty points connected to each other. You own a hole if all the stones connected to it are your color and when you build them you are said to be digging them.
     The size of a hole is the number of empty points it contains.


				
				
					Turns:Black plays first, then turns alternate. On your turn, you perform these actions in the following order:


     Place a stone of your color on an empty point adjacent to friendly and enemy stones simultaneously.
     Place a stone of your color on an empty point not adjacent to any stone.


When neither player can take the second action because the board is filling up, both players take only the first action.

				
				
					End of the game:The game ends when a player runs out of moves, the player winning under the following conditions in order of importance:


     The player with the most holes made up of stones of their assigned color wins.
     In case of a tie, the player with the biggest hole wins, the next biggest holes being used for the tiebreaker.
     In case of a tie in the size of the holes, the player whose biggest hole contains the most stones connected to him wins, the next biggest holes with the most stones connected to him are used for the tiebreaker.


If neither player managed to form a hole, it is a tie (it is very unlikely that any player will form at least one).

To balance the game, before starting, the first player places four starting stones, one black adjacent to a white, and one black one point away from a white. The second player chooses a side.

–description from designer

Bittake is a card game based on trick-taking. The cards in your hand determine "who" and "how many" bits you get. Win the game by guessing the bits to score points and collect the required number of chips as quickly as possible.

Assume that you are musicians in an orchestra. The conductor takes his place at the stand right before the rehearsal starts. You wait with slight trepidation: This rehearsal will decide, who is going to win the golden PARTYtur - a special price, handed out to one of extraordinary knowledge about orchestras in general, the Neue Philharmonie Westfalen (NPW), composers and symphonies. Every section is convinced to win the price. The race is on!

The board does show a track with different categories all around symphonical music, composers and instruments. Some categories require you to play an appropriate track of the enclosed CD (or streamed via a QR-code). You might need to identify the instrument you hear playing or answer questions on the piece you heard. The knowledge category tests your musical knowledge and the conductor category sees you conducting an appropriate beat. You advance 1-3 fields if you answer or solve your task correctly.

First player making it to the finish wins the golden PARTYtur.

In a solo game you play against the conductor and need to reach the finish before the conductor finishes the rehearsal (measured by metronome tokens)

When the goin’ gets tough, the tough get goin’. There are some players who can just turn "it" on when everything's on the line. Keep it close and back yourself into a corner because that’s when your boys are at their best.

Deliver take-out slides and headhunting pitches and knock your opponent’s best players out of the game. Force your opponent to discard players you don't want to see. Hurt them before they can hurt you.

Scour your deck for talent. Sift through your cards to find precisely the players you need when you need them. Then outmaneuver your opponent and overwhelm them with wave after wave of talent.

A cooperative survival game in three acts that uses two regular decks of cards (more if playing with more than two players). Players choose a character out of four classic RPG heroes and try to defeat a deck of enemies.

The story, set in Greek mythology, focuses on two villagers in the outskirts of Athens who find their village under attack by an army of men and monsters. When helps comes unexpectedly, they are given the chance to fight back - and they take the fight to the very Gods who are wrecking their beautiful land.

“Is there anyone who dares travelling from Peking to Paris in the next summer by car?”
Five daring crews with their cars left Peking for Paris on June 10th, 1907, to take up this incredible challenge: a 10,000 miles long trip over trails and mule tracks, across the mountains of China, the Gobi Desert, and the Russian steppes.
Pékin-Paris is a card-driven boardgame for 2-5 players that puts you behind the steering wheel of a vintage car, starting your trip with a limited hand of 4 action cards:
DRIVER: provides 1 coin or 1 gasoline resource.
MECHANIC: buy 1 resource cube from the market.
JOURNALIST: buy 1 new card from the board or play 1 event card.
BREAKING NEWS: recover your played cards and refresh the board.
This deck-building engine will increase your hand with new actions cards, adding much more options to your turn, because the acquired cards will be played as actions, resources or events. Use your resources and coins to get new cards, play events, improve your vehichle's capabilities, gain VPs, draw your trip map through the wildlands, and buy a Certificat d'Honneur. The driver with the most VPs at the end of the race is the winner.

Let's fly.

Introduction:Fursaz (from Proto-Germanic: fursaz, meaning "cascade", "waterfall" or "torrent") is a drawless territorial game for two players: Black and White. It is played on the intersections (points) of an initially empty hexagonal board with triangles. It can be played on a 7–sized board for long games or a 5–sized board for short games. Each player has a sufficient supply of identical stones, each with one color on the front and the other on the back.

				
				
					Definitions:
     A flank is a configuration where two friendly stones in a straight line (along one of the six cardinal directions) are at the same distance from a stone, with the same number of empty points (which can be zero) and no other stones in between.
     To flip is to replace a stone with a stone of the opposite color.
     A territory is one or more empty points flanked by friendly stones that are not flanked by enemy stones. You own a territory if the stones flanking it are your color.


				
				
					Turns:Black plays first, then turns alternate. On your turn, you can pass your turn or perform these actions in the following order:


     Place a stone of your color on an empty point.
     Flip all enemy stones flanked by friendly stones.
     Flip all friendly stones flanked by enemy stones.


If there are more flanked stones when performing the previous actions, perform actions 2 and 3 in that order until no more flanked stones remain at the end of your turn. In each stage, all applicable flips are resolved simultaneously before moving on to the next. If a stone is flanked by both colors in the same stage, the color of the most recent flanking that affects it is applied.

				
				
					End of the game:The game ends when both players pass their turn consecutively. At the end of the game, points are counted by adding up the friendly territory and the friendly stones on the board.

The player with the highest score in the final position wins.

To balance the game, before starting, the first player places a black stone on an empty point and the second player chooses whether to play with that stone or to continue with the white ones. This balancing method is called the pie rule.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Age of Steam Expansion: 18xxBelgium – The Road to Mechelen
description:
18xxBelgium: The Road to Mechelen is an expansion for Age of Steam, commissioned by 18xxBelgium in celebration of the fifth anniversary of 18xxBelgium.

The Road to Mechelen is a limited edition and is released as a paper-only version of 100 copies for the Spring Convention 2023 of 18xxBelgium in Mechelen, Belgium.

The object of the game is to raise money:

     by buying securities, to benefit from the advantages they provide (dividends, bonuses, etc.)
     by drawing certain "instruction cards"
     by playing horse racing and roulette


The winner is the first player who manages to total one million francs, or who has the most money, his opponents having gone bankrupt.

Safari Photo magazine is looking for its new wildlife reporter. You are sent to Africa to take the most beautiful pictures of the animals of the savanna. The photographer with the most points will be awarded the coveted position of reporter in the prestigious editorial office of Safari Photo magazine. But beware, you are not the only one to covet this position, and wild animals are not so easy to photograph.

A game of challenge for two players in which those Mighty Marvel Super Heroes battle the most awesome array of Vicious Villains ever assembled! A coin is spun to decide who plays first. Each player receives a shuffled deck of their trump cards.

Each trump card has three characteristics, the first player reveals their card, announces a characteristic, then the second player announces the corresponding characteristic and both cards are revealed. The lower scoring trump card is forfeit to the other player and adds it to a win pile.

The player who captures all the trump cards wins.

In the year 2350, humanity's exploration fleet finds itself stranded in an inhospitable and alien galaxy, far from home. As the commander of this stranded fleet, you stand at the edge of a wormhole that, if piloted correctly, can bring you back to Earth's solar system. However, navigating this wormhole is no easy task, as it is fraught with peril and unforeseen challenges. Only the most courageous and strategic commanders will be able to steer their spaceships through the wormhole and lead their crews back home. Are you prepared to prove your commanding skills and seize the opportunity to return home?

Your fleet comprises substantial motherships representing Earth, the Moon, and Mars, along with smaller companion spaceships. Your objective is to chart a course through the wormhole, aiming to land on a planet or dock at a space station within our home solar system. But beware, a cosmic storm looms, and once all spaceships have ventured into the wormhole, any that remain unlanded, undocked, or outside the safe zone will face destruction.

Can you rise to the challenge, emerge triumphant, and become the commander with the highest glory?

At the start of the game, you have the option to choose between a short, a normal, or a legendary game, which determines the number of missions you will play.

At the beginning of each mission, one starting condition card, one special rule card, and one victory point card are drawn. The starting condition card indicates the composition of your fleet for the upcoming mission, specifying which ships and how many are available to you. The special rule card modifies the game mechanics in a specific way. The victory point card determines the glory point values assigned to different types of ships.

During your turn, you select one of your ships (represented by discs) and flick it, aiming to successfully land the ship in a valid location.

The mission concludes once every player has played all of their ships (flicked all discs). At the end of a mission, each player tallies their glory points. The player with the highest glory points emerges as the winner of that mission. After completing a number of missions equal to the specified requirement for the chosen game mode (best of 3, best of 5, or best of 10), the player who has accumulated enough victories to secure an insurmountable lead becomes the ultimate commander and wins the game.

–description from designer

Mombatuk The Adventurer, Journey Into The Underworld Of Borneo’s Folklore is a roll and write game.

Journey into the underworld of the Island of Borneo’s folklore as you play as a fictional character called Mombatuk. Mombatuk the unwilling hero must travel into the depths of hell to rescue his beloved from the clutches of the Evil “Pulan” ruler of the underworld. In this adventure, takes the player through the first level out of seven levels of the underworld. Fight, find items and survive the first level and beat the big baddie at the end (the gate keeper).

The capital of Avalon is currently at the center of Fire Magic discovery. Mages from all over the land come to the capital to learn about Fire Magic and find new ways of spell casting in the Burning Tower of Avalon. As such, there are many disasters caused primarily by novices practicing their spell casting. To remedy this situation, an experienced group of mages known as "FIRE RESCUE MAGES" was formed by the Council of High Magic. Their role is to combat these accidental fires and rescue anyone trapped within the fiery tower.

Your task as a Fire Rescue Mage is to: Enter the burning tower, Rescue all trapped mages (Victims), and Lead them out of the tower safely. Unfortunately, fire creatures are very attracted to magical fires and can even be summoned by accident, so you will need to be cautious and prepared to combat these creatures while keeping your survivors safe.
