boardgame: Last Full Measure: The Battles of Kernstown
description:
The Battle of First Kernstown, March 23rd 1862.

Jackson moves against a force he supposes he outnumbers and is defeated at Kernstown.  The defeat causes the US to not pull forces from the Valley to reinforce McClellan at Richmond, so, in the end, it's a strategic victory for the Confederacy.

On July 24th 1864, Early on his way to Winchester meets Crook's Army of West Virginia at Kernstown, defeats him, and the Federals retreat across the Potomac as Early launches his raid to Chambersburg PA.

Both battles use simple victory-points for elimination to determine who wins.

Last Full Measure is a free print-and-play game system of battles of the American Civil War. Kernstown is the twelfth battle in the system.

A brigade scale game that uses regiment counters, as opposed to generic line markers, to spread the brigade over multiple hexes according to its size. Ranged artillery fire, cavalry charges, command and control, all in only eight pages of rules. Play aids include Player-aid card with CRT and other charts and tables; Eliminated Units card, 2 Order of Arrival cards, 307 2-sided counters, and a Battle/Scenario booklet.

Kernstown's Battle Book includes Set-ups for both the 1862 and 1864 battles.

The map measures 18" x 23" at a scale of 250 yards per 3/4" hex.

-description from designer

Marble-Pong is played by two players, each sitting at the opposing end of a table. Each player has a container with and incline, which contains space for 10 marbles. Players take turns trying to shoot marbles into their opponent's container. First one to fill all 10 spaces in their opponent's container, wins the game.

It is possible to play with teams.

The owners are out. The way is clear to try to snuggle down in the cosy Poof in the Lounge. Who will reach it and reign supreme? Here, you form your team before the others, and challenge your opponents. But watch out: just because you recruited your team first doesn't mean you'll win!
The game takes place in 2 phases: Building your team of Fluff Balls, then the assault on the Pouffe.

—description from the publisher

Les propriétaires sont de sortie. La voie est libre pour tenter de se blottir dans le douillet pouf du salon. Qui l’atteindra et régnera en maître ? Pour cela, formez votre équipe avant les autres et défiez vos adversaires. Mais attention, ce n’est pas parce que vous avez su recruter votre équipe le premier que vous serez gagnant ! 
Le jeu se déroule en 2 phases : la constitution de votre équipe de Boules de poils, puis l’assaut du Pouf.

—description from the publisher (French)

Look hard and find an identical Mask among the totems on the table, before the others do. You can win a whole totem, but you could also lose yours.
Who’ll be the winner?
The aim is to get as many cards as possible.

Observez et trouvez avant les autres un Masque identique dans les totems présents sur la table. Vous pourrez remporter un totem entier, mais vous pourrez aussi perdre le vôtre.
Qui sera le gagnant ?
But du jeu : remporter le maximum de cartes.

Bestiarium pauperum, or… a bestiary for the poor. A truthful description of our native, Slavic demons. A must-have pocket incunabula for every beasts lover. Take on forest creatures, spawns of the darkest magic, and filthy creatures lurking behind a dumpster. Be careful not to involve yourself in shady business with devils and other foul demons. A path lays ahead of you, even less hospitable than before. But who cares...down with the beasts!

New expansion to Beasts from the Barn expands path deck with 30 new beasts and adds 10 events highly related to Slavic culture and demonology. You'll also find here 6 new characters, 6 market cards, one blank beast card and one blank character card for you to fill in. Toss your two coins!

Beasts from the Barn is a card game mixing two universes - the Witcher and Jakub Wędrowycz's world. It's a mash up between Slavic flavour and rural fantasy. All that spiced with a grain of salt, big portion of satire and beautiful illustrations. Gameplay is full of scams and tricks.

Publisher pre-order bonus: every copy bought via publisher's store contains 11 cards expansion Świniopak ("Swine-pack").

—description from the publisher (translated)

SlideCore 6 is an abstract strategy game for 2 players of at least 8 years of age, with an average playing time of 30 minutes, where players try to form the biggest group comprising of discs of their colour. It is played on a hexagonal board, consisting of 91 spots, 6 on each side. Players place discs of both colours on the board, while keeping in mind the Core.

—description from the designer
Challenge mode for El Lavarropas, delivered in the May 2023 Ludoty Box.

Library of Jakkarth is a magical dungeon crawler labyrinth adventure for two to five players. You take on the role of Spellcasters who venture into the dark halls of the library and search the numerous rooms for rare spells and mysterious items. Your goal: find 5 magic keys of the same kind and be the first to use them to open the gate and win the game!

During your turn, you can perform up to 3 actions. The fundamental action is movement. Throw two dice and walk up up to as many fields as you rolled. When you reach the edge of a room, reveal the next room card and place it adjacent to yours. In each room you'll find bookshelves - when standing next to one, perform the loot action and draw up to two cards from the pile. You can loot not only the aforementioned keys, but also spells, instant spells and different magical relics.
Another basic action is casting spells: play one of your spell cards and perform its effect. There's a wide variety of spells. You can for example steal 2 cards from other players, teleport to a specific room, switch places with other game pieces or create fire in a room of your choosing. Other minor actions include equipping a relic card for a permanent buff or manipulating an obstacle in the nearby labyrinth.

But not only the other adventurers will use magic to obstruct your quest and try to win the game before you. The library itself is protected by something ancient - the guardians. Once their room cards are unveiled, they will focus and you and hunt you down. The more you discover the library, the more numerous and powerful they become. For each active guardian in the game, they gain an additional die for their walk actions. So, run away, use spells and obstacles to slow them down or shift their focus to your competitors.

The game ends when one of you manages to escape the library by opening the gate of knowledge with the five keys required. Or, the guardians catch all of you and trap you in the labyrinth for all eternity.

-description from the publisher

boardgame: Qing Tian: The Legend of Immortals Killer
description:
"Qing Tian : The Legend of Immortals Killer" is a cooperative game of card-driven combat set for 1-4 players. It is a legacy game that includes a total of 30 scenarios with rich storylines and ups and downs. All the scenes involved in the scenarios will be presented in the form of Pop-up Map. To explore the full story players could challenge them one by one.

There are 5 different sects in “Qing Tian : The Legend of Immortals Killer“, which have different powerful abilities. Some are good at dealing devastating damage to enemies, while others are good at backing up their teammates. When players begin their journey with the game, they need to select one of the available characters from these different sects. Each sect has its unique set of martial cards and a kind of Lingqi that it proficients in.

In each round, characters perform different actions by playing martial cards during their turn. Characters can flexibly select the martial cards they want to play and all of the martial cards will return to their hand at the end of round. Characters and monsters will act in sequence according to their speed.

Players need to take their characters on a series of quests to defeat the immortal gods. On their journey they'll face many challenges, including a large variety of enemies and horrific conspiracies. Throughout the game, players can accumulate jade (represents money) by killing monsters. The jade could be used to purchase gear、totum and martial cards. After each scenarios, characters could record their states and when the next game start, they could use it directly. And the game prepare lots of disposable components to change the map or the martial cards. This will allow players to have a fresh feeling in different scenarios.

boardgame: War of the Ring: The Card Game – Against the Shadow
description:
Inspired by the best-selling and award-winning War of the Ring board game, War of the Ring – The Card Game immerses players in the quest of The Lord of the Rings. The game allows them to explore their own version of the trilogy, using uniquely designed card decks representing the strengths and weaknesses of the peoples and characters who fought in the War of the Ring.

Its first expansion, Against the Shadow, enables players to experience the adventure alone or cooperatively. The player(s) control the Free Peoples, using the normal rules and cards included in the War of the Ring – The Card Game.

Automated players control the Shadow, using the new decks, which entirely replace the cards included in the core set and which are tailored for solo/cooperative play. In conjunction with a simple flowchart, the new Shadow decks provide a sophisticated challenge for one or two players.

-description from publisher
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable
family: Series: War of the Ring (Nexus/Ares Games)

boardgame: Ми-ми-мишки: Считаем с Кешей (Mi-mi-mishki: Count with Kesha)
description:
It is a versatile puzzle game for children and adults.

Indomitable Kesha glued together photos of himself and his friends to create captivating challenges. Help Tuchka and Lisichka solve them!

1. Place the task card in the center.
2. Gather the required set of friends on your cards.
3. Score 8 victory points and become the winner!

The year is 2301, and you are operating an agency to travel in time to correct issues created by beings called the Altuli.

On the Edge of Time is a cooperative 1-3 player game (with a 1 v all option).

The players attempt to complete missions to protect their timeline. These missions must be completed before the temporal stress reaches a critical mass, the universe splits and is destroyed.

During the game, two missions will be revealed, the Altuli will land on the game board and restrict your resources, temporal stress will be evaluated, and then players will take their turn.

In the player phase, individuals can spend credits to acquire units, use their temporal energy to travel and eliminate Altuli and utilize research to develop technologies.

Actions taken back in time will affect future points in time, sometimes with unintended consequences. Complete 6 missions and one final mission to succeed. Failure occurs if the temporal stress becomes excessive.

-description from designer

boardgame: A Brilliant Fight: The Battle of Queenston Heights, October 13, 1812
description:
“We shall save our own land—wipe away part of the score of our past disgrace, get excellent barracks and winter quarters, and at least be prepared for an early campaign another year.”—General Van Rensselaer’s summation of the benefits of gaining a secure foothold in the Niagara Peninsula before the onset of winter, September 1812.

A key element in the American campaign to take control Canada was securing the Niagara Peninsula in 1812.  This endeavor was increasingly important to the US war effort following the surrender of an entire American army at Detroit during the summer of 1812. However, a crossing of the Niagara River near Lake Ontario was guarded by Fort George, while those near Buffalo near Lake Erie were dominated by the Queenston Heights. The Americans gambled that a quick early morning assault on Queenston Heights could gain them a bridgehead that could very well lead to the capture of Ontario. Can you do as well, or better, than your historical counterparts? Learn and enjoy!

—description from the designer
family: Series: Battles for Canada (High Flying Dice)

description:
At the far end of the Glug nebula, third star system on the left, you’ll find a cube-shaped planet called Ghoul.

It may look like an idyllic utopia, but zoom in and you’ll find it overrun by dim-witted monsters pulling ridiculous faces.

For an age, tribes have formed volatile alliances and battled for control of Ghoul.

You, the hero, have formed a shabby band of monsters to settle the score once and for all, but there’s one small problem...

Your friends stand in the way.

Battledeck pits you and your horde against your friends as you fight to determine the one true ruler of planet Ghoul.

Each player receives 3 Ghoul cards face down, a HP card, and a Counter Gem. Players reveal their first Ghoul and the battle begins.

Dice rolls determine damage and healing, but also trigger special abilities unique to each Ghoul: slow death by wet nibble; shower of crusty monkey biscuits; and bounding embrace of boneless fists to name a few.

Using the HP card to track a Ghoul's health, Counter Gems may fall on Yellow Hearts – triggering Chance Card with ridiculous demands, or fall on a Purple Heart triggering Mayhem, upping the stakes of the game.

Players choose who to attack on their turn – beat the strongest player down to size? Or deal revenge on the Ghoul that gave them the Curse of Creeping Toe Fungus?

GHOUL Battledeck is a hoot and suited to the whole family. Its weighting makes it perfect for ages 8 and up.

-description from designer

boardgame: Professor Brainstorm Word Challenge Card Game
description:
A collectible card game where the players combat each other with words.

Each card has a letter, point value, ability, illustration of the being it represents, and attributes that influence its strengths and vulnerabilities. Blue-backed cards are consonants, and orange-backed cards are vowels and effects.

Each player starts with a deck of 50+ cards with no more than 3 copies of any card; it's recommended they have at least 20 orange cards and 30 blue cards. Players shuffle the blue and orange decks separately, play 5 blue cards and 3 orange cards to the table, keeping 3 orange cards in their hand.

The roles of Effector and Protector alternate each round. The players lay out a word from their cards, Protector first, and then attack the opponent's cards with their cards, Effector first. Then each player adds the points of their surviving cards to their total. The first player to 50 points wins.

The setting for the game is the World of Aura, and each card represents (in addition to a letter) a character from one of Aura's four kingdom/continents: 

     Terra: Earth-like creatures
     Dragonia: Dragons
     Hydra: Sea creatures
     Industria: Robots


—user summary

boardgame: Professor Brainstorm Equation Invasion Card Game
description:
A collectible card game where the players combat each other with mathematical equations.

Each card has a digit or operation, its point value, ability, illustration of the being it represents, and attributes that influence its strengths and vulnerabilities. Blue-backed cards are the digits 2-9, and orange-backed cards are the digits 0 and 1, and arithmetic operators (+-x÷).

Each player starts with a deck of 50+ cards with no more than 3 copies of any card; it's recommended they have at least 20 orange cards and 30 blue cards. Players shuffle the blue and orange decks separately, play 5 blue cards and 3 orange cards to the table, keeping 3 orange cards in their hand.

The roles of Effector and Protector alternate each round. The players lay out an equation from their cards, Protector first, and then attack the opponent's cards with their cards, Effector first. Then each player adds the points of their surviving cards to their total. The first player to 50 points wins.

The setting for the game is the World of Aura, and each card represents (in addition to a letter) a character from one of Aura's four kingdom/continents: 

     Terra: Earth-like creatures
     Dragonia: Dragons
     Hydra: Sea creatures
     Industria: Robots

Introduction:Lanquia (from Spanish: Flanking, which means "flanking") is a drawless game for two players: Black and White. It is played on the intersections (points) of an initially empty hexagonal board. The recommended size is 9 points per side, but boards of 7 or 11 are also valid. Each player has access to a sufficient supply of their own colored stones.

				
				
					Definitions:
     A flank is a configuration in which two friendly stones in a straight line (along one of the six cardinal directions) are at any distance from an enemy stone and without other friendly stones in between.
     A chain is a set of stones of the same color connected in a straight line. A single stone is also a chain.
     A live chain is a chain that is not flanked by enemy stones, otherwise it is dead and is removed from the board.
     A territory is a set of empty points that see more friendly stones than enemy stones in line. An empty point that sees them is also a territory. You are the owner of a territory if the stones that see them are your color.


				
				
					Turns:Black plays first and then they alternate. On your turn, you can either pass your turn or perform these actions in the following order:


     Place a stone of your color on an empty point that does not see more enemy stones than friendly stones in a straight line through empty points.
     Remove all dead stones.


				
				
					End of the game:The game ends when the board is full or both players consecutively pass their turn. At the end of the game, points will be counted by adding up the friendly territory and friendly stones on the board.

To make the game fair, before the game starts, the first player places a black stone on an empty point and the second player chooses a side. This balancing method is called the pie rule.

–description from designer

A new take on the classic board games of Chaturanga, Chaturaji, and Shatranj. This features several ancient variants of Chaturanga and related games, modern variants that the designer has found, and additional rules by the game designer. Extra game pieces are included to accommodate special optional rules.

Happy Doggo is a (typically) 2-4 player game of chaotic point collection! Pick your Doggo's and use your cards and abilities to bury as much treasure in your yard as you can before it's all gone! House rules are encouraged!

At the start of the game, each player will pick their favorite Dog character card to represent them and draw 3 cards from their deck, which is split up from the main deck included between each player. Each Dog has a once per game power they can use to get an advantage. Each turn, you will either play a card OR do what's called a Tug of War to try and steal other players cards. On your turn you may also use your Dogs power as well as playing a card or initiating a Tug of War.

There are 2 kinds of Action cards, red and blue cards. Blue cards represent positive Paw points and will always be played in your own Yard (the space in front of each player is a yard) while red cards will always be played in opponents yards.

Each card does something, so when played, resolve it's action and then pass the turn or use your Dog power. When, if for any reason, there are two copies of a card in someones' yard, resolve the card if it's being effect is being used, then immediately Bury the cards by flipping them over and putting them under your Dog card. At the end of each turn, you draw back up to 3 cards.

Tug of War's are done by picking a face up card in another players yard and saying you want to Tug of War for it, which means that the challenging player and the opposing player both reveal the bottom of their decks and compare who has the higher number. The highest number wins and whoever wins gets the card and the effect of the card is used again.

When a player can't draw a card, the game ends and each player counts up their points to see who is the winner!

-description from publisher'

Claire Fontaine is excited, as the next chapter in her life is about to start. She's about to graduate college and is engaged to her soulmate Benjamin Hathoway. Suddenly Benjamin breaks off their engagement and refuses to tell Claire why. Claire then goes missing. Eventually Claire's found dead in a partially frozen lake. Her car is found more than 20 miles away.

Claire Fontaine's family was never satisfied with how the case was handled. Now you must re-open and re-examine the case to find the truth.

Pour over evidence and use your deduction skills to solve what really lead to tragic death of Claire Fontaine. Analyze the autopsy report. Spot contradictions in testimony. Establish timelines for persons of interest.

An episodic game with twists and turns over 3 episodes. Answers questions at the end of each episode to prove your understanding of the case.

At first they are little worms! Roll the die to wriggle and make them grow and grow!
But be careful! If you run into another worm, you'll lose!
Try to escape or get out of the way!
Survive to the end to win!

—description from the designer

This is a simple "quizzing" game for children that includes 24 pairs of cards, each highlighting a different bird, such as the Scarlet Tanager or common Crow. One card lists simple questions in black text, relating to the physical characteristics or habits of the bird, while the other lists answers in red text.

The game seems to have been sold by Parker Brothers from 1897 to 1903.

This is a simple children's card game played along the same principles as "Old Maid." It includes 19 pairs of cards, each featuring a character from Joel Chandler Harris' Uncle Remus stories - Brer Fox, Brer B'ar, Brer Buzzard, Brer Gater, Brer Tarrypin, and so on — as well as a single Brer Rabbit card (the equivalent of the Old Maid).

The cards' modern appeal lies primarily in the beautifully lithographed color illustrations.

This NOT the same game as Roberts Brothers' 1910 game of the same name. However, Parker sold this game simultaneously under two titles at different price points/quality levels: "The Game of Brer Rabbit" for 30 cents, and (starting in 1901) "The Game of Uncle Remus" for 25 cents.

—user summary

Advanced human colonies in deep space suffer from attacks by a hostile alien civilization. The invasion spreads across the galaxy, so humanity creates an advanced experimental starship. This fighter must blast through the invasion of hostile monsters and destroy three alien flagships one by one to end the war.

One Page Space consists of a series of moves. Each turn, the player chooses where the ship will move on the star map. Each move the player finds himself in a certain location and must fulfill its conditions. To win the game, you need to destroy all three alien flagships and after that the final points are calculated.

-description from designer

Global War 1985 is a game of global conflict in the 1980s. Designed for 2 to 7 players, it has 3 scenarios:

1. Pacific Typhoon: a three player scenario between the Soviet Far East Command, the US Pacific Command, and China.
2. Red Thunder: a two to four player scenario between the Warsaw Pact, NATO, Soviet Global Command, and US Global Command.
3. Global ThermoNuclear War: a three to seven player including the entire global conflict and all seven playable factions.

Global War 1985 has several unique features. First, it represents a global, hypothetical conflict in the 1980s. Second, it allows player to utilize nuclear weapons, and execute possible 1980s nuclear attack options, as an integral part of a combatant’s strategy and planning, without ending the game automatically. Third, it showcases the Chinese economy and military in the 1980s, including relevant unit types and theorized strategies like People’s War. Finally, it is the only Global War series game, to date, that includes multiple scenarios.

We, the first of our kind, at the dawn of humankind, crawl out of, er, our caves maybe, to explore rich lands, meet sages, invent technologies, become a proper people, and herald a new era. Our deeds will become legend and live on, as awkward scribbles on white cards.

The Cradle is a legacy make-as-you-play PnP civilization game. You start with tokens for each player and a deck of blank cards. Each game, you add more content to your deck, turning blank cards into Lands, Sages, and Technologies, to be re-discovered by later ages. Each game lasts until one Tribe reaches technological supremacy. After a few games, a milestone technology will be discovered, ushering the whole game into a new Era that changes some rules and adds new actions and other possibilities as humankind as a whole advances through the ages.

-description from designer

Marco Polo has made a daring escape amid the chaos of the assault on Khutulun’s Amu River Encampment. Hot on his trail are three formidable women: Miriam, a diplomatic envoy of the Muhandasat guild; Boraqchin, seeking retribution on behalf of Khutulun herself; and Farvardeen, an inquisitor for the Order of Assassins, desperate for answers. Yet, they'll soon uncover that Polo isn't bound for Europe as anticipated, but towards the enigmatic Indigo Grotto, a vital clue to the mysteries of mythium and its influence on their world. Embark on a quest with the Indigo Sisterhood, locate the Grotto, and forever reshape history!

Worldbreakers: The Indigo Sisterhood is the inaugural expansion to Worldbreakers, a tactical two-player card game. You start the game with a Worldbreaker that has a unique special ability and a deck of 30 cards. As the game unfolds, you'll recruit followers to claim the board and develop location cards to accumulate resources and power. The first player to amass 10 power emerges victorious!

The Indigo Sisterhood enhances the gameplay with fresh events, followers, and locations for all four guilds. It also ushers in two new Worldbreakers: Miriam, entrusted by the Moon guild with the mission to locate the Indigo Grotto, and Kublai Khan, a distinguished master of the Stars guild. Additionally, the expansion extends the narrative of the Worldbreakers solo campaign, drawing players deeper into the unfolding saga.

–description from designer
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
★Synopsis★
One day, an announcement was made by the king:
Hire the most famous pastry chef in the country as the exclusive pastry chef for the royal family.
This is an unprecedented opportunity for you, who have just become independent as a craftsman.
You immediately begin to accept orders from nobles in order to spread your name in the desire-filled social world.
However, there are also rivals who dream of the same success...

★Game Overview★
This game involves players producing sweets (taking tiles) in turn and packing and shipping them according to orders from nobles.
The number on the order card becomes fame points.
In the end, the person who earns the most points wins.

★Tsurukame Puzzle★
This is a puzzle based on the Tsurukame calculation.
By combining tiles that satisfy the 4 conditions of the shape, type, and number of sweets on the order card,
you can intuitively solve the Tsurukame calculation by placing them on the board.
Read your opponent's intentions and complete your own sweets!

★Components★
Acrylic tiles x 76
Game board x 4
Shipping crystal x 20
Assistant chime x 4
Order cards x 20
Bonus tile cards x 8

-description from designer

Night Witches is a solitaire or two-player cooperative game in which the Night Witches–female pilots of the Soviet 588th Night Bomber Regiment–work together to fly harassment missions on the Eastern Front during WWII. Their planes are rickety and minimally equipped, but with their engines whirring quietly and with the dark of night to cloak them, the Night Witches are flying towards promotion to 46th Guards Night Bomber Aviation Regiment–and towards making aviation history!

Night Witches is a light wargame that consists of a ten-mission campaign, during which the pilots try to amass enough victory points to be promoted to form a Guards unit, a great honor in the Soviet military. Each mission lasts roughly 30 minutes, and each can be played on its own, separately from the campaign. On each mission, two planes will venture into unknown territory to complete a specific goal and to earn victory points. As they fly, players will observe the terrain around them by drawing tokens from a bag. Some tokens will merely represent the night, but the players will also need to avoid spotlights, navigate through clouds, respond to attacks from AAA, and more.

Each mission is made up of multiple sorties, with brief recovery times between. During sorties, players will evade enemy fire–and bomb targets–by pulling from a bag containing success, failure, and stress tokens. During recovery, they will have to manage their time, fuel supply, and stress levels. As pilots gain experience and complete missions, they will also be able to acquire upgrade cards. (Those victory points are good for something!) Will the Night Witches be able to dodge AAA, do maximum damage to the Nazis, and fly off into the sunrise?

-description from publisher

Not all battles are won by grand armies on large fields or in defensible castles. In some cases more subtle and perhaps deadly tactics are used. From fake letters and treaties, feigned retreats or advances, poisoned water supplies, assassinations and infiltration, many things can happen in the dark to throw an enemy into disarray before the main battle ever starts.

In War Chest: Nightfall you will have the opportunity to use specialized units to hamper and hinder your opponent’s attempts to effectively mobilize their forces. Mastering the art of subterfuge will be key to success with this new and exciting expansion for the classic War Chest strategy and tactics game!

—description from publisher

Mayors new and old—it’s time to get creative with your city plan! Architects have been cooking up innovative building techniques using wild resources that allow for a bit of flexibility. They’ve also designed cozy cabins as an alternative to cottages. New monuments are under construction, including one that moves from place to place, and another that gives you an additional, tinier town. On top of this, every building is getting a renovation to enhance its facade. With 30 new buildings and monuments, there’s never been a better time to build a tiny town!

-description from publisher

Competition for the annual Cube Cup is heating up. Two new colors of dice have joined the race, complete with awesome ability cards. Exciting new ability cards are also included for the base game’s original eight colors. A double-sided racetrack features new rewards and hazardous obstacles. Additional components allow you to race with up to 6 players. The runners are in position and the countdown has begun, so be fast or be last!

-description from publisher

A New Addition to the Space Base Galaxy!

Genesis is an expansion for every Space Base player, providing massive card variety and additional paths to victory for experienced players, and only a few simple rules for newer players.

Following the discovery of alien technology on Terra Proxima, the U.E.S. Science Corps worked feverishly to unlock its mysteries. Only one breakthrough emerged, but it has revolutionized hypernet travel. A new fleet with experimental drives and terraforming foundries are ready at your command!

-description from publisher

Your career is going really well: you started out as a ticket collector on a small railway, then you were a train driver and later, as a railway engineer, you also learned the ins and outs of track planning. Now you are the director of the Railway Development Company! You decide what length of main and branch lines you build, and how expensive or special the locomotives that you put on them will be. Be the most skilful rail network builder and get your favourite trains!

During the game, players draw railway lines on the map sheet using their own colored markers. If you have several sections that are joined (they can also be branch lines!), you can redeem them for a train card with the same number as the number of stages. You can also earn extra points by matching different formations of shapes.
In each round you can decide to draw one line with your color. Or you can reserve two lines with dotted lines in your color which you can draw later and others cannot take from you. Or you can decide to use up your lines to buy train cards. If the lines spent for a train card match a face up shape bonus card you get extra points. 
The game ends when there are no more empty lines on the map. Be careful because if you have lines left (dotted lines do not count) you get penalty points for each.

All the train cards in the game show a real train type that some day (or even today) was used on the Hungarian railway lines.

-description from publisher

boardgame: Harry Potter: Seek the Deathly Hallows
description:
One player is the searcher. They leave the room, and the other players hide their Deathly Hallows cards somewhere in the room.

The searcher now has to find those cards. The other players take turns to give hints about the location of the Deathly Hallows. The type of hint they can give, is determined by the top card of the hint card deck.

When the searcher finds one of the Deathly Hallows during a player's turn, that player is eliminated. When just one player is left to give hints, the searcher must find the last Hallow within the next 8 hints. If they do, both the searcher and the other remaining player win the game. If they don't, it's a draw

An abstract strategy game base on unique moves. Use your pieces to escape and free your king. Or get captured by the enemy.

Place all your pieces on your side of the board. The enemy does the same. Then you move every piece one at a time. This is a turn base strategy game. Every piece moves differently. The one common factor is every piece captures other enemy pieces. Most of the time you perform a triangle attack to capture the enemy on a hex board. you want to either capture the enemy king or have your king escape to the other side.

This is an abstract strategy game that you could relate to chess.

Every turn you move one piece and then pass the turn to the enemy.

You win by capturing the enemy king or escaping your king to the other side.

–description from designer

A diceless miniature battle game. Use color and a deck of cards to battle. Compare colors to deal with damage and use the deck to help out with figuring out rules.
The colors go into a bag and your deck build and battle.

Players draw a card and then collect colors.
Afterwards, they perform a main action then a free action

Most of the time players chose to attack.
Play a card and it will tell you how many colors you pick from the bag. Then compare colors to deal damage and resolve cards and damage

The game ends after 6 rounds.
Whoever kills the most models or scores the most points wins.

It's a small miniature scrimmage battle.

-description from designer

THE LAB is a competitive card game for 3 to 6 players in which you will be faced with some of the most dangerous microorganisms in existence. Be the first to earn 10 Cure points and try not to succumb to any of the insidious infections you will encounter throughout the game.

Before starting the game, each player receives 3 Marker tokens, 1 Blood Transfusion token, and chooses a Player board. The first player takes the Patient Zero token and goes first, determined by the person who was most recently sick in real life.

Infections cards are shuffled and placed as a deck on the table. Each player is dealt 5 Treatment cards, keeping them hidden from others, and the remaining cards are placed on the table. Lastly, the Bad Luck cards are shuffled and placed on the table.

Each Round begins with Patient Zero creating the Round deck by drawing cards from the Infections deck. Then, starting with Patient Zero and proceeding clockwise, each player takes their turn. They choose a card from the Round deck and fight the infection by playing Treatment cards. If they can't defeat it alone, they can seek help from other players. Successfully curing the infection earns them Cure points, while failure results in 1 Infection point. Players then draw new Treatment cards to replenish their hand, up to a maximum of 5 cards. Finally, the Patient Zero token is passed to the next player in clockwise order.

Evocabulary is a word and engine building game where building an efficient engine is more important than having a large vocabulary.

Players will take turns to create words from a hand of cards, activating the "team" responsible for working with a word of that length, allowing them to draw cards, gain points, recruit new team members and more.

On future turns a player can modify the word by adding, removing and rearranging letters to activate the same or a different team.

Once all the letter cards have been drawn, and no player can create any new words, the game ends and the player with the most points wins.

-description from designer

What are you willing to risk? And if you take that risk, do you have what it takes to be victorious? Enter the kingdom and squabbles of Pigs, Rats, Dogs and Cats. Of warriors, priests and spies. Of treachery and glory. Much is at stake - the reward great. Time to fight! Tooth and claw!

—description from the back of the box

Pigs and Rats has everything you want in a good card game - strategy, bluffs and battle. The game is composed of rounds and each round has 3 parts - the RISK, the MELD and BATTLE!

A game for the ages! Quick to learn but a lifetime to master. Play with 2 to 3 players in single play or discover the joy of team play with 4 players.

Inspired by the old world German game 'Bezique".

—description from the designer

Make it Six is an original roll and reface dice game

Each player has one Blue die that actually serves as the scorepad. It begins on ONE and the first player to get their Blue die to SIX wins the game.

Each player's turn begins with rolling the dice of the other colors. During the player's turn, they will use the abilities (while avoiding the downsides) of the dice. This will enable them to REFACE (manually change the facing on a die) or REROLL some of the dice in order to maximize the scoring opportunity on their turn.

At the end of their turn, they hopefully get to increase the number on their Blue die.

Then it is on to the next player.

—description from the publisher

“The ultimate dice battle game.”

Dittle is a simple and strategic new Dice battling game for kids and adults. Get your dice to the other side by rolling and jumping your enemy. When one player gets all seven dice to the other side, the game is over. But getting to the other side is only half of the battle!

Add up the face value of all dice that have reached the other side of the board for both players in the highest score wins!

Will you try to rush all of your dice to the other side, or will you go slow and steady focused on only having the highest number is facing up?

The seven on seven battle is quick and exciting. Like chess or checkers, gameplay can be as quick as 10 to 15 minutes. Or you can take your time and move strategically.

—description from back of box
A map for Nestorgames GP, with 3 loops.

boardgame: Nestorgames GP: Gate and Teleport Board
description: A more complicated board with a teleport space, a gate and a few extra pieces to implement it.

Didn't get enough glory the first time around?
Maybe you're ready for Munchkin Side Quests 2!

Even more difficult but oh-so-tempting goals and goodies to add to any Munchkin game – will you go straight for the win at Level 10, or will the extra rewards tickle your inner munchkin?

-description from publisher's website

If a tree falls in the forest, does anyone care?

Munchkin Druids adds 112 cards to the game . . . the new Druid class, and more nature-related monsters and weapons than you can shake a holy stick at. Watch out for the bloodthirsty Night Shade and wear - but don’t eat – the Death Cap!

Enjoy the good-natured silliness of this Munchkin expansion – shuffle it in with any of your favorite Munchkin games!

-description from publisher's website

Welcome to Zoorena, the intergalactic Battle Arena where some of the galaxy's fiercest Zoo Keepers pit their finest combatants against each other to emerge as the new Champion! Zoorena is a fun-filled fast-paced family game that is perfect for short bursts of fun on the go! (description on box)

Zoorena is a card game in which there are 10 types of combatants, numbered 1 to 10, each having a unique power. You always have a hand size of exactly 1. On your turn you have the option to initiate a fight. The winner claims the loser's card as a trophy which is worth points. You can invite others to gang up on one opponent. If you win, the weakest ally wins the trophy. The game ends if any player owns 5 trophies, or when the deck runs out.

—description from the publisher

Introducing Goat on a Boat, the rhyming charades game with the ever-so-slightly-strange sounding name. Inside this box, you’ll come across all sorts of weird and wonderful rhyming charades.

From “Moby Dick using his selfie stick” to “Angelina Jolie eating ravioli”, “King Kong playing ping pong” to “Bob Marley riding a harley”.

Your job is to describe these rhymes to your team by either acting them out, reading a riddle or explaining them without using any of the words on the card. Each round, you’ll have 30 seconds. Get your team to successfully shout out three rhymes and you’ll get to hunt for one of the matching rhyming pairs which lie face-down on the table. At the end of the game, the team that uncovers the most matching pairs, wins!

So if you’re looking to mix-up the traditional family games night and watch your grandpa mime out ‘Frankenstein drinking a bottle of wine’, then this might be the game for you. Although, just a reminder: this is a game about shouting out all sorts of strange rhymes... so you might want to give the neighbours a heads-up first.

In this expansion for Hack & Slash, you'll find 11 new quests and a new kind of reward – Treasures! They can add to your fame – or bend a rule for a future quest. Also comes with an additional always-available card, Shop at Wiz-Mart.

    11 Quest cards
    20 Treasure cards

1 new scenario, 2 new Forage Cards (2 copies each), and 1 Solo/Coop "Extraordinary Tactic" card for the CyMS! Add more replayability to your game with this Kickstarter exclusive pack!

2023. 100 years ago, the very first edition of the 24 Hours of Le Mans, the greatest car race in the world, was held.

100 years ago Jean Graton was born, the creator of the greatest driver of all: Michel Vaillant, multiple winner of the 24 Hours of Le Mans.

To celebrate this event with dignity, we propose a game in which you will be the one without whom victory would not be possible: the team manager

24 : Vaillante vs. Leader is a racing team management game. You will have to lead your team on the highest step of the podium by taking the right decisions, by asking your drivers to accelerate or, on the contrary, to take their foot off the accelerator, and to go back to give up their place to one of their teammates. You will have to take risks to stay ahead. That’s the price of victory.

24 Vaillante .vs Leader uses a stop-go mechanic accessible to all, elements for a total immersion in the universe of Le Mans, all served by the illustrations of Jean Graton. What more could you ask for?

boardgame: Donaumelodien Escape: Der Schatz im Stephansdom
description: Who doesn't know the legend of the treasure in the world-famous St. Stephen's Cathedral? Centuries ago, a chest full of gold is said to have been hidden there, and since then it has been considered lost. Immerse yourself in the Vienna of 1876, in a story full of riddles and mysteries, and go in search of the legendary treasure!

Meet Cruxy the Penguin and his polar friends! Their favorite game is to carve words on the ice floes. Come and join them!

In this game you will be filling the crossword grid with made-up words of the indicated length and required letters in them. The more set conditions you fulfill, the sooner you win! To make the game even more fun and exciting, use the powers of Cruxy and other animals ― their special effects may influence other players and bring you closer to success at the same time.

Four different game modes, fifty double-sided boards of increasing complexity and a unique combination of literacy and strategy make Cruxy a new word in the world of board games!

How to play?

The game is played by turns so players have some time to think over their options and come up with the word of the needed length, with the required letters, or a symbol occupied by the word. All the objections are marked on the playing board. Remember that you can only add a word to the grid using one or more letters already placed in the grid. Also the words to fill in should meet the criteria: a) a word cannot be used twice, b) proper nouns are prohibited, etc. Check the full list in the rules!

Once a player wrote down a word and crossed the things they used out from their box, it's the player's from their left turn.

To make the game even more fun, reach Cruxy's friends, different animals of the South Pole for the special effects they create! These effects may boost your own success or influence other players. 
For example, there is a Seagul that allows a player to use shorter or longer words for a moment, or a Kraken that pulls out an extra letter for your opponents, and many other creatures with their own power.

The player who fulfilled all the tasks but any 2 of any kind becomes the winner.


     Add new words to the grid and fulfil different conditions.
     Apply animals’ effects at the right moment to influence the game play.
     Be the first to cross out the most conditions in your player area and win the game!


—description from the publisher

Icarus Sun is a board game for 1-4 players.

It is a steampunk inspired game of airship adventure through the sky.

To win：
At the end of the game, the player with the most victory points wins.

How to play：
1. Each player selects a gadget to use.
2. Each player receives a "deep blue ore" as fuel.
3. Players flip through their deck to see how much fuel they need. If they can pay, they follow the instructions on the card. If not, the adventure is over for this round.
4. The player repeats flipping the deck until he/she cannot pay the required amount of fuel.
You can use the gadget of your choice at any time during the phase indicated on the gadget.
5. Spend the gear (which serves as money) acquired as a result of the adventure to add a card of your choice to the deck.
6. Repeat the above process for approximately 8 rounds, calculate the victory points, and determine the winner.

boardgame: Palec boży: Wakan Tanka God Promo
description: Wakan Tanka is the newest Promo God introduced together with Cherry Blossom expansion.

One player after the other rolls a die and moves their own duck figure so many fields further to collect flower chips. Once a player has three different chips he jumps into the water once he reaches the jetty and wins so the game.

You may notice that your daily communications are filled with abbreviations, shorthand, and emojis, which are often auto-corrected by predictive technology. Thankfully, there is a task force dedicated to celebrating and preserving the richness of our language.

Your mission, as a defender of the dictionary, is to protect the diversity of language from the forces that seek to oversimplify it. Armed with your collective wisdom, you must use your words to navigate the city and stop the villains from diminishing the beauty of language. After all, knowledge is power, and it must be cherished.

In Defenders of the Dictionary, players will cooperate to navigate the city of Wordopolis via letter-and-grid-based wordplay. For each mission, you need to achieve a specific task and follow a particular direction with your words before running out of turns. Completing a mission helps you gain valuable intel about the villains and advances your campaign.

Defenders of the Dictionary takes the familiar gameplay of grid-based word games and flips it on its head. You will need to collaborate together within this system, navigate specific directions, and overcome the challenges and obstacles set by the villains.

The campaign is composed of many short games where each game represents a mission.  Each mission may include some new and some previously introduced mechanisms.  The campaign is designed with gamer group flexibility in mind so it's not necessary to play every mission with the same group of people. Complete all the missions to determine which villain was the mastermind, and face-off with them in the final showdown.

-description from designer

boardgame: Dual Gauge: Netherlands and Eastern U.S.
description:
The third expansion map set for Dual Gauge!

These maps introduce Star Dits, which function as normal dits for most purposes – they count as a stop but cannot be tokened – but are worth more money. Depending on the map, players may also have an additional incentive to hit these stops over others. Both maps also see players in a race to grab bonuses, which also serve as another game end trigger.

The Netherlands map seats up to five players and features a new gauge conversion step, allowing you to flip narrow track to its standard side. Of course, what it doesn't do is change your narrow trains. You'll need to plan your train purchases carefully, and beware of opponents who might use this tactic offensively.

That's if you have enough time, of course! The standout feature of this map is a race to complete certain Goals. Achieving one of the map's eight Goals will win you a disc. This can be traded in later, either to place a station or to buy a precious second share of stock in a single round.

The Eastern US map is for three to four, and is a bit subtler. Preprinted track segments provide awkward chokepoints to either work around or embrace. Company turn order isn't fixed, but shifts from round to round depending on company stock value. Increases in that value are gated – tied to your dividends, so you'll need to work for big routes while your rivals try to block you with aggressive token play.

At the western end of the maps, there are the destination cities of Detroit and Chicago, each containing a set of Bonus Discs. When a company ends a run there, their President claims a disc. At the end of game, you'll get a payout based on the number of discs you've claimed.

-description from publisher

description:
Three years after the start of Prohibition, the battle for Chicago intensifies in an increasingly lucrative but unstable scenario for the gangs. The city has become too small, and the ambition of mobsters like Al Capone prevents them from settling for only a part of the pie. They want it all.

Bloody Business expands the Scarface 1920 universe by introducing mechanics and elements that multiply the game's narrative power. It does so by increasing its flow, facilitating the implementation of some actions, enhancing players' strategies, and turning each game into a memorable battle for the throne of Chicago. It's time to take action!

Flooruler is a competitive strategy board game that challenges 2 to 4 players to compete for ownership of 24 floors.

This game dynamically combines tactical decision-making with luck by utilizing a D6 dice. Players can collect gold from the Gold Fountain at the center of the map, using it to purchase powerful items like weapons and skill cards from stores located at the corners. These strategic cards enhance a player's chances of winning and hinder opponents from claiming the 24 floors.

The game follows a turn-based system, allowing players to perform actions such as movement, acquiring/spending gold, attacking, and using skills in separate turns. This system facilitates critical strategy decisions and self-defense in dangerous situations.

description:
Introduction:Nadraz (from Proto-Germanic: nadraz, meaning “snake”) is a drawless unification game for two players: Black and White. It is played on the hexes (cells) of an initially empty hexagonal board. The recommended size is 10 cells per side, where the edge cells are darker. Boards of size 6 or 14 are also valid. Each player has access to a single set of identical stones with two colors, one on the front and another on the back.

				
				
					Definitions:
     A snake is a maximal set of connected stones of the same color. A single stone is an egg.
     The head or tail of a snake is a stone adjacent to exactly one other stone of the same snake. Each snake must have a single head and a single tail.
     The length of a snake is the number of stones it contains.
     To flip is to replace an enemy stone with a friendly stone.
     To cut is to split an enemy snake into two or more snakes.


				
				
					Turns:Black plays first, and then turns alternate. On your turn, perform the following actions in a straight line, starting from exactly one friendly head, tail, or egg:


     Place one or more friendly stones, one by one, to grow a snake.
     Flip one or more groups of consecutive enemy stones (that are not a head, tail, or egg) found along the path, in order to cut enemy snakes.



         Each flipped group must be complete and must be followed by at least one empty cell, which you must occupy as a cut cell.
         You may perform several cuts in the same turn, always in the same direction and as long as each has its own cut cell.
    


You may choose to continue or stop on any cell along the path, as long as you meet the placement and, if applicable, the cut conditions. The sequence of placed or flipped stones must form a continuous path, growing from a friendly head, tail, or egg. If placing or flipping a stone merges two friendly snakes, you may not continue, unless you must occupy a cut cell to make a valid cut. Incorporating eggs to the snake does not interrupt the turn.

At the end of the turn, no friendly stone may be on the edge unless the edge cell is a cut cell. If you cannot place stones, pass your turn; otherwise, passing is not allowed.

				
				
					End of the game:The game ends when no player can place stones. The winner is the one with the longest snake. If there is a tie, the next longest snakes are compared until there is a winner. If the tie persists, the player who placed the last stone wins.

To balance the game, before starting, the first player places one white and one black stone (eggs) on empty cells. Then, the second player chooses a side. This balancing method is called two-stone pie rule.

				
				
					Reverse variant:The same rules apply, except the player with the fewest snakes of shortest length wins. In case of a tie, the next snake lengths are compared, and so on, until there are no more ties. Note that eggs count as snakes of shortest length.

—description from the designer

We know a great deal about the evolution on Earth (and we hope that Evolution: New World helped you with that too). It's an exciting story of various creatures acquiring defensive & offensive traits to adapt to the ever-changing environment. What we don’t know is if there might be other worlds where completely different creatures dwell. Will the same laws of evolution apply there too? It's time to find out!

Evolution: Another World is a card game in a beloved Evolution: the Origin of Species series. In the game, you create animals and give them traits, so they survive and thrive in the chaotic world of wondrous creatures. The goal of the game is to give your creatures enough energy to transmute and transcend their state of existence. The first player to transmute three creatures wins the game.

This game is Family-friendly because animals can’t die or be eaten by predators. Competition is peaceful, but you can attack other players' creatures to gain energy. This game has a straightforward win condition: you don't need to count VP at the end of the game. Transmute 3 of your creatures and you win the game!

Evolution: Another World – Special Edition is a Kickstarter version of the game that contains everything from Evolution: Another World core game plus several additional modules, special rules, and components for 5th and 6th player.

The aim of Scryper is to win three-game sets. The first player to place a brick on the opponent's back line wins the set. You must choose how many of your 16 bricks to deploy in each set. The four types of bricks have different features and strengths, and each can only be used once in a game.

These simple rules open up for gameplay that requires strategic and resource planning; are you aiming for a quick 3-setter, or do you try to wear out your opponent in a long 5-setter? It also requires social skills to read your opponent's moves and make appropriate adjustments, such as making the opponent over-invest resources on set, so you win the next with fewer resources.

The tactical gameplay in each set requires that you read how your opponent plays and opens up for bluffing, creativity, and outsmarting your opponent, which means that it is never pre-determined who will win a set with the preselected bricks.

Scryper is easy to learn and challenging to master, which means the game remains fun, as each game's dynamic is different.

—description from the designer

A logical thinking game for kids and grown-ups.
It is part of F.X. Schmid's Logiplay game series. Logiplay 4.
A 2-player game, advertised mainly for children (and "TV-tired grown-ups") by the German publisher F.X. Schmid in cooperation with Austria's Piatnik publisher.

Goal of SOLO is to move one of your pawns to the opposite end of the board. Moving and skipping (of own or opposite) is allowed. It has similarities to Chinese Checkers/Halma and Salta. The moved pawns create obstacles that can create shortcuts for the opponent.'

—user summary

The Battle of the Pantano de Vargas was an armed confrontation that took place in what is now known as the Pantano de Vargas, in the township of the municipality of Paipa, present-day Boyacá, Colombia. The battle began in the morning until the afternoon of the 25th. July 1819.

The opposing sides were the patriotic troops and the royalist troops in the liberation campaign for the independence of what was then New Granada, today Colombia, Panama, Venezuela and Ecuador.

In it, the army of "Gran Colombia" under the command of Simón Bolívar tried to block the way for the support forces of José María Barreiro who were heading to the city of Bogotá.

This was the bloodiest and most radical battle on the soil of New Granada during the liberation campaign. Thanks to this, the liberating army arrived in Tunja on August 4, 1819, and defeated the Spanish army in the battle of Boyacá on August 7, 1819.

This is a mechanical Roll And Write game in two player mode, or in solo mode with a minimum duration of 20 minutes.

To play it is recommended to have:

     The Battle Blade (It is recommended that the blade be found with a polypropylene protector)
     An erasable marker.
     Various D6 Dice


Objective: Eliminate 5 enemy battalions, before the end of the turn10. If at the end none of the contestants achieve their objective, the one with the most wins.
battalion units remove.

—description from the designer
family: History: Latin American Wars of Independence
Theme: Wetlands / Swamps / Bogs / Fens / Marshland

description:
A simple strategic game from F.X. Schmid's old Logiplay series (probably 1970). Logiplay game 3.
two players - two colours. Goal is to move all (!) of one's meeple's from one end of the board to the opposing end - in a row. It is similar to Salta and Halma, but much simpler and thus suitable for primary school children. No skipping allowed, only straight directions.
It teaches strategy gaming to kids.

—user summary

Each round one of the players is the bachelor and the others try to woo them by selecting two good attributes from their hand, while also giving one of their opponents a bad attribute to sabotage them. When introducing themselves to the bachelor, the players also draw a "traitor" card, which tells who revealed their red flag to the bachelor.
After the bachelor has decided who they'd prefer to date a new round begins, with the next bachelor.

The cards provide funny combinations, sometimes with dark humor and shock value.

In Worden, players utilize words to find clues and deduce what letters are hiding in their opponent’s hand. The first player to correctly guess all three letters in their opponent’s hand wins the game!

The game requires an alphabet deck comprised of 26 letter cards.

In Mish Match, players race to spot and slap cards that match in unusual ways.

Get in the mix with Mish Match, the fastest fun you’ll ever have! In this competitive, fast-action party game, be the first to spot and slap matching theme from a variety of Animals, Colors, Foods, and Moods! The objective changes as categories to match are called at the top of each round. Collect the stack if you’re on point. Call it, flip it, slap it, claim it! The player with the most cards when the deck runs out, wins!

"It is a game of strategy and skill which can be playing by young and old, by anyone who likes hockey. It is intensely interesting, but simple, and although dice are used, you will find that skill and not luck usually decides the winner. The National Hockey League Game offers endless changes to develop plays on attacks and defense, just as in the real game played on the ice." - From intro of rulebook

Players control their Forwards (3), Defensemen (2) and Goalkeeper (1). The players are simultaneously attacking and defending. Forwards try to get across the goal crease line past the defense and the goalkeeper. This will score a goal but 2 forwards must be past the blueline for this to count, otherwise it is offside. The defense can "check" the forwards by landing on the same square occupied by a forward which sends them back to their starting location. Goalies are restricted to movement inside the goal crease only and can make saves by "blocking".

All movement for the game is done with dice. The game's length can be set beforehand between both players but it is recommended to do a "best out of ___" style game of 3, 5, 7 etc goals.

This "Arrivals" pack provides a alternate set of starting shop cards with a language-independent shop power for players familiar with the game. Each of the six cards showcases a new piece of art by Sandara Tang showing one of the dragons arriving in town for the first time. The pack also includes a multi-language instruction card.

- description from publisher

The game comes with an octagonal board, 4 Pooffs (rubber bulbs), 2 small cork or rubber balls and a carry bag. The ball is placed in the center of the board.  Players secure their Pooff in the metal rings in the board and squeeze them to provide puffs of air to propel the ball into the "home circle" of the opposing players.

Players play simultaneously. The game ends when a player has reached 10 points or a time limit is reached. The other players then compare their scores and the player with the lowest number of points wins.

Pooff boards are handmade, The publisher tag in the center of the board varies (some with city/state, some without). 2 player version requires players to control 2 Poofs at once ; 4 player version is preferred.

—user summary

Double Thieves is a roll and write game for two players in which you'll play a pair of thieves racing around the map while stealing items. But though the two thieves may be working together, they're also bitter rivals. You never know if you'll be able to steal the item you're after, since your "partner" is always the one driving the car!

Use your skills and jammers well, and with a bit of luck, you might just be able to outwit your partner in crime! Finish your jobs before your rival does, and you'll have enough cash to leave them high and dry. Just keep an eye out for security—if you get caught, all of your hard work will be for nothing!

Degenesis: Clan Wars is an asymmetric, semi-cooperative, tactical-scale skirmish wargame in which one player embodies the Hostile, who controls the swarms of Cockroaches and Clanners ready to tear the Protectorate and its citizens to shreds, while everyone else takes on the role of individual Cult members: powerful, iconic figures ready to tip the balance of the Clan Wars in their favor.

Throughout the expansive campaign contained within the box, players have to manage their roster of characters, make life-and-death decisions in every moment, struggle to keep their team together in the face of overwhelming opposition, battle against the Hostile's own objectives and goals, and, hopefully, survive...if they’re lucky.

description:
In Charge & Spark, you and your opponent will try to reduce each other’s health to 0 or be the first to reach the goal space in the center of the board. Since there are two potential objectives, you must consider your opponent’s position and work to counter their dominant strategy—they might slip right past you if you aren’t careful!

In this game, players select a card to play for the turn and reveal them simultaneously. Certain cards have effects that cancel each other (Barrier reducing damage taken, Trap or dealing damage preventing Move, Counter reflecting damage, etc.), so it is important to consider what resources your opponent has at their disposal on any given turn. After paying the associated cost (if applicable) and resolving the card effects for the turn, used cards are placed into each player’s recharge area. If there is a card already in the recharge area at this time, it returns to each player’s hand. By looking at the card in your opponent’s recharge area, you will have a better idea of what they might do next.

The game proceeds as above until one player’s health tokens run out or one player reaches the goal space in the middle of the center board.


「チャージ＆スパーク」は、色々な効果を持つカードを駆使して、相手のライフを削りきるか、先にゴールすることを目指す2人専用カードゲームです。
