Paper Dungeons: Dangerous Dungeons is a mini expansion to the successful roll & write game Paper Dungeons.

This mini expansion adds 8 new scenarios, 3 new monsters in a 14 card expansion.

This scenarios adds hard challenges to the players and christmas monsters to play with in any other match.

In Da Vincis Schnappszahlen-Spiel every player gets 5 cards. Every player has to play a card in order and determine the sum of all card values on the table. If the value is a special number such as 11, 22, 33, 44, ... the player wins two step on the board. If the player skips such a special number he has to jump one step back if possible. After playing a card the player draws another one.

There exist some special cards such as the value 13 in which case the sum is reduced by 1 instead of increased by 13. For the card with value 12 the player can decide to add or subtract 1 or 2.

Winner is the player who first reaches 18 steps on the board.

This game is a kind of Pachisi played on a flexible domino board which changes its layout tho whole game.

As a players turn one rolls a die and moves one of his initially 6 figures as many fields as was rolled. The start position are two special domino stones with the players color. Each domino stone consists of exactly 3 landing points which are moved on at the players turn. If the figure of a player stops at the middle point of a stone this stone can be rearranged to a different position.

If a opponent figure can be captured it has to be done in which case this figure is removed from the board and can not enter again. The same happens if the number of required steps cannot be performed.

The player who has at least one surviving figure wins the game.

Card game that involves memorization (since the player has only 30 seconds to memorize his hand before the cards are fliped down) and a clash between the different Uruguayan bird cards. Each clash is won by the bird with the largest wingspan.

—user summary

German version of the Echo Stone promo.

Echo Stone is a gem that allows players to gain an additional aether on their turn if they have played another Echo Stone in the same turn.

A dungeon crawling game that puts players in a maze that is literally falling apart. Players are not allowed to turn around, so their adventure through the dungeon must be completed in a single shot. The location of items and encounters are always the same, but the maze is ever changing.

Traverse through 366 unique maps (one per day, one more if you start on a leap year!) and try to get the highest score possible!

The Instructive Game of 
MAGIC SPELLING
with
Bewitched Letters

The game has 22 large cards with a picture of an animal, its name, and its point value.  It also has a large quantity of smaller letter cards, with which to spell the animals' names.

Each player starts with one animal card face up, and the letter cards are dealt out equally to each player for their own face-down stack. Each player in turn reveals the top letter card of their stack and places it face up on the table. When someone sees that they can spell the name of an animal card on the table with the letters on the table, they call out that animal. They collect its card, and it's replaced with a new animal card. At the end of the game, the player with the most points in the animal cards they have collected wins.

Your loved ones soul has been taken by the ever envious Death himself, and you've made the brave decision to delve into the depths of the underworld to bring them back! The gameplay can almost be described as the love child of games like Mini Rogue and Escape the Dark Castle. The underworld is dark and full of danger. You'll be illuminating the paths in front of you, pushing you luck, managing your resources, and determining your odds before venturing further.

You'll also encounter many foul ghosts, ghouls, and creatures of the dark along the way. Combat involves its own unique system of rolling die simultaneously against your enemies, and using your arsenal to swiftly defeat your opponent while reaching your desired battle outcome. Each level of the underworld is protected by a Deaths fiercest minions. You'll have to get past them to continue advancing further into the unknown.

The game is full of immersive flavor text that help paint a story of your unique adventure. The artwork is meant to be dark and gritty, but also playful and approachable. It's sprinkled with bits of humor to remind you that although the theme is dark, its still fun.

—description from the designer

Dziedzictwo Kazimierza Wielkiego is a regional game presenting Lesser Poland voivodeship castles founded by Casimir III The Great, the last Polish king from Piast dynasty.

In each round after shared pawn movement, players fight for the castle trying to get the highest number with a dice roll and score boosting cards. Only one contestant leaves the battleground with  points and optional king emblem.

Whoever reaches certain number of owned points or emblems wins the game (15pts or 5emblems for 2 player game). The game may also end with last castle taken - then the player with highest score wins

Introduction:Valaturna (from the Proto-Germanic: Walaturna, meaning "ruling tower") is a no-draw game for two players: Black and White. It is played on the intersections (points) of an initially empty hexagonal board. It can be played on a 7–sized board for long games or on a 5–sized board for short games. Each player has access to a sufficient supply of stackable pieces of their own color.

				
				
					Definitions:
     A stack is a set of pieces located at the same point, one on top of the other. A single piece is also a stack.
     The color of a stack is the color of its top piece, which indicates its owner.
     The height of a stack is the number of pieces it contains.
     A chain is a set of stones of the same color connected in a straight line.
     A flank is a configuration where two friendly stones in a straight line (along one of the six cardinal directions) are at the same distance from one or more enemy chains, with the same number of empty points N in between and N empty points on both sides (where N can be zero), and no friendly stones in between.


				
				
					Turns:Black moves first, then turns alternate. On your turn, you can either pass your turn or take the following actions in the following order:


     Place a stack of your color with height 1 on an empty point that can see a friendly stack in a straight line through empty points.
     Place a piece of your color on any flanked enemy stacks during your placement to increase their height and take control of them.


				
				
					End of the game:The game continues until a player manages to create the tallest stack that cannot be captured. This means forming a stack that cannot be flanked by enemy stacks. The game ends at this point because the player who achieves this will generally not cooperate with their opponent to form stacks that reach or exceed that height, as it is not in their best interest.

To ensure fairness, before the game starts, the first player places a black stack and a white stack on an empty point, and the second player chooses a side. This balancing method is called the two-stack pie rule.

—description from designer

In the fantasy realm of Luthasun, the winds of war are blowing and the warring factions are preparing for battle. Armed with strength and fortitude and encouraged by the high priests' wise guidance, they are ready to fight for Goddess Misthral's favour.

With Sthenos, you will live fantastic epic battles with fearless knights and valiant archers, who are always ready to strike, faithful priests, who instil courage and pray for divine intervention, and you, as the brave and wise leader that you have to lead your army successfully into battle.

Sthenos is an innovative game since it has adapted the Command and Colors system as created by Richard Borg into a card game of low complexity for two players, which requires a maximum of 15 minutes to set up and explain the rules, but promises 60-90 minutes of fun, with critical strategic decisions and high replayability.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
The Gemstone Collector is a fun quick turn-based card game for 2 to 6 players. In the game, each player tries to be the first to collect 3 gemstone cards of the same kind.

The game starts with the deck of cards in the center, face down. Each player takes a turn picking a card from the pile. That card can be a Gem (12 different gems) or a special card (attack, defense, joker card). Depending on the card, the player may wish to use it immediately (e.g. change the gem type they are collecting, add it to their existing collection if it fits, play it against others etc), keep it for later (for non-gem cards only), discard ("burn") it or put it at the bottom of the pile ("stash").

The game ends when someone collects 3 same gem cards, or if the pile runs out. In the latter case, the winner is determined by the number of gems they have, the number of  attacks they successfully thwarted and/or the number of attacks they didn't block. If all else fails, the winner is determined by the type of gem they were collecting when the pile ran out.

One game will typically run for about 10-20 minutes, depending on how fast the players are, and how many players are in the game. It is easy to grasp, fast-paced, with quite a few hints of strategy, but luck also plays some part.

—description from the designer

("Hissa" is Finnish slang for history, especially as a school subject.)

Trivia board game with six categories of questions with three levels of difficulty.

The Board is a colorful circled labyrinth were you advance with correct answer to the question about history. First player to get in the middle circle with the correct answer from freely chosen category is the winner.

Game has six categories of questions:
I: From ice age to space age (blue)
II: War and peace (red)
III: Science and art (green)
IV: King and peasant (yellow)
Silver: Hard questions from all fields
Gold: Very hard questions from all fields

If you answer correctly to the question from four basic categories, then you will move one space forward. Silver category is worth two moves and gold gives you three moves forward. You can always choose to answer silver or gold question, otherwise the colour of your current space determines the category of the question. With the correct answer you'll go on until you fail to answer correctly, then it's next players turn.

Ending of the game is triggered, when somebody answers correctly to chosen question in the middle circle. Current round is then finished, so that everybody has same amount of turns. If several players are tied, then extra questions are made until winner is decided.

Introducing "DiceBlitz Hockey" - the exhilarating print-and-play ice hockey game that brings the thrill of the rink right to your tabletop! With fast-paced, quick turns, the game activates single players, keeping you on the edge of your seat as you strategize and maneuver your way to victory.

The game is still in "alpha" mode and is available as a print-and-play game for you to try out! Simply print, assemble, and let the action unfold as you roll the dice and experience the excitement of ice hockey like never before.

boardgame: Escape Quest: Achtung! Cthulhu – L'Armée des Profondeurs
description:
Escape Quest is a game book in which you must solve puzzles to advance in your adventure. Each page has its clues: combinations, labyrinths, word games, hidden images ... Your logic and your sense of observation will be put to the test!

In this issue:

February 1942.
The Nazis are developing a secret project called the Army of the Deep. Informed of their intentions, a determined adventurer risks his life and sanity to thwart their plans. He can count on the unwavering loyalty of Sergeant Red and the help of a scientist named Karen Baker. The little group travels through the world at war: Nazi Germany, England, the Black Sea coast, North Africa and all the way to the Philippines, where they find the entrance to a mysterious and frightening universe. The terrible truth comes to our friends: the Nazis and their Japanese allies have made an alliance with unspeakable aquatic creatures capable of invading any territory from the sea. If they succeed, England will fall, then America and the rest of the free world!

—description from the publisher's website (translated)

Who is the master of this land, the psychic who calls himself an angel? Selfish citizens? Or a schemer wearing a monstrous mask? Everyone said that God promised to become the master of this world after seven days. God never appeared, but from the first day, everyone's inner thoughts will be presented to everyone in the next morning.

Victory conditions for the three factions
Leviathan 1: Draw a card from the deck every round and distribute it to anyone other than yourself. Each player can have multiple cards. There were three declarations of artifacts on the field at the same time, and Leviathan won. Leviathan was not allowed to reveal or hint to others what the card he gave to others was.
Angel 1-2: The task of an angel is to preach. Each round marks a player in the spiritual field. When the mark reaches 3 (Leviathan needs seven marks), this character is regarded as a believer. The believer on the spot plus the angel Wins when the number is greater than half the number.
Humans 2-5: Conditions for victory. After seven days, neither Leviathan nor Angel wins.
Cooperation and deceit will of course be required to win, but remember that you are all God's children so in some cases your alignment will change.
At the beginning of the game, each player draws an identity card (Leviathan, human, angel), Leviathan shows his identity, and then draws a card from the pile to represent the beginning of a new day, everyone takes turns to speak, and finally Leviathan decides Who is this card for (the three rounds of getting the card can be activated), and then enter the night, people other than the angels close their eyes, the angels start to preach, and they can put a mark on the spirit collar. At dawn after 10 seconds, Leviathan draws the next card and repeats this cycle seven times.
There are three artifacts in the deck that can be used all the time, three events that happen once and a blank Bible book, a total of seven cards and seven rounds, representing what happened within seven days.
Artifact "Lantern"
You can check the identity of others. You can change the imprint arrangement in the spiritual realm
Artifact "laurel crown"
You can remove a marker from a player
Artifact "Sword"
You can kill a player, and whoever kills the Leviathan becomes the new Leviathan.

Event "The Dragon on the Spire"
Leviathan and Instigator get an extra chance to question
Event "Sermon not from the gods"
Directly discard the cards for the next round (the number of rounds minus one)
Event: "Dark March"
People who have been preached join the angel camp and no longer follow the civilians to victory.
Event 'Blank Bible'
Nothing happened.
Claim: Leviathan can't say what he is dealt, and the person who gets the card can claim that he is any one of the seven claims, and the effect can be activated as long as no one questions it. The person who doubts can open the station when the skill is activated. If it is true, he will be silenced for one round. If it is false, he will get this card from the opponent. No one doubts and can change his claim in the next round. Each person can only question once.

boardgame: Mech Command RTS: Advanced Recon Stealth Pack
description:
The Advanced Recon Pack adds 
2 x 14 new cards. Cloak Generators, Stealth Disruptors, Smart Missiles, Smart Guns, ECM Missile Jammers, Corrosive & Ion weapons, the Assassin Blade and its Elite Variant "Moonlight".
2 x 1 new stealth mechs

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Mech Command RTS: Tactical Warfare Pack
description:
Tactical Warfare Pack adds
2 sets of 14 cards. Turret Launchers, Mine Layers, Construction Arms, Drone Launchers, Shield Arms, Reinforced Armor packs, Extended ammo racks and more. 
2 sets of an engineer mech

Time of the Hunter is a mint tin solo game. Direct a small tribe's group of hunters, using your store of food resources to hunt and gather more food for the Winter ahead. Run out of food or hunting days and you'll lose, but gather enough to see the tribe through Winter and you win.

Your hunters start the day by grabbing some food from the store and heading out to hunt wild beasts. They can choose small or large beasts, with the varying rewards but also difficulty and threats they pose.

Dice rolls and allocation of dice to the sets needed to take down each animal decide success, but every dice roll or re-roll costs one food/energy.

The Legend of Kartlos is a board game based on Georgian mythology and is designed for 1-4 players. Each player takes on the role of the god, with three demigods at their side to help them conquer the mythical kingdom of ancient Georgia and become its ultimate ruler.

Each of the character has  unique strengths and abilities, making the game different and exciting every time you play. You can choose to play as Aiet - the King of Colchis and the father of the legendary Medea, Rokap - the master of the witches, Amiran - the great hero of Georgia, or Atinat - the powerful Goddess of the sun.

—description from the publisher

Panda Party is a fast-paced, family-friendly card game where players race to host the ultimate panda party. With quick-to-learn rules and playful strategy, it’s the perfect fit for game nights with friends, parties, or family afternoons.

Your mission? Be the first to collect five panda guests at your party. Each turn, you’ll draw cards, collect lovable panda characters, and unleash surprise actions to help yourself — or mess with your friends. Expect a little chaos, lots of laughs, and just the right amount of sabotage.

Panda Party combines light strategy with a sense of humour that appeals to both kids and adults. It’s easy to teach in under five minutes, and every round feels different thanks to the variety of card interactions and sneaky moves.

Why it stands out
  • A family-friendly card game that’s great for ages 8+
  • Plays in 15–45 minutes – perfect as a party starter or in between games
  • Compact and portable – take it anywhere, the party never stops
  • Easy to learn, hard to predict – plenty of replay value
  • Designed and illustrated in Australia with maximum mischief in mind

What’s included
  • 154 cards
  • 1 rulebook
  • 2-piece box (15 x 10 x 4.5 cm)

Whether you’re unleashing your inner party animal at a family gathering or setting traps for your friends on game night, Panda Party delivers joyful competition in one fluffy little package.

Introduction:Braunche (from Middle English: braunche, meaning "branch") is a drawless game for two players: Black and White. It is played on the intersections (points) of an initially empty hexagonal board with triangles. The recommended board size is 7 points per side, but boards of 5 or 9 are also valid. Each player has access to a sufficient supply of stones of their own color.

				
				
					Definitions:
     A group is a maximal set of connected stones of the same color. A single stone is also a group.
     A branch of a group is a subgroup of connected stones within the group that occupy the points on the edge. An isolated stone on the edge is also a branch.
     The value of a group is the number of branches it contains according to the triangular score, which is calculated by adding the consecutive natural numbers from 1 to the number of branches in the group.


				
				
					Turns:Black plays first, and then turns alternate. On your turn, perform one of the following actions:


     Place a stone of your color on an empty point.
     Pass your turn.


				
				
					End of the game:The game ends when both players pass their turn consecutively.  At the end of the game, points are counted by adding the value of all friendly groups, plus an extra half point to the player who passed his turn first.

To balance the game, before starting, the first player chooses an integer as the komi value and then the second player chooses a side. This balancing method is called komi pie.

—description from the designer

My 8 year old, Jasper, designed this dice game while we were traveling from home to Target. Happy to share it with you. It fits on a single card (rules included), so you can keep it with you wherever you go! You just need a bunch of dice, but I'm sure you (like us) carry dice everywhere you go anyway. Enjoy!

SETUP

Place the card between yourself and your opponent. Each player receives 1d6, 1d8, 1d10, 1d12, and 1d20. Each player places their token on the START space facing them.

HOW TO PLAY


     Both players roll their corresponding die at the same time. For example: if a player's token is on the d8 space, they roll a d8.
     The player with the lower value moves their token closer to the edge of the cliff.
     If both values are a tie, nothing happens.
     CRITICAL HIT: If the value rolled by a player is the highest value possible for their die, their opponent moves their token closer to the edge of the cliff unless they are on the last space (d20).
     CRITICAL MISS: If the value rolled by a player is a 1, they move their own token closer to the edge of the cliff unless they are on the last space (d20).
     First player to reach the SKULL loses!


—description from the designer

A card game to create and spread awareness about various places in Goa, India. Games designed by Veer Kashyap.

Concept 

     The concept of the game is based on virtual tour of our Goa
     The game is developed based on basic information available in open source for recreational purposes
     The objective is to introduce children and all Board Game lovers to Goa and various tourist attractions of Goa including dishes and restaurants.
     Each Player will be distributed with 10 cards / 08 cards (randomly) from the pack of cards. Every player views his/her cards and tries to match the card in the Discard Pile.
     Each player will place down one card (Tourist Card / Challenges Card) at a time in the Discard Pile. 
     Next player to match the card (Season Colour /Tourist attraction/Number) or undertake the challenge as per Challenge Card.
     Each player will locate the place on the Map to visualize the location. (Optional)
     All players should play their turn and try to completely drop all cards in turn held with them. The player who has NO card remaining at the end will call ‘GO GOA’ and will be declared as Tourist of the Day. Continue to play any number of rounds with your friends and family members.


Benefits

     Introduces to geography of Goa
     Introduces to various important places, tourist attractions, dishes and restaurants of Goa.
     Virtually know our Goa and basic details about tourist places, dishes and Restaurants


Board Game Content 

     Pack of Cards with Tourist Cards (each card grouped under four different seasons) and Challenge cards 
     Tourist Cards (108 cards) with Four different Colors (Summer (orange), Rainy (blue), Winter (grey) and Spring (green)) – Beaches(B), Churches(C), Temples(T), Mosques(M), Restaurants(R), Dishes(D), Forts(F), Museums (Mu), Heritage Houses(H), Eco Tourism(E).
     Challenge Cards (45 Cards) - Change Season, VIP Pass, No Entry, U Turn, Drop 2 Cards, Pick 2 Cards

Up or Down is a roll-and-write game that uses a set of polydice; d4, d6, d8, d10, d12, d20 (also known as RPG set). Players try to make an Up or Down in their turn, by creating a sequence of dice that is either ascending or descending. This earns them points. Scores are counted after 9 rounds and whoever has the most points wins.

Up or Down exists only as a free-to-play (personal use only!) Print and Play version. PDF with rules and scoresheet can be found in the Files section. In addition to the rules and scoresheet you need at least one polydice set to play.

—description from the designer

The Geological Factor is a mini-expansion for Barrage that introduces a new level of challenge and additional variety to the game.

Each basin will be characterized by a particular difficulty in using or building some components of the game and they will provide a specific effect linked to the component itself. This expansion can be used with all maps of the game and is compatible with all expansions released so far (i.e.,Leeghwater Project, The Nile Affair, Map for 4-5 players).

Fly your airplane across North American airports to collect lost luggage. Fill your plane with 26 pieces of A-Z luggage to win the game.

Setup

After assembling the map puzzle into a gameboard, players place an airplane at a random airport, then each grab three destination cards, a luggage card and dry erase marker.

Gameplay

On each turn, a dice roll provides the flight range for all players. Players simultaneously decide upon a destination airport on their route and move their plane to the city’s airport (maximum of dice roll hops).

Using the first or last letter of the city name, players mark a matching letter on their luggage card.

Players discard any matching destination card. Upon filling their luggage card with A-Z matches and having no more airport cards, that player wins.

—description from the publisher

The card game created by a 9 year old with a mission! 50% of all profits donated to conservation efforts in Africa.

In this fast-paced card game, you are part of an elite anti-poaching unit that must protect vulnerable wildlife from extinction!  Use your turn to save, swap or camouflage different species against the poachers.

On your turn, you may draw a card from the draw pile or discard pile.

If drawn from the discard pile, you must keep the card you chose.

If drawn from the draw pile, you may keep, discard, or play the special action - but beware! If you draw a poacher, the card must be played!

Be the first to save all 13 animals to win!

Handmade in Nigeria through partnership with Nibcard Games.

—description from the publisher

Armoured Storm: The Eastern Front is a quick-playing, simple-to-learn game of tank-versus-tank combat in World War Two. The rules are intended for battles between 15 and 30 tanks per side, plus 4 or 5 HQ tanks. This means, in game terms, that each player will command one or more companies of tanks organized into several platoons.

In Armoured Storm, you take the role of a commander first and a gunner/driver second. Your ability to give the right orders at the right time and bring your available firepower to bear more efficiently than your opponent, are more important at the level of command you’re representing. Out-thinking your opponent by being clear about your goals and through clever use of the Tactics Chart will bring you success.

THE ‘YOU COMMAND’ SERIES
‘You Command’ is a series of light wargames by Daniel Mersey. When you play a ‘You Command’ game, your goal is to defeat your opponent by out-thinking them tactically using the innovative Tactics Chart, and then by out-fighting their model army on the tabletop battlefield.

Please note that all Armoured Storm titles share the same basic rules; optional rules and tank stats are different according to the theatre of war.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Fallout: Wasteland Warfare – Enclave Wave Card Expansion Pack
description:
The ominous shadow of human ‘purity’ hangs over the wasteland as the rhythmic crunch of power armored feet echoes from ruin to ruin – The Enclave are making their presence known. Join the cause or fight your corner with new allies and fearsome enemies.

The Enclave themselves bring multiple power armor variants and heavy weapons to the wasteland, ready to reclaim it for their president. Their top brass take to the field, with Colonel Autumn commanding and Frank Horrigan bludgeoning his way though anything brave (or stupid) enough to stand in the way.

At the same time attacks have been increasing from the shoreline. Seasonal migrations and the vibrations of war draw creatures to the fray in increased numbers. Skittering and chittering across the sand, wave of chitinous Mirelurks find prey on the shoreline. Their hunters seek out potential food for hatchlings and their eggs, while larger specimens lay in wait in the depths. All the while sinister monsters hunt the twilight fogs on the shore, glimpsed then gone; few have seen them up close and lived to tell the tale…

All the rules for units, weapons, and equipment in the Enclave Wave are included in this card pack. Also included are new AI Cards, chems, mods, events, dangers and more, all for expanding the Fallout: Wasteland Warfare experience.

Included in the set are 45 poker size and 159 Euro mini cards for:

Enclave Core Box
Enclave High Command
Enclave Soldier Set
Enclave Tesla Set
Enclave Hellfire Set
Frank Horrigan
Unaligned T 45 Power Armour
Mirelurks
Mirelurk Hunters
Mirelurk Hatchlings + Eggs
Mirelurk Queen
Fog Crawler
Lorenzo Cabot

A new series of Core Units introducing exciting openings, requiring new strategies. This time, the box contains 5 sets of Units for each player - 20 cards in total. You can combine them with other additions to Elekt.

—description from the publisher (translated)

Nowa seria Jednostek Podstawowych wprowadzających ekscytujące otwarcia wymagające podjęcia nowych strategii. Tym razem, w pudełku znalazło się 5 zestawów Jednostek dla każdego gracza - w sumie 20 kart. Możecie je łączyć z pozostałymi dodatkami do Elekta.

—description from the publisher

In this expansion, we focus on the locations and events of the Outlands world. You get 10 Missions and 10 Influence and 2 Privileges. Their abilities will overturn your strategies or open up completely new opportunities for you.

—description from the publisher (translated)

W tym dodatku skupiamy się na miejscach i wydarzeniach ze świata Rubieży. Otrzymujecie 10 Misji oraz 10 Wpływów a także 2 Przywileje. Ich zdolności wywrócą wasze strategie albo odkryją przed wami zupełnie nowe szanse.

—description from the publisher

A game in which players race on a colorfully-painted game board. The object is to be the first player to successfully move their painter from the outside edge of the board to the painting ladder in the center. Occasionally, players will have the chance to swing the big rotating brush mounted on top of the ladder, in an attempt to knock over the painters of opposing players. If a player gets their three painters to the ladder first, they win.

—user summary

Goat Lords 2 by Gatwick Games - New Card Games for Families, Adults, Teens, and Kids

The Sequel to the #1 Voted Goat Game of The Year! ? ‣ Get ready for the most exciting Goat game of your life! Expect high drama, uncontrollable laughter & fast action strategy in this wildly popular card game. Ages 8+ | 2-6 player

Goat Lords 2 is a standalone game that brings more goat fun and craziness to the table! Play it by itself, or combine it with the original Goat Lords for the most epic adventure of your life

The goal of Goat Lords is to gather the biggest goat herd. You do so by stacking pairs of matching goat cards in front of you and stealing opponent's goats before the draw pile runs out.

Knowing how to play your cards, when to hold 'em, and who to attack greatly affect how many points you end up when the last goat is played.

Shiner utilizes stylized art deco illustrations to immerse you into the world of 1929's moonshining under world.

Players draft, steal, and swap their cards around the table to try and build the best 5 card moonshining operation possible, or grab the feds to turn rat and raid your opponents. The first player to score 50 points across the rounds wins the game.

This 18 card game has 15 unique cards falling into 5 categories.
Mash is used to set your base score. Cooks multiply the score. Federal Agents reduce the value. Muscle counters Federal Agents. Transport cards have no direct use but can be combined with cards of other categories to complete Raids or Special Operation hands.

—description from the designer

There are five participants in the game Blok Ekipa: Szacun na rejonie. The game consists of as many rounds as there are players, and each round is divided into 4 stages:
				
				
					Stage 1: Robbing LosersThe game begins by showing the Loser from the deck. Then we use Losers Detectors. Whoever sees Loser on it shouts "Loser" and takes the card. This is how you get your first Respects, which determine your victory.
				
				
					Stage 2: Fighting for goodsThe first 4 cards from the Goods deck are revealed and trade takes place. If one player is interested in the purchase - he pays the fee and takes the "shopping" (not necessarily legal). If there are more Participants willing, the "Robbing Losers" takes place again, but only with the use of the Losers Detectors. Whoever wins gets the "stuff". However, you should beware of the Ginger Snitch hidden in the cards. He will quickly inform the City Guard about shady business and bring inspection...
				
				
					Stage 3: InspectionThis is where a participant playing the City Guard comes into action. It may select a delinquent to be inspected and reviewed. If the participant does not have anything suspicious on him, compensation is paid and the contents of the pocket are cashed. However, if he has a shady product, the City Guard confiscates it and returns it to the owner without any profit. Of course, you can try to bribe uniformed officers with a bribe, but they don't have to agree to it!
				
				
					Stage 4: TradeNow it's time to count profits and exchange cash for Respects at the rate of 1 PLN = 1 Respect. As we know, their number will determine the victory. After all transactions are closed, the roles are switched: all characters move one player further (including the City Guard).

The cycle repeats for as many rounds as there are players (5 pawns are available). After the rounds are over, the exchange takes place and all accumulated Respects are counted. Who has the most? Who really "rules the area"? Just open the box to find out.

A push-your-luck game about getting a full night's sleep, despite that darn cat. Based on true events.

In this game, 2-4 players take turns rolling dice and then using the results to choose a span of numbered tiles to reveal. Each tile will either be a 'Z' or a 'Meow!' Score points for each Z, but if you hit any Meows you get nothing. Then, secretly put the tiles back, so only you know what tile is where! First player to forty Z's wins the game.

Hegemony of Faith is a card and party game for 4-8 players, every player's main purpose in the game is to find a way to collect the most believer cards and become the only winner.

The biggest game feature in Hegemony of Faith is that: every player never worries about death or being eliminated from the game. This game has a surrender and temporary cooperation mechanism. When a player does not have any believer card in his turn, he will find another player to surrender and swear allegiance (Of course not true allegiance), become a Follower in a sect, and cooperate against another player. However, the Follower players can still secretly recruit their forces in order to seek a comeback.

According to the surrendering mechanism above, it also has a betrayal mechanism. After becoming a Follower, players can use specific skills or action cards to betray and take away the believer cards of their Leader. In addition, of course, there are also special cards, that allow players to prevent betrayal.

In the game, players will experience what it feels like a sect Leader. You have to consider how to use the cards and the strategy to get the most benefit. You also may become another's Follower and conspiracy to betray. No matter what you do, you have to find a way to become the most powerful sect Leader to win the game.

description:
Immerse yourself in the world of Battle by Order, a strategic and intense two-player card game where champions of different classes engage in thrilling duels. Choose from one of four unique champions: the high-damage Fury Fighter, the cunning Poisoner who lays traps and drains opponents, the Gear Master who excels at building powerful combos, or the Shadow Wraith, a specter that deals burst damage and controls opponents.

In each round, players strategically select three cards from their hand of five and place them face down in anticipation of the upcoming clash. The tension builds as both players simultaneously flip their cards, revealing their chosen actions. The game features four types of cards: Action, Passive, Counter, and Trap, each offering a different strategic approach to the battle.

The game unfolds in a series of these fast-paced rounds, where players must outwit and outmaneuver their opponents, all while managing their hand and planning their next moves. The game ends when a player successfully reduces their opponent's HP from 20 to zero, claiming victory.

Battle by Order is a simultaneous action card game that balances strategy and intensity. The game's mechanics, combined with the unique abilities of the champions, ensure a dynamic and engaging gameplay experience. Whether you're a seasoned card game veteran or a newcomer to the genre, Battle by Order promises a thrilling ride that will keep you coming back for more.

-description from designer

Trash Stash is a simple children’s game for 2-4 players. In the game you draw a card and get to return one of your pieces of trash matching the item and color on the card. You drop that item into the garbage truck’s garbage can and then pull a lever to load the trash into the back of the truck.

Occasionally the truck will spit all the trash out at the active player. That player adds all the trash to their pile. The first player to get rid of all their trash is the winner.

"Ange Unite" is a two-player card game played one-on-one.

Following "Ange Vierge" which was developed as a trading card game from 2013 to 2017

boardgame: A game about seriously surreal creatures and trying to identify them after someone makes noises.
description:
This is a 'reimagining' of AGAWWCATTITASMN and AGACCCATTITASMN. 80 new creatures that beg the question, "What sort of noise would this creature make?"

Five seriously surreal creatures are shown in a central display. One player (the noise-maker) randomly chooses a target creature.

Everyone else closes their eyes whilst the noise-maker makes noises.

After opening their eyes and having time to consider, everyone votes which creature they think may have been the target creature.

Anyone in the majority gets a point, whether right or wrong. It was a sensible guess, as determined by your peers!

Now the target creature is revealed. If the majority was correct, the noise-maker gets a point. It was a good noise!

If the majority was NOT correct, then any player who made a correct guess also gets a point.

Replace the creatures. Repeat.

After everyone has had a chance to make a noise, the game is over. Most points wins.

-description from designer

The Dragon Castle is shrouded in dark legends. Do you dare to venture into its heart as a powerful magus, a fierce woman warrior, or an elusive female thief? Upgrade your character, fight monsters and overcome the pitfalls lurking at every step. Only the mightiest hero has a chance to defeat the dragon himself and get the most valuable treasure!

 Dragon Castle: New Challenges is improved over Dragon Castle not only by the design, but also in the rules, which have made it a much better and more balanced game. Above all, there is another dueling system (which almost eliminates the element of chance). The game as opposed to the original version offers extensive character development.

description:
Through tactics and karma to wealth and fame...

In 16th century India, the powerful empire of the Great Moguls rises between the Indus and the Ganges rivers. Taking on the role of rajas and ranis – the country's influential nobles – players in Rajas of the Ganges race against each other in support of the empire by developing their estates into wealthy and magnificent provinces. Players must use their dice wisely and carefully plot where to place their workers, while never underestimating the benefits of good karma. Success will bring them great riches and fame in their quest to become legendary rulers.

We will select members from within the idol group and organize a unit team.
Let's line up the members well so that you can create more areas of expertise than the other team in each genre!
At the end of the game, teams compete for each genre icon and the team with the most points wins. You also score from sun symbols visible by your team's formation.
The player who builds the best team and collects the most ♥ points wins the game.

description:
Set 12 years after Mercurial, Mercurial: Alchemia puts you in the shoes of an aspiring Alchemist in the thriving allied hub of Concordia. During nights, you'll cultivate a personal terrarium brimming with fantastical flora and fauna, harvesting them to unlock their potential for crafting elixirs. During the day, you'll explore the bustling Taliq'ir Bazaar to acquire seeds and eggs to introduce into your growing ecosystem. You’ll also visit Runaliths — colossal crystalline structures — to bestow enchantments on your terrarium. Chance a meeting with rival alchemists and their companion Arcanas, and you may forge collaborations amid competition.

As you brew experimental potions to sell to the Guild, your choice of ingredients used is vital. Their distinct qualities can enhance or negate each other, with your mastery and skill determining whether strides in magical advancement are made or utter failure.

Embark on an alchemical odyssey where your magnum opus awaits or perhaps even discover the fabled Philosopher's stone!

Welcome to Party Wanted, an exhilarating fusion of deck-building and roguelike gameplay, crafted for 1-8 players seeking a truly cooperative gaming experience. In Party Wanted, players embark on a journey through the Pyramid of Chaos, where they'll vanquish monsters, tackle powerful bosses, and above all, party as a unified team!

Adding a twist to the traditional card game format, Party Wanted offers optional drinking rules as well as social games that bring even more fun to the table, making it a perfect choice for game nights.

boardgame: The Silver Bayonet: The Carpathians Castle Fier
description:
High in the Carpathian Mountains stands the crumbling Castle Fier. Once home to a powerful warlord, the castle cast a dark shadow across the nearby villages, until crusaders attacked with sword and flame to put an end to its menace. Though history passed into folklore, the ruins of Castle Fier remained shunned by all as a cursed site. Now, horrors have been seen moving at night. An army gathers. Something has awoken in the ruins. With the political situation in the surrounding region becoming increasingly unstable, France, Prussia, Britain, and the other powers have dispatched their best agents to investigate the ruins, eliminate any threats, and acquire any treasures that could prove useful in the ongoing fight against the harvestmen… and each other.

The Carpathians: Castle Fier is a supplement for The Silver Bayonet: A Wargame of Napoleonic Gothic Horror, in which the special units must fight their way through the ruins of a menacing haunted castle. It features two campaigns – one competitive and one for solo or cooperative play – as well as new monsters to fight, soldiers to recruit, and treasure to unearth.

-description from publisher's website

boardgame: Castles by the Sea: Riptide Expansion

Castles by the Sea: Riptide Expansion introduces a new mechanic to the base game.

The Riptide Expansion is played exactly like the base game, but with one addition to the Hazard step. At the end of each player's Hazard step, they must roll the Tide Die and move the tide around the board!

This expansion can be mixed with the base game components, and includes options for prebuilt Kingdoms.

The base game is required for this expanion.

description:
Introduction:Sundraval (from Gothic: Sundrawald, meaning "separated territory") is a drawless game for two players: White and Black. It is played on the intersections (points) of an initially empty hexagonal board. The recommended size is 9 points per side, but boards of 7 or 11 are also valid. Each player has access to a sufficient supply of stones of their own color.

				
				
					Definitions:
     A group is a set of connected stones of the same color. A single stone is also a group.
     A territory is a maximal set of uninterrupted empty points bounded by stones of a single color that fill all adjacent locations.
     A path is a set of N uninterrupted empty points (where N can be zero).
     A strangled group is a group that, at the end of the game, meets the following 3 conditions:



     There is a path from there to larger enemy groups or with territory.
     It has no territory, nor a path from there to non-strangled friendly groups.
     There is no path from there to smaller enemy groups that are strangled.


				
				
					Turns:Black plays first and then turns alternate. On your turn, perform one of the following actions:


     Place a stone of your color on an empty point that has a path to enemy groups.
     Pass your turn.


				
				
					End of the game:The game ends when both players consecutively pass their turn.

Immediately before scoring, remove all strangled enemy groups from the board. Then, each of a player's groups with N adjacent territories earns (N^2 + N) / 2 points, i.e., according to the triangular number. In addition, the player with the group that contains the largest territory receives a half point extra (or the next largest).

The player with the total value of groups with territories in their possession wins.

To make the game fair, before it begins, the first player places two initial stones, one black and one white, at any empty point on the board, and the second player chooses the sides. This balancing method is called the two-stone pie rule.

				
				
					Notes:Special thanks to Drew Edwards and Dale Walton who valuable contributions to improve key aspects; Drew Edwards optimized a key rule, while Dale Walton refined the wording.

–description from designer

A trading card game based on famous arcade card game Sangokushi Taisen.
Players in this game can use characters from Three Kingdoms period of ancient China, to fight with the opponent. The player who break all opponent's wall card and attack the opponent will win the game.

You have a full day ahead of you. It's going to be just you, the lake, and a bunch of fish-like creatures waiting to join you on your journey for the most relaxing day ever. Day on the Lake is a light dexterity game that has you throwing a die in a lake print-and-play arena, catching fish, earning money, and upgrading your equipment. Will you be an angler champion this day on the lake?

—description from the designer

Roll a die and check the fish you caught. If you passed the criteria for success then you gain money. Try and collect the most money from different shaped fish in ten turns. If you have extra money you can upgrade your die, giving you much better odds at fishing.

Get ready to till the soil and sow the seeds of success! In our new single player tile laying game, you will become a master farmer, harvesting crops for the most points possible. Carrots, tomatoes, and potatoes are the crops of choice, and your goal is to cultivate the most fertile fields and maximizing your harvest. With easy to learn gameplay this game will challenge you, test your skills, and bring out the farmer in you! So grab your tools, get ready to get your hands dirty, and become the top farmer in the land.

—description from the designer

Pick up a random tile from the face down pile and place it down. Try to connect and chain the crop types so that you can get a higher market value for that crop. From the beginning, only one of the crops values are shown and later in the game after you scored 10 points the other two's values are revealed.

7 new city tiles to be shuffled with those of the base game, bringing even more setup variability and combo possibilities on the map !

Setup

Remove the Medicine City tiles from the game and the Medicine Income tiles from the game.
Add the new City tiles to the base game City tiles, then randomly draw the 7 City tiles to be used.

These tiles give you a permanent effect as soon as you place a cube on it :

- When you place a new Student on the Technology track, perform a level 2 Technology action
- When you play a Master Card, perform a level 1 Lessons action
- When you place a Development Tile on your desk, gain 3 coins
- When you perform a Travel action, you can get a 1-time discount of 1 coin when you pass through routes with a cost
- You can now as a quick action, pay 3 coins to add 2 to the value of the Basic Action you are performing
- You now gain 2 times the lowest value active income of your Study board during the end round phase
- When you place a piece of your color on an Objective tile, gain 4VP
This small promo expansion adds 4 new Book cards to play with the base game.
This small promo expansion adds 3 new Characters cards to play with the base game.

Embark on a journey into the captivating world of mathematics through an engaging card game that celebrates brilliant mathematicians and fascinating mathematical concepts. Battle one or two other players in knocking out mathematicians one by one. The last one left is the winner. Knock out mathematicians by drawing digit cards and using mathematical operations that calculate to your opponent's target number. Use Chaos Cards to gain an advantage. This is a game of strategy and numbers.

boardgame: Однажды в Чикаго: 1930 (Once upon a time in Chicago: 1930)
description:
Once Upon a Time in Chicago: 1930 is a development of Once Upon a Time in Chicago, but you don't have to go through the first game to play the new one. This is a detective game about the adventures of Chicago Tribune staffer John Fitzpatrick, an investigative reporter, in the thick of the events taking place in Chicago in the early 1930s.

At a time when the Great Depression was breaking out in the United States and Prohibition was about to be repealed and Prohibition had been in force for over a decade, the influence of gangsters was especially strong and no event in Chicago seemed to happen without their interference. Playing the role of journalist John Fitzpatrick, you have to make sense of six intricate stories, each with its own independent plot, while also being partly related to the others, its own unique set of figures and clues.

Cops and gangsters, musclemen and bootleggers, civilians and the Chicago Tribune-all the people you meet along the way can provide valuable information or just deceive you.

Detective experience, intuition and logic must guide you through a web of lies, innuendo and coincidence. Slipshod facts, suspicious events, cyphers and charades are the things that will help shed light on these mysteries and help unravel the most complex cases. More than 100 unique locations await you during the game. Among them, for example, the Chicago Tribune, the Boeing Aircraft Company's test centre, Imperial Restaurant, the University of Chicago, and the small town of Dixon, just a couple of hours away from Chicago.

The ever-busy newsroom of Paper Travels Magazine is looking for an intern to join the staff.

In the job description it says: "various tasks"... Well, someone has to bring the coffee!

If luck and cunning are in your favor and you manage to send all your reporters (run out of cards) you will reach the role of CEO and have more privileges than anyone else in the office. But beware! If you still have cards at the end of the round you will become an Intern and you will have to pay the floor fee...


This is a reimplementation of the traditional game Zheng Shangyou.

Quatsch! is a German language learning Party Game consisting of different vocabulary cards, Nouns, Verbs, Adjectives, and a judgement die. Players shuffle the Noun and Adjective decks together, and keep the verb deck separate.

Players take turns judging sentence submissions which are often nonsensical or silly. The judging player rolls the judgement die and plays one card from their hand, the other players will then create a sentence, incorporating the card played by the judging player that most closely matches the feeling of the result of the judgement die. The winning player then wins the vocabulary card presented by the judging player.

Players continue playing until the verb deck runs out of cards, the player with the most cards won from judgement is the winner.

Introduction:Gawi (from Gothic: gawi, meaning "land, region") is a drawless game for two players: Black and White. It is played on the intersections (points) of an initially empty hexagonal board. The recommended board size is 9 points per side, but boards of 7 or 11 are also valid. Each player has access to a sufficient supply of stones of their own color.

				
				
					Definitions:
     A group is a set of connected stones of the same color. A single stone is also a group.
     A liberty is an empty point adjacent to a group and is said to be obstructed if an enemy stone occupies it or is adjacent to it.
     A smothered group is a group with all its liberties obstructed.
     A territory is one or more connected empty points. You own a territory if all the stones connected to it are of your color.


				
				
					Turns:Black plays first, then turns alternate. On your turn, perform one of the following actions:


     Place a stone of your color on an empty point where it is not smothered, then remove from the board all smothered enemy groups.
     Pass your turn.


				
				
					End of the game:The game ends when both players pass their turn consecutively. At the end of the game, points are counted by adding up friendly territory and captured enemy stones, plus awarding an extra half point to the player who passed their turn first.

To balance the game, before starting, the first player chooses an integer as a komi value and then the second player chooses the sides. This balancing method is called pie komi.

–description from designer

boardgame: Spearhead: The Balloon Goes Up! Volume 1
description:
The Balloon Goes Up! is a series which simulates hypothetical armored warfare in Western Europe, the Middle East and Asia from the mid 1980’s to the present day, all using a consistent core set of rules coupled with a situation-specific playbook that allows for a variety of situations to be portrayed. The first game in the series, Spearhead, represents a summer 1986 conflict between elements of the Soviet and East German armies and United States, West German, and French armies. The game emphasizes how long-range detection and firepower technologies dramatically increased the depth of the theater of operations in modem warfare. This “Deep Engagement” waged far behind the front lines can determine the outcome of a campaign even before the opposing ground forces truly come to grips. Players must learn how to fight and win the Deep Engagement to achieve overall victory, taking into account the different ways the forces of the two sides were prepared to fight.

Spearhead scenarios range from smaller battle scenarios that deal with specific situations, locations, and forces, and the larger campaign style scenarios that portray the wider battle between Warsaw Pact and NATO forces. Battle scenarios depict Warsaw Pact drives into the province of Hesse, with objectives of Kassel, the Fulda Gap, Frankfurt, and ultimately the Rhine River.

The game provides players with accurate accurate orders of battle for the participants down to battalion level (based on 1986 force levels)—the US V Corps, West German III Korps, French 1st Army, Soviet 8th Guards and 1st Guards Tank Armies, and East German 3rd Army. Spearhead is based on Mark Herman’s Flashpoint: Golan system, which has been updated and refined. The game uses a chit-pull activation system, with divisions being the primary formations. Game scale is 4km per hex, turns are 1 day (12-hour activation impulses).

Components:
Two 22” x 34” map sheets that combine to form a 34” x 44” map
Five counter sheets containing 1170 double-sided 9/16” counters
Twelve 8.5” x 11” Player Aid Cards
One 10-sided die
One Rules Booklet
One Playbook

Players: 
2 but the game is well-suited to team play

-description from publisher

Lights Out Racing — A fast-paced board game inspired by FORMULA 1 (F1).

The first car to cross the finish line after completing a predetermined number of laps wins. Who will stand atop the podium?

Get ready for an intense race where you'll overtake opponents one by one, strategically store and deploy ERS to gain the upper hand, and follow close behind your opponents for that DRS boost. When you enter a braking zone, brake with precision or suffer wheel lockups.

Pit stops, track position, and track limit warnings add more to the mix! This game is the adventure you've been waiting for and makes spending quality time together so much fun. Unplug around a table to create memories that will last a lifetime and span generations.

All drivers playing the game share the same turn, and you each roll two colored dice simultaneously. Drivers move their cars the entire value of the dice roll, starting with the driver furthest from the lead.

But that’s just the start of it! Each turn is full of excitement as players do so much more than move their dice roll. Your turn incorporates strategy as you use your ERS, DRS, overtakes, braking zones, and pit stops.

-description from publisher

In this final 55-card installment to the Heroes of Krakoa Story Arc, the powerful X-Force team will give you the power to vanquish your opponents.

Just as there’ve been several X-Men over the decades, there’ve been plenty of other teams playing their part to support mutantkind. Taking just as active an approach is the team at the heart of Vs System 2PCG: X-Force.
