boardgame: Однажды в Чикаго: Ограбление поезда (Once upon a time in Chicago: Train Robbery)
description:
John Fitzpatrick's new investigation: at night, a gang of miscreants stopped and robbed an express train carrying a large sum of money for the city treasury at the entrance to Chicago Station.

In addition to the familiar newspaper articles, ciphers and clues, there are new, additional gameplay mechanics in this mission. For example, players now need to pay attention to the time of day, as the result of visiting some locations will depend on it.

The New Brotherhood is the second issue of the Heroes of Krakoa Story Arc. This 55-card set includes Super Villains across the Marvel Universe and chronicles the chaos they have unleashed upon humanity. Though mutants now find themselves thriving perhaps more than ever on Krakoa, there are still those that contest that harmony. Those foes and shifters of the status quo are on their way to your decks with Vs System 2PCG: The New Brotherhood.

When you build to great heights, you have more to lose when the flame rises. One thing is certain though, that nothing will deter mutantkind from their goals. With the X-Men and Brotherhood working together for the good of all, seemingly nothing can stop the united Homo Superior.

Play as 26 Main Characters including new versions of returning favorites and others appearing for the first time!

description from publisher's website

Harvest Fair is a tabletop game for 2 to 4 players aged 8+ which takes about 30 minutes to play. It recreates the excitement of a traditional community 'harvest fair' at which the players exhibit the produce they've grown that year and are awarded prizes.

In the game, each season tokens are drawn from the bag and allocated to Garden Centres. Eventually a player visits a Garden Centre to claim tokens. After three seasons the players have acquired their tokens, which represent various kinds of fruit, vegetables and flowers. As the game ends trophies and rosettes are awarded at the Harvest Fair, depending on a player’s tokens. The winner of the Grand Trophy takes the victory.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Undaunted: Normandy – Promo Scenario 13: Castle Itter
description:
A brand new Scenario for Undaunted: Normandy released with the first printing.

The last days of the war in Europe

Castle Itter – 5 May 1945

The Battle for Castle Itter was fought near the Austrian village of Itter on 5 May 1945, in the last days of the War in Europe. US soldiers joined forces with Wehrmacht Infantrymen, an SS officer, an Austrian resistance fighter, and recently freed French prisoners of war to defend an Austrian castle against an attacking force from the 17th SS Panzergrenadier Division. It has been called the strangest battle of WW2 and is the only known instance of US and German forces fighting side-by-side.

In Planets map pack, terrain might be struck by meteorites falling from the sky.

-description from publisher's website

Lost & Found is a historical strategy card game of community, family, and lost and found objects. Build your community, take care of your family, avert disasters, and don’t let any of your neighbors go destitute. Any or all can win—or if you are not careful, all can lose.

Lost & Found explores how a code of law called Mishneh Torah addresses lost and found possessions. Written in the 12th century by Maimonides, a great physician, philosopher, Jewish scholar, and sage, the law gives guideposts for behavior, but you decide how to solve dilemmas. Explore the ways in which this medieval religious legal system helped to hold society together and keep neighbors cooperating with each other.

In medieval North Africa, towns are built by families like yours. Show your strategic and collaborative skills in Lost & Found, a historical strategy game of balance between community and family needs. Take on one of five roles in a struggle to address dilemmas, overcome crises, and avert disasters that threaten to destroy everything you know and love. Look out for your family, but be sure to collaborate with your neighbors as well. Any number of players can win, but if anyone goes destitute, everyone loses.

Will you follow the laws of lost and found possessions? Will you break them? Will you go above and beyond the law?

—description from the publisher
publishers: MAGIC Center Initiative in Religion, Culture & Policy

description:
In Gold Rush, players will try to build their camps near gold deposits so they gain as many victory points as possible from expedition.

-description from publisher's website

In Amazon map pack, players will be researching new species of animals. The more they discover the more points they will receive.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Lost & Found: Order in the Court
description:
Lost & Found: Order in the Court – the Party Game is a humorous and historical storytelling game of objects lost and found.

“The court rules that if the bird drops meat on your field, you may keep it.” How did this case get to court? It’s up to you. In Lost & Found: Order in the Court – the Party Game, you’ll need to think fast to create accurate and humorous scenarios in a quest to explain religious laws as they were written in medieval Fustat (Old Cairo). Show off your on-the-fly storytelling skills in this easy-to-learn, yet mentally complex card game.


     Pretend you are a medieval Egyptian! Work your way backwards from the legal ruling to the original dispute. Whether you try for historical accuracy, humor, or presentation skills is up to you.



     You be the judge! Award points for the best story when it’s your turn in the judge’s seat.


Order in the Court, a new game in the Lost & Found series, explores how a code of law called Mishneh Torah addresses lost and found possessions. Written in the 12th century by Maimonides, a great physician, philosopher, Jewish scholar, and sage, the law gives the basis for many interesting court cases. Try to figure out how they might have gotten to a court in the first place! Explore the ways in which this medieval religious legal system helped to hold society together by explaining rulings in the law. Why can’t three people simultaneously read from the same scroll? Why is your lost basket more important to return than your teacher’s? Tell your story with Lost & Found: Order in the Court – the Party Game!

—description from the publisher
publishers: MAGIC Center Initiative in Religion, Culture & Policy

description:
In a remote town where adventurers gather, the annual "Fighting Tournament" is about to be held.
The rules of this year's tournament are tag-team battles.
The adventurers are trying to decide who will be their partner in order to win the tournament.

Each team takes turns to play their turn.
The winner is the team that eliminates both of the enemy team's characters from combat.

boardgame: アルケミストのアトリエ (Alchemist no Atelier)
description:
A new student at the Alchemist Academy is given the task of creating a cure for an epidemic that is ravaging the city.

In each turn players choose to do one of the actions, that is, going to tavern to look for allies, buying items on the market place, going to an expedition to gather materials, creating materalis at your workshop or taking takst from the guild.

The game ends when a player gets 30 points.

Coming with a smaller board than the base game, Tank Chess: Portable is a standalone expansion ideal for traveling, playing in reduced spaces, or in a time constraint.

The games played on a 12 x 12 in / 29 x 29 cm square board play out quicker, the gameplay is usually more dynamic, and tense, as the Command Tank's escape is looming from the first few turns, and clashes and first casualties can be expected, basically, from turn 2. You’ll want to pay close attention to corridors closing due to vehicle destruction at all times, as you don’t want to block your own pieces from preventing a quick escape of the opponent's Command Tank.

There is the same set of pieces and flags as in the Standard version (2 x 16 pieces), so you can create many completely different custom setups and get an infinitely replayable game out of this single expansion.

Tank Chess: Extended is a standalone Tank Chess expansion featuring a 24x24 board, that provides longer games, filled with thorough thinking and calculation at every stage. In general, in games on a board this large, with a lot of pieces, the battle phase is usually divided into more segments, as engagements take place in several areas on the board, and each player tries to shift the focus to the side where he has a better chance of gaining an advantage. The endgame tightly depends on the outcome of the battles, as the player who prevails in complex tactical battles usually has a free path for their Command Tank, or a clear opportunity to surround and destroy the opponent's Command Tank.

The games on the Extended, 24x24 square board games are more demanding in terms of space, and also long-lasting, with more elaborate initial strategies, and a lot of tactical maneuvering and depth. In addition to the pieces from the base game, TC Extended set also contains Super Heavy Tanks, Recon Tanks, Heavy Bulldozers, and Light Howitzers. The set contains these additional tanks in 4 colors, so if you combine the content of TC: Extended with the base game, you will have the possibility to play 4-player games with a full set of pieces.

Tank Chess: Fun Set Plus is an expansion that represents the continuation of the content-expanding line started with Tank Chess: Fun Set, which was the original Tank Chess expansion.

The Fun Set Plus expansion is focused mainly on new vehicles that allow even more diverse games, but there are also new obstacles added and the road feature, which speeds up movement.

The pieces featured in this expansion (non conclusive) are: 2 x Tankette; 1x Flamethrower Tank; 1x Heavy Tank Destroyer; 1x Smokelauncher Tank; 2x Infantry Tank; 1x Minelaying truck; 3x Landing craft; 2x Gun Boat; 1x Fast Boat and 2x Pillbox, in each color.

Given that Fun Set Plus contains wide water areas, as well as many amphibious vehicles and armed boats, the character of the game changes a lot as it provides additional opportunities for an extremely varied gameplay. There will be some interesting board setups with lots of water surfaces provided in the brochure, which can be used as an inspiration for creating even more custom setups.

boardgame: しあわせのいと (Shiawase no Ito)
description:
Competitive deduction game with up to 6 turns to settle the case
Beginner-friendly system that requires no notes
Enjoy the fascinating world view created in collaboration with Dr. Metawo Ueda

Mobs of Mayhem City is a tabletop game for 3 to 5 players aged 8+ which takes 10 to 15 minutes to play. Each turn, one of the players takes the set of Mayhem Tiles and drops them onto the table so they land on or near the bag, which represents Mayhem City. The players then have five seconds to decide what action to take. These include prospecting, cattle rustling, a bank heist, or an ambush. Everyone decides secretly and at the same time. When time's up, everyone reveals their decision and the actions are resolved. Rewards are paid in gold nuggets and gems from the pool, or are sometimes stolen from other players. When the pool is exhausted the game ends, and the richest player wins.

—description from the designer

Some newly added Tank Chess pieces feature a new mechanic, which makes them stand out from the pieces in the Fun Set Plus expansion. That is why there is a special Airborne (mini) Expansion. It contains a total of 12 pieces (6 in each color): 2x Airborne Light tank; 2x Armed Off-road vehicle (airborne), and 2x Anti-Aircraft tank.

Airborne pieces can, under certain conditions, land on almost any square. The main landing condition is that there are no Anti-Aircraft tank pieces nearby, as they deter planes carrying airborne pieces. This way, the game gets a completely new dimension, and, literally, it is the third dimension, because the players have to keep in mind that the opponent can at any moment drop a piece from the skies and change the outcome of the game. That is why the Anti-Aircraft tank pieces are of particular importance, especially when in vicinity of the Command Tank, defending it. 
The brochure for this expansion will feature many obstacle setups (for all board sizes) in which the Airborne pieces play the central role.

An exciting obstacle race - The race has begun and the riders are on the course! Can they beat the obstacles or will some of them fall back in the race? Who will get a head start and who will be the first past the finishing line? The tension lasts until the very last move due to the costant exciting back and forth movement of the ponies!

Introduction:Taugo (from Proto-Germanic: taugô, meaning "string") is a board game for two players: Black and White. It is played on the intersections (points) of a square board initially empty without edge lines, with the closest line to this edge called the perimeter line. The recommended board size is 16 points per side. Each player has access to a single set of identical stones in two colors, one color on the front and another on the back.

				
				
					Definitions:
     A string is a set of stones of the same color connected orthogonally to each other. A single stone is also considered a string.
     A knot is an area of 2x2 stones of the same color. The formation of knots within strings is prohibited.
     To flip is to replace a stone with one of the opposite color.
     A cut point is an empty point orthogonally adjacent to a single stone of an enemy string, such that to reach it, you must do so in a straight line from a friendly stone, crossing the string, even if there are more enemy stones in between.
     To cut is to flip enemy stones by accessing their cut points with your stones. You can only cut enemy stones orthogonally adjacent to at least two stones of the same color.
     The value of a string is the number of stones it contains at the perimeter line, calculated using triangular scoring, which involves summing the consecutive natural numbers from 1 to the number of stones at the perimeter line.


				
				
					Turns:Black plays first, and then turns alternate. On your turn, perform one of the following actions:


     Place (i) a stone of your color on an empty point not orthogonally adjacent to friendly stones or (ii) place one or more stones of your color, each on empty points in a straight line, with the first adjacent orthogonally to a friendly stone, possibly cutting enemy strings in the process.
     Pass your turn.


At the end of your turn, there must be no friendly stones in any empty point beyond the perimeter line, unless you use that point as a cut point.

				
				
					End of the game:The game ends when both players consecutively pass their turn. At the end of the game, points are tallied by summing the value of all friendly groups, plus a half-point bonus to the player with the fewer number of strings. Additionally, extra points are awarded for each stone that has reached the edge of the board according to triangular scoring, but without considering the strings to which they belong.

—description from the designer

A hidden role game in which players are children trapped in a house around which an Oni is lurking. But some of the players are puppets sent by the Oni who will try to lure the players into a false sense of security. A player will move to one of three types of locations: Rooms to search and obtain item cards, Hidding place to be safe from Oni and the Entrance, where a player can "peak through the window" (aka look at the top card of the Oni deck) to know which card will be moved in front of the entrance or place an item card under the current Oni card in front of the entrance.
At the end of the round, the card in front of the entrance is revealed. If the card is an Oni, every players outside a hiding place will be devoured. The card is then removed and the next card on the Oni deck is placed face down in front of the entrance.
The children win by placing a talisman at the entrance the turn an Oni will be revealed.
The Oni's Envoy win if there are less children alive remaining than them.

Translated game introduction;
Today, the same room, the same friends, the same people.
"What do you want to do?" "I want to play."
Such an ordinary day is suddenly disrupted.

Suddenly, It is pitch black outside the room that was brightly lit just a moment ago.
You hear a noise coming from the living room, which is supposed to be empty. Gently, You take a peek.
What is that? Is that...? Not a person, with horns? An Oni?
Your companions peek outside the room, livid and trembling.

If we continue to hide, we will be eaten eventually. It's obviously dangerous outside, but we must somehow find a way to get rid of the demon.

Working together with your friends, you are going to bring back that ordinary but happy life!

But...? There seems to be impostors among your friends... Something doesn’t add up...

boardgame: Shadows of Brimstone: DiceFest 2019 Promo Cards
description:
Dice Fest 2019 convention promos. They were available through the Web store.

Mask of Warai:
Forbidden Fortress artifact, Clothing, face, mask. +2 Sanity. Your to hit rolls of 1 do 1 automatic wound to a random model adjacent to you, ignoring defense.

Sword Cane:
Gear, Hand Weapon, Blade. 2 Handed. +1 Move. Your combat hits are +1 damage. Once per turn you may re-roll one of your defense rolls and one of your melee-to-hit rolls.

In Ruffians, you are an up and coming pirate captain, striving to become rich and famous. Over the course of the game you will recruit a ruthless crew of fuzzy critters, raid cities, collect treasure, and increase your bounty. After all, the best way to measure fame as a pirate captain is to watch your bounty rise!

The goal of Ruffians is to be the pirate captain with the highest bounty at the end of the game. Your bounty increases as you gain notoriety through raids. You will need to recruit a crew to take on raids and collect treasures.

—description from the publisher

In Hamlet: By the Lake, you take your village building lakeside! Form lakes, build boats, go fishing, and feed your Villagers seasonal meals that give them special abilities. Hamlet: By the Lake adds variability, customizability and more individual control of resources, taking the strategy levels for Hamlet well above sea level.

—description from the publisher

Kao the Kangaroo is an adventure board game for the whole family. Take on the role of the jumping Kao the Kangaroo and traverse abstract worlds familiar to fans of the video game.

Build the board by adding new tiles and move your Kangaroo collecting precious coins, treasures and cards. Jump on them and collect valuable coins, treasures in chests and cards. The player who collected most coins and oponent cards will be the winner.

Be careful! Opponents can cross your plans, so be sure bravely and cleverly overcome any diversity. You can use Challenge Cards to slow down other players and jump after jump get closer to victory!

A modular game board consisting of 37 tiles from places known from the video game!
Dynamic gameplay that shows you to take action during another player’s turn!
The simple mechanics of the platform game will provide hours of entertainment for the whole family!

—description from the publisher

An escape room game where you're trying to find captain Aamos Uutos' greatest treasure.

Aamos Uutos sailed lake Saimaa for decades, and you have the keys to his greatest treasure in your hands. You only need to solve his puzzles to find out what the treasure really is.

Aye, Dark Overlord! is a fantasy party game filled with humor that's fast to play and easy to learn.

Each player is an evil goblin servant of the evil Dark Overlord — Rigor Mortis, the Master of all Evils — and all of them are gathered at the Dark Overlord's knees to explain why they brought that last important mission, ordered by "His Evil Excellence" himself, to failure again. Panic wraps you in its freezing grip; what can you do to save your poor skin? Could you say you are not the one to blame, but your inept companions? Yes, of course, that's the solution! There is only one little problem: They've probably got exactly the same idea...and the Dark Overlord is not known for his mercy...

This roleplaying game is all about telling lies and shifting the blame on your poor fellow ones, so the righteous wrath of your Master can give them what they deserve!

Aye, Dark Overlord! Luxastra is a new version of Aye, Dark Overlord! that is playable on its own while also capable of being combined with the original game (red and/or green box).

				
				
					==============================================Ciò che non sarà salvato, sarà purgato!

Mille fuochi fatui si illuminano, quando fai ritorno nella sala del Trono Profano dopo un’altra missione andata male. Il Sovrano Piumato, primo fra i sette Dèi Antichi, non ne sarà certo compiaciuto! Presto sarai dinanzi al Suo cospetto, ed Egli ti interrogherà su questo ennesimo fallimento.

Che fare per conservare integra la pellaccia? Magari potresti raccontargli che la colpa non è tua, ma di quegli imbecilli dei tuoi compari? Ma sì, certo! Ecco la soluzione! C’è un solo, piccolo problema: la stessa cosa sarà venuta in mente anche a loro.

Racconta panzane! Scarica la Colpa! Salvati dalla delusione senza pietà del Re Corvo! Fa il suo grande ritorno Sì, Oscuro Signore, party game dal tono scanzonato e umoristico, questa volta in salsa Luxastra: l’amatissima campagna di gioco di ruolo in streaming su YouTube del gruppo di InnTale. Veloce da imparare e facile da giocare, per Sì Oscuro Signore – Luxastra sono sufficienti alcuni amici e un po’ di fantasia. Gli incompetenti e inetti servitori del Re Corvo, Dio antico dalle motivazioni imperscrutabili, dovranno giustificarsi davanti al loro Signore, inventando storie improbabili e scaricando la colpa sui loro compagni di sventura.

Sì, Oscuro Signore! Luxastra è un  gioco completo che può essere giocato singolarmente oppure può essere combinato in un unico grande gioco con il set rosso e verde di Sì, Oscuro Signore. Contiene nuovi modi di scaricare la colpa e un’enorme quantità di nuove e bellissime carte spunto.

Lebbod is  a strategical dice game, where you you try to be the first player that collects 3 pieces of a world wonder.
You do this by moving your tiles, based on the numbers you rolled. 
When you land on another player’s tile, you remove their tile from the board. The first to remove 4 tiles gets a piece of a world wonder.

Droggel was included as a bonus in the first edition of the video game The Whispered World. It consists of a game board and three twelve-sided dice that show Nordic runes instead of numbers. In addition, you need to provide one playing piece for each player.

The players race to the finish space. The player who reaches it first wins. During your turn, you roll the three runic dice and spend them on three possible actions:
Move: If a die shows the rune of an adjacent space, you may spend the die to move to that space.
Jump: If a die shows the rune of an adjacent space, and the color of that space matches the color of the die, you may spend the die to jump over that space to the next space with a matching rune.
Droggel: If multiple dice show the same rune, you may move directly to the next space showing that rune. No dice are spent during a Droggel. After a Droggel, you reroll all of your remaining dice.

If you move to a space that is already occupied by another player, you fight over that space. Fights are resolved using a simple dice-based combat mechanism. The loser of the fight has to move their piece one or more spaces backwards.

Step into the thrilling world of Breached, where two cunning thief squads find themselves holed up in a heavily fortified bank. But beware! Two relentless Emergency Response Teams are on their way, breaching the bank's defenses to capture the invaluable vault. In this high-stakes game of strategy and wits, every decision counts.

Prepare for the ultimate showdown as you and your squad delve into the heart-pounding action of Breached. Before the chaos unfolds, carefully select your arsenal, side arms, and the type of leadership that best suits your tactical style. Choose wisely, for the fate of your squad depends on it. With Breached, programming your moves is key to outmaneuvering your opponents. Each turn, you must strategically program three of the four powerful cards you possess.

Anticipate your adversaries' next moves, devise clever strategies, and prove your brilliance by staying one step ahead. Will you sabotage their efforts or rally your team to victory? Collaboration is vital in Breached. Work together with your fellow thieves or response team members, exploiting the unique advantages of your skills and weapons. Teamwork and coordination will unlock new levels of strategy, leading you closer to the ultimate prize—the coveted vault.

—description from the designer

The “Seagull Rocker” festival is just around the corner! As a lone guitarist, you must try to be the quickest to convince the best musicians to join your band! Here’s a hint: they love food!

In this cards game, should you collect or spend fish? These are the kinds of decisions you need to make to be the first to form your band of 4 musicians, with 1 drummer, 1 bassist, 1 singer and 1 keyboard player.

—description from the publisher

Objective:
Be the first of two players to capture three bounties by navigating through the galaxy, exploring wormholes, and overcoming challenges based on die rolls.

Wormhole Bounty Hunters, including the rules and tokens, is an A.I. (GPT-4) generated game for pen, paper, or printer, 2 dice and 2 players. (tokens were generated by OpenAi GPT-4 as .svg code)

Expanded description of the game Wormhole Bounty Hunters:
Wormhole Bounty Hunters is a thrilling two-player board game that takes place in the far reaches of the galaxy. Your objective as a player is to outsmart and outmaneuver your opponent to be the first to capture three bounties. The bounties represent (for instance) notorious criminals on the run, hiding in the vast expanse of space, waiting to be captured.

The game unfolds on a 6x6 matrix board, representing the galactic expanse with 36 possible locations. These locations can be empty space, have a bounty value from 1 to 6, or contain a wormhole.

Each player takes turns navigating through the galaxy, moving their spaceship from one location to another based on die rolls. The players begin the game at an xy-dice values based location and from there, they explore the galaxy, by rolling with their die and moving horizontally and or vertically, but not diagonally, the corresponding number of steps to a new (or same) location.

Exploring a location reveals what's hidden in that sector of space. If it's a bounty, the player captures it by trying to throw the corresponding die value, add the bounty to their collection and place a space station (offering the possibilities for upgrade and shield tokens).
If it's a wormhole, the player's spaceship is sucked in and spat out at a new xy location on the board, offering an unpredictable twist. 
If a bounty hunter arrives on a location where another bounty hunter is located, either by moving there, or via a wormhole, they can try to steal one of the opponents bounties by rolling a 6.

The first player to capture three bounties and return safely to one of its own Space Stations wins the game.

Wormhole Bounty Hunters is a game of strategy, luck, and exploration, filled with anticipation and surprises. Whether you're traversing through open space, capturing bounties, or tumbling through wormholes, every turn brings a new challenge.

Generated by GPT-4, Wormhole Bounty Hunters is more than just a game; it's an adventure waiting to unfold. So gather your dice, draw or print your tokens, and embark on a thrilling hunt across the galaxy!

Tetra Master is a card game that originated in the wildly popular video game Final Fantasy IX.

It was a digital card game that allowed players to collect, win and trade cards with non-plaver characters within the game's universe. The cards all feature iconic characters and monsters from within the game's vast and creative world. Player reception to Tetra Master was't great, as many people felt like there was too much randomness and battles relied heavily on hidden and cryptic in-game calculations. For this physical version we've tried to eliminate some of the more frustrating experiences (primarily by changing the complex and confusing mathematical calculations with more easily understood die rolls) to make the experience fun while being reminiscent of the Tetra Master experience in Final Fantasy IX.

Miezi & Mopsie is about placing 4-in-a-row cat or dogs discs on a variable playing grid out of cards. It's a game of cats (Miezis) against dogs (Mopsies). Each player has to place 4 of his discs in a row, possibly in multiple places or in rows longer than 4. Depending on certain conditions on neighboring/underlying grid fields, cat discs can be stolen (removed by the opponent), and dog discs may award bonus points at the end of the game.

—user summary

Bigger. Tougher. A fair bit slower.

This expansion adds 2 new game pieces to the game, and you guessed them:

Bombers and Balloons! (and a surprise third, the Bomb token)

—description from the designer

Evil witches are everywhere. Souring your milk, corrupting your files, sailing across the moon with a cackling laugh. What can you do? What you always do – kill them and take their stuff. Here are a dozen witchy monsters with Items and Curses to match!

This expansion works with any Munchkin set, but if you want witches to be a bit more central in your game, shuffle it into Munchkin Pathfinder and let the players be Witches, too!

The 30-card Munchkin Witches mini-expansion! This tuckbox includes new Munchkin cards to shuffle into the classic Munchkin game.
10-Card stretch goal promo pack (was only available to certain pledge levels).

Here's the latest 15-card set of monsters and Treasures for any Munchkin game. Steak your claim to this expansion before it's gone, because you'll veal sad if you miss it . . . and that's no bull.

This is an expansion for Munchkin. It is not a stand-alone game. These cards originally appeared in Munchkin Unicorns and Friends.

boardgame: Mech Command RTS: The Armory and 5-6 Player Expansion
description:
The Armory: An additional set of all mech models, weapon arms, item cards, mech boards, gems and punch-board tokens. For advanced players, an extra set of everything will allow you to spawn highly specialized combinations like dual missile mechs

5-6 Player Expansion: This set includes all unique components for the White and Violet mechs including white and violet versions of the LED Bases, 80 acrylic damage cubes, support unit miniatures, player tokens, and damage dishes.

This promo pack contains two new characters: Troq, the Well-Meaning Beast and River, the Dancing Fencer. It was originally included as part of the Puzzle Strike 2 Kickstarter as well as orders directly from Sirlin Games.

Troq's Character Cards
Ready to Smash - Add two purple gems to your super meter.
Giant Growth - Ante two extra gems (this card's effect is ongoing using the creature slot like Midori's Dragon Form from the base game).
Beast Unleashed - If you have at least 8 gems, draw two cards and crash.

River's Character Cards
Fencing Skills - Draw 3 cards, put the skill cards in your hand and discard the rest.
River Dance - Swap x5.
River Stance - +3 action points, you can only play cards that cost 0 or 1 this turn.

Each player plays as a figurine collector who is looking to manipulate the market to ensure that their figurines are the most valuable.

Each round players will simultaneously select a figurine from their collection to add to the market (which is a pyramid). In market order they will place the figures into the market, if they can be played legally as there are some restrictions on placement.

The round continues until the market is full and points are scored for figures left in a player's collection based on the position in the market. Play continues for 3 rounds with the player with the most points at the end winning.

boardgame: トリック喫茶レトロベリー (Trick Cafe Retro Berry)
description:
Trick-taking x Bingo x Retro Cafe

"Bid prediction" hit and get points. In addition, you can get a bonus by "achieving the goal"! And grab the victory with "Bingo established"!

Fashionable cute designs and themes. However, the appearance is sweet, but the game is not...! ?

The cheese race is on, but which mouse will win the game?
To advance in this race game, the player has to find out which colour is underneath the next square.

—description from the publisher

These turtles are mischievous and they love to eat! They have fun hiding lovely fruit and vegetables for their snack, but where is the carrot?
In this visual memory game, children have to remember what's underneath the tortoise or which tortoise is hiding which food to collect as many cards as possible and win the game.

—description from the publisher

Lynx eye or eagle eye – who will have the best vision in this matching race?
In this simple yet effective game, the players compete against each other to try to find the silhouette card that matches the illustrated card.

—description from the publisher

Nimbus is an abstract, high strategy, dynamic board game designed by Neural Forge. The goal in Nimbus is to eliminate your opponent's Nimbus piece by moving your pieces around the board, however you can change the movement mode of one of your pieces every turn, resulting in a challenging tactical experience.

—description from the publisher

On a school trip, the beaver teacher asks the pupils to get into groups of 3 as quickly as possible, but it isn't so easy when the similarities turn out to be more complex than expected.
In this update of the family game, to win you need to form families of 3 animals of the same colour or the same type of animal, but as you progress there are fewer cards and combinations.

—description from the publisher

In the Wild West, railroad companies are locked in a tough battle to have the longest train. The competition is fierce and you'll need the smartest strategies to attack the carriages without getting caught by the sheriffs. 
In this fast-moving game, each player is trying to come out on top to conquer the West.

—description from the publisher

Halloween is in full swing! Laura and Mike decide to explore the haunted manor in search of the monsters' sweets, but not all the monsters have sweets and if they come across the same monster three times they lose their entire haul.

boardgame: The Treasure of Montecristo Island: The Thief and the Purple Sapphire Expansion Pack
description:
This Expansion Pack continues the story from the Treasure of Montecristo Island base game. With the altars still built and lit, the monks of the island disclose that gold is not the only treasure there. The Thief, now a character of their own, has a newly inspired thirst for besting your crew.

Included in this chapter is a host of tools at the Thief’s disposal; two sabotage tokens, a rope trap, poison, a bear trap, and a kidnap token.

Dear Adventurers,

There is a Thief on the Island of Montecristo and they have targeted your band of Adventurers. Recognized only by a tattoo of a skull and snake and a scar across one eye, this thief is known throughout the lands for being equally resourceful and greedy. They are a master of crafting traps that will hinder your quest to discover the secret of Montecristo Island.

After failing to steal the first Treasure of Montecristo Island from you, the Thief is doubling their efforts. They will stop at nothing to obtain the Purple Sapphire and escape the island with this priceless gem.

The Thief’s ship was previously damaged while attempting a stealthy approach, so, to complicate matters they will also rob you and your team for resources in order to repair their ship. Once the Thief has repaired their ship, there is nothing to stop them from stealing this new treasure and disappearing into the horizon.

If the Thief successfully repairs their ship and returns to it with the treasure, the game is over and the Thief is the winner.

—description from the publisher

The heroes of the four crests conquer new territories, build up their houses and fight for a legendary artifact that will wake the dragon and allow the owner to conquer other crests.

Build up your domain wisely to get the most out of it and strengthen your crest.
Fight squads of medieval armies and fantasy giants. Over 200 warriors in a box. Heroes participate in battles and give their units an advantage.
You never know what resource you will have this time and what strategy you will need to choose to win.
Discover, grow and conquer!

—description from the designer

You are a witch in the stock market and you must use your magic and ingenuity to obtain more money than your opponents.

Witch Market is a no language dependent, family-friendly filler where players must buy or sell ingredients from the market at the most opportune times.

Buy: To take all the ingredients of the same color from the market for Free! You know... using magic.

Sell: Discard from your hand all the Ingredientes of the same color and get coins for them! This is how you win!

The value of each sold Ingredient will be equal to the number of ingredients of the same type sold on the Market! So plan your sales wisely.

Get rewards for being one of the fastest sellers! Sometimes selling first is better than selling a lot, so time your sales to get coin rewards.

But don't be greedy! At the end of the game, each ingredient that is still in your hand will subtract 2 coins! So try to take the necessary Ingredients and sell them in time.

Witch Market is a game of 20 minutes, where each movement will modify the Market and the decisions of all the witches.
Can you be the wealthiest witch? Or will your greed win you over?

—description from the designer

Introduction:Crumo (from Proto-Germanic: krūmô, meaning “fragment”) is a drawless territorial game for two players:  Black and White. It is played on the hexes (cells) of an initially empty hexagonal board. The recommended size is 6 cells per side, but boards of 5 or 7 are also valid. Each player has access to a sufficient supply of stackable pieces of their own color.

				
				
					Definitions:
     A stack is a group of pieces of the same color located on the same cell. A single piece also a stack.
     The height of a stack is the number of pieces it contains.
     A stack guards a cell when there is a straight connection between them at a distance equal to the stack’s height, even if there are other stacks along the connection. Stacks also guard the cells they occupy.
     A stack receives an amount of attack equal to the amount of enemy stacks guarding the cell it occupies, and an amount of defense equal to the amount of friendly stacks doing the same.
     A stack is dead if at least one of its stacks receives more attack than defense; otherwise, it is alive.
     A controlled cell is a cell that can only be occupied by friendly stacks.
     A region is a maximal set of controlled cells. An isolated controlled cell is also a region.
     The value of a region is equal to the height of the tallest adjacent stack of the color that controls it.


				
				
					Turns:Black plays first, then turns alternate. On your turn, perform one of the following actions:


     Place a stack of your color, of any height, on an empty cell that guards at least one cell not occupied by enemy stacks, unless all guarded enemy stacks have more defense than attack.
     Move an entire stack or a portion of it a distance equal to the number of pieces taken. After the move, remove all dead enemy stacks and then all dead friendly stacks. If you move an entire stack, you must place a stack according to the rule above.
     Pass your turn.


If the only stack you remove is the one you just placed, your placement is illegal. For better visual clarity, although capturing is mandatory, it is recommended that beginners start playing with optional captures.

				
				
					End of the game:The game ends when both players pass on consecutive turns. The winner is the player with the highest score, calculated by summing the value of all controlled regions by friendly stacks, the enemy pieces captured, and an extra half-point for the player who passed first.

To balance the game, before starting, the first player places an initial black stack and the second player chooses sides. This balancing method is called the pie rule.

—description from the designer

A dynamic game where chips are moving all the time creating fascinating bee hives!

The game accurately simulates a real life beehive. The player is given a starter beehive and must plan strategically and manage the hive's resources, bee production, comb space and stores to grow the hive as strong as possible. The players must endure a chaotic and ever changing environment that is uncertain, but somewhat governed by the different seasons, diseases and man-made events.

The purpose of the game is to grow the hive and to accumulate as many SWARMS as possible without letting the hive die of starvation or run out of bees.  Bee biology and seasons knowledge will give the players an edge but luck also helps.

—description from the designer

Wizcape Academy is an escape game that mixes puzzles, narrative and technology, immersing you in a magical world. An envelope full of enigmas and magical elements will come to your hands. You will have to use your wits to solve all the puzzles, pass the tests and join the magic academy.

In this new adventure, you will have to use your magical power and test all your senses to pass the entrance exam to the magic academy. Will you be able to perform the spells and prepare the potions correctly?

—description from the publisher

Finta is a drawless connection game for two players: Red and Blue. It is played on a square grid of dots arranged in rows and columns. The dots on the first and last rows are connected to their neighbors on the same row with horizontal red lines, and the dots on the first and last columns are connected to their neighbors on the same column with vertical blue lines.

Red plays first, then turns alternate. On your turn, connect with a straight line of your color two orthogonally or diagonally adjacent dots that are not yet connected with a straight line. Lines and chains of lines of different colors may touch but must not intersect. A chain of lines of the same color may intersect itself, on a dot or elsewhere.

You win by completing a chain of lines of your color touching the two opposite board edges of your color.

To make the game fair, Blue will have the option, on their first turn only, to swap sides with Red instead of making a regular move.

—description from the designer

Cablink is a drawless connection game for two players: Red and Blue. It is played on a square grid of dots arranged in rows and columns. The dots on the first and last rows are connected to their neighbors on the same row with horizontal red lines, and the dots on the first and last columns are connected to their neighbors on the same column with vertical blue lines.

Red plays first, then turns alternate. On your turn, you may connect with a straight line of your color two orthogonally adjacent dots that are not yet connected with a straight line.

Alternatively, if two parallel red lines and two parallel blue lines form a minimal square with dots as vertices, you may connect the midpoints of your two lines in the set with a straight line of your color, as long as the two enemy lines in the set are not yet connected with such a line.

Chains of lines of different colors may touch but must not intersect. A chain of lines of the same color may intersect itself.

You win by completing a chain of lines of your color touching the two opposite board edges of your color.

To make the game fair, Blue will have the option, on their first turn only, to swap sides with Red instead of making a regular move.

—description from the designer

INTRODUCTION: Word-Go-Round is a game of strategy and skill that can be enjoyed by anyone old enough to spell. All players stay involved throughout the game since each player is competing not only with other players to accumulate the largest sum of money, but also with himself in striving to make the best possible words from his available letter tiles.

OBJECT: The object of Word-Go-Round is to accumulate the greatest amount of money possible by filling one’s individual word card with as many well paying words as possible. Words are formed from letter tiles which are bought when a player’s marker lands on the letters on the playing board. Players earn money with each word successfully spelled out on their individual word cards.

Each round, one player will reveal a location card for the fight, which may offer bonuses for certain monsters. Then, all players secretly select which monster(s) they will send into battle. Finally, the monsters are revealed. Red beats Green, Green beats Yellow, Yellow beats Red, and Blue trumps all. The winner deals damage first to their opponents' minions and then to their Master Monster. Play continues likewise until only one player remains.

boardgame: Ardennes Plus+ Volume Two: Hitler's Invasion of France
description:
Ardennes Plus is a grand strategic level simulation of 20th century conflicts in the densely forested and rough terrain of the Ardennes region of France, Luxembourg and Belgium.  This game features scenarios for Hitler’s Invasion of France, May 1940. This is Vol 2 of Ardennes Plus+ with the series also including Vol 1 WWII 1944, and Vol 3 WWI 1914.  The game uses Army level command structures to simulate supply and command in divisional and corps level units.  The game optionally   represents the fog of war that was very common in the rough country of the Ardennes, by offering 2 hidden movement systems.

The low counter density, and high map scale is used to create conditions for fast play and resolution is possible in less than 2 hours.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: The Collapse: The Thirty Years War
description:
The Collapse is a wargame that will be published in Spanish and English, which has a point-to-point mechanic for 2 players (with an optional multiplayer rule up to 6 players) with a common deck (CDG), depicting the epic campaigns of the Thirty Years' War in Europe between 1618 and 1648. Each player takes on the role of a faction (Imperial or Protestant), seeking to direct the fate of the "Holy Roman German Empire" while pursuing their own national interests, allowing to reproduce bold moves and influence or control regions in the Holy Roman Empire, Flanders and the United Provinces, Northern Italy and Hungary. With the crucial support of foreign nations both military (troops) and with 'Subsidies' (financial resources) will help to achieve victory for one of the two game’ factions.

The game will include a historical and scenarios book with very interesting historical information about the conflict, and 9 scenarios with different duration (from 2 to 10 turns the full 30 years' war campaign scenario), and also 2 scenarios specially designed to learn the game mechanics from less to more difficult and game strategies.

The Collapse is an intense and very fun game, which is easy to play due to its main mechanics (point to point) and being a card engine (CDG), but with unique aspects such as the planning phase at the beginning of the turn, the prestige points that grant more options to the players (leaders, unique capabilities and much more), and the intense but impressive diplomacy of 9 nations and alliances (Saxony, Brandenburg, England, Papacy, Hungary, Spain, Denmark, Sweden and France). With the recommended optional rule that players can go one more turn with some modifiers if they want to continue playing once the scenario is over and they are having fun.

Each card has Operations Points (OPS), which allow you to play the card for Operations and/or the event by paying its cost from the OPS of the card, giving many different game options and strategies.

The different Operations that players can use with their operation points are Deploy, Movement (with the option to perform minor or major battles reflecting the unique nature of the conflict and the strategic options for you to decide how to invest your limited resources), Diplomacy, Naval Blockade, Siege and Replacements. The tension of the game will be increased by having players loot regions and fortresses to pay their units (the 30 years’ war was devastating and troops were frequently fed and paid with what was ravaged. And be careful not to pay the mercenaries in the game, because they can add revolts to the game!

The sequence of each game turn is divided into 4 main phases:


     Planning phase: depending on the prestige of each faction, various actions of the factions may have more or less options
     Campaign phase: players alternate cards from their hand (6 cards per turn)
     End of Turn Phase: where players use their replacement points to strengthen their troops and armies, determine possible attrition of units outside of their home regions, observe possible expansion of revolts, redeploy units to prepare for the next turn (campaign) and extra victory points can be earned for certain conditions.
     Victory Check phase: where at the end of each scenario the victory is checked. There is also the option for an automatic game victory.


—description from the publisher

description:
PK Eleven is a card game in which two players simulate the penalty kick procedure at the end of a soccer game.

Each player has an identical set of 11 cards.  The cards have one half showing the strength and direction a kick is made, and a second half showing the strength and direction the goalie blocks.

Players lay out their cards in a line and flip the left most one face up. They can peak at their next next. One player is designated to kick first and both players simultaneously point to either their revealed card or the face down one next to it.

If the kicker kicked in a different direction than the goalie defended, they scored. If the directions were the same, compare the strengths. Then, switch roles and if one player used their face up card, they reveal their leftmost card.

Repeat this procedure until the cards are used and the person with the most goals scored is the winner.

Say Fuu is a trick-taking game for exactly three players.  Using a mono-suit deck that ranges from -16 to 16, the cards are dealt out and each player chooses some cards to discard which are shuffled to form a deck.

At the start of each trick, the lead player will look at the top card of this deck and then play a card. The winner of a trick will collect each of the three cards played to the trick and the top card from the deck.  Every card is worth face value points.

The winner of a trick is determined by comparing the values played to the hidden card.

The player with the most points at the end of a hand wins.

boardgame: 逃げろ！とんかつザウルス (Run away! Tonkatsusaurus)
description:
Run away! Tonkatsusaurus is a team based game for 4 or 6 players.  Each player is assigned a certain food-themed creature variant, such as a tonkatsusaurus, with two players, on opposite teams, both having variants on the tonkatsusaurus - with and without sauce!

Each player has a card showing their animal in front of them, and there is a deck which contains cards corresponding to each animal - though the two tonkatsusauruses share one type of card.

During the game, the players are playing cards from their hand into a pyramid shape. To play a card, it must have two supporting cards, and one of them must be the same type. You could also move your animal into the pyramid; it will be worth points equal to the level where it is placed.

Before placing it into the pyramid, a player can also activate their animal to move a card from lower in the pyramid to higher.

The team with the highest sum of points wins.

Yubi Sugoroku is a "roll and move" adjacent game where players are racing along a path. Each player starts with one hand out, and 5 fingers extended. On their turn, they choose how many fingers to fold down, and that is how far they can move.

The path has been randomized and starts face down.  Each space of the path shows a drawing of a fist extending 0 to 5 fingers.  When you land on a space, you gain back the number of fingers shown and reveal all spaces up to that point.

However, if you land on a space while your fist shows 0 fingers (rock) or 2 fingers (scissors), you play rock-paper-scissors against the space you land on (if applicable). If you win, take another turn immediately; if you lose, you lose your next turn.  If there is a tie, you resolve as normal.

After the first round, remove the tiles showing 3 fingers and play another round. Then, remove the tiles showing 1 finger and play another round.

The person with the most points after three rounds wins.

A game in which everyone is jockeying for the top spot in the competitive world of book publishing!

In Book Jockeys, the goal is to present the most appealing book proposal to the publisher (aka, judge), in hopes that he or she will select your book proposal as the book “Most likely to...(fill in the blank).” In some cases, you’ll be aiming high (i.e., for the most appealing book), and in others, you’ll be aiming low (i.e., for the least appealing). Each author (aka, jockey) will use a combination of nouns, verbs, adjectives, and numbers to create their book proposal before taking turns pitching their proposed title (& accompanying plot). The author/jockey who earns the most successful pitches wins.

—description from the designer

boardgame: UNO Ultimate: Convention Promo – Thanos (Enemy card)
description:
Hey Gen Con! There are exclusive Thanos foil cards up for grabs with the purchase of any UNO Ultimate Marvel product only at the Mattel Games booth.

Card text:

Thanos

When Flipped
All players burn 4 cards.

While Attacking
When a player plays a “!” (Danger Deck card), the burn 2 cards

To Defeat: See Reference Card

------

“Reference Card”

When Flipped
Place the card Next to the Danger Deck for everyone to see. Thanos attacks all players.

While Attacking
When a player plays a card with a “!” they must Burn 2 cards.
Note: A player can be attacked by both an Enemy and Thanos. If a player is being attacked by another Enemy, they most resolve their enemy first, then Thanos.

To Defeat
All active players must Burn one Wild Card from their hand. At the beginning of a player’s turn, they may propose the above and if each player agrees and is able to do so, then Thanos is defeated and played in the Danger Discard Pile.
Note: No other method of defeating an Enemy can defeat Thanos.

Note: Only 1 Thanos Card can be played per game.

A set of miniatures War-game rules for Space Battles in the Star Trek Universe.

Battle Trek uses a points allocating system, which is the first thing you do in each turn.  Than the most important action is next, the initiative phase which decide which ship goes first in the action phase, but it might be better to go later in the turn.  Special actions phase is any orders which need to announce before the next phase which is the action phase where you move, fire and anything else.

Equipment you need is a number of Starship models, dice (D6), Ship Cards, three (four) sets of different colour counters and one set of Veteran Captain Cards, Position Cards and a pack of playing cards.

The models used in developing these rules was from the “Eaglemoss Star Trek Starship Collection” from 2013, which are mounted on 4 x 3 inches bases, so you need a big playing area, but if you use smaller scale miniatures, use centimetres instead of inches and you should be able to play a game on a kitchen table.

—description from the publisher

Battle Trek is nothing to do with the video game of the same name.

In ミラテル - Miracle Cocktail, players opened each their own bar in a city renown for its magical cocktails. They will compete to become the most popular bar in town, by obtaining the most popularity points.

Each round, magical ingredients are poured one next to the other from a magical cocktail shaker. Players will then have to choose a group of 3 contiguous ingredients, which will fill different storage bottles or even generate coins, depending on their amount and nature. Storing enough of these components or using them to create cocktail recipes will generate special actions, additional ingredients, coins, and popularity. Combos can be created in the process.

After 8 rounds, popularity points are tallied for every player and whoever has the most wins.

artists: Masakazu Takizawa (たきざわ まさかず)

description:
In late 1686, Sir William Phipps convinced investors to fund an expedition to what is now Haiti and the Dominican Republic to find sunken treasure, despite the location of the shipwreck being based entirely on rumor and speculation. In January 1687, Phipps found the wreck of the Spanish galleon Nuestra Señora de la Concepción off the coast of Santo Domingo. Some sources say they used an inverted container for the salvage operation while others say the crew was assisted by Indian divers in the shallow waters. The operation lasted from February to April 1687 during which time they salvaged jewels, some gold and 30 tons of silver which, at the time, was worth over £200,000.

Diving Bell takes the players on a journey to the bottom of the sea to find various treasures. But it is not a walk in the park to find them - various threats exist to stop the players from doing so. 
Diving Bell is a cooperative game that players will use a unique way of choosing actions to get their ships ready for diving. A diving bell is also an option to help with the success of each dive they are used. Spatial thinking, action selection,  resource management, and other mechanisms are available for the players who wish to conquer the deep.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Cross Roads: A Game of Buildings and Blocks
description:
In the game of "cross roads" each player becomes one of the builders of a "town".  The center of the Playing Board is a map of the Blocks and Crossroads of this "town".  The Blocks are divided into square lots and are zoned in color as to the type of buildings or landscaping that is permitted on each lot.  The "town" is divided into two, three or four lot Blocks.

The object of the game is to be the player with the highest total of bonus money when the game ends.

—user summary

A trading card game based on Japanese mobile game 'Shironeko Project'. In this game, players can use characters from the Shironeko world to fight the opponent. 
In this game, players should prepare a 40-card main deck and 10-card town deck. Town cards can produce resource which can be used to play character card. The player who makes the other player's deck to 0 will win the game.

You parked your drakkar at the beach as best you could. Now you need to hurry up, find the ring you lost, and take it to your significant other waiting for you at the top of the mountain.
Along the way you may meet a dragon, which you love cooked in the oven with asparagus, and decide to kill it.

To play, you will need a pencil and three 6-sided dice.
Explore the map and collect power-ups to become stronger and defeat the enemies you encounter.
You can use these upgrades to increase your Fighting skill or increase your Hit points.
Keep track of your characteristics by checking the boxes on your character sheet.
When you encounter enemies and have to fight, roll the dice, modify the result thanks to your special Fighting ability and compare the result with the values indicated next to the enemy; if the result is equal or greater you have won and can continue the mission.

To win you must find the ring and take it to the top of the mountain.

In Hydras, several hydras compete against each other to see which one is the first to complete their proper heads.

Deal a random Hydra Body card to each player, who will place it face up in front of themselves. This card determines your Hydra’s type.
Each player draws 3 Hydra Head cards from the face down deck and then adds them to their hand secretly. They must also draw 1 extra Hydra Head from the deck and place it face up on one of the necks of their Hydra’s body.

At the start of your turn, draw 1 Hydra Head card from the game deck and add it to your hand.

The winner is the first Hydra to have 3 Heads matching its Body type. Once a Hydra wins, the game ends.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Wings of Glory: World War 1 – Caquot M / Ae 800 Drachen
description:
The Caquot M / Ae 800 Drachen Special Pack is the perfect addition to the WW1 Wings of Glory game line. This pack brings the excitement of the "Knights of the Air" era to your tabletop, allowing you to experience the thrill of commanding your own observation balloon in the midst of the battles of World War I.

As the guardians of the skies, observation balloons played a critical role in the struggle for aerial and ground supremacy during the First World War. With their ability to support troops, gather intelligence, and assist with artillery fire, these massive Drachens were heavily defended by anti-aircraft guns, groups of machine guns, and patrolling fighter aircraft. But the brave balloon busters, equipped with special weapons like Le Prieur rockets, were up for the challenge of taking them down.

Now, you can recreate these intense and thrilling battles with the Caquot M / Ae 800 Drachen Special Pack. This pack features a beautifully pre-painted model, scenarios, ground units, new rules, new abilities, and even a decal sheet for customization. You can play a variety of missions, including defending your balloon from enemy attacks or attempting to take down an enemy balloon yourself.

The market has opened its doors! Stock your stall with the orcs' favorite nasty commodities and outsell your competition to win the game.

Set up your market stall with different Orc commodities. Cater to your customers better than your rivals and collect victory points. But, beware! Some of your customers will come back for more. Once the market closes its doors, it will be time to tally up the score.

The game is played in 3 rounds (Morning, Noon and Night), collecting points each round. Each round, points are awarded:


     For the face-up Customer cards at the center of the table, and
     For the hidden cards of each player.
     Face down Customer cards at the center of the table do NOT award points.


—description from the publisher

Contestants, wrap your hands and put on your gloves! Everything is prepared and the round-one bell rings. The crowd is full of excitement and expects nothing less than your strongest punches! Are you ready to become the best fighter in the ring?

In this set-collection game, players will battle each other, comparing the strength of their fighters' punches! The player with the strongest team will add the fighter cards to their Hall of Fame. Then, both teams will be replaced with new cards. These blow-for-blow battles will continue until someone lands the winning punch by fulfilling one of the win conditions. This will immediately end the game, granting that player the champion's title!

—description from the publisher

Populate the coral reef with your anemones and fill it with color. If you have more than anyone else at the end of the game, you’ll be the winner!

In Anemone, your goal is to have more anemones on the reef than anyone else.

On your turn, draw the top card of the deck and play it following the rules below.

All cards are composed of 3 different figures in all 3 possible colors.

Always play new cards partially or completely on top of the cards already at the center of the table (the reef):

     If you place it partially (one or two sections of the card), you must match at least one color on the cards it is being placed upon.
     If you place it completely, you must match two colors on the cards it is placed upon.


—description from the publisher

Travel through space to seek out elements that will let you go back home safely. Face enemies you meet and do not lose your route using the special warp drive.

To play, you will need a pencil and three 6-sided dice.
Explore the map and collect power-ups to become stronger and defeat the enemies you encounter.
You can use these upgrades to increase your Precision skill or your Survival skill.
Keep track of your characteristics by checking the boxes on your character sheet.
When you encounter enemies and have to fight, roll the dice, modify the result thanks to your special ability Accuracy and compare the result with the values indicated next to the enemy; if the result is equal or greater you have won and can continue the mission.

To win, you must collect the 4 elements scattered around the map and return them to their respective spaceports. Once they are all in place you can access the route to your planet and win the game.

—description from the designer

The night is your friend. The shadows are your shelter.Move quietly and avoid useless fights. Find your prey and complete your mission.

To play, you'll need a pencil and a 6-sided die. Walk the paths and look for the power-ups you meet along the way.
You can use these upgrades to increase your Hide skill or your Fight skill.
Keep track of your characteristics by checking the boxes on your character sheet.
When you meet enemies and have to fight roll the dice, modify the result thanks to your special abilities and compare the result with the values indicated next to the enemy; if the result is equal or greater you have won and can continue the mission.
To win you must reach the temple and defeat the final boss.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Lone Sherman and The Fields of Normandy 2: Two Solitaire Wargames
description:
Lone Sherman puts you in charge of a single Sherman Tank and its crew somewhere in France in World War II, and tasks you with navigating your Sherman through varied and challenging Missions. Whilst this game is not a ‘narrative driven’ game as such, with a little imagination it will provide memorable stories. The Missions allow the Player to invent any kind of back-story they like in order to enhance the feel of the game. Some events are randomly generated, but the Player always has at least some degree of control over the level of risk they wish to take and how vulnerable they leave their Tank to these Events.

The Fields of Normandy 2 (the sequel to The Fields of Normandy) is set in Normandy, France in 1944 and reflects loosely the conditions and situations faced by the US VIII Corps of the United States Army as it pushed inland as part of Operation Cobra. The fighting was intense and incredibly violent. German units were very well dug in and protected by artillery and mines. In the game, you will be controlling small groups of soldiers from a platoon, consisting of three Rifle Squads, a Machine Gun Team, a Bazooka Team and a Mortar Team. As the platoon Lieutenant, it is your job to command these squads and teams in order to clear the German defensive positions.

The aim of this book is to present two clear, accessible, and quick to learn war games, so that players can get into the action quickly. It does not try to replace more complex wargames which often take many hours to learn and to play. Each of the Missions can be played in around an hour and the game provides plenty of 'replayability'.

This games in this book are solitaire hex and counter style wargame. You play the games, and the enemies are controlled by the game book (or the Artificial Intelligence or ‘AI’). You will need to bring at least 5, but preferably 7, standard six-sided dice. Full colour 'Mission Maps' are included. Methods to play the game include:

     Cut out or copy the counters provided on the final page of the book, stick them to card and cut them out with a good quality craft knife or pair of scissors.
     Photocopy the maps and draw/write on the pages lightly in pencil and erase when necessary. You can use any annotations you choose for the units, that make sense to you.
     Lay the book flat (it’s well-bound, it can stand it!) and use cubes, miniatures, or similar tokens for the units.
     Use some kind of acetate and dry-wipe markers.


Take a photo of the maps on a tablet and use the “edit” function to draw and erase.

Find Fam Fun is a card game that normalizes the different types of families, created to be a vehicle for inclusion in family, school and social environments. Created with the goal of developing values such as empathy, self-esteem, inclusion or respect for diversity.

Find Fam Fun is a triple memory game, familiar and fun, with a duration of between 15 and 30 minutes, with simple rules, to be played at any time and place, suitable for all audiences.
It is a game for 2 to 6 players, for children from 3 years old. It is dynamic and each one challenges himself and the other players.

Find is a multi-level memory game. Fam is a set collection game, where skill, strategy and calculation will be your allies. Fun is a fun game of "push your luck" where you will have to achieve the goals indicated on the card and not draw the forbidden color that throws you out of the round.

Wow! You just found a strange high-tech device that looks like a phone... but it's much more interesting. It has an incredible collection of DinoBits, digital dinosaurs that can be projected in 3D holograms! Gather your friends and family and discover which are the longest, heaviest, tallest or oldest dinosaurs.

DinoLore it's a kids game that adults will also enjoy. During the game, players will take turns challenging the rest of the players: «My Stegosaurus is the longest! ». The other players can accept the challenge if they think they have a longest dinosaur in their hand and try to win all the cards played.

Compare dinosaurs and build the biggest collection of DinoBits!
Wow! You just found a strange high-tech device that looks like a phone... but it's much more interesting. It has an incredible collection of DinoBits, digital dinosaurs that can be projected in 3D holograms! Gather your friends and family and discover which are the longest, heaviest, tallest or oldest dinosaurs.

DinoLore it's a kids game that adults will also enjoy. During the game, players will take turns challenging the rest of the players: «My Stegosaurus is the longest! ». The other players can accept the challenge if they think they have a longest dinosaur in their hand and try to win all the cards played.

Compare dinosaurs and build the biggest collection of DinoBits!
Wow! You just found a strange high-tech device that looks like a phone... but it's much more interesting. It has an incredible collection of DinoBits, digital dinosaurs that can be projected in 3D holograms! Gather your friends and family and discover which are the longest, heaviest, tallest or oldest dinosaurs.

DinoLore it's a kids game that adults will also enjoy. During the game, players will take turns challenging the rest of the players: «My Stegosaurus is the longest! ». The other players can accept the challenge if they think they have a longer dinosaur in their hand and try to win all the cards played.

Compare dinosaurs and build the biggest collection of DinoBits!

The washing machine has gone crazy and wants to destroy all socks! Terrified by the situation, all the socks have run away, but in the midst of the chaos everyone has lost their family and friends.

In Apocalipsocks you'll have to help the socks find their pair, the only issue is that all of them are very similar. Beat the other players finding the pairs of socks the fastest, activate their special powers and become the great hero that stopped the Apocalipsocks.

"Route 66: Get Your Tricks" is a trick-taking card game for two players.

Enjoy the adventure of taking a road trip. Travel West along America's Main Street, stopping along roadside destinations and navigating Interstate Highways. This nostalgic twist on traditional European card games puts you right in the driver's seat.

The game is played over a series of rounds where players race to travel 66 miles. Depending on how well players pace their trip by winning rounds, they may be able to travel 1-3 states per round.

Greater rewards can be risked by closing the road. But choose your destinations carefully. If you fall behind, you'll have to rely on the dashboard warning lights to get you back up to speed. The first player to travel from Illinois to California wins the game!

Cayley HamiDElton is a competitive game about accounting shenanigans, as you try to report a lower income in order to pay less taxes, but could get caught.

The game consists of a deck of cards which show your profits on one side and the potential fine on the other. In a round, each player puts out one card, profit side down, and the players have an auction of decreasing bids, to declare how low of a profit you're willing to claim.

Once there is only one person left, they take on the title of accountant, and slowly reveal the cards everyone played.  If the sum is less than the declared amount, everyone takes their own card as points and the accountant also takes a bonus card. If the sum is more, the accountant takes all excess cards as penalties.

The person with the most points at the end of the game wins.

In Bremen Formation, each of the players takes on the role of an animal, trying to be the one to catch the hen. Players get a hand of action cards (e.g. move forward 1 space) and each animal has a special action.

The game play takes place on cards showing three spaces in a line, which form a loop. Players simultaneously choose a card from their hand and reveal it. Once each action is resolved, an item card takes effect.  The players see a queue of the next two item cards which will occur, and they have effects such as placing the hen on the animal on top of a stack, causing the top animal to pick up their used action cards, or eliminating a player from the game.

The first player to catch the hen twice wins.

Towards Guy Alley Card Game is a trick-taking game where players will play some of their cards twice. Specifically, the winner of each trick will take their own card as points, but the losers will place their cards face down in a special stack.  Once a player is out of cards, they pick up the cards they lost with, turn them over to a different rank range, and continue playing.

At the start of the round, players bid for how many points (not tricks) they will take. These bids must be formed by the sum of two cards, numbered 0 to 7.  A player also has the opportunity to play one of these two bid cards to a trick.

Players earn points for hitting their bid exactly and will otherwise lose points. The person with the most points after a number of rounds wins.

boardgame: Space Explorers: Age of Ambition Promo Pack
description: The Promo Pack was offered in the Gamefound campaign for the Age of Ambition expansion. Each promo component is marked with a Saturn icon. The promo pack contains 2 new Project tiles for the base game (Freedom 7/Sputnik 2, Luna 3/Surveyor 1)—available previously as Space Explorers: Spacecraft Projects Promo—, 1 new Project tile for Module 6 of the Age of Ambition expansion (Saturn V/First Crewed Docking), 4 new Goal tokens for Module 4 (G9 in each player color), and 2 new Talent tiles for Module 5.

boardgame: Griffin Dash (グリフォンダッシュ）
description:
Griffin Dash is a game in which players aim to escape safely from a cave while collecting precious jewels (griffin jewels) to prevent griffin from catching up with us.
The player aims for the exit while picking up jewels that have fallen in the cave, and jewels are awarded if the player escapes the cave safely.
The winner is the player who has the most jewels at the end of three rounds.
However, If you get along too well with other players, your legs will get entangled and you won't be able to move forward.
Moreover, the griffin, seeing an opportunity, will chase after you.
Another strategy is to sneak out through a loophole in the cave!

—description from the publisher

In Dino Gardens, dinos compete to grow the largest, most delicious garden they can before the meteor strike and the end of the world.

Dino Gardens is a strategy game where you must grow and eat plants while forming alliances, battling other dinos, and protecting your garden from Terrible T-Rex.  To help in growing their garden, each dino has a unique skill that may allow them to fly, run, swim, or have access to unique seeds.

In the Planting stage, dinos move freely around the land planting their seeds.  In the Growing stage, dinos must tend to their seeds and grow them into mature plants by revisiting them.  And finally, in the Eating stage, dinos must try to eat all the plants they can for precious dino calories.  At the end of the three stages, the dino who has consumed the most dino calories is the winner!

In each stage dinos must make important choices.  Planting in the wetlands will yield higher results, but other dinos may try and steal your plants.  Visiting dino hot springs has restorative health benefits, but will take time away from gardening.  In addition, Terrible T-rex is roaming the land looking for his lucky top hat, and if he finds it hidden in your garden, he will attack!

-description from designer

boardgame: Aeon Trespass: Twelve Sins of Herakles – Summer
description:
Twelve Sins of Herakles is a campaign game about adventures, exploration and fierce battles with giant monsters. Set in the same game universe as Aeon Trespass: Odyssey, it’s a co-operative, choice-driven boardgame experience played over multiple sessions.

In an alternate Antiquity, where a reality-shattering cataclysm killed the Olympian gods and unleashed the otherworldly Primordials, Twelve Sins of Herakles places the players in the roles of Argonauts, the only people who can fight off the darkness and make things right. The base game covers three regions and three campaigns.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Star Wars: Shatterpoint – Hello There Squad Pack
description:
"The burden is on me not to destroy all the droids until you arrive." -Obi-Wan Kenobi

A Jedi Master battles to maintain the Republic! With the Republic under attack from all sides, the noble Obi-Wan Kenobi steps forward to contain the Separatist threat. At his side are the Clone Marshal Commander known as "Cody" and the Phase II Clone Troopers of the renowned 212th Attack Battalion.

United in their resolve to preserve the Republic, they battle the Separatist threat throughout the galaxy. Known for their ability to strategize even in the thick of battle and ferocity in combat, Kenobi, Cody, and the 212th represent one of the Republic's best chances to restore peace to the galaxy. . .

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Star Wars: Shatterpoint – Twice the Pride Squad Pack
description:
"Your swords, please. We don't want to make a mess of things in front the Chancellor." -Count Dooku
