A sinister Sith Lord stalks the galaxy! Once a respected Jedi Knight and the padawan of Grand Master Yoda himself, Count Dooku has chosen the dark side. He now serves as the apprentice to Darth Sidious, carrying out his master's will in dark schemes from Geonosis to Coruscant and beyond using powerful pawns, such as the bounty hunter Jango Fett. Although he is often protected by IG-100 MagnaGuards, Dooku has unparalleled skill with a lightsaber and is more than a match for multiple Jedi.

With his knowledge of the dark side and the weight of the Separatist Army at this back, he has all the tools he needs to secretly orchestrate a galactic civil war.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Star Wars: Shatterpoint – Appetite for Destruction Squad Pack
description:
"Your screams are like music to my audio receptors!"
- General Grievous

The nefarious General Grievous terrorizes the Republic! Unleashing the Separatist Droid Army throughout the galaxy, Grievous has proven to be more than a match for his Jedi adversaries. A shrewd military strategist, he make use of brutal tactics to claim numerous victories and instill fear in his opponents. His reputation is only bolstered by prowess in combat.

Trained by Count Dooku himself, Grievous wields lightsabers claimed from his fallen foes with deadly efficiency. His cybernetic enhancements further accentuate his skills, making Grievance a key player in the Separatist's hopes to topple the Republic.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Star Wars: Shatterpoint – Plans & Preparation Squad Pack
description:
"Gentlemen, if you are quite finished, we have a battle to begin."
- Luminara Unduli

A wise protector and her apprentice step forward! Known of her great patience, Jedi Master Luminara Unduli's discipline and ability to stay calm under pressure make her a formidable duelist with a lightsaber.

A master of the Soresu form of combat, Luminara has passed her skills onto her padawan, Miralan Barriss Offee, who accompanies her on missions throughout the galaxy.

Joined by the Republic's elite Clone Commandos, they are ready to bring their skills to bear against those that threaten peace and democracy.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Star Wars: Shatterpoint – Jedi Hunters Squad Pack
description:
"Do you know the key to hunting a Jedi, friend? It is patience." -Grand Inquisitor

The last remnants of the Jedi Order tremble in fear as Inquisitors stalk them to the farthest corners of the galaxy! The Grand Inquisitor, a long-lived Pau'an, leads the Inquisitorius at Darth Vader's behest, an elite order of Force-sensitive agents strong in the dark side who hunt those who escaped the Order 66 purge. A former Jedi himself, he turned this fervor to the destruction of his former allies. He is joined in this task by Inquisitors referred to as brothers and sisters, each given a number demarking the order of their initiations.

Reva, the Third Sister, bold and ambitious, nurses a secret grudge against Lord Vader after surviving the slaughter of younglings at the Temple.

Fifth Brother, aggressive and self-sure, is ready to strike down any who stand in his way.

Fourth Sister, swift and deadly, is a hunter through and through. They are the Inquisitors, and the hunt has been called.

—description from the publisher

In Monster Workshop, you are a (slightly mad) scientist. Scour your lab to assemble the best available parts, and create the ultimate combat monster. Take your monster to battle and defeat the opposition! Be fastest, be strongest, be luckiest -- be victorious!

OBJECT OF THE GAME: Build the strongest complete monster you can before your opponents do, by drawing and discarding cards. Then defeat your rival in combat! To complete your monster, collect cards depicting 1 head, 1 torso, 1 left arm, 1 right arm, and 1 pair of legs. The first two players to complete their monster will send their creations to battle to see who emerges the victor. Lay your best card hand on your individual player board to “bring your monster to life”! The first player to bring their monster to life has the advantage of striking first -- roll the included 20-sided die to achieve a winning score. Win two battles, and you win the game! Your gameplay can range from simple to monstrously strategic. Create a monster and fight with your friends in this electrifying card game.

—description from the designer

Around a campfire, a group of aging adventurers reminisce about their adventurous past...
Count Lance Dur and his allies join your tale! Your verve will be put to the test, as their legendary feats demand to be narrated with passion!

This Splatter Face Battle game is sure to keep children entertained at home on rainy days! Simply squirt cream or put a wet sponge on the pie splatting hand, rest your chin on the ledge, and take turns twisting the spinner. Relax, keep your cool and prepare yourselves, as the two of you will get a big surprise of cream in your face! The question is… who will it be? Watch your friends squirm as they play to face their fate! Either player could get splattered at any moment!

—description from the publisher

First off, I should point out that "meatgrinder" is being used in professional literature about the battle.  An example is the 18th April 2023 article "How Long Should Ukrainian Forces Defend Bakhmut? Lessons from Stalingrad," by Gælan Hanlon on West Point's Modern War Institute website.

This seemingly conventional hex-and-counter combat-odds-resolution game is both operational and (intra-city) more tactical with different scaled maps for each.  Unit level is battalion to brigade.  No airpower which has been neutralized in the war but artillery including HIMARS and Russian long-distance glide bombs are present, and there is fortification - albeit flank-able - to mitigate those.

But the added dimension of the game is the expertise levels of the units, their promotion and/or reinforcement/replacement, and the (tragic) human - ergo political - loss factor which can disrupt or even conceivably halt the fighting and the game - a deliverance reversal of the "sudden death" game mechanic, if you will.

There are promote-able conscript and then/also regular army units for both sides.  On the Ukraine side there are the hardcore political Azovs and also, starting in 2023, NATO-trained units of initially unpredictable strength.  On the Russian side, there are promote-able "cost-free" convict/prisoner units and the mercenary Wagners.

Units are hidden until attacking or attacked.

The game could have as many as 6 players: on each side a northern operational player outside the city, the city battle commander, and the southern "outside" operational player, but there will have to be some authority established as to who gets reinforcements/replacements and artillery ammunition use or stockpiling.

The game has relatively simple combat mechanics (albeit having a wave-attack opportunity) and lasts 16 half month turns from the start of October 2022 until the battle's "historical" end ... right now (May 2023).

"Whether the Ukrainians choose to keep it going after having lost the city is ... right now ... problematic.  Hopefully, it will have convinced everyone of the intolerable human cost and futility of continuing the war."

Well, the Ukrainians *have* chosen to resume the battle as part of their offensive and have pushed the Russians back significantly on the northern and southern flanks, so I have added a Continuation Battle of 10 more turns on the Turn Record Track with a suggested order of battle/setup.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
A sci-fi board game where you don’t just stop a war… you redefine what peace really means!

A very very very cooperative board game!

? Ultimediation® is a 100% cooperative… and 100% crucial board game.
Earth is on the brink of collapse. A global war is raging. Humanity is falling apart.

You are part of an extraordinary team: the finest human mediators, supported by three extraterrestrials from a highly advanced civilization. Together, you return to Earth — not to fight… but to restore peace.

? Your mission?
Evacuate civilians. Stop the attacks. And above all: succeed in mediating between the warring factions… by uncovering the human needs hidden beneath the violence.

⏳ Time is running out.
? The survival of the planet is in your hands.
? Will you manage to build lasting peace — before it’s too late?

? GAMEPLAY OVERVIEW

Welcome to the mission. As mediators, you're not just observers — you're history shapers. Every decision you make can rewrite the fate of humanity.

? REMOVING ATTACKS
With each turn, new attack tiles strike different regions of Earth.
Your task? Undo the devastation left by war.

Thanks to your extraordinary power — the ability to travel slightly back in time — you can reverse these events before they become irreversible. In the game, this is known as “removing an attack.” It’s not just damage control… it’s rewriting the present.

?‍♂️ EVACUATION OF HUMANITY
Every life counts.

When an attack hits a zone inhabited by civilians, they either flee… or perish.
Your team’s mission is to save as many as possible — by teleporting them to safe zones: the "U base" the mothership in orbit.

Move fast. Time is running out, and lives are on the line.

?️ MEDIATION — THE HEART OF THE MISSION
Peace won’t come through power. Only through understanding.

To fulfill your mission, you must initiate mediation sessions with both warring governments — hidden deep within their submarines. There, you’ll need to decode their true motivations and guide them to express not just demands… but the human needs behind them.

Only then can real peace begin !

? WINNING & LOSING THE GAME

The fate of Earth hangs in the balance. The game ends when one of three conditions is met:

✅ Victory — Humanity Unites in Peace
You’ve successfully mediated with both factions. Congratulations — you've achieved what history never could!

❌ Defeat — Earth Overrun by War
A full horizontal line of attack tiles appears on the board.
That region is lost. Humanity follows.

❌ Defeat — Too Many Lives Lost, Too Little Time
The turn tracker and lost humanity cube meet.
Too many are gone, and time has run out for mediation.

-description from designer

Abunakkashii the Corruptor, a demon from another age and wandering ronin, makes the ground tremble with his steps. His presence is so powerful that it manages to reshape the borders of Jalaanx. The bone wall, an impassable barrier that protected the lands from external dangers, collapses in an instant, revealing dark and strange realms that no one dared to imagine.

Abunakkashii is an expansion for Gosu X. Inside the box you will find a new clan of 15 cards with 1 clan tile, as well as new tokens.

-description from publisher
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

boardgame: Middara: Unintentional Malum – Lupercalia 2023, Debauchery and Legends Bundle
description:
Lupercalia beckons.

Through decadence and debauchery these lustful figures can be called from the depths of Earth’s history. Espers made manifest only through esoteric quiet whispers shared amongst the elite.

This Bundle contains the entirety of our Lupercalia 2023 Resin Kit Sets in one immaculate bundle.
 This includes miniatures for The Kreshka - Apollyon of Vanity, The Dark Mother Aspect, Somoe - Deep Sea Naiad, and the Erotes.

This Bundle also includes two 50mm bases, three 25mm bases, 5x Loyal Esper Cards, 5x Opposing Esper Cards, 10x Item Cards, 10x Initiative Cards,1x Ability card, and 5x Art cards.

I Feel Attacked is a party game about the people you love to hate, and would hate to be!

Each round, the Judge will play a card, and it’s up to each player to pick the card in their hand that best matches the Judge’s card. The winner of the round will be the one whose card is judged to be just the right type of terrible!

Want to add some arguing to the game? Give each player an I Feel Attacked card at the start of the game. After the Judge makes their choice, but before the point is awarded, if you are offended by the choice for whatever reason, you can play your I Feel Attacked card to give an impassioned speech about why the Judge’s choice is bad, wrong, unfunny, or gross, then find an alternate choice on the table that you think is better. All players vote between the Judge’s choice and the alternate. If you win or tie, you get the point instead, regardless of who originally put the winning card down. Each player may play their IFA card once during the game.

Play for as long as you like. Whoever has scored the most cards at the end of the game wins!

-description from publisher

I Feel Attacked: NSFW Edition is a party game about the people you love to hate, and would hate to be!

Each round, the Judge will play a card, and it’s up to each player to pick the card in their hand that best matches the Judge’s card. The winner of the round will be the one whose card is judged to be just the right type of terrible!

Want to add some arguing to the game? Give each player an I Feel Attacked card at the start of the game. After the Judge makes their choice, but before the point is awarded, if you are offended by the choice for whatever reason, you can play your I Feel Attacked card to give an impassioned speech about why the Judge’s choice is bad, wrong, unfunny, or gross, then find an alternate choice on the table that you think is better. All players vote between the Judge’s choice and the alternate. If you win or tie, you get the point instead, regardless of who originally put the winning card down. Each player may play their IFA card once during the game.

Play for as long as you like. Whoever has scored the most cards at the end of the game wins!

—description from the publisher

You are a professional hacker & thief and have taken on an open contract to infiltrate several high security locations to retrieve valuable data chips containing information on rival assets for a nefarious corporation ‘Paladin International’. Other hackers, like yourself, have also taken on the same contract, and are after the same target data, possibly at the same time! The risk is high, but so is the reward...

Over the course of your mission, you will have to break into 3 different vaults, retrieving data chips. As each code is cracked, the security level increases on the next suspected target, but the reward value also increases.

Will you risk & race for the higher value vault first, or play the ‘long game’ and go for lower value job, which will increase as the plot unfolds?

Each combination row contains a data chip. To win the game, you must be the first to unlock each combination & retrieve all three different data chips, and then transmit the data back to your handler.

—description from the publisher
family: Players: Games with expansions that add solo play

description:
Shadowboxing - you’ve likely seen it on social media, watched friends do it, or even played yourself. It’s the game that quite literally blew up the internet.

Since we like games (and things that blow up) we, too, joined the TikTok trend and shadowboxed All. The. Time.

After a while, we began to think about how we could make the game more chaotic - in a way that everyone could enjoy.

After years of revisions, ideas, and arguments, we’ve created the perfect blend of fast-paced fun and strategy into a game that everyone loves!

It’s easier on your neck, too.

Rediscover the over-the-top excitement and pandemonium of wrestling’s golden age! Join the Diabolical Wrestling Council and challenge a colorful cast of superstars in search of the ultimate prize – the DWC World Championship. Gather a group of friends and pummel each other with words and moves … maybe even a 2×4!

Become a Victim of Diabolical Dave's Whimsical Management Decisions!
 In Diabolical Dave’s Rumble Throwdown, you build your own pro wrestling superstar and compete to become the DWC World Champion. Nearly endless combinations of wrestling styles, gimmicks, moves and attire all contribute to your success or demise and ensure you’ll never play the same way twice.

Playing as a Heel or Face, you’ll trade on the ultimate currency in pro wrestling – your popularity or infamy – to add moves and attire to your repertoire. You can even take on a manager to help you out. In an exciting race to the top of the promotion, you exact vengeance with one-shots, weapons, and angles. Bribe other players to run-in for you and even interrupt their plans along the way, just don’t get caught by the ref or you might be disqualified for cheating!

Make things even more interesting with a little roleplaying, giving your wrestler some personality, as you work your way through the opener, midcarder, and main eventer tiers.

Introducing the Garage Expansion (initially called Trading Paint)— the ultimate expansion for Thunder Rolls.

Unleash the full potential of your racing experience with over 100 cards packed into this exciting addition. Upgrade your gameplay with an assortment of new sponsor stickers, a variety of dynamic driver decks, and a revolutionary NDP deck/system that takes the game to new heights of speed and accessibility.

With the Garage Expansion, get ready to push the limits of racing fun as you embark on adrenaline-fueled adventures with your friends. Buckle up and prepare for the ultimate race of your life!

A new multi-layered card game that fuses chess and fantasy
Divided into black and white camps, you rotate your cards alternately one turn at a time, defeating and absorbing your opponent's pieces, sometimes merging with your allies, summoning your opponent's cards, etc., and in the end, whoever takes the opponent's king wins.Included in anomalous Chess Variant.


     By stacking (absorbing or merging) other cards, the movement of the stacked cards is also added and becomes a complex movement, so deep insight is required.
     Stacked cards can become opponent's cards if you are not careful, and they may expand rapidly.
     The hurdle is high, but by summoning cards, you can turn the tide at once.


—description from the designer

The supernatural is real. Unspeakable horrors await those who venture out at night. Vampires drain the life of their victims while cultists summon strange creatures. We're hoping that we can scrounge together enough supplies to survive without our minds snapping, but it is a heavy burden to bear.

Maximum Apocalypse: Gothic Horrors 2nd Edition is a revised standalone game that collects several smaller Maximum Apocalypse expansions — Gothic Horrors, Kaiju Rising, Jurassic Perils, and Allies of the Rapture — in one box that contains six apocalyptic scenarios: Cthulhu, Kaiju, Dinosaurs, Vampires, Yokai, and the Rapture.

Face off against life draining vampires, ghouls, and werewolves in a classic monster set or try to stop a bunch of cultists from summoning Cthulhu or other unspeakable Lovecraftian horrors. Each apocalypse brings with it new gameplay mechanisms such as the Insanity deck of phobias or the new night/day event deck.

This game includes five survivor characters: Priest, Army Ranger, Scientist, Adventurer, and Ronin. Each character comes with their own unique survivor deck of abilities and equipment cards.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Professor Brainstorm Equation Invasion Card Game: BrainForce Expansion 1
description:
The first and only expansion for the Professor Brainstorm Equation Invasion Card Game. It has 20 cards -- 9 blue, 11 orange -- and a data sheet on the members of BrainForce, each of whom is represented by a card in the set:

Arnee the Amazing Toad
Boltz the Robot
Gwendolyn, a human teen from Earth
Risky Racoon
Rumble the Hillfar Giant

As personalities enter the cultural scene, inevitably some are pushed to the very fringes of society. But there at the periphery, new bonds are formed as they find their common traits.

The goal of this set-collection game is to attain the lowest score possible by finding cards whose abstract background patterns match each other, and either ending the game while you're ahead, or pushing your luck to maximize the benefits of the sets you close.

With over 2,000,000 possible setups, every game feels different! Randomize or customize to your heart's content!
The goal is to be the first player to get rid of your deck. You start with only four cards, but they don't synergize very well! So build your deck wisely, first, then destroy it!

Players stack Flip-o-Rama Cards in the middle of the play area and take turns flipping them over. The first player to spot a Flip-o-Rama match dings the Bell. The player to collect the most Flip-o-Rama Cards wins!

You Who?! is the game of you knows who, because sometimes we know people better than they know themselves. The squishy baloney gets tossed when we know someone is full of it. But be careful, when it's your turn to ask the question, the squishy baloney may be thrown at you!

250 card game questions, broken into 5 rounds that go from light-hearted to more thought provoking. The game questions are based on real life, because real life is more funny than we think!

The Lunch Room Game is not your typical food fight fun! With 6 squishy tater tots to play for, you will have to be quick so you won't lose your lunch. Players compete to beat the odds in The Lunch Room Game and it's cast of characters, like the mean girls, to be the first player to fill their lunch tray to win the game.

No game board in The Lunch Room Game, each player gets their own mini lunch tray, just like the one at school.

Players draw a card and add it to the matching section in the tray. Cards may switch up the game by making you trade trays, fight over tots, etc.

boardgame: Pete the Cat: Pizza Pie Game
description:
Based on the New York Times Best-seller Pete the Cat

Cooperative game using a spin-and-move mechanic. Move the number of spaces resulting on the spinner. Land on a green space and collect that many pizza toppings. Land on a red space and give back that many. Land on a pizza+ space, take two toppings and give them to any other player.

When one person collects five toppings, they are allowed to place them on the pizza. The game ends when all toppings are successfully collected from the box and placed on the pizza!

boardgame: Daniel Tiger's Neighborhood: Early Reading Game
description:
Children explore letter names and sounds as they create simple words to help pave the path to reading success. Play and read with Daniel Tiger and his neighbors in this board game for beginning readers that encourages social skills, literacy and counting.

Spin the spinner and move that number of spaces. Players will try to complete a word using the letter they landed on as the starting letter, and a card from their hand to complete the word. Special rules apply for landing on Q or X, as well as vowels (collecting gift tokens, etc.).

The player with the most gift tokens once all players have crossed the finish line wins the game.

Introduction:Felduz (from Proto-Germanic: Felþuz, which means "field") is a card game for two to four players. It is played with a French deck of 40 cards (without 8, 9, or 10) and betting chips. This game began to be created in 2015 by Saïd Galdseid, but it took until 2023 to perfect its rules.

				
				
					Definitions:
     The field is the complete set of cards owned by each player with which the plays are made.
     The open field is a set of cards from a player's field that are visible to the other players.
     The closed field is a set of cards from a player's field that are hidden from the other players.
     The reserve is a set of N cards that a player discards when they manage to complete their card combination (where N can be zero).
     An event is a category or type of combination that determines how the winner of a round is decided. Events are classified in order of importance: "by points," "by length," and "by rank."
     An amphora is a mandatory initial bet that must be placed before the start of each round. The player who shuffles and the player to his left, who cuts, are the only ones who place the amphora. In tournament mode, the value of the amphora can increase every certain number of rounds, as agreed between the players before the start of the game.


				
				
					Card values:The J, Q and K cards are worth 8, 9 and 10 respectively, although they can optionally be replaced by 8, 9 and 10 if you wish to exclude face cards. Aces are worth 1 and other cards are worth their face value.

				
				
					Hierarchy of events:
     By points: refers to the lowest possible sum of points in each player's field, with a minimum of 40 points and taking into account the lowest value of the reserve in case of a tie in points. In case of a tie, we use the next event as a tiebreaker.
     By length: refers to the smallest number of cards in the field. In case of a tie, we use the next event as a tiebreaker.
     By rank: refers to the lowest card in the field. In case of a tie, we proceed to compare the next lowest card and so on until breaking the tie.


				
				
					Distribution:In the first round, a player is randomly designated as the dealer, who will deal five cards to each player, which will be their field. The remaining cards are placed face down in the center of the table to form the deck. At the start of each new round, one player shuffles their cards, and the player to their left cuts the deck.

Next, the player to the left of the dealer becomes the new dealer for the round. After the shuffle and cut, each player receives five cards, which form their field. Each player then places three of these cards face-up to form their "open field", leaving the other two in their hand to form the "closed field".

				
				
					Turns:The player to the left of the dealer starts their turn and performs these actions in the following order:


     Take a card from the deck and place it in your "closed field."
     Optionally bet any number of chips, as long as it is a multiple of the amphora.
     Choose one of the cards from your "closed field" and place it face up in your "open field".
     Decide to stand by choosing not to take any more cards from the deck and horizontally placing a number of cards from your "closed field." Reserve cards are not included in the sum because they are part of the discard.
     Pass your turn to the player on your left.


				
				
					Betting:Before the cards are dealt, the amphorae are placed. If a player decides to bet, the other players can:


     Accept the bet by saying "I’m in".
     Reject the bet by saying "I’m out" and lose the round.
     Raise the bet by saying "I raise".


When all players have accepted the bet, the chips accumulate in the "pot". The player who wins the round takes all the chips in the pot.

				
				
					End of the game:When a player runs out of chips to bet, they are eliminated from the game. The game ends when only one player remains who has not been eliminated, who is the winner.

boardgame: Dungeons & Dragons: Onslaught – Scenario Kit: The Benefactor
description:
Dungeons & Dragons Onslaught: Scenario Kit – The Benefactor is a companion to the Dungeons & Dragons Onslaught Harpers vs. Zhentarim core game, featuring six new scenarios and cards to use existing miniatures in the new scenarios. It’s a great way for players with a large number of D&D minis to quickly expand their Onslaught collection, for new players to try out our other miniature lines, and for painters to customize their Onslaught experience.

—description from the publisher

Space Privateer Command is a space game adventure played on 18 cards where players assume the character of a space privateer traveling planet to planet to trade different types of commodities using the dice trading prices on each planet.
Apart from the daily commerce, players may complete missions ranging from destroy freighters or pirates using the space radar combat system. Some privateer jobs are just simple escort VIPS missions or mercy mission to deliver food to planets. All jobs can be found through job postings from the job seeker computer and completing each job with satisfaction will increase the players reputation to satisfy certain scenarios for the game story line.  Earning Kredts(cash) will allow players to upgrade their ship. You may buy a new turret gun, add more missiles or even hire crew who will effect certain events and outcomes in combat.
Perhaps tired of the honest living, players could also smuggle some illicit cargo or even do some pirating themselves by attacking poor defenseless freighters to steal precious cargo will incurring the wrath of the local militia. The choice is yours!
There are a small range of enemy fighters to face of during the orbital phase and players will be rolling combat dice to inflict damage during space dogfights or bribe your way out of sticky situations.
Check your fuel gauge and your missiles and strap yourself into this semi “open world” space adventure!

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Legions - Fantasy Board Game is a unique turn-based strategy war game. It has the aspects of a trading card game mixed with a tabletop. You will use your Legion to defeat your opponent(s) on a battlefield. You will use your Legion led by your General, who will have unique buffs and abilities, leading your units to victory.

Our game will feature multiple modes to allow our players to have a wide variety of game experiences. Classic Mode - Is set up to get new players into the game or for those who just want to play casually. Legions Mode - Is set up for intermediate players who have played a variety of card or board games.

Gameplay typically takes up to 30-60 minutes to play, where you can play with up to 2 or 8 people. Featuring 4 unique decks with the bloodlines of Human, Elf, Inferna, and Celestia. Each deck contains 35 card decks each containing 1 General, 3 Officers, and 31 Action cards. A double sided game board with a 2 player battlefield, and 4 player battlefield. With 92 Diamond tokens, 8 sets of 10 multi-colored for units, 3 sets of 4 multi-colored for officers, and a rulebook.

—description from the designer

Ghost Busting! is a solitaire, worker placement & push your luck game
In New Jesselton ghost are running rampant, despite the city denying the existence of ghost. As a newly established Ghost Busting business, your crew have been cleaning up the town from the nasties; but you have stumbled upon a doomsday prophecy that the city is in peril from 3 apocalyptic size ghost.

This solo game roll and write game will have players managing their rooster of 3 ghost hunters to attend to haunted sites in the daily classifieds to bust some evil spirits that is haunting different premises. The work will earn money, provide intel and experimental slime which can be use to purchase and produce more useful equipment or hire an extra ghost hunter in preparation to the final face off with the final ghost boss.

Do you have what it takes to save the city from eminent disaster?

—description from the designer

Official link to game
https://thekealf.itch.io/ghost-busting

boardgame: オオカミ×トリック (Wolf x Trick)
description:
The player is a young man in a werewolf village who dreams of living in the city.

However, life in the city is full of dangers (circles)!

In order to be allowed to live in the city, you must prove that the circle does not transform you.
If you collect the most circles, you will transform, so aim for the second number of circles that you will not transform!

In Asteroid Miners you lead your crew of fellow belters in mining the asteroid belt, excavating valuable minerals in the cold of space.

Asteroid Miners is a 2-player dice-rolling game where you excavate precious metals using a precision drill and upgradable mining equipment, digging deeper and deeper into a series of asteroids. You will use a dice pool to manipulate your drill components to match the minerals in your current excavation section.

After successful excavations you may sell your minerals to a fluctuating Metal Market, trying to earn more profit than your competitor to win the game.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Warhammer 40,000: Kill Team – Ashes of Faith
description:
Slinking in the shadows, countless heinous, warp-worshipping Chaos Cults corrupt the weak of mind and soul across the Emperor’s realm. One of Humanity’s strongest lines of defence against such insidious threats is the Inquisition – a clandestine organisation that can call on the most zealous and feared operatives in all the Imperium. Their endless task is to root out and crush any sign of heresy they uncover – and on the world of Exhalus, blighted by war and warp alike, Inquisitorial Agents and mutated Chaos Cults stalk one another through gloomy underhive crawlways, steaming sumps, and rusting industrial ruins.

Kill Team: Ashes of Faith is a campaign expansion box for Warhammer 40,000: Kill Team, the ultimate sci-fi skirmish game. It includes 40 plastic miniatures to create extensive rosters for two new Kill Teams – the relentless Inquisitorial Agents and their Ancillary Support operatives, up against the sinister heretics of a Chaos Cult, who mutate into powerful new forms mid-game – as well as two books full of background material, rules for both kill teams, a new campaign system and missions, plus new campaign cards, game tiles, and reusable stickers.

—description from the publisher

Tentacles and suckers are everywhere!

Squid are supposed to be spineless, right? Then why are so many of them trying to kill you in this dungeon?

Everything is covered in slime, and even the food is attacking! Grab your Cuttle Bone, put on your Nautilus Shelm, and make sure to deliver some sorely needed Squid Pro Quo.

You'll want to seize Munchkin Squids with all eight of your arms – before it jets away!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Die Legenden von Andor: Die ewige Kälte – Der magische Sturm
description:
Single card expansion for Legends 2-4 of The Legends of Andor: The Eternal Frost.

When it comes into play, it temporary changes the function of some ice tiles.

boardgame: O Vale dos Monstros II: A Invasão da Cidade
description:
In O Vale dos Monstros II, the monsters invade the city.

In this re-implementation of O Vale dos Monstros, each player controls a type of monster, each with its own special ability.
Each round, players can choose to perform 3 out of 6 possible actions, aiming to scare the residents, invade, and control the houses in the town.
Players can also try to surround their opponents' monsters, which not only generates victory points, but also push the opponent's monsters away from the city.
At the end of the game, the player with the most points wins. Points can be awarded from the scares performed, houses controlled, opponent's monsters pushed away, and monsters in play.

A family-friendly, toilet-themed quirky trick taking game.
The goal is to be the first to claim 400 poop points, or to score the most before one of the draw decks runs out.

Players play poop cards into the play area, alongside toilet cubicles, to accumulate potentially claimable points. Once a poop is 'finished' players race to try to claim the points of that poop in play.

Turns can be based on grabbing points, sharing points or sabotaging other players' options. Let chaos commence!

—description from the designer
Meadow: Envelope O is a set of 4 unique cards featuring pests and poisonous flora. Shuffle these cards into their corresponding decks to further expand your Meadow.

7TV is a miniatures skirmish game that enables you to re-create the action set pieces of your favorite movies and TV series, or invent entirely new productions straight out of your imagination – on the tabletop!

7TV allows you to pick from a large range of cinematic archetypes to play any type of game, with any miniatures, in any setting! Pitting stalwart heroes against villainous alien invaders or zombie hordes or secret ninja commandos is easy with 7TV's intuitive and dynamic rules.

7TV has many similarities with other wargames. In addition to these rules, you'll need an opponent to play against, miniature models, dice, some tokens, and a flat surface to play on. You and your rival will each choose your forces and pit them against one another in any setting you can imagine from seedy city streets to ancient Mars to lost temples and post-apocalyptic wastelands. 7TV's borders are the limits of your imagination and your love of film and TV.

—description from the publisher
family: Series: action engine (Crooked Dice Game Design Studio)

description:
Will you succeed in baking your dream cake and surprise the jury with your superb creation? Or will it turn out to be a failure? Don't panic! A bit of bluffing will go a long way in Heel Weiland Bakt!

—description from the publisher (translated)

The title of this game refers to the TV show "Heel Holland Bakt", the Dutch edition of "The Great British Bake Off". In the game, players try to complete recipes by gather the appropriate ingredients. The do so by taking ingredient cards into their hands and discarding others. As they do so, they must also pay attention to what the other players are doing, as a player can also score points by predicting if the other players are successful. At the end of each round, player user their juror cards to bet on other player's success.

—user summary
family: Series: Kaarten met Koeien (The Game Master BV)

description:
Beyond Sector X is a tactical strategy game for one player.

Amidst the vast emptiness of space, you take on the role of a lone explorer, venturing forth on a perilous mission. When a ruthless band of extraterrestrial foes abducts your trusted companion, you must summon all your courage and cunning to infiltrate the enemy mothership, battle the monstrous creatures within, collect all the Energy Crystals throughout, and save your comrade from certain doom.

Use your skills and abilities to navigate through two corridors of the enemy spacecraft. In the first corridor, search for useful items to aid you in your mission. In the second corridor, race against a ticking clock to safely teleport out. Your ultimate goal is to defeat the hostile extraterrestrial beings hiding Beyond Sector X.

Will you emerge victorious, or fall victim to the dangers lurking in the darkness?

—description from the designer

In the doomed depths of the space hulk Gallowdark, elite kill teams hunt each other through crumbling passages. The space hulk is on the brink of annihilation, chunks of it torn away by the catastrophic crash of an Imperial prison barge. As it drifts ever-closer to destruction, long-confined distress signals emitted by an Ironkin of the Leagues of Votann finally break through to the galaxy beyond – and now The Reachfar, a cruiser of the Trans-Hyperian Alliance, speeds to the hulk to rescue their lost comrade. This will be no easy task, however, for they are hunted by a pack of Fellgor Ravagers – fearsome Chaos Beastmen hunters who stand among the Gallowdark’s most ferocious and cunning denizens.

Kill Team: Gallowfall is a full expansion box for Warhammer 40,000: Kill Team, the ultimate sci-fi skirmish game. It includes miniatures for two new Kill Teams – the hardened warriors of the Leagues of Votann’s Hearthkyn Salvagers, and the savage Chaos Beastmen of the Fellgor Ravagers. They’re packed together with loads of space hulk terrain, which is fully compatible with that found in Killzone Gallowdark, along with more than 500 transfers, a double-sided game board, and a rules manual including missions, background material exploring the mysterious setting, and full rosters for the two kill teams.

—description from the publisher

In Nuvens ("Clouds"), you and your friends are watching clouds in the park on a nice summer afternoon. Each round, one of the players assumes the role of Cloud Sculptor and must mold shapes with cotton balls to communicate a secret word draft by an app. The other players, one by one, are Observers who try to guess the secret word or pass the turn. However, if the Observer gets lost in his daydreams and takes too long to pass the turn, he will get wet in the rain and lose points. After two rounds, the player who accumulates the most points  wins the game.

—description from the publisher

Circle of Blood is a cooperative wargame, for two to four players, that uses 32mm miniatures and scaled scenery to recreate the tactical confrontations of one or more groups of adventurers, called guilds. The game also allows to play solo, for those adventurers who prefer to live the Gormalak experience by themselves. Or competitives one.

In Circle of Blood you take command of a guild made up of three adventurers. You can design them to your liking or use the predesigned characters (which we recommend to get started).
Game sequence
The first thing you have to do in order to play is choosing a mission. We recommend playing "New in town," featured in the complete manual.
Each player sets up their three models within the area specified by the mission. Then, one of the players starts the turn by activating one of their adventurers. Next the other player chooses one of their adventurers and activates it.
NPCs are always the last models to be activated (regardless of whether they are allies or not). Thus, after each guild has activated one adventurer, it’s the NPCs turn, who must activate the model that is the closest to an adventurer.
Remember to place an activation marker next to each model once it has been activated so you don't activate it again until the next turn.
This same activation order is repeated over and over again until there are no more models left to be activated, at which point the turn ends and we move on to the next one. Remove all activation markers at that time.

Game system
When activated, each model has a number of Action Points (AP), which is usually 4. Most of their actions (such as moving, jumping, or attacking an enemy) cost it 1 AP, though some may cost more. The model can repeat the same action a number of times equal to half its total AP (usually two). The only action that a model can freely repeat is to move.

Movement (MOV)
For each AP that the model uses to move, it will cover a distance equal to the one indicated in the MOV box of its template. When you move, the model can be facing in any direction you want.

Checks
Many actions are not automatic and require checking certain values from the Adventurer Sheet or creature template. Look up the value in question (which will range from 1 to 20) and roll 1d20. If the result is less than the number indicated on the Adventurer Sheet, the check is a success. If the result is exactly that number, it is a critical success. If the result is greater than the required number, it is a failure. Regardless of the final result of the check and whether it is successful or not, it always consumes the AP required to perform the action.

Penalties
External factors, which we call 'penalties', can negatively affect the course of a test. The sum of all the penalties changes the final value to be obtained in the check.
For example: A 2 penalty on a skill of 12 requires a d20 roll between 3 and 12.

Offensive actions
There are three types of offensive actions (melee, ranged, and magical).

    Melee: This type of attack requires base-to-base contact with the enemy model. The attacker rolls his Combat Skill (CS) and suffers a penalty equal to the opponent's Defense (DEF).
    Ranged: The target of the attack must be within the range of the weapon used. The attacker rolls his Shooting Skill (SS) and suffers various penalties (adjustments for long range, cover, movement, etc.).
    Magical: The target of the spell must be within range. The attacker rolls his Magic Skill (MS) and suffers penalties depending on the difficulty of the spell and the Magic Resistance (MR) of the victim.


When the offensive action succeeds, it will deal an amount of damage indicated on the weapon or spell’s description. This is subtracted from the victim's Armor (ARM) and the final result is subtracted from their current Hit Points (HP). When the model loses all of its HP, it will be defeated and must be removed from the game.
Reaction
If a model has not been activated yet, but suffers an offensive action, it has the chance to react. During a reaction, the model has access to only half its normal AP and can only attack the model that caused the reaction.
To determine who acts first, both models make a Dexterity (DEX) check. Whoever rolls the highest on a d20 wins. Alternate the actions of the models involved until they consume all their AP.

NPC Behavior
All the NPCs act according to the objective they must fulfill in the mission (attack, defend, secure an area, etc.). Each objective describes how the model must me moved during the scenario and what actions they will perform.
When another model or game circumstance “bothers” them (for example, attacking the NPC or making it impossible for them to fulfill their objective), we must consult their personality trait (rational, aggressive, or passive). This will determine their behavior, for example, causing them to run away from the model responsible, ignore it or even attack.

NPC combat
When they decide to fight, NPCs look for the most effective way of attacking based on the attributes of their weapons (CS, SS or MS). They always use the one that allows them to attack the greatest number of times taking into account, for example, if they are in base-to-base contact or further away from their enemy.

These are the most important mechanics in Circle of Blood. Now we invite you to take a look at the final manual to see everything that this narrative action wargame has to offer. Battle dozens of different enemies, gain fame and riches, and discover the sinister secret behind Gormalak!

—description from the publisher

Oghmu-Age of Discordance a fast-paced, dice-driven boardgame of skirmish battles in which each player choose a Faction and pits them against their rivals in battles across the ethereal demi-plane of Oghmu.

Each time you play the game you and your opponent each has to choose a Faction from the available 6 Factions. A Faction is a collection of Heroes from the same race, fighting for the same goals. Each Faction has its distinct characteristic and always consist of the same Heroes, identified by their Hero Sheets and represented by their individual tokens. Each hero can make Base Actions, Special Actions during the game, each Hero has a unique Innate Ability and each Faction has a Faction Ability which gives several options to the players in every round and make the battles very diverse.

You also need to choose one of the four available Battlefields to fight on. Each Battlefield is divided into hexes, which are used to determine the position of Heroes, and the distance between them.

The goal of the game is to eliminate all Heroes from the enemy Faction.

-description from publisher

A game that predicts the percentage of cards that will be put into play.

The parent player declares the winning move (to be scored), such as Big (majority) or Small (minority).
Then, everyone puts out their cards at once. Then, check the percentage of cards that came into play, and all the players who put out the cards corresponding to the declaration score!
　
There are cards that have different ratios from the beginning, and there are cards that change the ratio, leading to unexpected developments!
Who can predict the most!?
publishers: クードゥーラパン (Queue de Lapin)

description:
Mombatuk's Quest is a solitaire, roll and write dungeon crawl.

Journey into the Island of Borneo’s folklore as you play as a fictional character called Mombatuk.

Mombatuk’s Quest is a solo dungeon crawl write and roll game. Plays as Mombatuk the warrior as you take a quest to defeat the biawak leader (indigenous Lizardman) that has been terrorizing the village. You will have to search an abandon” long house” (abandon village) for the secret key to a secret hideout to defeat the biawak leader! Fight your way through numerous biawak soldiers and other dangerous mystical creatures. Gather items and find the traveling merchant and trade for weapons and Elixir for healing to help you on your quest.

—description from the designer

Official link to game
https://thekealf.itch.io/mombatuks-quest

Summer Season Promo 2023 of FryxGames, which you can buy on its own, or get for free if you order something at their webstore during Summer 2023. It will be later released as set with the other Seaons Promos of this year

Supercapacitors is a blue project card with a building and an energy tag

Effect: Converting Energy to Heat during production is optional for each Energy resource. 
Raise Megacredit production by one.

Promo card X46.

boardgame: Monopoly: MSC Cruises Special Travel Edition
description: Explore 22 unique ports of call featured in this exclusive edition of MONOPOLY. MSC Cruises is the world's largest privately owned cruise company and the number one cruise line in Europe, South America, South Africa and the Gulf. Journey around the board and invest in our exclusive islands, ships and destinations, Roll the dice and see where this cruise around the board' may take you! Follow in the footsteps of MSC Cruises the fastest growing cruise line in the world and watch your empire grow! Make deals with your fellow players, look out for bargains at auction and remember there is only room for one at the top. This special edition celebrates the entire MSC fleet and routes. What better way to celebrate your cruise holiday than playing with your new limited edition MONOPOLY board game!

DoomRoll was made for every Gamer that wants to have a ton of fun with their miniatures. DoomRoll is for every Gamer that doesn’t have 5 people around to play but, can always find one.  For every Gamer that thinks Warhammer is unbalanced.  It was made for every Gamer that wants to play, but doesn’t have the entire day.

DoomRoll was made to improve roll play.  To make it simple.  To make it fast.  To make it fun. Combat is based on percentage. Damage is based on 10 sideds.    Armor is subtracted from the total.  Each unit class has unique traits allowing game play and strategy to become unlimited. Every game becomes unique.

DoomRoll is balanced and creative.  Allowing an infinite selection of landscapes to be created on the Battlemat.  With conditions to Victory that go beyond a war of attrition.  You can play Capture the Flag in DoomRoll.  You can play DoomRoll on a timer.  You can play DoomRoll with teams.

—description from publisher

He has arrived in the city. The best museums have to create a new exhibition made up of works by great artists that have been forgotten.

Like a good adviser, you will have to assemble the best collection, in order to attract all the attention of visitors and onlookers, for which you will have to create a set collection of 8 pieces of art, in 8 rounds, with different parameters that will be scored the best art collection at the end of the game.

________________________________

Ha llegado a la ciudad. Los mejores museos tienen que crear una nueva exposición compuestas por obras de grandes artistas que han quedado olvidadas.

Como una buena asesora tendrás que montar la mejor colección, para así atraer toda la atención de visitantes y curiosos, para lo que tendrás que crear una colección de 8 obras de arte, en 8 rondas, con parámetros distintos que puntuarán al final del juego a la mejor colección.

The eagle banners of the Roman legions fly on the banks of the Rhine. While Nero strives for the throne of Imperium Romanum, the Roman settlements on the border of Germania blossom into large fortified cities that are now clearly distinguishable from one another.

Trade flourishes and new outposts emerge to protect against raids by Germanic tribes. 
However, as the cities grow in splendor, so does discord among the rival governors in competition for the glory of Rome.

As governor, you have four years to make your city the most impressive.
  
Use your individual advantages and decide whether you want to use the available outposts on the Magna Tiles to forge a strategic alliance to defend against Germanic attacks to reap valuable bonuses.

The Discordia Magna expansion contains 6 individual City Maps (player boards) along with the Magna Tiles representing the outposts.

—description from designer

After centuries of fighting, the ninja clans have merged into one, but for the new clan to work, they must be able to coordinate their training together.
Play as masters and organize ninja movements and attacks.
The future of the new clan is in your hands!

In this game each player acts as a ninja master and must face a board of 3x3 cards.
The goal in this game is to line up three ninjas with the same posture (4 types: pose, kick, smoke bomb, attack), either forming a row or a column. The color of the ninja does not matter to make these trios.

During their turn, each player can do one of these three actions:
1. Flip a card to reveal which kind of ninja or hidden action contain.
2. Swap two orthogonally adjacent cards.
3. Discard one of the 9 cards in exchange of a random card from the top of the game deck (training deck).

When a player lines up three ninjas with the same pose, the master must say the words "Nin-Nin". The master now can take and reserve the three ninjas to one side of the table (to count points at the end of the game), taking three new unrevealed cards from the training deck and turning over an already revealed card to each other players (masters).

The game will be over when the cards in the game deck are gone, and a master with all the cards revealed can no longer make any ninja trios. The player with the most ninja points will be the winner of the game.

-description from designer

La Révolte des Cipayes: 1857-1858 (The Sepoy Mutiny) is a Strategic level board-wargame that uses the same mechanisms as The Belgian Campaign, 1815 (VV124) and The Eagles of the Danube (VV144).

On 10 May 1857, the massacre at the British garrison in Meerut by indigenous troops of the Bengal Presidency erupted and spread to the north of the Indian subcontinent. It took more than two years for the British to regain control in the revolting provinces. Once the rebellion was squashed in 1859, the British Crown was forced to launch a series of major reforms to modify its system of government in India, notably by dissolving the East India Company, to which power had been delegated up until then.
 The game allows you to experience at an Operational level the first two years of conflict during which the main military operations occurred.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
In a not so far future, space garbage has turned out to be a really valuable resource.

You are part of Recaditos Siderales, the little space errands company. Go to the bulletin board and take a little space errand card (a Recadito Sideral in Spanish). This card will give you the victory condition.

Take your ship and a quadrant card. Traven through the different places of these cards, where you can find some bounty (throwing some dice), some merchants to commerce with or different kind of threats.

These threats are represented by cards and you will have to manage your resources (fuel and ammo) to defeat them by rolling some dice. Once defeated, these threats will drop some bounty too.

When you reach the end of a quadrant, if you didn't acomplish your mission, the threat level will be increased and you will be able to repair your ship for the next quadrant.

Recaditos Siderales is a single player game where each game lasts around 30 minutes.

—description from the designer

boardgame: From Muskets to Rifles…: Fast Play Wargame for 19th Century Conflicts
description:
From Muskets to Rifles… is based upon C’est la Guerre: Fast Play Wargame for the Age of Muskets. This system was designed to provide a fast yet satisfying game play, not necessarily a “realistic” simulation of battle recreation. Bases or ‘Units’ in the game do not represent a certain number of men or artillery pieces but are abstract representations of force.

From Muskets to Rifles… uses:


     a simple command structure to manage battlefield movement without dealing with morale or having the players issue orders,
     a troop grading system so that better trained more effective troops can have an advantage over poorly trained less effective troops, and
     a method to account for casualties and losses without an overly complicated accounting system.


At the beginning of the 19th Century European armies were mostly armed with smooth bore muzzle loading muskets and bronze smooth bore muzzle loading artillery. These armies fought in dense formations of infantry, and they relied upon the gallant cavalry charging in mass to break enemy lines. Not so very different from the weapons and methods used two hundred years before.

By the end of the 19th Century we see the introduction of machine guns, breach loading and bolt action repeating rifles, metal cartridge ammunition, accurate and powerful rifled artillery, and even the occasional airplane flying through the skies. We also see the mass production and industrialization of warfare that made these new technologies cheap, uniform, and abundant. This period of dynamic change, between the age of the musket and the ‘modern’ mobile warfare we see today, began at some point after the end of the Napoleonic Wars and came to its culmination during World War I. So that will be how we define the time period of ‘19th Century Warfare’ – 1820s to 1910s.

(publisher description)

Cheezy Chase is a stop-or-go game for 2 to 5 mice ages 5 and up.

Dorothy the little mouse has a rumbling tummy. To stock up, she has to get out of her hole and explore the house in search of cheese. So she ventures out in search of delicious little golden cubes. But beware! The house is littered with mouse traps. And if that’s not enough, Kitty is on the prowl, ready to pounce on her and eat her up.

On your turn, draw a card and place it face up in the play area. Repeat this action as many times as you like, until:

     You get caught because you have at least 3 traps or 3 cats in the game area. Your turn end and you lose the cards you have revealed.
     You stop before getting caught and you win all the cards in the game area and place them in a pile in front of you.
     You stop and win all the card in the game area if you find a 3 cheeses card!


The game ends when all cards have been drawn. Each player counts the cheeses on their cards that they have won.
The player with the most cheeses wins the game!

boardgame: Dungeons & Dragons: Onslaught – Expansion: Red Wizards 1
description:
Welcome four new fierce Red Wizard characters with Dungeons & Dragons Onslaught: Expansion – Red Wizards 1! Devise new strategies to tactically take out the opposition. Expand your collection of characters for your magocratic Red Wizard faction. Shoot your poison phrases at your enemies as Zhaleshah the Bard. Harness the destructive and restoring power of fire and torch the competition with Stagharr the Druid and their Wildfire Spirit. Weave your web to outmaneuver your rivals with Vendrah Regalle the Ranger and their spiders. No time for cool downs with Jalana Stalos the Paladin. Instead, gain and spend divine charges to smite your foes. Whether you are coming in hot with some fierce front-line forces or stealthily crawling and slashing your way through enemies, the Red Wizards 1 Expansion offers new exciting ways to add to the onslaught.

Contents:

4 fully-detailed, pre-painted miniatures
4 Combat Dial Cards
8 Level Up Cards
1 Summon Card
Gameplay tokens
Rules sheet

-description from publisher's website

Dungeons & Dragons Onslaught Many-Arrows expansion brings four new characters to your game, offering new, tactical advantages over each scenario. The specialist and support characters are an area-control and melee focused faction that can buff allies, debuff opponents, and add new tactical options. Utilize each character's individual abilities to battle opponents and foes, in order to conquer each scenario in a whole new way.

Diarmuid the Fairy Nature Cleric finds hidden foes scattered across the battlefield and heals the party. Sarzumina the Half-Elf Phantom Rogue poisons her foes and moves easily across treacherous terrain. Falenyn the Triton Abjuration Wizard protects companions with powerful wards and dispels magical effects. Thurkear the Wise is a Lizardfolk Archfey Warlock, who makes friendly characters invisible and uses magical spells to avoid attacks.

Contents:

4 fully-detailed, pre-painted miniatures
4 Combat Dial Cards
8 Level Up Cards
Gameplay tokens
Rules sheet

-description from publisher's website

The Dungeons & Dragons: Onslaught Expansion - Harpers 1 expands your tactical options with four exciting new characters to add to your games!  Ayur, the Aarakocra Monk utilizes mobility to take on multiple opponents while Adnan Zarehm the Fire Genasi Cleric brings light down to burn foes and bolster allies.  Eileen Rumrunner the Halfling Sorcerer can win big when gambling on her Wild Magic, and Nurith the Locathah Rogue is a master of the ambush!

Expansion Packs are a perfect way to expand your collection with exciting new characters that ensure you have the right tools for any scenario or opponent you may face. This product adds four new character options to the Harpers faction.

Contents

4 highly-detailed, pre-painted miniatures
4 Combat Dial Cards
8 Level Up Cards
gameplay tokens
rules sheet

-description from publisher's website

The Dungeons & Dragons: Onslaught Expansion – Zhentarim 1 brings four new characters, each with their own unique strategies to your games! Vonkar Zzod the Hobgoblin Cleric wades into battle supported by his spiritual weapon, and “Ruby Eye” Doireann the Dwarf Barbarian dares you to engage. Sha’Bel, Life-Sever the Goliath Fighter excels in the front lines while Daiyu Tseng, the Human Bard hides in shadows, lashing out with psychic blades and malevolent whispers.

Expansion Packs are a perfect way to expand your collection with exciting new characters that ensure you have the right tools for any scenario or opponent you may face. This product adds four new characters options to the Zhentarim faction.

Contents

4 highly-detailed, pre-painted miniatures
4 Combat Dial Cards
8 Level Up Cards
gameplay tokens
rules sheet

-description from publisher's website

Busted Barrel Racing is a madcap card game that involves mad tasks and zany challenges. It's a game of card collecting, tile laying, and simple track building. Fans of Rodeo style competition and horse riding will especially enjoy the Western theme of the game. Everyone else will love the zany tabletop card game designed for 2 to 4 players, but best played by 4.

Players take turns drawing cards and laying down tiles, trying to build a zany race course that must zig zag its way around the competition Barrels and back home to the Finish. At the same time players have to maneouver their Horse and Rider along the course, randomly dealing with hilarious physical tasks and challenges. The player who completes a track and makes it home first is the winner.

2-4 players, all ages.
Game play is 30 to 90 minutes depending on the number of players and varying game experiences.

Purchase this game at The Game Crafter: https://www.thegamecrafter.com/games/busted-barrel-racing

-description from designer

The world is beginning to understand the impermanence of all things, and entertainment is fading from this world. The "DJ club" of the secret society "LSG" stands up to bring back entertainment to the world! It is up to you whether you want to fight against the society as a "DJ club" or betray the "LSG" to master the front and reach the paradise.

In each turn players take part in events to collect resources, you can give event cards to other players to block one of their's event cards.

The game ends when any of the players becomes the TOP DJ by having over 1000 cash and 500 charisma or the High priest by reaching the 10th level of virtue.

The player becomes a roof painter in a certain town, and repaints the roofs using three colors of red, blue, and yellow in response to the instructions of the government office and requests from the residents.

Depending on the status of the request, the roof that was just painted blue by a colleague may be immediately repainted red.

The turn order is determined by trick taking, and chips are placed on a sheet with the image of the roof of a city. Points are scored if you can create a theme sequence consisting of three colors: red, blue, and yellow.

The theme line is won on a first-come, first-served basis, so of course you want to win with tricks, but the player who was lower in the trick can also gain an advantage depending on the result of the previous player repainting the roof or the situation of the theme. Also, during the trick, players who could not follow the lead have a chance to score, making it a play that is not straightforward.

boardgame: オタク騎士くんにやさしいギャル令嬢は存在する。(Otaku Kishi-kun ni Yasashii Gyaru Reijiou wa Sonzaisuru.)
description:
In this world gyaru are friendly to otaku. In some families daughters become gyaru, which otaku who became knight are trying to support while aristocracy is trying to fix them through the disciplinary comitee.

In each of the 9 rounds players play one of the 10 numbered cards, the losing card gets turned, usually loses the card with lower value, however each turn has an event which can change the course of action.

After 9 turns winner is the player whose sum of not turned cards is greater, alternatively if in one round both the gyaru daughter and the disciplinary comitee gets played, the player who played the gyaru daughter immediately loses.

"Let the chase begin, shall we?" 
Armeria is a cooperative game of witches vs. students. The goal is to escape from the pursuing witches.

The players are divided into students and witches, goal of the student is to ring the bell at the top of the school, witches try to stop them from doing that. To ring the bell, at least one of the students has to leave the 3rd floor. To do that they have to acquire keys to each subsequent floor.

In students' turns they roll the dice and use cards with magic to travel through a floor to search for keys, they can only leave if they find 2 keys.

In witches' turn they attack students and destroy corridors using magic.

You play as a manager trying to help Reach Doll idols get their debut.

In your turn you try to match 3 cards of the same Idol by drawing from one of the 3 decks or the associated queues. By doing that you get a ticket to their concert.

The game ends when a player gets 7 concert tickets.

boardgame: Ciudad de Patriotas: Madrid 2 de mayo de 1808
description:
Game that recreates the popular uprising against the French invasion of Madrid on May 2, 1808 after kidnapping the royal family. It will begin after the uprising, the French War or the Peninsular War.

Game for two players: French and Spanish, with 6 turns.

Historical game or wargame whose objective is for the French player to control the districts of the city of Madrid.
The Spanish player must prevent this control and eliminate the greatest number of French troops, decreasing the French victory level.

COMPONENTS:
- 30 Cards
- 2 player help sheets
- 2 sheets of die-cut tokens (total 192)
- 42*57cm map
- (+2 sheets to help the player by stretch goal if possible)
- Rules of the game (16 pages including: 6 rules pages, 2 optional rules pages, 2 tables pages, 1 scenario page and 5 commentary and historical simulation pages)
- (+1 optional scenario sheet per Stretch Goal if possible)
- Box of 30*22*4.5 cm


                                                                        
                                                                    
                                                                
                                                            
                                                        
                                                    
                                                
                                            
                                        
                                    
                                
                            
                        
                    
                
            
        
    


Juego que recrea el levantamiento popular contra la invasión francesa de Madrid el 2 de mayo de 1808 tras secuestrar a la familia real. Comenzará después del levantamiento, la guerra del francés o guerra peninsular o guerra de la independencia.

Juego para dos jugadores: francés y español, de 6 turnos.

Juego histórico o wargame cuyo objetivo es que el jugador francés controle los distritos de la ciudad de Madrid. 
El jugador español debe impedir este control y eliminar el mayor número de tropas francesas disminuyendo el nivel de victoria francés.

COMPONENTES:
- 30 Cartas
- 2 hojas de ayuda al jugador
- 2 hojas de fichas troqueladas (total 192)
- Mapa de 42*57 cm
- (+2 hojas de ayuda al jugador por Strech goal si es posible )

- (+1 hoja de escenario opcional por Strech Goal si es posible)
- Caja de 30*22*4.5 cm

-description from designer
