family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
Build your perfect cloud architecture in this strategy deck-building game!

AWS BuilderCards challenges players to migrate from on-premises infrastructure to cutting-edge cloud solutions. Starting with basic resources, players gradually build their portfolio, combine services strategically, and earn Well-Architected points for following cloud best practices.

Ideal for both AWS professionals and those wanting to explore cloud services through gameplay!
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Exoland is a truly strategic pure balancing game played with calibrated weights.

Players take turns in a race to be rid of their "fleet" of varied weights by playing them onto a balancing gameboard into risk-reward zones.

Causing a crash or being unable to make a play during the game adds a dangerous penalty piece to the fleet.

A concentric "Crash Factor" grid allows players to anticipate the crash risk of any weight placed anywhere.

Playing risky moves away from the center of balance can win advantages or eliminate penalties.

The game ends once a player has deployed all their spaceships and space junk OR when a player collects 7 of the 8 planets.

Play at your own risk!

boardgame: Drive! A Travel, Road Trip Game
description: DRIVE! A Better-Than-Bingo road trip game—with the scavenger hunt elements everyone enjoys! Just take five cards in your hands & start spotting clues...in five widely-varied categories. Game is available as a Print & Play digital download via Etsy. Comes with rules and 72 printable cards. Overview: Each of the 5 decks of 12 cards is quite different from the rest. Every player receives one card from each deck. Players must complete their 5 cards in order...going from the D card, to the R card, to the I card, etc. until they've spelled Drive! Very portable.

In each round of Schrödinger, one of the player is the Austrian physicist and place inside the box 3 of the 5 components in the game: a wooden cat, two water token and two poison tokens. The other players are his assistants. On each turn, they will shake the game box and use audition and deduction to try to discover what happened to the Doctor's feline . Is it alive, dead or do we have a paradox?

Getting the cat's correct whereabouts give the players points and after every player acted as the doctor twice, whoever has the most points is the winner.

-description from designer

Ivion combines the intensity of a fighting game with the strategy of deck-building card games to create an experience unlike any other. In Ivion, your deck IS your character, and you build it from a variety of classes and specializations. Each character type has numerous cards from which to choose, along with wildly different playstyles. Mix and match them to create your own, unique character!

THE FLETCHER is a force to be reckoned with - her keen eye and steady hand make her a master archer, feared by foes and beloved by allies. From tourney to tourney, she travels the realm, winning over the hearts of the people with her exploits and undeniable charm. When the Baron commits his latest act of villainy, she knows that target practice is over. With bow in hand and arrowheads at the ready, she sets out to deliver a message the Baron won't soon forget.

---

THE BARON has earned every scrap of power he clutches. His tactics are as slimy as they are effective - lying, thieving, poisoning - everything must be on or under the table if he wants to sit at the head of it. He didn’t write the rules; he simply realized there are none. After his most recent power grab, one of the peasantry seems to be more agitated than usual… but that’s nothing he can’t smooth over with one of his special glasses of wine.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Court in Chaos: A Game of Blame
description:
With her Realm on the brink of ruin, the Queen is determined to root out those responsible and punish them. You are a royal advisor, and your life is on the line. You must find a scapegoat and dump the blame on them if you want to keep your head!

Court in Chaos is a game for 2-4 players, lasting 15-30 minutes.

Each turn, you must play a card from your hand to the Blame pile and follow the instructions on the card played.

Each character has a special rule that modifies the instructions.

Characters can change their loyalty, bury secrets (putting cards out of play) and gain Disgrace or Favour.

The game ends when a player with 6 or fewer cards declares. Everyone counts up the BLAME points in their hand - the character with the highest BLAME is beheaded, those with one or more BLAME are banished and the blameless are blessed. If you declare, you cannot be banished - if someone has more BLAME than you, you are exonerated, otherwise, the executioner awaits!

–description from designer

boardgame: The Game of Life: Target Edition
description:
Spin to discover the joy of everyday life with The Game of Life: Target Edition board game! First, pick a shopping list, filled with items for a specific occasion. Have a list for Pet’s First Birthday? Then head to the Pets Department to get that birthday crown on your list. Players push their red shopping cart movers along the store aisles on the board – in any direction! – to browse Departments and collect all items on their list. Prices vary, so try to nab the cheapest. Along the way, collect Impulse Buy tokens (some things are too good to resist). And draw cards for relatable shopping moments: score items from team members, find deals, and receive texts with shopping curveballs (“R U STILL AT TARGET???? We need toilet paper!!!”). The game ends once everyone reaches Checkout. The player with the most money left wins!

SPIN TO DISCOVER THE JOY OF EVERYDAY LIFE: Ready for a Target Run? Spin the spinner and move that many spaces, gathering everything on your shopping list. Reach Checkout with the most money left to win

8 THEMED TARGET RUNS: Each player’s Target Run shopping list is for a different event, from a Summer Block Party to Family Movie Night. Visit each Department to collect every item on a list

FUNNY FAMILIAR MOMENTS: Draw cards to score deals, get help from team members, receive texts (and shopping requests) from family, and enjoy other relatable moments like “stop and smell ALL the candles”

—description from the publisher

boardgame: The Siblings Trouble: Expanded Deluxe Edition
description:
The Siblings Trouble is a card-driven, cooperative, storytelling game inspired by finding the mysterious places in your backyard. It boils down all the core elements of an RPG (storytelling, exploration, discoveries, treasures, and encounters) into a fun-to-play narrative romp with your family or friends!

Go on an epic 30-minute adventure inspired by childhood imagination.

- Build your Adventure Deck and jump right into the story.
- Explore a mysterious place, find loot, escape hazards, and defeat foes with treasure, tales, and Trouble Dice.
- Play as Danger, Adventure, Mischief, Mayhem, Chance, or an original kid crafted from your own childhood.

This Expanded Deluxe Edition includes the original game plus MUCH MORE!

- Play as Chance, a new character with their own ability and Trouble Die
- Play as Destiny, a new mode that adds a Story Guide with special cards
- Visit 2 brand new locations: The Spooky Old House and Museum of Mystery
- Discover new encounters and bosses for all of the previous locations
- Find awesome new treasures and Epic Treasures on your adventure
- Continue your journey with new expanded adventures between locations

-description from publisher

As a topiary expert you've been tasked with building the perfect tree. Can you construct a tree with a given shape and flower composition to fulfill your clients request?

Arbortect is an 18 card solo only game where each card represents a different section of a tree crown. As you play cards you will need to layer these cards in such a way as to build the tree to a specific size/shape while maintaining a balance of 3 different types of flowers. Complete these requirements and you win!

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
You’re in charge of putting together the best fireworks show.  But no matter what you do you have to get the Grand Finale right. Can you use all the firework cards and end the night with the perfect Grand Finale?

Grand Finale is an 18 card solo only game where you play cards within an 3x3 grid in an attempt to finish the night with a pre-determined display. But be careful, you'll need to play cards so that no two adjacent fireworks are the same size or color. Move played cards up higher in the sky to help fulfill your goal but just make sure your last card played completes your Grand Finale!

This Timeline game is a LEGO employee exclusive to celebrate 90 Years of Play.

Like other Timeline games, LEGO Timeline is a card game  where each card depicts an important moment in Lego history with the year of the moment.

Players take turns placing a card from their hand in a row on the table. After placing the card, the player reveals the date on it. If the card was placed correctly with the date in chronological order with all other cards on the table, the card stays in place; otherwise the card is removed from play and the player takes another card from the deck.

The first player to get rid of all his cards by placing them correctly wins. If multiple players go out in the same round, then everyone else is eliminated from play and each of those players are dealt one more card for another round of play. If only one player has no cards after a bonus round, he wins; otherwise play continues until a single player goes out.

Dive into the sea depths and become the greatest explorer of the underwater world! To find out what the ocean hides, you need to demonstrate perceptiveness and sensational memory! However, you are not the only one to explore underwater mysteries, so good reflexes will also come in handy. Play all the mini-games and earn the title of the best explorer of the sea depths.

5 new cards to add more variety to your game of Lux Aeterna.

Includes 1 new Glitch card (Deadlocked Processor), 2 new main deck cards (Paradox and Beyond the Infinite) and 2 alternative System cards (CAT and JCN)

This expansion adds 1 tile - cut (Kyanite). It works like normal kyanite except that when you acquire collections with it you don't put it into the sack, but instead you put in on the player board, at the scoring phase it's always worth 2 points.

With these 13 new Promo Cards for Hack & Slash, you'll find 9 new quests and 4 new Treasures! Thus giving you more variety and replayability than with the core game and expansion alone.

Contents
9 Quest cards
4 Treasure cards

This expansion adds 1 tile – Ore(Anthracite). It works as a normal anthricite except that when you have it at the end of the game and you have more minerals than anyone else, every mineral is worth one point more.

Alarm in Helsinki! The "Great Coffee Safari" is about to begin! A revolutionary new coffee recipe developed by professor T.C. Papu has been stolen by one of the competitor's industrial spies. The thief has escaped abroad and now it's up to the players to follow, trying to find the stolen recipe and collecting four bags of different varieties of coffee beans from around the world (Medellin, Mysore, Kenia, Santos), all of which are required for the coffee mix. There are also several copies of the recipe around; these are worthless however, but only until the original recipe is found, at which point they become just as good for victory purposes.

Once anyone finds the original coffee recipe, a race back to Helsinki begins, and the player who first returns there with either the original recipe or a copy of it and the required four bags of beans, wins the game.

The game is quite clearly inspired by the most popular Finnish boardgame ever, Afrikan tähti, and plays very much like it, with copies of the recipe taking the place of the horseshoe token at the end of the game, for example. Major differences are the deck of event/trivia cards in this game, the lack of "jewels" or similar of lesser value than the Afrikan Tähti (=coffee recipe), and the small set collection element of acquiring the coffee beans.

This game is likely from the 1980's as it's produced by Nelostuote Ky.

The game is a bootleg imitation of the Japanese manga "Pretty Guardian Sailor Moon" which was extremely successful in Italy. As the box says, a 3-Dimension game! (whatever it means...)

The game is for 2-4 players. 
You are one of the fantastic Sailing Women and you have to fight Malvaghya, the Queen of the Evil Kingdom, and her evil army.

You move across the board using a spin to determine the movement and the weapon you will use against the enemies (monsters as Terybhlya and Lychantropa) then you have to walk on the dangerous elevated road and you will launch your character against Malvaghya herself to dethrone her and to win the game.

—user summary

The game of Uno with a Barbie The Movie theme.

A special “Played With Too Much” rule will have players both discarding and drawing cards.

Game play matches the classic card game where players match colors and numbers to get rid of all their cards.
family: Versions & Editions: Versions with IP after the original release

description:
100 Wacky Things players will do some ridiculous things while laughing uncontrollably!

Always..
..stand up and act like a bird by flapping your arms and whistling.

Whenever..
..someone stands, march up to the person and salute!

One Wacky thing definitely leads to another in this zany game of craziness!

—description from the publisher

To achieve victory on this board of squares, you must overwhelm the center! The object of the game is to locate three of your own tiles in the centre row on a 5x5 grid.

Players take turns to do one of: place a tile, move a tile, or use a tile's special power. The total value of tiles permitted in any row is 8, constraining both players. Tiles are double-sided, and you are not permitted to examine the back of an opponent's tile before a special power flips it.

The first player to deplete their hand is the winner! A card can be discarded only if it matches the card already atop the discard pile (in color or face value), but every card also has an additional effect. Look for chances to exploit unique possibilities as they present themselves! You may even get to play some cards out of turn.

—description from the publisher

In Instant Celebrity, the players are all guessing how the next card revealed from a deck will relate to the previous one. Correct guesses are award points and incorrect guesses grant a loss of points.

Once a player has reached at least 15 points, they have won. The other players continue until a majority of players have won or three of a certain symbol have been revealed from the deck.

boardgame: パチスロシミュレーター (Pachislot Simulator)
description:
Pachislot Simulator is a stochastic deduction game, where players try to determine which of six unidentified stacks of cards has specific makeups.

Each stack of cards has a different population of eight types of cards; one may have an abundance of "grape" cards, and another may have a paucity.

To start the game, the stacks are randomly labeled 1 through 6, and during the game, the players attempt to determine which stack has which makeup.  (The makeups are printed on the back of a player screen.)

The game takes place over four rounds, and in each round, the players have 4 minutes to look through the deck(s) of cards and try to do their deduction.  More specifically, each player has one deck and a time, and can do the following procedure, in real-time. Look at 3 cards from the top of the deck, and note what you saw. Then, shuffle that deck, look at the top 3 cards, take any notes, and repeat.

After each round of searching, players have a limited amount of time to take notes before the next round begins.

The person to correctly identify the most correct correspondences of the numbered decks to their actual composition wins.

In Bungu Squad, two players play an abstract strategy game, alternating turns, and trying to get 5 of their mark in a row.

However, the game also uses a deck of cards, and if a player has certain combination in their hand, they can activate the use of erasers, tape, scissors, and glue (or a stapler or compass with rules on the publisher's website), to manipulate both the marks on the board and the board itself.

In Cookdown, players are high level chefs, put through a gameshow-esque series of tests to be judged by their peers. Whoever wins the most food battles wins! The players are given a hand of different ingredient cards, and two people at a time compete to create a certain type of meal, based on a meal card played for that round.

After choosing three cards from their hand (and accounting for any special rules on the meal card), the competing players reveal their meals, and the discussion begins.

Anyone playing the game can argue for or against either meal, and this discussion session can last as long as people need before all non-competing players have decided who they think is best. All at once, the players vote, and whoever wins gets two points, and whoever loses gets none (in the event of a tie, they each get one).

The game continues as people refill their hands with cards from the deck and the winner of each match competes with the next person clockwise around the circle. Once all the ingredient cards have been used up, the game is over, and whoever has the most points wins.

-description from designer

You're in charge of a Vegas style casino. It's up to you to bring in as much money as you can. In order to do this you'll need to attract visitors to your casino. You'll be able to attract these visitors by building new and exciting amenities, but watch out, your rivals will be doing the same thing! As the casino boss, you'll be able to expand the four essential casino areas: Gambling, Drinking, Entertainment and Dining.

On your turn, you'll choose how to use your buying action. You can improve your casino, pave a parking lot, or select an available special. You'll only be able to select one per turn so you'll want to plan wisely. Then after all have gone, the visitor deck is dealt out and the casino with the most matching symbols brings in that visitor. Income and rewards are collected after the deck is dealt, and then you set up the marketplace for the next round. Sound simple right?

The game starts with the first era in Old Vegas when times were simple and casinos can get by with basic slots and a simple bar. You'll play each era 3 times before moving on to the next one. As the new era is revealed, new options will open for you and your rivals to build. New visitors are also added to the game as it continues. They'll be looking for more sophisticated casinos. You'll need to make sure to build the newest options available to continue to keep your doors open. The game also adds in specials to keep you on your toes and each game different than the last.

After the last turn is played on the final era all the money is counted up and the casino who raked in the most is proclaimed the winner!

Do you have what it takes to be King of the Casinos?

-description from designer

boardgame: Hey Hey it's Saturday: The Board Game!
description:
The game is a traditional roll and move (single die) and when players land on key squares (e.g. Hey Hey, Red Faces, Phunny Foto, Molly's Hat, Plucka Duck, Celebrity Stars, Dikies Van), actions are performed.  The overall aim is to collect 6 Silver Dongers which can then be traded for a Gold Donger.  To win the game a player must be the first player to reach the finish square with a Gold Donger with an exact throw.

The key squares have the following conditions:

1) Hey Hey Cards - Take a Hey Hey Card and do exactly as it says - some my say steal a Silver Donger from another player

2) Red Faces Cards - To a card and either a) perform the impersonation or mine, b) take the dare.  If successful, they receive a Silver Donger

3) Phunny Foto Cards - When a player collects two half of the same phunny photo they can exchange for a Silver Donger

4) Molly's Hat - if land on this square get a gold record - 
three gold records can be traded for a SIlver Donger. A Gold Record can also be traded with another player for a Phunny Foto

5)Plucka Duck - take a special path and if you land on Play Plucka Duck, do what is on the wheel

6) Celebrity Star - if a character lands on the star of the playing piece character, they get a Silver Donger

7) Dickie's Van - a player only takes the Dickie's Van path if they land on the appropriate square.

From back of game box, the game is described as a "Hilarious Fun for the Whole Family!

"Become your favourite "Hey Hey" character in a game of T.V. hijinks that pits you against your fellow cast members in a fun filled frollick through some of Hey Hey's zaniest segments! Enter Red Faces to impersonate a man wrestling a crocodile or maybe try belly dancing for a dare! Collect the pieces to make your very own phunny foto or try your luck on Plucka's wheel receiving prizes on the way, all in the race for T.V's ultimate accolade - the gold donger!"

Two discovery tiles to be used for Beacon Patrol.

Artwork includes one tile featuring a section of sea with dolphins, and another that features a ranger station with a windsock on an elevated platform on land.

The top right-hand corner of each tile is marked with a red arrow to indicate that it is a promotional tile.

In this game you play as an older sibling and your goal is to summon younger sisters.

In each turn players' draw new cards from the deck, burn some to get IP(Imouto Power), and place other cards with special powers.

First player to get to a certain number of imouto wins.

US history board game. Use your knowledge about America to claim the most states. Play tactically and steal oponents states. Will you be lucky and win two states with the correct answer includes game board 400 game cards 8 categories 2 spinners instructions and counters

boardgame: Kingdom Death: Monster – Stonesmasher Promo Card
description:
The Stonesmasher Gear card is a promo card that was included with the Gnostin Stonesmasher miniature, which itself is not part of the game.

The Gear card comes with a Glossary card to explain its rules. It reads:

Stonesmasher (Savage, Demolish, Irreplaceable): This gains +4 luck when attacking a knocked down monster.

Lagomorphs steal the poorly guarded eggs appearing in nests around their settlement. They return to the settlement and feast on the glowing green yolks before the ornate shells become too tough to break. The raw eggs are easily gained but rarely sit well in the survivors' digestive tract. To prepare for the blood rites, survivors will spend a lantern year incubating the eggs with their body's warmth, until the iron-hard shells crack, disgorging the helpless downy creatures they consume for their preparatory feasts. When it came time for Lolowen to incubate her egg, she lacked the patience to produce a sacrifice for the feast. Lolowen kit includes a painters scale model perfect for hobbyists and a gamers scale model for gamers looking to include Lolowen's story in their campaign.

Lolowen is packaged in a graphic box that includes:

1 x Hard plastic Lolowen miniature
1 x Hard plastic Kingdom Death 30mm base and scenic insert
1 x Large matte art print
1 x Seed Pattern card
1 x Seed Pattern Gear card
2 x Glossary cards
1 x Install guide for playing Lolowen in your Monster campaign

boardgame: らくらく冒険者の幸せ異世界ライフ (Rakuraku Boukensha no Shiawase Isekai Life)
description:
Being reincarnated into another world with OP abilities and fighting monsters is a cliche, in this game your goal is to have the best time with your partner.

Each game lasts 12 rounds, divided into 4 seasons. One round is one month. In each round you either choose a task from a variety that guild offers or build your base. Once per season there is also a special seasonal event.

Winner is the player who after 12 rounds has the most victory points.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

boardgame: 新幕 桜降る代に決闘を 新劇拡張：神座桜緣起前篇 (A Duel for Falling Cherry Blossoms: New Act – 8th Expansion)
description:
"New Drama Expansion: Kanza Sakura Origin Part 1" is an extension of the "Duel in the Age of Cherry Blossoms" series. A new story inspired by the movie version. The first part of the story depicts an old mystery and the origin of the Kanza Sakura , which is challenged by Renri, who drifted back in time to the Warring States period.

"New Drama Expansion" is a large-scale set in a new form, and includes 2 new Megami and 6 Megami Challenge series, which is a special battle mode.

abacus Megami Akina is the personification of economic games. She invests her capital, and withdraws it in due time, and cunningly collects her profits.
Saw Megami Shisui goes through a harsh thorny road. Give both of them lacerations that open with a time delay, and then screw the blow into the gap with a blow that converts pain into power.

In the Megami challenge, the product versions of Korunu, Hagane, Yukihi, Hatsumi, Himika, and Utsuro.

Contents
Cards: 42, Megami Tarot: 2, Special Board: 1, Special Token: 11
1 rulebook, 2 papers, 2 card folders, 1 card update pack

桜降る代の動乱は決着したかのように見えた。しかし或る来訪が旧き謎の紐を解く。それは時を遡り、戦国の時代へと流れついた夜山恋離の物語 ―― そしてその果に潜む神座桜の縁起だった。旧き天秤を担いし算盤のメガミ「アキナ」とかつて桜に挑みし鋸のメガミ「シスイ」が参戦。本作には彼女たちが持つすべてのカードが収録されているぞ。

『新劇拡張：神座桜縁起 前篇』は『桜降る代に決闘を』シリーズの拡張です。劇場版をイメージした新たな物語。その前篇――時を遡り戦国の時代へと流れ着いた夜山恋離の挑む、旧き謎と神座桜の縁起が描かれます。

『新劇拡張』は新しい形態での大型セットであり、２柱の新たなメガミと特殊な対戦モードであるメガミへの挑戦シリーズ６作が収録されています。
算盤のメガミ・アキナは経済ゲームの体現者です。資本を投資、そして然るべき時に回収し、狡猾に利益を取り立てましょう。
鋸のメガミ・シスイは過酷なる茨道を進みます。両者に時間差で開く裂傷を与え、痛みを力に変えた一撃を間隙へとねじ込むのです。

メガミへの挑戦ではコルヌ、ハガネ、ユキヒ、ハツミ、ヒミカの製品版、そして本作限定収録のウツロが参戦します。クレイジーな原初札と新たな挑戦をお楽しみください。

内容物
カード：42枚、メガミタロット：2枚、特殊ボード：1枚、特殊トークン：11枚
ルールブック：1冊、ペーパー類：2枚、カードフォルダ：2個、カード更新パック：1個

We know you've been dying for another hearse-load of Undead. This 30-card set has more monsters, more Treasures, and more postmortem fun for any Munchkin game. We couldn't keep it buried any longer!

This is an expansion for Munchkin. It is not a stand-alone game. These cards originally appeared in Munchkin: Unicorns and Friends.

-description from publisher's website

This is a small expansion card set that depicts the aftermath of the main story of Shiawase no Ito.
This set includes cards for the five original characters created in the first version of the project, as well as a new scenario as a bonus.

Earth’s future is in your hands, but will you choose to save it?
In Climate Change The Game, you’re the bad guy. Each player in the game owns a major global energy corporation. Your goal is to crush your competitors by securing the biggest share of the Earth’s precious natural resources and supply energy to the world’s great cities. But beware, as fossil fuel emissions get pumped into the atmosphere, the Earth will be battered by floods, famine and fires, and people will point the finger of blame at you!

Strategy game with Euro elements

Some Key Elements of Gameplay:
As the CEO of a fossil fuel corporation, your primary goal is to maximise shareholder profits. 
Whether the world ends in flood and fires, or whether fossil fuels are banned to usher in a clean, green economy, the player with the highest stock price wins.

ROLL
At the beginning of your turn, roll the two 6-sided dice. Every player with a power plant bordering a region that matches the rolled number picks up a corresponding resource card. As the roller, if you didn’t get a match, you still get a consolation resource card. After everyone has rolled once, turns continue until someone rolls a 6, which triggers a Climate Summit and unleashes the number of Disasters determined by the Global Carbon Footprint.

TRADE
On your turn, you may  initiate trades with other players.

WORLD NEWS EVENTS
You can play 1 World News card per turn. These include the joys and horrors of the 21st century: Brexit, Bolsonaro, Trump Tweets. It's all there!

BUILD YOUR NETWORK 
Expand your network by building pipelines and power stations. Each action has its associated costs which you pay by discarding resource cards.

CLIMATE SUMMIT
At the end of each round, all players get on their private jets and fly to a secluded resort in the Swiss Alps to talk about what they will do to avert climate disaster. The player who bids the most resources becomes the “Sustainability Tsar” for the next round, moves one GreenPiece to anywhere on the board to block resource extraction, increases the Global Public Opinion meter by one step and in the next round wears the Sustainability Tsar and goes first.

—description from the designer

This third expansion for Napoleon Saga gives you the chance to completely revamp your Strategy Decks. You can implement new tactics for the five armies already available and also surprise your opponents on new terrains to achieve new Objectives. This expansion is updated with erratas and modifications developed with the help of the Napoleon Saga player community.

description:
There have been vicious falsehoods circulating, such as "Hoshou Marin is in her 30s." This is truly deplorable.
The captain would never lie about his age!
Look at her profile on the official website, it says she is "roughly 17 years old!"
What is needed to counteract the hoax and correct the perceptions of the Internet community is good assertiveness.
Let us, the members gathered here today, compete in our assertions and improve our skills for the coming day.
Now let's get to the question, Hosho Marin is 17 years old!

The numbers 0 to 5 and 8 to 10 appear, but 0, 5, and 10 have different reverse patterns, so the player deduces his or her hand by using that as a clue.
Since the same numbers can be treated as one card, the sexy structure of the game increases both risk and chance at the same time as the number of cards in the hand increases.
If you are the only one left in the hand, you have a chance to score a lot of points, and so on!

Bitaak is a climbing game for 3 players, where you bid to face-off against a team of two to see who will shed out first. 
It is in the same family as Dou Dizhu (Fight the Landlord) and Big Three.
It uses easier to remember meld combos and a non-standard deck with 3 jokers.
The game name comes from a slang expression used in Bangalore Kannada. Bitaak means Let it go.

Two contenders for the Imperial Seat meet at a teahouse to ‘discuss’, both are ready to ambush the other.

It is a small tactical and bluffing card game for 2 players. Each player tries to protect their own emperor whilst simultaneously trying to find and kill the opponent.

—description from the designer

Answer your own questions correctly to earn points. Collect strong points by predicting how many questions your opponents will successfully answer.

Each card has a category known in advance and 5 questions relating to that category.

Use your luck (dice) and programming ability to
get the first treasure fish and take it home!

Nyaice! CODE is a programing style board game that uses dice to program the actions of cats.

Tie the flowers together with a ribbon to create a beautiful bouquet.

You can play 4 types of games using cards with 7 types of flowers and ribbons drawn in beautiful watercolors.

"My Bouquet"
The goal is to arrange the limited 8 cards well and create more and more valuable bouquets.

"Everyone's Bouquet"
Arrange the cards together and make a bouquet.
We aim to contribute to the creation of bouquets by sometimes carpooling, sometimes interfering, and sometimes rushing out of the way.

"Bouquet loop"
Make a bouquet using 8 cards. However, the ribbon must be looped!
Make effective use of special actions (reset tableau piles, rearrange).

"Bouquet Order"
While presenting the flowers ordered by the player next to you, leave the flowers for yourself well to complete a good bouquet.

—description from the publisher (translated)

リボンで花を束ねて、素敵な花束をつくりましょう。

7種類の花とリボンがきれいな水彩で描かれたカードを使って、4種類のゲームが遊べます。

「わたしの花束」
限られた８枚のカードを上手く並べていき、より価値の高い花束を、より多く作ることを目指します。

「みんなの花束」
全員でカードを並べ、花束をつくっていきます。
時には相乗り、時には邪魔し、時には抜け駆けして、花束作りに最も貢献することを目指します。

「花束ループ」
8枚のカードを使って花束をつくります。ただし、リボンはループになっていないとダメ！
特殊アクション（場札リセット、置き直し）も効果的に使いましょう。

「花束オーダー」
隣のプレイヤーに注文された花を差し出しつつ、自分用の花をうまく残して、良い花束を完成させましょう。

—description from the publisher
publishers: iop games (イオピーゲームズ)

description: Draw honey pieces from the board to complete sets of 8. The player who gets three sets of 8 honey pieces first wins. If you draw honey equaling 8 you can keep the honey, but if you go over, you lose it.

boardgame: とれにゃーはんたー (Treanyure Hunter)
description:
Cat hunters who search for treasure every day. The site this time is in a mountain where fossils have slept... We have to collect fossils before they are taken by other cats! I want you to lend me your strength as a new member!
Aiming for treasure, the hunters are now on the move!

"Torenya Hanta" is a treasure hunt game where you collect scattered fossils from a random map. Be the first to collect 3 fossils!

—description from the publisher (translated)

日々お宝(とれにゃー)を探すねこハンターたち。今回の現場は化石が眠った山の中…他の猫にとられる前に、化石を集めねば！新たな隊員として君も力を貸してほしい！
お宝を目指して、今、ハンターたちが動き出す！

『とれにゃーはんたー』は、ランダムマップの中から、バラバラになった化石を集めるお宝探しゲームです。誰よりも早く、化石を三つ揃えよう！

—description from the publisher
publishers: 合同会社 スタジオつくし(STUDIO294 LLC.)

description:
Desert Generals is a medium-complexity operational level game of the Desert Campaign, from 1940 to 1942.The game  captures the ebb and flow of the North African Campaign from late 1940 to November 1942 with a unique emphasis on leadership and command. The game also recreates critical supply constraints. A player refighting the Desert Campaign, like his real-life counterparts, cannot fight continuously – he launches an offensive, pauses to build up supply, then resumes his offensive…unless his opponent has preempted him and launched his own offensive first. As in real life, offensives tend to run out of gas (literally) as they approach the enemy’s home base.

Historical elements include air support, minefields, engineers, artillery, anti-tank guns, Axis fuel shortages, and Allied command and control problems. From Operation Compass to Second Alamein, Desert Generals covers the Desert War from start to finish.

—description from the designer

This game is based on the 2005 Doctor Who episode Dalek.  A damaged Dalek is being held in a secret underground complex in Utah where the Doctor and Rose find it.  The Dalek absorbs Roses’ Time Energy which heals the Dalek and then escapes its cell and attempts to get to the surface.  The game represents the escape of the Dalek with three squads of Guards trying to stop it.

You will need a pack of 52 playing cards with two jokers, one Dalek miniature and three Guard miniatures to represent the Guard squads.

The game is set up by placing cards on a table in a 5 by 5 grid pattern to make a playing area, with the playing pieces moving about on them by playing cards.

—description from the designer

description: Dice chess is similar to chess but uses dice instead of standard pieces. Each die face has a set of legal moves, like chess pieces, but each player has 11 dice on a 7x7 board. Dice can either be moved or changed, where two adjacent dice add their numbers and redistribute them. The 1 pip acts as the king.

DCR (Dirty Cheat Racing) is a game with the theme of a machine race over the universe.
If you run the course and reach the goal, you will get points, and the person with the most points at the end of the 3 rounds will win.
However, even if it's a race, you don't need machine driving techniques to win.
All it takes is a little bit of luck, a sneaky cheat, and a blast of fucking guts!

—description from the publisher (translated)

『DCR（Dirty Cheat Racing）』は大宇宙をめぐるマシンレースをテーマにしたゲームです。
コースを走ってゴールするとポイントゲット、３ラウンド終了時に最も獲得ポイントが多い人が優勝します。
ただしレースと言っても、勝つためにはマシン運転のテクニックなんていりません。
必要なのは、ちょっぴりの運とこっそりのチート行為、そして圧倒的なクソ度胸です！

—description from the publisher

Tiny Troy is a 1-2 player card game in which players cooperate to get the Trojan Horse into the city of Troy.
Use Drachma Tokens to buy different cards to keep Soldier Tokens under control, and use special combinations of cards to get better results.
To win, the player/s need to make it through a certain amount of nights inside the Trojan Horse, counted by the Darkness Deck, from which cards good and bad might appear.
 
     Draft and play different cards - There are a variety of cards available to buy and use in strategic ways.
     Manage the mayhem of a grumpy army - Think the opposite of worker placement: worker removal!
     Ultra-fast turns - Each turn takes only a few seconds, resulting in quickfire gameplay.


The worker placement game where you need to REMOVE workers!

—description from the designer

In the two-player strategy game, Children of the Sun, players take control of the mighty dragon children of Ino'itl, the Sun god. The two were sent to Earth in order to create a beautiful landscape as an ode of love to Xochanama, the Moon goddess forever fated to be out of Ino'itl's reach.

During the game, players must summon forth the elements in order to craft their magnificent landscapes. On each turn, players will draw and place an Elemental Token onto a 6x6 game board, flanking opponent pieces and flipping them over. For every opponent token flanked, along with the token placed, the player gains corresponding Elemental Resources. Earth, Water, Life, Void, and Spirit.

Resources are used to craft Creation Cards, which represent the fantastic landscape being formed by the players. Creation Cards come in five different types:

- Terra Cards grant points to the player who creates the most expansive landscape.
- Aqua Cards score based on the difference in value from one card to the next.
- Species Cards score higher the more diverse a player’s ecosystem becomes.
- Source Cards generate extra resources and score for each corresponding Creation Card made.
- Wonder Cards award points for each token flipped to the player’s side at the end of the game.

Players must also balance the interconnected ecosystem between their cards. Enough Terra must be generated to support Aquatic creations, or else they are discarded at the end of the game. Aqua is needed to keep Species alive, with each type requiring different amounts of water. If a Habitat icon on a Species card matches one shown on a Terra card, players gain valuable bonus points.

Along the way, players can acquire and use Dragon Powers to gain a one up on their opponent. These abilities include actions like creating additional cards on your turn, flipping tokens on the board, increasing your hand size, and even discarding an opponent's Creation Card. And once per game, each player may summon the aid of the Sun god and Moon goddess to recharge their powers or redraw a new hand of Creation Cards.

The winner of Children of the Sun is the player who crafts the most captivating landscape, pleasing the Moon goddess and securing favor from the Sun god!

In Várzea, the players of an amateur soccer team must deal with the attacks of the opposing team, the irregularities of the field and a cow.

On each turn the active team may have up to 3 flicks on the ball. Before each flick, the opposing team can move the cow to one of the other black dots on the field. The team must flick the ball at least one time before a goal. If the flick aims to score a goal, the team must announce to the opponent team. After the announcement and before the flick, the opponent team can replace the goalkeeper. The shift ends if the team flicks the ball 3 times, if the team throws the ball out of bounds or if the team scores a goal. The game ends as soon as one of the teams scores 10 goals and becomes champion!

—description from the designer

Rando au pic des neiges is a team racing game with a question-and-answer session on the theme of mountain hiking.

Rando au Pic des Neiges is a team-based mountain hike (except for the 2-person version) set on a game board. Players must first acquire suitable equipment and favorable weather conditions, then set off from the hamlet to reach the summit of Pic des Neiges.

They advance with the help of dice, and along the way must answer numerous questions on the themes of nature, respect for the environment, knowledge of the mountain environment and, of course, safety (in the medium and high mountains). Correct answers will enable them to gain ground, but they will also have to choose their route strategically and use their yeti map skilfully.

Finally, they'll need to respect the mountains and show team spirit if they're to win by being the first team back to the hamlet after reaching the Pic des Neiges.

—description from the designer (translated)

boardgame: Star Saga: The Fear in the Darkness
description:
As the dust settles on planet Eiras, the survivors of Blaine's team return to a meeting with Blaine aboard his starship, 'Dictator'. In their possession is something of exceptional value, an item sure to fetch a great price from some of the most wealthy and powerful beings in the galaxy. However, the Council of Seven are not the only beings with an interest in what was taken from Eiras.

Emerging from mist-shrouded worlds on the edge of corporate space, ‘The Nameless’ are shuffling, rolling, and oozing forth. From tentacled swarms of octopedal minions to titanic ‘ogres’ with thick, crab-like shells and man-crushing claws, there is no end to the horrors of the Nameless horde.

Terrible events in their own planetary system and the very real possibility of mass extinction have led some Nameless factions to resort to extreme measures in order to survive. Seemingly random incursions into GCPS space and attacks on its facilities are all designed to find a means of salvation for their race.

Now, following a mysterious tip off that revealed the location of Blaine and his prized cargo from Eiras, a strike team of tentacled creatures attacks Blaine’s hideout, forcing Blaine himself to fight for his life. Faced with the ruthless onslaught of the frenzied Nameless, can Blaine and his mercenary crew survive their toughest challenge yet?

—description from the publisher

boardgame: 梨卦如理科子の直鎖アルカン教室 (Straight-chain alkane classroom of Rika Nyurikako)
description:
梨卦如理科子の直鎖アルカン教室 (Straight-chain alkane classroom of Rika Nyurikako) is a game where players will try to build simple molecules.
In turn order, each player draws an atom card from the deck and use it to create a new molecule (if they don't have one already) or to expand it. When adding an atom to a molecule, the direction of the chemical bond indicated on the card must be followed (cards can not be flipped sideways). A molecule must be completed to start a new one and score points. Any player creating a peroxide bond (-O-O-) is eliminated from the game. Drawing a 13C isotope allows to start a new molecule even if the previous one is still unfinished. Molecules containing halogen and/or carbon atoms are worth more points.
Once the atom card deck is depleted, victory goes to the player who has tallied the most points.

—user summary

まっすぐつないだ炭素原子を骨格に、分子を完成させていきます。何やら難しそうな雰囲気ですが、カードの手を直観的につなげばOK！
大きな分子が完成する達成感が堪りません。
ただし酸素が2つ並んだ-O-O-ペルオキシドには注意して！危険な物質なので、即ゲームオーバーです

—description from the publisher

A molecule is completed with straight carbon atoms as a skeleton. It looks like it's going to be difficult, but if you intuitively connect the hands of the cards, you'll be fine!
I can't stand the sense of accomplishment when a large molecule is completed.
But watch out for -O-O-peroxides with two oxygens in a row! It's a dangerous substance, so it's game over immediately.

—description from the publisher (translated)

In Carbon Lean, you represent a citizen, having the capacity to “consume” different products, goods and services. Each of them has a “carbon cost” in CO2 emissions – calculated in Kilos Equivalent CO2 (kgCO2) according to official and referenced figures. Your objective is to reduce your “total carbon footprint” by choosing products, goods and services that consume the least compared to other players!

The Carbon Lean game can be used in different variants, of increasing difficulty (1, 2 and 3), thus allowing the whole family to play. It also allows you to estimate your own carbon footprint. With its 3 variants, you can play with your children (V1-V2), or race between teenagers and adults (V3).
The publisher advises playing variants 1 and 2 at least once, to better discover the cards and their “cost” in “kilograms CO2 equivalent”, before starting many games of variant 3.

—description from the publisher

In 有機大富豪 (Organic President), players use the reactant cards in their hand to move from one molecule to another on the reaction map provided. The first player who depletes their card hand wins.

Different molecules can be present on the reaction map and they can sometimes react together.
Explosive molecules can be created. They will set on fire all other molecules present on the reaction, unless other players use CO2 cards. Whoever manages to extinguish the fire becomes the next player, otherwise the player who started the fire gets another turn.

—user summary

有機大富豪は、有機化学のさまざまな反応を身につけられるカードゲームです！理系高校生はもちろん、大学生や文系でも楽しめるゲーム性がオススメポイント！ 
有機大富豪では、各プレイヤーが手札を使って反応を起こし、化学変化をまとめたマップ(反応経路図)の上を動き回ることでゲームが進行します。すなわち、ゲームをやればやるほど有機化合物の反応が身についていくのです！！ 勉強は疲れたけど、遊ぶわけにもいかない……そんなマジメなあなたにこそ、有機大富豪をおすすめします！

—description from the publisher

Organic President is a card game where you can learn various reactions of organic chemistry! The recommended point is the gameplay that can be enjoyed not only by science high school students, but also by university students and humanities!
In Organic President, each player uses his or her hand to cause a reaction, and the game progresses by moving around on a map (reaction path diagram) that summarizes chemical changes. In other words, the more you play the game, the more you learn about the reactions of organic compounds! ! I'm tired of studying, but I can't even play... For those of you who are serious, I recommend Organic President!

—description from the publisher (translated)

boardgame: ディノスクラフト (Dinos Craft)
description:
Players explore an area of ​​4 squares x 4 squares.
During exploration, you can find materials such as various dinosaurs and ores.
Earn victory points by befriending dinosaurs and creating bases using collected materials!

—description from the publisher (translated)

プレイヤーは4マス×4マスのエリアを探索します。
探索では、様々な恐竜や鉱石などの素材を見つけることができます。
恐竜を仲間にしたり、集めた素材を使って拠点を作成したりして、勝利点を獲得しましょう!!

—description from the publisher
designers: あんきろわーくす (Ankilo Works)
publishers: あんきろわーくす (Ankilo Works)

description:
Toss the dwarf chip, harvest mushrooms, and build buildings to earn points. Players can build depending on colour and number of mushrooms. So watch the building cards on the field and the forest conditions for mushrooms to be harvested. The player who has the highest total of 10 points on his or her building cards wins the game. However, beware of poisonous mushrooms, which give players negative points when harvested.

—description from the publisher

A 2-3 player push-your-luck adventure card game set in a hostile, post-apocalyptic badland. Players are simultaneously rivals and reluctant allies.

Players reveal Encounters, reading them out loud and choosing how to overcome them. Once a path is chosen, all players participate together, drawing from the Resource deck in an attempt to gather the most cards into their Packs, and finding useful equipment and collections along the way.Victory Points are awarded for the quantity of Resource cards, with extra points awarded from special cards collected and equipped, and a bonus unique to each Character.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

boardgame: Carbo 覇道の王権 (Carbo Dominant Throne)
description:
It's a fighting game.
Secure territory, increase the number of people, and fight using various items obtained in forests and waterfronts.
Stack pieces to increase your battle score and defeat your opponent.

—description from the publisher (translated)

対戦型ゲームです。
領地を確保し、民を増やし、森や水辺で手に入れた各種アイテムを用いて戦います。
駒を重ねて戦闘点数を増やし、相手を撃破しよう。

—description from the publisher

BORD is a two-player game of area control and dice battles between viking armies. BORD is also a viking word that meant "the side of a ship" (as in "starboard") or "a plank of wood" (literally a board, as in "board games"). BORD distills the wargame experience into a short, yet strategic game that takes less than an hour to play.

BORD is played on four hex boards. These hex boards have a grid of half-hexes (shores) and whole-hexes (high ground or low ground), and they are used to set up one or more islands (different set-ups for different games). The eight objectives — grassland, marsh, woodland, and stronghold — are then placed on those islands. Each objective has two sides: light or dark. One player is light, and the other is dark. You control an objective when your side (light or dark) is face up. To take control of an objective, you must have the most armies adjacent to it at the end of a turn, after which you flip it so that your side (light or dark) is face up. You score victory points at the end of each season (summer and winter) for each objective that you control. At the end of the second season (winter), the player who has the most victory points wins.

Each of your armies has an objective shown on it: grassland, marsh, woodland, or stronghold. If your army is adjacent to that objective, you get the bonus shown on that army: dice, action points, or victory points. Dice are used in battle. Action points are used to land, move, and battle. Victory points are used to win the game. You can choose to settle (discard) an army to earn a victory point. (This represents a viking becoming a farmer.) You get a new army at the beginning of your turn, but only if you have fewer than six armies. Thus, settling an army when you have only six will get you a new army next turn, but you might leave an objective unguarded in the meantime...

Battles are won by rolling dice (one die for each army on, or adjacent to, the battleground). You roll additional dice if those armies are on high ground or adjacent to their stronghold. If the attacker gets the highest roll, the defender flees to sea and cannot land again until the next season, but still counts as one of that player's six armies. If the attacker does not get the highest roll, nothing happens, so the risk of attacking is that you might waste your turn. To win the game, you might not need to battle at all, but winning the right battle at the right time might be the difference between winning and losing.

—description from the designer

A flip-and-score game where you try to find and collect as many wondrous beetles as possible. Players play pattern cards to dig up the dirt hiding these beautiful beasts of the soil. Pay attention though! Beetles move and aren't always where you expect them to be.

—description from the designer

Lay out the beetle cards in a 9x9 grid. Flip over four of them to start. Pick up one of the cards from the draw deck and score the beetles down a row matching that cards layout. Flip over all  cards in that row and move another row randomly. Try to get the most points on your score card to win!

—user summary

boardgame: It's Lit! Broadview Literature Card Game
description: This card game is a promotional item published by Broadview Press. It re-implements the card game Timeline, asking players to order 52 literature and philosophy titles published by Broadview Press, an academic book publisher. The cards show the covers of 52 classic works that belong to the English-language literary canon at large, from Antiquity to the mid-20th century, and are mostly comprised of classic books originally published in English. The gameplay is in every way identical to Timeline.

Welcome to Million Data_ Baby, where you will dive into the ruthless universe of startups ready to do anything to enrich themselves thanks to the personal data of their users.

Here, ethics is relegated to the background, the profitability of the personal data business being the absolute priority.

Competition is fierce and low blows are common in this frantic race to dominate the market and become the biggest data provider!

Each of your applications has a boycott level that limits the collection and resale of personal data. Without mining this data, you will never be able to accumulate valuable crypto-bifs to enrich yourself.

Million Data_ Baby offers many ways to accelerate your growth, whether through hacks, espionage, negotiations or even legal manipulation. However, beware of your competitors, as they will do anything to prevent you from thriving, whether by pursuing justice, rallying whistleblowers, activists, or resorting to piracy.

The road to glory may be strewn with pitfalls, but you are determined to engrave your name in the pantheon of the greatest data traffickers of this world.

So get ready to dive into a ruthless world where data is king, and show the world what you're made of!

All Million Data_ Baby cards, without exception, were created from news articles and public studies. And the worst part is that you have access to all the sources. If you want to feel disillusioned, feel free to wander around our Million Data_ Baby site.

—description from the publisher

Descend into the Beast's undersea world with Kraken Rum.

Deal out the deck of cards and then each player's goal is to collect all the cards and be declared the winner.

The current player will declare one attribute on his card and compare to the rest, the player with the highest wins and takes all the cards and is the current player for the next round. Last player standing is the winner and gets to drink their favourite cocktail.

Basically top trumps gameplay on some nice art cards produced by Kraken Rum as a limited edition bonus gift with their rum.

Clash of Corgis is a card game with three unique game modes.

Clash of Corgis - Original is a 2-4p card game where players aim to be the first player to get rid of all of their cards in their hand.

Clash of Corgis - Treasure Island is a 2-4p trick-taking card game where players aim to be the first player to gain 10 loot.

Clash of Corgis - Capture the Illusionist is a 2p card game where players take alternative turns in this luck, memory, and deductive game, revealing opponent's cards in attempts to capture the opponent's Illusionist twice.

- 55 cards, each with a unique artwork

- 2-4 players

- 10-15 minute playtime

- Ages 13+

- 3 unique ways to play

—description from the designer

In Train on Tracks, player tries to lay railway tiles to connect coal mines to the power station, then trains depart each round. The goal is to create efficient railway layout so that returning empty trains don't block full ones.
The player assumes a role of a railway track company in the age of steam with goal to build railways. 
The innovative laying system giving player freedom to create any tracks with almost any intersections.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Filòn: Al plu guz da Livign
description:
Filòn in Livigno dialect means 'shrewd, cunning individual'.

To come out of adversity, to face hardship and turn it to one's advantage, one has to be shrewd: Livigno is precisely the fruit of technique, ingenuity, entrepreneurial spirit, a nose for business and strategies.

Filòn's goal is to stay in the game without failing. The last one left in the game is the winner! In order to win, you will have to be able to haggle, make the right investments, bankrupt your opponents and prove that you are 'Al pluz guz da Livign' - the shrewdest in Livigno. Be wise in using the 'Oscsgiòn', opportunity, at your disposal and the dynamics of the game!

It all begins when...
While organizing our move, we found a lost box, forgotten a long time ago. Upon opening it, we discovered many photographs. We quickly called everyone to come and see the photos and remember each moment that was captured.
Do we have the same memories of those moments? What feelings do we perceive when we look at them?
We sat in a circle and pulled out the first photo...

Players: 2+

How to play:

The game is played in a series of levels, and players win by reaching level 5. The game begins with two revealed pictures, and one player must choose a feeling card and secret selects a picture that he/she thinks best relates to the feeling, then the other players have to vote on which of the open pictures corresponds to the chosen feeling.

Then they must choose a single voting token that indicates how many spaces the pawn will move on the track, depending on whether the guess is correct or incorrect. Then a new picture is opened, and the next player to the left must remove the chosen picture and reveal a new one, and they must open a new feeling card so that there are always 4 cards available.

If the voted picture is the one chosen by the player, the level marker moves to the right the number of spaces indicated on the voting token. If the guess is incorrect, the marker moves to the left.

If the level marker reaches 0, everyone loses immediately, regardless of the level (it doesn't go back to the previous level). When the picture is chosen correctly, the marker moves to the right the number of spaces indicated on the voting token. If the marker reaches the star, the next level is reached. The marker goes back to space 5, and an additional picture is revealed. There are 3 pictures at level 3, 4 pictures at level 4, and 5 pictures at level 5. To win, you need to reach the star at level 5.

Adrian is a Romanian kid who travelled into The New World together with his father who found job as undertaker.

In 19th century there was a lot of work out there and it was easy to find richness with that specific job. Adrian loved the life in the New World, one of his best hobbies was to jump on the various tombs and create funny games with a Wooden Stake.

One night He was still awake when his father went to sleep, a lightning bug entered in his room and He wanted to catch it. The lightning bug escaped but finally Adrian was able to catch it jumping inside one of his fathers’ tombs, when suddenly Billygoat appears.

Compatible with "The Faceless" & "The Faceless - Amusement Park"

—description from the publisher

In the jungle, everyone knows who the king is, and none of the animals dares to challenge the mighty lion. But on a magical night, 6 amulets were created. Now the kingdom can belong to anyone!

Objective: Win consecutive battles until reaching 12 points, challenging opponents with "The Kingdom is Mine!" in each battle.

There are six different types of animals, each one stronger than the previous one. All animals have 4 levels. So a level 3 snake defeats a level 2 snake but loses to a level 1 lion.

In each round, a new amulet will be opened indicating which animal will be the trump of the round. This animal will win against any other, regardless of their ranking. The round is worth as many points as the number of players who decide to compete in it, with an additional point if all players participate. The winner is the first player to reach 20 points.

boardgame: Rad Zone Totality: Science Lab Expansion
description:
This is the first expansion of Rad Zone Totality and is designed to be used in conjunction with the core rules. It includes the search matrix for the Science Laboratory and the building map. All the search matrix references for the map are found below, so this is a totally new location and map for your survivors.
The Science Lab (SCL) search matrices are listed first. Search modifiers do not apply to all tables in the matrix: guidance is provided in the title of the table. Some of the tables are designed to increase the random nature of searching so may point you to another table. At the back of the book are the maps. They included large and smaller formats; on half pages to be stuck together and on a full page giving two print options. Because this is a digital product these pages can be printed individually.

Situation

You have discovered a large building which appears to be a science centre or laboratory. There could be useful loot inside, but you can see from the outside that there has already been some looting and it looks like there may even be people living within the structure. However, there could potentially be medical supplies and handy equipment for the bunker.
You may now select the Science Laboratory when out on mission!

boardgame: Rad Zone Totality: Exhibition Centre Expansion
description:
This is the second expansion of Rad Zone Totality and is designed to be used in conjunction with the core rules. It includes the search matrix for the Exhibition Centre and the building map. All the search matrix references for the map are found below, so this is a totally new location and map for your survivors.

The Exhibition Centre (EXC) search matrices are listed first. Search modifiers do not apply to all tables in the matrix: guidance is provided in the title of the table. Some of the tables are designed to increase the random nature of searching so may point you to another table. At the back of the book are the maps. Because this is a digital product these pages can be printed individually.

UkGamesExpo

This Rad Zone Totality expansion was released to coincide with our first appearance at the 2023 UKGamesExpo in Birmingham, England. It has been designed in homage to this awesome event, although the map provided here is nowhere near the size and scale of the NEC.
Situation

On the outskirts of the industrial sector of the city, you have discovered a large building with a sign at the front reading “Exhibition Centre”. There is a rumour that this place was once used as a base for a small bandit group, but they joined the Quarantine Denizen and haven’t been seen since. You suspect there could be useful loot inside, including Equipment and Raw Materials. There is a huge sun symbol, with a V in the centre, painted on the outside (the symbol of the Quarantine Denizen) and you wonder if this has put off potential looters. You might be in luck today!

You may now select the Exhibition Centre when out on a mission! Refer to the tables in this expansion when exploring the building.

—description from the designer

Choose one card from your hand and place it face down in front of you, revealing all the cards at the same time.
The revealed card determines whether it becomes a meteor or is placed.
The player with the fewest meteors at the end of the game wins.

—description from the publisher (translated)

手札から1枚を選んで自分の前に裏面に置き、すべて同時にカードを公開します。
公開したカードによって流星になるか配置するかが決まります。
ゲーム終了時に流星が最も少ないプレイヤーが勝利します。

—description from the publisher

It is a game that can be played with a game card (business card) and one coin.
This is a simple game where you flip a coin with your finger and put it in a business card.
It's a business card, so it's free.

—description from the publisher (translated)

ゲームカード(名刺)とコイン1枚でプレイできるゲームです。
コインを指ではじいて名刺に入れる簡単なゲームです。
名刺なので無料です。

—description from the publisher

In a not-too-distant place, there is a farm where the chicken Marieta and her friends live, along with the dog Matias and the cat Mia.

The chickens of Matinados farm are raised outdoors, breathe pure air and feed on 100% natural cereals, therefore, they are the happiest in Portugal. They lay organic eggs with optimal nutritional properties for our health.

Players take turns rolling the dice and moving back and forth around the farm stopping at its various gates to collect tiles.
Alternatively, players may place tiles in their hands onto their individual boards, trying to complete it.
In the end, players will score their individual boards according to the tiles placed there. Best score wins.

—description from the designer

You are the creator of the universe. Place beautiful stars to build an enchanting universe. However, if you focus only on making things, you will allow invasion from other creators. Let's create your own universe while making full use of cards so as not to get in the way!

—description from the publisher (translated)

あなたは宇宙の創造主です。美しい星々を配置して、魅力的な宇宙を構築しましょう。 しかし、作ることだけに熱中すると、他の創造主からの侵略を許してしまいます。 邪魔をされないようにカードを駆使しながら、あなただけの宇宙を作り上げましょう！

—description from the publisher

boardgame: 猫 X 東京 ミニ (Neko Kakeru Tokyo Mini)
description:
Worker placement × luck factor
Easy rules that can be played immediately × Advanced strategy and moderate stimulation
Detailed artwork × original cat piece

—description from the publisher (translated)

ワーカープレイスメント × 運要素
即プレイ可能な簡単ルール × 高度な戦略性と適度な刺激
細密なアートワーク × オリジナルの猫駒

—description from the publisher

up, upbringing, borrowed
Grow buds and make your own flowers bloom! The player who makes flowers bloom by arranging 3 flowers in one row vertically, horizontally, or diagonally wins!
Oh, by the way... watch out for those hungry caterpillars!

—description from the publisher (translated)

うえて、そだてて、かりとられ
つぼみを育てて自分の花を咲かせよう！たて・よこ・ななめのどれか１列に３つ並べて花を咲かせたプレイヤーの勝ち！
そうそう、くれぐれも…お腹を空かせたイモムシたちには気をつけて！

—description from the publisher

Like the previous ``OHANARABE sold in 2021, the rules have been renewed while maintaining the fun and cuteness of ``playing with flowers blooming on the board! !

This time we will make flowers bloom in the flower bed with tiles.

Again this year, a competition will be held here in OHANA Village to determine the best gardener.

You are an apprentice gardener. If you win this competition, you have a chance to be recognized as a full-fledged person!

Will you be able to win a splendid victory while amazing skills gather from all over the country every year?

—description from the publisher (translated)

前回2021で販売した「OHANARABE」と同様に“盤上でお花を咲かせて遊ぶ”という楽しさと可愛らしさはそのままに、ルールは一新！！

今回はタイルで花壇に花を咲かせます。

今年もここOHANA村で　いちばんの庭師を決める大会が開催されます。

あなたは見習い庭師。この大会で優勝すれば、一人前として認められるチャンスです！

毎年全国からのスゴ腕達が集まるなか、あなたは見事優勝することができるでしょうか？

—description from the publisher

Crónicas Nemedias is a card game that transports you into the epic universe of Conan the Barbarian, created by Robert E. Howard. This game not only immerses you in adventures filled with magic and danger but also makes you feel like the hero of a legendary story. Throughout the game, you and up to one more player will face challenges, defeat enemies, and complete missions, all with the goal of having your exploits immortalized in the Nemedian Chronicles.

The game combines strategy and action, requiring clever planning to complete epic missions filled with intrigue, combat, and risky decisions. With each mission you complete, you'll feel closer to becoming the most legendary adventurer of all time.

The game’s elements, such as hero cards, enemies, equipment, and adventures, blend into a rich and immersive experience where you'll need to fight with courage, wit, and above all, the desire to leave your mark on history. Additionally, the detailed and well-designed components, along with the possibility to play solo or cooperatively, ensure that each session is unique and thrilling.

Are you ready to have your adventures remembered forever in the Crónicas Nemedias?

—description from the designer

Crónicas Nemedias es un juego de cartas que te transporta al épico universo de Conan el Bárbaro, creado por Robert E. Howard. Este juego no solo te permitirá sumergirte en aventuras llenas de magia y peligros, sino que te hará sentir como el héroe de una historia legendaria. A lo largo del juego, tú y hasta otro jugador más podréis enfrentar desafíos, vencer enemigos y resolver misiones, todo con el fin de que vuestras hazañas queden inmortalizadas en las Crónicas Nemedias.

El juego combina estrategia y acción, requiriendo una planificación inteligente para completar misiones épicas llenas de intrigas, combates y decisiones arriesgadas. Con cada misión superada, sentirás cómo te acercas a convertirte en el aventurero más legendario de todos los tiempos.

Los elementos del juego, como cartas de héroes, enemigos, equipo y aventuras, se mezclan en una experiencia rica y envolvente, en la que deberás luchar con valor, ingenio y, sobre todo, el deseo de dejar tu huella en la historia. Además, los componentes detallados y bien diseñados, junto con la posibilidad de jugar en solitario o en equipo, hacen que cada partida sea única y emocionante.

¿Listo para que tus aventuras sean recordadas para siempre en las Crónicas Nemedias?

—description from the designer (Spanish)

boardgame: A game about auctioning cubes after winning an auction to become the auctioneer
description:
Spend your money, trying to get sets of the same-colour-cube for maximum points. But the winning bid will go to the current auctioneer, who has paid for that privilege!

Do you like struggling to work out how much something is worth?

Do you like the tension of spending money, hoping to make a profit?

Do you like the possibility of losing a game on the first turn?

Alternating between blind auctions (to become the auctioneer) and open English auctions (to buy the cubes).

—description from the designer

This is a top-level game like Old Maid, where two players' karma intertwines. Each player builds a deck of three rounds with four fate-illustrated cards in their hand and on the field, aiming for nirvana at the end of the cycle of samsara by discarding the fate that matches each other's.

—description from the designer

This is a stop-type game similar to the Japanese card game "Daihugō" for two players, where insight and decisiveness are the deciding factors. Each player starts with a set of planet cards with stars and numbers drawn on them, and the goal is to outwit the opponent by playing the cards and gain control of the galaxy.

—description from the designer

Colonel Absaloot Feast is Rival Restaurant's first cross-over chef. Add a little extra flavor to your game as he sinks his teeth into your opponents.

—description from the publisher

This promo, which contains one chef card and one cardboard standee, is included for free in the deluxe edition of Illiterati or can be purchased directly from the publisher.

Blue the Dog and Mother Hen are ready to help out on the farm. The Chicken! Eggspansion includes two new dice and a cloth Seed Bag to mix things up on the farm! Blue the Dog may bark to scare away Foxes, but like any good boy, he loves a nap, and might not be the most reliable helper. Mother Hen is laying Eggs and hatching Chicks that will maximize your scoring potential, but look out! More Foxes are after this fabulous fowl. The Seed Bag is not just a useful tool to hold all your components for storage, it also gives you a fun new way to push your luck on the dice you roll throughout the game. In the Expansion, you get to choose whether you hatch new dice from those available in the Coop, or pull them randomly from the Seed Bag.

In Space Trap, players assume the roles of four intrepid astronauts trapped within an alien vessel on the brink of self-destruction.

Space Trap is an engaging move-and-write game featuring dynamic player interactions, as they race to unravel the ship enigmas. Players strategically maneuver assistant bots across the spacecraft, hunting for crucial information that could ultimately tip the scales in their favor.

—description from the designer

Aquarium is a fast-playing dice game using standard six-sided dice or the Steve Jackson Games "Ocean Dice." If using standard dice, you will need six dice for each player, and each player will need a set of differently-colored dice.
Players compete to be the first to fill their aquariums with one of each type of sea creature by rolling and passing dice. 
Players roll their dice at the same time, and set aside any creatures rolled (or "1" results). Some dice will get passed to other players, and then everyone rolls again. Play continues until someone has collected one of each type of creature in their "Aquarium."

These two new environments will provide you with exciting games!

"Coastline" represents a trendy club where operators will move through a series of brightly lit lounges and bars to neutralize the enemy threat. To achieve their goals, operators must stay mobile and adapt their defenses to this subdued environment, a paradise tainted by the chaos of combat.

Featuring a checkpoint composed of old and new buildings, "Border" is a very open environment that encourages clashes on the outer edges of the building. Operators must make their way through the offices of this secure complex, converging on a central room that will inevitably be the scene of gunfire.

—description from the publisher

Kagoun is a game of card collection and confrontation.

In Paris in 1900, while the Universal Exhibition is in full swing, a few spiritism enthusiasts discover the existence of Kagouns. These fantastic entities are geometric-shaped butterflies of light.

From that moment on, the Kagoun hunters become obsessed with capturing them and uncovering their mysteries, using their mystical glasses to attract Kagouns. It's teamwork, but not entirely, as nothing prevents a Kagoun hunter from stealing a Kagoun from their neighbor, turning them into adversaries.

On your turn, you can (1) capture cards from the center (free-roaming Kagouns); (2) assemble your Kagoun collection; (3) steal an unprotected Kagoun card from a rival; or (4) discard unwanted cards from your hand to draw replacements.

You can have up to 3 pairs of unassembled Kagouns in front of you. If you assemble Kagouns next to each other, matching their edge shapes, you build your collection and points. Further, only unassembled Kagouns or the Kagouns at either end of your collection can possibly be stolen by rivals; your central collected Kagouns are protected.

To hinder opponents progressing faster than you, you can try to steal cards from them, but they will have an opportunity to defend themselves each time.

To win, you must reach 10 points and announce "Kagoun!" at the start of your intended final round, giving your rivals one last chance to either stop you or, if they also called "Kagoun!" that same round, to exceed your score. If, at the end of this round, you have at least 10 and the most points, you win.

Tumble-Top: A Topping Top Game is a delightful family game that takes inspiration from the beloved spinning top games of the 1940s and 50s. Players spin tops onto a vibrant, colorful board, each aiming for the top to tumble into one of several holes, each assigned with a different point value. The first to reach a score of 100 is the victor. This game is not only a test of skill but a spectacle of chance and anticipation as players eagerly watch their tops spin, wobble, and finally settle into a scoring hole. Packaged in a retro-inspired box, Tumble-Top embodies the charm and simplicity of yesteryear's games while providing timeless fun for today's families.

—description from the publisher

March of the Wicked Horde is a card game that simulates fantasy battles. The setting is medieval fantasy including factions such as elves and undead. The gameplay is a mixture of chess-like strategy and mechanics similar to trading card games but with no randomness like shuffling or dice.

Players choose a faction and determine who goes first. They then take turns placing land and units, moving units, attacking, activating schemes and using scheme abilities. There is no board but cards are used as tiles and arranged in a grid relative to other cards. The object of the game is to kill the enemy commander.

The elves hail from forested lands, using assassins with acrobatics and bow tactics to consume an enemy's forces. The undead hordes emerge from graveyards and grow faster as they kill the enemy. New factions are planned and each new faction that comes out will increase the number of players for the game by one.

March of the Wicked Horde is all about skill. There are no dice, no shuffling and no face-down cards to draw. The movement may remind you of chess but units can only be attacked when your threat is greater than the enemy's reinforcement. You may need to pick off weakly reinforced enemy units, pose a greater threat by ganging up on a strong enemy unit, or combine schemes in a clever way. Any two games are very unlikely to go exactly the same way as there are many possible actions on each turn and many layered strategies to experiment with.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Strategtree is a cardgame consisting of 52 cards with trees on them. Each tree has different properties: Treetop shape (three shapes), treetop color (five colors), trunk color (black or white or both), number of fruit (1-4). 
9 cards are laid out on the table in an X-shape. Players take turns placing a card from their hand. It has to match at least one property with each adjacent color. Score one point for each match. Each player also holds on supercard that can be placed on top of an existing card to score extra points. When all cards have been placed, the player with the highest score is the winner.

The Russian Campaign, 1941-45

After spectacular success in the West, Germany and its Axis partners turned East in June 1941 and launched the greatest land invasion in history against the Soviet Union. The goal of Operation Barbarossa was to decisively defeat the Soviet Union in a short lightning campaign before the onset of winter.

During the early stages of the campaign, the Wehrmacht caught the Red Army completely unprepared and had advanced its armored spearheads across the Dnepr in only a few short weeks. By mid-October, the Axis invasion appeared unstoppable and on the verge of ultimate success.

Unfortunately for the Axis, the time for a decisive victory had run out with the arrival of Russia’s notorious muddy season and the worst winter in a century. Despite suffering catastrophic losses over four months, the Soviet military was finally able to regroup and halt the invaders at the gates of Moscow. The Axis would never come so close to victory again and the initiative would slowly fall from its grasp.

As the Axis, can you do better and seize Moscow before the snow falls?

The adorable puppies are piled up, and you want your puppy at the top!

Each player has a secret puppy. Over the course of the game, players will move various puppies up and down through the pile. Try to figure out your opponent's puppies and keep them lower in the pile while you try to move your secret puppy to the top.

When the PICK A WINNER card is drawn, the top dog wins!

—description from the publisher

In Disney Villainous: Filled with Fright, one player takes on the role of Oogie Boogie from Tim Burton's The Nightmare Before Christmas and attempts to dethrone Jack Skellington, the Pumpkin King.

Note that this is a single character expansion and must be combined with a Disney Villainous standalone game.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Crimescene is a simple drawing and deduction party game.
At the start of the game, two teams of witnesses are given their 'crime scene' - an animal, a place, a school subject, etc - which only they know but which their detective must guess. Teams take turns selecting from a grid of 25 random transparent cards (clues) with preprinted graphics.
