They can then use dry-erase markers to draw additions to their clue before placing it onto their detective's investigation sketchboard, painting a picture of their crime scene.
The team whose detective correctly guesses their crime scene from their investigation sketchboard first, wins the game. Watch out though, as opposing witnesses and detectives have ways to keep your mouth shut!

—description from the designer
family: Components: Dry Erase Markers / Dry Erase Surfaces

description:
The object of the game is to collect all cards of one of the seven commodity types (to form a “corner”) by way of trading cards with other players.

Cards are shuffled with seven dealt to each player (the number of commodities in play corresponds to the number of players). The players are given time to arrange their cards and decide which commodity they may wish to corner. The commodities have different point values (coffee 100, eggs 50, etc.)

Play occurs simultaneously with players trading one or more cards with each other. Once a corner is made play ends and that player scores the value of the commodity cornered. The first player to reach 250 points wins the game.

This is Selchow and Righter’s version of the commodity trading games published at the turn of the 20th century. Similar in play to Commerce, Gavitt’s Stock Exchange, and Pit.

Corporate Onboarding is a solo journaling game all about navigating corporate bureaucracy. Players use a standard deck of cards and two six-sided dice to create an evolving map of their new office as they try to find the six bosses they need to report to.

Along the way, they will encounter random events in rooms that are generated based on their cards and dice rolls. These will impact how they play the game and how quickly they can achieve success.

Players can also be reprimanded by HR and will need to deal with the reprimands as they arise - if they fail to do so adequately they will end up with a warning. If a player receives five warnings they are fired and lose the game.

Corporate Onboarding includes two A4 tri-fold pamphlets (that can be printed at home) and is based on the Firelights game system.

—description from the designer

Moebius Circuit Race is a print and play racing game on a moebius strip.

Innovation meets retro style: Play an arcade car racing game like back in the 80's on a sheet of paper!

You only need a pen and one sheet printed double sided for each player.

Tick icons, switch lanes and race, but keep an eye on the skidding risk!

—description from the designer

Miasma Chronicles the boardgame is based on the video game of the same name developed by The Bearded Ladies and published by 505 Games. It is a science fiction adventure set in a post-apocalyptic version of the United States.
In the role of Elvis and with the help of his trusted robot Diggs, you are entrusted by the Mayor with the task of tracking down a missing miner. You will have to investigate by asking around among your acquaintances and interpret the traces you will discover to complete the mission.
During the game you will encounter Battle Challenges cards that will ask you to use the board and pawns to set up a combat field and face the enemies. Each Battle Challenge is a riddle on where to place Elvis and Diggs' checkers to get a win.

This new version of the WARS series has been completely revamped to take place in a fantasy world. The battles are set to occur on the lost continent of Gamora, where players' armies will have to face a new adversaries and conquer rich territories.

In this game release you will find, in addition to a new and full-color graphical layout:

3x Armies (The Sylvanids, the Knights of the Silver Marches and the ancient Eternals)
100x Detachments Bases (representing all the army units)
45x Battle cards
27x Army specific Strategic Cards
24x Base Strategy cards
80x Counters
10x Scenic elements

With WARS Fantasy, players will now experience an added layer of strategic unpredictability through the use of Battle Cards, which has been added to the classic Alternate Command Activation system. They players will still have the option of choosing Strategy Cards, which have made the WARS system popular amongst three-dimensional battle games enthusiasts.

Despite the changes, the game still maintains its ease of play and quick duration, which lasts around 60-90 minutes. Additionally, its compact size, only requiring a table of 80x100 cm, makes it one of the most convenient options for organizing tournaments or events.

In summary, WARS Fantasy is not only simple to learn but also an engaging game that provides a thrilling experience for all players. The game's updated elements have breathed new life into it, making it an excellent choice for those looking for a fresh, exciting battle game.

—description from the designer

Gli Eroi della Mitologia is a free Print & Play Italian game for 2, 3 or 4 players set in the Heroic Age of Greek Mythology and allows players to assume the role of the Gods of Olympus trying to control the destinies of their Heroes on Earth. Each player has a certain number of points which he must use to try to control the heroes who have objectives to achieve based on the cards drawn. If they succeed, they score victory points.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Welcome to the world of One Card Vegas, the game that will take you on an exciting journey through the glitz and glamour of a Las Vegas casino... all in one card! The goal of the game is to be the first player to reach $50,000 or be the only player left in the game with money. There are 5 different games available that allow players to win (or lose) money: Roulette, Poker, Slot Machine, Craps and Black Jack. Each of these games is based on the version that is actually played in casinos, but has been adapted to be played on a single card and with a limited number of components.

—description from the designer

Royal Builders is a print at home roll and write game for one to four players where you take on a job of constructing buildings in a kingdom, given to you by the Crown. During the game you will gather resources and use them to build infrastructure that will satisfy the King.

—description from the publisher

Two players compete on a circle shaped board, moving their four counters around it. 
Cards in their hands indicate coloured triangles and squares that they should form by moving those counters, in order to gain points.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Firefly: The Game – 10th Anniversary Collector's Edition
description:
In Firefly: The Game – based on the popular Firefly television series created by Joss Whedon – players captain their own Firefly-class transport ship, travelling the 'Verse with a handpicked crew of fighters, mechanics, and other passengers. As a captain desperate for work, players are compelled to take on any job — so long as it pays. Double-dealing employers, heavy-handed Alliance patrols, and marauding Reavers are all in a day's work for a ship's captain at the edge of the 'Verse.

Players begin with a ship, and travel from planet to planet, hiring crew, purchasing ship upgrades, and picking up cargo to deliver (jobs) all in the form of cards. Some crew and cargo are illegal, and can be confiscated if your ship is boarded by an Alliance vessel. Traveling from planet to planet requires turning over "full burn" cards, one for each space moved. Most do nothing, but you can also encounter an Alliance ship, have a breakdown, or even run into Reavers. Completing jobs gets you cash. First player to complete the story goals wins!

Firefly: The Game – 10th Anniversary Collector's Edition is the whole 'Verse in a box. Containing every expansion ever made in a single container, along with a new Still Flying expansion, this collection features upgraded components such as:

— 15 hand-painted resin ships
— Enamel metal first-player dinosaur token
— Complete rulebook
— Custom made multi-piece storage solution
— Over 1,000 cards (including every promo card ever made)
— Neoprene mat with a stitched edge of the whole 'Verse.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description: A wooden flipper for 2 players. It is made of solid wood and a plexiglas on top with a hole for the metal ball on both sides. Also it has got 4 bumpers.

Kadri-Cube is a wooden Maze which is played in Teammode. It can be played as a Race 2 vs 2 or you can try to beat the clock or play a full collaborate game.

On one side of the wooden box is the labyrinth and on the other side is the playing area which is plain and where the playing pieces are moved by the players. On the other side you can see the true way without any obstacles and you have to explain the other players how to move. In various modes the communication is restricted.

—user summary

Kulling is a Swiss curling variant played on the table. Two players or two teams try to use a wooden slide to slide their own discs (called Kull) into the target area. Similar to curling, precision is important. Because the pucks with the spinning ball in the middle should come to a standstill as close as possible to the center of the target. If a disc rolls too far, it falls into the penalty area. Playing over the boards and pushing the opponent's discs with your own is allowed. Kulling is also suitable for on the go: the accessories can be stowed in the secured upper compartment and the entire game can thus be transported using the carrying handle.

She has to be quick and nimble, the mouse who wants to be the first to climb the peak of the cheese. The higher the players make it in a predetermined time, the more points they get. This requires a lot of skill, after all, with Sourisquick the ball inside the mouse must not roll through one of the cheese holes. The mouse navigates up and around these traps using two drawstrings, which players grasp at their ends and use to navigate the mouse. The choice of the ball - a larger one made of wood and a smaller one made of metal - and the set angle of inclination of the game board (three are possible) determine the level of difficulty.

Over time, a large number of smaller table games developed from classic billiards, all of which have remained true to the successful basic principle of sinking the ball. The Spirobille also comes from the sphere of billiard-like games, a variation that is as unusual as it is fascinating and whose game principle is extremely simple. A steel ball is brought into play via the center piece and rolls down the sloping field towards one of the players. He should remove the ball from his goal as soon as possible using the flipper lever and, if possible, hammer one of the teammates into the goal. This borrowing from the pinball game that emerged in the early 1950s gave it its current speed. (In its predecessor, the Jeu de Marteau, you still had to maneuver the balls in the direction of the opponent's goals with small clubs.) Spirobille is best played with four players, but it's just as much fun - the vacant goals are then blocked by insertable steel brackets lockable – for two or three people. The Spirobille, which is made entirely in Switzerland, is part of the standard range of so-called toy libraries there – a kind of libraries for games – in which it has to withstand all the requirements of lending. It is accordingly solidly manufactured and lasts far beyond the total exhaustion of the players.

A very old game where the players throw metal disc from 2-3 meters away and try to get the disk in the frogs mouth (most points) or a hole (lesser points). After the throwing you count the points and it is the next players turn.

With a strong, but if possible not too energetic snap, the players set their colored marbles on their way. The middle lane serves as a launch ramp, on which the balls accelerate to roll over as many waves as possible after a turn. The up to four players take turns with one of their marbles each. Three scenarios are possible after the start: The ball can 1. get stuck in one of the troughs on the left or right track as hoped (the further it gets, the better), 2. come to rest at the front of the ramp or 3. in the Groove getting stuck at the end of the ramp. The large metal ball serves as a joker - each player may use it once per game to move opponent's marbles out of the way or into the penalty groove or to bring their own marbles forward.

boardgame: Defenders of the Realm: Legends Edition
description:
The last decade has been an unprecedented age of prosperity, peace, and growth across the known realm. We have not known a tranquility like this in all our recorded history. The young have no conception of what life was like during the war against the Dark Lord, and they are far better for it. However, this peace balances unsteadily on a knife’s edge. The shadow of evil grows tall and long. Rumors of dragons come from the far reaches. Goblins organize into roving bands. The dead stir in the dust of long-forgotten battlefields. The scent of sulfur and brimstone permeates the air to the south, bringing with it a dread promise of demons.

Ancient tomes in the libraries of Monarch City tell us one thing: the shattering of our peace is upon us.

Defenders of the Realm: Legends Edition is a fresh take on the co-operative fantasy classic. Be the heroes who adventure together across the land to destroy the forces of darkness. The game features six unique heroes, each with a unique deck as well as variable player powers. Manage your hand as the more cards you play, the more resources you give to the enemy. Save Monarch City from crushing hordes of goblins, kobolds, skeletons, and demons and those that command them.

—description from the publisher

Place the wolfman card somewhere in the lower half of the monster deck.

When the wolfman appears at the party, it is a good idea to place him next to the window from which he can see the moon. If you do, when you score the fireworks event, you will get 3 points for the wolfman (instead of 1 point, as you would with the other monsters).

Topper Knocker is a competitive, turn-based board game where players build the map as they go, and work to slow down their opponents as they race to victory.

2-6 players start out with towers on hex tiles in the center of the map. On their turns, they can choose to either discover new tiles to place adjacent to their towers, grow their existing towers, spread out and build new towers, or draw cards that can have powerful effects that can either benefit or harm them. The first player to reach 5 level 3 towers wins!

—description from the designer

Add a whole new theme to your Rulebenders game. You'll travel through all of Game Brewer's games, past and future. Game includes 35 theme cards, one miniature, one plastic game token, and one storage box.

The annual “BRIGADA PULGA” competition, which seeks to find the strongest, most agile and fearless, has the fleas eager to meet this year's winning team. After months of training, the representatives of each team must manage to cross as many members as possible to the other side of the city, avoiding a series of obstacles mounted on a dog.

Bet on a dog, the indicated number of flea tiles and secretly play one of your challenge cards to form the path to follow. Reveal and overcome the challenges to be able to reach the other end of the city with your fleas. Whoever crosses with the highest number will be the winning team.

—description from the publisher (translated)

La competencia anual “Brigada Pulga”,  que busca encontrar a las más fuertes, ágiles e intrépidas, tiene a las pulgas  ansiosas por conocer al equipo ganador de este año. Después de meses de entrenamientos, las representantes de cada equipo, deben lograr cruzar al mayor número de miembros hasta el otro lado de la ciudad, evitando una serie de obstáculos montadas sobre un perro.

Apuesta sobre un perro, el número indicado de fichas de pulgas y juega, en secreto, una de tus cartas de desafíos para formar el camino a recorrer. Revela y supera los desafíos para poder llegar con tus pulgas hasta el otro extremo de la ciudad. Quien cruce con mayor número, será el equipo vencedor.

—description from the publisher

A 31 card tuck box including 24 Challenge Cards, 6 Mission cards and 1 Achievement card. Allows players to face a new threat and tell a new story of survival horror on the SSO.
The new deck's theme features an infestation by small, fast breeding, furry creatures who will choke up the ship, taking over locations and even draining morale.

—description from the designer

Oh, no! The greatest balloons party of all time was about to start when, all of a sudden, the balloons got mixed up. You and your friends are in charge of selecting the balloons that better fit the party.

Balloons! is a kids game where you'll have to be the quickest collecting balloon cards in order to win. Everyone plays simultaneously, so laughter and craziness are a given!

This game is adapted to people with visual impairments, as all the balloons have unique colours and shapes.

—description from the publisher

A 36 card tuck box including 24 Challenge Cards, 6 Mission cards, four new location cards, 1 new crew card and 1 Achievement card. Also includes a divider card, cardboard tokens and two wooden meeples. Allows players to face a new threat and tell a new story of survival horror on the SSO.
The new deck's theme features a terrifying alien creature which stalks and hunts crew through the ship, attacking and killing them. It also include the Vet crewmember and two new locations, the Vents and the Bio Dome.

—description from the designer

Claustro is an abstract two player game, with super simple rules (goal, movement, capture) like many others, but it has a twist on the capture mechanism that makes it engaging, thoughtful and fresh.
Captured pieces are not removed from the board but must be placed back on the board by the player that captured it.
This simple twist make the game hard to master and engaging.
The game is free to be crafted at home and needs just two sets of different color pawns and a 5 by 5 grid (as you can see in the pictures).

Setup
take a five by five grid and place it diagonally oriented between the players, the two corners closest to the players  are each other players' respective goals to reach with one of four pawns, the other two corners are non-occupiable squares.
Each player stars with the 4 pawns on the four squares to the left and right border of their closest corner.

Gameplay and Win
Players alternatively take turns moving one square orthogonally towards the goal or capture opponent's pawn diagonally (in all four directions), a captured piece must be immediately placed back on the board on any unoccupied square by the capturing player before passing the turn. The first player that walks a pawn in the goal wins the game.

—description from the designer

family: Game: Cluedo / Clue (Parker Brothers, et al)

description:
We go back to the years around 1925. The Voynich Manuscript, which no human has been able to translate to this day is written in an unknown script and language and profusely illustrated with images of unidentified plants, enigmatic astronomical drawings, and puzzling human figures. Find the missing pages of the medieval codex and discover the secret behind the magical drawings and mysterious glyphs. Compete against your competitors and be the first to solve all puzzles. But be careful, agents are on their way to steal your discoveries and knowledge. Take advantage of the world's largest Zeppelin airships, airplanes and ocean liners to get anywhere quickly. Who will be the first to solve the big riddle and thus become world famous or keep his occult secret to himself in order to become one of the most powerful people in the world?

Codex is a board game with strategic and cooperative elements for two to six players in the race for one of the greatest riddles of bibliophile and occult history. The game mechanic is a combination of deck building, set collection, worker placement, area movement and engine building.

In Codex, players choose a character and secret mission to complete. Players then use travel tickets to move to different cities in the world to collect clues and artifacts. Codex' is semi-competitive. Competing players may also be in the same city, trying to meet the same agents and could end up stealing from you or killing your agent.  The first player to get the points and meet the requirements of their secret mission, wins the game.

.

Join Lightning McQueen, Sally, Mater, and Doc Hudson in a thrilling race for the Piston Cup! On a track filled with danger and excitement, overtaking is not allowed in pit stop lanes and stopping can cost you an extra lap. The game is governed by a special dice; roll between 1-4 to move forward, but a tire symbol means a flat tire, causing you to lose your turn. If you roll Lightning McQueen, however, you get to advance to one space ahead of the lead car. Be wary of oil spills, they can push you forward or pull you back by three spaces. With suspense at its peak, the first car to cross the finish line claims the victory. Remember to visit at least two pitstops for refueling and repairs. Let's put the pedal to the metal!

—description from the publisher

Working as a monster in an evil wizard's dungeon isn't easy. It's dark, damp, and your boss has a habit of turning employees into frogs during annual performance reviews. But perhaps the worst thing is the boredom. Sure, it's exciting when the occasional hero stumbles past and you have a bit of a scuffle. However, most of the time you're just hanging about watching mould grow on the walls.

Deep in the depths of Hoodez Dungeon, Toothache the Troll and Gumrot the Goblin became fed up of sitting around doing nothing. Borrowing the jaws of a nearby skeleton (asking politely first, of course) and some gems they found in a treasure chest, they invented a fun new game of teeth collecting which made the hours between battling adventurers just fly by.

In Battle Dentale, you play either Gumrot the Goblin or Toothache the Troll, competing to collect the best collection of teeth from a jawbone. Toothache favors the cleaner teeth because they're shiny, while Gumrot likes the decaying ones as the smell reminds them of their mum. Winning requires strategy, efficient decision-making, and sometimes evil moves to sabotage your opponent! To win, gather a more highly valued collection of teeth than the other player.

boardgame: Betwixt and Between: The Faery Queen's Whim
description:
The Faery Queen's Whim is the final expansion for Betwixt and Between that introduces thematic Whim Cards with different events and requests for every round of the game. The expansion also comes with 7 unique familiars that may accompany your Mystic on their adventure. Join path with a cunning crow, a loyal dog, a quick hare, an adventurous cat, a spiky hedgehog, a sneaky fox, or a loyal horse!

—description from the publisher

BIRTH or BURST is a cooperative game for 3-6 players. In this game, players work together to control the temperature in order to successfully hatch penguin eggs.

To win:
The game will be cleared if the temperature can be successfully adjusted without bursting for 10 turns.

How to Play:
Each player plays one card each turn that regulates the temperature. The sum of the numbers of the cards played will be the temperature to be increased or decreased.

A 2-4 player hand management game about building a flower crown. You have a choice to collect your flowers from two different decks the forest and the meadow. Manage your hand by trading with the corresponding flower stall so you only have to play what you want to play. Play three of the same color flowers into your basket and get one for your crown. Be on the lookout for the unique wild flowers that can be played as any color! Every time a deck is reshuffled a new seasonal wild flower is placed into that deck. The game ends when someone has completed their crown or when the decks have been depleted 4 times.

—description from the designer

The Advanced D6 Class Starter Pack comes with:


     7 Glory cards
     7 Heroic Quest cards
     7 Advantage cards 4 New Wooden Monster tokens
     7 Class-Colored Gelatinous Cubes Minis
     7 Travel Stickers
     D6 Hero Dice Pad*
     2 Dice Pads (combine them with the box to make it into a dice tray)
     19 Rule Correction Stickers!

boardgame: Endangered Orphans: The Last Winter of Benny Harris
description:
This expansion comes with everything you need to beef up Endangered Orphans of Condyle Cove (2nd Edition) to make it a four player game. You’ll get a fourth-player card mat, a fourth-player orphan/ghost (Benny) pawn, fourth-player cards, more Cove cards, more Cul-De-Sacs…more, more, MORE!


        This is not a standalone game. Requires the Endangered Orphans of Condyle Cove base game.**
    



        This expansion includes all the booster packs from the 1st Edition of Endangered Orphans of Condyle Cove.**

boardgame: 分数陣取りゲーム「フラテリ」(Fraction Territory)
description:
If you place the color pieces that correspond to the fraction cards you have and complete your own color circle, you win!
Double the bargaining! Aim for a one-shot reversal with a function card!
You never know who will win until the end! ? Let's find out which circle will be completed!

—description from the publisher (translated)

手持ちの分数カードに対応した、色ピースを置いて自陣の色円を完成させたら勝ち！
駆け引きが倍増！ファンクションカードで一発逆転を狙え！
最後まで勝負は分からない！？どこの円が完成するかしっかり見極めよう！

—description from the publisher
publishers: ragazzo ragazza laboratorio (ラガラガ・ラボ)

description:
You can move forward while freely placing circle chips that indicate your destination.
Area chips can be placed on the route where the piece has progressed, and it becomes your own position.
If you can place all your area chips splendidly, you win! !

—description from the publisher (translated)

自分の行先を示すサークルチップを自由に配置しながらコマを進めていきます。
コマが進んだルートはエリアチップを置くことができ、自分の陣地となります。
自分のエリアチップを見事全て置ききることができれば勝利！！

—description from the publisher

In this cooperative board game, players try to guide three animals to find enough food and find shelter while the forest is gradually destroyed by deforestation, pollution, and fire. Key to success is a mix of right strategy and luck. Besides, it raises players' awareness of current environmental and ecological problems going along with ruthless destruction of rainforest habitat.

Duel across the hidden realms in a unique face-paced trading card game!

The Age of Artifacts Collectible Trading Card Game is a fast-paced, easy-to-learn battle between two Champions across three lanes of combat.
The first starter kit contains two 40 card decks, Boro and Eldred, which each boast a different play style to try.

The goal of the game is to use your champion, units, and spells to reduce the opposing player's life total to 0.

The game is played in a series of rounds where you play powerful creatures and spells in a series of three lanes. Once you or your opponent can't play anymore cards a combat round is initiated. This causes your creatures to attack which in turn can deal direct damage to your hero if they are not well protected. Spells can create sudden twists in the game causing opponents to loose creatures or initiating early combat when your opponent might not be ready.  Are you ready to take on the fight for the Age of Artifacts?

boardgame: Votes for Women: Two-Card Promo Set
description:
A two-card promo set for Votes for Women.
Per the designer, tuck them into the Late deck for each side.
Gives background and game statistics for VASA players in the VOID / Urban War Universe.
Gives background and game statistics for Viridian players in the VOID / Urban War Universe.

Spin to Win Words is a simple spelling game. Two to four players make words by spinning letters on an everchanging board. Three-letter words are the primary target, however two- and -four letter words are possible.

Gain points by aligning arrows in lines similar to tic-tac-toe but with letters.

SETUP:

Start with one of the random 3x3 game boards and two stacks of single letters and bigram letters cards.

GAME PLAY:

Each player maintains two cards of each type (single & bigram). Combining two cards, a player calls out a word and spins the top card to point at the player.
When three cards in a straight line point to a player, that player scores one point. However, if the cards are all of the same type (single or bigram), the player scores two points.

Play continues until someone scores 10 points.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Vengeance: Roll & Fight – Boss Baby Promo Card
description:
Gamefound Exclusive Baby Boss promo card

Title: "Boss Baby"
Health: 3
Effect: "When the Boss Baby is killed, draw a Blocker in every room."

It is that time of year! The little mice of the town must share their annual harvest among everyone in the town, as "evenly" as possible, but trying to keep the best part to become the... King/Queen of the Harvest Festival!

Harvest Festival is a no language dependent, family-friendly game that takes the mechanic "I cut, you choose" and gives it a little twist.

In your turn you must play a single card in one of the piles that are in the plaza (Center of the Table) and if the card under the played card matches... You will be able to play another card!

Beware! The time of the day will indicate if the card is played face up or facedown.

Try to get rid of all your cards. This will give you a lot of points! In addition, if you no longer have cards, you can take one of the piles from the plaza to score at the end of the day.

But there is a catch! The leftover pile on the plaza will go to the Mayor, The Festival Organizer! And this will dictate the value of each of the fruits and vegetables of the festival, making them worth many points... Or even zero!

And let's not forget the invited mice, each one of them that you take will ask you for food and if you are the host with the most mice fed, you will get a bonus!

So play your cards strategically, make the piles attractive to everyone and score as many points you can!

At the end of the 3 days of the festival, the player with the most points will be the winner! And the King/Queen of the Harvest Festival!

—description from the designer

boardgame: The Girl and the Robot: The Card Game – Kickstarter Exclusive Pack
description:
As an added reward for the success of the Kickstarter campaign, Flying Carpet Games teamed up with three indie video game developers to include cards inspired by their games.  There are three cards in total:

Insignia's Cursed Hat (King of the Hat)
Weapon: When played, place this weapon face down. After defeating a Demon, you must destroy another weapon on the table if able.

Colos and Suses (The Messenger)
Action: Destroy a weapon on the table or force a player to discard 2 cards.

Yamata no Orochi (Abyss and Dark)
Action: To play this card, you must also discard 2 cards. Destroy 2 weapons on the table if able. Gain 2 Ability Points this turn. Draw 2 cards.

You take on the role of Heldo Braveheart and try to find the daughter of King Throngold. You'll venture through the Shadow Forest and into a dungeon. In your search for the princess you will encounter friendly and hostile creatures. But the path itself is a mystery every time. Find the right path through the forest and the walls by correctly placing the available path pieces level by level.

boardgame: Marvel: Crisis Protocol – Crimson Dynamo & Darkstar
description:
The Winter Guard arrives in Marvel: Crisis Protocol with the Crimson Dynamo & Darkstar Character Pack! Leading the way is Dimitri Bukhran, the fifth man to bear the title of Crimson Dynamo. His suit of power armor grants him abilities that make him a dedicated soldier for the Russian people. He is joined by Laynia Petrovna, known as Darkstar. Born with the power to access the energy of the Darkforce dimension, she can shape it into constructs or unleash it in powerful blasts.

Together, these characters form the backbone of the Winter Guard, Russia’s answer to the Avengers. In addition to introducing this new affiliation to Marvel: Crisis Protocol, this pack also includes three Team Tactic cards that unlock even more power within Crimson Dynamo, Darkstar, and the other members of the Winter Guard.

Contents:
2 Miniatures
  ‣ Crimson Dynamo
  ‣ Darkstar
6 Bases
  ‣ 2 Round Scenic (50mm)
  ‣ 4 Round Scenic (35mm)
2 Character Stat Cards
3 Team Tactic Cards
1 Affiliation Card

Game about the epic of the conquest of the Poles during the years 1908 to 1912 with a system of pushing your luck and with historical explorers both in the North Pole and in the South Pole.
Each player will organize an expedition and launch it on an adventure, winning the one that achieves the best milestones (closest to the Poles).


     Expeditions are organized by spending money that each Explorer (with a card that represents it) contributes, by means of sledges, animals, provisions and equipment.
     The development of each expedition combines chance with Weather and Terrain cards and the decision to push your luck... with Freezing, Hunger or Disorientation problems.
     Victory conditions depend on how far the Expeditions go (closest to the two Poles), which will give Victory points.
     It can be played alone or with two players and each one can take several Historical Explorers.

The Gin Crafters Spirits Expansion Set adds three elements to Gin Crafters: base spirits, distillers, and supporters.

This package requires the original Gin Crafters game in order to play.
publishers: JUGAME STUDIO (ジュゲムスタジオ)

description:
Blocky is a family game for the whole family! BLOCKY can be played EVERYWHERE! At home, outside, on a slope, around obstacles, everywhere!

Try to get your dice as close as possible to the multiplication die and collect points. The first to 40 points wins.

—description from the publisher

Work cooperatively with your fellow players to complete a beautiful Celtic knot design.

Each turn, you take a new rolled die and add it to your personal pool, then either add one of your dice to the shared design or throw out your die. But if the players throw away 3 dice total, they lose.

Once you complete the design, all remaining dice are thrown away - so you need to complete the design using almost all of the dice. You also need to complete the 3 goals (chosen to set the difficulty level of each game) in order to declare it a win and move on to the next level.

-description from designer

Ten Hours or No Sawdust pits players against each other as they compete to control mills, influence newspapers, recruit from taverns, and lobby politicians in order to prevail in a labor dispute over the twelve hour work day of the north woods in 1881.

Players deploy their followers strategically throughout Eau Claire, Wisconsin hoping to hold majorities in as many of the key locations they can at the end of each round.

Gameplay is both straightforward and taxing, with a rondel forcing players to think a few moves ahead as they try to expand influence while preventing their opponents from utilizing treacherous powers such as converting workers or calling in the state militia.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

boardgame: 24 Days Escape: 3D Adventskalender – Sherlock Holmes im Schatten des Big Ben
description:
Sherlock Holmes has received an invitation to the clocktower of Westminster. Upon arrival, he is confronted with an unsolved crime from his past.

To play this 3D advent calendar, you first assemble an 86cm/34 inch model of the clocktower in which the story is set. 
Over the course of 24 days you find mysterious objects and clues that help you find out what happened 20 years ago. Each solved puzzle brings you another step closer to the solution of the puzzle.
family: Misc: Advent / Christmas / Jule calendar game

description:
In Takibi, the players are tending to a campfire. Each turn, players will choose and simultaneously reveal a card.  Most of the cards have two halves: firewood and cocoa.

The strength of the fire increases for each player who plays a firewood card. Depending upon the resulting strength of the fire, these players may earn points or take a penalty. If the fire goes out, players who were drinking cocoa will take a penalty.  There are also firestarter cards.

The player with the most points after a series of rounds wins.

In It's on me!, players have ingredient cards for making cocktails and recipes for those cocktails. There is also a common deck of ingredients.

During the game, players have the opportunity to draft ingredients out of the common deck and replace with other cards from their personal ingredient pool.

Players will discard recipe cards and then ingredients are revealed from the common pool. If no players can complete a recipe from their hand, reveal an additional ingredient card.  If all players can complete a recipe, then the ingredient deck is adjusted again, and players discard additional recipe cards.

However, if only some people can complete a recipe, those recipes are completed and given to a player who could not make one.

The game ends after a number of rounds or when someone is too drunk - based upon the alcohol content of the drinks which have been placed in front of them.  The winner is the least drunk player.

boardgame: ドラフトポーカー (Draft Poker)
description:
In Draft Poker, players draft cards and score points. Each card in the game has a letter and shows scoring conditions.

Players each contribute a card from their initial hand to a tableau of scoring conditions which is at first hidden. The scoring cards are shuffled, and then one is revealed.  Players then keep 3 cards from their hand and pass 4 to a neighbor. Next, a second scoring card is revealed, and players pass 3 cards. Then, a third scoring card is revealed, and players pass 2 cards. Lastly, a fourth scoring card is revealed, and players pass 1 card.

Then, players score the 7 cards in their hand based upon the scoring tableau.

Capital Horse is a trivia game in which 6 randomly chosen Japanese prefectures are running as the horses in a race.  Each race, a category is chosen (such as size of prefecture or population), and the players make up to 3 wagers from 7 different options. Accurate wagers give a player points based upon the rankings of the prefectures.

In CastleScape: Gates and Guilds, the world of guild competition and castle construction expands into new realms of challenge. Building upon the foundation established in the original CastleScape game, this expansion introduces an array of new features, including the introduction of the ability to build "Gates", as well as an assortment of new cards and guild powers.

The King is not happy with the simplicity of his castle. There must now be grandiose gates built in desirous locations to augment the grandeur of his might. Guild masters, driven by ambition and a thirst for renown, seek to prove their guild's worth by being the ones to build and control these Gates. To succeed, guilds must adapt their strategies, collect increasingly diverse cards and utilize their guild powers.

Will your guild rise to the king’s call? Prepare to use everything at your disposal to become the victor in the expanding world of CastleScape.

-description from designer

August 1863. Preparations begin for the Salon, the largest and most prestigious art exhibition in the world! The Louvre halls will be filled with paintings from floor to ceiling, and you will be one of those responsible for selecting the works.
The task is not easy: hundreds of works have been entered, and you will need to choose the best ones for the room you are curating.

To be successful, it is fundamental to identify which themes are most in vogue this year and to reject the very modern paintings, which have no place in a classic Fine Arts salon, but which may dictate the new directions of art in the future.

In Salon, players bid on paintings with different styles and formats and then place them on their player boards according to the highlight they want to give the selected painting. Paintings not chosen are sent to the Hall of Rejects and may give bonuses to the player who places them there.

Throughout the game, players try to get medals for their paintings, make the themes of their interest more valuable and prepare for the next auctions.

The player with the most victory points at the end of the game will be considered the best art curator in the Salon de Paris.

Cat Face is a cat meme party game brought to you by the crazy cat people at The Pet Collective. An internet cat game that you don't play on the internet. Featuring cats from some of your favorite viral videos, players compete to find the funniest combination of cat faces and funny captions (no litter box required).

—description from the publisher

Find the community tile pattern on the board before your opponents. Tiles can be in any orientation, or across multiple tiles. Find the pattern and score a tile from the board. Replace the board tile with the community tile, and draw a new community tile. If there is no matching pattern on the board, then be the first to touch the community tile to score that tile.

Collecting sets of pictures, earning points for completed sets, and strategically using Toilet Cards to flush away opponents' collections. The player with the most points at the end emerges as the winner.

Each player is dealt a hand of cards, and the objective is to form completed sets by either having two cards with the same picture or by connecting pictures. When a player completes a set, they earn a point.

To determine the dealer, a game of rock, paper, scissors is played, and the next player in line draws a card from the dealer's hand. The player who draws the card checks if any pictures line up in their hand, and if they have a completed set, they place the cards in front of them. Then, the next player draws a card from the first player's hand, and this process continues in order.

Players take turns drawing cards and checking for completed sets until everyone's hands are empty. At the end of the game, the player with the most points wins.

Additionally, the game introduces a special card called the "Toilet Card." If a player has two Toilet Cards, they can select a collection of another player's cards, known as a "poo collection," and "flush" it away. The flushed poo and toilet cards are placed together on the side. The Toilet Cards can be used at any time, but if a player holds onto them, another player might take them. Collecting all four types of Toilet Cards grants a bonus of four points.

The card game has a theme centered around a crane machine and collecting prizes. The players take on different roles: Chief Officer, Crane Player, Cheerleaders, and Referees. Players aim to accumulate the most prizes and coins to achieve different titles at the end of the game.

To set up the game, the cards are folded and placed on the game board along with a prize. Each player starts with three coin cards. The Chief Officer, Crane Player, and remaining players are assigned their roles.

During gameplay, the Chief Officer gives one of their coin cards to the Crane Player, who acts as the crane operator. The Crane Player uses their hand, mimicking a crane arm, to try and grab prizes from the game board. The Chief Officer verbally guides the Crane Player on the movement of the crane arm.

The Crane Player closes their eyes and moves the crane arm based on the Chief Officer's instructions. The Chief Officer can give instructions to move right, go forward, and drop a prize. The Crane Player tries to grab the prize using their thumb and index finger. If successful, they must rely on intuition to return the prize to the game box without opening their eyes.

If the Crane Player successfully returns the prize to the game box, the Chief Officer receives the prize. However, if the Crane Player fails to grab the prize or drops it midway, their team's turn is over. The game continues with each player taking turns as the Chief Officer and the Crane Player moving to the left.

After everyone has completed a round as the Chief Officer, the second round begins with new assignments. After each player has had three turns as the Chief Officer, the game ends. The player with the highest number of special prizes becomes the "Best Player," while the player with the most coins becomes the "Best Crane Player." The player with the most prizes and coins earns the title of "Crane Master."

Strategically collect friends to throw a successful party. Players aim to gather all their friends and score points while competing to be the first to reach 20 points.

At the start of the game, the kigushun cards are shuffled and placed face down. Each player is dealt four cards, and the remaining cards are kept within reach. Players carefully examine their cards and consider which friends they want to bring together.

During their turn, players add a card to their hand and place one card face up in play. The next player can either draw from the deck or take a card from the face-up cards. This process continues until someone is ready to "party."

When a player has five cards and believes all their friends have been gathered, they declare "Agari!" and reveal their cards. Each card is checked to see if all the friends have been collected. Successfully completing the party without any missing friends earns points. Each friend collected is worth one point.

The game continues until a player accumulates 20 points, at which point they become the winner.

Players compete to place their figurines on a precarious tower based on dice rolls. The game requires dexterity, strategy, and a bit of luck. The player who successfully places all their figurines without causing the tower to collapse wins the game.

Gameplay: Players take turns rolling a die and placing their figurines on the corresponding colored levels of the tower. If the tower wobbles and figurines fall off during a player's turn, that player is responsible for picking them up.

Goal: The goal of the game is to be the first player to get rid of all their figurines. Players strategically choose where to place their figurines on the tower, aiming to create a stable structure while also trying to make it more challenging for the next player. Rolling the die determines which level to place a figurine or perform other actions.

Players test their stacking skills and hand-eye coordination. The objective is to carefully add colored pieces to a swinging tree structure without causing it to collapse. Each player takes turns rolling the die and placing pieces according to the color rolled. The game incorporates strategy as players must decide where to add their pieces, considering the stability of the tower and the availability of colors. The tension escalates as the tower becomes more precarious with each new addition. The player who topples the tower by causing the pieces to fall loses the game.

The Battle of Dulcimer Strait is a 2 player sci-fi tactical wargame where players go head-to-head in battle using 7 ships, skill cards and dice rolls. Games can last between 30 to 60 min depending on game play options. Each ship uses specific movements and rules letting the player develop different strategies for different situations.

Two factions had been at peace for over a century. New leadership has lead to strained relations between the two. War had been looming for many years. It would take only one small spark to begin a war known as the Broken Alliance War. On May, 24th 2463 a small squadron of the United Planets of Earth fighter ships and bombers were escorting the USS  Zumwalt 3 DDG(X)1011 Battleship through the small corridor of space known as the Dulcimer Strait. They met the Villtarian Federation Battleship, The Grawling. It was flanked by fighter ships and bombers of the royal Villtarian military. Both sides were fairly matched and as for who fired the first shot is still up to debate.

Number of Players: 2
Ages: 14 and up
Playing Time: 30 - 60 min
Complexity: 5.5 on a 9 scale

Game Components:
• One 16-page, color instructions booklet
• One 14" x 22" game board
• 2 Player control boards
• 1 Militia control board
• 7 United Planets of Earth ships
• 7 Villtarian Federation ships
• 1 Militia ship
• 2 six sided dice
• 67 red cubes
• 36 Yellow cubes
• 6 Blue cubes
• 36 Skill Cards
• 4 wingman stands
• 1 player sequence reference sheet

Game art, design and concept: Justin Wade Gibson
Watch for Kickstarter soon

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
Experience unparalleled Napoleonic wargaming as you step onto the legendary battlefield of Waterloo on June 18, 1815. Witness the strategic brilliance of Wellington as he establishes a formidable defensive line along the Ohain Road, with key strongholds at Hougoumont, La Haye Sainte, and Papelotte farms. Command renowned leaders like Emperor Napoleon, Marshal Ney, General Kellerman, and General D'Erlon, and even alter history with the arrival of Marshal Grouchy, providing support to Napoleon's forces. With a high-quality battle map, unit displays, icons, and easy-to-follow game instructions, customize your army, unleash your strategies, and embark on an exhilarating gaming adventure.

Take command over 100 units per side and experience realistic Napoleonic-era battles like never before with our Waterloo game. Our game is perfect for anyone looking to enjoy an immersive strategy game without needing an in-depth knowledge of the era. You'll be able to easily apply tactics and benefit from an outstanding gameplay experience. Feel the responsibility over your army as you make decisions in this thrilling recreation of Napoleon Total War.

Game content

The Waterloo ready to build edition offers an extensive range of content designed to enhance your gaming experience. Here are the key components included:


     Campaign Map: Immerse yourself in the epic battles of Waterloo with a meticulously crafted campaign map. Measuring 160 x 90 cm (62.99 x 35.43 inches), this high-quality map is constructed from frontlit waterproof material that is both durable and easy to clean.



     Unit Displays and Stickers: Bring your armies to life with customizable unit displays and stickers. The unit displays, crafted in polystyrene plastic, provide a sturdy platform for showcasing your troops. Utilize the waterproof stickers to easily mark and differentiate units, ensuring clarity during gameplay. These stickers are designed to withstand prolonged use, ensuring durability throughout your gaming sessions.



     Dice: Enhance the element of chance with four specially designed dice. Each die is equipped with stickers that correspond to the game mechanics, adding an extra layer of excitement to your strategic decisions.



     Damage Icons and Timeline Icons: Keep track of the unfolding battle with the included damage icons and timeline icons. These stickers allow you to monitor unit status and track the progression of events on the battlefield, contributing to a dynamic and immersive gaming experience.



     Extensive Unit Variety: Experience the full breadth of the Waterloo conflict with a comprehensive collection of 247 units. This includes:



         118 French army units
         51 British army units
         32 Dutch army units
         10 Brunswick corps units
         36 Prussian army units
         10 Generals
    


Gaming time: up to 4 hours and more. Play 1vs1 or 2vs 2.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Fight through three waves of enemy ships, preserving your own hull integrity, upgrading your ship from salvaged enemy craft.

Place cards representing enemies and choose a path through, taking on each enemy in turn. Various strengths of enemy craft, with predetermined attack patterns.

Each battle takes place using dice, player choosing one of two weapon options (missile or lasers) and rolling dice to attack, either matching a pattern or rolling a dice sum greater than the enemy shield value. Then the AI enemy responds and the pattern repeats until one is destroyed.

Le Rouge et Le Noir is a card game for 2 or 4 players that can be played in 15 minutes. This is a modern, smoother arrangement of a traditional French card game Le Truc from the 16th century.

Your goal is to win two tricks and score as many as points you can. On your turn, you play a card from your hand of three cards with its face up to win, or play face down to call a double (by bluffing, perhaps). To earn more points, it’s the key to make smart decisions on when to call a double, pass, or take!

A blackjack-based gameplay. Get an item card and build up your team through draft, similar to blackjack. Whoever end up the closest to 21 will accomplish the quest but be careful, going above or under can have dire consequences.

Quests have reward values in ★. First hero to accumulate 5★ wins the game!

Game exist in Red, Blue and Green versions but the rules are the same just each version has a different theme.

This supplement for Frostgrave brings a new dimension to games of Frostgrave, allowing players to lead expeditions into the wilderness north of the city, searching for undiscovered ruins and facing the perils of the Wildwoods.

For years, spellcasters have flocked to the Frozen City, braving the perils of its ruins in the hunt for relics and forgotten magic. While the city still holds many such secrets, newly arrived wizards must delve deeper and fight harder to make their mark. A few turn from the city, seeking instead the remote settlements that surrounded ancient Felstad. Such expeditions are not without their own dangers, however – in the vast forests of the Wildwoods, death lurks in the empty cookpot and torn waterskin, in the cold of night and the looming storm, and in the teeth and claws of the beast behind the next tree…

This supplement for Frostgrave takes players beyond the ruins with rules for running expeditions in search of secret places and forgotten treasures in the dark Wildwoods. New soldiers and magic items may improve wizards' chances of survival, but dwindling supplies, the hostile environment, and terrifying new creatures will push them to their limits in a range of scenarios set in these deep, foreboding forests.

-description from publisher's website

boardgame: Bolt Action: Campaign – Italy: Tough Gut
description:
A new supplement for Bolt Action focusing on the later Italian Campaign, following on from Soft Underbelly, adding new units, scenarios, and special rules.

In Italy, the fight continues!

As the Allies push north up the Italian Peninsula, they have encountered heavy resistance from the Axis and their multitude of fortified and defensive lines, slowing the allied advance to a crawl. The 'soft underbelly' of Europe proved to be false, instead it was a 'tough gut'.

This supplement for Bolt Action, and the counterpart to Soft Underbelly, focuses on the battles ranging from the Gustav Line in 1944 all the way to those of the Gothic Line in 1945, with the breakouts at Anzio and Monte Cassino of particular focus. Containing a host of scenarios to refight these famous battles, along with new units, special rules, and Theatre Selectors, this book contains everything players need to bring an end to the war and liberate Italy.

-description from publisher's website

boardgame: Down the Chimney Dice & Card Game
description:
Players roll dice to collect Gift Cards worth different values; at the end of five rounds the player with the highest total value Gift Cards is the winner.

The game includes its own dice tower and specialty dice. Each of the dice has six different holiday symbols on them: gift, holly, candy cane, snowflake, star, and coal. Players are each dealt three Gift cards, which have different quantities of the symbols on them -- the more symbols, the higher the value. The active player drops the six dice down the tower, then tries to match the rolled symbols to the cards in their hand. If there is a match with a card, those dice are set aside and the remainder re-rolled. Also, the card that matched is set aside for later scoring. Each player may roll the dice up to three times on their turn. If a coal symbol is rolled, that die remains on the table, and if the player accumulates three coal symbols, their turn is over and they may not score any cards.

After the third roll, all other player may attempt to match the symbols on the set-aside dice to those on their Gift cards, and those Gift cards are kept by that player, so everyone is potentially accumulating points. If three coal symbols are rolled, no player scores any points that turn. If the active player rolls three of a kind of any symbol (except coal) they may decide to take an Action card, which must be played immediately. The Actions are 'Nice' (take an extra roll of all six dice), 'Naughty' (next player loses their next turn), and 'Gift Exchange' (the active player may swap one of their Gift cards with a higher-value Gift card of another player, or from the Gift card deck).

When the active player's turn ends, play passes clockwise to the next player. Once all players have had five turns (five rounds), the game ends and each player's collected Gift cards are totaled. The player with the highest total wins.

This game is a tie-in to the publisher's "Elf on a Shelf" system.

The game that turns the garden upside down! Cunning rabbits, take advantage of the gardeners' siesta to eat cabbage from the vegetable garden without being spotted! Attentive gardeners, will you be able to find the rabbits hidden under the cabbages?

An asymmetrical game of hide-and-seek in the vegetable garden

Quick set up: open the box and play!

Very simple rules for a subtly strategic game :

At each turn, the rabbits eat the cabbage under which they are hidden and move in any direction. The farmers then move around trying to catch the rabbits hidden under the cabbages.

—description from the publisher (translated)

Le jeu qui met le jardin sens dessus de chou ! Lapins rusés, profitez de la sieste des jardiniers pour manger les choux du potager sans vous faire repérer ! Jardiniers attentifs, parviendrez vous à retrouver les lapins cachés sous les choux ?

Un jeu de cache-cache asymétrique dans le potager

Mise en place rapide : ouvrez la boîte et jouez !

Des règles très simples pour un jeu subtilement stratégique

—description from the publisher
family: Components: Game Box Used In Play

description:
Take control of a football/soccer club and battle for glory in the league and cup! Can you guide them up from Division 3 to the very top? Can you eventually become the champions of Europe? Buy and sell players, develop youths and improve your stadium along the way.

Draft your squad
You’ll start in Division 3 with a mediocre squad of mostly ageing players. Can you shape them into promotion contenders in your first season? Will you supplement the squad with youngsters or dabble in the transfer market?

Place your staff
Every week you’ll need to decide how to best use your staff members. Will you get to work on the training pitch, or develop youths, or scout your next opponents, or build club facilities, or, or…there are so many options but not enough time in the week! A smart manager will need to deploy their staff strategically throughout the season.

Play your fixtures!

description: National Park Trivia Board Game is a trivia board game where players will be answering questions about National Parks' history, landmarks, wildlife and more. boardgame: A Quinta Série que habita em nós
description:
A Quinta Série is a fun and irreverent festive game that will guarantee hours of fun and laughter with friends based on jokes with double meanings, "fifth grade" level, which have never ceased to inhabit the hearts and minds of those who call themselves adults .

Each round, one player in the group will randomly pick a situation card with a gap to fill in, while the other players will pick a card with the famous “fifth grade humor” to fill in the gap.
The funniest combination wins the round!

But be careful, this is a game for OLDER PEOPLE, with a bold sense of humor and who like an infamous joke, ideal to play with joking friends.

Get ready to laugh out loud and be surprised by the combinations of cards that can appear in each round!

"RouYouJin" is a fairy tale-themed game.

Now, in explaining the features of "RouYouJin", let's explain the outline of the play of this game, not the text of the 9 types of villager cards.

First of all, the goal of the game is to increase the number of victory points and have the most. Victory points are sheep or gold.
1 sheep is 1 point, 1 gold is also 1 point, but if 1 sheep is converted to gold, it will be doubled to 2 gold.

Set the order card and the villager card and put them out on the back.
First, turn only the order cards into a table and arrange them so that the number on the left is small and the number on the right is large.

The rightmost villager in the row becomes a wolf.
Villager cards other than wolf are turned face up.
The number of society is written on the villager card, and if it is greater than or equal to the number of society on the left villager card and less than or equal to the number of society on the right villager card, the villager card is in the correct position.
Villagers in the correct row will trigger effects based on their role.
Some villagers have text effects even if they are not in the correct position.
As a result, you can collect sheep that are victory points, convert sheep into gold, or steal other people's sheep.

To win the game, your villagers must be in the correct position in the line.
It requires a high degree of reading on your part and a bit of luck.
The reason is that you need to combine the order card in your hand and the three villager cards well.
Aim for the front of the line, or for the back, or whether your plan will succeed... it's all up to you!

That said, it's basically an orthodox card game.
It is almost impossible to get lost in the play itself!
The slightly complicated rules and the short playing time of about 20 minutes will give you the joy of playing a dense game!

In addition, although it is basically an interpersonal battle with 4 to 5 people, it is fully equipped with rules for 1 to 3 people including automatons. Therefore, it is a game that is not easily influenced by the number of players.

boardgame: Shake That City: Wild Nights Micro-Expansion
description: Wild Nights adds a set of new Wild Cubes to the mix. Add these 8 white cubes one at a time to the Cube Shaker for the first 8 rounds. When dispersed into the 3 x 3 pattern, players can assign them to match the color of any other cube also in the pattern. All players have a choice to add the Wild Cubes to their chosen color. Choose carefully because Wild Cubes that come out of the shaker are not returned! Includes a Cube Shaker themed to this expansion.

Put your reflexes to the test in the Mini Brands Grocery Grab head-to-head card game! It's going to take your keen eye and quick reflexes to win this game. Flip cards and look for matching colors or brands. When you see one, be the first player to grab the Mini Brands Collectable and score one token. Keep flipping and grabbing to score enough tokens to win!

This Mini Brands card game includes your favorite grocery store products straight from the shelf such as Kraft Macaroni and Cheese and Country Time Lemonade but in teeny tiny form. Hershey's Syrup - your favorite topping to put in milk, on ice cream, or just eat by the spoonful- comes in the smallest form you've ever seen! This game is fun for kids and adults alike. It makes the perfect stocking stuffer, fidget toy, or gift item for the collector in your life. Plus, it might just make your kids excited to go on that grocery store errand with you next time.

Dive into the realms of Icelandic and international music and film culture with this exciting board game. The game features 700 thought-provoking questions and puzzles that challenge your knowledge and critical thinking skills. With one beautifully crafted game board and 10 game pieces, players can participate in teams or as solo competitors.

The game mechanics are straightforward - players take turns asking each other questions and solving intriguing puzzles that span a variety of categories, including general trivia, quote excerpts, finish the song lyric, multiple choice, true or false, and even fun activities like singing and acting. Aim to reach the final spot on the board to emerge as the champion. Once you secure victory, you're crowned the star, a symbol of your triumph and knowledge.

This crown can be worn to showcase your achievement to friends and family, signifying that you're truly gifted in navigating the fascinating world of music and film culture. A perfect choice for family game nights or social gatherings with friends, this game offers endless hours of fun and entertainment. The game also includes easy-to-follow instructions to ensure a seamless playing experience.

An early British two-player action game, simulating Association Football (Soccer) Penalty Kicks.  The game consists of two opposing plywood figures: the penalty taker and a goalkeeper in a wooden goal frame.  The object is to score most goals by shooting the ball into the goal using the Striker's spring-loaded leg. The goalkeeper attempts to save the shot by moving across the goal mouth along a horizontal wire guide.
A simple game typical of the period, it dates from the early 1950's.

description: Maid Mafia KY: Battle for Yokohama is a standalone expansion to Maid Mafia Kai, adding in a new Factional Conflict mode.

Originally published in 2009 and designed by Hisashi Hayashi (Yokohama, Trains, Metro X), String Railway is a 20-minute game in which players create their own map directly onto the table, complete with borders, mountains and rivers, then build atop it a network of railways, all made out of string.

The sequel String Railway: Transport (now renamed String Railway: Cubed) combined the premise of String Railway with the weighty decision-making of games like Railways of the World, producing a mid-weight 45-minute game. In the game, players start with a map of stations already built and must balance the desire to upgrade their engines, and fulfill bespoke delivery contracts, with the need to quickly build a flexible train network that can transport goods to a range of destinations.

The String Railway Collection packs both of these standalone games into a single small-box package and features vibrant updated artwork, improved graphic design, and a refined ruleset.

boardgame: Journey Through the Center of the Earth
description:
Journey was originally designed to be included in an issue of Dragon magazine, but was rejected. Tom Wham revised it to be published as a giveaway for GaryCon XII.

In the game up to four explorers travel through a hollow Earth in search of treasures. Movement is from one "decision point" to the next; on a turn a player assigns dice sides to the arrows leaving their current point as they wish, then roll a die which determines which one is used. If a hazard/reward area is entered  the player rolls on a table to determine what they gain or lose.

Players cannot exist on the same space and so bump each other, but the player being bumped decides where they move to.

Players are each given two random special abilities at the start of the game.

The game ends when all players have exited the map or when the treasure deck runs out; at that time the player with the greatest total value of treasure wins.

boardgame: 1940: Swallows of Death – Moroccan Troops in the Battle of France
description:
In August 1939, France’s Armée d’Afrique mobilized ten Moroccan infantry regiments; all of them would fight in the coming campaign in France, three of them forming the elite 1st Moroccan Infantry Division. The “Swallows of Death” already carried a fearsome fighting reputation from the trenches of the First World War, and they would only add to their black swallow insignia’s legend in the Second.

1940: Swallows of Death is an expansion book for our Panzer Grenadier: 1940 The Fall of France, focused on the exploits of Moroccan troops in the 1940 campaign. You get 30 new scenarios for 1940: The Fall of France, plus 88 new die-cut, silky smooth pieces showing the Moroccans in their own special color scheme plus elite French recon troops, horsed machine-gun units and the unusual WT15 tank destroyer. The crack horsed cavalry of the Spahis Marocains get their own pieces and scenarios, too.

Scenario design is by Philippe Léonard, with the book organized into six chapters with battle games for each to link them together. And as you expect in an Avalanche Press book, plenty of historical background. You’ll need 1940 The Fall of France and Road to Dunkirk to play the scenarios.

description:
In General Palmer each player has their own slightly different board on which they build track and stations. This is done by means of action cards; players start with some in hand and draft a new one each round. Each player also plays a card each round which gives them an action and also permits a certain number of track tiles to be built. Actions can include buying trains and building stations of various types.

The cards to be drafted also determine a random event each round and a location on the boards for that event.

The right to draft first (and possibly choose the event location next round) is auctioned off.

After using their actions and building track players run their trains for revenue.

The game ends when the deck runs out. Players then score for connecting Denver to Salt Lake City and also for eight out of the ten possible victory conditions; each player knows at least one of these, but some are hidden until the end.

While the game was designed for up to five players, there are six boards and the rules do explain how to play with six if you really want to.

Learn in 3 minutes and play 1000 times!

Once you get used to playing, you can also create an original deck by combining occupations.

Game modes are:

Use different game modes depending on the number of people playing.

Dish of Spurs (DoS) is a set of miniature rules for skirmish actions during the Border Reivers period.

In DoS players lead bands of raiders or the forces out to stop them. Each unit gets two actions per turn with which they may move, shoot, herd cattle, break down doors, etc. Captains confer bonus actions on their troops and heroes inspire their fellows to greater bravery.

Actions generally require a die roll to succeed, and all dice are normal d6. To shoot for example, you must roll higher than your shoot score. To rally, you must roll higher than your morale.

The game is scenario driven, with victory varying according to circumstance. A raider may win by getting a certain number of cattle off the board, the local marshal wins by preventing them. Or perhaps two clans fight over a wedding, one seeking to prevent the union, the other to see it carried through.

The game includes an optional hidden movement rule which while slowing play adds a lot of nuance to the tactics of each side.

-description from designer

Gameplay is turn based. Each player will, in order, play only one Play Card from their hand and resolve the actions as noted on the card played. The game ends either by successfully resolving the “Laser Katana of Judgement” card, or if all cards have been played.

The heavenly world and the demon world were once one human world, called Earth.

Realizing the limitations of the Earth, humans abandoned their homeland and built a giant habitat satellite "Noah" in satellite orbit and moved there.

However, there were those who were left behind on earth.

Thousands of years later, the people on earth called Noah the "Heavenly Realm," while the people of Noah called the earth the "Demon Realm.

Players are in charge of either the Heavenly Realm or the Demon Realm, and choose four types from six unique units to form their own army.

Each unit grows and becomes more advanced in battle, and its abilities change as well.

Each unit is commanded using "stance tiles," which look like mahjong tiles, to determine its stance, such as attack, defense, or balanced.

The reading of these "stance tiles" creates a heated psychological battle!

The player must deploy his/her army on the map, making full use of item cards with different effects in the Heavenly Realm and the Demon Realm,

The player who destroys the enemy's stronghold, the "Tower," wins.

description:
A game about how to make a video game
In this game, players play the role of game coders to create and produce games.
There are 4 different consoles in the game, and each player must make 1 game for each of these game consoles.
Coding is done by code cards and each player must complete his coding operation by putting the cards together correctly.
At the end of each coding stage, the player gets points that can be used to purchase different characters from the game market and add them to the game monitor.
The final stage of the game includes testing the game codes. Each player must measure the correctness and accuracy of the codes he used in his turn. In the meantime, some wrong codes may cause bugs or even glitches and cause serious problems in the coding process.
Finally, the game ends by completing the process of making 3 games (1 less than the number of players) and the player who has the most number of correct codes and characters added to the game monitor will win the game.

The Midnight Market is a big event in the middle of the night where evil spirits of rivers and mountains walk around. Now, now, tonight, too, unusual guests are coming to the market for special products. You are a merchant who dreams of becoming a millionaire. Your job is to sell your goods at the highest possible price. But beware, in a pitch-dark marketplace, merchants don't know what each other has in store. Some unscrupulous merchants may take advantage of this and participate in the bidding with the aim of lowering the price even if they do not have the product that the customer is looking for. Will you be able to win the midnight market and become a millionaire?

Yarr Matey! Rumors be floating about, with tales of great fortune said to lie on Treasure Island! Be the first to make it there and you’ll be rich beyond compare! But beware, you aren’t alone on this journey, as it seems some other scallywags have decided to hunt for the treasure as well. Keelhauled is a deck-building game in which 2-8 players take on the role of pirate captains to see who can be the last captain standing. During the course of the game, players will gain weapon, crew, tool, and ship cards from the cargo deck to build their decks. Along with gaining cards, players will draw cards from the seas deck to represent scenarios that take place on their voyage throughout the game, as well as fighting deadly sea monsters from the monster deck.

Minikin Berry Brawl is a fast-paced, family-friendly area-control game set in a fantasy forest theme.

Play as "minikin" with cute miniatures and roll your dice to move and control biomes. Gain redberries (currency) and Brawl for Golden Berries. Get 6 Golden Berries to win!

The game allows for plenty of replayability with randomized tiles, Power Berry cards, secret Quests cards to fulfill, and an ability dice that can turn the game around.

boardgame: Dark Matter (fan expansion for Arkham Horror Card Game): Science Expansion
description:
Save the solar system from a dark and ancient primordial force, using new tools of Science! Play as Kate Winthrop, The Scientist, purchase new and real Science tools and instruments, and explore the scientific achievements of humanity across the solar system in Dark Matter – Science Expansion!

To celebrate Dark Matter Day (October 31st), Axolotl, Dr Xavier Bertou (Head of the particle and radiation detection laboratory at the Centro Atomico Bariloche), Levi Thornton (The Cardboard-naut), and Mint Tea Fan have collaborated to bring a new 20 card science-themed expansion to the fan-made Dark Matter Campaign!

The Scientist:

Researched Cards:

Frontiers of Space:
This new expansion also adds 5 new scanning cards to shuffle into the finale of Dark Matter, Starfall. These cards offer a chance to uncover some of humanity’s greatest achievements in space exploration.

Hint: You may want to purchase a few Science cards if you are planning to play with these new scanning cards.

Evidence: Hush Money is murder mystery crime-solving game, combining physical components and an online progression system. Answer questions on the Online Detective Portal to advance through the game and uncover the murderer.

description: This is your farm where you grow different types of vegetables and fruits as seasons changes it's time to harvest them. But wait there are weeds growing up as the seasons passing by. its your job to clear them all and harvest your crops properly. good luck.

Step deep into the shadow for only then you might find what you seek. Embrace the darkness sewn from flesh and become one with the truth. Explore, grow and learn, loot, and gather. Escape the death’s blade by a hair and you might be rewarded. Survive, combine and, through great effort, lead your mortal to victory against both horrors of the world and the ones within. If you don’t like the feeling of a knife being stabbed into your back, only trust yourself. At all times. Your decisions will be your ultimate weapon. Wield the artifacts of the fallen gods and unravel the history behind the curtains of massacre. Make sacrifices along the way and rise. Be cunningly humble and fearlessly greedy. Forcing your own playstyle against the game or your fellow players will be the pillar of your ascent. Or your downfall.
