August 10th, 2021 - At 11:10am the world’s first commercial space station, the Delta One, lost power and all communication with ground control for 40 minutes. During the blackout, a mysterious accident claimed the life of Sandra Ivey, CEO of Astro IV, the multi-billion dollar space tech company responsible for launching the station.

There were five other crew members aboard the Delta One and none claim to know what happened to Sandra while the power was out. Sandra’s former chief of security, Solomon Hart, was sent skyward to investigate, but only given 24 hours aboard the Delta One to make his case. Though it was officially ruled an accident, he suspects there’s more to this story than meets the eye.

In order to solve this cold case murder mystery game you’ll need to find 3 separate clues. First prove the space station was sabotaged, then prove that Sandra Ivey was murdered, and finally pin her killer and bring them to justice. With 3 different mysteries to solve in order to play the game, this case will challenge your investigative abilities.

To get past each step of the case you’ll need to visit an online answer page that requires you to prove you cracked it. If you don’t have the answer right you can keep investigating without being spoiled by the right answers.

Can you help Solomon find out what really happened to Sandra?

-description from publisher's website

Bernadette Octavia the Witch has been cursed by the twelve Zodiac demons. She now aim to defeat the red dragon Jamil Hendock to acquire his book of curse removal. Hiring adventurer to help her achieve her goal, so did the dragon. A battle of epic proportion ensue.

Skullbites is a tricktaking game where the loser (lowest card) of the trick get damaged by all the other player's cards. Each player is given two Adventurer cards with an health value on them and a passive skill, one is played face up and the other is hidden face down. The two camp bosses are hidden among those adventurers. Once an adventurer is defeated, a player reveal their hidden adventurer and continue the game with the newly revealed character.

When losing a trick, you can block incoming damage by exhausting one of the three elemental shields chip each player has (wind, water and fire) but each shield has a one time use so be careful when to use it!

Some storytellers will help bring balance, but ultimately the war won't end until one of the two camp leader is defeated.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Roll & move game described in “A Compact Encyclopedia of Games Games Games for People of All Ages” by Mary Hohenstein.

Players move about a winding path, trying to move between a village & an inn three times. Certain die rolls grant players tokens, which they can spend on extra movements.

Players double their dice roll on the second leg of the race, & may eliminate others by landing on them during the third leg.

The sample board has multiple paths, but is too small to use; players are supposed to draw their own board.

—user summary

First expansion to Piratas, only for its 2nd edition.
9 ship cards, ghost pirates and cursed pirates.

"Primeira expansão para o Piratas!, só compatível com sua 2ª Edição.

Nesta expansão são inseridas embarcações, um novo tipo de carta que tem efeitos especiais que ajudam o seu dono a vencer. Além das embarcações (são 9 cartas), também são inseridas novas cartas de tripulação, o Pirata Fantasma e o Pirata Amaldiçoado.

Essa expansão é modular e você pode jogar com as embarcações e os novos Piratas, como pode jogar só com um deles."

Dominion At Dawn is an immersive two-player strategical print-and-play wargame, where each step is a gamble for survival and supremacy. Takes place in Asteria, a ravaged planet where a devastating conflict between two formidable factions, Tycho Consortium and the Kepler Corporation rages on. It is an extraordinary strategic game that challenges players to outwit and outmaneuver their opponents in a thrilling race for dominance.

Spanning across several battlefields, players take turns deftly positioning their battalions with precision, rolling dice to determine their strength in combat. The victor, who captures or eliminates all enemy battalions first, emerges triumphant from this breathtaking crucible of strategy.

With its intuitive mechanics and meticulously playtested balance, the game provides an engaging and challenging experience for two players, with each typical match lasting between 30 to 60 minutes. As you engage in this thrilling clash of strategies, every turn brings a new surprise and the ever-present risk of defeat.

Launch into this intricate spectacle of aggression and dominance, and decide the fate of Asteria. Let the battle ensue - may the best commander prevail in Dominion At Dawn!

-description from publisher

boardgame: Krimi al Dente: Göttliche Antike – von wegen unsterblich
description:
Göttliche Antike is a Mystery Dinner game set in ancient Greece. Players play the roles of Greek gods and godesses. Zeus' wife Hera has been murdered, and all the deities are suspects, including Zeus himself.
Players gather for a dinner party. Over the course of several 30 minute rounds, they will have to find the player who is lying. In each round, new information becomes available, until at last, the truth comes to light (or not). Aside from trying to find the murderer, each player has side tasks to fulfill that decide their final score.

boardgame: Pokémon TCG: Sword & Shield – Crown Zenith
description:
Experience the full beauty of the Pokémon TCG with Crown Zenith’s Galarian Gallery! Witness the ghoulish gaze of Hisuian Zoroark VSTAR under a full moon, observe Darkrai VSTAR over a moonlit lake as it wields the power of nightmares, and take a stroll with the ambitious Cynthia as she researches the myths and legends of Sinnoh on these special alternate-art cards.

–description from the publisher

boardgame: Krimi al Dente: Bunte 90er – Tödliche Geschmacksverirrung
description:
Julia Pfeffer, inventor of a weight-loss drink, has been murdered. The murderer is among you - can you find out who did it?

Players gather for a dinner party. Over the course of several 30 minute rounds, they will have to find the player who is lying. In each round, new information becomes available, until at last, the truth comes to light (or not). Aside from trying to find the murderer, each player has side tasks to fulfill that decide their final score.

Pokémon ex are back and stronger than ever!

Pokémon ex originally debuted almost 20 years ago in EX Ruby & Sapphire and were a mainstay in the Pokémon TCG until EX Power Keepers in 2007. Now they're back in Scarlet & Violet, and while they may look a bit different, they maintain the essence of the original mechanic—powerful two-Prize Pokémon that can also evolve from one-Prize Pokémon. Pokémon ex look similar to the more recent Pokémon-EX from the Black & White Series and the XY Series, but Pokémon-EX were almost always Basic Pokémon, while Pokémon ex can appear at any stage of Evolution!

—description from the publisher

In the year 1890, Brazil has just become a provisional republic. One of its goals is to keep the market thriving, and for that, goods from all over the country need to circulate more easily and in larger quantities.

Thus, a plan was created for the development of railway lines in Brazil, with the objective of connecting the new railway routes with the existing ones, as well as utilizing well-functioning river routes.

The plan sparked interest from many companies, which led to significant investment and internal and external interferences. However, despite being one of the boldest plans that could theoretically improve freight transportation in the country, it fell into disuse. 1890: National Railways Plan is a game that brings to the table the experience of this plan, applied over three decades.

In this game that blends elements of 18xx with euro games, we are investors buying and selling shares of companies interested in investing in Brazil's railway lines. The stock market is constantly changing as it reacts to players' interactions with the market itself, as well as the development or lack thereof, of the companies.

These companies, in turn, will buy trains, construct routes, collect revenues, and pay dividends to investors or not. Such crucial decisions will be made by their respective presidents, meaning the player who holds the highest percentage of the company in question.

The winner will be the one who can best manage the companies and manipulate the stock market, as the individual with the most money and the highest-value portfolio will be the victor of 1890.

boardgame: Pokémon TCG: Scarlet & Violet – Paldea Evolved
description:
Evolve New Strategies with Powerful Abilities

Pokémon Abilities can make or break a deck, and there are plenty of new ones to look forward to in Scarlet & Violet—Paldea Evolved. Get your game off to a strong start with Squawkabilly ex’s Squawk and Seize Ability, which allows you to discard your hand and draw six cards. Squawk and Seize is reminiscent of Dedenne-GX’s Dedechange Ability or Crobat V’s Dark Asset Ability, with two key differences—you don't have to play the Pokémon from your hand to activate this Ability, but it can be only be used during your first turn. If you’re looking for some explosive Energy acceleration, Forretress ex can flood your board with up to five Basic  Energy at a cost—when you use its Exploding Energy Ability, Forretress ex is Knocked Out, giving up two Prize cards. Pelipper and Tinkaton can lend a hand, too, with the former helping you search out or recover a Supporter card and the latter drawing you three cards a turn.

—description from the publisher

GORKBULL, the horde invasion, is a miniature game for 1 player. Through several scenarios, linked in the form of a campaign, you will face creatures managed by the game itself with the aim of finishing in the most heroic way possible and developing your characters by following a gripping narrative. The simplicity of the game, thanks to a unique and ingenious system, makes it possible to keep the tactical interest of this type of game, but also to reinvent the style with a very original sequencing of actions for even more strategy. The management of activation cards and your power tokens will offer pleasant tactical choices. The objective is to combine ease of access with great depth of play. Let the adventure begin!

—description from the publisher (translated)

GORKBULL, l'invasion de la horde, est un jeu de figurines pour 1 joueur. À travers plusieurs scénarios, reliés sous forme de campagne, vous allez affronter des créatures gérées par le jeu lui-même dans le but de terminer de la manière la plus héroïque possible et faire évoluer vos personnages en suivant une trame narrative prenante. La simplicité du jeu, grâce à un système unique et ingénieux, permet de garder l’intérêt tactique de ce type de jeu, mais aussi de réinventer le style avec un séquençage des actions très original pour toujours plus de stratégie. La gestion des cartes d’activations et de vos jetons de pouvoirs offriront des choix tactiques plaisants. L’objectif est d’allier une simplicité d’accès à une grande profondeur de jeu. Que l’aventure commence !

—description from the publisher

description:
Karl Gloringer, owner of a jazz club in 1920s Berlin, has been murdered. The murderer is among you - can you find out who did it?

Players gather for a dinner party. Over the course of several 30 minute rounds, they will have to find the player who is lying. In each round, new information becomes available, until at last, the truth comes to light (or not). Players compete as a team with over 100 campaign paths. Each playthrough is a unique cinematic experience offering endless replayability. Players make strategic decisions and interact with each other to achieve their group and secret individual objectives, some of which involve betrayal, making for an unpredictable gameplay experience.

—description from the designer

In Quest Inc. you are an NPC in an electronic RPG game that owns a mission distribution company. Your plan is to make profit through the quest industry.

It's that old story, people need to solve problems and they won't mind paying a few gold coins to solve it. And that's where you come in! Your company will take on the mission to receive the treasure, but for that you'll need to hire the bravest (and why not the cheapest) heroes, after all, the money left over is yours!

Furthermore, no one will question if some valuable items rescued by the heroes ended up in your hands. “It's part of the mission,” you'll reply. In case of your mission to profit more.

But be careful, competing companies will be around, trying to take the best opportunities before you!

The game is played in rounds, each round has 2 phases: in the first one players engage in an inverted auction to see who gets what quest and the money.

In the next phase players go to a location in the city, to pay to contract adventures (such as Rogues, Warriors, Mages or Clerics), each location has a special rule change.

By completing a quest a player receives loot cards that can give single use abilities and/or can be sold at the end of the game, and if this is part of a set the profit will be higher.

The game ends after some rounds (depending on the number of players), then the player with the most money wins.

Don't fool yourself! You must play your highest card in the round, but you only score if you bluff, or catch who is bluffing... or who made a mistake, as the numbers in this game tend to want to change value! Pay close attention not to get caught in the deception.

Each round each player needs to show the higher card of a hand of three. But this can be a bluff.

Each player simultaneously can check if another is bluffing or not, you get points for catching someone bluff, however they score points if they were telling the truth. If nobody bluffed the value of a successful bluff increase.

Number in constant change: every round some number can exchange value with another, become the higher or lower, add with another.. so the player needs to pay attention in order to not get confused.

The game ends when someone scores 12 or more points.

Each player in turn throws a number die and moves along his line of advance by the number thrown until he reaches the middle space 13. This space can only be reached if the corresponding number of free spaces is rolled. If, on the other hand, the player has thrown more eyes than there are spaces up to 13, he must wait until he rolls the right number. The player now has the right to roll two number dice and the movement die immediately after his last roll.
The player advances or retreats to the number that results from the calculation of the symbols and numbers on the three dice.
During the game, the play money regulations indicated at the edge of the game board apply.
If a figure hits a space occupied by the other player, it is moved back by one space.
From the spaces of numbers 76 to 91 you only play with the two number dice, from the number 92 to the end only with one number die. The number 100 can only be reached in the same way as the number 13.

—user summary

Players play a manager that receive set of orders and need to decide quickly (under 30 sec) each turn how to allocate and process them using different specialist resources in order to complete them and pass the finale approval into "done".

The gameplay mechanics is Based on the popular "Agile Kanban Board" used by professional teams worldwide, this game brings rapid decision-making, strategy, and a bit of luck.

End gamer and Duration:
The game consists of 10 rounds (each is about 2 minutes) or one sprint, the player with the highest number of "DONE" orders end of each sprint emerges as the winner, people play 3 to 5 sprints to determine the ultimate "Daily master."

Arm yourself with technical readouts, playable scenarios, an in-depth look at the weaponization of gift giving in the Draconis Combine, a glimpse into Clan Sea Fox’s customer service, an article on the importance of battlefield salvage, and more—all from elite BattleTech authors and recruits who have earned their stripes.

Racing Line is a Formula 1 inspired board game designed for everyone to enjoy, not just F1 fans.

Choose your car and compete across a Championship to score the most points and be crowned Racing Line Champion.

Use your incident cards to hinder your opponents or boost your chances.

The specially designed track offers excitement and jeopardy in equal measure.

So grab your family and friends and prepare to flip the board as the pressure mounts in the exciting world of Racing Line.

This is the 1st expansion for the Arctic Epic Through Ice & Snow. This expansion requires the base game to play, and includes 3 new game modules:


     Walking on ice: Relive the anguish of the arctic expeditions that abandoned their ship and tried to survive on foot. 



     Inuit Myths: Discover the Inuit mythology and feel the power of its legends. 



     Legendary Expeditions Lead the great explorers with their ships and historical events.


Besides these modules, the expansion includes 2 new solo scenarios: The mysterious epidemic & The voyage of Amundsen.

—description from the publisher

France Rails is the next incarnation that uses the same Turn Order mechanism found in the previous games in this series, namely Preußische Ostbahn, then German Railways and now Prussian Rails by Rio Grande.

French Rails provides 8 railroads, each having a special ability or limitation, based upon the history of that railroad. The names of the railroads are simply the name of the city where they begin the game. This is due to the ever shifting mergers, bankruptcies and takeovers found in the history. No single railroad kept the same name from beginning to end!

The game accommodates 3-5 players and takes less than an hour for experienced players. When all railroads have connected to at least two other railroads, or that is determined to be impossible, the game is over. The player with the most cash wins, as stocks are just tools to generate money.

Thott follows the life of Birgitte Olufsdatter Thott. She was a Danish and Swedish noble, landowner and royal county administrator. She was one of the biggest landowners in Scandinavia, and her estates played a role in politics in Sweden and Denmark.
Send your spies on secret missions to Sweden or Denmark, and return them safely home. Use the resources they acquired to earn influence and avoid being arrested and sentenced to death or life in monastery.

—description from the designer

This roll and write dice game about the Roman Empire follows players as they explore new territories, build settlements, and conquer enemies over 20 days (turns), after which points are tallied to determine the winner. Each turn, players roll dice to determine their actions and score points by collecting items, gaining technology, discovering lands, defeating enemies, and building wonders. The game requires strategic planning and risk-taking as players balance expanding their territory along with attacking enemy armies and ships.

A card/board game for 1-5 players with a non-linear episodic storyline that pits players as epic heroes vs a great evil. 1-4 Players may assume the role of mighty heroes while another player may take the reins of the great evil in an attempt to stop them. The heroes will go on random drawn side quests or engage in challenging main quests to thwart the advance of evil and save the world. The game has a RPG feel as you use dice to battle your way through dungeons, ruins and even full scale wars gaining level and powerful artifacts along the way in your journey to become a legend. Encounter special locations, build a retinue of helpful NPCs and see where your adventure takes you.

—description from the designer

Depredador Natural ("Natural Predator") is a trick-taking card game, where the players are predators looking for preys. You must lead hunts, compete for food, avoid invasive species, and take Refuge in a Biome when its gets dangerous.

In a less thematic way of speaking, you need to take tricks with colors cards (preys), avoiding black invaders cards (Negative Points). The catch is that you don't know what color preys will give you positive points, because only when you play your "Refuge" special card in the round, you will set the Biome you will live, scoring only that biome preys. I.e. You play your Refuge and take shelter in the Jungle Biome (Green), so, in the Scoring, you will only score positive points for Green Jungle Preys. Only one player can live in each biome in a round, so the timing to take refuge is crucial.

This game forms part of the "Selección Natural" Saga of Games, all sharing a theme of natural species, biomes, evolving, hunting, etc.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Long Shot: The Dice Game – Horse Set 7 (Trophy) Mini Expansion
description:
This set focuses on the Scoring.
8 Horse Cards (Set 7 - Trophy)

We recommend mixing several of these horses with other sets to complete a full set of 1-8 to play.

—description from the publisher

Meek Heroes: Victory is a Biblically inspired, 2-4 player competitive strength deck-building game.  Each player moves their hero throughout the city to collect resources, purchase upgrades, and charge their mech.  When the player feels they are ready for battle (which can happen at any time), they start battling bosses at the henchmen level and work their way up through the agents and finally to the principalities.

To battle a boss, a player uses their deck to play cards adding up strength points, similar to a game of war against a target number.  A player wins a battle if the strength points are equal to or greater than the boss's strength and have not played an auto-loss card that they could not negate.

Players may call upon allies for support and continue to upgrade as they press through battles.  Enemies will grow stronger.  The first player to redeem 3 henchmen, redeem 2 agents, and banish 2 principalities is the winner.

It is a race, so go, fight, win!

-description by Joe of Meek Heroes Gaming'

In this fast-paced game, players are adventurers entering a cave full of monsters, using artifacts and magic to defeat as many of them as possible.

Each round, each player chooses an artifact to use, and can take turns playing magics to improve their artifacts or make their opponents' artifacts worse. At the end of the round, the player with the best artifact wins the monster currently in the cave, and each player chooses to complete their hand of cards or change their hand of cards completely.

A new monster is revealed and a number of rounds dependent on the number of players are held.

At the end of the game, each type of monster scores in a different way and whoever has the most points wins.

Ancient Egypt. As he could not lie at home, old Ahmed spent his days playing “Thebes Lies” with his bazaar clients. He used postcards of the city, Cleo, the Bello Camel, the Crazy Mummy and the famous architect, Baser. There’s still room for someone else in the next game, would you like to join?

In Thebes Lies, you will become one of Ahmed's bazaar clients and you will gamble for your purchase in a game of cunning and deception. “Mmm... I have my doubts...” Will you manage to win and leave without having to pay? Give it a try, your fabrics and spices seem to be expensive.

GAME IDEA

In Thebes Lies, you play an uncertain number of rounds until someone gets 3 new amphorae or 3 broken amphorae. In both cases the game ends and there will be a winner. During each round, you must play your cards astutely, either by tricking your opponents or — if you think they are lying — by revealing the cards played and checking them.

—description from the publisher

The dazzling world of SM is recreated in a card game,
Can you make 10 beautiful women your mistresses by captivating them with your pleasure and abuse?

A trainer brings beautiful young girls into a world of dazzling pleasures.

How many beautiful women you will be able to make captive of SM and make them your mistress depends on your technique.

boardgame: Carbon City Zero: A Collaborative Board Game
description:
In this deck-building game, the team picks a mayor to support that starts at 50 on the Carbon Tracker, who must race against the timer playing piece on the Time Tracker. Each player starts with an identical deck of cards, and their turn is made up of three parts: Action, Recycle, Draw.

In each turn, you can buy additional cards from a shared marketplace with the powers to create a more sustainable city. But, amongst the deck of cards you have snag cards, global cards and special power cards, all valued in cost and carbon that could speed or slow your path to victory. Balancing the need to generate income with reducing carbon, you can follow numerous paths to victory, but collaboration is key.

You'll need to bring together governments, industries, and the public. It’s a conversation making game, where you will have to discuss tactics with your teammates.

You win by getting the carbon level to 0 before you reach ’Time’s Up’ and run out of time. And if you can't bring carbon levels to zero before it's too late, everybody loses. The stakes couldn't be higher.

The game can also be played competitively by each player taking a mayor to race down the carbon tracker, or solo by taking two draw decks to play from.

-description from publisher

boardgame: 花手水に福来たる (Fortune Comes to Hanachuzu)
description:
Hanachōzu is a bowl of colorful flowers that is used to purify one's hands at temples and shrines.
Prosperous business, success in love, longevity in good health...

As the chief priest, you will decorate the flower chozubachi beautifully and make the temple the most blessed.
A competitive set collection game where you spread flowers on a 3x7 water square and compete for the number of profitable roles.

By forming sets of 3 or more of certain flowers, claim your blessings from the Gods. If the blessing belongs to others, steal them by outdoing them with your flower display
Don't forget to set bamboo in your chozubachi to hold ladles for people to use
Whoever holds the most blessing at the end of the game is the winner

A skill-building two-player battle card game that tests your choices and courage with simple rules!

Three interesting points
① Psychological reading!
Each player takes out one card from their hand and wins! Compare the numbers on the cards and the bigger one wins!
Guess what cards will come out from the skill used by the other party and read the back of the back!

② Lose and build skills!
Activate skills by manipulating the souls of the dead who lost the game! Take advantage of the next game!
Dare to lose and build skills! ? Build while losing and aim for victory with a keen eye!

③ Hire and increase your hand!
Hire unique mercenaries to increase your strength! Aim for victory with various skills!
First-come, first-served from the two cards on the front!
The timing to acquire is also an important choice!

Victory/End Conditions There are three conditions for victory !
The player who fulfills any of these conditions wins!
If you win 10 times before your opponent, you win!
You win when your opponent runs out of cards!
If you beat the king with a clown or angel, you win!

Become a mighty forest spirit, master of space and time, protector of mushrooms and trees. Alone or with others, you face an evil creature from ancient times. Ally with trolls, goblins and stranger creatures of the forest. Will you be able to solve all the puzzles and save the forest?

The goal of the game is to escape from a time loop. To achieve that, the party has to make decisions which cost time points. When the time points are used up, the party has to restart the game, but keeps collected items and information that can be combined to solve the mysteries. The group wins when it escapes the time loop with a perfect game.

Meigo is a territory game for two players: Black and White. It is played on the intersections (points) of an initially empty square grid (board). There is also an off-board location called prison. Each player has access to a sufficient supply of stones of their own color. One of the two sides of each stone is marked.

				
				
					DefinitionsA chain is a stone along with all stones one can reach from it through a series of steps onto orthogonally adjacent stones of its color.

A liberty of a chain is an empty point orthogonally adjacent to it.

A stone is marked if its marked side is up, and unmarked otherwise.

A point reaches a stone if there is a free path along the lines of the board between the two.

				
				
					PlayBlack plays first, then turns alternate. On your first turn of the game, place an unmarked stone of your color on an empty point. On any other turn, play or hold. To hold, remove an enemy stone from the prison. To play, follow these steps:


     Select an empty point.
     If the selected point reaches an unmarked friendly stone and no marked stones of either color, or the selected point is orthogonally adjacent to an unmarked friendly stone, place an unmarked friendly stone on that point. Otherwise, place a marked friendly stone on that point.
     If the chain that includes your placement has both marked and unmarked stones, unmark (by flipping them) all marked stones in that chain.
     Move to the prison all enemy chains without liberties. If the prison now contains stones of both colors, remove pairs of opposite-colored stones from it until at most one color remains.


At the end of your turn, the stone you just placed must be part of a chain with at least one liberty. You cannot place a marked stone and move a marked stone to the prison on the same turn if your opponent just placed a marked stone.

The last player to play or hold wins.

Before the game begins, to make it fair, the first player places a number of black stones in the prison, and then the second player chooses sides. For handicap games, the weaker player takes Black and opens instead by placing on the board a number of unmarked black stones proportional to the skill gap between the players.

				
				
					NotesMeigo is a seemingly finite game that plays almost like Go. It was inspired by Cavity, a Go variant created by Michael Amundsen. Christopher Field provided valuable input that helped refine the rules of the game. The prison mechanism was introduced by David Wolfe and Elwyn Berlekamp in their book Mathematical Go: Chilling Gets the Last Point.

-description from designer

Published in Wargames Illustrated 075, a very simple set of miniatures wargames rules aimed at mid C18 conflicts, including the Seven Years' War, the French & Indian Wars and the Jacobite Rebellions.

They are designed for quick battles, played to a conclusion, using a moderate number of units each with a small number of figures (e.g. 8 figs/infantry battalion). The key concept within the rules is to assume that obvious things are happening i.e. there is no 'musketry' sub-system, it is just assumed that if a unit is in effective range, it is firing.  There are some optional rules for including command and control issues, and a few optional rules for specific conflicts.

boardgame: Imperial Fever: Great Power Competition in the Late 19th Century
description:
Imperial Fever is a deckbuilding game for three to four players that recreates the period between 1881 and 1915. Players will vie for world dominance as they take on the roles of the United Kingdom, France, the Central Empires (Germany and Austria-Hungary) and the Emergent Powers (the USA and Japan). The game features a unique approach to this complex and important period of world history, including the following innovative aspects:


     A deckbuilding mechanism that will allow players to pursue different strategies.
     A variety of other mechanics, including area majority, push your luck, tug of war, technology tracks, and set collection.
     Competition between players in different areas: national prestige, military power, naval dominance, colonial expansion, and key strategic areas.
     International tensions that may culminate in the outbreak of the First World War.
     A wide range of political agendas that will allow players to choose among different paths to victory.
     A rich Event deck that poses additional challenges to players, ranging from international conflicts to political mishaps at home.
     The influence of non-player powers such as Russia, China, Holland, Belgium, Spain, and Portugal.
     The full game is playable in under four hours, once players are reasonably familiar with the rules.


—description from the publihser

In the depths of the cosmos, a battlefield awaits in Aurora Citadel. This gripping game seamlessly combines the elements of deckbuilding and area control, immersing players in a dynamic conflict.

With each turn, Aurora Citadel presents a captivating challenge: how will you maneuver through the battlefield? As you accumulate control, the lure of purchasing new units beckons, but choose wisely.

Prepare to engage in a symphony of warfare as Aurora Citadel unveils its diverse array of units. Infantry, swift and adaptable, prove formidable against ranged units, whose precision strikes can decimate the armor division. Yet, armored behemoths possess the resilience to withstand the relentless onslaught of infantry forces.

Enter the cosmic arena and stake your claim in Aurora Citadel. With seven unique units across three distinct categories, the possibilities for strategic mastery are endless. The path to victory lies in your hands as you command your troops, chart your course, and seize control.

—description from the designer

Tick Tack Bumm variant with a color flashing bomb. The players have to guess words which fit to the category which matches the color of the bomb. When the bomb explodes, the player who had it, will get a card as minus-point. After 8 points the game ends and the player with less points wins the game.

A guessing game with funny themes. Try to guess the next card you’ll draw, immerse yourself in the world of Friends, with this game that uses all the codes of the series: humor, fun and its little crazyside.

Modrá kráva (Blue Cow) is an reaction party game where nothing is certain and words do not reflect reality. Basically, it's simple - just turn over a card and say what's on the card. Is there a blue cow? But what if blue is actually green and the cow is actually a dog?

Players take turns flipping over animal cards from the deck and saying what animal is shown on the card and what colour it is, taking into account all the rules in the game - for example:

If the following rules are in play and a player draws a card with a BLUE DOG on it, he must say RED COW:
DOG = COW
BLUE = RED

If any other player feels that the active player has made a mistake, they will announce it and then they proceed to check all active rules. If the player actually made a mistake, he loses a life. If everything was correct, the player who announced the mistake loses a life. When a player looses his last life, they are eliminated from the game.

There are also special cards in the deck, that will force you to either make additional combinations of the rules or change the existing ones.

After all cards from the deck has been flipped over, the round is over. Shuffle the discard pile to make a new deck so the new round can begin.
 The game ends when there is only one player left or after four rounds.

Traxit is an abstract boardgame, where the goal is to be as close to the top as possible at the right moment. The catch is that the opponent to your right decides which of 16 path tiles you must use for your next movement.

Each player has 2 pawns and 16 path tiles. Each of the 16 paths are unique. Your path tiles are given to the player to your right. Players each chose a side on the lower level, and place their pawns freely outside of the corners.

You opponent chooses which path you have to use. You can rotate the path any way you like, giving you up to 8 different moves per pawn for assymetrical paths. After use, the path tile is discarded. You can't move through other pawns, nor outside the board, and if you can't move at all, your opponent takes one of your pawns and moves it to the lowest level. This move is called a Traxit, which is latin for being pulled.

Scoring
At the end of every 4 rounds (4, 8, 12 and 16) you score points for the position of your pawns (0, 25, 50 or 100) depending on how close to the top they are.

Bonus
A 50 point bonus is added for getting both your pawns to the top at any time.

Variants
There is also a short version of the game, where you only play 12 of the 16 path tiles, and a team variant where you team up with the player opposite you, to play against the players to your sides.

Swap Pall Mall for Goodrich Castle and Mayfair for Pendennis castle in this special English Heritage Castles edition of Monopoly.

English Heritage’s Castles edition of the world famous Monopoly game is an exciting adventure through some of England’s finest fortifications. Starting from GO, discover English Heritage’s most famous sites, while trying to avoid paying tithe taxes or having to repair your battlements after a siege!

Acquire various castles' colour groups to begin building market stalls and barns, increasing the rent and strengthening your purse ready for more acquisitions! Stay sharp- because there’s only room at the top for one. For everyone else, there’s bankruptcy. Compete to take all the sites and become ruler of the kingdom!

Welcome to the wacky, tail-wagging world of Wacky Puppies! An explosively fun card game where strategy and dog puns collide.

Your mission? Be the last puppy standing by avoiding the explosive combo of the bomb and detonator cards because…Bam! You're out if you nab both. But fret not! You can dodge those explosive mishaps and still stay in the game if you play your cards right.

It's a game of high stakes hide-and-seek with a deck of 100 wild cards, giving you the power to dodge doom, mess with friends, or bring in a tidal wave of silliness. As the deck dwindles, the suspense (and giggles) just keeps growing!

—description from the designer

Prepare your sheet, make plot decisions, roll the dice, fight, equip, imagine what will happen, consult the oracle… because now you are the protagonist. If you are reading this paragraph, it means that you have decided to enter Black Sphynx, a collection of books designed for solo roleplaying.

This full-color book in PDF format is a visual oracle whose main purpose is to give you the opportunity to live an incredibly ‘replayable’ adventure. As such, it will be in some ways unlike anything you’ve seen before. In short, what distinguishes this oracle from many others is its ability to create a very visual story. As a result, you will travel through a world that will always be different, set against the backdrop of a goal-based adventure. So, from a starting point, you will shape the game universe by going through different pages.

As you progress, you will discover images that will present you with a variety of challenges. These may be physical, mental, social or resource-related. Successfully overcoming one of these challenges can earn you Action Points, so that you can progress from one act to the next until you reach the final challenge and end the adventure.

If you are already used to using an oracle, you will find that this one will guide you quite a lot, giving you the necessary guidelines to enter the world presented to you and live your story. To play this book, you will also need the first volume of the Black Sphynx collection, entitled The Sibyl’s Scrolls. This is an extensive collection of oracles, easy and quick to use, which will be very useful as you move through the game universe. In this way, we will use The Sibyl’s Scrolls to provide narrative, description, additional information and answers to the events of your adventure. Don’t worry if you have any questions about this now, as they will be answered throughout the rules.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Fields of Fire III: The Parachute Regiment
description:
Arnhem, The Netherlands, 1944: 2 PARA’s chaplain is pinned down by German fire while trying to cross the street to get to injured soldiers. The commanding officer of A Company, Major Digby Tatham-Warter, runs to him and says, "Don't worry about the bullets, I've got an umbrella” before escorting him back across the street.

In this new, stand-alone volume from the team behind Fields of Fire Deluxe Edition, Fields of Fire Volume III sees the long awaited introduction of the British to Ben Hull’s masterpiece of tactical infantry command. From the burning streets of Arnhem to war-torn Helmand Province, Afghanistan, and via the bleak South Atlantic Falklands Islands, we follow the 2nd Battalion of The Parachute Regiment, commonly known as “2 PARA”, across over 60 years of military history.

As Britain’s elite Airborne Infantry, the Parachute Regiment are used as spearhead troops and are often inserted behind enemy lines, by parachute or in modern times by helicopter. Lightly equipped due to the nature of their insertion, they need all their training, resilience, and fighting spirit to survive until other units can fight their way to them.

Highlights – What’s New in Volume III


     When the company radios fail, make do with more uniquely British options like bugles.
     Use unique British weaponry such as PIAT anti-tank weapons during WWII and vehicles like Scorpions and WMIK in the more modern theatres of the Falklands and Afghanistan.
     Urban Defensive missions! Fight amongst rubble and fires of Arnhem, and try to hold on to your pocket by whatever means necessary as the enemy attacks utilizing a completely new urban offensive enemy hierarchy chart.
     Experience the constraints of modern day combat in Afghanistan as you try to determine whether groups of people and incoming vehicles are hostile, or merely civilians going about their lives.
     Collapsing buildings, an enemy helicopter assault, mine-sweeping duties, night assaults, and much, much more!


—description from the publisher

Murder We Wrote is a true crime podcast themed adult party game. As the host of the podcast, reveal the name of the victim for that round. Other players will assume the role of someone close to the victim, playing cards and giving an interview on who they think murdered the victim and how.

At the end of the round, the host will decide what the true ending is and who gets points. The player who with the most points, wins the game.

With hundreds of different potential witnesses, victims, murders, and deaths, there are infinite stories to tell!
